name: Dermatitis or eczema of perianal area
definition: Dermatitis (eczema) involving perianal skin.

name: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa with pyloric atresia
definition: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa with pyloric atresia is a severe subtype of junctional epidermolysis bullosa characterised by generalised blistering at birth and congenital atresia of the pylorus and rarely of other portions of the gastrointestinal tract.
synonyms: JEB - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa] with pyloric atresia | JEB-PA - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa with pyloric atresia] | JEB-PA | Carmi syndrome | epidermolysis bullosa junctionalis with pyloric atresia | junctional epidermolysis bullosa-pyloric atresia syndrome

name: Dermatophytosis of nail
definition: Fungal infection of the nail plate due to dermatophyte fungi (tinea unguium). Infection results in a range of clinical signs including white or yellow discolouration, detachment of the plate from the nail bed (onycholysis), keratinous thickening under the nail plate (subungual hyperkeratosis) and fragility and fragmentation of the abnormal nail plate.
synonyms: Onychomycosis due to dermatophyte | Ringworm of nails | Tinea of nail | Tinea unguium | dermatophytic onychomycosis

name: Perianal lichen simplex
definition: Circumscribed pruritic lichenification of perianal localisation.

name: Adverse reaction to injection of neurotoxin
definition: Adverse event resulting from use of neurotoxins, especially botulinum toxin, into the skin. This is most commonly administered for aesthetic reasons. Recognised problems include ptosis, diplopia and hypersensitivity to the toxin. Details of the reaction should be coded separately.
cn_name: 注射神经毒素的不良反应

name: Mental conditions affecting the skin
definition: This group includes cutaneous artefacts and disorders of cutaneous image and perception including delusional states and body dysmorphic disorder.
synonyms: psychological or psychiatric conditions affecting the skin

name: Desmoplastic trichoepithelioma
synonyms: desmoplastic trichoepithelioma

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, scapula
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, scapula

name: Discoid lupus erythematosus
definition: Discoid lupus erythematosus is characterised by the presence of discoid plaques showing varying degrees of oedema, erythema, scaling, follicular plugging and atrophy. It typically involves the face, ears and scalp, but widespread dissemination may occur. It can cause marked disfigurement with prominent facial scarring and permanent hair loss.
synonyms: CDLE - [Chronic discoid lupus erythematosus] | Chronic discoid lupus erythematosus | DLE - [Discoid lupus erythematosus] | discoid lupus NOS | DLE | scarring alopecia of lupus erythematosus
snomed: 13902000 "discoid lupus erythematosus";156365002 "discoid lupus erythematosus";200938002 "discoid lupus erythematosus";238927000 "discoid lupus erythematosus"

name: Cutaneous disorders attributable to acquired or iatrogenic immunodeficiency
definition: Skin manifestations of immune deficiency which may present with unusual and exuberant forms of infection, a predisposition to cutaneous malignancy or a range of other poorly understood, often inflammatory skin disorders.

name: Giant cell arteritis with polymyalgia rheumatica
Mandibuloacral dysplasia
definition: Mandibuloacral dysplasia is characterised by postnatal growth retardation (late closure of the fontanelles), craniofacial anomalies and skeletal malformations, such as mandibular and clavicular hypoplasia (conferring an appearance of a receding chin and slopping shoulders), and mottled cutaneous pigmentation. Dental anomalies, acroosteolysis, joint rigidity and small stature have also been reported. They can be associated with adipose tissue loss in the limbs and sometimes with premature ageing
synonyms: MAD - [mandibuloacral dysplasia]

name: Exacerbation of constitutional dermatitis due to occupational exposure to contact allergens
definition: Exacerbation of constitutional dermatitis such as atopic eczema due to superadded allergic contact dermatitis from allergen encountered at work.

name: Varicose ulcer of nasal septum
synonyms: nasal septum varicose ulcer

name: Folliculitis decalvans
definition: Folliculitis decalvans is characterised by the progression of scalp folliculitis to extensive inflammation, follicular destruction and scarring. In a variant called tufted folliculitis the hairs become clumped into tufts containing a dozen or more hairs. In contrast to the course in the vast majority of people who develop a bacterial pustular folliculitis of the scalp, individuals with folliculitis decalvans appear unable to eradicate Staphylococcus aureus from the hair follicle even with approp

name: Alopecia areata of beard
definition: Alopecia areata affecting the beard area

name: Drug-induced toxic epidermal necrolysis
cn_name: 中毒性表皮坏死松解症

name: Atopic eruption of pregnancy
definition: Atopic eruption of pregnancy is the commonest inflammatory skin problem associated with pregnancy. In contrast to other pregnancy dermatoses, it usually manifests itself within the first two trimesters. Although there is no history of previous atopic eczema in 80% of cases, patients often report having sensitive skin and/or a personal or family history of atopic disease. Most patients have elevated immunoglobulin E levels. Clinically the eruption may be obviously eczematous or appear as a widesp
synonyms: Atopic prurigo of pregnancy | AEP | besnier's prurigo gestationis | eczema in pregnancy | linear IgM disease of pregnancy | nurse's early-onset prurigo of pregnancy | prurigo of pregnancy | pruritic folliculitis of pregnancy | spangler's papular dermatitis of pregnancy

name: Psoriasis of specified site or distribution
definition: The appearance, management and impact of psoriasis can vary considerably according to its location. Important variants are listed under this heading.

name: Cutaneous markers of internal malignancy
definition: A range of generally uncommon skin signs which may point to the presence of an internal malignancy
cn_name: 内部恶性肿瘤的皮肤标志

name: Systemic bacterial infection affecting skin
definition: Skin involvement associated with bacteraemia or internal foci of bacterial infection (e.g. endocarditis). The skin manifestations may represent the first sign of fulminant life-threatening infection as in meningococcal septicaemia.

name: Toxic porphyria cutanea tarda
definition: Porphyria cutanea tarda resulting from exposure to hexachlorobenzene and other polychlorinated aromatic hydrocarbons. It has occurred in epidemic form due to ingestion of hexachlorobenzene-treated wheat seedlings.
synonyms: Porphyria turcica

name: Burn of scalp, any part
synonyms: scalp burn

name: Umbilical sinus
definition: If only the distal end of the vitelline duct closes a proximal sinus in the umbilicus will remain which leads to a potential source of infection in the newborn

name: Venous dilatation
synonyms: vein dilatation

name: Alopecia or hair loss
definition: Disorders characterised by abnormal temporary or permanent loss of hair, particularly from the scalp and beard.
synonyms: alopecia NOS | calvities | hair loss/balding | hair loss/baldness

name: Corrosion of unspecified degree of trunk
Chronic urticaria due to pseudoallergy
definition: Chronic urticaria caused by food or drug intolerance.

name: Demodex infestation of eyelid
definition: Demodex folliculorum and Demodex brevis are commonly found in eyelash follicles and in meibomian glands of the eyelid. Heavy infestation has been associated with madarosis and with treatment-resistant chronic blepharitis.
synonyms: Dermatitis of eyelid due to Demodex species

name: Autosomal recessive hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia
definition: Ectodermal hypohidrotic or anhidrotic dysplasia is an ectodermal dysplasia syndrome characterised by decreased or absent sudation, intolerance to heat, dry, sparse hair, very fine skin, total or partial lack of teeth, characteristic facial dysmorphism, all of which are often associated with ocular, digestive, or pulmonary disorders.

name: Dominant bullous dermolysis of the newborn
definition: A variant of dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa presenting with variable degrees of blistering in the neonatal period due to a deficiency of type VII collagen at the dermoepidermal junction. An increase in type VII collagen at the dermoepidermal junction over the first few weeks or months of life is accompanied by clinical improvement.
synonyms: DDEB-BDN - [dominant bullous dermolysis of the newborn]

name: Skin injury due to exposure to corrosive substances
definition: Damage to the skin caused by contact with corrosive or caustic substances. These include acids, alkalis and a large range of other agents (e.g. wet cement, phenolic compounds, peroxides, halogenated solvents).

name: Lymphoedema due to dependency and immobility
definition: Lymphoedema occurring in immobile individuals with reduced muscle pump activity as a result of paralysis or infirmity. It is particularly liable to develop in those who are unable to sleep recumbent.
synonyms: Armchair legs | Lymphoedema secondary to dependency and immobility | Wheelchair legs

name: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to cytochrome P450 oxidoreductase deficiency
definition: This refers to any of several autosomal recessive diseases resulting from mutations of genes for enzymes mediating the biochemical steps of production of cortisol from cholesterol by the adrenal glands (steroidogenesis). This diagnosis is due to cytochrome P450 oxidoreductase deficiency.
synonyms: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to cytochrome POR deficiency

name: Dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, nails only
definition: Dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, nails only is a rare subtype of dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa that shows no blistering and that is characterised by dystrophic or absent nails.
synonyms: DDEB, nails only - [dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, nails only] | DDEB-na - [dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, nails only] | Dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, nails only

name: Drug-induced Stevens-Johnson syndrome
definition: This is one of the four principal forms of severe cutaneous adverse reaction to drugs (SCARs) and is characterised by inflammation, blistering and erosion of skin and mucous membranes. By definition, there is involvement of at least one mucous membrane and skin detachment is limited to less than 10% of body surface area. Most cases occur within the first 8 weeks of drug exposure. The drugs most commonly involved are antimicrobial sulfonamides, anticonvulsants, allopurinol, nevirapine and oxicam–

name: Lichen sclerosus of vulva
definition: Lichen sclerosus of the vulva is an inflammatory disorder of unknown aetiology affecting the skin of the vulva and perianal area. Typically it affects women in the fifth and sixth decades of life though is not uncommon in prepubertal girls. It presents with pruritus, soreness or dyspareunia. The affected skin is white and atrophic, though secondary changes including maceration, purpura and erosion may dominate the clinical picture. In longstanding cases there may be marked shrinkage of the vulva
synonyms: Kraurosis vulvae | Lichen sclerosus et atrophicus of vulva | Vulval lichen sclerosus | kraurosis vulvae

name: Neonatal iron overload
definition: A disorder in which iron there is deposition of iron in the liver and extrahepatic tissues and which may present in the neonatal period with jaundice and oedema. There is a high mortality from fulminant liver failure. The causes of iron overload may be apparent (e.g. intrauterine blood transfusion for Rhesus haemolytic disease) though a range of mechanisms including poorly understood maternal factors are likely to be involved in neonatal haemochromatosis, the commonest clinical presentation.

name: AREDYLD syndrome
definition: AREDYLD stands for acral-renal-ectodermal-dysplasia-lipoatrophic-diabetes, and is characterised by lipoatrophy, diabetes mellitus, generalised hypotrichosis, ectodermal dysplasia, renal alterations, dental abnormalities and other manifestations.
synonyms: Acrorenal defect - ectodermal dysplasia - diabetes

name: Perforating dermatoses
definition: A group of skin disorders characterised by trans-epidermal elimination of abnormal matter, especially collagen or elastin, from the dermis to the exterior.

name: Extramammary Paget disease of anus
definition: An intraepithelial adenocarcinoma of apocrine gland-bearing anal and perianal skin. It presents as irritating, itchy, burning, red scaly patches or plaques that may be solitary or multifocal and may be mistaken for an inflammatory dermatosis.

name: Methylmalonic aciduria
definition: Methylmalonic acidemia is an inborn error of vitamin B12 metabolism characterised by gastrointestinal and neurometabolic manifestations resulting from decreased function of the mitochondrial enzyme methylmalonyl-CoA mutase.
synonyms: Methylmalonic acidaemia

name: Infestation by mites
synonyms: acariasis | infestation by mites NOS | mite infestation

name: Speckled or lentiginous melanocytic naevus
definition: Speckled and lentiginous melanocytic naevus is a relatively uncommon form of congenital melanocytic naevus in which multiple grouped punctate pigmented lentiginous macules are confined within a well circumscribed patch of pale macular pigmentation which resembles a café-au-lait macule.

name: Hypertensive ischaemic leg ulcer
definition: Hypertensive ischaemic leg ulcers (Martorell ulcers) manifest as painful necrotic ulcers typically located on the lateral aspect of the calf, which in 50% of cases are bilateral. Histologically there is subcutaneous stenotic arteriolosclerosis. All patients are hypertensive and over half are diabetic. Differentiation from pyoderma gangrenosum and calcific arteriolopathy is important as both prognosis and management are different.
synonyms: Martorell ulcer

name: Erythema nodosum
definition: An erythematous eruption commonly associated with drug reactions or infection and characterised by inflammatory nodules that are usually tender, multiple, and bilateral. These nodules are located predominantly on the shins with less common occurrence on the thighs and forearms. They undergo characteristic colour changes ending in temporary bruise-like areas. This condition usually subsides in 3-6 weeks without scarring or atrophy.
synonyms: erythema contusiformis

name: Raynaud phenomenon due to nonorgan specific systemic autoimmune disorder
definition: Raynaud phenomenon attributable to underlying autoimmune diseases including systemic sclerosis, systemic lupus erythematosus, Sjögren disease or dermatomyositis.
synonyms: Raynaud phenomenon secondary to non organ specific systemic autoimmune disorder

name: Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to metals or metal salts
Erythema ab igne
definition: A characteristic reticular telangiectatic and pigmented dermatosis resulting from repeated or prolonged exposure to infrared radiation (heat) of insufficient energy to produce a burn. It most commonly affects the lower extremities or lower back as a result of habitually sitting too close to a heat source (radiator or open hearth) or from keeping a hot water bottle held against the skin.
synonyms: EAI | fire stains | toasted skin syndrome
snomed: 77140003 "erythema ab igne";156367005 "erythema ab igne";200866009 "erythema ab igne";238510001 "erythema ab igne"

name: Miscellaneous occupationally-acquired skin disorders
definition: Skin disorders of other specified types which can be attributed to occupation

name: Marie Unna congenital hypotrichosis
definition: Marie Unna hereditary hypotrichosis is a rare isolated anomaly of the hair that may be normal, sparse or absent at birth. When present it remains fine and sparse in the first few years of life, then typically during the third year of life it becomes coarse and twisted irregularly. When the hair is absent at birth it may regrow at this stage. After puberty, progressive loss begins at the vertex and produces a severe scarring atrophy.
synonyms: HYPT4 - [Hypotrichosis ] (MIM 146550) | Hypotrichosis 4 (MIM 146550) | Marie Unna hypotrichosis

name: Dermatoses provoked by physical or environmental factors
definition: A group of skin disorders due to exposure of the skin to various external physical, chemical or environmental insults including pressure and pressure sores, cold, heat, sunlight, ionizing radiation, water or the marine environment and venomous or noxious animals.

name: Hypomelanosis resulting from cosmetic procedure
definition: Loss of skin pigmentation attributable to a cosmetic intervention and particularly associated with chemical peels.
cn_name: 美容操作导致的色素减退

name: Cellular neurothekeoma
synonyms: cellular neurothekeoma

name: Plasma cell vulvitis
definition: Plasma cell vulvitis (Zoon vulvitis) is a rare benign chronic inflammatory condition of the vulva of unknown aetiology that presents with symptoms of pruritus, burning, dyspareunia, and dysuria, typically in postmenopausal women. Histologically it is characterised by dermal infiltration with plasma cells, vessel dilatation, and haemosiderin deposition. Its homologue in men is plasma cell balanitis.
synonyms: Zoon vulvitis

name: Self-induced lip trauma
definition: Self-inflicted damage to lips by biting, picking, chewing etc.

name: Multiple carboxylase deficiency due to holocarboxylase synthetase deficiency
definition: Holocarboxylase synthetase deficiency is a life-threatening early-onset form of multiple carboxylase deficiency (see this term), an inborn error of biotin metabolism, that, if untreated, is characterised by vomiting, tachypnoea, irritability, lethargy, exfoliative dermatitis, and seizures that can worsen to coma.

name: Methylmalonyl-CoA epimerase deficiency
definition: This refers to deficiency of an enzyme that catalyzes the isomerization of methylmalonyl-CoA to succinyl-CoA and it is involved in key metabolic pathways. It requires a vitamin B12-derived prosthetic group, adenosylcobalamin, to function.
synonyms: Methylmalonyl-CoA racemase deficiency

name: Port-wine stain
definition: A port-wine stain is defined as a macular telangiectatic area of skin which is present at birth and does not spontaneously involute. Port-wine stains may be localised or extensive and they are often associated with an underlying disorder.
synonyms: Naevus flammeus | CM | nevus flammeus | port-wine stain | PWS

name: Hypohidrosis of undetermined aetiology
definition: Reduced or absent sweating for which no explanation has been found.

name: Gnathostomiasis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the parasitic worm Gnathostoma. This disease is characterised by painful, itchy swelling under the skin from movement of the parasite under the skin. This disease may also initially present with fever, lethargy, abdominal pain, vomiting, or diarrhoea, and may infect other parts of the body (lungs, bladder, eyes, ears, nervous system). Transmission is commonly by ingestion of undercooked contaminated freshwater fish, eels, frogs, birds, or reptiles, or ingest
synonyms: Gnathostomosis | Wandering swelling

name: HFE-related hereditary haemochromatosis, Type 1
definition: Hemochromatosis type 1 (classic) is the most common form of hereditary hemochromatosis (HH), a group of diseases characterised by excessive tissue iron deposition. Hemochromatosis type 1 causes chronic fatigue, bronzed skin pigmentation and tissue damage in the liver, pancreas, joints, bone, endocrine glands, heart, which result in various complications in adulthood including liver fibrosis (cirrhosis with a risk of hepatocellular carcinoma), diabetes mellitus, arthropathy, osteoporosis, hypogon
synonyms: C282Y/C282Y haemochromatosis | Classic haemochromatosis

name: Infantile napkin dermatoses
synonyms: diaper dermatosis | napkin dermatosis

name: Graft-versus-host disease
definition: Graft-versus-host disease (GVHD) occurs when lymphoid cells from an immunocompetent donor are introduced into a histo-incompatible recipient incapable of rejecting them. This usually occurs as a result of haematopoietic stem cell transplantation. The main targets attacked by the donor lymphocytes are the recipient’s skin, gastrointestinal tract and liver. Acute GVHD, normally occurring within the first 100 days following transplantation, has a high mortality. The acute phase may be followed by c
synonyms: GVH - [graft-versus-host] disease | GVH - [graft-versus-host] reaction | GVHD - [graft-versus-host disease] | graft-versus-host reaction or disease | GVHD

name: Filariasis due to Brugia malayi
definition: This is a parasitic disease (usually an infectious tropical disease) that is caused by thread-like nematodes (roundworms) belonging to the superfamily Filarioidea, also known as \"filariae\". This diagnosis is due to a nematode (roundworm), one of the three causative agents of lymphatic filariasis in humans.
synonyms: Brugiosis | Malayan filariasis

name: Mycobacterium ulcerans infection
definition: Mycobacterium ulcerans infection (Buruli ulcer) typically presents as a subcutaneous nodule which breaks down to form a deep painless ulcer which commonly reaches a size of 15 cm in diameter but may extend further to cause extensive tissue damage. The organism is found in wetlands of tropical and subtropical regions of the world, particularly Africa \[Dermatology TAG\].
synonyms: Buruli ulcer | bairnsdale ulcer | searle's ulcer

name: Mikulicz disease
definition: Mikulicz disease is a disorder first reported by Johann von Mikulicz in 1892 and characterised by symmetrical swelling of the lachrymal, submandibular, and parotid glands, with massive infiltration of these glands by mononuclear cells. Serum autoantibodies, such as anti-Ro/SS-A, are usually negative and serum IgG4 concentration may be increased. Unlike Sjögren disease, IgG4-Mikulicz disease is characterised by the formation of lymphoid follicles, but shows lower levels of lymphocytic infiltratio
synonyms: mikulicz disease | sicca syndrome | SjS

name: Asteatotic eczema
definition: Asteatotic eczema develops from asteatosis cutis. In the latter cracking and crazing of the epidermal stratum corneum produces a flaky skin with a reticulate erythema beneath the scales. It occurs particularly on the lower legs. It is more common in the elderly and is provoked by a combination of defatting and desiccation of the epidermis. If inflammation progresses then it may become more pruritic and eczematous, asteatotic eczema.
synonyms: Asteatotic dermatitis | Eczema senilis | Eczéma craquelé | Xerotic eczema | asteatotic eczema | exsiccation eczematic irritant contact dermatitis

name: Dowling-Degos disease
definition: Dowling-Degos disease is a rare autosomal dominant condition which usually presents post-pubertally with multiple small, round pigmented freckle-like macules. They become progressively more numerous and may become reticulate with time. The axillae and groins are the usual sites, although other areas may be involved, including the intergluteal and inframammary folds, neck, scalp, trunk, arms and genitalia. Pigmentation is symmetrical and progressive, but is otherwise asymptomatic.
synonyms: Reticulate pigmented anomaly of the flexures | reticular pigmented anomoly of the flextures> | reticulated pigmented anomaly of the flexures | dark dot disease | reticular pigment anomaly of flexures

name: Leptospira interrogans canicola infection
synonyms: leptospirosis canicola

name: Acquired pseudoxanthoma elasticum
definition: Acquired pseudoxanthoma-like skin changes due to a variety of different mechanisms. In some forms the changes are limited to the skin but in others internal organs may also be affected.

name: Erythema annulare centrifugum
definition: Erythema annulare centrifugum is the prototypic \"annular erythema\". Although it has several characteristic clinical features, it is a diagnosis of exclusion and must be distinguished from the many other specific causes of annular erythema including lupus tumidus, Lyme disease and leprosy. It is characterised by the development of small numbers of pink infiltrated papules which very gradually enlarge whilst fading centrally to form ring-shaped elevated plaques. It most commonly affects the thig
synonyms: EAC | erythema perstans | palpable migrating erythema

name: Superficial frostbite involving multiple body regions
synonyms: Multiple superficial frostbite, NOS

name: Bowen type intraepidermal squamous cell carcinoma
Neutrophilic dermatoses
definition: A group of inflammatory skin disorders characterised by neutrophilic infiltration of the skin.

name: Fish-eye disease
definition: Fish eye disease (FED) is a form of genetic LCAT (lecithin-cholesterol acyltransferase) deficiency characterised clinically by corneal opacifications, and biochemically by significantly reduced HDL cholesterol and partial LCAT enzyme deficiency.
synonyms: Dyslipoproteinaemic corneal dystrophy | Ophthalmic ichthyosis | Partial LCAT - [lecithin-cholesterol acyltransferase] deficiency | Partial lecithin-cholesterol acyltransferase deficiency

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to clothing or footwear
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to exposure to allergens found in clothing and footwear\: these include colophony, p-phenylenediamine (PPD), disperse dyes, potassium dichromate and formaldehyde resins.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to clothing or footwear
cn_name: 衣物或鞋类引起的变应性接触性皮炎

name: Neonatal phototherapy burn
definition: Burn resulting from phototherapy administered to neonate, usually for the treatment of neonatal jaundice.

name: Dirofilariasis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the parasitic worm Dirofilaria. This disease presents with symptoms which depend on the site of infection. In the lungs, this disease may present with chest pain, cough, fever, chill, or haemoptysis. In the skin, this disease may present with subcutaneous nodules that are tender or painful. Other body sites may also be affected. Transmission is through the bite of an infected mosquito. Confirmation is commonly by histopathology of affected tissue.
synonyms: infection by dirofilaria | Dirofilaria infectious disease | Infection by Dirofilaria

name: Colonic infection due to Haemophilus ducreyi
definition: This is an infection in the colon due to a fastidious gram-negative coccobacillus, Haemophilus ducreyi, causing the sexually transmitted disease chancroid, a major cause of genital ulceration in developing countries characterised by painful sores on the genitalia.

name: Meningovascular syphilis
definition: This is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is involving the meningovascular.

name: Urticaria pigmentosa
synonyms: Maculopapular cutaneous mastocytosis | macropapular cutaneous mastocytosis | UP/MPCM

name: Olfactory reference disorder
definition: Olfactory Reference Disorder is characterised by persistent preoccupation with the belief that one is emitting a perceived foul or offensive body odour or breath that is either unnoticeable or only slightly noticeable to others. Individuals experience excessive self-consciousness about the perceived odour, often with ideas of reference (i.e., the conviction that people are taking notice, judging, or talking about the odour). In response to their preoccupation, individuals engage in repetitive an

name: Dermal melanocyte hamartoma
definition: Circumscribed or segmental congenital hamartomatous dermal melanocytosis affecting areas other than Naevus of Ota and Naevus of Ito. It is significantly less common than either of the latter.

name: Turner syndrome
definition: Karyotype missing one X chromosome (45,X0 or 45,X0/46,XX mosaicism); gonads\: ovaries (streak); phenotype female with short stature, amenorrhea (hypergonadotropic hypogonadism), absence of sexual development, webbed neck, low set ears, posterior hairline, widely-spaced nipples, short fourth metacarpals, and increased carrying angle at the elbow (cubitus valgus). Often associated with renal, cardiac and ocular abnormalities.
synonyms: Monosomy X | Ullrich-Turner syndrome | X0 syndrome

name: Genital warts of vulva
definition: Human papillomavirus (HPV) infection of vulval skin. Vulval warts are usually transmitted by sexual contact. On the vulva they may appear as flat moist papules and plaques on the labia or as cauliflower-like papillomata involving the labia, perineum and perigenital skin. In some women the papillomata may become deeply pigmented resembling seborrhoeic keratoses. Infection by oncogenic strains of HPV may induce neoplastic change in the vulval epithelium (vulval intraepithelial neoplasia).
synonyms: Condylomata acuminata of vulva | HPV - [Human papillomavirus] infection of vulva | Vulval warts

name: Chronic ulcerative stomatitis
definition: Chronic ulcerative stomatitis (CUS) is a rare disease which presents with chronic oral mucosal erosions and ulceration resembling erosive lichen planus but refractory to standard therapies. It typically occurs in older women. Diagnosis requires the demonstration of speckled granular perinuclear IgG deposits in the basal and parabasal layers of oral epithelium (stratified epithelium-specific antinuclear or SES-ANA pattern). There is increasing evidence that CUS is an autoimmune disease provoked b
synonyms: CUS - [chronic ulcerative stomatitis]

name: Early chronic graft-versus-host disease
definition: Early chronic graft-versus-host disease is characterised by a lichenoid rash with involvement of oral and occasionally genital mucous membranes. As the disease process continues the skin becomes more poikilodermatous and fibrotic. Reduced tear and saliva production may result in dry eyes and mouth.
synonyms: Chronic lichenoid graft-versus-host disease | chronic lichenoid GVHD - [graft-versus-host disease] | early chronic GVHD - [graft-versus-host disease]

name: Pemphigus
definition: Pemphigus is a group of chronic autoimmune skin diseases characterised by blister formation on the skin and the mucous membranes. The exact causes of the disease are unknown but the disease is mediated by auto-antibodies to desmosome components. Three clinical forms have been characterised. Pemphigus vulgaris, pemphigus foliaceus and pemphigus vegetans. Other variants exist, namely intercellular IgA dermatosis and paraneoplastic pemphigus.

name: Furuncular myiasis
definition: Infestation of the skin by the larvae of the flies Dermatobia hominis (human botfly), Cuterebra, Cordylobia anthropophaga (tumbu fly), Cordylobia (Stasisia) rodhaini, Wohlfahrtia species and Hypoderma species (warble flies). Painful boil-like swellings form where the larvae are deposited, usually on exposed skin of the face, scalp or extremities. They may be accompanied by lymphangitis and regional lymphadenopathy.

name: Inflammatory dermatoses of the perianal area
Lymphoproliferative disorder associated vasculitis
definition: Lymphoproliferative disorders, such as T- and B-cell lymphoma, may present with small vessel vasculitis often limited to the skin, causing palpable purpura or ulceration, and having the histologic features of leukocytoclastic vasculitis. Systemic necrotizing vasculitis has been seen in association with hairy cell leukemia.

name: Borderline leprosy
definition: Borderline leprosy is seen in patients with levels of cell-mediated immune response to Mycobacterium leprae intermediate between those found in tuberculoid and lepromatous leprosy. This designation may be used for those cases which have not been further categorized as borderline tuberculoid or borderline lepromatous and for those categorized as mid-borderline (BB) leprosy. Lesions typically consist of annular plaques with an erythematous infiltrated rim and an almost normal looking centre. Borde
synonyms: Hansen disease, borderline | Mid-borderline leprosy

name: Itching of skin
definition: The sensation of itch in the skin. For persistent itch of unknown cause the term \"Pruritus of unknown cause\" should be used.
synonyms: Pruritus of unspecified aetiology

name: Lamellar nail dystrophy
synonyms: Onychoschizia lamellina

name: Certain nematode infestations affecting the skin
Herpes simplex infection of genitalia or urogenital tract
definition: Herpes simplex infection affecting the vulva and vagina in women and the penis in men. It is more commonly due to infection with Herpes simplex type 2 virus than with type 1 virus.
synonyms: Herpes genitalis | genital herpes NOS
cn_name: 生殖器或泌尿生殖道单纯疱疹病毒感染

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, hand
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, hand

name: Giant cell arteritis without polymyalgia rheumatica
Cutaneous microvascular occlusion due to systemic coagulopathies
definition: Purpura and cutaneous infarction resulting from systemic coagulation disturbances, particularly of the thrombomodulin–protein C/S anticoagulant pathway. Thrombosis may be largely limited to the skin (coumadin necrosis) but cutaneous signs may presage fulminant sepsis as in disseminated intravascular coagulation secondary to meningococcal septicaemia.

name: Xanthoma disseminatum
definition: Xanthoma disseminatum is a rare non-familial disease, characterised by proliferation of histiocytic cells in which lipid deposition is a secondary event. The disease predominantly affects male children and young adults, with involvement of the skin, mucous membranes of eyes and upper respiratory tract, the meninges and rarely other organs including liver, spleen and bone marrow. The skin lesions of xanthoma disseminatum are erythematous, yellow-brown papules and nodules, which are symmetrically
synonyms: disseminated xanthosiderohistiocytosis

name: Cutaneous microvascular disturbances due to monoclonal cryoglobulins
Allergic contact dermatitis of male genitalia
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis affecting the male genitalia. The most commonly encountered allergens are to rubber (condoms etc.) or to constituent chemicals of toiletries and medicaments.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema of male genitalia

name: Tuberculosis of male urethra or penis
definition: A form of periorificial tuberculosis affecting the urethra and glans penis which is usually secondary to tuberculosis of internal genitourinary organs such as kidney and bladder. Patients present with a superficial tuberculous ulcer on the penis. Tuberculous urethritis may result in chronic urethral stricture. Infection may be transmitted to female partners, producing tuberculous ulceration of the vagina or vulva.
synonyms: Penile tuberculosis

name: Apocrine carcinoma
definition: An apocrine sweat gland cancer that derives_from epithelial cells of glandular origin.

Cutaneous or mucocutaneous Crohn disease

synonyms: Cutaneous Crohn disease

name: Juvenile dermatomyositis with myocarditis
definition: Juvenile dermatomyositis with myocarditis is an early-onset systemic inflammatory disorders affecting the skeletal muscles, the skin, and the heart. Symptomatic cardiac involvement is uncommon in acute disease, but up to 50% of dermatomyositis patients will have asymptomatic cardiac involvement on non-invasive testing. Early detection of myocarditis significantly affects survival given that cardiac complications are a more common cause of death.

name: Allergic contact eczema due to low molecular weight allergens not elsewhere classifiable
Hypertrophy of tongue papillae

Proteus syndrome
definition: Proteus syndrome is a sporadic disorder affecting tissue derived from any germinal layer and resulting in tissue overgrowth in a mosaic pattern. The clinical manifestations are highly variable and include macrodactyly, vertebral abnormalities, and asymmetric limb overgrowth and length discrepancy. Hyperostosis, overgrowth of viscera (spleen, thymus), abnormal and disproportionate fat distribution, asymmetric muscle development, connective-tissue nevi, epidermal nevi, and vascular malformations m

name: Oral-facial-digital syndrome type 2
definition: Oral-facial-digital (OFD) type 2 is characterised by hand and feet deformities, facial deformities, midline cleft of the upper lip and tongue hamartomas.

name: Parasitic infestation of eyelid in loiasis
definition: This refers to a parasitic infestation of the eyelid, due to a skin and eye disease caused by the nematode worm, loa loa.

name: Immersion hand or foot
definition: Injury to the skin and soft tissues of the feet and, less commonly, the hands due to prolonged exposure to non-freezing cold and wet conditions. Originally reported in soldiers as trench foot during World War I, it is now more commonly seen in the homeless either because of vagrancy or as the result of conflict. Affected extremities are initially cold, numb, swollen and pulseless; this is followed by a period of intense hyperaemia and pain which then gives way to increased sweating and cold sens

name: Disseminated pthiriasis
definition: Disseminated infestation by Pthirus pubis not only of the pubic region but also of other hairy areas of the body including eyebrows, eyelashes, beard, axillary and areolar hair. In men with extensive body hair, widespread infestation may occur.
synonyms: Disseminated crab louse infestation | Disseminated infestation by pubic lice | Disseminated phthiriasis

name: Oculocutaneous albinism type 3
definition: OCA3 is caused by a mutation in the TYRP1 gene and is commonly called rufous oculocutaneous albinism (ROCA). It occurs principally in Sub-Saharan Africa and is characterised by bright copper-red colouration of the skin and hair and by dilution of the colour of the irides.
synonyms: OCA3 - [Oculocutaneous albinism type 3] | Red oculocutaneous albinism | Rufous oculocutaneous albinism | Xanthism | Xanthous oculocutaneous albinism

name: Infantile Refsum disease
definition: The infantile form of Refsum disease belongs to the group of peroxisomal diseases, a subset of the leukodystrophic diseases. Clinical signs appear at birth, frequently with retinitis pigmentosa often leading to blindness, and with severe hearing loss, associated with cerebellar ataxia, nystagmus, hypotonia, growth retardation, intellectual deficit, mild facial dysmorphism, osteoporosis, hepatomegaly and hypocholesterolaemia.

name: Autosomal recessive basal epidermolysis bullosa simplex
definition: Forms of epidermolysis bullosa simplex with autosomal recessive inheritance involving predominantly keratin 5, keratin 14 and plectin.

name: Drug-induced capillaritis
synonyms: Pigmented purpura due to drug | medicament-induced capillaritis

name: Miliaria profunda
definition: Miliaria profunda is due to rupture of eccrine sweat ducts at the level of or below the dermo–epidermal junction. It nearly always follows repeated attacks of miliaria rubra and occurs most commonly in the tropics. It manifests as multiple small asymptomatic firm pale papules located typically on the trunk but sometimes also on the limbs.
synonyms: Miliaria tropicalis | deep ductal occlusion | mammillaria

name: Xanthoma in association with lipid storage diseases
definition: Xanthomata presenting in association with rare genetic disorders including cerebrotendinous xanthomatosis and sitosterolaemia. Tendinous and tuberous xanthomata may be seen.

Black hairy tongue

name: Acute miliary cutaneous tuberculosis
definition: Acute miliary cutaneous tuberculosis is a disseminated form of tuberculosis due to haematogenous spread. It may be a component of generalised miliary tuberculosis but the underlying disease may not be manifest. Profuse crops of minute bluish papules, nodules, vesicles or pustules develop; some may become necrotic to form small ulcers. Young children or immunosuppressed patients are at particular risk.
synonyms: Tuberculosis cutis miliaris disseminata

name: Dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans
definition: Dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans is a locally invasive tumour arising in the dermis and showing fibroblastic differentiation. It typically presents as a very slowly enlarging firm nodular dermal mass on the trunk or proximal limbs. In its early stages it may be mistaken both clinically and histologically for a benign fibrous histiocytoma. Local recurrence after resection is common.
synonyms: Bednar tumour of unspecified site | DFSP - [dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans] | pigmented dermatofibrosarcoma | DFSP

name: Burn of head or neck except face
definition: Injury to the tissues of the head and neck caused by contact with, for example, heat, steam, chemicals, or electricity.
synonyms: burn of head and neck, degree unspecified | burn of unspecified depth of head and neck | burn or corrosion of head and neck
cn_name: 头或颈部（除外面部）烧伤

name: Hereditary trachyonychia
synonyms: NDNC1 - [nail disorder, nonsyndromic, congenital 1] (MIM161050) | Twenty nail dystrophy

name: HIV-associated lipodystrophy
definition: Lipodystrophy in patients infected with human immunodeficiency virus is characterised by lipoatrophy of the face, extremities and trunk, together with a double chin, accumulation of fat on the back of the neck (`buffalo hump') and intraabdominal obesity. HIV-associated lipodystrophy is currently the most frequent form of lipodystrophy.
synonyms: Lipodystrophy in Human Immunodeficiency Virus-infected patients | Lipodystrophy-HIV related

name: Adverse reaction to injection of botulinum toxin
Mucous membrane pemphigoid with oral or oesophageal involvement
definition: Mucous membrane pemphigoid affecting oral and/or oesophageal mucous membranes
synonyms: Cicatricial pemphigoid of oral mucous membranes

name: Trichodental syndrome
definition: Trichodental syndrome is characterised by the association of fine, dry and short hair with dental anomalies.

name: Muir-Torre syndrome
definition: Muir-Torre syndrome is an alternative form (variant) of hereditary non-polyposis colorectal cancer (HNPCC) with sebaceous tumours and keratoacanthocytomas. People with Muir-Torre syndrome are also at risk for developing certain skin changes in adulthood that may form in the sebaceous glands. Two genes have been linked to Muir-Torre syndrome, MLH1 and MSH2. People with Muir-Torre syndrome have an increased risk of the types of cancer seen in HNPCC.
synonyms: cutaneous sebaceous neoplasms and keratoacanthomas, with gastrointestinal and other carcinomas | MTS

name: Subcutaneous fat necrosis of the newborn
Nodular prurigo
definition: A chronic highly pruritic dermatosis of poorly understood aetiology which presents with multiple warty nodules on the skin, particularly on the limbs. Exudation, crusting and scale result from repeated scratching.
synonyms: Hyde's disease | Picker's nodules | Prurigo nodularis

name: Onychomycosis
definition: Fungal infection of fingernails and/or toenails due most commonly to dermatophytes (tinea unguium) or yeasts, especially Candida species.
synonyms: Fungal infection of the nails

name: Self-healing juvenile cutaneous mucinosis
definition: Self-healing juvenile cutaneous mucinosis is a rare disorder characterised by an early age of onset accompanied by inflammatory phenomena and spontaneous resolution over a few months. Clinically, there are papules, nodules or plaques with a predilection for the head and trunk. Histologically, there is dermal mucinosis and a mild increase in fibroblasts and mast cells. It is postulated that a temporary alteration of fibroblast synthetic function occurs, possibly as a result of a viral infection.

name: Congenital reticular ichthyosiform erythroderma
synonyms: CRIE - [congenital reticular ichthyosiform erythroderma] | confetti ichthyosis | congenital reticular ichthyosiform erythroderma | CRIE | ichthyosis variegata | ichthyosis with confetti

name: Secondary cutaneous mucinosis
definition: Cutaneous mucinosis may be found in a variety of different settings either purely histologically or with clinical manifestations, usually papular mucinosis. Examples include L-tryptophan poisoning, malignant atrophic papulosis and lupus erythematosus.

name: Congenital syphilitic synovitis

definition: Forms of cutis laxa with autosomal recessive inheritance

name: Behçet disease
definition: Behçet disease is a disease of incompletely understood aetiopathogenesis characterised by recurrent oral and/or genital aphthous ulcers accompanied by cutaneous, ocular, articular, gastrointestinal, and/or central nervous system inflammatory lesions. Small vessel vasculitis, thrombotic vasculopathy, arteritis and arterial aneurysms may occur. It has a high prevalence from the Eastern Mediterranean across Central Asia to China and Japan.
synonyms: Adamantiades-Behçet disease

name: Neonatal acne
definition: Acne presenting at birth or shortly afterwards, generally with predominantly comedonal lesions of the cheeks and a paucity of inflammatory lesions. It is thought to be due to hyperactivity of the sebaceous glands stimulated by neonatal androgens from the testes in boys and adrenals in girls.

name: Gorlin syndrome
definition: Gorlin syndrome, also known as naevoid basal cell carcinoma syndrome (NBCCS), is a hereditary condition characterised by a wide range of developmental abnormalities (odontogenic keratocysts of the jaws, hyperkeratosis of palms and soles, skeletal abnormalities, intracranial ectopic calcifications, and facial dysmorphism) and a predisposition to develop malignant neoplasms (such as multiple basal cell carcinomas or medulloblastoma), and benign neoplasms in the jaw, heart, or ovaries.
synonyms: NBCCS - [Naevoid basal cell carcinoma syndrome] | Naevoid basal cell carcinoma syndrome | nevoid basal cell carcinoma

name: Amicrobial pustulosis of the folds
definition: A rare pustular neutrophilic dermatosis which may be associated with systemic autoimmune diseases, particularly systemic lupus erythematosus. It presents most commonly in young adult women with multiple small sterile pustules affecting predominantly the face, scalp, and skin flexures.

name: Early congenital syphilis, symptomatic
definition: A disease affecting newborns or children up to 2 years of age, caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum pallidum in utero. This disease is characterised by premature birth, hepatosplenomegaly, skeletal abnormalities, and bullous skin disease. Transmission is by vertical transmission.
cn_name: 早期先天性梅毒，有症状

name: Extramammary Paget disease of skin
definition: An intraepithelial adenocarcinoma of apocrine gland-bearing skin and mucous membrane. Clinically it presents as sharply demarcated erythematous plaques most commonly affecting the vulva in women and perianal skin in men.
synonyms: EMPD - [extramammary Paget disease]
cn_name: 乳腺以外的皮肤佩吉特病

name: Skin fragility - plakoglobin deficiency
definition: A rare autosomal recessive skin fragility syndrome due to mutations in the JUP gene which encodes plakoglobin, a protein essential for epidermal integrity. It presents with widespread erosions, sparse woolly hair, nail dystrophy and focal palmoplantar keratoderma.
synonyms: EBS-plakoglobin

name: Barber-Say syndrome
definition: Barber Say syndrome is a genetic syndrome characterised by the association of hypertrichosis, atrophic skin, ectropion and microstomia.

name: Genetic syndromes with abnormalities of the hair shaft
cn_name: 遗传性毛干异常综合征

name: Myelitis due to Treponema pallidum
definition: An inflammatory disorder of the spinal cord due to infection by Treponema pallidum. The symptoms and signs may be acute or subacute in onset, and include paraparesis or paraplegia, sensory loss appropriate with the level of the spinal cord affected, loss of sphincter control, and lower motor neurone signs. The spinal fluid usually shows a lymphocytosis, elevated protein level, and a normal or slightly low glucose level. Diagnosis may be confirmed by neuroimaging, spinal fluid analysis, and serol
synonyms: spinal cord syphilis

name: Cholinergic urticaria or related conditions
definition: A range of urticarial disorders associated with heat and activation of sweating.

IgA vasculitis
definition: Vasculitis, with IgA1-dominant immune deposits, affecting small vessels (predominantly capillaries, venules, or arterioles). Often involves skin and gut, and frequently causes arthritis. Glomerulonephritis indistinguishable from IgA nephropathy may occur.

name: Staphylococcal scalded skin syndrome
definition: A syndrome caused by an infection with the gram-positive bacteria Staphylococcus. This syndrome is characterised by fever, blisters, erythema, large areas of skin peel, or Nikolsky’s sign. Transmission is by direct or indirect contact with an infected individual, through fomites, or by iatrogenic transmission. Confirmation is by identification of Staphylococcus in a blood or skin sample.
synonyms: Ritter disease | SSSS - [staphylococcal scalded skin syndrome] | pemphigus neonatorum | ritter's disease | SSSS | Dermatitis exfoliativa neonatorum | Pemphigus neonatorum | Scalded skin syndrome | Staphylococcal scalded skin syndrome | Toxic epidermal necrolysis, subcorneal type

synonyms: Secondary syphilitic iridocyclitis

name: Oral mucositis due to radiotherapy
definition: This is the painful inflammation and ulceration of the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, usually as an adverse effect of chemotherapy and radiotherapy treatment for cancer, due to the use of ionizing radiation, generally as part of cancer treatment to control or kill malignant cells.
synonyms: radiation induced oral mucositis | radiation-induced oropharyngeal mucositis

name: Diffuse dyslipidaemic plane xanthoma
definition: This is due to extensive lipid deposition in the skin and is associated with familial hypercholesterolaemia or hyperlipidaemia type 3 (Type III hyperliproteinaemia). It manifests as widely distributed yellow macules or flat plaques.

name: Prurigo pigmentosa
definition: A rare pruritic inflammatory dermatosis characterised by recurrent eruptions of itchy erythematous macules and papules succeeded by reticulate pigmentation affecting preferentially the trunk. The cause is unknown but there is a neutrophilic infiltrate in early lesions and the inflammation responds clinically to agents known to suppress neutrophilic chemotaxis including minocycline and dapsone. It is reported most commonly in young women from Japan.

name: Acquired hypermelanosis
definition: Increased melanin pigmentation of the skin resulting from disease or from other stimuli including ultraviolet radiation and hormones.

name: Cryptotia

name: Oral-facial-digital syndrome type 5
definition: Oral-facial-digital syndrome, type 5 is characterised by median cleft of the upper lip, postaxial polydactyly of hands and feet, and oral manifestations (duplicated frenulum).

name: Wrinkly skin syndrome

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to dental materials
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to sensitisation to agents used in dentistry. Dentists, dental nurses and dental technicians are generally at greater risk than their clients. Important allergens include acrylates and methacrylates, disinfectant aldehydes and fragrances.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to dental materials
cn_name: 牙科材料引起的变应性接触性皮炎

name: Cutaneous lupus erythematosus
definition: Lupus erythematosus involving the skin. This ranges from acute cutaneous lupus as may accompany a flare of systemic lupus erythematosus to a variety of chronic forms which are in the majority of cases limited to the skin.

name: Localised lipoatrophy due to injected corticosteroid
definition: Localised lipoatrophy due to injected corticosteroid is the localised loss of subcutaneous fat attributable to intramuscular or intralesional injection of corticosteroids.

name: Koilonychia
definition: A concave deformity of one or more nail plates which may be hereditary or acquired. It may be a sign of iron deficiency.

name: Extrafacial congenital infiltrating lipomatosis
definition: Congenital infiltrating lipomatosis affecting the trunk or extremities. The non-encapsulated fatty masses may reach a substantial size. Similar changes may be seen as a component of the Bannayan-Riley-Ruvalcaba syndrome.

name: Porphyria or pseudoporphyria affecting the skin

name: Allantoic duct remnants or cysts

synonyms: Malformations of urachus | Urachus remnants or cysts

name: Diabetic dermopathy
definition: Diabetic dermopathy occurs in about 10% of diabetics. It presents with hyperpigmented, atrophic, well-demarcated skin lesions, typically located on the shins. An association between diabetic dermopathy and large-vessel disease (particularly coronary artery disease), neuropathy, nephropathy and retinopathy has been identified. Thus, diabetic dermopathy may be a clinical marker for the severity of systemic diabetic complications.   Always assign an additional code for the type of diabetes mellitus

name: Congenital ptosis, unilateral
definition: This is a congenital drooping or falling of the upper or lower eyelid. The drooping may be worse after being awake longer, when the individual's muscles are tired. This condition is sometimes called \"lazy eye\", but that term normally refers to amblyopia. If severe enough and left untreated, the drooping eyelid can cause other conditions, such as amblyopia or astigmatism. This diagnosis is occurring in one eye (unilateral).

name: Omphalitis not of newborn
synonyms: infected umbilicus | infection of navel

name: Contact dermatitis of female genitalia
definition: Contact dermatitis not specified as irritant or allergic affecting the vulva and surrounding skin. Irritant contact dermatitis is, however, much commoner than allergic contact dermatitis in this area.

name: Ear-lobe keloid
definition: A common type of keloid which usually follows ear-piercing
synonyms: Earlobe keloid

name: Genitocrural intertriginous candidosis
definition: Secondary infection of genitocrural intertrigo with Candida species.
synonyms: candidosis of the groin

name: Gastric varices
definition: Abnormally dilated veins developed as portosystemic shunts in the lining of stomach (fundus and/or cardia) in patients with portal hypertension. Once gastric varices develop, they continue to grow, and bleeding from gastric varices can be fatal.
synonyms: Gastric varices NOS | gastric varix | stomach varix

name: Stucco keratosis
definition: Stucco keratoses manifest typically in older men as multiple small pale flat keratotic papules distributed symmetrically on the extensor surfaces of the lower legs, including the dorsa of the feet. The histopathology is distinct with dense orthokeratosis and \"church-spire\" acanthosis, as may also be seen in acrokeratosis verruciformis and Darier disease.
synonyms: keratosis alba

name: Syndromic ichthyosis
definition: Hereditary disorders in which ichthyosis is associated with significant other abnormalities.

name: Trichoteiromania
definition: Hair damage and loss from chronic self-inflicted compulsive rubbing of scalp hair.

name: Drug-induced onycholysis
synonyms: medicament-induced onycholysis

Chronic urticaria due to underlying infection or infestation
definition: Chronic urticaria provoked by an identifiable infection or infestation. The triggers may be bacteria, viruses or parasites.

name: Acute radiodermatitis following radiotherapy
definition: The reaction of the skin, and in particular the epidermis, to acute exposure to ionising radiation directed at the skin for therapeutic purposes. It manifests as inflammation, erosion and crusting.

name: Cutaneous reactions to zoonotic mites
definition: Cutaneous reactions to mites which normally infest non-human hosts.

name: X-linked dominant congenital generalised hypertrichosis
definition: X-linked congenital generalised hypertrichosis is an extremely rare type of hypertrichosis lanuginosa congenita, a congenital skin disease, which is characterised by hair overgrowth on the entire body in males, and mild and asymmetric hair overgrowth in females. It is associated with a mild facial dysmorphism (anteverted nostrils, moderate prognathism), and, in a kindred, it was also associated with dental anomalies and deafness.
synonyms: Macias-Flores syndrome

name: Complete trisomy 18
definition: Trisomy 18 is a chromosomal abnormality associated with the presence of an extra chromosome 18 and characterised by growth delay, dolichocephaly, a characteristic facies, limb anomalies and visceral malformations.
synonyms: Edwards syndrome | abnormal autosomes 18 | complete trisomy 18 syndrome | e3 trisomy | trisomy 18 | trisomy 18 chromosome | trisomy 18 syndrome

name: Frostbite with tissue necrosis of head
synonyms: Frostbite of head with tissue necrosis

name: HIV-modified psoriasis
definition: Psoriasis may appear de novo or worsen considerably in people with uncontrolled HIV infection or AIDS. HIV-associated psoriasis can be clinically confusing because several comorbid skin disorders in patients with HIV can mimic psoriasis. Furthermore, the management of moderate and severe HIV-associated psoriasis is challenging\: most conventional systemic treatments are contraindicated but patients typically improve with highly active antiretroviral therapy.

name: Cellulite
definition: Cellulite is a common architectural derangement of subcutaneous adipose tissue which results in dimpling and nodularity of the overlying skin. It is seen most commonly in postpubertal women and affects principally the pelvic region, lower limbs, and abdomen. It is thought to result from herniation of multiple small aggregates of subcutaneous fat through the fibrous tissue at the dermohypodermal junction. Obesity predisposes to but is not necessary for its development. The term is in widespread u

name: Parkes-Weber syndrome
definition: Parkes-Weber syndrome (OMIM 608355) is characterised by one or more cutaneous capillary malformations associated with underlying multiple micro-arteriovenous malformations and soft tissue and skeletal hypertrophy of the affected limb. Cases in which the there are multiple capillary malformations have been found to have a mutation in the RASA1 gene. It differs from Klippel-Trénaunay syndrome in which the vascular malformations are slow flow and there is no association with the RASA1 gene.

name: Raynaud phenomenon caused or exacerbated by occupation
definition: Raynaud phenomenon attributable to work. It is well recognised as a consequence of repeated, prolonged use of hand-held vibrating machinery. More commonly, exposure to cold at work may interfere with the ability of individuals with primary Raynaud disease (or with secondary Raynaud phenomenon from diseases such as systemic sclerosis) to continue in work where such exposure is unavoidable.

name: Dorfman-Chanarin disease
definition: This refers to an autosomal recessive disorder characterised by accumulation of triglycerides in the cytoplasm of leukocytes, muscle, liver, fibroblasts, and other tissues.
synonyms: NLSDI - [Neutral lipid storage disease with ichthyosis] | Neutral lipid storage disease with ichthyosis | Chanarin-Dorfman syndrome

name: Yellow oculocutaneous albinism
definition: Yellow OCA is a variant of OCA-1B where patients are born with no pigment and associated severe visual problems, but develop some pigmentation, principally yellow-red phaeomelanin, from early childhood. This form is primarily reported in the Amish population of North America.
synonyms: Oculocutaneous albinism, Amish type

name: Junctional malignant melanoma in naevus junction, unspecified site
Specified cutaneous complications of cosmetic procedures
definition: Adverse outcomes from interventions performed on the skin primarily for aesthetic reasons
synonyms: Postprocedural complications of cosmetic procedures performed on the skin

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 2B
definition: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 2, attenuated form, the less severe form of mucopolysaccharidosis type 2, leads to a massive accumulation of glycosaminoglycans and a wide variety of symptoms including distinctive facies, short stature, cardiorespiratory and skeletal findings. It is differentiated from mucopolysaccharidosis type 2, severe form by the absence of cognitive decline.
synonyms: Hunter syndrome type B | Iduronate 2-sulfatase deficiency type B | Mucopolysaccharidosis type 2, attenuated form | Mucopolysaccharidosis type 2, mild form

name: Menopausal hot flush
definition: A condition affecting females, caused by hormonal changes associated with menopause. This condition is characterised by recurrent and transient periods of flushing, sweating, and a systemic sensation of heat. This condition may also present with palpitations, anxiety, or periods of heat followed by chills.
synonyms: Hot flush

name: Adult linear IgA bullous dermatosis
definition: Adult linear IgA bullous dermatosis is an autoimmune skin disease characterised by blisters on the skin and mucous membranes. The exact causes of the disease are unknown but the disease is mediated by IgA autoantibodies to hemidesmosome and basement membrane components BP180 and its shed ectodomain, BP230 and LAD285. The disease affects adults of all ages including young adults but is most common in old age. Those rare patients with very prominent mucosal involvement with scarring are now classi
synonyms: Linear IgA bullous dermatosis, adult form

name: Silica granuloma
definition: Silica granulomata are granulomatous reactions resulting from traumatic inoculation of sand, glass particles or other silica-containing materials into the skin. They present months to years after injury, which may not be recalled, as single or multiple erythematous, firm, non-tender, dermal or subcutaneous nodules.
synonyms: Foreign body granuloma due to silica

name: Streptobacillosis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Streptobacillus moniliformis. This disease is characterised by systemic illness with fever, chills, rash, and polyarthralgias. Transmission is commonly by direct contact through the bite or scratch of an infected rat. Confirmation is by identification of Streptobacillus in blood or joint samples.
synonyms: Epidemic arthritic erythema | Erythema arthriticum epidemicum | Haverhill fever | Rat-bite fever due to Streptobacillus moniliformis | Streptobacillary rat-bite fever | erythema arthriticum epidemicum | haverhill fever

definition: Varicella zoster virus of skin and mucous membranes including chickenpox and Herpes zoster

name: Occupationally-acquired dermatosis due to exposure to cold
definition: Skin disorder caused by occupational exposure to cold or a cold environment. Examples include cold urticaria, frostbite and chilblains.

name: Mansonelliasis due to Mansonella ozzardi
synonyms: Infection due to Mansonella ozzardi | filariasis ozzardi | mansonella ozzardi filariasis | ozzardian filariasis

name: Vibratory angioedema
definition: Vibratory angioedema is a rare type of physical skin reactivity in which vibration from stimuli such as jogging, vigorous towelling or contact with vibrating machinery causes the rapid development of localised erythema and oedema; these normally settle within a few hours. The condition is usually familial but may be sporadic. It may be associated with other forms of physical urticaria.

name: Oral allergy syndrome
definition: Type I IgE-mediated immediate immune reaction limited to the lips, oral cavity, tongue and throat caused by direct contact with allergen in sensitized patient. Symptoms include mucosal swelling, itching or a burning sensation.
synonyms: Pollen-food allergy syndrome | pollen-associated food allergy syndrome

name: Allergic contact gingivostomatitis
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis affecting the gingivae and oral mucosa.
synonyms: Allergic contact stomatitis

name: Microstomia
definition: A condition of the mouth, caused by congenital genetic mutation, burns, or injury. This condition is characterised by reduction in the size of the oral aperture with or without involvement of the entire oral cavity.

name: Otomycosis
definition: Fungal infection of the ear. Otomycosis is due to Aspergillus spp., especially A. niger, in 75% or more of cases and Candida in most of the remainder.

name: Chronic blistering skin disorder
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic blistering skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature. Examples include chronic vesicular dermatitis of the hands and feet, epidermolysis bullosa and immunobullous disorders such as bullous pemphigoid.
synonyms: Unspecified bullous dermatosis

name: Congenital lipoid adrenal hyperplasia due to STAR deficiency, nonclassic form
definition: This is an endocrine disorder that is an uncommon and potentially lethal form of congenital adrenal hyperplasia (CAH). This diagnosis is due to STAR deficiency, nonclassic form.

name: Callus
synonyms: callosity | intractable plantar or palmar keratosis | tyloma

name: Hand and arm vibration syndrome
definition: Hand Transmitted Vibration is mechanical vibration arising from powered processes or tools which enters the body at the fingers or the palm of the hands. As a consequence of this exposure some people develop a secondary Raynaud’s phenomenon and / or peripheral sensory neuropathy affecting the hands.
synonyms: HAVS - [hand arm vibration syndrome] | Vibration white finger | pneumatic hammer disease | spastic anaemia | traumatic vasospastic disease of the hand | dead finger | hand-arm vibration syndrome

name: Postinfective hypomelanosis
definition: Hypomelanosis attributable to preceding infective disorders such as pityriasis versicolor and varicella. The pigment loss is most apparent in dark-skinned individuals and may cause significant psychological distress but usually gradually resolves.

name: Birt-Hogg-Dubé syndrome
definition: Birt-Hogg-Dubé syndrome is a rare familial disorder characterised by benign skin lesions (fibrofolliculomas, trichodiscomas and skin tags), benign and malignant renal tumours, and pulmonary cysts that may be associated with pneumothorax.
synonyms: Fibrofolliculomas with trichodiscomas and acrochordons

name: Eosinophilic granulomatosis with polyangiitis
definition: Eosinophil-rich and necrotizing granulomatous inflammation often involving the respiratory tract, and necrotizing vasculitis predominantly affecting small to medium-sized vessels, and associated with asthma and eosinophilia. ANCA is most frequent when glomerulonephritis is present. (Arthritis Rheum 1990;33\:1094-1100)
synonyms: Allergic granulomatous angiitis | Churg-Strauss syndrome | Churg-Strauss vasculitis | allergic angiitis and granulomatosis | churg-strauss granuloma | churg-strauss vasculitis | cutaneous extravascular necrotising granuloma | granulomatous vasculitis of churg-strauss | palisaded neutrophilic and granulomatous dermatitis | PNGD

name: Complete trisomy 21
definition: Trisomy 21 is a chromosomal abnormality, characterised by the presence of a third (partial or total) copy of chromosome 21, which clinical manifestations include variable intellectual deficiency, muscular hypotonia and joint laxity, often associated with facial dysmorphism and variable malformations (essentially heart and digestive) and a risk of complications (epilepsy, leukemia, auto-immune and endocrine pathologies, earlier aging and Alzheimer disease.
synonyms: Chromosome 21 trisomy | Down syndrome | Trisomy 21 NOS | Trisomy 21 syndrome | abnormal autosomes 21

name: Hereditary palmoplantar keratodermas
definition: Heritable disorders of keratinisation of the skin of the palms and soles.

name: Skin peeling

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, multiple sites
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, multiple sites

name: Abscess of periodontium
definition: localised purulent infection of periodontal tissues; common clinical feature in patients with moderate or advanced periodontitis.
synonyms: Periodontal abscess

name: Hashimoto-Pritzker syndrome
definition: Hashimoto-Pritzker syndrome, also known as congenital self-healing reticulo-histiocytosis or Hashimoto-Pritzker histiocytosis (HPH) is a variant of Langerhans cell histiocytosis. Classic signs and symptoms include congenital or early development of red-brown skin lesions in the form of painless papules, nodules or plaques with spontaneous regression in 2-3 months, and increase of a type of immune cells known as Langerhans cell histiocytes. An excess of immature Langerhans cells usually forms tum
synonyms: HPH - [Hashimoto-Pritzker histiocytosis] | Hashimoto-Pritzker histiocytosis

name: Familial hypobetalipoproteinaemia
definition: Familial hypobetalipoproteinemia is the familial form of hypobetalipoproteinemia, which is characterised by permanently low levels (below the 5th percentile) of apolipoprotein B and LDL cholesterol. It can be severe with early onset (abetalipoproteinemia/ homozygous familial hypobetalipoproteinemia; see this term) or benign (benign familial hypobetalipoproteinemia; see this term).

name: Macroglossia
definition: Macroglossia is the medical term for a large or enlarged tongue. It may be due to a variety of congenital and acquired conditions. Isolated macroglossia has no determinable cause. It is seen commonly in Down syndrome. The most common causes of tongue enlargement are vascular malformations (e.g. lymphangioma or haemangioma) and muscular hypertrophy. It may, however, be due to infiltration, as with primary systemic amyloidosis.
synonyms: acquired macroglossia NOS

name: Punctate palmoplantar keratoderma, type 1
synonyms: Brauer-Buschke-Fischer | Keratoderma punctata | PPKP1 - [Punctate palmoplantar keratoderma, type 1] (MIM 148600)

name: Systemic sclerosis
definition: Systemic sclerosis is a systemic disorder of the connective tissue; manifested by hardening and thickening of the skin, by abnormalities involving the microvasculature and larger vessels, and by fibrotic degenerative changes in various body organs including the heart, lungs, kidneys, and gastrointestinal tract.
synonyms: Systemic scleroderma | progressive scleroderma | progressive systemic sclerosis | scleroderma | SSc

name: Mevalonate kinase deficiency with recurrent fever
definition: Mevalonate kinase \[MVK\] deficiency with recurrent fever, with or without hyperIgD, is an autoinflammatory disease associated with mutations in the mevalonate kinase gene. This results in clinical attacks of inflammation in the form of fever, lymphadenopathy, enlarged spleen and serositis along with increased acute phase reactants.
synonyms: Dutch-type periodic fever | HIDS - [Hyper-IgD syndrome] | Hyper-IgD syndrome | Hyperimmunoglobinaemia D with recurrent fever | Hyperimmunoglobulinaemia D with periodic fever | MVK [mevalonate kinase deficiency] with recurrent fever

name: Protein contact dermatitis due to animal protein
definition: Contact dermatitis due to exposure to animal material such as dander, feathers or body fluids; or to animal-derived products including milk, eggs, seafood, fish and meat.

name: Ocular rosacea
definition: Manifestations of ocular rosacea range from minor irritation, foreign body sensation, dryness, and blurry vision to severe ocular surface disruption and inflammatory keratitis. Patients frequently describe a gritty feeling, and they commonly experience blepharitis and conjunctivitis. Other ocular findings include lid margin and conjunctival telangiectases, eyelid thickening, eyelid crusts and scales, chalazia and hordeola, punctate epithelial erosions, corneal infiltrates, corneal ulcers, cornea

name: Soft corn
definition: A soft corn is a painful callosity extending from the side of one toe across to the side of the adjacent toe. It is normally caused by lateral pressure from ill-fitting footwear and appears white as a result of maceration of the excess keratin.
synonyms: Heloma molle | heloma molle

name: Acute guttate psoriasis
definition: Acute onset of guttate psoriasis in the absence of current plaque psoriasis and typically triggered by streptococcal sore throat or tonsillitis.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 3 of iliac crest
synonyms: stage 3 decubitus ulcer of iliac crest

name: Sézary syndrome
definition: A generalised peripheral (mature) T-cell neoplasm characterised by the presence of erythroderma, lymphadenopathy, and neoplastic, cerebriform T-lymphocytes in the blood. Sézary syndrome is an aggressive disease.
synonyms: Sezary syndrome | SS

name: Rosacea fulminans
definition: Rosacea fulminans is characterised by an abrupt onset of inflammatory papules, pustules and fluctuant nodules on a background of facial oedema and erythema. It typically affects the nose, malar region, chin, and temporal and frontal regions. Seborrhoea and facial flushing frequently precede rosacea fulminans. Constitutional symptoms are usually mild with low grade fever and/or myalgia in only a minority of cases. Recurrence is rare but hypertrophic or varioliform scars may remain. Its aetiology
synonyms: Pyoderma faciale | pyoderma faciale

name: Macular amyloidosis
definition: Macular amyloidosis presents as confluent or rippled macular hyperpigmentation, most commonly over the backs of the shoulders and upper back. It is often pruritic and is possibly induced by rubbing and scratching. The amyloid material is thought to originate from degenerate keratinocytes.

name: Ulcer of skin of uncertain nature, upper limb
definition: This denotes the presence of a skin ulcer located on an upper limb but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the ulcer might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Hyperkeratotic seborrhoeic keratosis
definition: A seborrhoeic keratosis with an exophytic growth pattern and prominent hyperkeratosis in addition to papillomatosis and acanthosis. Horn cysts are usually absent.

name: Neonatal lupus erythematosus
definition: Neonatal lupus erythematosus results from trans-placental transfer of maternal antibodies, in particular anti-Ro/SSA and anti La/SSB. It manifests with an erythematous rash which may be obviously photosensitive and is closely associated with congenital heart block. The mother may have known lupus, especially subacute cutaneous lupus erythematosus, but she may be asymptomatic. The rash normally subsides within the first few months of life.
synonyms: NLE

name: Diabetic cheiroarthropathy
definition: Diabetic cheiroarthropathy is a condition associated with long-standing diabetes and is characterised by stiffness and contractures of the skin, soft tissues, tendon sheaths and small joints of the hands. It is associated with an increased risk of microvascular complications. Flexion contractures of the fingers may prevent apposition of opposing fingers when the hands are placed in the \"prayer\" position. Increased glycosylation of the skin and periarticular tissue, impaired collagen degradatio
synonyms: Diabetic sclerodactyly

name: Subcutaneous swelling, mass or lump, trunk

name: Tyrosinaemia type 2
definition: Tyrosinemia type 2 is an inborn error of tyrosine metabolism characterised by hypertyrosinemia with oculocutaneous manifestations (eye redness, photophobia, excessive tearing and pain, palmoplantar hyperkeratosis) and, in some cases, intellectual deficit.
synonyms: Hepatic tyrosine aminotransferase deficiency | Keratosis palmoplantaris - corneal dystrophy | Oculocutaneous tyrosinaemia | Richner-Hanhart syndrome | Tyrosine transaminase deficiency | Oculocutaneous tyrosinemia

name: Lichen sclerosus
definition: Lichen sclerosus is a chronic inflammatory dermatosis of unknown aetiology. It affects both women and men of all ages. It is characterised by the development of white, smooth, atrophic plaques on vulval and perianal skin in females and on the prepuce and glans penis in males. It often results in scar formation leading to a narrow introitus or phimosis with impairment of urinary and sexual function. The risk of anogenital squamous cell carcinoma is slightly increased. synonyms: LS et A - [Lichen sclerosus et atrophicus] | Lichen albus | Lichen sclerosus et atrophicus | lichen sclerosus et atrophicus

name: Verruga peruana
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Bartonella bacilliformis. This disease is characterised by multiple nodular and red-to-purple vascular skin lesions, subsequent to Oroya fever. Transmission is through the bite of infected sandflies from the genus Lutzomyia.
synonyms: Cutaneous Carrión disease | Cutaneous and mucocutaneous bartonellosis due to Bartonella bacilliformis | Peruvian wart | cutaneous and mucocutaneous bartonellosis | cutaneous bartonellosis | mucocutaneous bartonellosis | peruvian wart

name: Scalp-ear-nipple syndrome
definition: Scalp-ear-nipple syndrome is characterised by the following triad\: areas of hairless raw skin over the scalp (present at birth and healing during childhood), prominent, hypoplastic ears with almost absent pinnae, and bilateral amastia.

definition: Adverse reactions to contact with aquatic invertebrates of the phylum Cnidaria, which includes jellyfish, Portuguese men-of-war, coral and sea anemones.

name: Dermatosis papulosa nigra
definition: Dermatosis papulosa nigra is a common dermatosis in individuals of African descent and is characterised by the appearance from middle age onwards of multiple small 1-3 mm diameter pigmented papules located primarily on the face. Histologically these are seborrhoeic keratoses.
synonyms: DPN

name: Juvenile hyaline fibromatosis
definition: Juvenile hyaline fibromatosis (JHF) is a genetic condition characterised by papulonodular skin lesions, soft tissue masses, gingival hypertrophy, and flexion contractures of the large joints due to perivascular hyaline deposition. Typical diagnostic criteria are multiple hyaline subcutaneous fibroma, filamentous tumours of the skin, gingival fibromatosis, muscle contractures of the extremities and multiple osteolytic bone destructions.
synonyms: fibromatosis hyalinica multiplex juvenilis | hyaline fibromatosis syndrome | JHF | murray-puretic-drescher syndrome | systemic hyalinosis, juvenile

name: Neoscytalidium dermatomycosis
definition: Superficial infection of palmoplantar skin, toe webs and nails caused by the pathogenic plant fungi, Neoscytalidium dimidiatum and Neoscytalidium hyalinum. The clinical features mimic those produced by Trichophyton rubrum. Infection is usually acquired in tropical regions including the Caribbean, West Africa and Thailand.
synonyms: Hendersonula dermatomycosis | Scytalidium dermatomycosis

name: Recessive bullous dermolysis of the newborn
definition: An autosomal recessively inherited counterpart of dominant bullous dermolysis of the newborn in which neonatal blistering gradually heals over the first few weeks or months of life.

name: Benign vascular neoplasms of infancy or childhood

name: Pseudofolliculitis barbae
definition: Pseudofolliculitis barbae results from shaving tightly curled beard hair, leaving sharp ends which can retract under the skin and embed, or curl over and penetrate the skin. Both events will predispose to a foreign body inflammatory reaction with papule and pustule formation. Mild cases may be transient, but severe cases are destructive with scarring. Initial inflammation is sterile, but secondary infection is not uncommon. The condition is most commonly in men of Sub-Saharan African ancestry bu
synonyms: Ingrowing hairs | Shaving bumps
