Citrullinaemia type 2
definition: Citrullinemia type 2 is a severe subtype of citrin deficiency characterised clinically by adult onset (20 and 50 years of age), recurrent episodes of hyperammonemia and associated neuropsychiatric symptoms such as nocturnal delirium, confusion, restlessness, disorientation, drowsiness, memory loss, abnormal behaviour (aggression, irritability, and hyperactivity), seizures, and coma.
synonyms: Adult-onset citrin deficiency | CTLN2 - [Citrullinaemia type 2] | Citrullinuria type 2

name: Epidermal fragility

synonyms: Sebaceous hyperplasia | SGH

name: Body dysmorphic disorder with poor to absent insight
definition: All definitional requirements of body dysmorphic disorder are met. Most or all of the time, the individual is convinced that the disorder-specific beliefs are true and cannot accept an alternative explanation for their experience. The lack of insight exhibited by the individual does not vary markedly as a function of anxiety level.

name: Cutaneous listeriosis
definition: This is a bacterial infection caused by a Gram-positive, motile bacterium, Listeria monocytogenes. Listeriosis occurs primarily in newborn infants, elderly patients, and patients who are immunocompromised.

name: Brachioradial pruritus
definition: Brachioradial pruritus is a neurogenic itch syndrome of the upper extremities. It is typically localised to the skin on the dorsolateral forearm overlying the proximal head of the brachioradialis muscle, but involvement of the upper arms and shoulders is also common. It may be unilateral or bilateral. Scratching reportedly only makes the discomfort worse, and most patients discover that application of cold packs is often the only therapy that provides symptomatic relief.

name: Familial defective apolipoprotein B-100
definition: This is an autosomal dominant disorder resulting from a missense mutation which reduces the affinity of apoB-100 for the low-density lipoprotein receptor (LDL Receptor).

name: Dermatoses resulting from exposure to water
Seborrhoeic psoriasis
definition: Seborrhoeic psoriasis (“sebopsoriasis”), so called because of its similarity in morphology and anatomical distribution to seborrhoeic dermatitis, may occur either in isolation or associated with plaque psoriasis elsewhere. Sites of involvement are the nasolabial folds, medial cheeks, nose, ears, eyebrows, hair line, scalp, pre-sternal and interscapular regions. Characteristically the lesions are thin, red and well-demarcated though in some cases they can be recognised as a form of psoriasis only
synonyms: Sebopsoriasis | Seborrhiasis

name: Secondary Sjögren syndrome
definition: Secondary Sjögren syndrome is a progressive inflammatory autoimmune disease affecting the exocrine glands in the presence of other systemic autoimmune diseases, such as rheumatoid arthritis, systemic lupus erythematosus, and systemic sclerosis. Lymphocytic infiltrates replace functional epithelium, leading to oral and ocular dryness.

name: Autosomal recessive epidermolysis bullosa simplex due to BP230 deficiency
definition: A mild autosomal recessive form of EBS due mutations in the DST gene which result in an absence of bullous pemphigoid antigen-1 (BP230) in basal keratinocytes.
synonyms: EBS - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex] new subtype | EBS - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex] other specified autosomal recessive | EBS-AR BP230

name: Acrodermatitis enteropathica
definition: Acrodermatitis enteropathica is an uncommon autosomal recessive disorder of intestinal zinc absorption. Signs usually appear within the first months of life with an exudative and crusted erythema located predominantly around body orifices (mouth, anogenital) and on the scalp and distal extremities. The signs are often misdiagnosed as being due to infection. The condition responds rapidly to zinc supplementation which must be continued throughout life.
synonyms: Brandt syndrome | Danbolt-Closs syndrome | primary zinc malabsorption syndrome | AE | autosomal recessive zinc deficiency

name: Comedonal acne
definition: Acne in which the principal manifestation is the presence of open and/or closed comedones, respectively blackheads and whiteheads.
synonyms: Acne comedones | Blackheads | Whiteheads | whiteheads | blackheads

name: Late congenital syphilitic arthropathy
definition: This is a late congenital, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with syphilitic arthropathy.

definition: A chronic disseminated form of histoplasmosis, which is found in immunocompromised host. Chronic histoplasmosis is a serious disease that can result in death.

name: Auricular pit
definition: Small indentation in the lower part of the ascending helix, concha, or in the crus helix.

name: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to side-chain cleavage enzyme deficiency
definition: This refers to any of several autosomal recessive diseases resulting from mutations of genes for enzymes mediating the biochemical steps of production of cortisol from cholesterol by the adrenal glands (steroidogenesis). This diagnosis is due to side-chain cleavage enzyme deficiency.

name: Tuberculous ulceration of vulva
definition: Tuberculosis of the vulva presents with ulceration and is usually secondary to tuberculosis of internal genitourinary organs. Rarely the infection may be primary as the result of sexual transmission from a man with periorificial penile tuberculosis.
synonyms: Vulval tuberculosis

name: Neonatal cutaneous haemorrhage
synonyms: Petechiae in fetus or newborn | cutaneous haemorrhage of fetus or newborn | haemorrhage of skin of fetus or newborn

name: Necrotising fasciitis of the scrotum, penis or perineum
definition: This is a severe, rapidly extending sub-type of polymicrobial necrotising fasciitis occurring in elderly men and is caused by a mixture of aerobic and anaerobic bacteria. It typically starts in the scrotum and then spreads to the perineum and abdominal wall. It may less commonly involve the penis and in the rare cases where the penis is the major site of infection has been termed Corbus disease. It has a high mortality if not treated aggressively.
synonyms: Fournier gangrene

name: Small congenital melanocytic naevus
definition: A congenital melanocytic naevus with a projected or final adult maximal diameter of less than 15 mm.

name: Generalised excess and redundant skin due to disease or malnutrition
definition: generalised excess and redundant skin occurring in previously normal individuals who have lost excessive body weight as a result of starvation or diseases resulting in malabsorption or excessive catabolism.

name: Ankyloblepharon - ectodermal defects - cleft lip or palate
definition: Ankyloblepharon - ectodermal defects - cleft lip/palate syndrome, designated as Hay Wells syndrome, is characterised by congenital ectodermal dysplasia with wiry, sparse hair; dystrophic nails; slight hypohidrosis; scalp infections; ankyloblepharon filiform adnatum (apposing eyelid margins connected by abnormal tissue strands); hypodontia (missing teeth); maxillary hypoplasia; and cleft lip/palate.

name: Contact dermatitis of male genitalia
definition: Contact dermatitis not specified as irritant or allergic affecting the male genitalia.

name: Acute cutaneous distension syndrome
definition: A common sequela of acute oedema, especially of the lower extremities. It manifests as blisters, which may be mistaken for an immunobullous disorder, (acute oedema blisters); or as superficial fissuring and inflammation of the skin (eczéma craquelé).

name: Chemical burn due to skin contact with corrosive substance
definition: A chemical skin burn is an acute, severe irritant reaction to skin contact with corrosive or caustic substances sufficient to cause cell necrosis. The corrosive potential of substances depends on their chemical properties, concentration and pH, and on the duration and type of skin contact. Occlusion has an additive effect.
cn_name: 皮肤接触腐蚀性物质造成的化学性烧伤

name: Minor anomalies of pinnae
definition: Any condition caused by failure of the pinna to correctly develop during the antenatal period. These conditions are characterised by asymptomatic abnormalities of the visible part of the ear.
synonyms: congenital deformity of pinna of ear | deformity of pinna of ear | malformations of pinnae

name: Freckles
definition: The presence of multiple ephelides (ephelis = freckle) as is commonly seen in sun-exposed skin of individuals with phototype I (sun-sensitive) skin. They occur as a profusion of light brown macules, particularly on the face and upper extremities, and become more prominent after sun exposure. In contrast to lentigines there is no keratinocyte proliferation histologically.
synonyms: Ephelides | freckles

name: SHORT syndrome
definition: SHORT' represents an acronym for a syndrome associating short stature (S), hyperextensibility of joints or hernia (inguinal) or both (H), ocular depression (O), Rieger anomaly (R), and teething delay (T). Patients also present with a characteristic triangular face with small facial bones, deep-set eyes, mid-face hypoplasia, thin nasal alae, and micrognathia, with a thin, dry, and wrinkled skin resulting in a progeroid appearance. The short stature is accompanied by delayed bone age and developme
synonyms: Aarskog-Ose-Pande syndrome | Lipodystrophy - Rieger anomaly - diabetes | Rieger anomaly - partial lipodystrophy

name: Mosquito bite
definition: Bites by culicid flies including the genus Anopheles, responsible for transmitting malaria. Mosquitoes are also vectors for dengue fever, yellow fever and filariasis. The immune response to mosquito bites is often brisk with formation of very itchy weals and bullae.

name: Cutaneous botryomycosis
definition: Cutaneous botryomycosis is a rare opportunistic infection of the skin and soft tissues characterised by chronic purulent and granulomatous lesions in which the organisms form granules composed of bacterial masses. It is most commonly associated with Staphylococcus aureus but other organisms including Pseudomonas aeruginosa have been implicated. It often affects extremities and may be misdiagnosed as mycetoma. Although it has been reported in association with impaired immunity from, for instance,

name: Non-HFE-related hereditary haemochromatosis, Type 3
definition: Type 3 hemochromatosis is a form of rare hereditary hemochromatosis, a group of diseases characterised by excessive tissue iron deposition of genetic origin. It resembles type 1 (HFE-related) hemochromatosis and presents with liver disease, hypogonadism, arthritis, diabetes and skin pigmentation.
synonyms: TFR2-related haemochromatosis

name: Dermatophytosis due to anthropophilic dermatophytes
definition: Infection by keratinophilic fungi which are specific to Homo sapiens.

name: Malignant neoplasms of scrotum
synonyms: cancer of scrotum | malignant neoplasm of scrotum | malignant neoplasm of skin of scrotum | malignant scrotal tumour | malignant tumour of scrotum | primary malignant neoplasm of scrotum | scrotal cancer

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to hairdressing products
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to hair care products such as dyes, permanent wave and bleaching agents and shampoos. Such allergens may show occupational relevance among hairdressers although consumers are most commonly affected.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to hairdressing products
cn_name: 美发产品引起的变应性接触性皮炎

name: Alopecia due to iron deficiency
definition: Chronic diffuse telogen hair loss attributable to profound iron deficiency
synonyms: Alopecia secondary to iron deficiency

name: Mosaicism, 45, X, 46, XX or XY
definition: A disease caused by embryonic fusion, or by the loss of one of the sex chromosomes from a cell early in embryonic development; Gonadal status\: normal or variable abnormalities of sexual anatomy, maturation or function. Phenotype\: normal, or abnormal sexual development.

name: Acute pseudomembranous oral candidosis
definition: An acute form of oral candidosis in which white patches on the oral mucosa can be peeled off to reveal an underlying eroded epithelium. It is common in the neonatal period. Severe forms are associated with immunodeficiency and general debility.
synonyms: Oral thrush

name: Benign cutaneous melanocytic neoplasms
synonyms: Benign melanocytic naevus | Melanocytic naevus | Mole | Naevus pigmentosus | Pigmented naevus | mole | nevocellular nevus

name: Pityriasis rotunda
definition: Pityriasis rotunda is an uncommon dermatosis of unknown aetiology in which circular, sharply defined patches of dry skin with ichthyosiform scaling form on the buttocks, thighs, abdomen, back or upper arms. They may be single or multiple and tend to enlarge slowly and coalesce over time before then remaining fixed and unchanged throughout life. It is seen more commonly in East Asia and Southern Africa than in Europe or the Americas.
synonyms: pityriasis circinata | tinea circinata

name: Diffuse actinic keratinocyte dysplasia
definition: Diffuse actinic dysplasia develops after repeated exposure of skin to ultraviolet radiation, usually over decades, and results from cumulative DNA damage within the nuclei of epidermal keratinocytes. It is characterised initially by subtle diffuse skin changes including mottling, erythema, telangiectasia and irregular fine scaling. It is seen most commonly on the unprotected scalp skin of bald men. Histologically there are early signs of dysplasia in the basal epidermis. As damage accumulates th
synonyms: \ | Diffuse actinic dysplasia | pediculosis pubis | pubic lice
cn_name: 弥漫性光线性表皮发育不良

name: Waardenburg syndrome type 1
definition: This type is due to mutations in PAX3 gene and has the additional phenotype characteristic of dystopia canthorum.
synonyms: WS1

name: Mycosis fungoides
definition: A peripheral (mature) T-cell lymphoma presenting in the skin with patches/plaques or less commonly with tumours or erythroderma. It is characterised by epidermal and dermal infiltration of small to medium-sized T-cells with cerebriform nuclei.
synonyms: MF

name: Multicentric reticulohistiocytosis
definition: Multicentric reticulohistiocytosis is a rare non-Langerhans cell histiocytosis characterised by the association of specific nodular skin lesions and destructive arthritis.

name: Folliculitis cruris pustulosa atrophicans
definition: A folliculitis prevalent in Sub-Saharan Africa due to the custom of applying of greasy ointments to the lower legs. It presents as an inflammatory folliculitis which may result in follicular scarring and atrophy.
synonyms: Dermatitis cruris pustulosa atrophicans | Dermatitis cruris pustulosa et atrophicans | Folliculitis cruris pustulosa et atrophicans
cn_name: 萎缩性脓疱性小腿毛囊炎

name: Phototoxic reaction to skin contact with tar or tar derivatives
Nephrogenic systemic fibrosis
definition: Nephrogenic systemic fibrosis (NSF) is an iatrogenic systemic fibrosing disorder which affects principally skin and joints and which is due to exposure of patients with moderate to severe kidney failure to gadolinium-containing contrast agents. It manifests with induration, thickening, and hardening of the skin, especially of the extremities, and may be associated with joint contractures. Histopathologically there is a proliferation of dermal fibroblasts and dendritic cells with deposition of co
synonyms: nephrogenic fibrosing dermopathy

name: Parakeratosis pustulosa
definition: Parakeratosis pustulosa is a localised non-infective scaling erythematous and often pustular inflammatory disorder of periungual skin in children. Usually only a single digit is affected, more commonly a finger than a toe; the adjacent nail plate is dystrophic. The condition is thought to predispose to psoriasis later in life.

name: Multifocal infantile haemangioma
definition: Infantile haemangioma is multifocal in up to 25% of cases with numbers ranging from a few to many dozens. If more than 5 cutaneous tumours are present there is an increased risk of associated internal haemangiomatosis, especially of the liver.
synonyms: Multiple strawberry haemangiomata

name: Inflamed seborrhoeic keratosis
definition: There is a heavy lichenoid inflammatory cell infiltrate in the upper dermis. Apoptotic keratinocytes are usually quite numerous. Sometimes there is a heavy inflammatory cell infiltrate, including neutrophils, which may not have lichenoid features. Squamous eddies are often present in the epidermis.
synonyms: Irritated seborrhoeic keratosis

name: Vulval psoriasis
definition: Psoriasis affecting the external surfaces of the vulva and often extending to the mons pubis and the natal cleft.

name: Sequelae of frostbite with tissue necrosis of neck
PIBIDS syndrome
definition: PIBIDS syndrome is a form of trichothiodystrophy (sulphur-deficient brittle hair) characterised by Ichthyosis, Brittle hair, Intellectual impairment, Decreased fertility, and Short stature (like in IBIDS syndrome) combined with photosensitivity.
synonyms: Trichothiodystrophy - sun sensitivity | Trichothiodystrophy type F | photosensitivity, ichthyosis, brittle hair, infertility, developmental delay, short stature syndrome

name: Candidosis of skin or mucous membranes
synonyms: Thrush | candidiasis of skin | skin candida | skin candidiasis

name: Macular syphilide
definition: The earliest manifestation of secondary syphilis which appears on average eight weeks after initial infection as widespread, symmetrical, copper-red, round and oval macules which, on the back, follow the lines of skin cleavage. It is asymptomatic and can be easily overlooked.

name: Multiple carboxylase deficiency
definition: Multiple carboxylase deficiency (MCD) is a term used to describe inborn errors of biotin metabolism characterised by reduced activities of biotin-dependent enzymes resulting in a wide spectrum of symptoms, including feeding difficulty, breathing difficulties, lethargy, seizures, skin rash, alopecia, and developmental delay. There are two major clinical forms of MCD depending on their underlying cause\: biotinidase deficiency and holocarboxylase synthetase deficiency.

name: Focal dermal hypoplasia
definition: Focal dermal hypoplasia or Goltz syndrome is a X-linked inherited syndrome characterised by a polymorphic cutaneous disorder and highly variable anomalies affecting the eyes, teeth, skeleton and the central nervous, urinary, gastrointestinal and cardiovascular systems. Skin changes are constituted by areas of cutaneous atrophy and periorificial papillomas that predominate around the mouth, genitalia and/or anus. Skeletal disorders include syndactyly, ectrodactyly and/or aplastic fingers and toes
synonyms: Goltz syndrome | Goltz-Gorlin syndrome | goltz-gorlin syndrome

name: Circumscribed lymphatic malformation
synonyms: Circumscribed lymphangioma

name: Repetitive self-biting behaviour
definition: Self-inflicted skin and mucous membrane damage resulting from a compulsive urge to bite.
synonyms: Compulsive self-biting behaviour

name: Carcinoma in situ of penis
definition: This comprises both squamous carcinoma in situ and extramammary Paget disease of the penis. The former is an uncommon precancerous disease of penile skin. Lesions usually appear on the glans or inner aspect of the foreskin and are almost always found in uncircumcised men. If left untreated, 10-30% of cases develop into invasive squamous cell carcinoma of the penis. When it affects the skin of the shaft or prepuce it is commonly called Bowen disease. If it affects the glans or inner surface of th
synonyms: PIN - [penile intraepithelial neoplasia]

name: Plasma cell cheilitis
definition: Plasma cell cheilitis is an idiopathic benign inflammatory condition characterised by dense plasma cell infiltrates in the lips. It presents as circumscribed flat or elevated erythematous patches, usually on the lower lip.

name: Miscellaneous specified granulomatous disorders of skin
Punctate keratoderma of the palmar creases
definition: A common asymptomatic autosomal dominant condition affecting the palms principally of people of African descent. It is characterised by punctate keratotic plugs and pits, generally no more than 1-2 mm in diameter, confined to the creases of the palms and the palmar surfaces of the fingers. The keratotic plugs have been shown to occur directly above the acrosyringium of eccrine sweat ducts. Pits develop when the keratin plugs detach.

name: Late syphilis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum pallidum. This disease is characterised by gummas, neurological abnormalities, or cardiac abnormalities. Clinical signs normally manifest approximately 3-15 years after initial infection. Transmission is commonly by sexual contact.
synonyms: Tertiary syphilis | late syphilis, unspecified | late tertiary syphilis | late syphilis

name: Autoimmune pancreatitis with other organ involvement
definition: This is an autoimmune inflammation of the pancreas which requires immediate medical attention and hospitalization during an attack that has multiple causes and symptoms, with other organ involvement.

name: Wart virus proliferation in immune-deficient states
definition: Enhanced proliferation of human papillomavirus as a result of a failure of immune surveillance. This may be due to a genetic defect, disease or iatrogenic immunosuppression.
cn_name: 免疫缺陷状态下疣病毒增殖

name: Mucocutaneous early congenital syphilis
synonyms: Congenital syphilitic mucous patches

name: Hordeolum internum lower eyelid
definition: This is a cyst in the eyelid that is caused by inflammation of a blocked meibomian gland, usually on the upper eyelid. Chalazia differ from styes (hordeola) in that they are subacute and usually painless nodules. They may become acutely inflamed but, unlike a stye, chalazia usually point inside the lid rather than on the lid margin. This diagnosis is of the lower eyelid.

name: Chylocele of tunica vaginalis in loiasis
Disorders of the sebaceous gland
definition: A group of disorders in which sebaceous gland size, location, anatomy or secretion is abnormal.

name: Griscelli syndrome type 3
definition: This type, caused by mutations in MLPH or MYO5A genes, presents with hypopigmentation alone.
synonyms: GS3 - [Griscelli syndrome type 3] (MIM 609227)

name: Fire sponge dermatitis
synonyms: Fire sponge dermatitis (Tedania ignis)

name: Vitamin B12 responsive methylmalonic aciduria type cbl A
definition: This refers to vitamin B12 responsive, type cbl A, stemming from several genotypes, all forms of the disorder usually diagnosed in the early neonatal period, presenting progressive encephalopathy, and secondary hyperammonemia.

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, pelvic region or thigh
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, pelvic region or thigh

name: Funnel-web spider envenomation
definition: Envenomation following a bite by certain species of Australian funnel-web spiders of the subfamily Atracinae. The venom contains a mixture of neurotoxins which can rapidly result in agitation, confusion, pulmonary oedema, metabolic acidosis, elevated intracranial pressure and death.

name: Mucosal vitiligo
definition: Vitiligo of mucous membranes

name: Obesity-related stretch marks
synonyms: Striae distensae due to obesity

name: Streblodactyly
definition: Streblodactyly is a rare heritable X-linked dominant disorder characterised by congenital flexion deformity at the metacarpophalangeal joints of the thumbs and the proximal interphalangeal joints of the fifth fingers.

name: Localised vitiligo
definition: Vitiligo confined to one body area.

name: Loricrin palmoplantar keratoderma with ichthyosis
synonyms: Vohwinkel syndrome - ichthyosis

name: Syphilitic arthritis
synonyms: syphilitica deformans arthritis

name: Complaint of abnormal sweating
definition: Complaint that sweating is abnormal (most commonly that it is increased) without sufficient evidence to make a specific diagnosis.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 4 of heel
synonyms: stage 4 decubitus ulcer of heel

name: Pigmentary abnormalities of skin due to drug
definition: Disturbances of skin colour due to an ingested or injected drug. These may result from a number of different mechanisms including the colour of the drug itself, disturbed melanisation of the skin or deposition of pigments by drug breakdown products.
synonyms: discolouration of skin due to drug | drug application site discolouration

name: Herpes simplex infection of male genital tract, not elsewhere classified
synonyms: Herpes simplex infection of male genital tract NOS | Herpesviral infection of male genital tract NOS

name: Syndromes with acanthosis nigricans
definition: Syndromes in which acanthosis nigricans is an obligate part.

name: Klinefelter syndrome, male with more than two X chromosomes
definition: A disease affecting males, caused by the presence of more than two X chromosomes in each cell. This disease is characterised by impaired sexual development, intellectual disability, distinctive facial features, skeletal abnormalities, poor coordination, and severe problems with speech. This disease may be differentiated from classic Klinefelter syndrome by increased severity of symptoms. Confirmation is through observation of more than two X chromosomes by karyotyping.
synonyms: male Klinefelter karyotype with more than two x chromosomes | male Klinefelter with more than two x chromosomes
cn_name: 男性Klinefelter综合征，伴多于两条X染色体的

name: Temperature sensitive oculocutaneous albinism
definition: This type of OCA is characterised by white hair in the warmer areas (scalp and axillae) and progressively darker hair in the cooler areas (extremities) of the body. Tyrosinase assay demonstrates a loss of activity above 35-37 degrees Celsius.
synonyms: OCA1-TS - [Temperature-sensitive oculocutaneous albinism]

name: Lymphocytoma cutis
definition: Lymphocytoma cutis is a benign, cutaneous B-cell lymphoproliferative disorder. It presents as papules, nodules or plaques usually on the head and neck and pursues a chronic course. It occurs as a response to known or unknown antigenic stimuli that result in the accumulation of lymphocytes and other inflammatory cells.
synonyms: Benign cutaneous lymphoid hyperplasia | Benign lymphocytoma cutis | Pseudolymphoma of Spiegler-Fendt | Spiegler-Fendt benign lymphocytoma | CLH | cutaneous B- or T- cell pseudolymphoma | cutaneous lymphoid hyperplasia | LABC | lymphadenosis benigna cutis | lymphocytoma cutis

name: Wiedemann-Rautenstrauch progeroid syndrome
definition: Neonatal progeroid syndrome is a premature aging syndrome in which features of human aging are apparent at birth, including larger than normal sized head; prominent scalp veins; triangular, aged face; wrinkled skin; and decreased fat under the skin. This differentiates this syndrome from other premature aging syndromes such as Hutchinson–Gilford progeria syndrome (HGPS) (more commonly called progeria) in which characteristics of premature aging typically become apparent some time after birth. Al
synonyms: Neonatal progeroid syndrome

name: Cobb syndrome
definition: Cobb syndrome is defined by the association of vascular cutaneous (venous or arteriovenous), muscular (arteriovenous), osseous (arteriovenous) and medullary (arteriovenous) lesions at the same metamere or spinal segment. This segmental distribution may involve one or many of the 31 metameres present in humans.
synonyms: Cutaneomeningospinal angiomatosis | SAMS 1-31 - [Spinal arteriovenous metameric syndrome] | Spinal arteriovenous metameric syndrome

name: Kaposi sarcoma of skin
definition: A Kaposi sarcoma arising from the skin. It presents with patches, plaques, or nodules.

name: Lip-biting
definition: Lip-biting is a common obsessive-compulsive disorder characterised by compulsive biting or chewing the lips.
synonyms: Tic de lèvres

name: Congenital thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura due to ADAMTS-13 deficiency
definition: This is a rare disorder of the blood-coagulation system, causing extensive microscopic clots to form in the small blood vessels throughout the body, due to ADAMTS-13 deficiency.
synonyms: Upshaw-Schulman syndrome | congenital thrombocytopaenic purpura

name: Tuberous xanthoma
definition: Tuberous xanthomas are firm yellow-red nodules that occur over sites of pressure\: they usually indicate the presence of hyperlipidaemia type 3 (Type III hyperlipoproteinaemia). They start as small xanthomas, usually over the extensor aspects of the elbows and knees, but can develop into quite exuberant exophytic lesions several centimetres in diameter and height. They can develop over other pressure sites particularly the heels and plantar surfaces of the feet.

name: Superficial frostbite of wrist or hand
Halo melanocytic naevus
definition: A benign melanocytic nevus with a halo appearance.

synonyms: Follicular mucinosis associated with mycosis fungoides

name: Angiokeratoma of the scrotum
definition: Fordyce angiokeratomas of the scrotum are the commonest form of angiokeratoma. They increase in prevalence and in number with increasing age and in one study were found in 1 in 6 men aged over 70 years. They are normally asymptomatic.

name: Primary cutaneous plasmacytosis
definition: A skin disorder resulting from focal or multifocal dense infiltration of the skin by plasma cell aggregates. It may be associated with high levels of serum IgG4. It typically presents as widespread reddish-brown papules, nodules and pigmented indurated plaques involving the trunk and limbs but may present as a single nodule or plaque. The majority of patients with this uncommon skin disorder are of East Asian descent. There is a risk of progression to systemic lymphoproliferative malignancy.
synonyms: Primary cutaneous plasmacytoma
cn_name: 原发性皮肤浆细胞增多症

name: Gorlin-Chaudhry-Moss syndrome
definition: Gorlin-Chaudhry-Moss syndrome is characterised by midface hypoplasia, conductive hearing loss, hypertrichosis, coarse hair, shortened metacarpals and distal phalanges, short stature with a stocky body build and multiple ocular abnormalities.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 4 of ischium
synonyms: stage 4 decubitus ulcer of ischium

name: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex, localised
definition: Localised epidermolysis bullosa simplex (EBS-loc), formerly known as EBS Weber-Cockayne, is an autosomal dominant basal subtype of EBS due to mutations in the KRT5 and KRT14 genes encoding keratins 5 and 14 respectively. The disease is characterised by blisters occurring mainly on the palms and soles, exacerbated by warm weather.
synonyms: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex, Weber-Cockayne | localised EBS- [epidermolysis bullosa simplex]

name: Syringocystadenoma papilliferum
definition: Syringocystadenoma papilliferum is a skin hamartoma, which is believed to derived from apocrine sweat glands or, less often, from eccrine sweat glands. Three clinical types have been described. The plaque type presents mostly as a hairless area in the scalp, often in association with a sebaceous nevus of Jadassohn; the linear type, which is usually seen on the neck or face, and the solitary nodular type, which shows predilection for the trunk.
synonyms: papillary syringocystadenoma of unspecified site | papillary syringadenoma

name: Acute skin eruption of uncertain or unspecified nature
definition: A provisional diagnosis for an acute skin eruption of less than six weeks' duration of unknown, uncertain or unspecified nature.
synonyms: Acute pustular skin eruption | Acute rash | Acute skin eruption without established diagnosis
cn_name: 性质未确定或未特指的急性皮疹

name: Seborrhoeic dermatitis and related conditions
definition: A group of related inflammatory skin disorders affecting predominantly the scalp, face, upper trunk and flexures and characterised by variable amounts of erythema, scale, inflammation and exudation. It is thought that Malassezia yeasts play an important role in their pathogenesis. Although these disorders are common, they are seen with increased frequency and severity amongst persons infected with HIV.
synonyms: Seborrhoeic dermatitis | Seborrhoeic eczema
cn_name: 脂溢性皮炎及相关疾病

name: Pityriasis versicolor
definition: A disease of the skin, caused by an infection with the fungi Malassezia. This disease is characterised by white, pink, fawn, brown, or often coalescing lesions that may be covered with thin furfuraceous scales. This disease commonly presents on the trunk, shoulders and arms, or neck and face. Transmission is by opportunistic transmission. Confirmation is by identification of Malassezia in a skin sample.
synonyms: Tinea versicolor | tinea versicolor

name: Spider telangiectasis
definition: A benign vascular ectasia consisting of a central dilated terminal arteriole from which radiate several ectatic capillaries, giving rise to a spider-like appearance.  They occur most commonly on the upper trunk and proximal upper limbs.   Large numbers may develop in association with pregnancy or liver disease.
synonyms: araneus naevus | spider angioma | spider naevus | stellar naevus | stellate angioma | vascular spider | nevus araneus | spider telangiectasia
cn_name: 蜘蛛样毛细血管扩张症

name: Early congenital syphilis
definition: A disease affecting newborns or children up to 2 years of age, caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum pallidum in utero. Transmission is by vertical transmission. Confirmation is by prenatal screening.

name: Hypervitaminosis A
definition: Because vitamin A is fat soluble and can be stored, primarily in the liver, routine consumption of large amounts of vitamin A over a period of time can result in toxic symptoms, including liver damage, bone abnormalities and joint pain, alopecia, headaches, vomiting, and skin desquamation. Hypervitaminosis A appears to be due to abnormal transport and distribution of vitamin A and retinoids caused by overloading of the plasma transport mechanisms. Very high single doses can cause transient acute
synonyms: hyperalimentation of Vitamin A

name: Allergic dermatitis of eyelid
synonyms: allergic blepharitis

name: Hereditary angioedema type III
definition: Hereditary angioedema due not to abnormalities of C1 inhibitor production or function but to increased kininogenase activity and consequent increased bradykinin release. It may be triggered by pregnancy or oestrogen-containing oral contraceptive preparations.
synonyms: HAE-III - [Hereditary angioedema type III] | Hereditary angioedema with FXII mutation | Hereditary angioneurotic oedema type 3 | Inherited oestrogen-dependent angioedema | Inherited oestrogen-dependent angioneurotic oedema

synonyms: second degree sunburn

definition: In addition to a cystic hygroma, fetus also exhibits anasarca, including but not limited to pleural effusions and ascites.

name: Burn of back, any part, epidermal burn
synonyms: Erythema of back, any part
cn_name: 背部（任何部位）表皮层烧伤

name: Late syphilitic posterior uveitis
definition: This is a late, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with posterior uveitis.

name: Arachnid bites or sting
definition: Injury due to the bites or stings of spiders, ticks or scorpions.

name: Basal cell carcinoma of vulva
definition: A slow growing, locally infiltrating carcinoma that arises from the vulva. It is characterised by the presence of malignant cells that resemble the basal cells that are present in the epidermis.
synonyms: basal cell carcinoma of the vulva | Basal cell carcinoma of Vulva

name: Burn of breast, epidermal burn
synonyms: Erythema of breast

name: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton schoenleinii
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton schoenleinii, the organism responsible for favus, a severe inflammatory scarring alopecia.

name: Acantholytic dyskeratotic epidermal naevus
synonyms: Linear Darier disease | Zosteriform Darier disease | acantholytic dyskeratotic epidermal nevus

name: Vesicular dermatitis of hands or feet
definition: An eczema of unknown cause affecting principally the palms, soles and sides of the fingers and toes. It is commonly known as pompholyx and is characterised by eruptions of itchy, often multiloculated blisters, which tend to rupture and become secondarily infected. It occurs most commonly in adolescents and young adults. Its relationship to other forms of eczema and to allergic sensitisation, especially to nickel, remains to be adequately elucidated.
synonyms: Dyshidrotic eczema | Pompholyx | Vesicular eczema of hands and feet | acute and recurrent vesicular hand dermatitis | pomphoylox | Cheiropompholyx | dyshidrosis | DYSHYDROTIC ECZEMA | Vesicular eczema of hands and/or feet

name: Vulval dysaesthesia syndrome
synonyms: Dysaesthetic vulvodynia | Vestibulodynia | vestibulodynia

name: Childhood atopic eczema, prurigo pattern
definition: A variant of childhood atopic eczema characterised by multiple itchy, often excoriated papules.
synonyms: Atopic prurigo of the child | Childhood atopic dermatitis, prurigo pattern

name: Neonatal mucocutaneous candidosis
definition: Widespread neonatal infection of skin and mucous membranes by Candida yeasts. Typically, discrete vesicles or pustules on an erythematous base are seen initially on the face and chest but then extending following delivery. The condition is believed to be due to contamination of the skin surface with Candida yeasts during birth.

name: Erythema nodosum provoked by inflammatory bowel disease
definition: Erythema nodosum attributable to Crohn disease or ulcerative colitis.

name: Waardenburg syndrome type 3
definition: This type is caused by mutations in PAX3 and is phenotypically similar to type 1 with the additional feature of limb abnormalities.

name: Juvenile dermatomyositis with cutaneous vasculitis
definition: Juvenile dermatomyositis with cutaneous vasculitis is an early-onset form of dermatomyositis, characterised by proximal muscle weakness, evocative skin lesion, and systemic manifestations, along with cutaneous vasculitis and ulcerations

name: Multiple benign melanocytic naevi
definition: The presence of multiple benign melanocytic naevi (often taken as more than 20 naevi >2mm in diameter), an independent risk factor for the development of melanoma with highest risk associated with highest numbers of naevi (>100).

name: Lymphoedema of lower limbs secondary to obesity
Oedema due to impaired or obstructed venous return
definition: Oedema resulting from impaired or obstructed venous return from such diverse causes as venous thrombosis, right heart failure or vena caval compression as in pregnancy or from abdominal tumours.
synonyms: Oedema secondary to impaired or obstructed venous return

name: Verrucous lupus erythematosus
definition: A disfiguring and often treatment-resistant from of chronic cutaneous lupus erythematosus characterised by marked warty hyperkeratosis of involved skin. If hyperkeratosis is marked, a warty lesion with a red, slightly raised edge results. Verrucous lupus erythematosus is most commonly seen on the nose, temples, ears and scalp but may also occur on the palms and soles.
synonyms: Warty lupus erythematosus | verrucous lupus erythematosus

name: Primary generalised hyperhidrosis
definition: Primary generalised hyperhidrosis is characterised by sweating that exceeds the amount necessary to maintain thermal regulation.
synonyms: Essential hyperhidrosis

name: Drug-induced oral mucositis
synonyms: drug-induced oropharyngeal mucositis

name: Autosomal dominant basal epidermolysis bullosa simplex
definition: Forms of epidermolysis bullosa simplex with autosomal dominant inheritance involving predominantly keratins 5 or 14.

name: Acute discoid or annular skin eruption
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists of multiple circular or ring-shaped patches and plaques. Although infection due to dermatophyte infection (tinea or ringworm) can produce this pattern, many other skin disorders may have a discoid or annular configuration\: these are frequently misdiagnosed as tinea. Common examples include atopic eczema, nummular dermatitis and psoriasis.

name: Lupoid sycosis
definition: Lupoid sycosis is a destructive, chronic inflammatory form of deep bacterial folliculitis in which follicles are progressively replaced by scar tissue. Inflammatory follicular papules and pustules coalesce into oedematous plaques around the advancing margin, behind which is left a pink atrophic scar. The process usually begins in front of one ear or under the chin and extends irregularly in any direction. The scalp may be extensively involved.
synonyms: Sycosis lupoid

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, other joints in foot
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, other joints in foot

name: Pemphigus foliaceus
definition: Pemphigus foliaceus is a chronic autoimmune skin disease characterised by superficial blister formation on the skin mediated by auto-antibodies to the desmosome component desmoglein 1.
synonyms: Superficial pemphigus | pemphigus foliaceous

name: Cutaneous flap necrosis, partial
definition: Incomplete necrosis of a surgical skin flap

name: Irritant contact cheilitis
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis of lips. This may be caused by lipcare products, especially lip plumpers.

name: Median rhomboid glossitis
synonyms: glossitis rhomboidea mediana | persistent tuberculum impar | superficial midline glossitis | central papillary atrophy

name: Dermatoses resulting from anticoagulant therapy
cn_name: 抗凝治疗引起的皮肤病

name: Lichenified onchodermatitis
definition: Lichenified onchodermatitis (LOD) is characterised by raised, discrete, pruritic, and hyperpigmented papulonodular plaques associated with lymphadenopathy. The lesions may be confluent, with or without the presence of excoriations. APOD or CPOD may also be present.

name: Oesophageal varices with bleeding in autoimmune hepatitis
Melanonychia
definition: Melanin pigmentation of the nail plate. This may be of no significance but may signify the presence of melanoma arising from the nail matrix

name: Guttate psoriasis
definition: An acute, usually widespread eruption of small (<1cm) papules of psoriasis associated in a majority of cases with preceding streptococcal infection, particularly tonsillitis and streptococcal sore throat. This form of psoriasis is seen typically in children and young adults. If untreated it tends to resolve over a period of four to six months.
synonyms: Psoriasis guttata | eruptive psoriasis

name: Paraneoplastic pruritus
definition: Pruritus attributable to underlying malignant neoplasm is rare but may be an initial presenting symptom as in carcinoid.

name: Late syphilitic retrobulbar neuritis
definition: This is a late, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with retrobulbar neuritis.

name: Gummata and ulcers of yaws
definition: This is a soft, non-cancerous growth resulting from the tertiary stage of syphilis and ulcers, caused by a tropical infection of the skin, bones and joints caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum pertenue.
synonyms: gummata and ulcers due to yaws | gummata of yaws | gummatous frambeside | gummatous framboeside | gummatous yaws | nodular late yaws | ulcerated nodular late yaws | ulcerative gumma due to yaws | ulcers of yaws | yaws gumma

name: Cellulitis of floor of mouth
synonyms: Angina Ludovici | Ludwig’s angina | cellulitis of floor of mouth | Ludwig angina

name: Viral oral mucositis
synonyms: viral oropharyngeal mucositis

name: Drug-associated immune complex vasculitis
definition: Small vessel vasculitis typically presenting predominantly in the skin due to systemic exposure to certain drugs in predisposed individuals.
synonyms: Drug-induced leucocytoclastic vasculitis

name: Symptom or complaint relating to hair or scalp
definition: A very non-specific term to indicate an actual or perceived problem affecting the hair or the scalp which cannot be more precisely coded elsewhere.
cn_name: 毛发或头皮相关症状或主诉

name: Ataxia-telangiectasia-like disorder
definition: This is a rare, neurodegenerative, inherited disease causing severe disability. Ataxia refers to poor coordination and telangiectasia to small dilated blood vessels, both of which are hallmarks of the disease.
synonyms: ATLD - [Ataxia-telangiectasia-like disorder]

name: Scurvy
definition: Scurvy is a disease caused by a lack of vitamin C (ascorbic acid) in the diet. Vitamin C plays a central role in collagen and ground-substance formation, metabolism of aromatic amino acids (phenylalanine, tyrosine), reduction of folic acid to folinic acid and a broad range of biochemical redox reactions. Clinical features include perifollicular haemorrhages, ecchymoses, swollen bleeding gums, stomatitis and epistaxis.

name: Louse-borne relapsing fever
definition: A spirochaetal infection caused by the human to human transmission of Borrelia recurrentis by the human body louse. Epidemics are associated with poor living conditions as may result from famine or war. Episodic fever may progress to severe jaundice, haemorrhage, confusion and death. Confirmation is by identification of Borrelia in blood films.
synonyms: Relapsing fever due to Borrelia recurrentis

name: Pressure ulceration grade 1 of heel
synonyms: stage 1 decubitus ulcer of heel

name: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency
definition: This refers to any of several autosomal recessive diseases resulting from mutations of genes for enzymes mediating the biochemical steps of production of cortisol from cholesterol by the adrenal glands (steroidogenesis). This diagnosis is due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency.

name: PUVA lentiginosis
definition: The presence of multiple lentigines in fair-skinned people who have received an excessive cumulative exposure to ultraviolet A radiation during psoralen photochemotherapy treatment, usually for psoriasis. The lentigines are typically darker in colour than actinic lentigines and may appear clinically bizarre and irregular. It is however rare for them to progress to melanoma.

name: Amyopathic juvenile dermatomyositis
definition: Juvenile-onset amyopathic dermatomyositis is an uncommon variant of juvenile-onset dermatomyositis (JDM), characterised by the hallmark cutaneous features of dermatomyositis for at least 6 months without clinical or laboratory evidence of muscle disease. Cutaneous calcinosis, vasculopathy, and interstitial lung disease frequently complicate the course of classic JDM, but are infrequent in amyopathic JDM. Approximately 75% of amyopathic JDM patients will remain free from muscle disease after year

name: Plasma cell balanitis
definition: Plasma cell balanitis presents in middle-aged or elderly uncircumcised men as one or more indolent circumscribed shiny smooth red plaques on the glans. It is thought that retention of urine and squames between the tightly apposed and infrequently or inappropriately bathed, commensally hypercolonized epithelial surfaces of the prepuce and glans results in chronic inflammation. It is important that this condition is differentiated from penile intraepithelial neoplasia which may have a similar clin
synonyms: Balanoposthitis plasmacellularis | Pseudoerythroplastic balanitis | Zoon balanitis

name: Transient acantholytic dermatosis
synonyms: Grover disease

name: Congenital ectodermal defect
synonyms: ectodermal anomaly | ectodermal deformity

name: Adenoid seborrhoeic keratosis
definition: A seborrheic keratoses in which there are interlacing thin strands of basaloid cells, often pigmented, enclosing small horn cysts. This variant often evolves from an actinic lentigo.

name: Neonatal gestational pemphigoid
definition: Neonatal gestational pemphigoid (neonatal pemphigoid gestationis) is a short-lived autoimmune skin disease arising as a result of transplacental transmission to the neonate of maternal antibodies. It is characterised by blister formation on the skin and the mucous membranes mediated by auto-antibodies to the hemidesmosome component BP180.

name: Burn of perineum or genitalia, epidermal burn
synonyms: Burn of perineum and genitalia, first degree | Erythema of genitalia, external
cn_name: 会阴或生殖器烧伤，表皮烧伤

name: Sneddon syndrome
definition: Sneddon syndrome associates livedo reticularis and neurological signs. Livedo is permanent, cyanotic, with no infiltration, and affects the limbs, trunk and sometimes the face. Neurological signs appear later and include cerebrovascular accidents, epilepsy, vertigo and more rarely a pseudobulbar syndrome, chorea, episodes of amnesia or transient amaurosis.
synonyms: idiopathic livedo reticularis with cerebrovascular accidents

name: Erythropoietic porphyrias
definition: Erythropoietic porphyrias are associated clinically with photosensitivity and biochemically with abnormal accumulation of porphyrins in erythrocytes. They include erythropoietic protoporphyria and the very rare congenital erythropoietic porphyria.

name: Eczema craquelé due to acute distension of the skin
Annular lipoatrophic panniculitis of the ankles
Enteroviral vesicular pharyngitis
synonyms: Herpangina | acute lymphonodular pharyngitis | aphthous angina | aphthous pharyngitis | coxsackie sore throat | coxsackievirus infection of the pharynx | coxsackievirus pharyngitis | enteroviral lymphonodular pharyngitis | vesicular pharyngitis

name: Hindfoot callosities
definition: Callosities of the heel and ankle area resulting typically from structural deformities of the foot or abnormalities of gait. They may also result from ill-fitting footwear. A distinct and common form is due to sitting back onto the feet from a kneeling position, thereby exposing the skin overlying the talus to repeated pressure and friction (talar callosity).
synonyms: Clavus of heel | Heel callus

name: Idiopathic pure sudomotor failure
definition: Idiopathic pure sudomotor failure is a disorder with sudden onset pain, cholinergic urticaria and generalised anhidrosis. The disorder is often associated with elevated serum IgE levels and responds to parenteral corticosteroids.

name: Persistent insect bite reaction
definition: Bite reactions lasting for months as inflamed papules and nodules, this is particularly likely to be seen with tick bites and mosquito bites. These may be confused with lymphoma histologically, with a dense inflammatory infiltrate of lymphoid cells, histiocytes, eosinophils and plasma cells together with the presence of atypical mononuclear cells.
synonyms: Insect bite granuloma
cn_name: 昆虫叮咬后持久性反应

name: Intertriginous dermatitis due to friction, sweating or contact with body fluids
definition: Intertriginous dermatitis (intertrigo) is a form of irritant contact dermatitis of the skin folds (axillary, submammary, genitocrural, abdominal apron) caused by repetitive shearing forces of skin on skin. Sweat, other body fluids, occlusion and obesity all contribute to its development.
synonyms: Chafing | Intertriginous eczema | Intertrigo
cn_name: 摩擦、出汗或接触体液引起的间擦皮炎

name: Palmar fascial fibromatosis
definition: This is a fixed flexion contracture of the hand where the fingers bend towards the palm and cannot be fully extended (straightened).
synonyms: Contracture of palmar fascia | Dupuytren contracture | Dupuytren disease of palm | Dupuytren's contraction or disease | Palmar fibromatosis | Retraction of palmar fascia | dupuytren's diathesis | dupuytren's disease | palmar fibromatosis

name: Superficial folliculitis associated with normal skin flora
definition: A common, usually chronic but asymptomatic superficial folliculitis from which Staphylococcus epidermidis or other coagulase negative staphylococci can be isolated.

name: Hairy leukoplakia
definition: Oral hairy leukoplakia is a focal epithelial hyperplasia of the oral mucosa associated with Epstein-Barr virus. It is closely associated with HIV and occurs in both immunocompromised and immunocompetent HIV-infected individuals. It is also, however, found in uninfected people who are immunosuppressed, e.g. organ transplant recipients. It presents as white patches with a corrugated or hairy appearance affecting particularly the lateral borders of the tongue.
synonyms: Hairy leucoplakia | Oral hairy leukoplakia

name: Patchy alopecia areata of scalp
definition: The commonest form of alopecia areata in which one or more usually circular patches of scalp hair loss develop.
synonyms: Alopecia areata, circumscribed of scalp

name: Dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa pruriginosa
definition: A form of dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa the onset of which is often delayed until adulthood. It is characterised by intense pruritus in affected areas, typically the shins, and may readily be mistaken for other pruritic dermatoses.
synonyms: DDEB pruriginosa - [dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa pruriginosa] | DDEB-Pr - [dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa pruriginosa]

name: Solar urticaria
definition: Solar urticaria is an uncommon form of physical urticaria provoked by skin exposure to visible light and/or ultraviolet radiation.
synonyms: Sunlight-induced urticaria

name: Familial partial lipodystrophy, Köbberling type
definition: Familial partial lipodystrophy, Köbberling type belongs to the group of lipodystrophy syndromes characterised by anomalies of adipose tissue distribution and often associated with severe insulin resistance manifested by hyperinsulinaemia (insulin levels above 20mUI/l), carbohydrate intolerance or diabetes, hypertriglyceridaemia with low levels of high-density lipoproteins (HDL) and arterial hypertension. Acanthosis nigricans, hepatic steatosis and polycystic ovary syndrome are also frequent.
synonyms: FPLD1 - [Familial partial lipodystrophy type 1] | Familial partial lipodystrophy type 1

name: Burn of wrist or hand, deep full thickness or complex burn
synonyms: Burn of wrist and hand, fourth degree
cn_name: 腕或手深全层皮肤烧伤或复合烧伤

name: Progressive macular hypomelanosis
definition: Progressive macular hypomelanosis (PMH) is a common acquired dermatosis occurring in all skin types but most obvious in darker skin types. It is characterised by the progressive appearance of ill‐defined nummular non-scaling macules mainly affecting the trunk. It typically presents in adolescence or early adulthood and then persists. The cause of the pigment loss is unknown but Propionibacterium species have been implicated.
synonyms: PMH - [Progressive macular hypomelanosis]

name: Mycosis fungoides with limited patches or plaques
definition: Mycosis fungoides with patches, papules and/or plaques affecting less than 10 % of body surface
synonyms: Mycosis fungoides stage T1

name: Atrophoderma of Pasini and Pierini
definition: This is considered to be an atrophic variant of morphoea. One or more round or oval patches of atrophic skin measuring up to many centimetres in diameter develop insidiously and without symptoms, usually in the second or third decade of life. The patches are smooth, slate-coloured or violet brown, and are slightly depressed below the level of the entirely normal surrounding skin. The back is almost always involved, the chest and abdomen frequently, and the proximal parts of the limbs occasionall
synonyms: atrophoderma of pasini and pierini | dyschromic and atrophic version of scleroderma | morphea plana atrophica | sclerodermie atrophique d'emblee

name: Neurofibromatosis type 3
synonyms: Mixed central and peripheral neurofibromatosis | NF3 - [Neurofibromatosis type 3] (MIM 162260)

name: Dermatitis or eczema of male genitalia
definition: Dermatitis (eczema) involving male external genital organs.
cn_name: 男性生殖器皮炎或湿疹

name: Foreign body reaction to cactus spines
definition: Foreign body granulomata resulting from penetration of Opuntia cactus spines (glochids) into the skin. They present as multiple small papules which can persist for up to eight months before resolving spontaneously.

name: Median cleft lip or mandible
definition: Midline cleft of lower lip is a rare anomaly defined as Cleft No. 30 in Tessier's classification. These cases show a broad variation in severity, ranging from a simple notch in the vermillion to a complete cleft of the lip involving the tongue, the chin, the mandible, the supporting structures of the median of the neck, and the manubrium sterni. The tongue is often attached to the cleft alveolar margin (ankyloglossia). Other associated anomalies were described in a number of cases.
synonyms: Central cleft lip | Median cleft lip/mandible | midline cleft lip

name: Vasculitis or capillaritis involving the skin
definition: A range of conditions characterised by inflammation of cutaneous blood vessels with or without extravasation of red blood cells into the interstitium.
synonyms: Vasculitis and inflammatory purpura involving the skin
cn_name: 累及皮肤的血管炎或毛细血管扩张

name: Calcific uraemic arteriolopathy
definition: Calcific arteriolopathy (calciphylaxis) occurring in association with chronic kidney disease or renal transplantation. This is much commoner than calcific arteriolopathy attributable to non-uraemic disorders.
synonyms: uraemia-associated calciphylaxis

name: Olfactory reference disorder with poor to absent insight
definition: All definitional requirements of olfactory reference disorder are met. Most or all of the time, the individual is convinced that the disorder-specific beliefs are true and cannot accept an alternative explanation for their experience. The lack of insight exhibited by the individual does not vary markedly as a function of anxiety level.

name: Sequelae of burn or corrosion of head or neck
Gummatous late congenital syphilis
synonyms: Congenital syphilitic gumma | congenital gumma | congenital syphilis with gumma

name: Pressure ulceration, ungradable of trochanter
synonyms: Pressure ulceration, ungradable of hip

name: Karyotype 46, X iso Xq
definition: A disease affecting females, caused by one of the two X chromosomes consisting of two q arms, which are structurally identical and contain the same genes. This disease may present with short stature, extra folds of skin on the neck, a low hairline at the back of the neck, puffiness or swelling of the hands and feet, skeletal abnormalities, ovarian hypofunction or premature ovarian failure, kidney problems, or heart defects. This disease may be differentiated from classical Turner Syndrome by a n

name: Antisynthetase syndrome
definition: Antisynthetase syndrome (ASS) is characterised by an inflammatory myositis associated with interstitial lung disease (ILD) and antisynthetase antibodies. Other symptoms, including arthritis, Raynaud's phenomenon and mechanic's hands, are also associated with ASS. Several antisynthetase antibodies have been described, with anti-Jo1 being the most common.

name: Contact cheilitis
definition: Cheilitis due to contact with irritants or allergens

name: Primary essential cutis verticis gyrata
definition: Primary essential cutis verticis gyrata is a rare, progressive dermis disorder characterized by thickening of the scalp resulting in redundancy of the skin which gives rise to folds and grooves that give the scalp a cerebriform appearance. Folds cannot be corrected by pressure or traction and typically are symmetric and extend anteroposteriorly from vertex to occiput and/or transversely in occipital region. Additional features may include mild subungual hyperkeratosis and distal onycholysis of t

name: Primary irritant napkin dermatitis
definition: A type of irritant dermatitis seen most frequently in infants localised to the area in contact with a napkin (diaper) and occurring most often as a reaction to prolonged contact with urine, faeces, or retained soap or detergent.
synonyms: Diaper dermatitis | Diaper rash | Irritant napkin dermatitis | Napkin dermatitis | Nappy rash | Primary irritant diaper dermatitis
cn_name: 原发性刺激性尿布皮炎

name: Elastoma
definition: A rare, genetic or acquired, dermis elastic tissue disorder characterized by asymptomatic, solitary or multiple, firm, skin-colored to yellowish papules or nodules of variable size that are disseminated or grouped in clusters and typically located on the trunk, buttocks, thighs or face, among others. Histologically, focal increase of thickened, tortuous elastic fibers in the reticular dermis, without signs of degeneration, is reported. Isolated cases, as well as cases associated with osteopoikil
synonyms: nevus elasticus

name: Distichiasis, unilateral
definition: This is an eyelash that arises from an abnormal spot on the eyelid. Distichiae usually exit from the duct of the meibomian gland at the eyelid margin. This diagnosis is occurring in one eye.

name: Congenital toxoplasma encephalitis
synonyms: congenital encephalitis due to Toxoplasma gondii | congenital encephalitis in toxoplasmosis

name: Amelo-onycho-hypohidrotic syndrome
definition: A rare ectodermal dysplasia syndrome characterized by the association of hypocalcified and hypoplastic tooth enamel, distal finger and toenail onycholysis with subungueal hyperkeratosis, and functional hypohidrosis. Additional manifestations include seborrheic scalp dermatitis and rough, dry skin. Lacrymal punctae may be occasionally absent. There have been no further descriptions in the literature since 1975.

name: Eczematous dermatitis of eyelids
definition: Any eczematous condition of the eyelids which cannot be more precisely attributed to atopic eczema, seborrhoeic dermatitis or contact with irritants or allergens.
synonyms: Eyelid eczema

name: Irritant contact dermatitis due substantially to occupational exposure to irritant
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis caused mainly or exclusively by exposure to skin irritants at work.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema due substantially to occupational exposure to irritant

name: Irritant contact dermatitis related to stoma or fistula
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis of skin surrounding stomas or fistulas caused by prolonged or repeated contact with gastrointestinal secretions, faeces, urine, pus, mucus, or cleansing materials.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema related to stoma or fistula
cn_name: 与造口或瘘管有关的刺激性接触性皮炎

name: Malignant neoplasm metastasis in skin of jaw
Sycosis
definition: Infection of the bearded part of a man's face, caused by a bacterial infection.
synonyms: sycosis, nos

name: Pigmented spindle cell naevus of Reed
definition: Pigmented spindle cell naevus of Reed is a benign melanocytic naevus showing dark pigmentation clinically and a proliferation of spindled melanocytes histopathologically.
synonyms: Reed naevus

name: Cutaneous sarcoidosis
synonyms: Sarcoidosis of skin | Cutaneous sarcoid | cutaneous sarcoidosis

name: Genetic defects of the hair shaft
Nagashima-type palmoplantar keratoderma
definition: Keratosis, Nagashima-type is a transgressive and nonprogressive palmoplantar keratoderma resembling a mild form of mal de Meleda.

name: Hand-Schüller-Christian disease
definition: This is associated with multifocal Langerhans cell histiocytosis and is associated with a triad of exophthalmos, lytic bone lesions (often in the skull), and diabetes insipidus (from pituitary stalk infiltration).
synonyms: Multifocal eosinophilic granuloma

name: Anogenital pruritus
definition: Persistent itching of the perianal skin and/or external genitalia.
synonyms: Anogenital itch

name: Crusted scabies
definition: Crusted scabies results from unchecked proliferation of the human scabies mite in individuals who are unable to mount an adequate immune response to infestation. Extensive thick crusts containing vast numbers of mites form over the skin, particularly of the extremities. Because itching is usually absent, the diagnosis is frequently overlooked. Patients with crusted scabies may serve as the source for widespread outbreaks of scabies in institutions such as hospitals and care homes.
synonyms: Norwegian scabies | norwegian scabies

name: Familial multiple café-au-lait macules
definition: Neurofibromatosis type 6 (NF6), also referred as café-au-lait spots syndrome, is a cutaneous disorder characterised by the presence of several café-au-lait macules without any other manifestations of neurofibromatosis or any other systemic disorder.
synonyms: Café-au-lait spots syndrome | NF6 - [Neurofibromatosis type 6] | Neurofibromatosis type 6

name: Acquired changes in hair colour
definition: Abnormal diffuse or circumscribed alterations in natural hair colour. Causes include drugs, idiopathic premature greying of the hair, differential shedding of pigmented hair in alopecia areata or hair pigment loss in association with vitiligo or regressing melanocytic naevi.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 4 of upper limb
synonyms: stage 4 decubitus ulcer of upper limb

name: Tietz hypomelanosis – deafness syndrome
definition: Tietz hypomelanosis - deafness syndrome is an autosomal dominant condition characterised by generalised hypomelanosis, blue eyes and profound sensorineural deafness. Affected family members gradually gain some pigmentation and have fair skin and blond to white hair as adults, with white eyebrows and eyelashes. The syndrome is due to a missense mutation in the basic region of the MITF gene.

name: Allergic contact dermatitis of scalp
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis affecting the scalp.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema of scalp

name: Ulerythema ophryogenes
definition: Ulerythema ophryogenes is a skin disorder characterised by inflammatory keratotic papules occurring on the face, which may be followed by scars, atrophy and alopecia.
synonyms: Ulerythema ophryogenis | ulerythema, unspecified

name: Lichen simplex of male genitalia
definition: Circumscribed pruritic lichenification of male genitalia. The scrotum or the base of the penis are commonly affected sites.
cn_name: 男性生殖器单纯性苔藓

name: Midge bite
definition: Bites due to blood-sucking ceratopogonid flies of the genera Culicoides, Leptoconops, Austroconops and Forcipomyia. These small flies breed in rivers, swamps and marshes. They often occur in swarms and will readily attack any mammal in their vicinity.
synonyms: Ceratopogonid fly bites | Punkie bites

name: Non-venereal treponematoses
synonyms: endemic treponematoses

name: Non-syndromic genetically-determined hypermelanosis or lentiginosis
cn_name: 非综合征性遗传性黑变病或黑子病

name: Mature T-cell or NK-cell lymphomas and lymphoproliferative disorders, primary cutaneous specified types
definition: Primary cutaneous T-cell lymphomas (CTCL) and NK-cell lymphomas are malignant lymphoproliferative diseases of unknown cause that are thought to originate from T-lymphocytes in the lymphoid tissue of the skin and by definition are confined to the skin at initial diagnosis. Included in the class is also a small number of lymphoma-like primary cutaneous lymphoproliferative disorders which are not considered to be truly malignant.
synonyms: Primary cutaneous peripheral T-cell lymphoma
cn_name: 成熟T细胞或NK细胞淋巴瘤和淋巴增殖性疾病，原发性皮肤特指类型

synonyms: ehlers-danlos syndrome type 1 | ehlers-danlos syndrome type 2

definition: A category to enable the presence of a skin disorder to be recorded without making assumptions as to the precise nature of the disorder in question.

cn_name: 性质不确定或未特指的皮肤疾病

name: Variegate porphyria
definition: Variegate porphyria is a form of acute hepatic porphyria characterised by the occurrence of neuro-visceral attacks with or without the presence of cutaneous lesions (bullous photodermatitis).
synonyms: Porphyria variegata | Protocoproporphyria | Protoporphyrinogen oxidase deficiency | VP - [variegate porphyria]

name: Actinic telangiectasia

name: Dermo-odontodysplasia
definition: Dermo-odonto dysplasia belongs to the group of tricho-odonto-onychial dysplasias and is characterised by signs of variable severity\: dry and thin skin, dental anomalies, nail alteration and trichodysplasia.
synonyms: Dermoodontodysplasia

name: Allergic contact dermatitis of face or neck
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis affecting the face and neck.
