name: Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome
definition: Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome (BWS) is a genetic disorder characterised by overgrowth, tumour predisposition and congenital malformations.
synonyms: Exomphalos-macroglossia-gigantism syndrome

name: Coffin-Siris syndrome
definition: Coffin-Siris syndrome is a malformation syndrome characterised by the association of absent fifth finger and toenails with intellectual deficit. Other signs include microcephaly, coarse facial features, sparse scalp hair, early feeding problems, recurrent respiratory infections, short stature and growth deficiency.

name: Special types of melanocytic naevus
definition: Melanocytic naevi with distinct clinical and/or histopathological characteristics.

name: Cytophagic histiocytic panniculitis
definition: Cytophagic histiocytic panniculitis is a very rare form of panniculitis manifesting as recurrent multiple subcutaneous nodules (which may progressively become ecchymotic and ulcerated), and histologically characterised by lobular panniculitis with lymphocytic and histiocytic infiltration in the subcutaneous adipose tissue. The histiocytic infiltration often involves lymph nodes, bone marrow and other tissues of the reticuloendothelial system. Severe fever, malaise, pancytopenia, hepatosplenomega

name: Pulmonary dirofilariasis
definition: Infection induced by Dirofilariasis immitis larvae, which lodge in small pulmonary arteries causing thoracic pain (both pleural and nonpleural), purulent or haemoptoic sputum, haemoptysis, dyspnea, malaise and myalgia.

name: Skin rash due to distant or systemic bacterial infection, not elsewhere classified
synonyms: Skin rash secondary to distant or systemic bacterial infection not elsewhere classified

name: Superficial burns or corrosion
synonyms: first degree burns or corrosions

name: Excess vitamin A administered as drug
Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to clothing or footwear
Gaucher disease type 2
definition: Gaucher disease type 2 is the acute neurological form of Gaucher disease. It is characterised by early-onset and severe neurological involvement of the brainstem, associated with an organomegaly and generally leading to death before the age of 2.
synonyms: Acute neuronopathic Gaucher disease | Cerebral acute Gaucher disease | Infantile Gaucher disease | Infantile cerebral Gaucher disease | gaucher syndrome type 2

name: Congenital palmoplantar or perioral keratoderma
definition: A keratosis characterized by a bilateral mutilating palmoplantar keratoderma and periorificial keratotic plaques with severe pruritus of lesions.
synonyms: Olmsted syndrome | mutilating palmoplantar hyperkeratosis with periorificial keratotic plaques | OLMS | palmoplantar and periorificial keratoderma

name: Necrobiotic xanthogranuloma
definition: Necrobiotic xanthogranuloma (NXG) is a rare multisystem histiocytic disease involving the skin in which widespread infiltrated xanthomatous nodules and plaques are associated in the majority of cases with paraproteinaemia and a variety of underlying lymphoproliferative disorders including myeloma. NXG differs from diffuse normolipidaemic plane xanthomatosis in that the skin lesions are palpably infiltrated and elevated rather than flat and diffuse and in that necrobiosis and Touton giant cells a
synonyms: necrobiotic xanthogranuloma with paraproteinemia

name: X-linked cutis laxa
definition: Occipital horn syndrome (OHS) is a mild form of Menkes disease (see this term), a syndrome characterised by progressive neurodegeneration and connective tissue disorders due to a copper transport defect.
synonyms: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type IX | OHS - [occipital horn syndrome] | Occipital horn syndrome

name: Citrullinaemia
synonyms: ASA synthase deficiency | Argininosuccinate synthetase deficiency | Citrullinuria | deficiency of citrulline-aspartate ligase | metabolic disorder of citrulline

name: Eosinophilic cellulitis
definition: Eosinophilic cellulitis (Wells syndrome) is characterised by a distinctive clinical picture resembling cellulitis, and a typical histology with tissue eosinophilia, oedema and ‘flame’ figures (clusters of eosinophils and histiocytes around a core of collagen and eosinophilic debris). It can affect either sex, usually in adult life. Any site may be involved, with single or multiple lesions, and recurrences are common. Initially, the lesions are itchy erythematous plaques with features resembling
synonyms: Wells' syndrome | eosinophilic cellulitis

name: Primary Raynaud disease
definition: Raynaud phenomenon unassociated with any concomitant disease, drug or other provoking trauma. Criteria for diagnosis include\: bilateral symmetrical episodic attacks without evidence of peripheral vascular disease or tissue injury, normal nail fold capillaroscopy, negative antinuclear antibody and normal erythrocyte sedimentation rate.
synonyms: Raynaud disease

name: CATCH 22 phenotype
definition: Monosomy 22q11 (DiGeorge Velocardiofacial syndrome, DGS/VCF) syndrome is a chromosomal anomaly characterised by the association of several variable malformations\: hypoplastic thymus and parathyroid glands, congenital conotruncal heart defects, a subtle but characteristic facial dysmorphism, cleft palate or velar insufficiency, and learning difficulties.
synonyms: 22q11 deletion syndrome | CATCH 22 | Conotruncal anomalies face syndrome | DiGeorge sequence | DiGeorge syndrome | Monosomy 22q11 | Pharyngeal pouch syndrome | Sedlackova syndrome | Shprintzen syndrome | Velocardiofacial syndrome

name: Eccrine angiomatous hamartoma
definition: Eccrine angiomatous hamartoma is a rare benign malformation composed of eccrine sweat glands and angiomatous capillary channels. It may present as profuse localised sweating.
synonyms: Sudoriparous angioma

name: Bird mite dermatitis
definition: An inflammatory, intensely pruritic reaction to contact with any of a variety of parasitic avian mites. Bird mite dermatitis presents typically as a profuse asymmetrical urticarial, papular or papulovesicular eruption. The diagnosis is frequently obscured by secondary excoriation and impetiginizion. Commoner causes include Dermanyssus gallinae (the red poultry mite) and Ornithonyssus sylviarum (the northern fowl mite).
synonyms: Avian mite dermatitis | Poultry mite dermatitis | dermatitis due to Dermanyssus gallinae | dermatitis due to Liponyssoides sanguineus | dermatitis due to Ornithonyssus sylviarum | infestation by dermanyssus gallinae | liponyssoides sanguineus infestation | poultryman's itch

name: Acute meningococcaemia
definition: A condition caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Neisseria meningitidis that leads to a severe systemic inflammatory response. This condition is characterised by fever, chills, myalgia, nausea, or petechial rash, with progression to shock and disseminated intravascular coagulation. Transmission is by direct contact or droplet transmission. Confirmation is by identification of Neisseria meningitidis in blood samples.
synonyms: acute meningococcal septicaemia (deprecated)

name: Autoimmune xerostomia
definition: Xerostomia associated with sicca syndromes (Sjögren and Mikukicz) or associated with other non-organ-specific systemic autoimmune disorders.
synonyms: Dry mouth due to sicca syndrome | Xerostomia due to Sjögren syndrome

name: Atypical fibroxanthoma
definition: Atypical fibroxanthoma is a nonencapsulated dermal tumour composed of large, fibrocytic, spindle-shaped and anaplastic cells which typically develops rapidly in sun- or radiation-damaged skin. It may be mistaken clinically and histologically for an aggressive malignant tumour. Although local recurrence and spread to lymph nodes may occur, it usually does not recur after surgical removal.
synonyms: superficial variant of malignant fibrous histiocytoma

name: Ross syndrome
definition: Ross syndrome is a disorder composed of tonic pupil (unilateral or bilateral), absent muscle stretch reflexes and progressive segmental impairment of sweating.

name: Patent urachus
definition: The urachus is a fibrous remnant of the allantois which is a canal that drains the urinary bladder of the fetus that joins and runs within the umbilical cord. If the urachus remains patent there will be leakage of urine through the umbilicus.
synonyms: Patent urachal duct | Persistent urachus | Remnant urachus | Urachal fistula

name: Pyoderma gangrenosum, acne conglobata and suppurative hidradenitis
synonyms: PASH - [pyoderma gangrenosum, acne conglobata and suppurative hidradenitis]

name: Chronic infantile neurological, cutaneous and articular syndrome
definition: Chronic Infantile Neurological, Cutaneous, and Articular (CINCA) syndrome is an autoinflammatory syndrome characterised by skin rash, joint involvement, chronic meningitis with granulocytes and, in some cases, sensorineural hearing loss and ocular signs.
synonyms: CINCA - [Chronic infantile neurological, cutaneous and articular] syndrome | IOMID - [Infantile-onset multisystem inflammatory disease] | Infantile-onset multisystem inflammatory disease | NOMID - [Neonatal-onset multisystem inflammatory disease] | Neonatal-onset multisystem inflammatory disease | Prieur-Griscelli syndrome | chronic infantile neurologic cutaneous and articular syndrome | neonatal-onset multisystem inflammatory disease | NOMID

name: SIBIDS syndrome
definition: SIBIDS syndrome is a form of trichothiodystrophy (sulphur-deficient brittle hair) characterised by Ichthyosis, Brittle hair, Intellectual impairment, Decreased fertility, and Short stature (like in IBIDS syndrome) combined with osteosclerosis.
synonyms: Trichothiodystrophy - osteosclerosis

name: Infantile haemangioma of the respiratory tract
definition: An extracutaneous infantile haemangioma located in the larynx or trachea, characteristically just below the vocal cords. It may cause varying degrees of airway obstruction.

name: Immunobullous systemic lupus erythematosus
definition: Immunobullous systemic lupus erythematosus is an often transient autoimmune skin disease characterised by blisters on the skin and, less commonly, mucous membranes occurring in the setting of systemic lupus erythematosus. The exact causes of the disease are unknown but it is mediated by autoantibodies to the basement membrane zone.
synonyms: Bullous systemic lupus erythematosus | bullous eruption of systemic lupus erythematosus

name: Skin fragility
definition: Fragility of the skin due principally to genetic or acquired abnormalities of dermal matrix proteins.

name: Erythema multiforme provoked by other specified bacterial infection
Allergic contact dermatitis due to cosmetics or fragrances
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to sensitisation to any of a large number of allergens which may be found in cosmetics and other products to which fragrances are added. Common allergens include colophony, fragrances such as hydroxycitronellal, emollients such as lanolin (wool wax alcohols), surfactants such as cocamidopropyl betaine, or preservatives such as isothiazolinones, parabens or formaldehyde-releasing agents. Fragrances are found in many household products as well as in cosmetics.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to cosmetics or fragrances | Perfume allergic contact dermatitis | allergic contact dermatitis due to cosmetics
cn_name: 化妆品或香料引起的变应性接触性皮炎

name: Insect bites or stings
definition: Bites by arthropods of the class Insecta. Human ectoparasites responsible for tungiasis, myiasis, cimicosis, hirudiniasis, phthiriasis and pediculosis are represented under Infestations due to ectoparasites and are excluded.

name: Pityriasiform drug eruption
definition: This is an uncommon pattern of reaction to drugs. It clinically resembles pityriasis rosea. It has been most commonly reported with gold therapy; other drugs implicated include metronidazole, captopril, isotretinoin and omeprazole.
synonyms: Pityriasis rosea-like drug eruption

name: Tricho-odonto-onychial dysplasia with bone deficiency in fronto-parietal region
Trichiasis without entropion lower eyelid
definition: This refers to abnormally positioned eyelashes that grow back toward the eye, touching the cornea or conjunctiva. This can be caused by infection, inflammation, autoimmune conditions, congenital defects, eyelid agenesis and trauma such as burns or eyelid injury. This diagnosis is without a condition in the lower eyelid which the eyelid folds inward. It is very uncomfortable, as the eyelashes constantly rub against the cornea and irritate it.

name: Disseminated intravascular coagulation of fetus or newborn
definition: Neonatal purpura fulminans is a potentially lethal disorder characterised by progressive haemorrhagic necrosis of the skin associated with cutaneous vascular thrombosis. It is usually due to a genetically transmitted thrombophilic disorder\: most commonly homozygous deficiency of protein C or, less frequently, protein S.
cn_name: 胎儿或新生儿弥散性血管内凝血

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, acromioclavicular joint
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, acromioclavicula joint

name: Pyogenic arthritis - pyoderma gangrenosum - acne
definition: Pyogenic sterile arthritis, pyoderma gangrenosum, and acne (PAPA) syndrome is a rare pleiotropic autoinflammatory disorder of childhood, primarily affecting the joints and skin, characterised by pauciarticular, nonaxial, sometimes recurrent, sterile arthritis beginning in childhood; pyoderma gangrenosum; and severe cystic acne in adolescence and beyond.
synonyms: PAPA- [pyogenic arthritis, pyoderma gangrenosum and acne] syndrome | FAMILIAL RECURRENT ARTHRITIS | Pyogenic arthritis - pyoderma gangrenosum - acne | pyogenic arthritis, pyoderma gangrenosum, acne conglobata syndrome
omim: 604416 "PYOGENIC STERILE ARTHRITIS, PYODERMA GANGRENOSUM, AND ACNE"

name: Citrullinaemia type 1
definition: Citrullinemia type 1 is a rare autosomal recessive urea cycle defect characterised clinically by hyperammonemia, progressive lethargy, poor feeding and vomiting in the neonatal form (Acute neonatal citrullinemia type 1, ) and by variable hyperammonemia in the later-onset form (Adult-onset citrullinemia type 1, ).
synonyms: Argininosuccinate synthase deficiency | CTLN1 - [Citrullinaemia type 1] | Citrullinaemia type 3 | Citrullinuria type 1 | Citrullinuria type 3 | Classic citrullinaemia

name: Syphilis
definition: A predominantly sexually transmitted infection caused by Treponema pallidum ssp. pallidum.
synonyms: Lues | luetic disease | syphilis NOS | lues

name: Lupus erythematosus tumidus
definition: Lupus erythematosus tumidus is a dermal form of chronic cutaneous lupus erythematosus. It is characteristically photosensitive and located in sun-exposed sites. It presents with infiltrated erythematous nodules and plaques which become annular. It does not scar and is clinically very similar to Jessner lymphocytic from which it cannot always be distinguished.
synonyms: Tumid lupus erythematosus | tumid lupus

name: Pseudomonas infection of nail
definition: Pseudomonas infection occurring between the nail plate and the nail bed, and/or between an artificial nail coating and the nail plate and resulting in a green discolouration of the nail.

name: Fire coral sting
definition: envenomation by the nematocysts of Millepora hydroids (fire corals), presenting as severe pain and swelling at the points of contact. Systemic reactions may occur in young children.
synonyms: Hydroid coral sting | Millepora sting

name: Endogenous non-melanin pigmentation
definition: Pigmentation of the skin resulting from endogenous pigments other than melanin. The most important of these is haemosiderin.

name: Malignant lesion involving overlapping sites of penis
Aplasia cutis congenita of limbs
definition: Aplasia cutis congenita of limbs is a developmental anomaly described in a few cases and characterised by congenital absence of skin in either the upper or lower limbs or both that healed spontaneously leaving a clinical appearance similar to that of the hypotrichotic scar of an old burn. There was no trunk or scalp involvement.
synonyms: Recessive aplasia cutis congenita of limbs

name: Epidermolytic ichthyosis
definition: Epidermolytic ichthyosis is a genetic skin disorder due to mutations in the keratin 10 gene (KRT10). Inheritance is usually autosomal dominant but up to 50% of cases are sporadic. It may manifest at birth with erythroderma and severe blistering but with age blistering tends to be less severe and erythema less prominent. Progressive hyperkeratosis develops, accentuated at joint flexures. Life expectancy is usually normal although the disease can be severely incapacitating.
synonyms: Bullous congenital ichthyosiform erythroderma of Brocq | Congenital bullous ichthyosiform erythroderma | Epidermolytic hyperkeratosis | bullous congenital ichthyosiform erythroderma | bullous ichthyosis | EHK | EI | epidermolytic hyperkeratosis | Bullous ichthyosiform erythroderma | epidermolytic ichthyosis

name: Oesophageal varices without bleeding in primary biliary cholangiopathy
definition: These are extremely dilated sub-mucosal veins in the lower third of the oesophagus without bleeding in an autoimmune disease of the liver marked by the slow progressive destruction of the small bile ducts of the liver, with the intralobular ducts (Canals of Hering) affected early in the disease.
synonyms: Oesophageal varices without bleeding in primary biliary cirrhosis

name: Lymphocytic vasculitis due to other specified cause
Ectodermal dysplasia - acanthosis nigricans
definition: Lelis syndrome is characterised by the association of ectodermal dysplasia (hypotrichosis and hypohidrosis) with acanthosis nigricans.
synonyms: Lelis syndrome

name: Genetic syndromes with hypertrichosis
definition: Genetic syndromes in which excessive non-androgen-dependent hair growth is associated with other abnormalities.
cn_name: 遗传学综合征，伴多毛（症）

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to topical medicaments
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to exposure to topical medicaments such as corticosteroids, antibiotics, antimycotics, disinfectants, local anaesthetics or NSAIDs.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to topical medicaments | allergic contact dermatitis due to drugs in contact with skin
cn_name: 局部用药引起的变应性接触性皮炎

name: Follicular psoriasis
definition: An uncommon form of psoriasis where involvement is centred around pilosebaceous follicular ostia.

name: Acute urticaria of undetermined aetiology
definition: Acute urticaria for which no cause can be found (idiopathic)
synonyms: Acute idiopathic urticaria

name: Junctional melanocytic naevus
definition: A nevus characterised by the presence of an intraepidermal proliferation of nevus cells. The nevus cells form multiple nests in the dermal-epidermal junction. It presents as a small, slightly raised, pigmented skin lesion.
synonyms: Junctional naevus

name: Citrin deficiency
definition: Citrin deficiency is a rare autosomal recessive urea cycle defect characterised clinically by recurring episodes of hyperammonemia and associated neuropsychiatric symptoms in the adult-onset form (citrullinemia type 2, ), and by transient cholestasis and variable hepatic dysfunction in the neonatal form (neonatal intrahepatic cholestasis due to citrin deficiency, ).
synonyms: Aspartate-glutamate carrier deficiency

name: Erythema multiforme
definition: Erythema multiforme is a self‐limiting reactive inflammatory dermatosis triggered by cell‐mediated hypersensitivity, most commonly to drugs or infection, particularly Herpes simplex. It is characterised by an eruption of macules, papules, nodules, vesicles and/or bullae affecting preferentially the dorsal aspects of the hands and forearms. It may also involve oral and genital mucous membranes.
synonyms: EM - [erythema multiforme] | EM

name: Human herpes virus infections involving skin or mucous membrane
definition: Infection of the skin and mucous membranes by herpes viruses, particularly Herpes simplex virus and Varicella zoster virus.

name: Recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa centripetalis
definition: Centripetalis recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa is an extremely rare subtype of dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, characterised by blistering which begins acrally and then progressively spreads toward the trunk.
synonyms: RDEB - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa centripetalis] centripetalis | RDEB-Ce - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa centripetalis] | RDEB centripetalis

name: Papular amyloidosis
definition: Papular amyloidosis (lichen amyloidosis) presents as multiple discrete, firm, pruritic, skin-coloured or hyperpigmented papules which have a tendency to coalesce into plaques. These are located most commonly on the shins though the thighs and upper extremities may also be affected. The amyloid material is thought to originate from degenerate keratinocytes. Papular amyloidosis tends to be persistent and resistant to treatment.
synonyms: Lichen amyloidosis | lichen amyloidosus

name: Follicular digitate keratoses
definition: Conditions characterised by follicular and perifollicular hyperkeratosis.

name: Benign symmetrical lipomatosis
definition: Benign symmetrical lipomatosis is an uncommon condition characterised by progressive symmetrical accumulation during adult life of adipose mass at the level of the head, neck and upper trunk . The condition occurs principally in males between 30 and 60 years of age. It is often associated with excessive alcohol consumption. Insulin resistance has also been described. Mitochondrial DNA mutations have been identified in some cases, mainly when familial symmetrical lipomatosis occurs in the context
synonyms: Central non-encapsulated lipomatosis | Familial benign cervical lipomatosis | Launois-Bensaude adenolipomatosis | Madelung disease | launois-bensaude's lipomatosis | benign symmetric lipomatosis of launois-bensaude | madelung disease | cervical Symmetrical Lipomatosis | Launois-Bensaude's lipomatosis | LIPODYSTROPHY, CEPHALOTHORACIC

name: Dermatophytosis due to geophilic dermatophytes
definition: Infection by fungi which normally live in soil but which are capable of invading human skin.

name: Venous leg ulcer
definition: Venous leg ulcers are chronic skin ulcers of the gaiter area (ankle and lower leg) due to chronic peripheral venous hypertension. They are often associated with other manifestations of chronic peripheral venous insufficiency of the lower extremities including lower limb varicose veins and lipodermatosclerosis.
synonyms: Gravitational ulcer | Stasis ulcer | Varicose ulcer | leg ulcer NOS

name: Brittle nails
synonyms: Onychorrhexis | onychoclasis | fragile nails | lamellar exfoliation | onychoschizia

name: Hypohidrosis
definition: Abnormally diminished or absent perspiration. Both generalised and segmented (reduced or absent sweating in circumscribed locations) forms of the disease are usually associated with other underlying conditions.
synonyms: Anhidrosis | Impaired sweating | Oligohidrosis

name: Ataxia-telangiectasia
definition: Ataxia-Telangiectasia (A-T) is a DNA repair disorder defined by the association of severe combined immunodeficiency (affecting mainly the humoral immune response) with progressive cerebellar ataxia. It is characterised by neurological signs, telangiectasias, increased susceptibility to infections and increased risk of cancer.
synonyms: Louis-Bar syndrome | AT | louis-bar syndrome

name: Oesophageal varices without bleeding in hepatic fibrosis
definition: These are extremely dilated sub-mucosal veins in the lower third of the oesophagus without bleeding in chronic liver disease characterised by replacement of liver tissue by fibrosis, scar tissue and regenerative nodules (lumps that occur as a result of a process in which damaged tissue is regenerated), leading to loss of liver function.

name: Paronychial herpes simplex infection
definition: Herpes simplex infection affecting the nailfold (herpetic whitlow). This is typically acquired occupationally by health care and paramedical workers from people with active orofacial Herpes simplex infection.
synonyms: Herpes simplex infection of nail | Herpes simplex infection of paronychium | Herpetic whitlow | herpes simplex whitlow | herpesviral whitlow | herpetic felon

name: Chronic idiopathic telogen effluvium
definition: Chronic telogen effluvium is a primary, idiopathic condition affecting middle-aged women. It must be distinguished from androgenetic hair loss and chronic diffuse telogen effluvium secondary to organic causes. Women describe a sudden onset of increased hair shedding persisting for at least six months. It contrasts with acute telogen effluvium by its prolonged fluctuating course and much less frequent occurrence. It occurs mainly in females between 30 and 50 years of age. Although some cases foll

name: Ectropion of lip
definition: An eversion of the lip resulting from contractures involving adjacent perioral skin. The lower lip is much more commonly affected than the upper lip. Ectropion of the lower lip is a particular complication of severe facial burns. Ectropion may also result as a complication of surgery or cryotherapy involving perioral skin, e.g. for removal or destruction of skin cancers. Rarely it may result from genetic skin conditions, especially harlequin ichthyosis.
synonyms: eclabium

name: Dermatophytosis of toenail
definition: Dermatophyte (tinea) infection of toenail.
synonyms: Onychomycosis of toenail due to dermatophyte | Tinea of toenail | Tinea unguium of toes

name: Trichoepithelioma
definition: Trichoepithelioma is a benign tumour of hair follicle germ cell origin which typically presents in adults as a sporadic solitary cutaneous papule or nodule on the head or neck. Multiple trichoepitheliomata, typically appearing on the face, neck and upper trunk during adolescence, represent the commonest manifestation of the autosomal dominant hereditary hamartoneoplastic disorder, Brooke-Spiegler syndrome. Desmoplastic trichoepithelioma presents in young adults as a single slowly enlarging plaqu

name: X-linked dominant Fabry disease
definition: This is a rare X-linked lysosomal storage disease, which can cause a wide range of systemic symptoms. The inheritance is X-linked dominant.

name: Oesophageal varices with bleeding in hepatic fibrosis
definition: This refers to abnormally dilated veins in the lining of oesophagus with sign of bleeding in patients with hepatic fibrosis.

name: Plane warts
definition: Plane warts (flat warts) are clinically distinct from common warts and manifest as multiple small flat-topped, often lightly pigmented papules on the face or extremities. They are caused by human papillomavirus (HPV) subtypes 3 and 10.
synonyms: Flat warts | Verruca plana | flat warts | plane warts

name: Necrosis or ulceration due to extravasation of drug into the skin
definition: Skin ulceration or necrosis due to leakage of an irritant drug preparation, usually during administration by infusion through a cannula but also from direct intravenous injection. Anthracyclines, platinum compounds, vinca alkaloids and taxanes represent some of the more common agents responsible.
synonyms: Extravasation reaction

name: Atrophoderma vermiculatum
definition: Atrophoderma vermiculatum is a variant of keratoses pilaris atrophicans in which multiple keratotic follicular papules appear on the cheeks, upper lip, and forehead during mid childhood and then resolve with reticulate atrophic scarring resembling honeycomb. In contrast to other forms of keratoses pilaris atrophicans there is no involvement of the eyebrows, eyelashes or scalp. Progression stops after puberty.
synonyms: Folliculitis ulerythematosa reticulata | Honeycomb atrophy of face | Vermiculate atrophoderma of face | acne vermoulante | atrophoderma reticulata symmertica faciei | folliculitis ulerythema reticulata | folliculitis ulerythemosa | honeycomb atrophy | ulerythema acneiforme

name: Carney complex
definition: Carney complex is a dominantly inherited hamartoneoplastic syndrome caused bs caused by mutation in the protein kinase A regulatory subunit-1-alpha gene on chromosome 17q. It is characterised by spotty skin and mucosal pigmentation (lentiginosis and blue naevi); overactivity affecting multiple endocrine glands, the manifestations of which may include acromegaly, Cushing syndrome and tumours of the thyroid and testes; and myxomas in the heart, skin and breast.
synonyms: Carney syndrome | Myxoma - spotty pigmentation - endocrine overactivity

name: Full thickness skin graft failure
definition: Failure of a full skin thickness graft to engraft as intended

name: Pressure ulceration grade 2 of sacrum
synonyms: stage 2 decubitus ulcer of sacrum

name: Lichen simplex or lichenification
definition: If a circumscribed area of skin is subjected to repeated rubbing or scratching, localised epidermal thickening or lichenification will ensue. This may occur as a discrete patch within normal skin (lichen simplex) but frequently complicates eczema or other pruritic dermatoses.

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, cardiac valvular type
definition: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, cardiac valvular type is a form of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome characterised by joint hypermobility, skin hyperextensibility and cardiac valvular defects.

name: Acquired malformations of cutaneous blood vessels
synonyms: telangiectasis NOS

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, periodontitis type
definition: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, periodontitis type is a type of Ehlers-Danlos syndromes (EDS),a heterogeneous group of hereditary connective tissue diseases characterised by joint hyperlaxity, cutaneous hyperelasticity and tissue fragility, and is characterised by severe early-onset periodontal disease in conjunction with the presence of plaques of scar tissue in the pretibial zones (hyperpigmented atrophic scars).
synonyms: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type 8

name: Erosive lichen planus of vulva or vagina
definition: A severe, painful and recalcitrant form of lichen planus in which mucosal surfaces are eroded. At the edges of the erosions the epithelium is mauve and a pale network (Wickham's striae) is sometimes seen. As erosions heal synechiae and scarring can develop. Symptoms include pain, dysuria and dyspareunia.
synonyms: Mucosal lichen planus of vulva and vagina | Mucosal lichen planus of vulva or vagina

name: Benign acanthosis nigricans
definition: Benign acanthosis nigricans is a thickening and pigmentation affecting predominantly flexural skin, especially the neck, axillae and groins. It is thought to be due to high concentrations of insulin binding to insulin-like growth factor receptors, with resultant keratinocyte proliferation. It is strongly associated with insulin resistance and obesity. Type 2 diabetes and the metabolic syndrome are commonly associated.

name: Williams-Beuren syndrome
definition: Williams syndrome is a rare genetic disease characterised by a developmental disorder associating a cardiac malformation (most frequently supra valvular aortic stenosis, SVAS) in 75% of cases, psychomotor retardation, a characteristic facial dysmorphism and a specific cognitive and behavioural profile.
synonyms: 7q11.23 deletion | Monosomy 7q11.23 | Williams syndrome

name: PHACE syndrome
definition: PHACE is an acronym used to describe a rare syndrome characterised by the association of Posterior fossa brain malformations, large facial Haemangiomas, anatomical anomalies of the cerebral Arteries, aortic coarctation and other Cardiac anomalies, and Eye abnormalities.
synonyms: PHACE - [posterior fossa malformations – haemangiomas – arterial anomalies – cardiac defects – eye abnormalities – sternal cleft – supraumbilical raphe] syndrome

name: Burn of abdominal wall, epidermal burn
synonyms: Erythema of abdominal wall

name: Genetic disorders of porphyrin metabolism affecting the skin
Involvement of eyelid in tuberculosis
definition: This is a common, and in many cases lethal, infectious disease caused by various strains of mycobacteria, usually Mycobacterium tuberculosis. This diagnosis is with involvement of the eyelid.

name: Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to hairdressing products
Carcinoma in situ of anal margin or perianal skin
definition: Carcinoma in situ of anal margin or perianal skin is most commonly squamous and related to oncogenic HPV strains, HIV infection or both. It may present as warty pigmented patches (Bowenoid papulosis).
