Occupations where there is a substantial risk include agricultural workers, florists and gardeners.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema due to plants or other vegetable matter
cn_name: 植物或其他蔬菜物质引起的刺激性接触性皮炎

name: Eccrine poroma
definition: Eccrine poroma is a benign tumour arising from eccrine duct epithelium of sweat-bearing skin. It most commonly develops in palmar or plantar skin where it presents as a moist, exophytic pink or red nodule, often with a surrounding keratinous collar. It can, however, be located on any area of the skin containing sweat glands, in which situation the diagnosis can rarely be made clinically.

name: Adult atopic eczema, nummular pattern
definition: Atopic eczema in adults with a predominantly nummular or discoid pattern of skin involvement.
synonyms: Adult atopic dermatitis, nummular pattern | Discoid atopic eczema of the adult

name: Skin manifestation of other specified neonatal mineral deficiency
Metastatic melanoma involving skin
definition: Metastatic malignant melanoma of the skin presents most commonly as pigmented papules and nodules adjacent to a primary tumour (local metastasis) or in association with regional lymphatic spread. In advanced disease it may be found widely dispersed or in distant sites as a result of haematogenous dissemination from a known or unknown primary tumour. Rarely patients may present with a generalised diffuse melanosis.

name: Subcorneal pustular dermatosis
definition: Subcorneal pustular dermatosis is a rare, benign, chronic disease characterised by sterile pustular eruption, typically involving the flexural sites of the trunk and proximal extremities.
synonyms: Sneddon-Wilkinson disease | Sneddon-Wilkinson disease or syndrome

name: Familial Mediterranean fever
definition: FMF is an autoinflammatory disease associated with mutations in pyrin resulting in enhanced IL1 beta production. This results in clinical attacks of inflammation in the form of fever and serositis in the form of peritoneal, pleural or synovial inflammation along with increased acute phase reactants.
synonyms: Armenian disease | FMF - [familial mediterranean fever] | Hereditary amyloid nephropathy | Periodic disease | Riemann periodic disease | Siegal Cattan Mamou disease | familial non-neuropathic amyloidosis | familial recurrent polyserositis | paroxysmal polyserositis | periodic familial peritonitis

name: Oral mucositis
definition: Ulcerative mucositis is the inflammation of the oral mucosa with inflammation, ulceration and frequently with bleeding.
synonyms: Oral ulcerative mucositis | oral mucositis NOS | oropharyngeal mucositis NOS

name: Temporary cutaneous sensory disturbance resulting from cutaneous surgical procedure
definition: Temporary anaesthesia, hypoaesthesia or dysaesthesia resulting from injury to cutaneous sensory nerves from skin surgery

name: Benign neoplasms of epidermal appendages
definition: A range of benign neoplasms arising from the hair follicle, its associated glands or from sweat glands.
synonyms: tumour of skin appendage

name: Plaque psoriasis with pustules
definition: The development of pustulation around existing plaques of psoriasis, often provoked by irritation from topical therapies such as tar or anthralin (dithranol) applied to unstable psoriasis. It may progress to acute generalised pustular psoriasis.
synonyms: Psoriasis cum pustulatione

name: Uraemic pruritus
definition: Pruritus in patients with chronic renal failure. Although common in untreated chronic kidney disease, it is particularly prevalent in patients receiving peritoneal or haemo-dialysis. The itch is not due to elevated serum urea levels. The precise mechanisms are not fully understood.
synonyms: Nephrogenic pruritus | Pruritus due to chronic kidney disease | Renal pruritus

name: Superficial haematoma in fetus or newborn
synonyms: superficial haematomata in fetus or newborn

name: Papillomatoverrucous palmoplantar keratoderma – dysplastic dentition
synonyms: Jakac-Wolf keratoderma

name: Frostbite of hip with tissue necrosis
Odonto-tricho-ungual-digito-palmar syndrome
definition: Odonto-tricho-ungual-digito-palmar syndrome is characterised by neonatal teeth, trichodystrophy and malformations of the hands and feet.

name: Exacerbation of skin condition classified elsewhere during pregnancy
definition: The situation in which a preexisting inflammatory skin disease such as atopic eczema or psoriasis worsens during pregnancy rather than improves, as is more usually the case.

name: Organoid naevus
synonyms: Naevus sebaceus of Jadassohn | Sebaceous naevus

name: Zunich-Kaye syndrome
definition: Zunich-Kaye syndrome is characterised by early-onset migratory ichthyosiform dermatosis, bilateral ocular coloboma, conductive hearing loss, seizures, intellectual deficit, and characteristic facial features\: brachycephaly, mild upslanting of the palpebral fissures, pale blue irides, hypertelorism, flat midface and philtrum, anteverted nostrils, thin upper lip and excessive creases around a wide mouth. The acronym CHIME was proposed for the Zunich-Kaye syndrome\: Colobomas of the eye, Heart def

name: Infantile acropustulosis
definition: Infantile acropustulosis is an uncommon pustular dermatosis of unknown aetiology. It occurs in infants of all ethnicities, though it was first described in black infants. It is characterised by recurrent crops of intensely itchy vesicles and pustules located principally on the soles and sides of the feet, and on the palms.

name: Systemic sclerosis with myopathy
synonyms: Myopathy in scleroderma

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, tarsus
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, tarsus

name: Localised excess and redundant skin
definition: Excess and redundant skin limited to a localised area such as the breasts and typically occurring following significant weight loss.

name: Malignant neoplasm of body of penis
synonyms: cancer of body of penis | malignant neoplasm of corpus cavernosum | malignant tumour of body of penis | malignant tumour of shaft of penis | primary malignant neoplasm of body of penis

name: Oedema due to increased peripheral blood flow
definition: Oedema resulting from increased capillary blood flow as may occur as a physiological response to an increase in body core temperature from heat exposure or exercise or as a result of arteriovenous malformations or shunts.
synonyms: Oedema secondary to increased peripheral blood flow

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, forearm
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, forearm

name: N-acetylglutamate synthase deficiency
synonyms: NAGS - [N-acetylglutamate synthase deficiency]

name: Universal vitiligo
definition: Vitiligo involving more than 80% of the body surface.

name: Burn of abdominal wall, full thickness burn
Burn of face except eye or ocular adnexa, epidermal burn
synonyms: Burn of face except eye and adnexa, first degree
cn_name: 面部（除外眼或眼附属器）表皮烧伤

name: Anogenital herpes simplex infection
definition: A condition of the anogenital region, caused by an infection with herpes simplex virus type 1 or 2. This condition is characterised by vesicles, or may be asymptomatic. Transmission is by sexual contact. Confirmation is by identification of herpes simplex virus type 1 or 2.
synonyms: anogenital HSV - [herpes simplex virus] infection | anogenital herpes | anogenital herpes simplex virus infection | anogenital herpesviral infection, unspecified
cn_name: 肛门-生殖器单纯疱疹病毒感染

name: Disseminated infantile seborrhoeic dermatitis
definition: A widespread form of infantile seborrhoeic dermatitis affecting the napkin area, scalp, face, neck, axillae and anterior trunk. In contrast to atopic eczema, pruritus is not usually evident and the infant remains otherwise well.
synonyms: generalised seborrhoeic eczema of infancy
cn_name: 播散性婴儿脂溢性皮炎

name: Disseminated secondary eczema
definition: The development of eczematous inflammation at sites distant from the primary site of an eczema or dermatitis. The onset of such dissemination may occur days or weeks after the primary dermatitis. The distribution of the secondary eczema tends to be symmetrical. Venous eczema, allergic contact dermatitis, acute irritant contact dermatitis and infective dermatitis are all potential triggers. The precise aetiopathology is as yet poorly understood.
synonyms: Autosensitization dermatitis | Cutaneous autosensitization | Eczematid | autoeczematisation | autosensitisation dermatitis | cutaneous autosensitisation | id reaction | sensitization dermatitis | generalised eczema

name: Perianal dermatitis of the newborn
definition: Perianal dermatitis of the newborn presents with perianal erythema during the first week of life, which in more severe forms may progress to oedema and superficial erosion of perianal skin. Although it usually occurs alone, perianal dermatitis may sometimes be associated with primary irritant napkin dermatitis. It is commoner in infants receiving cow's milk formulations than in breast-fed infants; it is assumed that it represents an irritant response to faecal constituents.

name: Lichen planus of the nails
definition: Lichen planus of the nail most commonly presents as nail plate thinning with longitudinal grooving and ridging. Hyperpigmentation, subungual hyperkeratosis, onycholysis, and longitudinal melanonychia can also occur. Rarely, the matrix can be permanently destroyed with prominent pterygium formation. Lichen planus has been linked to childhood idiopathic nail atrophy and may overlap with twenty-nail dystrophy of childhood.

name: Erythema induratum
definition: Erythema induratum (nodular vasculitis) is a chronic, recurrent reactive disorder characterised by subcutaneous nodules located preferentially on the posterior aspects of the lower legs of adult women. The nodules commonly develop after exposure to cold and may break down to form irregular ulcers. It is a reactive process which may be provoked by a number of different mechanisms but is most commonly associated with active tuberculosis (erythema induratum of Bazin).
synonyms: Nodular vasculitis | nodular vasculitis

name: Peutz-Jeghers syndrome
definition: Peutz-Jeghers syndrome (PJS) is an autosomal dominant inherited disorder characterised by intestinal hamartomatous polyps in association with a distinct pattern of skin and mucosal macular melanin deposition. Patients have an increased risk of developing intestinal cancer.

name: Childhood atopic eczema, flexural
definition: Childhood atopic eczema affecting predominantly the flexures including neck and limb flexures.
synonyms: Childhood atopic dermatitis, flexural | Flexural atopic eczema of the child

name: Corrosion of third degree of shoulder and arm, except wrist or hand
synonyms: corrosion of third degree of shoulder and upper limb, except wrist and hand

name: Acute radiodermatitis, not elsewhere classified
definition: Acute skin injury resulting from accidental or intentional non-therapeutic exposure of skin to ionizing radiation.

name: Familial lecithin-cholesterol acyltransferase deficiency
definition: Familial LCAT (lecithin-cholesterol acyltransferase) deficiency (FLD) is a form of lecithin-cholesterol acyltransferase deficiency (LCAT) characterised clinically by corneal opacities, haemolytic anaemia, and renal failure, and biochemically by severely decreased HDL cholesterol and complete deficiency of the LCAT enzyme.
synonyms: Complete LCAT - [lecithin-cholesterol acyltransferase] deficiency | Complete lecithin-cholesterol acyltransferase deficiency | Familial LCAT - [lecithin-cholesterol acyltransferase] deficiency | phosphatidylcholine-sterol acyltransferase deficiency

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome with periventricular heterotopia
definition: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS) with periventricular heterotopia is a variant of EDS with features consistent with EDS, including joint hypermobility, skin fragility and aortic dilatation and periventricular heterotopia (PH), which is characterised by focal epilepsy usually beginning in the second decade of life. Intelligence is generally normal.

name: Sepsis due to Erysipelothrix infection with septic shock
Merkel cell carcinoma of skin
definition: A rare malignant cutaneous tumour seen in elderly patients. Its usual location is on the head, neck and extremities. The tumour is composed of small round cells with scanty cytoplasm arranged in a trabecular pattern, or in ill-defined nodules or in a diffuse pattern. The tumour cells contain cytoplasmic membrane-bound dense core granules resembling neurosecretory granules. There is strong evidence implicating Merkel cell polyoma virus in a majority of cases of Merkel cell carcinoma.
synonyms: Merkel cell cancer | Merkel cell carcinoma of unspecified site | Neuroendocrine carcinoma of the skin

name: Pigmented seborrhoeic keratosis
definition: Pigmented seborrhoeic keratoses are in every way similar to usual seborrhoeic keratoses, but in addition demonstrate pronounced epidermal melanin pigment. They may be mistaken clinically for melanoma.

name: Alopecia due to deficient nutrition
definition: Chronic diffuse telogen hair loss attributable to deficient nutrition such as in anorexia nervosa.
synonyms: Alopecia secondary to deficient nutrition

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, carpus
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, carpus

name: Eczematous cheilitis
definition: Unspecified eczematous inflammation of the lips
synonyms: eczema of lips | lip eczema

name: Burn of hip or leg, except ankle or foot, deep full thickness or complex burn
synonyms: Burn of hip and leg, except ankle or foot, fourth degree
cn_name: 臀或腿（除外踝或足）深全层皮肤烧伤或复合烧伤

name: Non-melanin pigmentation due to topical medicament
definition: Pigmentation of the skin due to topically applied therapeutic agents such as anthralin and potassium permanganate.

name: Dermatophytosis of fingernail
definition: Dermatophyte (tinea) infection of fingernail.
synonyms: Onychomycosis of fingernail due to dermatophyte | Tinea of fingernail | Tinea unguium of fingers

name: Superficial thrombophlebitis
synonyms: Phlebitis NOS

name: Heterotopic sebaceous glands of lips
synonyms: Fordyce spots of lips

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy Type IA
definition: Hereditary sensory autonomic neuropathy type I is an autosomal dominant (mutation in the SPTLC1 gene that encodes for subunit 1 of serine palmitoyltransferase) sensory radiculo neuropathy that presents in the second decade. It present with distal pain and nociceptive and thermal perception deficits, later to be followed by anhidrosis, trophic ulcers, acral injuries, stress fractures and osteomyelitis.
synonyms: HSAN1A - [Hereditary sensory autonomic neuropathy Type IA

name: Syphilitic craniotabes
synonyms: syphilis craniotabes

name: Mpox
definition: A disease caused by an infection with monkeypox virus. In the first phase, this disease is characterised by lymphadenopathy, fever, headache, or malaise; in the second phase, this disease is characterised by a rash that starts as maculopapules and progresses to vesicles, then pustules, followed by crusts (may occur on the face, palms of the hands, soles of the feet, body, and mucous membranes). Transmission is by direct contact with infected animals (including body fluids or lesions), direct con
synonyms: MPX (deprecated) | monkeypox | monkeypox disease

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to photographic chemicals
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to photographic chemicals (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to photographic chemicals).

name: X-linked ichthyosis
definition: X-linked ichthyosis is an X-linked recessive genodermatosis associated with steroid sulfatase deficiency and elevated plasma cholesterol sulfate. Generalised scaling is present at or shortly after birth, most prominently over the extremities, neck, trunk, and buttocks. It occurs only in males and may be associated with testicular disease and corneal opacities.
synonyms: Sex-linked ichthyosis | X-linked ichthyosis with steryl-sulfatase deficiency | X-linked recessive ichthyosis | steroid sulfatase deficiency | X-linked ichthyosis with steryl-sulphatase deficiency | X-linked placental steryl-sulphatase deficiency
snomed: 402771003 "x-linked recessive ichthyosis";205552006 "x-linked recessive ichthyosis";254160003 "x-linked recessive ichthyosis";3944006 "x-linked recessive ichthyosis";72523005

name: Late congenital syphilis, latent
cn_name: 晚期先天性梅毒，潜伏性

name: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum ferrugineum
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum ferrugineum, which typically causes an ectothrix infection of scalp hair.

name: Harlequin ichthyosis
definition: Harlequin ichthyosis (HI) is the most severe variant of autosomal recessive congenital ichthyosis (ARCI; ). It is characterised at birth by the presence of large, thick, plate-like scales over the whole body associated with severe ectropion, eclabium, and flattened ears, that later develops into a severe scaling erythroderma.
synonyms: Harlequin colour change syndrome | harlequin baby | harlequin fetus | ichthyosis congenita gravior | ARCI4B | harlequin ichthyosis | harlequin type ichthyosis congenita | harlequin type ichthyosis fetalis

name: Granulosis rubra nasi
definition: Granulosis rubra nasi is an uncommon disorder characterised by a marked focal increase in sweat production on the nose and central face. It usually starts in early childhood with peak incidence from age 7-12 years before resolving spontaneously at puberty. Occasionally, however, it persists indefinitely, in which case telangiectasia affecting the nose becomes the predominant feature.

name: Hypoglossia or aglossia
definition: Isolated aglossia and hypoglossia are terms covering the spectrum from partial to total absence of the tongue. These congenital malformations have been classified as part of the group of oromandibular-limb hypogenesis syndromes (OLHS).

name: Sunburn erythema
synonyms: Erythema solare | Solar erythema | first degree sunburn

name: Impairment of normal functioning resulting from skin disease
definition: A category to enable documentation of functional impairment caused by skin disease (cf. Visual impairment including blindness).

name: Hyperkeratotic dermatitis of hands or feet
definition: Form of eczema (dermatitis) characterised by highly irritable, scaly, fissured, hyperkeratotic patches on the palms and/or soles. The aetiology is unknown. This disorder takes a chronic course and may be extremely refractory to treatment.
synonyms: Hyperkeratotic eczema of hands and feet

name: Allergic contact urticaria, disseminated
definition: Widespread urticaria resulting from skin contact with allergen. Angioedema and anaphylaxis and may occur.

name: Acquired idiopathic generalised anhidrosis
definition: Chronic idiopathic anhidrosis is a disorder characterised by total body anhidrosis, episodes of flushing and overheating associated with weakness, in which skin biopsies may show peri-eccrine infiltration with small lymphocytes.
synonyms: AIGA - [acquired idiopathic generalised anhidrosis] | Chronic idiopathic anhidrosis

name: Lymphatic malformation of axilla
synonyms: Lymphangioma of axilla

name: Friction blister
definition: A blister due to disruption of epidermal integrity as a result of repeated frictional stress. They usually form in areas with a strong, thick epidermis, i.e. the palms, fingers, soles and sides of the feet and toes. Common causes include repetitive heavy manual tasks or ill-fitting footwear.

name: Miscellaneous specified disorders of lips or oral mucosa
cn_name: 唇或口腔黏膜其他特指的疾患

name: Adverse reaction to substance applied to neonatal skin
Sporadic camptodactyly
definition: Camptodactyly without demonstrable familial inheritance.

name: Neonatal purpura fulminans
synonyms: congenital severe protein C or S deficiency

name: Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome
definition: Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome is a rare hereditary immune deficiency with recessive inheritance linked to the X chromosome (Xp11.22-p11.23), characterised by the association of thrombocytopenia with small-sized platelets, eczema and repeated infections. The deficiency occurs early in childhood, during the first decade and usually before the age of 3 years.
synonyms: Aldrich syndrome | Eczema-thrombocytopaenia-immunodeficiency syndrome | Immunodeficiency with thrombocytopenia and eczema

name: Lichen scrofulosorum
definition: Lichen scrofulosorum is characterised by clusters of tiny pink or yellow-brown lichenoid papules surmounted by fine scale. It is located mainly on the trunk. It most commonly occurs in children with nodal or bone tuberculosis

name: Tactile hallucinations
definition: Hallucinations involving the perception of being touched (e.g., feeling like bugs are crawling on the skin, pins being stuck into one's finger) that are not restricted to the period of awakening or the onset of sleep.

name: Disseminated cutaneous herpes simplex infection complicating other skin diseases
cn_name: 播散性皮肤单纯疱疹感染合并皮肤病

name: Acute irritant contact dermatitis of hands
definition: Acute irritant dermatitis of the skin of the hands due to exposure to potent irritants such as acids and alkalis. This is frequently due to occupational exposure to irritants.
synonyms: Acute irritant hand dermatitis

name: Dermatoses due to arterial or arteriolar disease
Dystrophic calcification of the skin of uncertain or unspecified aetiology
definition: Abnormal deposition of calcium in the skin and subcutaneous tissues of unknown (idiopathic) or unspecified cause.
synonyms: Calcinosis cutis of uncertain or unspecified aetiology
cn_name: 不确定或未特指病因的皮肤营养不良性钙化

name: Hidradenitis suppurativa of anogenital region
definition: Hidradenitis suppurativa commonly affects the buttocks, perianal skin, inguinocrural folds and, in women, the vulva. Gross anatomical distortion may result from repeated episodes of acute inflammation and scarring with sinus tracts and lymphoedema.
synonyms: Acne inversa of anogenital region

name: Pustular miliaria
definition: Pustular miliaria (miliaria pustulosa) develops from miliaria rubra with the appearance of multiple small pustules on the skin. Although it has been postulated that extracellular polysaccharide produced by Staphylococcus epidermidis may be important in the pathogenesis of miliaria rubra, pustular miliaria is usually sterile, though may be associated with secondary Staphylococcus aureus infection.
synonyms: Miliaria pustulosa

name: Pachydermodactyly
definition: Pachydermodactyly is characterised by epidermal and dermal thickening of the skin of the lateral aspects of the middle three digits of the hands. It is felt to be due to repetitive rubbing of the affected fingers of each hand against the fingers of the other hand and as such may be regarded as a form of obsessive-compulsive behaviour.

name: Onchocercoma
definition: A condition of the skin, caused by an infection with the parasitic worm Onchocerca volvulus. This condition is characterised by subcutaneous nodules containing the parasitic worm (commonly affecting the iliac crests, ribs, knees, or trochanters). Transmission is through the bite of an infected Simulium fly. Confirmation is by identification of Onchocerca volvulus in a skin sample.

name: Systemic lupus erythematosus with skin involvement
definition: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) involving the skin. This may present with a malar \"butterfly\" erythema or with extensive necrolysis of sun-exposed skin, particularly on the head, neck and upper torso.
cn_name: 系统性红斑狼疮累及皮肤

name: Familial hypoalphalipoproteinaemia
synonyms: familial hypolipoproteinaemia | familial lipoprotein deficiency

name: Viral exanthems
definition: Acute viral infections characterised by the appearance of a skin rash.

name: Non-melanin pigmentation due to drug
definition: Discolouration of the skin due to deposition of pigments from drugs or their metabolites, usually after long-term use. Well-known causes include minocycline, mepacrine, clofazimine and amiodarone.

name: Major recurrent aphthous stomatitis
definition: This is a major recurrent type of mouth ulcer that presents as a painful open sore inside the mouth or upper throat characterised by a break in the mucous membrane.
synonyms: major aphthous stomatitis

name: Dementia due to neurosyphilis
definition: Dementia due to neurosyphilis, a disease of the central nervous system caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum, with brain involvement, and supportive clinical, imaging and laboratory features of the disease without another identifiable cause of cognitive dysfunction. There may be associated psychiatric symptoms. This type of dementia is considered to be treatable and, therefore, reversible (at least partially).
synonyms: syphilitic dementia paralytica

name: Mucocutaneous or cutaneous pain syndromes
definition: A range of chronic focal pain disorders affecting skin or mucosal sites, with a predilection for the orocervical and urogenital regions. They are diagnoses of exclusion and should be made only when no other explanation for the symptoms can be found. They are frequently associated with severe psychological distress.
cn_name: 皮肤黏膜或皮肤疼痛综合征

name: Palmoplantar keratoderma with scleroatrophy of the extremities
definition: A rare autosomal dominant disorder characterised by atrophy and fibrosis of the skin of the limbs, nail hypoplasia and palmoplantar keratoderma. Aggressive squamous cell carcinoma of affected skin occurs in about 1 in 6 affected individuals, mostly in the third to fourth decade, with early metastasis formation. There is also an increased risk of bowel cancer.
synonyms: Huriez palmoplantar keratoderma | Palmoplantar keratoderma-sclerodactyly syndrome | Sclerotylosis | HRZ | palmoplantar keratoderma with scleroatrophy | sclerotylosis

name: Primary cutaneous gamma/delta T-cell lymphoma
definition: An aggressive cutaneous lymphoma of mature, activated gamma/delta T-lymphocytes. It usually presents with disseminated plaques and nodules. Involvement of mucosal sites is frequent. However, involvement of lymph nodes, spleen, or bone marrow is uncommon. Morphologically, there are three patterns of cutaneous involvement\: epidermotropic, dermal, and subcutaneous. Often, more than one pattern may co-exist in a single biopsy specimen, or may be present in different biopsy specimens from the same p
synonyms: PCGD-TCL - [primary cutaneous gamma/delta T-cell lymphoma] | PCGD-TCL

name: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 17-alpha-hydroxylase deficiency
definition: This refers to any of several autosomal recessive diseases resulting from mutations of genes for enzymes mediating the biochemical steps of production of cortisol from cholesterol by the adrenal glands (steroidogenesis). This diagnosis is due to 17-alpha-hydroxylase deficiency.

name: Borderline tuberculoid leprosy
definition: Borderline tuberculoid (BT) leprosy is similar to polar (TT) tuberculoid leprosy but lesions are smaller and more numerous and nerves are less enlarged. Nerve damage is common \[Dermatology TAG\].
synonyms: BT - [borderline tuberculoid] leprosy

name: Trichomycosis pubis
definition: A corynebacterial infection of pubic hair seen predominantly in males and manifesting as yellow or red nodules attached to hairs around the scrotum and base of the penis. There may be associated odour and discolouration of sweat.

name: Photo-allergic contact dermatitis due to medicaments other than NSAIDs
definition: Photo-allergic contact dermatitis due to exposure to systemic medicaments other than NSAIDs. Well recognised photo-allergens include chlorpromazine, promethazine, quinidine and sulfanilamide.
synonyms: Photo-allergic contact eczema due to systemic medicaments

name: Porokeratoses
definition: Porokeratoses result from a clonal disorder of keratinization. They are characterised by one or more atrophic patches surrounded by a clinically and histologically distinctive ridged hyperkeratotic border (cornoid lamella). Multiple clinical variants of porokeratosis are recognised.

name: Polycystic ovary syndrome
definition: Condition defined by the presence of at least 2 of the following 3 criteria\: oligo/anovulation; clinical or biochemical signs of hyperandrogenism; presence of polycystic ovaries as identified by ultrasound.
synonyms: PCOS - [polycystic ovary syndrome] | Sclerocystic ovary syndrome | Stein-Leventhal syndrome | PCOS

name: Giant cell arteritis
definition: Arteritis, often granulomatous, usually affecting the aorta and/or its major branches, with a predilection for the branches of the carotid artery. Often involves the temporal artery. Onset usually in patients older than 50 and often associated with polymyalgia rheumatica.
synonyms: GCA - [giant cell arteritis] | Horton disease | cranial arteritis | temporal arteritis

name: Cutaneous reactions to Porifera
definition: Adverse reactions to contact with sponges.

name: Perianal threadworm dermatosis
definition: Perianal irritation and inflammation resulting from infestation of the lower digestive tract by the parasitic roundworm Enterobius vermicularis, also known as threadworm or pinworm. The female worms leave the rectum at night to deposit their eggs on perianal skin. Vaginal irritation may occur if the worms migrate to the vagina instead of returning to the rectum. Transmission is by ingestion of eggs, which are readily transferred to the hands and mouth.
synonyms: Pruritus ani due to threadworm or pinworm infestation

name: Acne agminata
synonyms: FIGURE - [facial idiopathic granulomas with regressive evolution] | Facial idiopathic granulomas with regressive evolution | Lupus miliaris disseminatus facei

name: Viral warts due to acquired immunodeficiency
definition: Abnormal proliferation of human papillomavirus due to immunodeficiency arising from causes such as infection (e.g. HIV), malnutrition or disease (e.g. lymphoma).

name: Neonatal intrahepatic cholestasis caused by citrin deficiency
definition: Neonatal intrahepatic cholestasis due to citrin deficiency is a mild subtype of citrin deficiency characterised clinically by low birth weight, failure to thrive, growth retardation, transient intrahepatic cholestasis, multiple aminoacidemia, galactosemia, hypoproteinemia, hepatomegaly, decreased coagulation factors, haemolytic anaemia, variable but mostly mild liver dysfunction, and hypoglycaemia.
synonyms: NICCD - [Neonatal intrahepatic cholestasis caused by citrin deficiency]

name: Primary syphilis of lip
synonyms: primary syphilitic lip

name: Intermediate-sized congenital melanocytic naevus
definition: A congenital melanocytic naevus (CMN) with a predicted or final adult maximal diameter of between 15 and 399 mm. The category of intermediate-sized CNM incorporates both medium (15-199 mm) and large 200-399 mm CMNs. They may be associated with multiple smaller satellite naevi (congenital or tardive), hypertrichosis, lipomas or benign proliferative nodules and they carry an increased risk of pre-pubertal cutaneous or central nervous system (CNS) melanoma. Leptomeningeal melanocytosis or focal neu

name: Glomangioma of skin
synonyms: Cutaneous Glomangioma | Glomangioma of skin

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome
definition: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS) is a heterogeneous group of inherited disorders of connective tissue, principally collagen, that range in severity from mild joint hypermobility to life-threatening fragility of soft tissue and vasculature.
synonyms: EDS - [Ehlers-Danlos syndrome] | Ehlers-Danlos syndrome NOS | cutis hyperelastica | EDS

name: Body dysmorphic disorder with fair to good insight
definition: All definitional requirements of body dysmorphic disorder are met. Much of the time, the individual is able to entertain the possibility that his or her disorder-specific beliefs may not be true and is willing to accept an alternative explanation for his or her experience. At circumscribed times (e.g., when highly anxious), the individual may demonstrate no insight.

name: Papulo-erosive napkin dermatitis
definition: A severe form of primary irritant napkin dermatitis characterised by multiple small papules and erosions which develop into shallow ulcers with raised crater-like edges.
synonyms: Jacquet dermatitis

name: Benign tumours of cutaneous smooth muscle
definition: Benign neoplasms derived from dermal smooth muscle. They present as dermal papules, nodules or plaques. The most common form is the pilar leiomyoma which arises from arrector pili muscle, located adjacent to hair follicles. Pilar leiomyomata normally present in adult life as solitary or multiple dermal papules (pilar leiomyomata); when multiple they are often familial and are characteristically painful. Cutaneous angioleiomyomata arise from smooth muscle within the walls of arteries and veins. G

name: Complete trisomy 13, translocation
synonyms: partial trisomy 13 in patau syndrome | translocation trisomy 13 | translocation trisomy 13 chromosome | translocation trisomy 13 syndrome

name: Lues maligna
definition: A fulminant nodulo-ulcerative form of secondary syphilis which is more prevalent in individuals with HIV infection.

name: Tubero-eruptive xanthoma
definition: Xanthoma showing features of both tuberous and eruptive xanthoma, with a central tuberous xanthoma surrounded by several smaller eruptive xanthomata. As with tuberous xanthomata they are usually indicative of hyperlipidaemia type 3 (Type III hyperlipoproteinaemia).

name: Genetically-determined epidermolysis bullosa
definition: Epidermolysis bullosa (EB) is the name given to a heterogeneous group of blistering disorders which in the majority of cases are due to genetically-determined defects in structural proteins of the epidermis and dermo-epidermal junction. The genetic forms are to be distinguished from the immunobullous disorder, epidermolysis bullosa acquisita (qv).
cn_name: 遗传性大疱性表皮松解症

name: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton equinum
definition: This is a clinical condition caused by fungal infection of the skin in humans. The term \"ringworm\" is a misnomer, since the condition is caused by fungi of several different species and not by parasitic worms. This diagnosis is due to the fungus genus trichophyton equinum.

name: Superficial thrombophlebitis of lower limbs
definition: Inflammation and thrombosis of the superficial veins of the lower limbs affecting particularly varicose superficial leg veins.
synonyms: Phlebitis of superficial veins of lower extremity | Superficial macrothrombusi's of lower extremity | Thrombophlebitis of superficial veins of lower extremity

name: Pilar leiomyoma
definition: Pilar leiomyoma is a benign cutaneous tumour derived from the arrector pili muscle of the pilosebaceous follicle. Solitary lesions may develop anywhere on hair-bearing skin, particularly the trunk and limbs. Multiple lesions have a predilection for the face, back and extensor surfaces of the extremities and are often painful. Multiple pilar leiomyomata may be familial with autosomal dominant inheritance and associated with uterine leiomyomata in women, and, in some pedigrees, renal carcinoma (HL
synonyms: Cutaneous leiomyoma | Piloleiomyoma

name: Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to rubber chemicals
Hypohidrosis due to acquired neurological injury or disorder
definition: Hypohidrosis due to a neurological disorder or injury at any level from the brain, especially organic lesions of hypothalamus, pons and medulla, to the peripheral nerves. Syringomyelia is a well-known cause.
synonyms: Hypohidrosis secondary to acquired neurological injury or disorder

name: Developmental capillary vascular malformations of the skin
cn_name: 皮肤毛细血管发育畸形

name: Cherry angioma
synonyms: Campbell de Morgan angioma | Campbell de Morgan spot | Senile angioma | de Morgan's spots | campbell-de morgan spot | cherry hemangioma | senile angioma | Senile hemangioma | Senile naevus of skin

name: Persistent melanocytic naevus
definition: The remnants of a melanocytic naevus which has been incompletely excised, usually by shave excision, and which histologically can be difficult to distinguish from melanoma.
synonyms: Pseudomelanoma | Recurrent melanocytic naevus

name: Diffuse non-epidermolytic palmoplantar keratoderma
definition: Diffuse non-epidermolytic palmoplantar keratoderma (MIM #600962 ) is an autosomal dominant disorder caused by mutations in the keratin 1 gene. It presents in early childhood even, very thick, yellow hyperkeratosis over the whole of the foot and spreading later to the palms. There is a sharp cut-off at the wrist and no tendency to spread to extensor surfaces. Hyperhidrosis is usual and dermatophyte infection is common.
synonyms: Tylosis | Unna palmoplantar keratoderma | diffuse nonepidermolytic palmomplantar keratoderma | NEPPK | Thost-Unna Syndrome | tylosis | Unna-Thost Syndrome

name: Necrobiotic granulomatous skin disorders
cn_name: 渐进性坏死性肉芽肿性疾病

name: Non-melanin pigmentation due to substance encountered at work
definition: Pigmentation of the skin from exposure to chemicals such as picric acid, dinitrophenol and chemical dyes.

name: Tessier number 6 facial cleft
definition: Cleft extending from the zygomatic arch to the eye

name: Glomus tumour of the skin or nail apparatus
definition: A benign tumour of the myoarterial glomus, composed of vascular channels surrounded by proliferating glomus cells. The tumours have variable quantities of glomus cells, blood vessels and smooth muscle. According to the relative proportions of quantities of glomus cells, blood vessels and smooth muscle they may be classified as solid glomus tumour, glomangioma or glomangiomyoma. They present as painful pink nodules and are often located on the fingers or under the nail plate.

name: Traumatic scar of skin
definition: Cutaneous scar resulting from traumatic injury.

name: Epidermolysis bullosa acquisita, Brunsting-Perry type
definition: The Brunsting-Perry variant of epidermolysis bullosa acquisita is a rare mechanobullous autoimmune skin disease characterised by blistering and scarring of facial or scalp skin and the presence of IgG autoantibodies to the basement membrane anchoring fibril, collagen VII. This distinguishes it from the clinically similar Brunsting-Perry variant of cictricial pemphigoid in which this antibody is not found.

name: Fibroepithelial basal cell carcinoma of skin
definition: This tumour usually appears as an elevated flesh-coloured or erythematous nodule that may resemble a seborrhoeic keratosis or acrochordon. It is most often found on the back and is rarely multiple. Prior radiotherapy may be a predisposing factor. The histopathology is characterised by an arborising network of cords of basaloid cells that extend downwards from the epidermis and create a fenestrating pattern.
synonyms: Fibroepithelioma of Pinkus | fibroepithelioma of pinkus | Fibroepithelioma of Pinkus type | Pinkus tumor

name: Lucio phenomenon
definition: A type II leprosy reaction occurring exclusively in patients with Lucio type diffuse lepromatous leprosy and manifesting as numerous areas of cutaneous infarction. Histologically there is an intense necrotizing vasculitis of small capillary vessels and medium to large arteries in the dermis and subcutis.

name: Pruritus due to myeloproliferative or lymphoproliferative disorder
definition: Pruritus due to abnormal or neoplastic proliferation of bone marrow-derived cells. Important causes are polycythaemia vera and Hodgkin lymphoma.

name: Oculotrichodysplasia
definition: Oculo-tricho-dysplasia is characterised by retinitis pigmentosa, trichodysplasia, dental anomalies, and onychodysplasia.

name: Drug-induced hair colour change
definition: A variety of drugs are associated with changes in hair colour; in particular, premature greying of the hair may be caused by tyrosine kinase receptor inhibitors which interfere with melanin synthesis, and chloroquine may result in hair colour change by interfering with phaeomelanin synthesis.
synonyms: Hair colour change attributable to drug | medicament-induced hair colour change

name: Pustular vasculitic reactions
definition: A clinical reaction pattern characterised clinically by an eruption of multiple small superficial non-follicular sterile pustules, each typically with an erythematous halo surrounding it, and histologically by a vasculitic reaction pattern. It is seen typically as a feature of Behçet disease and as a consequence of exposure to bacterial antigens as in chronic meningococcaemia and gonococcaemia and in the bowel-associated dermatosis-arthritis syndrome.

name: Ichthyosis - alopecia - eclabion - ectropion - intellectual deficit
Recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa pruriginosa
definition: This recessively inherited dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa (RDEB-pr) is characterised by intense pruritus, especially of pretibial skin.
synonyms: RDEB - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa] pruriginosa | RDEB-Pr - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa pruriginosa]

name: Pressure ulceration grade 1 of iliac crest
synonyms: stage 1 decubitus ulcer of iliac crest

name: Postherpetic neuralgia
definition: Postherpetic neuralgia is defined as pain persisting for ≥ 3 months following the onset or healing of herpes zoster. The innervation territory of the first (ophthalmic) branch of the trigeminal nerve and thoracic dermatomes are the locations most frequently affected by chronic pain after herpes zoster. Postherpetic neuralgia may emerge in continuation of the acute pain associated with the skin rash or develop after a painless interval. Spontaneous pain may be continuous or manifest in intermitte

name: Pressure ulceration grade 3 of lower back
Haemodialysis-associated pruritus
definition: Generalised pruritus attributable to haemodialysis rather than to chronic kidney disease. The causes are not well understood.
cn_name: 血液透析相关性瘙痒症

name: Trichiasis without entropion
definition: This refers to abnormally positioned eyelashes that grow back toward the eye, touching the cornea or conjunctiva. This can be caused by infection, inflammation, autoimmune conditions, congenital defects, eyelid agenesis and trauma such as burns or eyelid injury. This diagnosis is without a condition in which the eyelid (usually the lower lid) folds inward. It is very uncomfortable, as the eyelashes constantly rub against the cornea and irritate it.

name: External hirudiniasis
definition: Infestation of the skin by leeches. Sensitisation to antigenic substances deposited in the skin can result in urticarial weals and bullae.
synonyms: Leech infestation NOS

name: Chronic erythematous oral candidosis
definition: Oral candidosis associated with denture use and normally confined to the palate and gums. It is often, however, associated with angular cheilitis. The affected mucous membranes show a variable bright red or dusky erythema beneath the denture.
synonyms: Denture sore mouth | Denture stomatitis

name: Psoriatic nail dystrophy
definition: Unspecified nail dystrophy due to psoriasis

name: Viral exanthem due to unknown or unspecified agent
definition: An exanthematic rash with symptoms suggestive of a viral aetiology where the agent is either unknown or unspecified.
synonyms: Viral exanthem | nonspecific exanthematous viral infection | nonspecific viral exanthem | nonspecific viral rash | viral disease characterised by exanthem | viral enanthema NOS | viral exanthem, unspecified | viral exanthema NOS | viral exanthemata | viral rash NOS
cn_name: 未知或未特指病原体引起的病毒疹

name: Burning of skin
definition: A burning sensation in the skin which usually arises without obvious explanation.
synonyms: Burning sensation | Disturbance in temperature sense

name: Deafness – onychodystrophy, autosomal recessive
definition: Deafness Onychodystrophy Onycholysis Retardation (DOOR) syndrome is characterised by sensorineural hearing loss, distinctive nail abnormalities and bone malformations, and mild to profound mental retardation.
synonyms: DOOR syndrome (MIM 220500) | DOOR syndrome, autosomal recessive

name: Penoscrotodynia
definition: An uncommon but distressing somatoform disorder affecting men in which there is a clear and precise complaint of genital pain and/or a skin burning sensation for which no underlying cause can be found.
synonyms: Dysaesthetic penile and scrotal pain

name: Dermatoses due to certain filamentous bacteria
definition: Skin disorders due to filamentous bacteria including cutaneous actinomycosis, cutaneous nocardiosis and actinomycetoma.

name: Pitted keratolysis
definition: Pitted keratolysis is a dermatosis of plantar skin caused by digestion of epidermal keratin by bacteria. Various bacteria including Kytococcus sedentarius, Dermatophilus congolensis and species of Corynebacterium, Actinomyces and Streptomyces. Prolonged occlusion and hyperhidrosis predispose to bacterial proliferation and the production of bacterial proteinases which destroy the stratum corneum, creating multiple pits. The affected skin is often white and macerated as a result of the accompanyin
synonyms: keratolysis plantare sulcatum | ringed keratolysis

name: Heterotopic sebaceous glands
synonyms: Ectopic sebaceous glands | Fordyce spots | anomalous sebaceous glands | ectopic sebaceous gland | fordyce's spots | heterotopic sebaceous glands

name: Schnitzler syndrome
definition: Schnitzler syndrome is a rare acquired auto-inflammatory disorder characterised by recurrent fever, urticaria, fatigue, bone and joint pain, lymphadenopathy, leukocytosis and a monoclonal IgM component. Mean age of onset is around 50. The disease follows a chronic course. A large number of immunosuppressive agents are ineffective but the IL-1 receptor antagonist anakinra appears to provide rapid and sustained relief. The monoclonal IgM component is a defining feature and may result in AA amyloid
synonyms: Chronic urticaria with macroglobulinaemia

name: Early-onset sarcoidosis
synonyms: Sporadic Blau syndrome | Sporadic autoinflammatory granulomatosis of childhood

name: Oesophageal varices without bleeding in viral hepatic diseases
definition: These are extremely dilated sub-mucosal veins in the lower third of the oesophagus without bleeding and damage to or disease of the liver.

name: Acute excoriation of skin
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists of multiple excoriations. In the majority of cases the excoriation is secondary to intense pruritus arising either from an underlying systemic disorder such as cholestatic jaundice or from a pruritic skin disease such as eczema. In some cases, however, psychogenic factors may be responsible.

name: Corrosion of second degree of head or neck
Mentophyma
definition: Cushion-like, firm swelling of central forehead, caused by hypertrophy of the sebaceous glands.

name: Cutaneous reactions to Bryozoa
definition: Adverse reactions to contact with aquatic invertebrates of the phylum Bryozoa, in particular sea chervil, the cause of Dogger Bank itch.

name: Self-healing collodion baby
definition: Self-healing collodion baby (SHCB) is a minor variant of autosomal recessive congenital ichthyosis (ARCI; ) characterised by the presence of a collodion membrane at birth that heals within the first weeks of life.
synonyms: desquamination of the newborn | lamellar exfoliation of the newborn | self-improving collodion baby

name: Iatrogenic tattoo
definition: Tattoos administered under direct medical supervision, most commonly as markers for defining radiotherapy treatment sites or for cosmetic reasons such as in patients with vitiligo or for hiding vascular malformations. They may also arise as an unwanted side effect, particularly of parenteral iron injections.
synonyms: Medical tattoo

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 1S
definition: Scheie syndrome is the mildest form of mucopolysaccharidosis type 1 (MPS1), a rare lysosomal storage disease, characterised by skeletal deformities and a delay in motor development. The disease commonly manifests after the age of 5 years but are so mild that diagnosis is often not considered until adulthood.
synonyms: MPS1S - [Mucopolysaccharidosis type 1S] | Mucopolysaccharidosis type 1, mild form | Scheie syndrome

name: Non-follicular punctate or digitate keratoses
definition: Disorders of epidermal keratinization characterised by the development of multiple spiny keratinous spicules and papules from the surface of the epidermis rather than from the pilosebaceous follicle.

name: Lucio type diffuse lepromatous leprosy
definition: Diffuse lepromatous leprosy (Lucio-Latapi leprosy) is a special form of lepromatous leprosy found only in Central America, where it is the predominant type. It is distinguished by widespread, diffuse infiltration of the skin by leprosy bacilli without formation of nodules or plaques. This may produce a full \"baby-face\" appearance and loss of eyebrows. Patients are predisposed to a fulminant type II leprosy reaction which can result in widespread cutaneous infarction (Lucio phenomenon) \[Dermat
synonyms: Hansen disease, diffuse lepromatous | Lucio-Latapi lepromatous leprosy

name: Malignant naevus of unspecified site
synonyms: naevocarcinoma of unspecified site

name: Squamous cell carcinoma in situ of skin of penis
definition: Squamous cell carcinoma affecting the skin of the prepuce or of the shaft of the penis and commonly called Bowen disease. HPV infection and chronic exposure to psoralen photochemotherapy are predisposing factors.
synonyms: Bowen disease of skin of penis
cn_name: 阴茎皮肤鳞状细胞原位癌

name: Primary non-essential cutis verticis gyrata
definition: Primary non-essential cutis verticis gyrata is a rare, genetic, dermis disorder characterized by slowly progressive thickening of the scalp, which becomes raised and forms ridges and furrows with symmetrical distribution resembling the cerebral gyri and cannot be flattened by traction or pressure, associated with ophthalmologic (e.g. congenital cataract) and/or neurological abnormalities (e.g. intellectual disability, epilepsy, microcephaly, encephalopathy).

name: Mucocutaneous erythema multiforme
definition: Erythema multiforme with mucosal involvement, usually of oral and/or genital mucous membranes. It causes significantly more morbidity than erythema multiforme confined to the skin.
synonyms: Erythema multiforme majus | Erythema multiforme with mucosal involvement

name: Malignant neoplasm of sebaceous glands
synonyms: Sebaceous carcinoma of skin

name: Exacerbation of constitutional dermatitis due to occupational exposure to skin irritants
definition: Exacerbation of constitutional dermatitis such as atopic dermatitis attributable to contact with skin irritants at work.
synonyms: Exacerbation of constitutional eczema due to occupational exposure to skin irritants

name: Autosomal dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa
definition: Dystrophic forms of epidermolysis bullosa with autosomal dominant inheritance.

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to plants or other specified organic matter
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to plants and other organic matter (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to allergenic haptens derived from plants and organic matter).

name: Colloid milium
definition: Colloid milium is a degenerative change characterized clinically by the development of yellowish, translucent papules or plaques on light-exposed skin, and histologically by the presence of colloid, thought to be derived from degenerate elastic fibres, in the dermal papillae. It is uncommon but probably under-recognised; it usually affects fair‐skinned, outdoor workers living in sunny climates. A rare familial juvenile form presents before puberty and is thought to derive from degenerate keratin
synonyms: Colloid degeneration of the skin | Colloid pseudomilium | colloid degeneration of the skin | elastosis colloidalis conglomerata | miliary colloidoma

name: Neonatal staphylococcal scalded skin syndrome
definition: Staphylococcal scalded skin syndrome occurring during the neonatal period.

name: Knuckle pads – leukonychia – sensorineural deafness
definition: Knuckle pads are circumscribed, keratotic, fibrous growths over the dorsa of the interphalangeal joints. Leukonychia is a medical term for white discoloration appearing on nails. Sensorineural deafness is a type of hearing loss in which the root cause lies in the vestibulocochlear nerve (Cranial nerve VIII), the inner ear, or central processing centres of the brain.
synonyms: Bart-Pumphrey syndrome (MIM 149200)

name: Neonatal mineral deficiency affecting the skin
Epidermolysis bullosa simplex, autosomal recessive keratin 14 deficiency
definition: This rare severe autosomal recessive form of epidermolysis bullosa simplex (EBS-AR K14) results from mutations in the KR14 gene.
synonyms: EBS-AR K14 - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex - autosomal recessive keratin 14]

name: Xanthoma in association with secondary hyperlipidaemia
definition: Xanthomata of any type arising as the result of secondary hyperlipidaemia from disorders such as diabetes mellitus and nephrotic syndrome.

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, scapula
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, scapula

name: Papillary intralymphatic angioendothelioma of unspecified site
Skin injury due to occupational exposure to corrosive substances
definition: Damage to the skin attributable to exposure to corrosive substances such as acids or alkalis at work.

name: Skin disorders localised to the scalp
definition: Skin disorders affecting preferentially or exclusively the scalp.
cn_name: 仅累及头部的皮肤疾病

name: Cutaneous Mycobacterium kansasii infection
definition: Mycobacterium kansasii infection occurs principally in immunocompromised individuals. Cutaneous infection may present with either localised or disseminated papulonecrotic skin lesions \[Dermatology TAG\].

name: Leptospiral meningitis
synonyms: aseptic leptospiral meningitis | meningitis in leptospirosis

name: Superficial white onychomycosis
definition: Superficial invasion of the surface of the nail plate from its dorsal surface, resulting in white flecks or patches on the nail surface. It is a well recognised pattern seen with infection by Trichophyton mentagrophytes or by Fusarium species.

name: Trichinosis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the parasitic worm Trichinella. This disease is characterised by fever, nausea, diarrhoea, vomiting, fatigue, or abdominal discomfort. This disease may also present with headache, chills, cough, swelling of the face and eyes, or aching joints and muscle pains. Transmission is by ingestion of contaminated meat. Confirmation is by detection of antibodies against Trichinella in a blood sample.
synonyms: Trichinella spiralis infestation | Trichinelliasis | Trichinellosis | Trichiniasis

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy Type IC
definition: HSAN Type IC is the result of a mutation in the SPLTC2 gene and presents with loss of pain and temperature sensations, occasional autonomic involvement, variable sensorineural deafness and distal motor involvement.
synonyms: HSAN1C - [Hereditary sensory autonomic neuropathy Type IC]

name: Tropical acne
definition: Follicular acneiform eruption that results from exposure to extreme heat.
synonyms: Acne tropica

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, head
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, head

name: Neonatal staphylococcal pyoderma
definition: Staphylococcal skin infections in the newborn.

name: Chronic graft-versus-host disease
definition: Chronic graft-versus-host disease (GVHD) presents more than 100 days after engraftment of immunocompetent donor lymphoid cells. It has specific clinical features by which it can be distinguished from acute GVHD. It may arise de novo but frequently follows acute GVHD. Less commonly, it occurs concurrently with acute GVHD. The earlier stages of chronic GVHD are characterised by a widespread lichenoid rash, poikiloderma and involvement of nails and oral mucous membranes. If the disease remains acti
synonyms: chronic GVHD - [graft-versus-host disease] | chronic graft-versus-host-disease | cGVHD

name: Atopic eczema of hands
definition: Atopic eczema involving predominantly the hands. Individuals with an atopic diathesis are particularly prone to hand eczema. It may be provoked by repeated contact with irritants at work as in hairdressers, cooks and health care workers. It may manifest in a discoid pattern of eczema.
synonyms: Atopic hand dermatitis

name: Localised cutaneous erysipeloid
definition: Direct contact between meat infected with the bacterium Erysipelothrix rhusiopathiae and traumatized human skin results in erysipeloid, which most commonly presents as a localised infection of traumatized skin. Lesions most commonly affect the hands, mainly the webs of the fingers; however, any exposed area of the body may be affected. Lesions consist of well-demarcated, bright red-to-purple, warm, tender plaques with a smooth, shiny surface. They leave a brownish discoloration on the skin when

name: Primary genital syphilis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum pallidum. This disease is characterised by a single chancre in the genital region. Transmission is commonly by sexual contact.
synonyms: Genital syphilitic chancre | Syphilitic chancre NOS | chancre | genital chancre | genital syphilis | mixed chancre | primary syphilis | primary syphilitic chancre | syphilitic bubo | syphilitic chancre

name: Tattoo infection
definition: Infection classified elsewhere attributable to the administration or presence of a tattoo. Examples include both local (e.g. viral warts or pyogenic bacteria) and systemic (e.g. viral hepatitis, HIV and bacterial sepsis) infections.

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum F
definition: Xeroderma pigmentosum complementation group F (XPF) is a generally milder subtype of xeroderma pigmentosum (XP), a rare photodermatosis predisposing to skin cancers. Most patients demonstrate very mild skin symptoms and no ocular or neurological disease.
synonyms: XPF - [xeroderma pigmentosum F]

name: Delayed acute graft-versus-host disease
definition: Acute graft-versus-host disease presenting more than 100 days after engraftment.
synonyms: delayed acute GVHD - [graft-versus-host disease]

name: Ashy dermatosis
definition: Ashy dermatosis is an asymptomatic disorder of skin pigmentation of disputed aetiology characterised by widespread persistent and often symmetrical pale slate-blue macules in healthy individuals. It shares both clinical and histological features with lichen planus but mucous membranes are unaffected. Preceding erythema around the margins of the pigmented macules may be noted in a minority of patients, in which case the disorder may be referred to as erythema dyschromicum perstans. It has been de
synonyms: Ashy dermatosis of Ramirez | Dermatosis cenicienta | Erythema chronicum figuratum melanodermicum | Erythema dyschromicum perstans | Idiopathic eruptive macular pigmentation | ashy dermatitis | ashy dermatosis | cinderella dermatosis | dermatosis cenicienta | EDP

name: Exfoliative cheilitis
definition: Exfoliative cheilitis is a chronic superficial inflammatory disorder of the vermilion border of the lips characterised by persistent scaling. It is most commonly attributed to self-inflicted injury from chewing, sucking or picking.

name: Active stage of trachoma
definition: This refers to the active stage of an infectious disease caused by the Chlamydia trachomatis bacterium which produces a characteristic roughening of the inner surface of the eyelids.
synonyms: Egyptian ophthalmia | Trachomatous pannus | marginal corneal pannus | trachoma pannus

name: Drug-induced erythema nodosum
definition: Erythema nodosum triggered by medication. Many drugs have been implicated but if these have been given for treating an infection it may be difficult to determine whether the infection or the drug is responsible for the reaction. recognised triggers, however, include high dose oestrogen-containing oral contraceptive preparations, sulfonamides and bromides.

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome due to tenascin-X deficiency
definition: A form of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome characterized by generalized joint hypermobility, skin hyperextensibility and easy bruising without atrophic scarring. Other common features include foot and hand deformities (piezogenic papules, pes planus, broad forefeet, brachydactyly, and acrogeric skin of hands), severe fatigue and neuromuscular symptoms including muscle weakness and myalgia.

name: Lobular capillary haemangioma
definition: Historically called pyogenic granuloma, this is a common benign proliferation of capillary blood vessels which may be induced by trauma or by certain drugs. It presents as one or more bright red papules or nodules often located around the mouth or on a terminal phalanx in relation to the nail. Bleeding, ulceration and crusting frequently occur. BRAF mutations within vascular endothelial cells may be present, indicating that this is, in at least a proportion of cases, a true neoplastic process.
synonyms: Granuloma pyogenicum | Granuloma telangiectaticum of skin | Lobular capillary haemangioma of skin | Pyogenic granuloma | Pyogenic granuloma of skin | botryomycoma | eruptive hemangioma | granulation tissue-type hemangioma | granuloma pyogenicum | lobular capillary hemangioma

name: Frostbite of wrist with tissue necrosis
synonyms: frostbite with tissue necrosis of wrist

name: Hereditary alopecia or hypotrichosis
definition: Genetically-determined absence or sparsity of hair.
