name: Skin lesion of uncertain nature, lower limb
definition: This denotes the presence of a skin lesion on a lower limb but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Candidosis of lips or oral mucous membranes
definition: A disease of the lips and oral mucous membranes, caused by an infection with the fungi Candida. This disease commonly presents with white patches or plaques on the oral mucous membranes, angular cheilitis, or dysphagia. Transmission is by opportunistic transmission. Confirmation is by identification of Candida in an oral or skin sample.
synonyms: Candida of mouth | Candida stomatitis | Candidal stomatitis | Mouth candidiasis | oral candidiasis | oral yeast infection | oropharyngeal candidiasis | throat candidiasis
cn_name: 唇或口腔黏膜念珠菌病

name: Unstable psoriasis
definition: Unstable psoriasis is an inflammatory form of psoriasis which may be the precursor of erythrodermic or generalised pustular psoriasis. It is characterised by intense inflammation around the edges of existing plaques and/or the appearance of multiple small fresh inflammatory papules and plaques. Some patients may have a lifelong tendency to unstable psoriasis. It tends to be difficult to control without resort to systemic therapy.

name: Cold panniculitis of the newborn
definition: Cold panniculitis resulting either from exposure of neonates to low environmental temperature or from local application of cold objects (e.g. ice packs for management of neonatal supraventricular tachycardia). The newborn are particularly susceptible as a result of a high saturated/unsaturated fat ratio in subcutaneous fat with a consequent elevation of the freezing point of fat.

name: Juvenile macular degeneration - hypotrichosis
definition: Hypotrichosis with juvenile macular degeneration is a very rare syndrome characterised by sparse and short hair from birth followed by progressive macular degeneration leading to blindness.

name: Noma neonatorum
definition: Noma neonatorum is a life-threatening disease of the newborn resulting in the majority of cases from Pseudomonas septicaemia. It presents as acute gangrene and ulceration of soft tissues of the nose, lips, mouth, eyelids, anus and scrotum. Predisposing factors include malnutrition, prematurity and low birth weight.

name: Penile pemphigus
definition: Pemphigus vulgaris involving the mucocutaneous epithelium of the penis and presenting as ulceration of the glans penis and prepuce. Although blistering and ulceration is usually apparent elsewhere, the pemphigus may be confined to the penis and represent a diagnostic challenge.
synonyms: Pemphigus vulgaris of the penis

name: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa, localised
definition: Localised junctional epidermolysis bullosa (JEB-loc) is characterised by localised blistering, and dystrophic or absent nails.
synonyms: JEB - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa] non-Herlitz, localised | JEB-loc - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa, localised | JEB-nH loc - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa, non-Herlitz, localised]

name: Certain genetically-determined metabolic disorders with skin involvement
definition: Heredofamilial metabolic disorders with skin manifestations not listed elsewhere in this chapter.

name: Klinefelter syndrome with karyotype 47,XXY, regular
definition: Karyotype 47 XXY; gonads\: testes (hypogonadism) small and firm with decreased spermatogenesis ; phenotype male with associated congenital abnormalities (decreased virilization due to decreased testosterone production, long arms and legs, short trunk, psychosocial problems).
synonyms: Klinefelter syndrome karyotype 47, xxy | xxy syndrome

name: Granulomatous slack skin
definition: Granulomatous slack skin is a T-cell lymphoma characterised by pendulous skin folds in axillae and/or inguinal region. Histologically, there are numerous foreign-body type giant cells with elastolysis and neoplastic lymphocytes.

name: Lobular capillary haemangioma of oral mucosa
definition: This is a primarily oral disease which appears as an overgrowth of tissue due to irritation, physical trauma or hormonal factors.
synonyms: Gingival pyogenic granuloma | Granuloma pyogenicum of oral mucosa | Oral pyogenic granuloma | Pyogenic granuloma of oral mucosa

name: Primary cutaneous aggressive epidermotropic CD8-positive T-cell lymphoma
definition: A highly aggressive T-cell lymphoma composed of CD8 positive atypical T cells localising primarily in the epidermis. Patients present with cutaneous nodules and ulcers.
synonyms: Berti lymphoma | primary cutaneous aggressive epidermotropic CD8+ T-cell lymphoma

name: Disorders of epidermal keratinisation
definition: This group incorporates dermatoses characterised by scaling (ichthyoses and hyperkeratoses), epidermal thickening (acanthoses and keratodermas), loss of cohesion (acantholytic dermatoses and skin peeling) or xeroderma.

name: Acanthosis nigricans
definition: Acanthosis nigricans is characterised by dark, thick, velvety skin in body folds and creases. It is most commonly encountered in association with obesity and type 2 diabetes though may be a component of a number of genetic syndromes. Hyperinsulinaemia and insulin resistance are important underlying factors. Acanthosis nigricans may rarely be due to underlying malignancy (paraneoplastic acanthosis nigricans).

name: Gastric varices without bleeding
definition: This refers to abnormally dilated submucosal veins in the lining of stomach with portal hypertension, without sign of bleeding.

name: Linear IgA bullous dermatosis
definition: Linear IgA bullous dermatosis is an uncommon immunobullous disorder which occurs in both adults and children. It is characterised by linear deposition of IgA along the epidermal basement membrane. Although the clinical picture may resemble dermatitis herpetiformis, it is not associated with gluten enteropathy and it has different clinical and immunopathological attributes.
synonyms: Linear IgA disease | IgA bullous pemphigoid | LABD

name: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton simii
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton simii, the main natural hosts of which are monkeys.

name: Primary cutaneous CD30-positive T-cell lymphoproliferative disorders
definition: Primary skin disorders characterised immunohistologically by infiltration by neoplastic CD30+ lymphocytes.
synonyms: primary cutaneous CD30+ T-cell proliferations
cn_name: 原发皮肤CD30阳性T细胞淋巴组织增殖性疾病

name: Childhood atopic eczema, impetiginised
definition: Atopic eczema in childhood with significant secondary staphylococcal infection and manifested clinically by weeping and crusting.
synonyms: Childhood atopic dermatitis, impetiginised | Infected atopic eczema of the child

name: Body dysmorphic disorder
definition: Body Dysmorphic Disorder is characterised by persistent preoccupation with one or more perceived defects or flaws in appearance that are either unnoticeable or only slightly noticeable to others. Individuals experience excessive self-consciousness, often with ideas of reference (i.e., the conviction that people are taking notice, judging, or talking about the perceived defect or flaw). In response to their preoccupation, individuals engage in repetitive and excessive behaviours that include repe
synonyms: Dysmorphophobia | dysmorphophobia

name: Cutaneous reactions to venomous or noxious animals
cn_name: 对有毒或有害动物的皮肤反应

name: Fibrosis or scarring following cosmetic procedure
definition: Unsatisfactory scarring and fibrosis following a procedure designed to improve cosmesis.
cn_name: 美容操作后的纤维化或瘢痕形成

name: Non-melanin pigmentation due to ingested or injected substance
definition: Pigmentation of the skin resulting from exogenous pigments derived from substances ingested or injected into the body. The pigment may be dietary (especially betacarotenes), from drugs or their metabolites (e.g. clofazimine) or from other causes such as transfusional haemosiderosis.

name: Sjögren syndrome with renal tubulo-interstitial disorders
Postherpetic pruritus
definition: Persistent itch at site of healed Herpes zoster infection.

name: Auricular cartilage ossification
definition: Petrified ear with ossification of cartilage.

name: Erysipeloid
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-positive bacteria Erysipelothrix rhusiopathiae. This disease is characterised by localised cellulitis. Transmission is by direct cutaneous contact with Erysipelothrix rhusiopathiae, often in individuals handling seafood and raw meat.
synonyms: Erysipelothrix infection | diamond skin disease | pseudoerysipelas

name: Thermal burns resulting from cutaneous laser surgery
definition: Undesired damage to skin as the result of heat injury from laser surgery

name: Irritant contact cheilitis due to lip-licking
definition: Inflammation of the vermilion of the lips due to habitual licking.
synonyms: lip-lick cheilitis

name: Contact dermatitis of hands
definition: Contact dermatitis not specified as irritant or allergic affecting the hands.

name: Infantile haemangioma
definition: This is a congenital capillary benign, and usually a self-involuting tumour, (swelling or growth) of the endothelial cells that line blood vessels and is characterised by increased number of normal or abnormal vessels filled with blood.
synonyms: Congenital capillary haemangioma

name: Heterotopic sebaceous glands of penis
synonyms: Fordyce spots of penis

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type III
definition: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy, type 3 (HSAN3) is an autosomal recessive disorder seen primarily in Ashkenazi Jewish children caused by a mutation in the I-kappa B kinase associated protein.  It is characterised by sensory dysfunction and severe impairment of the autonomic nervous system activity, resulting in multisystem dysfunction.  Symptoms can include insensitivity to pain and temperature, intact visceral pain, alacrima, hypoactive corneal and tendon reflexes and absence of lin
synonyms: Familial dysautonomia | HSAN3 - [Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type 3] | Riley Day syndrome | HSAN 3 | neuropathy, hereditart, sensory and autonomic type 3 | riley-day syndrome

name: Pressure ulceration grade 4 of sacrum
synonyms: stage 4 decubitus ulcer of sacrum

name: Benign dermal fibrous or fibrohistiocytic neoplasms
definition: Benign dermal neoplasms due to abnormal proliferation of fibroblasts, myofibroblasts or primitive mesenchymal cells.
cn_name: 良性真皮纤维或纤维组织细胞瘤

name: Burn of ankle or foot
definition: Injury to the tissues of the ankle and foot caused by contact with, for example, heat, steam, chemicals, or electricity.
synonyms: burn and corrosion of ankle or foot | burn of ankle or foot of unspecified degree

name: Otophyma
definition: Hypertrophy of the sebaceous glands, causing swelling of the ears, primarily affecting the lower half of the helices and the lobes of the ears.

name: Xerosis cutis due to leprosy
definition: Residual severe and diffuse skin xerosis in patients with diffuse leprosy, caused by a combination of neuropathy and damage to epidermal appendages from active or past skin infiltration by Mycobacterium leprae bacilli \[Dermatology TAG\].

name: Benign lymphocytic infiltration of the skin
definition: Benign lymphocytic infiltration of Jessner is a chronic benign T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder characterised by the presence of non-scarring red tumid nodules, usually on facial skin. It may be difficult to distinguish from cutaneous lupus erythematosus.
synonyms: Jessner lymphocytic infiltration

name: Burn of head or neck except face, full thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of head or neck except face, third degree
cn_name: 头和颈部（除外面部）全层皮肤烧伤

name: Early syphilis involving the musculoskeletal system
definition: This is an early sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is involving the musculoskeletal system.

name: Necrobiosis lipoidica
definition: Necrobiosis lipoidica is an uncommon skin condition in which degenerate dermal collagen is surrounded by a granulomatous inflammatory response to produce shiny, red-brown or yellowish patches in the skin, particularly on the shins. In severe cases the affected skin may ulcerate. It is associated in the majority of but not all cases with underlying diabetes mellitus, the onset of which it may precede.

name: Hereditary angioedema type II
definition: Hereditary angioedema associated with gene mutations resulting in functionally defective C1 inhibitor.
synonyms: C1 esterase inhibitor [C1-INH] qualitative deficiency | C1-INH - [C1 esterase inhibitor] deficiency | HAE-II - [Hereditary angioedema type II] | Hereditary angioneurotic oedema type 2

name: Berloque dermatitis
definition: Phototoxic dermatitis from application of photoactive fragrances to the skin, typically the sides of the neck. It may present with hypermelanosis rather than inflammation.
synonyms: Berlock dermatitis

name: Acute eruptive lichen planus
definition: An acute generalised form of lichen planus.
synonyms: Eruptive lichen planus | Exanthematic lichen planus | eruptive lichen planus | exanthematous lichen planus

name: Dermatoses resulting from immunosuppressive therapy
cn_name: 免疫抑制治疗引起的皮肤病

name: Lower limb deep vein thrombosis
definition: Thrombosis within the deep venous system of the lower limb.
synonyms: DVT - [deep vein thrombosis] | DVT NOS - [deep vein thrombosis not otherwise specified] | Thrombosis of deep vein of leg | Thrombosis of deep vein of lower extremity | Venous thrombosis of deep veins of lower extremity | deep vein thrombosis NOS

name: Keratinopathic ichthyoses
definition: Heritable ichthyoses resulting from mutations in keratin genes.

name: Skin lesion of uncertain nature, head
definition: This denotes the presence of a skin lesion on the face or scalp but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Chronic effects of ultraviolet radiation on the skin
cn_name: 紫外线辐射对皮肤的慢性效应

name: Necrotising fasciitis, pelvic region or thigh
Yaws osteitis
synonyms: osteitis of yaws

name: Organoid naevus syndrome
definition: Linear nevus sebaceous syndrome (LNSS) is characterised by the association of a large sebaceous nevus, usually appearing on the face or on the scalp, with a broad spectrum of abnormalities that may affect every organ system, including the central nervous system (brain neoplasms, hemimegalencephaly and lateral ventricle enlargement).
synonyms: Jadassohn naevus phakomatosis | Linear naevus sebaceous syndrome | Linear organoid naevus syndrome | Schimmelpenning syndrome | Schimmelpenning-Feuerstein-Mims syndrome | Sebaceous naevus syndrome | Solomon syndrome | ENSs | jadassohn syndrome | nevus sebaceus syndrome

name: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency, classic form
definition: This refers to any of several autosomal recessive diseases resulting from mutations of genes for enzymes mediating the biochemical steps of production of cortisol from cholesterol by the adrenal glands (steroidogenesis). This diagnosis is due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency, classic form.
synonyms: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency, salt-losing

name: Protein contact dermatitis
definition: Immediate contact dermatitis due to exposure to proteins from plants, animal tissue and other organic matter.

name: Drug-induced anaphylaxis
definition: Anaphylaxis attributable to a drug. When severe it may be fatal. This systemic reaction usually develops within minutes to hours of administration of the drug, is often severe and may be fatal. The most frequent drugs causing anaphylaxis are antibiotics, particularly penicillins. Clinically there may be premonitory dizziness or faintness, skin tingling and erythema, followed by urticaria, angio-oedema, bronchospasm, abdominal pain and vasomotor collapse.
synonyms: Anaphylaxis due to drugs and medicaments | medicament-induced anaphylaxis

name: Skin problem resulting from ileostomy
definition: An entity to be used to link a specific skin problem (e.g. psoriasis or parastomal pyoderma gangrenosum) to its proximity to an ileostomy.

name: Extracutaneous infantile haemangioma of other specified site
definition: Infantile haemangioma located elsewhere than skin, liver, respiratory tract or orbit.

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, hip joint
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, hip joint

name: Aspartylglucosaminuria
definition: Aspartylglycosaminuria is an autosomal recessive lysosomal storage disease belonging to the oligosaccharidosis group that is due to deficiency in N-aspartylglycosaminidase. Clinical signs include slowly developing intellectual deficit, beginning with clumsiness, late speech, and hyperkinesia, mild facial dysmorphism, and slight kyphoscoliosis.
synonyms: Aspartylglucosaminidase deficiency

name: Staphylococcal toxic shock syndrome
cn_name: 葡萄球菌中毒性休克综合征

name: Coated tongue
synonyms: tongue coat

name: Hypobetalipoproteinaemia
definition: Hypobetalipoproteinemia (HBL) constitutes a group of lipoprotein metabolism disorders that are characterised by permanently low levels (below the 5th percentile) of apolipoprotein B and LDL cholesterol. There are two types of HBL\: familial hypobetalipoproteinemia and chylomicron retention disease (CMRD; see these terms). The familial form can be severe with early onset (abetalipoproteinemia/homozygous familial hypobetalipoproteinemia; see this term) or benign (benign familial hypobetalipoprotei

name: Jugular venous aneurysm
synonyms: jugular vein aneurysm

name: Plane seborrhoeic keratosis
definition: Plane seborrhoeic keratoses present as well-defined pigmented macules or plaques with minimal elevation. There is mild hyperkeratosis, often mild basal pigmentation and only minimal acanthosis. There are no horn cysts. The cells contrast with those of the adjacent normal epidermis by being more compact.
synonyms: Flat seborrhoeic keratosis

name: Skin disorders specific to the perinatal or neonatal period
definition: This group incorporates both skin disorders of the neonate and other disorders of the neonate with skin manifestations
cn_name: 围产期或新生儿期特有的皮肤障碍

name: Perineural lipomatosis
definition: Perineural lipomatosis (lipomatosis of nerve) is a rare condition in which the epineurial and perineurial compartments of the affected nerve are infiltrated by mature adipose tissue admixed with fibrous tissue which dissects between and separates individual nerve bundles. It is frequently first noted at birth or in early childhood, but affected individuals may not present for treatment until adulthood. In some cases it is associated with macrodactyly of the digits innervated by the affected nerv

name: Prenatally acquired infections with neonatal skin manifestations
definition: Infections acquired in utero with skin manifestations at birth or in the neonatal period. These include the TORCH complex of congenital toxoplasmosis, congenital rubella, congenital cytomegalovirus and congenital Herpes simplex infections.

name: Suspected deep pressure-induced tissue damage, depth unknown of trochanter
Allergic contact sensitisation to topical medicaments
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to topical medicaments (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to topical medicaments).

name: Certain noninflammatory disorders of subcutaneous fat
cn_name: 某些非炎性皮下脂肪疾病

name: Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to food flavours or additives
Stevens-Johnson syndrome or toxic epidermal necrolysis
definition: A spectrum of severe and life-threatening hypersensitivity disorders affecting skin and mucous membranes, most commonly precipitated by an idiosyncratic reaction to medication. Stevens-Johnson syndrome (SJS) always involves mucosal surfaces but the skin involvement is limited by definition to <10% body surface area (BSA). Toxic epidermal necrolysis (TEN) may sometimes spare mucous membranes but skin involvement is by definition >30% BSA. An intermediate form is recognised in which mucosal involv
synonyms: SJS/TEN - [Stevens-Johnson syndrome and toxic epidermal necrolysis]
cn_name: Stevens-Johnson综合征或中毒性表皮坏死松解症

name: Adnexal carcinoma of vulva
synonyms: Bartholin gland cancer | Bartholin gland carcinoma | appendageal carcinoma of vulva

name: Sequelae of frostbite with tissue necrosis of ankle or foot
Cutaneous markers of endocrine and metabolic disorders
definition: Cutaneous signs and symptoms which may indicate underlying systemic disease such as pretibial myxoedema in autoimmune thyroid disease.

name: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa, generalised severe
definition: Generalised severe junctional epidermolysis bullosa (JEB-gen sev) is a life-threatening autosomal recessive blistering disorder with extensive epidermal separation at the level of the lamina lucida from birth. There is a high mortality in infancy.
synonyms: Epidermolysis bullosa letalis | Herlitz junctional epidermolysis bullosa gravis | Herlitz syndrome | JEB-H - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa, Herlitz] | JEB-gen sev - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa, generalised severe] | epidermolysis bullosa letalis | Herlitz type epidermolysis bullosa junctionalis | Herlitz-Pearson-type epidermolysis bullosa | JEB-H | JEB-Herlitz type

name: Livedoid vasculopathy

name: Neonatal pyogenic skin infections

cn_name: 新生儿化脓性皮肤感染

name: Dermatoses provoked by friction or mechanical stress
cn_name: 摩擦或机械应力引起的皮肤病

name: Vesicular dermatitis of feet
definition: A vesicular eczema of uncertain cause and otherwise known as dyshidrotic eczema or podopompholyx localised to the plantar surfaces and sides of the feet and toes.
synonyms: Dyshidrotic eczema of feet | Podopompholyx

name: Disorders of eccrine sweat glands or sweating
definition: This group incorporates disorders characterised by increased or reduced sweating (hyper- and hypohidrosis respectively), and by eccrine duct occlusion (miliaria).
synonyms: Eccrine sweat disorders | Sweating disorder

name: Corrosion of first degree of wrist or hand
Epstein-Barr Virus-positive diffuse large B-cell lymphoma of the elderly
definition: An aggressive diffuse large B-cell lymphoma affecting patients older than 50 years. Epstein-Barr virus is present in all cases. There is no known history of immunodeficiency or prior lymphoma. The majority of patients present with extranodal disease.
synonyms: EBV-positive diffuse large B-cell lymphoma of the elderly
cn_name: 老年EB病毒阳性弥漫大B细胞淋巴瘤

name: Chancroid
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Haemophilus ducreyi. This disease is characterised by painful ulcer(s) on the genitalia. Transmission is by sexual contact. Confirmation is by identification of Haemophilus ducreyi from the ulcer exudate.
synonyms: Bubo due to Haemophilus ducreyi | Soft chancre | Ulcus molle | soft chancre | ulcus molle | Ulcus molle, skin

name: Vesicular dermatitis of hands
definition: A vesicular eczema of uncertain cause and otherwise known as dyshidrotic eczema or cheiropompholyx localised to the palmar surfaces and sides of the hands and fingers.
synonyms: Cheiropompholyx | Dyshidrotic hand eczema

name: Ablepharon-macrostomia syndrome
definition: Ablepharon macrostomia syndrome is characterised by the association of facial (absent eyelids, eyebrows and eyelashes, low nasal bridge, rudimentary external ears, hypoplastic and anteverted nostrils, absence of the zygomatic arches, as well as fusion defects of the mouth resulting in an enlarged, fish-like mouth), genital (ambiguous genitalia), and cutaneous features (coarse and dry skin, with redundant folds), and delayed language development.

name: Filariasis due to Wuchereria bancrofti
definition: This is a parasitic disease (usually an infectious tropical disease) that is caused by thread-like nematodes (roundworms) belonging to the superfamily Filarioidea, also known as \"filariae\".
synonyms: Bancroftian filariasis | Wuchereriosis

name: Oedema due to dependency or immobility
definition: Pitting oedema of the lower extremities in individuals with poor mobility who spend prolonged periods with the legs dependent. In the early stages these changes are reversible but if the problem is not addressed irreversible lymphoedema may ensue.

name: Developmental disorders of hair colour
definition: The presence of hair of abnormal or varied colours may be associated with a variety of recognised syndromes or may occur as an isolated phenomenon. Heterochromia of scalp hair may be segmental or Blaschkoid in distribution, indicating underlying somatic mosaicism.

name: Inguinal lymphatic malformation
synonyms: Inguinal lymphangioma

name: Pressure ulceration grade 4
definition: Pressure ulcer with visible or directly palpable muscle, tendon or bone as a result of full thickness loss of skin and subcutaneous tissue. Slough or eschar may be present. The depth varies by anatomical location\: grade IV pressure ulcers can be shallow in areas with little or no subcutaneous fat (e.g. bridge of the nose, ear, occiput and malleolus) but are typically deep and often undermine or tunnel into adjacent structures.
synonyms: bedsore stage IV | pressure injury stage 4 | pressure injury stage 4 with full thickness tissue loss | pressure ulcer category 4 | pressure ulcer with full thickness skin and subcutaneous tissue loss with exposure of deep tissues | stage 4 decubitus ulcer | stage 4 pressure ulcer | stage IV pressure injury

name: Syphilitic aneurysm of aorta
definition: This is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum, causing an aneurysm of the aorta.

name: Mild plaque psoriasis
definition: Psoriasis may be regarded as limited or mild if it a) involves no more than 3% of the skin surface, b) has a limited impact on functioning and quality of life and c) can be adequately managed by the patient or carer with topical therapy alone.

name: Dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, acral
definition: Acral dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa is a rare subtype of dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, characterised by blistering confined primarily to the hands and feet.
synonyms: Acral dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa | DDEB-ac - [dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, acral] | DDEB-acral - [dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, acral]

name: Parasitic infestation of eyelid in leishmaniasis
Lymphoedema due to venous insufficiency
definition: Permanent lymphoedema, usually of the lower extremities, resulting from venous hypertension and chronic gravitational oedema.
synonyms: Lymphoedema secondary to venous insufficiency | Phlebolymphoedema

name: Dermatitis herpetiformis
definition: Dermatitis herpetiformis is an immunobullous skin characterised by recurrent eruptions of intensely itchy papules, vesicles or bullae, which are typically grouped symmetrically on the extensor surfaces of the limbs and on the buttocks and back. The primary lesions are frequently obscured by excoriation. An incompletely understood abnormal response to dietary gluten provokes the formation of autoantibodies to tissue and epidermal transglutaminases and granular deposition of IgA in dermal papillae
synonyms: DH - [dermatitis herpetiformis] | Duhring–Brocq disease | DH | duhring's disease | Dermatosis herpetiformis | Duhring's disease

name: Dermatitis or eczema of certain specified sites
definition: Dermatitis (eczema) involving certain sites where the presentation or aetiology may be distinct.

name: Oesophageal varices with bleeding in primary biliary cholangiopathy
definition: This refers to abnormally dilated veins in the lining of oesophagus with sign of bleeding in patients with primary biliary cirrhosis.
synonyms: oesophageal varices with bleeding in primary biliary cholangitis

name: Childhood atopic eczema, generalised erythematous
definition: Acute or subacute generalised inflammatory atopic eczema in childhood.
synonyms: Childhood atopic dermatitis, generalised erythematous | Erythrodermic atopic eczema of the child

name: Infantile systemic hyalinosis
definition: Infantile systemic hyalinosis is a very rare disorder belonging to the heterogeneous group of genetic fibromatoses and is characterised by progressive joint contractures, skin abnormalities, severe chronic pain and widespread deposition of hyaline material in many tissues such as the skin, skeletal muscle, cardiac muscle, gastrointestinal tract, lymph nodes, spleen, thyroid, and adrenal glands.
synonyms: ISH

name: Traumatic injury to nail bed or matrix of nail of foot
definition: Penetrating, crush or avulsion injury to the nail bed or matrix of a toe which may result in permanent nail dystrophy or nail loss.

name: Ankyloblepharon filiforme adnatum
definition: Isolated ankyloblepharon filiforme adnatum is characterised by the presence of single or multiple thin bands of connective tissue between the upper and lower eyelids, preventing full opening of the eye.
synonyms: isolated ankyloblepharon filiforme adnatum

name: Bruise or contusion of skin
definition: Extravasation of blood into the dermis and subcutaneous tissue as the result of focal blunt trauma.
synonyms: Bruise of skin or soft tissue | Contusion of skin or soft tissue | Contusion of unspecified body region | Ecchymosis | bruise

name: Secondary lichenification
definition: Thickening of skin affected by a primary dermatosis as the result of repeated rubbing and scratching.

name: Mycetoma of unknown or unspecified type
definition: Mycetoma is a destructive localised chronic infection of skin, subcutaneous tissue and bone, most commonly affecting the foot. It can be caused by either fungi (eumycetoma) or filamentous bacteria (actinomycetoma). Where possible it should be classified more precisely as either actinomycetoma, the commonest type, or eumycetoma
synonyms: Madura foot | madura foot | maduromycosis
cn_name: 足分支菌病，类型不明或未特指

name: Acute seborrhoeic otitis externa
definition: Acute seborrhoeic dermatitis of the external ear.

name: Pityriasis capitis
synonyms: Dandruff

name: Steatocystoma multiplex
definition: Steatocystoma multiplex is a rare autosomal dominant genodermatosis characterised by the development of multiple intradermal cysts with sebaceous gland lobules in their wall and sebum in their lumen. It usually first manifests in adolescence or early adult life with multiple smooth compressible dermal nodules involving the trunk, particularly the presternal area, and proximal limbs. Suppuration and scarring commonly occur.
synonyms: sebocystomatosis

name: Miscellaneous neurological conditions affecting the skin
definition: A heterogeneous group of disorders not classified elsewhere due to neurological dysfunction and manifesting with skin symptoms or signs.

name: Epiblepharon, bilateral
definition: This is characterised by a congenital horizontal fold of skin near the margin of the upper or lower eyelid caused by the abnormal insertion of muscle fibres. This extra fold of skin redirects the lashes into a vertical position, where they may contact the globe. This diagnosis is occurring in both eyes.

name: Tinea nigra
definition: A disease of the skin, caused by an infection with the fungi Tinea nigra. This disease is characterised by brown to black macules; small, flat circumscribed changes in the colour of skin. This disease commonly presents on the palmar surfaces, soles, or other skin surfaces. Transmission is by direct contact with contaminated soil, wood, or vegetation. Confirmation is identification of Tinea nigra in a skin sample.
synonyms: Keratomycosis nigricans palmaris | superficial phaeohyphomycosis | tinea nigra palmaris et plantaris

name: Ancylostomiasis
definition: This is inflammation of the duodenum due to Ancylostoma hookworms.
synonyms: ancylostoma infestation | hook-worm infestation by Ancylostoma

name: OL-EDA-ID syndrome
definition: Anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia - immunodeficiency - osteopetrosis - lymphoedema syndrome is characterised by severe immunodeficiency, osteopetrosis, lymphoedema and anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia.
synonyms: Anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia - immunodeficiency - osteopetrosis - lymphoedema | OLEDAID - [Anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia - immunodeficiency - osteopetrosis - lymphoedema] syndrome

name: Granuloma inguinale
definition: A disease caused by infection with the gram-negative bacterium Klebsiella granulomatis. It commonly presents with painless genital ulceration following contact with an infected sexual partner. Small, painless nodules appear after an incubation period of about 10–40 days; later the nodules break down to create open, fleshy, oozing ulcers which gradually extend, mutilating the infected tissue. The lesions occur at the region of contact and are typically found on the shaft of the penis, the labia,
synonyms: Donovanosis | Granuloma inguinale tropicum | Granuloma pudendi tropicum | granuloma genitoinguinale | granuloma inguinale | granuloma inguinale tropicum | granuloma venereum | granuloma venereum genitoinguinale | lupoid form of groin ulceration | serpiginous ulceration of the groin

name: Irukandji syndrome
synonyms: Box jellyfish sting

name: Histiocytoses of uncertain malignant potential
definition: Disorders characterised by abnormal proliferation of dendritic cells and macrophages. The proliferation may or may not be clonal and the prognosis is unpredictable.
cn_name: 恶性潜能未定的组织细胞增多症

name: Naevoid hypertrichosis
definition: Congenital circumscribed area of abnormal terminal hair growth
synonyms: Congenital localised hypertrichosis

name: Iatrogenic skin injury resulting from antenatal interventions
Hutchinson triad
synonyms: Hutchinson teeth or incisors | mulberry molars

name: Sclerotic fibroma
definition: A rare benign neoplasm arising from the fibrous soft tissues. It is characterised by the presence of spindle-shaped myofibroblasts and collagenous stroma formation.
synonyms: Storiform collagenoma

name: Trichoadenoma
synonyms: trichoadenoma of nikolowski

name: Penile fibromatosis
definition: A condition characterised by induration of the corpora cavernosa of the penis producing a painful fibrous chordee within the soft tissue of the penis and inflammation of the tunica albuginea. This condition may be associated with trauma or injury to the penis. This condition may also present with pain during erection, erectile dysfunction, shortening, or abnormal curvature of the penis when erect. Confirmation is by ultrasonography.
synonyms: Induratio penis plastica | Peyronie disease | Plastic induration of penis | peyronie's disease

name: Cutaneous or subcutaneous xanthomata
definition: An abnormal accumulation of lipid in the skin or soft tissues, most frequently due to an associated dyslipidaemia.
cn_name: 皮肤和皮下组织黄瘤病

name: Stasis dermatitis of lower legs
definition: A chronic eczematous process affecting the skin of the lower legs in association with chronic lower limb lymphoedema and immobility. It is often associated with lipodermatosclerosis. Failure of the normal \"muscle pump\" to aid venous return is an important component in the aetiology. It is common in the morbidly obese. It is characterised by low grade inflammation with variable scaling and desquamation.
synonyms: Gravitational eczema | Hypostatic dermatitis | Stasis eczema

definition: Autosomal recessive palmoplantar hyperkeratosis and congenital alopecia (PPK-CA) is a rare genetic skin disorder characterized by congenital alopecia and palmoplantar hyperkeratosis. It is usually associated with cataracts, progressive sclerodactyly and pseudo-ainhum.To date, autosomal recessive PPK-CA has been reported in two families (seven affected individuals). An additional sporadic patient was likely affected by the same condition.Similarly to the dominant variant, autosomal recessive PPK-
synonyms: Wallis syndrome

name: Secondary yaws
definition: Secondary yaws results from lymphatic and haematogenous spread of Treponema pallidum subsp. pertenue spirochaetes from the initial inoculation site and appears from a few weeks to 2 years after the primary infection. The commonest initial symptoms are non-specific and include arthralgia and malaise. Secondary skin lesions consist of multiple papules and nodules similar to the initial lesion but smaller. name: Oculo-dento-digital dysplasia
definition: Oculodentodigital dysplasia (ODDD) is a developmental anomalies syndrome characterised by craniofacial, neurologic, limb and ocular abnormalities. It is caused by heterozygous mutations in the GJA1 gene (6q22-q23), which encodes the gap junction protein connexin 43 (Cx43).

name: Metastatic carcinoma involving skin
definition: Metastatic carcinoma may invade the skin may arise as a result of direct spread from an underlying malignant neoplasm, most commonly carcinoma of the breast, or as the result of lymphatic or haematogenous spread from a distant site. Appearances vary considerably from discrete tumour nodules, which may ulcerate, to diffuse dermal infiltration with secondary changes due to disturbances of vascular or lymphatic drainage.

name: Infantile myofibroma or myofibromatosis
definition: Infantile myofibromatosis is an unusual mesenchymal disorder characterised by the proliferation of tumours in the skin, muscle, bone, and viscera. Although it is a rare condition, it is the most common fibrous tumour of infancy. There are two distinguishable types of infantile myofibromatosis\: the solitary type, defined by the presence of one nodule in the skin, muscle, bone or subcutaneous tissue, and the multicentric type, which can be further divided into two sub-types. name: BCG granuloma
definition: A localised granulomatous plaque at the site of BCG immunization \[Dermatology TAG\]

name: Mal de Meleda
definition: Onset is in early childhood, and the development of hyperkeratosis is preceded by erythema. Patches of waxy ivory–yellow hyperkeratosis extend across the whole palms and soles, and on to the dorsal surfaces of hands and feet. Similar lesions of knees and elbows may develop. The erythematous component often persists in central palms and soles, with hyperhidrotic maceration and malodour.

Limb-mammary syndrome
definition: Limb-mammary syndrome (LMS) is a rare disease belonging to the group of ectodermal dysplasias and characterised by severe hand and/or foot anomalies, and hypoplasia/aplasia of the mammary gland and nipple.

name: Orf
definition: Orf is a virus infection of the skin contracted from sheep and goats. Orf is caused by a parapox virus which infects mainly young lambs and goats. Human lesions are caused by direct inoculation of infected material. Orf is not uncommon among sheep farmers, shearers, freezing workers, vets and farmers' wives or their children who bottle-feed lambs. They occur most commonly on the fingers, hands or forearms but can appear on the face.

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy Type IE
definition: HASN type IE is an autosomal dominant condition due to mutations in the DNMT1 gene. It presents in child or adulthood with severe sensory loss, ulceromutilation, sensorineural hearing loss, early onset dementia and no autonomic symptoms.
synonyms: HSAN1E - [Hereditary sensory autonomic neuropathy Type IE]

name: Rash localised
definition: A very non-specific term to denote a localised acquired visible alteration of the skin from normal in situations where a more precise description or diagnosis cannot be made. If a diagnosis cannot be made then a choice from the classes Acute skin eruption of uncertain or unspecified nature and Chronic skin disorder of uncertain or unspecified nature is preferred.

name: Unsatisfactory surgical scar of skin
definition: A surgical skin scar with a poor functional or cosmetic outcome.
synonyms: unsatisfactory surgical scar of skin NOS

name: Candida otomycosis
definition: Infection of the external auditory canal with Candida yeasts, especially Candida parapsilosis. The infection may present with whitish greasy debris in, or discharge from the external auditory canal, or with erythema, oedema and pain. Candida otomycosis is less common than otomycosis due to Aspergillus. Chronic infection may be associated with perforation of the eardrum.
synonyms: Candida otitis externa | Candidosis of external ear | Otomycosis due to Candida | candidal otitis externa | candidal otomycosis | otitis externa candida | otitis externa in candidiasis

name: Cheek-biting
definition: Cheek-biting is a common obsessive-compulsive disorder characterised by compulsive biting or chewing the insides of the cheeks.
synonyms: Morsicatio buccarum | chronic cheek biting | chronic cheek chewing

name: Lipodermatosclerosis
definition: Lipodermatosclerosis is a form of panniculitis of the lower legs that develops in the context of venous insufficiency, giving rise to features that include oedema, erythema, hyperpigmentation and induration. In the acute phase tenderness, erythema and oedema predominate and may mimic cellulitis. As the condition becomes chronic, post-inflammatory pigmentation, fibrosis and lymphoedema predominate, sometimes resulting in the lower leg assuming an “inverted champagne bottle” appearance.
synonyms: Lower limb sclerosing panniculitis | chronic panniculitis with lipomembranous changes | hypodermitis sclerodermiformis | sclerosing panniculitis

Cold-induced sweating syndrome
definition: Cold-induced sweating syndrome is characterised by profuse sweating (involving the chest, face, arms and trunk) induced by cold ambient temperature.

name: Squamous cell carcinoma of penis
definition: A squamous cell carcinoma arising from the penis. It occurs chiefly in the squamous epithelium of the glans, coronal sulcus, and foreskin. Etiologic factors include phimosis, lichen sclerosus, smoking, ultraviolet irradiation, history of warts or condylomas, and lack of circumcision. Human papilloma virus is present in a subset of penile squamous cell carcinomas. name: Linear atrophoderma of Moulin
definition: Linear atrophoderma of Moulin is a distinctive disease pattern characterised by hyperpigmented atrophoderma following the lines of Blaschko, presenting usually in childhood or adolescence without preceding inflammation or subsequent induration or scleroderma.
synonyms: Atrophoderma of Moulin

name: Fetal varicella syndrome
definition: fetal varicella syndrome occurs following maternal varicella infection, typically between the 13th and 20th week of pregnancy. Transplacental transfer of Varicella Zoster virus results in fetal viraemia and subsequent cutaneous dissemination. Fetal varicella syndrome is characterised by low birth weight and a wide range of abnormalities involving the skin, central nervous system, eyes, skeleton, gastrointestinal tract and genito-urinary system. Amongst the manifold abnormalities which may occur
synonyms: Varicella embryopathy | varicella embryopathy

name: Morphoea profunda
definition: Morphoea profunda is an uncommon panniculitic form of morphoea in which there is no overlying involvement of the epidermis, cutaneous adnexa or dermis. Clinically, it presents with ill-defined indurated subcutaneous nodules or plaques. It is more frequent in females than in males and has a predilection for the shoulders, upper arms, and trunk.

name: Lethal restrictive dermopathy
definition: Lethal tight skin contracture syndrome is an inherited syndrome characterised by the skin being drawn tightly over the face causing a narrow, pinched nose, small mouth, limited jaw mobility, and entropion. Severe and constant intrauterine growth retardation is observed as well as multiple joint contractures, and rigid and tense skin is easily eroded with fissures in the axilla and neck. The major manifestations are the result of restricted in utero movements secondary to defective skin.
synonyms: Hyperkeratosis-contracture syndrome | Tight skin contracture syndrome, lethal | hyperkeratosis-contracture syndrome | Infantile restrictive dermopathy | lethal restrictive dermopathy | Lethal tight skin contracture syndrome | tight skin contracture syndrome

name: Primary hypertrophic osteoarthropathy
definition: Primary hypertrophic osteoarthropathy (PHO) is a clinically heterogeneous familial disorder characterised by digital clubbing and osteoarthropathy associated with mutations in the HPGD gene. Variable features include pachydermia in pachydermoperiostosis and delayed closure of the fontanelles in cranio-osteoarthropathy.

name: Burning scrotum
definition: Burning scrotum or red scrotum syndrome is a chronic condition of incompletely understood aetiology consisting of persistent scrotal erythema, burning discomfort and hyperalgesia. Typically it is resistant to any form of treatment.
synonyms: Red scrotum syndrome | Scrotodynia

name: Angiokeratoma circumscriptum
definition: Angiokeratoma circumscriptum is characterised by aggregates of hyperkeratotic, erythematous to violaceous papules that may later become verrucous and coalesce to form plaques. Females are commonly affected, with the male-to-female ratio being 1\:3. It may be either congenital or acquired and although many cases present at birth, some do not develop until childhood or adulthood. Common sites are the leg, foot, thigh and buttock. name: Neurosyphilis
definition: A disease of the brain or spinal cord caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum pallidum. This disease is characterised by four different forms\: meningovascular, tabes dorsalis, general paresis, or may be asymptomatic. Clinical signs normally manifest approximately 4-25 years after initial infection. Transmission is commonly by sexual contact.

name: Pachydermoperiostosis
definition: Pachydermoperiostosis is a form of primary hypertrophic osteoarthropathy characterised by digital clubbing, pachydermia and subperiosteal new bone formation associated with pain, polyarthritis, cutis verticis gyrata, seborrhoea and hyperhidrosis. It is more prevalent in males and is usually first evident during adolescence. It is associated with mutations in the HPGD gene.
synonyms: Touraine-Solente-Gole syndrome | POH | primary hypertrophic osteoathropathy

name: Scabies
definition: A highly contagious infestation of the skin by the mite Sarcoptes scabiei var. hominis. It may result in epidemics when introduced into institutions such as schools and nursing homes. The mites burrow into the skin, favouring the extremities, genitalia and, in infants, the axillae. The characteristic widespread intensely pruritic papulovesicular rash results largely from the host response rather than directly to burrowing by mites. Where such a response is absent as in immunosuppressed or debili
synonyms: infestation by Sarcoptes scabiei var. hominis | sarcoptic itch | scabies NOS

definition: Irritant contact dermatitis affecting skin of scalp.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 4 of trochanter
synonyms: stage 4 decubitus ulcer of trochanter

name: Miscellaneous specified forms of psoriasis
definition: This class incorporates a number of important clinical subtypes of psoriasis with particular triggers or clinical presentations.

name: Meningitis due to Treponema pallidum
definition: This is an infectious disease caused by the inflammation of the protective membranes covering the brain and spinal cord known as the meninges. It develops in response to a bacterial infection of the cerebrospinal fluid by Treponema pallidum.
synonyms: Meningeal syphilis

name: Evans syndrome
definition: Evans syndrome is characterised by the association of autoimmune haemolytic anaemia with another haematological anomaly. The thrombocytopaenia may precede, occur concurrently with, or secondary to the autoimmune haemolytic anaemia.

name: Scorbutic purpura
definition: Scorbutic purpura is a cutaneous haemorrhagic manifestation of scurvy with petechial exanthema and ecchymosis at pressure points. They are secondary to defective formation of connective tissues and collagen in skin and blood vessels, leading to their fragility.
synonyms: Purpura and bleeding due to scurvy | Purpura and bleeding due to vitamin C deficiency

Dermatitis or eczema of external ear
definition: Dermatitis and eczema affecting the skin of the external auditory canal, pinna and retroauricular sulcus. It is often complicated by or is a complication of infection by organisms including Staphylococcus aureus, Candida spp. and Pseudomonas spp. It may be a component of constitutional eczema (seborrhoeic and atopic) and is frequently complicated by contact with irritants or by the development of contact allergy.

name: Chronic multifocal oral candidosis
definition: A form of chronic oral candidosis seen typically in male smokers without other predisposing factors for Candida infection. It involves multiple oral sites with angular cheilitis, retrocommissural leukoplakia, median rhomboid glossitis and palatal lesions. Although it responds to appropriate antifungal therapy it frequently recurs.

name: Adult atopic eczema, prurigo pattern
definition: A variant of atopic eczema characterised by multiple itchy, often excoriated papules. It may continue into adulthood in children with this pattern but may also develop de novo in adult life, sometimes associated with pregnancy.
synonyms: Adult atopic dermatitis, prurigo pattern | Atopic prurigo of the adult

name: Klinefelter syndrome
definition: Klinefelter syndrome defines a group of chromosomal disorders in which there is at least one extra X chromosome compared with the normal 46,XY male karyotype. The effects on physical features and on physical and cognitive development increase with the number of extra X's, and each extra X is associated with an intelligence quotient (IQ) decrease of approximately 15-16 points, with language most affected, particularly expressive language skills.
synonyms: klinefelter syndrome NOS | klinefelter syndrome in males

name: Cellulocutaneous plague
definition: Cellulocutaneous plague is a zoonotic disease caused by Yersinia pestis (formerly known as Pasteurella pestis) involving the skin around the flea bite which transmitted the pathogen.

name: Giant cell granuloma, central
definition: A benign condition of the jaws. It is twice as likely to affect women and is more likely to occur in individuals between the age of 20 and 40. Central giant cell granulomas are more common in the mandible and often cross the midline.
synonyms: Central giant cell reparative granuloma of jaw | Giant cell granuloma, NOS

name: Collagenous and elastotic marginal plaques of the hands
definition: Collagenous and elastotic marginal plaques of the hands is an acquired dermatosis affecting predominantly the radial aspect of the index finger, first web space and ulnar aspect of the thumb on the dominant hand. A combination of chronic photodamage and repetitive mechanical trauma is thought to be responsible. Histologically haphazardly arranged thickened collagen bundles, basophilic elastotic material and calcium deposits are found.
synonyms: Degenerative collagenous plaques of the hands | Digital papular calcific elastosis | Keratoelastoidosis marginalis

name: Familial partial lipodystrophy

synonyms: Hereditary partial lipodystrophy | FPLD

name: Lymphoedema due to malignant infiltration
definition: Lymphoedema resulting from obstruction of draining lymphatics as a result of infiltration by malignant, usually metastatic cells.
synonyms: Lymphoedema secondary to malignant infiltration

name: Lichen planus and lichenoid reactions of oral mucosa
definition: Oral lichenoid reactions represent a common end point in response to extrinsic agents (drugs, allergens), altered self-antigens, or superantigens. Clinical presentation can vary from asymptomatic white reticular striae to painful erythema and erosions. Cutaneous and mucosal involvement of other sites is common. Although oral lichen planus is by definition idiopathic, oral lichenoid reactions may be caused by medications or exogenous agents such as cinnamates and other flavourings.

name: Neuropathic hypohidrosis
definition: Reduced or absent sweating attributable to injury to or degeneration of sympathetic nervous system pathways. Well-recognised causes are diabetes mellitus and leprosy.

name: Histoplasmosis due to Histoplasma duboisii
definition: This form of histoplasmosis is endemic to Sub-Saharan Africa and is generally less virulent than histoplasmosis due to H. capsulatum, the classical form which occurs predominantly in tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas but is also seen in Africa and Asia. Otherwise known as African histoplasmosis, histoplasmosis due to Histoplasma duboisii usually involves the skin and subcutaneous tissue, lymph nodes and bones and rarely the lungs and other internal organs.
synonyms: African histoplasmosis | Histoplasmosis duboisii

name: Vitiligo of upper eyelid or periocular area
definition: This is a condition of the upper eyelid and periocular area that causes depigmentation of sections of skin. It occurs when melanocytes, the cells responsible for skin pigmentation, die or are unable to function. The cause of vitiligo is unknown, but research suggests that it may arise from autoimmune, genetic, oxidative stress, neural, or viral causes.

name: Propionic aciduria
definition: Propionic acidemia is an organic aciduria due to propionyl-CoA carboxylase deficiency characterised by onset of manifestations soon after birth including ketoacidotic coma, hyperammonemia and convulsions. Apart from acute metabolic decompensation, the major complications are neurological disorders (central grey nuclei), cardiomyopathies, and acute pancreatitis.

name: Localised lipoatrophy due to injected drug
definition: Lipoatrophy resulting from injection of drug. The lipoatrophy may occur following a single injection, especially of depot corticosteroid preparations, or after repeated injection into the same site, especially with insulin.
