name: Fixed cutaneous sporotrichosis
definition: Cutaneous sporotrichosis which remains localised to the area of inoculation.
cn_name: 固定型皮肤孢子丝菌病

name: Chronic cicatrizing conjunctivitis, ocular cicatricial pemphigoid
Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to systemic medicaments
Otitis externa
definition: Inflammation of the outer ear including the external ear canal, cartilages of the auricle, and the tympanic membrane.
synonyms: OE - [otitis externa] | external ear inflammation | external otitis | otitis externa NOS

name: Low-set ear
definition: The upper third of the pinna is not above a plane defined by the lateral canthi and the maximum convexity of the occiput.
synonyms: congenital malposition of external ear | melotia

name: Pruritus due to endocrine or metabolic disorder
definition: Both hyper- and hypothyroidism may result in pruritus. Polyneuropathy may be responsible for itch in diabetics.

name: Nodular acne
definition: Acne in which large inflammatory nodules and fluid-filled cystic swellings as well as more superficial lesions are present. Systemic therapy with antibiotics or retinoids is usually required.
synonyms: Cystic acne | Nodulocystic acne | Acne cystica | Cystic acne vulgaris

name: Cutaneous flap necrosis, total
definition: Complete necrosis of a surgical skin flap

name: Infected epidermoid cyst
definition: An epidermoid cyst which has become secondarily infected by, most commonly, Staphylococcus aureus. It manifests as pain, swelling and erythema of a preexisting cyst and is predisposed to rupture.
synonyms: Infected epidermal inclusion cyst | Infected sebaceous cyst

name: Neonatal cephalic pustulosis
definition: Neonatal cephalic pustulosis is characterised by erythematous papules pustules located especially on the cheeks but also affecting the chin, eyelids, neck and upper chest. Comedones are not usually seen. It has been postulated that it is due to an inflammatory reaction to lipophilic Malassezia yeasts which can proliferate in infants with high sebum production. It is a self-limiting disorder which usually heals without scarring in 1–3 months.
synonyms: Malassezia pustulosis of newborn | neonatal acne

name: Aplasia cutis - myopia
definition: Aplasia cutis - myopia syndrome is characterised by the association of aplasia cutis congenita with high myopia, congenital nystagmus and cone-rod dysfunction.

name: Mansonelliasis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the parasitic worm Mansonella. This disease is characterised by pruritus, dermal pigmentary changes, fever, or lymphadenopathy, or may be asymptomatic. Transmission is through the bite of an infected midge (genus Culicoides) or blackfly (genus Simulium). Confirmation is by identification of Mansonella microfilariae in a skin or blood sample.
synonyms: Mansonellosis | mansonella infestation

name: Vulvovaginal candidosis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the fungi Candida. This disease is characterised by genital itching, burning, or vaginal discharge. Transmission is by endogenous spread, or sexual contact. Confirmation is commonly by identification of Candida in a vaginal swab.
synonyms: candida vulvovaginitis | candidal vaginitis | candidal vulvovaginitis | vaginal candidiasis | vulvovaginal candidiasis | vulvovaginal thrush

name: Dermatomyositis
definition: Dermatomyositis is an inflammatory myopathy, showing progressive, symmetrical muscle weakness, low muscle endurance, and cutaneous manifestations such as Gottron’s papules, heliotrope rash, shawl sign, V-sign and mechanic’s hand. Internal organ manifestations such as interstitial pneumonia (pneumonitis) and myocarditis sometimes develop. The skin rash may precede the muscle symptoms and may be the only clinical sign of dermatomyositis in some patients (clinically, amyopathic dermatomyositis).
synonyms: Petges-Clejat syndrome | Amyopathic dermatomyositis | dermatopolymyositis | Polymyositis with skin involvement

name: Contact dermatitis of scalp
definition: Contact dermatitis not specified as irritant or allergic affecting the scalp.

name: Early or late hypertrophic periostitis of yaws
synonyms: hypertrophic periostitis of yaws

name: Other specified form of acquired hypomelanosis
Subcutaneous infections due to dematiaceous fungi
Burn of face except eye or ocular adnexa, deep full thickness or complex burn
synonyms: Burn of face except eye and adnexa, fourth degree
cn_name: 面部（除外眼或眼附属器）深全层皮肤烧伤或复合烧伤

name: De Grouchy syndrome
definition: Monosomy 18p refers to a chromosomal disorder resulting from the deletion of all or part of the short arm of chromosome 18. The dysmorphic syndrome is very moderate and non-specific. The main clinical features are short stature, round face with short philtrum, palpebral ptosis and large ears with detached pinnae. Intellectual deficiency is mild to moderate. A small subset of patients, about 10 to 15 percent of cases, present with severe brain/facial malformations evocative of holoprosencephaly s
synonyms: 18p deletion syndrome

name: Subacute lichen planus
definition: The commonest form of lichen planus affecting the skin. It may be limited to a few papules or plaques but may be widespread. It may continue to extend over months and may remain active over several years.

name: Digital myxoid pseudocyst
definition: Digital myxoid cysts (DMCs) are benign ganglion cysts of the digits, which typically present as a small dome-shaped, often translucent papule on the dorsum of the terminal phalanx and/or as longitudinal \"guttering\" of the nail plate which is focally compressed by the cyst as it develops from the underlying nail matrix. In the majority of cases a stalk connecting the cyst with the adjacent distal interphalangeal joint can be demonstrated, accounting for the alternative names of digital ganglion
synonyms: Digital ganglion cyst | Digital mucous cyst | Digital myxoid cyst | Digital synovial cyst | Synovial cyst of digit | cutaneous myxoid cust | digital myxoid cyst

name: Werner syndrome
definition: Werner syndrome (WS) is a rare inherited syndrome characterised by premature aging with an onset in the third decade of life and with cardinal clinical features including\: bilateral cataracts, short stature, greying and thinning of scalp hair, characteristic skin pathologies and premature onset of additional age-related disorders.
synonyms: Pangeria | progeria of the adult

name: Lacrimo-auriculo-dento-digital syndrome
definition: LADD is an acronym for Lacrymo-Auriculo-Dento-Digital syndrome, characterised by obstruction of the nasal lacrimal ducts that can lead to epiphora, and chronic conjunctivitis due to alacrymia. Aplasia or hypoplasia of the salivary glands may also be present, leading to dry mouth and early onset of severe dental caries.

name: Vocal cord varices
synonyms: vocal cord; varix

name: Zoster keratoconjunctivitis
synonyms: Herpes zoster keratoconjunctivitis | Keratoconjunctivitis due to zoster

name: Oculocutaneous albinism type 1B
definition: OCA-1B is due to partial absence of TYR with some enzymatic production of tyrosinase and a less severe clinical phenotype than OCA-1A.
synonyms: OCA1B - [Oculocutaneous albinism type 1B]

name: Contact dermatitis of external ear
definition: Contact dermatitis of external ear may be due to irritants or allergy. Antimicrobial aural preparations are common causes of allergic contact dermatitis.

name: Cutaneous markers of disorders of the kidney or urinary tract
definition: Cutaneous disorders which are associated with disorders of the kidney and urinary tract and which may assist in the diagnosis of the latter.

name: Erosive lichen planus of penis
definition: An uncommon variant of lichen planus in which painful erosions form on the glans penis. It is the male counterpart of erosive lichen planus of the vulva and may also be associated with erosive oral lichen planus.

name: Poison-bun sponge dermatitis
synonyms: Poison-bun sponge dermatitis (Fibulila spp.)

name: Hypertrophic lichen planus of vulva
definition: Hypertrophic lichen planus affecting vulval skin.

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum
definition: Xeroderma pigmentosum (XP) is a rare genodermatosis characterised by extreme sensitivity to ultraviolet (UV)-induced changes in the skin and eyes, and multiple skin cancers. It is subdivided into 8 complementation groups, according to the affected gene\: XPA to XPG, and XP variant (XPV). The severity of the clinical manifestations and the age of onset are extremely variable and are in part dependent on exposure to sunlight and the complementation group.
synonyms: XP - [xeroderma pigmentosum] | atrophoderma pigmentosum | XP

name: Streptococcal necrotising fasciitis
synonyms: Necrotising fasciitis type 2
cn_name: 链球菌性坏死性筋膜炎

name: Abscess of lower eyelid
definition: This is a collection of pus (neutrophils) that has accumulated within a tissue because of an inflammatory process in response to either an infectious process (usually caused by bacteria or parasites) or other foreign material, of the lower eyelid.

name: Burn of hip or leg, except ankle or foot, epidermal burn
synonyms: Burn of hip and leg, except ankle or foot, first degree
cn_name: 臀或腿（除外踝或足）表皮烧伤

name: Congenital erythropoietic porphyria
definition: Congenital erythropoietic porphyria, or Günther disease, is a rare autosomal recessive disorder due to mutations in the gene encoding uroporphyrinogen III synthase. This results in the accumulation of abnormal porphyrins in erythrocytes and manifests as very severe and mutilating photosensitivity.
synonyms: Günther disease | Hereditary erythropoietic porphyria | Uroporphyrinogen III synthase deficiency | CEP | gunther disease

name: Acanthoma fissuratum
definition: A distinctive discrete flesh-coloured papule or plaque arising in the retroauricular sulcus or on the side of the nose as a result of pressure and friction from ill-fitting spectacles. The lesion is often centrally grooved where it is in contact with the spectacle frame. It is often painful.
synonyms: Granuloma fissuratum | Spectacle-frame acanthoma

name: Pressure ulceration grade 1 of upper limb
synonyms: stage 1 decubitus ulcer of upper limb

name: Acanthotic seborrhoeic keratosis
definition: This is the archetypal seborrhoeic keratosis and is composed of broad columns or sheets of basaloid or squamoid cells with intervening horn cysts. There may be varying degrees of hyperkeratosis, papillomatosis and acanthosis.

name: Histoplasmosis dacryoadenitis
definition: This is a disease caused by the fungus Histoplasma capsulatum. This diagnosis is with inflammation of the lacrimal glands (the tear-producing glands).

name: Erythema multiforme provoked by systemic fungal infection
definition: Erythema multiforme provoked by acute systemic fungal infection including pulmonary histoplasmosis and coccidioidomycosis.

name: Classical homocystinuria
definition: Classical homocystinuria due to cystathionine beta-synthase (CbS) deficiency is an autosomal recessively inherited disorder of methionine metabolism characterised by the multiple involvement of the eye (ectopia lentis), skeleton genu valgum and pes cavus), central nervous system intellectual deficit, psychiatric illness), and vascular system (thromboembolism).
synonyms: CBS - [Cystathionine beta-synthase] deficiency | Cystathionine beta-synthase deficiency | Homocystinuria due to cystathionine beta-synthase deficiency | cystathionine synthase deficiency | homocystine metabolic disorder

name: Phakomatosis caesiomarmorata
synonyms: Phakomatosis pigmentovascularis type 5

name: Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to preservatives or biocides
Non-allergic contact urticaria
definition: Non-allergic contact urticaria is a form of urticaria caused by cutaneous or mucosal contact with a substance or substances capable of inducing the release of vasoactive mediators from mast cells or having a direct effect on cutaneous vasculature without involving the immune system.

name: Actinic cheilitis
definition: Actinic cheilitis results from chronic exposure of the lower lip vermilion to ultraviolet solar radiation. Outdoor workers with fair skin who live in tropical or subtropical areas are most at risk. The condition presents as diffuse or circumscribed scaling and variable thickening of the epithelium, which is poorly protected from the harmful effects of UV radiation. Progression to invasive squamous cell carcinoma may occur.
synonyms: actinic cheilosis

name: Lichenoid mucositis
definition: Oral lichenoid mucositis is a term describing clinicopathological features of the oral mucosa which represent a common end point in response to extrinsic agents such as drugs or contact allergens, or to presumed altered self-antigens as in lichen planus. Clinical presentation can vary from asymptomatic white reticular striae to painful erythema and erosions. Idiopathic lichen planus cannot always be distinguished from lichenoid reactions to external agents and in such circumstances it is appropr

name: Giant condyloma acuminatum of Buschke and Löwenstein involving penis
Sequelae of frostbite of head or neck
Facial ectodermal dysplasia
definition: A rare, genetic, multiple congenital anomalies/dysmorphic syndrome characterized by craniofacial dysmorphism (including an abnormal skull shape, hypertelorism, downslanting palpebral fissures, epicanthal folds, low-set ears, depressed nasal bridge, micrognathia), short stature, ectodermal anomalies (such as sparse eyebrows, eyelashes, and scalp hair, hypolastic toenails), developmental delay, and intellectual disability. Additional features may include cerebral/cerebellar malformations and mild

name: Lupus panniculitis
definition: Lupus panniculitis is characterised by a destructive inflammation of subcutaneous fat. This manifests itself clinically as indurated plaques which resolve with localised fat atrophy. Depending on the intensity of the inflammation a patient may first present with atrophy rather than induration. The overlying skin may show changes of chronic cutaneous lupus erythematosus. The face, upper arms, upper trunk, breasts, buttocks and thighs are most commonly affected.
synonyms: Lupus profundus | Subcutaneous lupus erythematosus | lupus panniculitis | subcutaneous lupus erythematosus

name: Acute papular skin eruption
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists of multiple skin papules. A wide variety of infectious and inflammatory skin disorders may present in this way. Examples include guttate psoriasis, lichen planus, pityriasis lichenoides, insect bites, scabies and secondary syphilis.

name: Kerion
definition: Kerion results from a severe host inflammatory response to dermatophyte infection of the hair follicles of the scalp or beard. It typically presents as a single painful, severely inflammatory, suppurating boggy mass and is most commonly a reaction to a zoophilic dermatophyte infection especially Trichophyton verrucosum (cattle ringworm) or Trichophyton mentagrophytes.

name: Axillary hyperhidrosis
definition: Excessive axillary sweating, sometimes in response to emotional stress but often persistent and disabling.
synonyms: Sweaty armpits

name: Lentigo maligna
definition: An atypical proliferation of atypical melanocytes in the dermal-epidermal junction, without infiltration of the papillary or reticular dermis. The melanocytic proliferation is associated with actinic damage and epidermal atrophy. It usually occurs in the sun-exposed skin of elderly people. It is a form of melanoma in situ and in approximately 5% of cases it progresses to lentigo maligna melanoma.
synonyms: Hutchinson melanotic freckle

name: Pigmented skin lesion of uncertain nature, neck
definition: This denotes the presence of a pigmented skin lesion on the neck but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Ganglion of yaws
synonyms: yaws ganglion

name: Xanthoma due to specified disorder of lipid metabolism
definition: Lipid accumulations in the skin and soft tissues resulting from disordered lipid metabolism.

name: Xerostomia due to other specified salivary gland disorder
definition: Xerostomia attributable to abnormalities of salivary glands other than from autoimmune disease or radiation damage.

name: Actinomycetoma due to Streptomyces species
definition: Actinomycetoma due to Streptomyces species. These are the predominant organisms responsible for actinomycetoma in Sudan and Saudi Arabia but are also an important cause of mycetoma elsewhere in Africa and Asia.

name: Normal nails
definition: The absence of any demonstrable abnormality of the nails.

name: Non-uraemic calcific arteriolopathy
definition: Calcific arteriolopathy in the absence of kidney disease or renal transplantation. Associations include primary hyperparathyroidism, alcoholic liver disease and non-organ-specific systemic autoimmune disorders.
synonyms: Calciphylaxis not associated with uraemia | Non-uraemic calciphylaxis

name: Xanthoma in association with primary chylomicronaemia
definition: Typically eruptive xanthomas due to accumulation of chylomicrons in the circulation as a consequence of defective lipoprotein lipase (chylomicron retention disease).
synonyms: Xanthoma in association with chylomicron retention disease

name: Pediculosis corporis
definition: A condition of the skin, caused by an infection with the parasite Pediculus humanus corporis. This condition is characterised by pruritus which may lead to sores or thickened discoloured skin. Transmission is by direct or indirect contact with an infected individual or animal. Confirmation is by identification of Pediculus humanus corporis eggs or Pediculus humanus corporis.
synonyms: Body lice | Body-louse infestation | Infestation by Pediculus corporis | clothing lice | pediculosis corporis

name: Pthiriasis of eyelashes
definition: Infestation of the eyelashes by the crab louse, Pthirus pubis. This commonly affects children who acquire the infestation from close contact with adults with pthiriasis pubis.
synonyms: Crab-louse infestation of eyelashes | Phthiriasis of eyelashes | Phthiriasis palpebrarum

name: Bee sting
definition: Stings by bees, the venom of which causes a local toxic reaction in the unsensitised but may result in anaphylaxis in those previously sensitized. The severity of the reaction depends on the dose of venom and degree of previous sensitisation.

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to industrial biocides, cutting oils or disinfectants
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to industrial biocides, cutting oils and disinfectants (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to industrial biocides, cutting oils and disinfectants).

name: EDA-ID syndrome
definition: Hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia with immunodeficiency is characterised by the malformation of ectodermal structures such as skin, hair, teeth and sweat glands, and associated with immunodeficiency.
synonyms: EDA-ID syndrome (MIM 300291)

name: Parasitic infestation of eyelashes by Pthirus pubis
definition: Infestation of eyelashes by the pubic crab louse, Pthirus pubis. This is commonly acquired by children from contact with an adult with active infestation of pubic hair but may also be acquired by adults.
synonyms: Parasitic infestation of eyelid in phthiriasis | Phthyriasis of eyelids | Pthyriasis of eyelashes

name: Lichen simplex of nape of neck
definition: Circumscribed pruritic lichenification of nape of neck.
synonyms: Lichen nuchae

name: Generalised pustular psoriasis of pregnancy
definition: Pregnancy appears to be a risk factor for developing generalised pustular psoriasis. It is clinically indistinguishable from other forms but management is more difficult in view of the more limited options for treatment.
synonyms: Impetigo herpetiformis

name: Acquired disorders of eyelashes
synonyms: acquired disorders of eyelashes NOS

name: Pyoderma
synonyms: pyodermia

name: Cutaneous insect bite reactions
definition: Skin reactions to known or presumed insect bites. Commonly the nature of the insect responsible is unknown.
synonyms: bug bite | insect bite hypersensitivity NOS

name: Lipoatrophic panniculitis
definition: Lipoatrophic panniculitis is a rare, possibly autoimmune disease affecting predominantly children. It manifests as tender erythematous subcutaneous nodules and plaques which are followed by circumferential bands of lipoatrophy, most commonly around the ankles.

name: Hereditary haemochromatosis
definition: This is a hereditary disease characterised by excessive intestinal absorption of dietary iron resulting in a pathological increase in total body iron stores.

name: Severe inflammatory acne
definition: Intensely inflammatory acne which may be acute (acne fulminans) or subacute and chronic (acne conglobata).

name: Abnormal vascular reactivity to cold
definition: Skin disorders in which low absolute temperature or a reduction in temperature bring about abnormal vascular responses.

name: Pachyonychia congenita
definition: Pachyonychia congenita is a rare genodermatosis predominantly featuring painful palmoplantar keratoderma, thickened nails, cysts and whitish oral mucosa.

name: Contact urticaria
definition: Urticaria resulting from skin or mucosal contact with a substance or substances capable of inducing wealing either by immunological or by non-immunological means.
synonyms: contact urticaria syndrome | immediate contact reaction | protein contact dermatitis

name: Nested seborrhoeic keratosis
definition: Nested (or clonal) seborrhoeic keratosis is a histopathologically distinct variant of seborrhoeic keratosis characterised by intraepidermal nests of basaloid cells (the Borst-Jadassohn phenomenon).
synonyms: Clonal seborrhoeic keratosis

name: Pemphigus vegetans
definition: Pemphigus vegetans is an uncommon form of pemphigus vulgaris. Onset occurs in middle age. Autoantibodies directed against desmosome components, chiefly desmoglein 3, cause intraepidermal clefting. The disease presents with superficial blistering usually of the flexures evolving into vegetating masses which become which eventually become dry, hyperkeratotic and fissured (Neumann type) or pustules that soon progress to vegetating plaques (Hallopeau type). Oral lesions are always present.

name: Unsatisfactory outcome from cosmetic laser surgery
definition: The outcome from an intervention using lasers designed to improve cosmetic appearance which is considered by the practitioner who performed the procedure to be less satisfactory than anticipated.
cn_name: 美容激光手术效果不满意

name: Disorders of skin colour
definition: This group includes not only abnormalities of melanin pigmentation (e.g. vitiligo and melasma) but also skin colour changes due to other pigments (e.g. carotenaemia and argyria).

name: Scarring alopecia due to lepromatous leprosy
definition: Scarring alopecia due to diffuse active or past skin infiltration by Mycobacterium leprae bacilli
synonyms: Scarring alopecia secondary to lepromatous leprosy

name: Occupational contact dermatitis
definition: Inflammatory skin reaction caused by contact with allergens, irritants, photoallergens or phototoxic substances encountered at work.
synonyms: industrial dermatitis | occupational dermatitis | occupational eczema | Occupational allergic contact dermatitis | Occupational eczema

name: Anagen effluvium due to toxins or poisons
definition: Anagen effluvium due most commonly to the toxic effects of heavy metals such as arsenic.

name: Immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome
definition: This is a condition seen in some cases of AIDS or immunosuppression, in which the immune system begins to recover, but then responds to a previously acquired opportunistic infection with an overwhelming inflammatory response that paradoxically makes the symptoms of infection worse.
synonyms: IRIS - [immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome] | Immune reconstitution syndrome

name: Splenic varices
synonyms: spleen varix | spleen varix rupture | varices of spleen

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, trunk
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, trunk

name: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with pyloric atresia
definition: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with pyloric atresia (EBS-PA) is an autosomal recessive basal subtype of EBS due to mutations in the PLEC gene encoding plectin. It is characterised by generalised severe blistering with widespread congenital absence of skin and pyloric atresia.
synonyms: EBS - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex] with pyloric atresia | EBS-PA - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex with pyloric atresia] | EBS-PA

name: Macrocystic lymphatic malformation
definition: A condition caused by failure of the lymphatic system to correctly develop during the antenatal period. This condition is characterised by large, soft, smooth clear masses under normal or bluish skin. This condition may be associated with cellulitis, bleeding within the malformation, pain, or leakage of lymphatic fluid internally.
synonyms: Cavernous lymphangioma | Cavernous lymphatic malformation | Macrocystic lymphangioma | cavernous lymphangioma | LM | lymphangioma | lymphangioma simplex | cystic hygroma

name: Ovarian varices
synonyms: ovarian varicocele | varix of ovary

name: Neonatal streptococcal pyoderma
definition: Neonatal skin infection by Streptococcus pyogenes.

name: Pityriasis rubra pilaris type IV, circumscribed juvenile
definition: This form of pityriasis rubra pilaris presents in prepubertal children with well circumscribed plaques of erythema and follicular hyperkeratosis on the elbows and knees. Palmoplantar keratoderma may be associated. It may remit during adolescence.

name: Erythropoietic protoporphyria due to gain of function of erythroid-specific aminolevulinic acid synthase 2
synonyms: EPP due to gain of function of erythroid-specific aminolevulinic acid synthase 3 | X-linked dominant protoporphyria | X-linked protoporphyria | XLDPP - [X-linked dominant protoporphyria] | XLP - [X-linked protoporphyria] | XLDPP

name: Cerebellar ataxia - ectodermal dysplasia
definition: Cerebellar ataxia - ectodermal dysplasia is a very rare disease, characterised by hypodontia and sparse hair in combination with cerebellar ataxia and normal intelligence. Imaging demonstrates a cerebellar atrophy.

name: Cutaneous allergic or hypersensitivity reactions to Hymenoptera venom
definition: Cutaneous reactions to Hymenoptera venom are hypersensitivity reactions classified into normal local reactions and large local reactions. Large local reaction is defined as a swelling exceeding a diameter of 10 cm which lasts longer than 24 h; blisters may rarely be present.
synonyms: Local cutaneous reaction | local large reaction
cn_name: 膜翅目毒液引起的皮肤过敏或超敏反应

name: Acquired thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura due to anti-ADAMTS 13 antibodies
definition: This is an acquired, rare disorder of the blood-coagulation system, causing extensive microscopic clots to form in the small blood vessels throughout the body. This diagnosis is due to ADAMTS 13 antibodies.

name: Focal primary cutaneous mucinosis
definition: Forms of primary cutaneous mucinosis in which there are focal accumulations of mucin in the dermis.

name: Specified developmental anomalies affecting the skin
cn_name: 累及皮肤的特指发育异常

name: Flegel disease
definition: Flegel disease is an inherited autosomal dominant disorder and is characterised by the development in early adult life of a profusion of 2–3 mm diameter keratotic papules with discrete irregular margins over the calves and the extensor surfaces of the ankles. Irritation may be severe and response to any form of treatment is poor.
synonyms: hyperkeratosis lenticularis perstans

name: Superficial subcutaneous lipoma
definition: A benign well-circumscribed mesenchymal neoplasm composed of mature adipocytes and commonly known as a lipoma.

name: Infantile atopic eczema, impetiginised
definition: Impetiginized infantile atopic eczema is due to superinfection with Staphylococcus aureus. It manifests as weeping and crusted patches of eczema.
synonyms: Infantile atopic dermatitis, impetiginised | Infected atopic eczema of the infant

name: Plaque morphoea
definition: The commonest form of morphoea which presents as indurated waxy plaques, often with a violaceous border and commonly affecting the trunk, especially in the submammary folds and around the waist. The cause is unknown. It is commoner in women than men.
synonyms: Circumscribed scleroderma | localised scleroderma | plaque morphea | circumscribed scleroderma | localized scleroderma

name: Scrotal calcinosis
definition: Scrotal calcinosis is an uncommon condition characterised by the appearance in adolescence or early adult life of multiple calcified dermal nodules within scrotal skin. Although some cases may be related to preexisting epidermoid cysts, the pathogenesis remains obscure.

name: Filariasis due to Brugia timori
definition: This is a parasitic disease (usually an infectious tropical disease) that is caused by thread-like nematodes (roundworms) belonging to the superfamily Filarioidea, also known as \"filariae\". This diagnosis is due to a human filarial parasitic nematode (roundworm) which causes the disease \"Timor filariasis.\"

name: Burn of forearm or elbow, epidermal burn
synonyms: Erythema of forearm or elbow

name: Ichthyosis Curth-Macklin
definition: Ichthyosis hystrix of Curth-Macklin is a rare type of keratinopathic ichthyosis that is characterised by the presence of severe hyperkeratotic lesions and palmoplantar keratoderma.
synonyms: Ichthyosis hystrix, Curth-Macklin type

name: Intercellular IgA dermatosis
definition: Intercellular IgA dermatosis is a chronic autoimmune skin disease characterised by blister formation on the skin. The exact causes of the disease are unknown but the disease is mediated by IgA class autoantibodies to desmosome components.
synonyms: IgA herpetiform pemphigus | IgA pemphigus | IgA pemphigus foliaceus | Intercellular IgA vesiculopustular dermatosis | Intraepidermal neutrophilic IgA dermatosis

name: Lamellar ichthyosis
definition: Lamellar ichthyosis (LI) is a keratinization disorder characterised by the presence of large scales all over the body without significant erythroderma.
synonyms: Collodion baby | collodion fetus | ichthyosis congenita | ichthyosis congenita type 2 | LI | non-erythrodermic autosomal recessive lamellar ichthyosis

name: Asymptomatic vulvovaginal candidosis
definition: Asymptomatic colonization of the vulva and vagina by Candida yeasts.

name: Erythrodermic psoriasis
definition: Erythrodermic psoriasis is a severe generalised inflammatory form of psoriasis characterised by confluent intense erythema involving more than 90% of the skin surface. Erythrodermic psoriasis usually develops from preceding extensive, active plaque psoriasis but may arise de novo. Precipitating or trigger factors include withdrawal of systemic glucocorticosteroids, and, less frequently, abrupt discontinuation of methotrexate, phototherapy burns, or intercurrent infections. Patients may develop h

name: Lepromatous leprosy
definition: Lepromatous leprosy occurs in individuals with a poor or absent cell-mediated immune response to infection with Mycobacterium leprae. This allows the bacillae to multiply unhindered in skin and nerves (multibacillary leprosy). It is characterised by multiple poorly defined erythematous macules, papules, nodules and plaques which are distributed widely but usually symmetrically over the body. The face and limbs are sites of predilection. A symmetrical peripheral neuropathy of the hands and feet i
synonyms: Hansen disease, lepromatous

name: Self-inflicted skin disorders
definition: This heterogeneous group of disorders all result from self-inflicted skin injury or pathomimicry directly induced either by psychopathological behaviour or by intentional deception.

name: Infantile gluteal granulomata
definition: A complication of primary irritant napkin dermatitis characterised by the development of livid purple nodules on the convexities of the napkin area including not only the gluteal area but also the thighs and suprapubic skin. Use of potent topical corticosteroids is implicated in many cases.
synonyms: Granuloma gluteale infantum

name: IgG4-related sclerosing cholangitis
definition: Sclerosing cholangitis that occurs as a IgG4-related disease. This is distinct from primary sclerosing cholangitis.
synonyms: Cholangitis of IgG4-related sclerosing disease

name: Papuloerythroderma of Ofuji
definition: Papuloerythroderma of Ofuji is a distinctive dermatosis predominantly of elderly men manifested by an extensive eruption of pruritic, brownish-red, flat-topped papules which coalesce into sheeted erythema over the limbs and trunk with striking sparing of abdominal skin creases; the face and flexures tend to be spared. Histologically, there is a dense inflammatory infiltrate with prominent eosinophils. It is a regarded as a reaction pattern for which a cause is rarely identified, though it has be

name: HIV-associated pruritus
definition: Pruritus attributable to HIV infection where a more specific diagnosis such as HIV-associated papular pruritic eruption cannot be made.

name: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum canis
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum canis, the main natural hosts of which are cats and dogs. It typically causes an often inflammatory ectothrix infection of scalp hair.

name: Mucosulfatidosis
definition: Multiple sulfatase deficiency (MSD) or Austin's disease is an extremely rare lysosomal storage disease characterised by a deficiency of all sulfatases whether lysosomal or not. Clinically, the disease combines variable proportions of clinical signs of metachromatic leukodystrophy (arylsulfatase A deficiency), mucopolysaccharidoses (types II, IIIA, IIID and VI are due to sulfatase deficiencies) and X-linked ichthyosis (steroid-sulfatase deficiency).
synonyms: Austin disease | Juvenile sulfatidose, Austin type | Multiple sulfatase deficiency | austin disease | mucosulfatidosis

name: Dermal melanocytosis
definition: The presence at birth of functional melanocytes within the dermis. Most commonly this is as a result of incomplete migration of melanocytes to the epidermis as in lumbosacral dermal melanocytosis (Mongolian spot). Less commonly it is due to circumscribed hamartomatous proliferation of melanocytes in the dermis (e.g. Naevus of Ota).
synonyms: congenital dermal melanocytosis | mongolian spot

name: Burn of shoulder or arm, except wrist or hand
definition: Injury to the tissues of the shoulder and arm (except wrist and hand) caused by contact with, for example, heat, steam, chemicals, or electricity.
synonyms: burn and corrosion of shoulder and upper limb, except wrist and hand | burn of shouler or arm of unspecified degree
cn_name: 肩或臂（除外腕或手）烧伤

name: Xerosis cutis or asteatosis
definition: Dryness of the skin surface commonly due to defatting of the epidermis by excessive exposure to soaps and detergents or desiccation from prolonged exposure to low ambient humidity. It occurs most commonly in the elderly and is seen particularly on the lower legs. It is a major cause of pruritus in the elderly. In more severe cases the skin may become inflamed (asteatotic eczema).

name: Female pattern hair loss, androgenetic
definition: Androgen-mediated hair loss in women. It differs from male pattern hair loss not only in being generally less pronounced than in men but also by the fact that the normal frontal hair line is usually preserved. A quarter of women will develop clinically detectable pattern hair loss by the age of 70.
synonyms: female androgenetic alopecia

name: Acquired hypofibrinogenaemia
synonyms: acquired afibrinogenaemia | acquired fibrinogenopenia

name: Scorpion sting
definition: Envenomation resulting from a sting by any one of the many known species of scorpion. Clinical manifestations are usually localised pain and swelling but a bite by the North American bark scorpion, Centruroides sculpturatus, may cause systemic illness with tachypnoea, tachycardia, hypertension, weakness and muscle spasms.

name: Fissured tongue
synonyms: furrowed tongue | lingua plicata | plicated tongue | scrotal tongue

name: Eosinophilic enteritis due to Ancylostoma
definition: A disease of the gastrointestinal tract, caused by the parasitic worm Ancylostoma caninum for whom humans are accidental hosts. This disease is characterised by abdominal pain, diarrhoea, weight loss, or melaena and eosinophilia. This disease may also be asymptomatic. Transmission is by direct contact with larvae from soil or sand contaminated with dog or cat faeces (by percutaneous migration of larvae), or ingestion of larvae. Confirmation is commonly by identification of Ancylostoma caninum in

name: Tanning due to exposure to artificial sources of ultraviolet radiation
definition: Increased melanin pigmentation as a result of deliberate (sunbeds and tanning booths) or unintentional exposure to UV.

name: Irritant contact dermatitis due to wet work
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis caused by prolonged or repetitive wet work. It usually affects predominantly the skin of the hands and wrists but may affect other sites if clothing is repeatedly drenched. Although water and sweat alone (especially from under occlusive protection gloves) may be responsible, the risk is increased by exposure to defatting agents and irritants including soaps, detergents and cooling fluids. It is seen commonly in those looking after dependent relatives, especially young
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema due to wet work | irritant contact dermatitis due to detergents

name: Lichen planus
definition: Lichen planus is an inflammatory disease of skin and mucous membranes characterised by intense inflammation at the interface between epidermis/epithelium and dermis/corium. Its clinical manifestations vary according to how acutely it develops and to where it attacks. On the skin it typically presents as a symmetrical eruption of itchy, flat-topped pink or purple papules or plaques. Involvement of the scalp or nail matrix can produce permanent loss of hair or nails respectively. Although mucous m
synonyms: LP - [lichen planus] | Lichen ruber planus | Wilson lichen ruber | lichen planus of wilson | ruber planus | lichen ruber planus | LP | Lichen, ruber planus

name: Scarlet fever
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-positive bacteria Streptococcus pyogenes. This disease is characterised by a sore throat, fever, and a red rash. Transmission is commonly by inhalation of infected respiratory secretions, direct skin contact, or indirect contact.
synonyms: Scarlatina NOS | scarlatina

name: Large local reactions to Hymenoptera sting
definition: Large local reaction is a Hymenoptera sting immune-mediated reaction characterised by a swelling exceeding a diameter of 10 cm which lasts longer than 24 h; blisters may rarely be present. It has been mentioned as indication for allergen immunotherapy.

definition: A range of nail disorders in which the integrity of the nail plate is disturbed.

definition: Onycholysis attributable to fungal infection of the nail, most commonly by dermatophytes.

definition: Any condition of the skin and mucous membranes, caused by an infection with fungi other than Candida and dermatophytes.

definition: Adult moths and butterflies rarely cause cutaneous reactions but their caterpillars may cause injury through penetration of their setae (spines or hairs) into the skin. In some families (Megalopygidae, Arctiidae and Saturniidae) these contain venom and produce a toxic cutaneous or, more rarely, systemic reaction (erucism). synonyms: Erucism | caterpillar dermatitis

name: Flexural and intertriginous psoriasis
definition: Psoriasis involving flexures (retro-auricular folds, axillae, crural folds) and/or intertriginous areas (groins, under the breasts and, in obese individuals, abdominal apron fold). It may occur on its own or in association with seborrhoeic psoriasis or chronic plaque psoriasis. Plaques are thin, shiny and beef-red in colour with minimal scale. They may become secondarily fissured and/or macerated.

name: Hard corn
definition: A discrete area of painful hyperkeratosis resulting from repeated pressure and friction over a bony prominence, most commonly a metatarsal head or an interphalangeal joint of the forefoot.
synonyms: Heloma durum | heloma durum

name: Sepsis due to Erysipelothrix infection without mention of septic shock
definition: A severe infection due to the bacterium Erysipelothrix, a Gram-positive, catalase-negative, rod-shaped bacterium, causing a potentially deadly medical condition characterised by a whole-body inflammatory state (called a systemic inflammatory response syndrome or SIRS).
synonyms: sepsis due to Erysipelothrix NOS

name: Mucocutaneous lymph node syndrome
definition: Mucocutaneous lymph node syndrome (Kawasaki disease) is a globally distributed acute vasculitis of young children affecting medium to small calibre arteries and if untreated leads to coronary artery aneurysms in about a quarter of cases. Cardinal signs include cervical lymphadenopathy, conjunctival injection, rash (maculopapular, erythrodermic or erythema multiforme-like), strawberry tongue, oropharyngeal erythema, erythema and swelling of hands and feet and periungual desquamation. The cause is
synonyms: Acute febrile mucocutaneous lymph node syndrome | Kawasaki disease | Kawasaki syndrome | KD | mucocutaneous lymph node syndrome

name: Complex epidermal hamartoma
definition: Hamartomatous malformation composed of elements deriving from several components of the developing epidermis and epidermal appendages.

name: Primary syphilitic extragenital chancre

name: Ligneous conjunctivitis
definition: Ligneous conjunctivitis (LC) is a rare form of chronic conjunctivitis characterised by the recurrent formation of pseudomembranous lesions most commonly on the palpebral surfaces. It is most frequently reported as a clinical manifestation of severe homozygous or compound-heterozygous hypoplasminogenaemia. Most cases involve infants and children.

name: Dermatitis or eczema
definition: Dermatitis and eczema are synonymous and describe an inflammatory reaction pattern in the skin characterised histologically by spongiosis with varying degrees of acanthosis, and a superficial perivascular lympho-histiocytic infiltrate. The clinical features may include itching, redness, scaling and clustered papulo-vesicles. The condition may be induced by a wide range of external and internal factors acting singly or in combination.
synonyms: dermatitis | dermatitis NOS | eczema | eczema NOS | eczematous dermatosis | skin inflammation

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, X-linked
definition: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type 5 is a type of Ehlers-Danlos syndromes (EDS), a heterogeneous group of hereditary connective tissue diseases characterised by joint hyperlaxity, cutaneous hyperelasticity and tissue fragility, and is characterised by hyperextensible skin but tissue fragility and joint hyperlaxity are mild.
synonyms: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type 5

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to metals or metal salts
Paraneoplastic hypertrophic osteoarthropathy
definition: Hypertrophic osteoarthropathy attributable to underlying malignant neoplasia especially of the lung

name: Artefactual panniculitis
definition: Self-inflicted damage to subcutaneous fat usually caused by injection of irritant or corrosive substances.
synonyms: Factitious panniculitis

name: Candida onychomycosis
definition: This is an infection of the nail plate caused by Candida yeasts. It may be associated with Candida paronychia or may be secondary to nail dystrophy, particularly onycholysis, from other causes. It is also a component of chronic mucocutaneous candidosis.
synonyms: Candida infection of the nail plate | Candida onychia | candidal onychia | onychia candidiasis

name: Syringoma
definition: Syringomata are small benign adnexal neoplasms that are almost always multiple. They are composed of sweat gland epithelium within adensely sclerotic stroma. They most commonly affect the eyelids and periocular skin as multiple 1-3 mm diameter firm smooth flesh-coloured papules. Less commonly a profusion of syringomata may erupt on the trunk as multiple small asymptomatic dermal papules (eruptive syringomata or hidradenomes éruptifs).
synonyms: chondroid syringoma of unspecified site

Skin lesion of uncertain nature, upper limb
definition: This denotes the presence of a skin lesion on an upper limb but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: DRESS syndrome
definition: DRESS syndrome (Drug Rash with Eosinophilia and Systemic Symptoms) is a hypersensitivity reaction characterised by a generalised skin rash, fever, eosinophilia, lymphocytosis and visceral involvement (hepatitis, nephritis, pneumonitis, pericarditis and myocarditis) and, in some patients, reactivation of human herpes virus 6.
synonyms: DIHS - [drug-induced hypersensitivity syndrome] | Drug rash with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms | Drug-induced hypersensitivity syndrome | DRESS | drug rash with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms | DHIS

definition: Christ-Siemens-Touraine syndrome is an ectodermal dysplasia (ED) characterised by a triad of signs comprising sparse hair (atrichosis or hypotrichosis), abnormal or missing teeth (anodontia or hypodontia) and inability to sweat due to lack of sweat glands (anhidrosis or hypohidrosis). Christ-Siemens-Touraine syndrome is the most common ED syndrome.
synonyms: Christ-Siemens-Touraine syndrome

name: Hereditary anonychia
definition: An autosomal recessive disorder characterised by isolated congenital anonychia or hypoplasia of the fingernails and/or toenails.

name: Callosities of hands or fingers
definition: Callosities on the hands and fingers may be due to a large variety of repetitive actions which result in focal friction and pressure. They are often occupational (e.g. from use of hand tools such as planes and screw-drivers) but can result from sporting and leisure activities (e.g. rowing, weight-lifting, gardening, violin or guitar-playing) or from everyday tasks such as holding a pen. They may also result from habit tics in which skin is repeatedly picked or bitten. Commonly affected sites inc

name: Oculocutaneous albinism type 4
definition: OCA4 is characterised by hypopigmentation of the skin and hair plus the characteristics of all other types of OCA. The amount of cutaneous pigmentation in OCA4 ranges from minimal to near normal. Hair colour may darken with time, but does not vary significantly from childhood to adulthood. It is caused by a mutation in SLC45A2 gene.
synonyms: OCA4 - [Oculocutaneous albinism type 4]

synonyms: superficial thrombophlebitis migrans

name: Subcutaneous dissecting haematoma
definition: A haematoma, usually located on the lower legs, due to traumatic rupture of subcutaneous blood vessels in individuals with skin fragility, most commonly resulting from advanced age, long-term corticosteroid exposure and/or anticoagulant therapy. Because of the fragility of the tissues, minor trauma can result in extensive extravasation of blood into the virtual space between the muscular fascia and subcutaneous fat with distension of the overlying skin. If the haematoma is not drained, the cutan

name: Axillary trichomycosis
definition: Axillary trichomycosis is a bacterial infection affecting axillary hair due to overgrowth of corynebacteria (predominantly Corynebacterium tenuis). It occurs in all ethnic groups from both temperate and tropical climates but is commoner in men than women. It manifests as yellow, red or black adherent concretions encircling axillary hair shafts.

name: Salmon patch
definition: A common skin condition of neonates, characterised by flat, deep-pink localised areas of capillary dilation that occur predominantly on the back of the neck, lower occiput, upper eyelids, upper lip, and bridge of the nose. The areas disappear permanently by about 2 years of age.
synonyms: Naevus simplex | Stork bite

Ectodermal dysplasia – skin fragility syndrome
definition: Ectodermal dysplasia-skin fragility syndrome is a rare form of suprabasal epidermolysis bullosa due to reduced or absent plakophilin 1 as the result of mutations in the PKP1 gene. It is characterised by erosions and crusting of the skin, nail dystrophy, sparse woolly hair and hyperkeratosis of the palms and soles.
synonyms: EBS-plakophilin | ED – SF - [Ectodermal dysplasia – skin fragility] syndrome (MIM 604536) | Plakophilin 1 deficiency | Skin fragility - ectodermal dysplasia syndrome

name: Radionecrosis of skin attributable to diagnostic procedure
definition: Necrosis and ulceration of skin and soft tissues as a result of overexposure to ionizing radiation during one or more diagnostic radiological procedures. This has been most frequently reported from prolonged or repeated fluoroscopy in patients undergoing cardiac catheterization, when it typically affects the lateral chest wall.

name: Leptospira infection of liver
definition: The disease due to Leptospira icterohaemorrhagiae is Weil’s disease, which can result in jaundice, renal failure and haemorrhages. Other types of leptospirosis are generally less severe than Weil’s disease.

name: Latent yaws
definition: Latent yaws is defined as yaws with no clinical signs and only serological evidence of infection (reactive treponemal and non-treponemal tests). Infectious relapses may occur in latent cases for up to 5 and, rarely, 10 years. The total duration of infectivity for an untreated yaws patient, including relapses, is thought to be about 12-18 months.
synonyms: Yaws without clinical manifestations, with positive serology

name: Bamforth-Lazarus syndrome
definition: Bamforth-Lazarus syndrome (Bamforth syndrome) is a rare form of syndromic congenital hypothyroidism that associates athyreosis and cleft palate.
synonyms: Athydroidal hypothyroidism – spiky hair – cleft palate | Bamforth syndrome | Hypothyroidism - cleft palate

name: Primary yaws
definition: Primary yaws results from primary inoculation of Treponema pallidum subsp. pertenue into the skin, manifesting 2-12 weeks later as a localised papule (initial, primary or ‘mother' yaw) before developing into a large non-tender ulcerating nodule, often resembling a raspberry (hence the name ‘framboesia’). synonyms: Chancre of yaws | Primary framboesia | initial framboesia | initial lesions of yaws | mother yaw

name: Neonatal cold injury syndrome
definition: Neonatal cold injury syndrome is characterised by a core body temperature below 35°C (95°F) due to exposure of the newborn to prolonged or extremely low environmental temperatures. Clinically it is characterised by coldness to touch, apathy, immobility, decreased urine output and refusal of food. In addition oedema and redness of the extremities, especially the hands, feet, and face, are observed. It is commonly fatal and survivors may have evidence of brain damage.
synonyms: Cold injury syndrome of newborn

definition: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy, type 5 (HSAN5) is characterised by loss of pain perception and impaired temperature sensitivity, in the absence of any other major neurological anomalies.

name: Pressure ulceration
definition: Pressure ulcers result from localised injury and ischaemic necrosis of skin and underlying tissues due to prolonged pressure, or pressure in combination with shear; bony prominences of the body are the most frequently affected sites; immobility and debility are major contributing factors.
synonyms: Decubitus ulcer [pressure ulcer] without mention of stage or grade | Pressure sore [decubitus sore] without mention of stage or grade | bed sore | bed ulcer | bedsore | decubitus pressure sore | decubitus ulcer | plaster ulcer | pressure injury | pressure necrosis of skin

It is thought to be due to mutations in the KIT ligand gene, mutations of which are also implicated in hereditary universal dyschromatosis.

definition: A disease caused by an infection with the parasitic worm Ancylostoma. This disease is characterised by pruritus at the site of larval penetration. In mild infections, this disease may be asymptomatic; in moderate to severe infections, this disease may present with cough, pharyngeal irritation during larval migration in airways, iron-deficiency anaemia, abdominal pain, nausea, bloody diarrhoea, fatigue, or delayed development (mental or physical). The process normally affects several bones and is thus polyostotic; early bone changes are usually visible by plain radiography and periosteal thickening is often palpable. name: Eccrine syringosquamous metaplasia
definition: Eccrine syringosquamous metaplasia describes a distinctive microscopic finding of eccrine duct damage in which an insult, most commonly cytotoxic chemotherapy, has resulted in a transformation from the normal cuboidal to squamous epithelium, which may simulate squamous cell carcinoma. Patients present with erythematous papules, often in a generalised distribution and sometimes with vesicles and pustules. It may also present with acral erythema. definition: This refers to any of several autosomal recessive diseases resulting from mutations of genes for enzymes mediating the biochemical steps of production of cortisol from cholesterol by the adrenal glands (steroidogenesis). This diagnosis is due to 11-beta-hydroxylase deficiency.

Cronkhite-Canada syndrome
definition: Cronkhite-Canada syndrome (CCS) is a sporadically occurring, noninherited disorder of generalised gastrointestinal polyps (hamartomas), cutaneous pigmentation, alopecia, and onychodystrophy. The possibility of progression to cancer is considered to be low. Chronic diarrhea and protein-losing enteropathy are often observed.

name: Benign familial hypobetalipoproteinaemia
definition: Benign familial hypobetalipoproteinemia is the benign form of familial hypobetalipoproteinemia characterised by permanently low levels (below the 5th percentile) of apolipoprotein B and LDL cholesterol.

name: Artefactual cheilitis
definition: Self-inflicted damage to lips from biting, chewing and/or picking.

name: Congenital ptosis

name: Hordeolum
definition: An acute focal infection usually by Staphylococcus aureus involving the eyelash follicle and its associated meibomian and Zeis glands. If the principal focus of infection is the follicle, it presents with a painful boil which discharges pus at the eyelid margin (external hordeolum or stye). If the infection is centred on the meibomian gland (internal hordeolum) then suppuration onto the conjunctival surface occurs.

name: Hypocomplementaemic urticarial vasculitis
definition: Vasculitis accompanied by urticaria and hypocomplementemia affecting small vessels (i.e., capillaries, venules, or arterioles), and associated with anti-C1q antibodies. Glomerulonephritis, arthritis, obstructive pulmonary disease, and ocular inflammation are common.(Chapel Hill Consensus Conference, 2011)

name: Familial partial lipodystrophy associated with PPARG mutations
definition: Familial partial lipodystrophy associated with PPARG (peroxisome proliferative activated receptor gamma) mutations belongs to the group of lipodystrophy syndromes characterised by anomalies of adipose tissue distribution and often associated with severe insulin resistance manifested by hyperinsulinaemia (insulin levels above 20mUI/l), carbohydrate intolerance or diabetes, hypertriglyceridaemia with low levels of high-density lipoproteins (HDL) and arterial hypertension. Acanthosis nigricans, hep
synonyms: FPLD3 - [Familial partial lipodystrophy type 3] | Familial partial lipodystrophy type 3

Dermatoses of the anus, perianal area or perineum
definition: Disorders affecting the skin of and surrounding the anus including the intergluteal cleft and genitocrural folds.

name: Squamous cell carcinoma of vulva
definition: An invasive squamous cell carcinoma arising from the vulva. Risk factors include the human papilloma virus and cigarette smoking. Precursor lesions include the vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia, lichen sclerosus with associated squamous cell hyperplasia, and chronic granulomatous vulvar disease such as granuloma inguinale. Symptoms include vulvar pruritus or irritation, discharge, bleeding, and pain. The following morphologic variants have been identified\: keratinizing, non-keratinizing, basaloi

name: Burn from exposure to therapeutic ultraviolet radiation
cn_name: 医用紫外线照射后烧伤

name: Type II leprosy reaction
definition: This phenomenon, also named downgrading reaction, occurs in borderline leprosy states and is associated with a decrease in cell-mediated immunity with a shift towards the lepromatous end of the clinical spectrum.
synonyms: Type II downgrading reaction

name: Nodular granulomatous candidosis of the napkin area
definition: Infantile gluteal granulomata (granuloma gluteale infantum) associated with napkin candidosis. Candidosis is thought to be implicated in a majority but not all cases of infantile gluteal granulomata.”

name: Recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, generalised severe
definition: Severe generalised recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa is the most severe subtype of dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa. It was formerly known as the Hallopeau-Siemens type. It is characterised by generalised cutaneous and mucosal blistering and scarring associated with severe deformities and major extracutaneous involvement. Affected individuals are at high risk of aggressive squamous cell carcinomas in areas of chronic ulceration.
synonyms: RDEB Hallopeau-Siemens | RDEB-GS - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, generalised severe] | RDEB-gen sev - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, generalised severe]

name: Carcinoma cuniculatum
definition: A slow-growing infiltrative verrucous carcinoma occurring typically on the sole of the foot. It is associated with human papilloma virus infection. It is often painful.
synonyms: carcinoma cuniculatum | epithelioma cuniculatum

name: Ectodermal dysplasia - absent dermatoglyphs
definition: Ectodermal dysplasia - absent dermatoglyphs syndrome is characterised by ectodermal dysplasia (including nail and skin anomalies), absence of dermatoglyphs and furrows on the fingers.

name: Bullous lichen planus
definition: Bullous lichen planus is a variant of rare lichen planus characterised by the development of vesico-bullous lesions. Prevalence is unknown and only a few cases, both sporadic and familial, have been described in the literature. The disease manifests during childhood or adolescence. Bullous lesions develop on top of preexisting lichen planus papules or on normal skin and generally affect the lower limbs or the lower mucosal lip, and in some rare cases the torso. In this condition, the epithelium
synonyms: Lichen planus bullosus

name: Keratosis pilaris atrophicans
definition: Variants of keratosis pilaris in which follicular atrophy is a prominent component.

name: Subcutaneous swelling, mass or lump of multiple sites
definition: One or more subcutaneous soft tissue masses of undetermined or unspecified nature involving multiple sites
synonyms: localised mass of multiple sites | localised swelling of multiple sites | localised swelling, mass and lump, multiple sites

name: Halogen eruption
definition: Vegetating acneform reactions to exposure to halides (bromides and iodides).

name: Nail shedding
synonyms: Onychomadesis | nail shedding

name: Benign cutaneous neoplasms with eccrine differentiation
synonyms: Eccrine dermal duct tumour | benign tumour of sweat gland

name: Hypomyopathic juvenile dermatomyositis
definition: Hypomyopathic juvenile dermatomyositis is young-onset subset of dermatomyositis characterised by a lack of subjective symptoms but with subclinical evidence of muscle inflammation on muscle biopsy, electromyography, or other procedures.

name: Neurological conditions affecting the skin
definition: Skin conditions resulting from degeneration of or damage to the nervous system
cn_name: 累及皮肤的神经系统疾病

name: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency, classic form, salt wasting
definition: This refers to any of several autosomal recessive diseases resulting from mutations of genes for enzymes mediating the biochemical steps of production of cortisol from cholesterol by the adrenal glands (steroidogenesis). This diagnosis is due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency, classic form, salt wasting.

name: Superficial frostbite of trunk, not elsewhere classified
synonyms: superficial frostbite of trunk NOS

name: Dermatitis of hands
definition: Dermatitis or eczema involving predominantly the hands.
synonyms: dermatitis of hand | hand dermatitis | hand eczema

name: Lichen simplex of vulva
definition: Circumscribed pruritic lichenification of female external genitalia.

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to food flavours or additives
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to food flavours and additives (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to food flavours and additives).

name: Congenital hemidysplasia with ichthyosiform erythroderma and limbs defects
definition: CHILD (Congenital Hemidysplasia with Ichthyosiform nevus and Limb Defects) syndrome is a rare multisystem birth defect characterised by unilateral erythema and scaling, with a distinct demarcation in the middle of the trunk.
synonyms: 3-beta-hydroxysteroid C-4 dehydrogenase deficiency | CHILD - [Congenital hemidysplasia with ichthyosiform erythroderma and limbs defects] syndrome

name: Occupational acne or folliculitis due to exposure to mineral oils
definition: Acne and folliculitis caused by occupational exposure to mineral oils such as paraffin, naphthenic and aromatic oils. These are widely used as coolants, hydraulic fluids, cutting oils and lubricants. Follicular papules, comedones, pustules, nodules and scars are seen particularly on the arms, thighs and buttocks. Friction from clothing is often a contributory factor.

name: Oral-facial-digital syndrome type 6
definition: Joubert syndrome with orofaciodigital defect is a very rare subtype of Joubert syndrome related disorders (JSRD, see this term) characterised by the neurological features of JS associated with orofacial anomalies and often polydactyly. Typical oral findings include bifid or lobulated tongue, lingual hamartomas and multiple oral frenulae, but cleft lip and/or palate can also be present. Polydactyly is typically mesaxial with Y-shaped metacarpals, but can also be preaxial or postaxial.
synonyms: Joubert syndrome with orofaciodigital defect

name: Autoimmune or other photodermatoses
definition: A heterogeneous group of dermatoses mostly involving an interaction between the immune system and ultraviolet radiation or visible light
cn_name: 自身免疫或其他光线性皮肤病

name: Deficiency of interleukin-36 receptor antagonist
synonyms: DITRA - [Deficiency of interleukin-36 receptor antagonist]

name: Perianal eczema
definition: Eczema affecting perianal skin without specification of type or cause.

name: Adverse cutaneous effects of diagnostic procedures
definition: Skin problems arising from diagnostic procedures. Examples would be radiation necrosis from prolonged fluoroscopy or anaphylaxis from use of radiocontrast media.
cn_name: 诊断处置过程中所致的皮肤不良反应

name: Plantar fascial fibromatosis
definition: Plantar fascial fibromatosis is a relatively uncommon form of superficial fibromatosis affecting the deep connective tissue of the sole of the foot. It is characterised by the presence of spindle-shaped fibroblasts, hypercellularity, and an infiltrative growth pattern. It presents clinically with nodules or cords in the plantar fascia and tendons. In severe cases contractures of the toes may develop, making walking painful.
synonyms: Contracture of plantar fascia | Ledderhose disease | Plantar fibromatosis | ledderhose's disease

name: Klinefelter syndrome with karyotype 48,XXYY
definition: The 48,XXYY syndrome represents a chromosomal anomaly of the aneuploidic type characterised by the presence of an extra X and Y chromosome in males, and can be considered as a variant of Klinefelter syndrome as it shares the same clinical phenotype (tall stature, infertility, microorchidism, hypergonadotropic hypogonadism) but in addition includes behavioural problems (anxiety, aggressiveness, communication difficulties), psychiatric problems (hyperactivity, depression, etc.) and general minor l

name: Warfarin necrosis
synonyms: acquired protein C dysfunction

name: Burn of shoulder or arm, except wrist or hand, full thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of shoulder and arm, except wrist and hand, third degree
cn_name: 肩或臂（除外腕或手）全层皮肤烧伤

name: Warts of lips or oral cavity
definition: Infection of the lips and/or oral cavity, particularly the keratinized surfaces of the gingiva and palate, with \"skin\" type human papillomavirus (types 2 and 4). Focal epithelial hyperplasia (Heck disease) is a specific form of oral human papillomavirus infection caused by types 13 or 32 and of high prevalence in certain communities in the Americas.

name: Burn of perineum or genitalia, deep full thickness or complex burn
synonyms: Burn of perineum and genitalia, fourth degree
cn_name: 会阴或生殖器深全层皮肤烧伤或复合烧伤

name: Orbital varices
synonyms: orbit varix

name: Nail hypertrophy
synonyms: Onychauxis

name: Hypomelanosis due to exposure to chemicals
definition: Loss of skin pigment due to exposure to depigmenting agents such as hydroquinone, which is frequently employed as a cosmetic skin lightener, and industrial chemicals such as 4-tert-butylcatechol (PTBC) and para-substituted phenols (PSP).

name: Amyopathic dermatomyositis
definition: Amyopathic dermatomyositis is a variant of dermatomyositis that is characterised by the typical skin rash but without the muscle abnormalities. The amyopathic state is defined by a lack of muscle weakness and through diagnostic tests, including serum muscle enzymes, electromyogram studies, and muscle biopsies, that are usually normal or show only minimal abnormalities. Despite the lack of weakness, many patients complain of debilitating fatigue. The amyopathic form is more commonly seen in adult

name: Skin lesion of uncertain nature, neck
definition: This denotes the presence of a skin lesion on the neck but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Developmental venous malformations involving the skin
definition: Certain genetically-determined syndromes presenting with venous anomalies in the skin
cn_name: 累及皮肤的静脉发育畸形

name: Vulval aphthosis
definition: Vulval aphthae are vulval ulcers not attributable to infection or other identifiable cause. They typically occur on the medial aspects of the labia minora in young females (second decade of life). They may be associated with fever and malaise or with oral aphthae.
synonyms: Aphthosis of vulva | Vulval aphthae

name: Photo-allergic contact dermatitis
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis caused by sensitisation to a photoproduct of a compound either applied directly to the skin or taken up by the skin via the systemic circulation. The parent compound does not elicit an allergic reaction until it is chemically modified by exposure to ultraviolet radiation.
synonyms: Photo-allergic contact eczema

name: Juvenile hereditary haemochromatosis, Type 2A
definition: This is a membrane-bound and soluble protein in that is responsible for the iron overload condition known as juvenile hemochromatosis in humans, a severe form of hemochromatosis.
synonyms: Hemojuvelin-related haemochromatosis

name: Mycosis fungoides with extensive patches or plaques
definition: Mycosis fungoides with patches, papules and/or plaques affecting more than 10 % of body surface
synonyms: Mycosis fungoides stage T2 | Patch or plaque stage mycosis fungoides

name: Occupational acne or folliculitis
definition: Follicular occlusion and inflammation resulting from occupational exposure to oils, greases, tars and halogenated hydrocarbons.
synonyms: Occupational acne

name: Dissecting cellulitis
definition: Dissecting cellulitis is a destructive inflammatory disorder of the scalp characterised by widespread perifolliculitis with dermal abscess and sinus tract formation and extensive scarring. It occurs predominantly in black males aged between 18 and 40 years. It may be associated with hidradenitis suppurativa and acne conglobata (follicular occlusion triad), and with pilonidal sinus. Painful swellings develop around the vertex of the scalp and coalesce to form irregular undulating oedematous ridge
synonyms: Perifolliculitis capitis abscedens et suffodiens | perifolliculitis NOS | perifolliculitis abscedens et suffodiens

name: Irritant contact gingivostomatitis
definition: Inflammation of gingivae and oral mucosa due to contact with irritants.
synonyms: Irritant contact stomatitis

name: No significant skin abnormality found
definition: The situation where signs of skin disease have been sought but not detected.

name: Annular epidermolytic ichthyosis
definition: Annular epidermolytic ichthyosis is a rare clinical variant of epidermolytic ichthyosis characterised by the presence of a blistering phenotype at birth and the development from early infancy of annular polycyclic erythematous scales on the trunk and extremities.

name: IgG4 related disease
definition: IgG4 related syndrome (IgG4-related disease\: IgG4-RD) is a clinical disease characterised by elevated serum IgG4 concentration and tumefaction or tissue infiltration by IgG4-positive plasma cells. The diagnostic criteria for IgG4 related syndrome have been proposed, and it may be present in a certain population of patients with a wide variety of diseases, including Mikulicz disease, autoimmune pancreatitis, hypophysitis, Riedel thyroiditis, interstitial pneumonitis, interstitial nephritis, pros

name: Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, type 2A
definition: Autosomal recessive cutis laxa type 2A (ARCL-2A) is a rare genetic disease resulting from mutations in a gene encoding a proton pump (ATP6V0A2) required for normal Golgi processing of tropoelastin. External manifestations include redundant and wrinkled skin, short nose with broad nasal bridge, down-slanting palpebral fissures, bitemporal narrowing, broad forehead, and retrognathia. It may be associated with severe central nervous system defects including microcephaly, hypotonia, seizures, myopia
synonyms: ARCL-2A - [Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, type 2A] (MIM 219200) | Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, Debré type

name: Repetitive excoriation
definition: A compulsive skin-damaging disorder characterised by repetitive gouging through the full thickness of the epidermis of normal skin for relief of tension. It may be regarded as a severe form of skin picking disorder. The injuries inflicted result in exudation, crusting and scarring. It is commonly seen on the upper back, shoulders and upper arms. A similar clinical picture may be seen as a result of severe generalised pruritus secondary to systemic disorders such as primary biliary cirrhosis.

name: Superficial thrombophlebitis in the puerperium
synonyms: puerperal thrombophlebitis | superficial puerperal phlebitis | superficial puerperal thrombophlebitis

name: Partial deficiency of methylmalonyl-CoA mutase
definition: This refers to partial deficiency of an enzyme that catalyzes the isomerization of methylmalonyl-CoA to succinyl-CoA and it is involved in key metabolic pathways. It requires a vitamin B12-derived prosthetic group, adenosylcobalamin, to function.
synonyms: Mutase methylmalonic acidaemia | Mutase methylmalonic aciduria

name: Diabetic bullae
definition: Diabetic bullae (bullosis diabeticorum) are uncommon cutaneous complications of diabetes mellitus affecting about 0.5% of diabetics. Tense blisters with a non-inflamed base occur typically on the lower legs and feet. The trunk is rarely affected. The bullae appear spontaneously and may be clear or haemorrhagic. The pathogenesis is not well understood but diabetic angiopathy and polyneuropathy are assumed to be in part responsible.
synonyms: bullous eruption of diabetes mellitis | diabetic bullae

name: Sequelae of frostbite with tissue necrosis of head
Syphilitic peritonitis
definition: This is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum, that causes an inflammation of the peritoneum, the thin tissue that lines the inner wall of the abdomen and covers most of the abdominal organs.
synonyms: Late syphilitic peritonitis

name: Classical phenylketonuria, phenylalanine hydroxylase partial deficiency
definition: Phenylketonuria (PKU) is an autosomal recessive metabolic genetic disorder characterised by a mutation in the gene for the hepatic enzyme phenylalanine hydroxylase (PAH), rendering it nonfunctional, with phenylalanine hydroxylase partial deficiency.

name: Irritant contact dermatitis due in part to occupational exposure to irritant
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema due in part to occupational exposure to irritant

name: Multiple mononeuropathy vasculitis with Wegener granulomatosis
definition: Wegner`s granulomatosis can be associated with both mononeuritis multiplex as well as symmetric neuropathies. Neuropathies may develop during the course of the disease. In addition to the features of multiplex neuropathy and symmetric neuropathies, cranial nerve involvement (II, extraocular nerves, V, VII, VIII, IX) occurs and are related to the characteristic granulomatous process. In addition system involvement of kidneys, airways and lung, and focal inflammation of the oral and nasal cavities

name: Fingertip dermatitis
definition: A distinct pattern of eczema involving the palmar surface of the tips of some or all of the digits of the hand. The skin is dry, cracked and may break down into painful fissures. The thumb, forefinger and middle finger of the dominant hand are the most commonly affected sites. Repetitive friction and exposure to degreasing agents are important aetiological factors but contact allergy is responsible in some cases.
synonyms: Fingertip eczema

name: Systemic multiple mononeuropathy vasculitis
definition: Vasculitic neuropathies of this type can be associated with systemic diseases as\: CREST, giant cell arteritis, infections, Behcet`s disease, drugs (e.g. amphetamine; cocaine; heroin; ephedrine; phenylpropanolamine, penicillin; sulfonamide; phenytoin; allopurinol; propylthiouracil) and systemic disease as\: PAA, RA, Sjögren, SLE, Wegner and microscopic polyangiitis

name: Nail-biting
definition: Nail-biting is a common obsessive-compulsive disorder characterised by compulsive biting or chewing the nails and nailfolds.
synonyms: Onychophagia

name: Neuropathic skin damage
definition: Skin changes attributable entirely or in part to sensory or autonomic nerve damage.

name: Drug-induced gingival hyperplasia
definition: Gingival hyperplasia most commonly associated with ciclosporin therapy
synonyms: medicament-induced gingival hyperplasia

name: Skin injury due to bite by mammal
definition: Injury of the skin resulting from bite by a mammal. Because of the proximity of dogs and cats to humans, their bites, together with those inflicted by humans on each other, are amongst the most commonly encountered.

name: Oral haemorrhage, not elsewhere classified
synonyms: bleeding from mouth | haemorrhage from mouth | stomatorrhagia

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type IIB
definition: HSAN Type IIB is an autosomal recessive disorder due to mutations in the FAM134B gene. It presents in childhood with severe loss of pain and temperature sensations, ulcero-mutilations and autonomic dysfunction
synonyms: HSAN2B - [Hereditary sensory autonomic neuropathy type 2B]

name: Repetitive scratching behaviour
definition: A disorder characterised by a chronic overwhelming compulsion to scratch the skin which is out of proportion to any demonstrable underlying organic itch-provoking stimulus. It may have a markedly negative effect on sleep patterns and on overall quality of life. The underlying neurophysiological mechanisms are as yet poorly understood.
synonyms: Compulsive scratching behaviour

name: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum audouinii
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum audouinii, which typically causes an ectothrix infection of scalp hair.

name: Severe plaque psoriasis
definition: Psoriasis may be regarded as severe if it a) involves at least 10% of the skin surface, b) has a major impact on functioning and quality of life or c) is refractory to treatment.

name: Striate palmoplantar keratoderma
definition: Striate palmoplantar keratoderma is an isolated, focal, hereditary palmoplantar keratoderma characterized by linear hyperkeratosis along the flexor aspect of the fingers and on palms, as well as focal hyperkeratosis of the plantar skin. Patients present with painful thickening of the skin on palms and soles, with occasional fissuring, blistering and hyperhidrosis. Rarely, hyperkeratosis on other areas may be seen (knees, dorsal aspects of the digits). Histopatologically, widened intercellular sp
synonyms: Brunauer-Fuhs-Siemens keratoderma | Keratoderma palmoplantaris striata | brunauer-fuhs-siemens syndrome | focal NEPPK | keratosis palmoplantaris areata | keratosis palmoplantaris striata | sPPK | wachter-type striate palmoplantar keratoderma

name: Traumatic injury to nail bed or matrix of nail of hand
definition: Penetrating, crush or avulsion injury to the nail bed or matrix of a finger or thumb which may result in permanent nail dystrophy or nail loss.

name: Keloidal surgical scar
definition: A surgical scar which heals with an overgrowth of fibrous scar tissue which extends beyond the limits of the original surgical wound.

name: Episodic angioedema with eosinophilia
