name: Mycobacterium leprae dacryoadenitis
definition: This is a bacterium that causes leprosy (Hansen's disease). It is an intracellular, pleomorphic, acid-fast bacterium. This diagnosis is with inflammation of the lacrimal glands (the tear-producing glands).

name: Distichiasis, bilateral
definition: This is an eyelash that arises from an abnormal spot on the eyelid. Distichiae usually exit from the duct of the meibomian gland at the eyelid margin. This diagnosis is occurring in both eye.

name: Unspecified frostbite of arm
synonyms: unspecified frostbite of upper limb

name: Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura specified as refractory
Encephalocraniocutaneous lipomatosis
definition: Encephalocraniocutaneous lipomatosis is a rare sporadic neurocutaneous syndrome involving tissues of ectodermal and mesodermal origin and characterised by profound mental retardation, early onset of seizures, unilateral temporofrontal lipomatosis, ipsilateral cerebral and leptomeningeal lipomatosis, cerebral malformation and calcification, and lipomas of the skull, eye, and heart.
synonyms: Fishman syndrome | Haberland syndrome | haberland syndrome

name: Xanthoma in association with primary hypercholesterolaemia
definition: Xanthomata associated with primary hypercholesterolaemia are most commonly tendinous xanthomata though planar and tuberous xanthomas may also develop
synonyms: hypercholesterolemic xanthoma

name: Cholestatic pruritus due to liver disorder
definition: Pruritus due to genetic or acquired liver diseases associated with bile retention such as biliary atresia or drug-induced cholestasis.

name: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex superficialis
definition: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex superficialis (EBSS) is a suprabasal subtype of epidermolysis bullosa simplex (EBS, ) characterised by generalised or acral superficial erosions in the absence of blisters.
synonyms: EBS - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex] superficialis | EBSS - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex superficialis] | EBSS

name: Dermatomyositis in neoplastic disease of unknown behaviour
Acne resulting from anabolic steroid use
definition: Acne attributable to or exacerbated by the use of anabolic steroids given either for medical reasons or, more commonly, for increasing muscle bulk in \"body-builders\" etc. It most commonly occurs on the chest but may also develop on the face, neck, back and arms.

name: Frostbite
definition: Frostbite is injury from ice formation within tissues resulting from contact with cold air, liquids or metals. It is most commonly due to excessive exposure of skin to sub-zero environmental temperatures. The risk of injury is exacerbated by wind. The most commonly affected sites are fingers, toes, ears, nose, cheeks and chin with injury limited to skin and soft tissues. Full thickness skin necrosis and injury to deeper structures including muscles and bone may occur with prolonged exposure to c
synonyms: Frostbite NOS

name: Secondary Raynaud phenomenon
synonyms: Raynaud syndrome

name: PUVA keratoses
definition: Keratoses, usually multiple, involving skin exposed over a prolonged period to psoralen and UVA photochemotherapy

name: Phakomatosis pigmentokeratotica
definition: Phacomatosis pigmentokeratotica is a rare syndrome defined by the association of an organoid naevus (occasionally with sebaceous differentiation), a speckled lentiginous naevus, and other extracutaneous anomalies.

name: Occupational contact dermatitis of hands
definition: Contact dermatitis of hands of unspecified type attributable to work or occupation.

name: Neonatal pemphigus
definition: Neonatal pemphigus vulgaris is a short lived autoimmune skin disease arising as a result of transplacental transmission to the neonate of maternal antibodies. Neonatal pemphigus is characterised by blister formation on the skin and the mucous membranes mediated by auto-antibodies to the desmosome component desmoglein 3.

name: Mondor disease
definition: A form of localised superficial venous thrombophlebitis typically affecting the chest wall and manifesting as a fibrous cord with surrounding skin retraction and an absence of overlying cutaneous inflammation. No cause is found in many cases but trauma and breast surgery are often implicated.
synonyms: Sclerosing thrombophlebitis

name: Hypertrophic oral lichen planus
definition: A rare inflammatory disease of obscure etiology that has been identified as T-cell mediated autoimmune disease that affects the skin and mucus membranes including the oral cavity in which lesions may be asymptomatic but burning sensation or pain may be present; itching is frequent in the hypertrophic variety.

name: Oedema due to increased capillary pressure
definition: Increased capillary pressure increases the leakage of fluid from the vascular compartment to the interstitial tissues, resulting in oedema. Causes include impaired or obstructed venous return (fluid overload, venous thrombosis, right heart failure, venous compression from tumour tissue), increased blood flow (physiological response to heat exposure, arteriovenous malformations, disturbed cutaneous vasomotor control due to drug or autonomic neuropathy), or reduced plasma oncotic pressure due to h
synonyms: Oedema secondary to increased capillary pressure

name: Tattoo reaction
definition: Adverse reaction to the administration or presence of a tattoo, including allergic, papulonodular and granulomatous reactions

name: Cutis marmorata
synonyms: cutis marmorata

name: Amelo-cerebro-hypohidrotic syndrome
definition: Amelo-cerebro-hypohidrotic syndrome or Kohlschütter-Tönz syndrome is a degenerative disease characterised by amelogenesis imperfecta, seizures, mental regression and yellow teeth due to defective enamel. So far, 19 cases have been reported. Spasticity and developmental delay are also variable manifestations. It is transmitted as an autosomal recessive trait.
synonyms: Epilepsy - dementia - amelogenesis imperfecta | Kohlschütter-Tönz syndrome

name: Hemiatrophy of tongue
definition: Atrophy of one half of the tongue.

name: Burn of breast, deep partial thickness burn
Palmoplantar hyperhidrosis
definition: Excessive sweating of palms and soles. This is usually bilateral. Palmar hyperhidrosis may be triggered by emotional stress but, in severe cases, can be continuous and cause major disability by inhibiting normal social interaction and interfering with everyday tasks such as writing, preparing food or handling papers. Plantar hyperhidrosis may accompany palmar hyperhidrosis but may occur independently.

name: Muckle-Wells syndrome
definition: Muckle-Wells syndrome (MWS) is an autoinflammatory syndrome characterised by chronic recurrent urticaria, periodic arthritis, sensorineural deafness, general signs of inflammation and secondary amyloidosis (AA type).
synonyms: Urticaria - deafness - amyloidosis | MWS

name: Autosomal dominant cutis laxa
definition: Autosomal dominant cutis laxa results from mutations in the elastin gene ELN which result in the development of folds of loose, inelastic, redundant skin that typically become more prominent with age. In about a third of cases the condition results from a new mutation. It may first be recognised at any time from birth to early adulthood. Characteristic facial features include an aged appearance, long philtrum, high forehead, large earlobes, and beaked nose. Systemic manifestations include aortic
synonyms: ADCL - [Autosomal dominant cutis laxa] (MIM 123700) | ADCL

name: Horse-fly bite
definition: Bites inflicted by biting, blood-sucking tabanid flies, which includes deer flies, clegs and March flies. Tabanid flies act as vectors for loiasis and tularaemia.
synonyms: Cleg bites | Deer-fly bites | Tabanid fly bites

name: Cutaneous Mycobacterium fortuitum infection
definition: Infection of the skin and subcutaneous tissue with M fortuitum complex mycobacteria (including M fortuitum and M chelonae). The clinical picture varies from cellulitis to nodules, ulcers and extensive subcutaneous necrosis and sinus formation \[Bolognia Dermatology 2003\]
synonyms: Cutaneous M chelonei infection

name: Acantholytic dermatoses
definition: A group of skin disorders characterised by defective epidermal cell-cell adhesion. The causes range from hereditary conditions such as Darier disease to immunobullous disorders such as pemphigus.
synonyms: Acantholysis

name: Median cleft of the upper lip or maxilla
Systemic contact dermatitis due to implanted allergen
definition: Dermatitis provoked by insertion or implantation of metal (e.g. as part of infusion cannula or prosthesis) to which the individual has previously been sensitized by topical exposure.

name: Allergic contact sensitisation
definition: The presence of specific delayed type IV hypersensitivity of the immune system to a given substance without imputation of past or current disease. Such sensitisation is normally acquired by prior contact of the skin or mucous membranes with the substance or with one chemically closely related to it (cross-reactivity). Subsequent contact with the substance may provoke an allergic reaction. In certain circumstances such sensitisation may prevent an individual from taking up or continuing employmen

name: Burn of ankle or foot, epidermal burn
synonyms: Burn of ankle or foot, first degree

name: Allergic contact dermatitis of hands
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis affecting the hands.
synonyms: Allergic contact hand eczema

name: Complete trisomy 21, meiotic non-disjunction
synonyms: trisomy 21- meiotic nondisjunction | trisomy; syndrome, 21, meiotic nondisjunction

name: Subungual exostosis
definition: Subungual exostosis presents as an acquired isolated, slow growing, benign osteochondral neoplasm arising from the distal phalanx, most commonly of the great toe, and frequently lifts the overlying nail plate. It has been linked to gene rearrangements affecting the collagen genes COL12A1 and COL4A5. It may present as a symptomless nodule but associated pain is common.

name: Photoaggravated psoriasis
definition: Psoriasis which is reproducibly exacerbated by exposure to summer sunlight or other sources of ultraviolet radiation. Whereas psoriasis normally improves on exposure to summer sun, photoaggravated psoriasis gets worse in the summer.

name: Burn of face except eye or ocular adnexa, full thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of face except eye and adnexa, third degree
cn_name: 面部（除外眼或眼附属器）全层皮肤烧伤

name: Disseminated paracoccidioidomycosis
definition: Disseminated paracoccidioidomycosis results from haematogenous and lymphatic dissemination of yeasts from the lungs and aerodigestive tract. Cutaneous involvement, seen in 25% of cases, presents as crusted papules, ulcers, nodules, and verrucous plaques. Lymphadenopathy occurs commonly in the cervical region, but all lymph node chains can be involved. Adrenal glands are commonly affected with a significant risk of adrenal insufficiency and Addisonian crisis. Long bones such as ribs, humeri, and
synonyms: Disseminated chronic paracoccidioidomycosis | South American disseminated blastomycosis | generalised paracoccidioidomycosis

name: Perforating folliculitis
definition: A largely asymptomatic eruption of chronic follicular pinpoint papules sited predominantly on the limbs of young adults. Histology demonstrates dilated hair follicles plugged with keratin with focal perforation of the follicular epithelium into the dermis.

name: Vaccinia
definition: A poxvirus which was formerly used to protect against smallpox. Its use as a vaccine can be complicated by a generalised rash secondary to viraemia, accidental infection of other sites or other individuals, progressive infection at the site of vaccination or, rarely, encephalomyelitis and myopericarditis.

name: Neonatal haemochromatosis
definition: Neonatal hemochromatosis is an iron storage disorder of unknown etiology present at birth, and characterised by the association of severe hepatocellular failure with hyperbilirubinemia, signs of haemorrhage, oedema, ascites, hypoglycaemia, and lactic acidosis with little to no elevation of transaminases.

name: Erythroderma
definition: Erythroderma is the term applied to a persistent inflammatory erythema, typically lasting at least several weeks, affecting more than 90% of the skin surface. It may be due to a range of inflammatory and neoplastic processes include eczema, psoriasis, drugs and lymphoma. If unqualified, it should be considered a provisional diagnosis, although it is not always possible to identify a specific underlying cause even after extensive investigation. Whatever the cause it can be life-threatening as a r
synonyms: Exfoliative dermatitis | exfoliative dermatitis | exfoliative erythroderma

name: Petechiae
definition: Petechiae result from focal leakage of blood from dermal capillaries into the adjacent dermal connective tissue. They present as multiple pin-point non-blanching red or purple macules. The many underlying causes range from the innocuous (e.g. coughing or straining) to life-threatening conditions (e.g. meningococcal septicaemia).
synonyms: Petechial haemorrhage | blood spots on skin | cutaneous haemorrhage | subcutaneous haemorrhage

name: Superficial thrombophlebitis of antepartum
synonyms: antepartum thrombophlebitis

name: Verrucous squamous cell carcinoma of penis
definition: A slow growing, locally recurring, very well differentiated papillary squamous cell carcinoma that arises from the penis. It is characterised by the presence of acanthosis and hyperkeratosis. The neoplastic infiltrate extends into the underlying stroma with a pushing border. Koilocytotic atypia is not present.
synonyms: Verrucous carcinoma of penis

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to cosmetics or fragrances
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to cosmetics and fragrances (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to cosmetics and fragrances).

name: Pityriasis rubra pilaris type I, classical adult
definition: This is the most common and recognisable form of pityriasis rubra pilaris. It presents as a rapidly evolving scaly erythematous rash which is initially follicular but typically progresses to erythroderma, within which small islands of uninvolved skin characteristically remain. Untreated, it tends to resolve spontaneously after an average of three years. It affects the sexes equally with the highest incidence between 40 and 60 years of age. The cause is poorly understood though gain-of-function m

name: Crohn disease of anal region
definition: Crohn disease commonly involves the anus and perianal area and may precede any other gut involvement by years. The constellation of symptoms and signs which may occur include pruritus ani, maceration, skin tags, fissures, fistulae, erosions and secondary infection with abscesses.
synonyms: Anal Crohn disease | Crohn disease of anal canal | Perianal Crohn disease

name: Phymatous rosacea
definition: The hallmark of phymatous rosacea is rhinophyma but the forehead, chin and ears may also be affected. It is characterised by hypertrophy of the affected tissue which can produce gross distortion, particularly of the nose. The pathological changes include a variably severe mixed inflammatory infiltrate, sebaceous gland hyperplasia and dermal fibrosis. The aetiology is poorly understood. It is not always accompanied by other features of rosacea.
synonyms: Hypertrophic rosacea

name: Hypertrophy of tongue
synonyms: enlargement of tongue | lingual frenulum hypertrophy

name: Autoimmune pancreatitis without other organ involvement
definition: This is an autoimmune inflammation of the pancreas which requires immediate medical attention and hospitalization during an attack that has multiple causes and symptoms, without other organ involvement.

name: Griscelli syndrome type 1
definition: This syndrome is characterised by pigmentary dilution of hair and skin and primary neurologic abnormalities resulted from mutations in MYO5A gene.
synonyms: GS1 - [Griscelli syndrome type 1] (MIM 214450) | Partial albinism with primary neurological impairment

name: Pruritus due to systemic disorder
definition: Pruritus due to underlying systemic disorder such as renal failure or cholestatic jaundice.
cn_name: 系统性疾病引起的瘙痒症

name: Metabolic or nutritional disorders affecting the skin
definition: This group includes dermatoses resulting either from disturbed metabolic processes or from defective nutrition
cn_name: 累及皮肤的代谢性或营养性障碍

name: Frostbite involving multiple body regions
definition: Frostbite is damage to tissues as the result of exposure to low environmental temperatures.
synonyms: Multiple frostbite NOS | frostbite of multiple body regions
cn_name: 累及多处身体区域的冻伤

name: Creeping myiasis
definition: A form of migratory cutaneous myiasis caused by larvae of certain species of Gasterophilus, the horse bot-fly. It resembles cutaneous larva migrans.

name: BCG-induced scrofuloderma
definition: Scrofuloderma secondary to BCG immunization \[Dermatology TAG\]

name: Secondary syphilitic gastritis
definition: Symptomatic secondary syphilitic gastritis is uncommon, occurring in less than 1% of patients with syphilis in the antibiotic era. The most common symptoms include nausea, vomiting, epigastric pain and anorexia; rarely, acute bleeding, gastric perforation and/or obstruction may occur.
synonyms: Treponema pallidum-associated gastritis

name: Papulopustular rosacea
definition: Papulopustular rosacea is characterised, as the name implies, by erythematous papules and sterile pustules affecting facial skin. These are typically located on the cheeks, central chin, nose and central forehead. The perilesional skin is inflamed and may be oedematous. In contradistinction to papulopustular acne, comedones, nodules and cysts are not a feature.

name: Lymphoedema, microcephaly and chorioretinopathy syndrome
definition: This is a condition of localised fluid retention and tissue swelling caused by a compromised lymphatic system, with microcephaly and choriorentinopathy syndrome.

name: Vasoconstriction of extremities
definition: Disorders characterised by peripheral vasospasm including Raynaud disease and ergotism.

name: Lysosomal diseases with dermal mucin deposition
definition: Mucopolysaccharidoses and mucolipidoses in which there is deposition of mucin in the dermis

name: Onychomycosis due to non-dermatophyte mould
definition: Fungal nail infection due to organisms other than Candida and dermatophytes. These include Scopulariopsis brevicaulis, Neoscytalidium dimidiatum, Fusarium spp., and Aspergillus spp., which may not respond to therapies directed at the more common causes of onychomycosis.
cn_name: 非皮肤真菌所致甲真菌病

name: Gumma of bone of late yaws
synonyms: yaws gumma of bone

name: Neonatal macronutrient deficiency affecting the skin
Netherton syndrome
definition: Netherton syndrome is a skin disorder characterised by congenital ichthyosiform erythroderma, a distinctive hair shaft defect (trichorrhexis invaginata) and atopic manifestations.
synonyms: Comèl-Netherton syndrome | Netherton disease

name: Acquired perforating dermatosis
definition: A condition commonly seen in association with longstanding diabetes mellitus, particularly in association with renal failure, in which multiple large follicular and non-follicular keratotic papules develop on the trunk and limbs. Trauma from scratching pruritic skin may be the initiating event leading to transepidermal elimination of degenerate collagen and elastic fibres from the dermis.
synonyms: Acquired reactive perforating dermatosis | Kyrle disease

name: Cutaneous nocardiosis
definition: Cutaneous nocardiosis may be due to direct infection of the skin where it presents either as a solitary cold abscess or as a lymphangitic process in which infection spreads up lymphatic channels to form a linear array of suppurative nodules. Skin involvement is also present in a third of cases of systemic nocardiosis.
synonyms: nocardia abscess

name: Chronic urticaria due to other specified mechanism
definition: Chronic urticaria due to an identifiable cause that is not autoimmunity, infection or pseudoallergy.

name: Waardenburg syndrome type 2
definition: This type resembles type 1 except that dystopia canthorum is absent. It is caused by mutations in MITF/SNAI2 genes.
synonyms: WS2

name: Radiation keratosis following radiotherapy
definition: Radiation keratosis arising as sequela of radiotherapy to the skin

name: Langerhans cell histiocytosis involving the skin
synonyms: Class I histiocytosis involving the skin | Histiocytosis X involving the skin
cn_name: 朗格汉斯细胞组织细胞增多症累及皮肤

name: Seborrhoeic dermatitis of face
definition: Seborrhoeic dermatitis affecting the face, most typically the nasolabial folds and chin.
synonyms: Seborrhoeic eczema of face

name: Congenital meningovascular syphilis
synonyms: Late congenital meningovascular syphilis

name: Acne or acneiform reactions attributable to drugs
cn_name: 药物引起的痤疮或痤疮样反应

name: Hypohidrosis attributable to defective sudomotor innervation or function
cn_name: 汗泌运动神经分布缺陷或功能障碍引起的少汗症

name: Skin condition resolved
definition: The situation where a previous skin disorder is no longer present, either as a result of spontaneous resolution or as a result of treatment.

name: Pellagra
definition: Pellagra is a potentially life-threatening disorder due to niacin deficiency and is observed in malnourished individuals, especially alcoholics, and as a complication of isoniazid therapy. The diagnosis is often overlooked or delayed. Pellagra manifests as diarrhoea, dermatitis, dementia, which usually appear in this order. Gastrointestinal tract symptoms always precede skin involvement, which presents initially with a sunburn-like blistering erythema, typically affecting the dorsal surfaces of
synonyms: Pellagroid syndrome

name: Zlotogora-Ogur syndrome
definition: Zlotogora-Ogur syndrome is a cleft lip/palate-ectodermal dysplasia syndrome with limb involvement, characterised by cleft lip and palate, ectodermal dysplasia (sparse short and dry scalp hair, sparse eyebrows and eyelashes), and partial syndactyly of the fingers and/or toes.

name: Frostbite of hand with tissue necrosis
synonyms: frostbite with tissue necrosis of hand

name: Erosive pustular dermatosis of scalp
definition: Erosive pustular dermatosis of the scalp is a distinctive scalp disorder of the elderly characterised by the development of sterile pustules, erosions and crusts in areas of chronically sun-damaged scalp skin. Local trauma may also play an aetiological role. It normally responds to high-potency topical corticosteroids.
cn_name: 头皮糜烂脓疱性皮肤病

name: Pili annulati
definition: Pili annulati (Ringed hair disease) is a very rare disorder of the hair shaft characterised by alternating light and dark bands giving a shiny appearance to the hair.
synonyms: Ringed hair | ringed hair

name: Parastomal pyoderma gangrenosum
definition: Pyoderma gangrenosum developing adjacent to a surgical stoma, usually but not always in the context of inflammatory bowel disease.

name: Congenital macroglossia
definition: A condition caused by failure of the tongue to correctly develop during the antenatal period. This condition is characterised by a larger than normal tongue.
synonyms: Congenital hypertrophy of tongue

name: Lichen sclerosus of penis
definition: Lichen sclerosus of the penis develops almost exclusively in uncircumcised males and is the result of chronic occluded contact of susceptible epithelium to urine. It often causes dyspareunia and difficulties with micturition. It manifests typically as a sclerotic phimosis of the prepuce, which it may not be possible to retract, together with inflammation and sclerosis of the mucocutaneous surface of the prepuce and glans penis. Stenosis and occasionally obliteration of the external meatal orific
synonyms: Balanitis xerotica obliterans | Penile lichen sclerosus | balanitis xerotica obliterans

name: Familial isolated trichomegaly
definition: Familial isolated trichomegaly is a rare genetic hair anomaly characterised by a prolonged anagen phase of the eyelash hairs, leading to extreme eyelash growth that may result in corneal irritation. Increased growth of hair on other parts of the face (eyebrows, cheeks, forehead) and/or the body (chest, arms, legs) may be associated.
synonyms: Familial isolated trichomegaly of the eyelashes | long eyelashes | TCMGLY

name: Papular purpuric gloves and socks syndrome
definition: This acute acral dermatosis is characterised by an intensely pruritic papular and often purpuric eruption affecting the hands, wrists, feet and ankles. This is frequently accompanied by oral inflammation and ulceration, malaise and fever. It affects adults predominantly and had been linked to a range of viral infections, most commonly to parvovirus B19.
cn_name: 丘疹紫癜性手套和短袜综合征

name: Mycosis fungoides, erythrodermic
definition: Mycosis fungoides with confluent erythema affecting more than 80% of body surface.
synonyms: T4 stage mycosis fungoides

name: Anal warts
definition: Infection of the anus or perianal skin by human papillomavirus (HPV). Although the majority of such infections are sexually transmitted and caused by HPV subtypes responsible for genital warts, autoinoculation from common warts, especially on the hands in children, may also cause perianal warts.
synonyms: Condyloma acuminatum of anus | Condylomata acuminata of anus | HPV - [Human papillomavirus] infection of anus

name: Chronic deep bacterial folliculitis
definition: A chronic pyogenic infection by Staphylococcus aureus involving the whole depth of the hair follicle. Sycosis occurs mostly in males after puberty, and commonly involves the follicles of the beard. Most cases begin in the third or fourth decade. Unknown host factors appear to be important in the chronicity of the infection. Extensive follicular destruction and scarring may ensue (lupoid sycosis).
synonyms: sycosis vulgaris | ulerythema sycosiforme
cn_name: 慢性深部细菌性毛囊炎

name: Syphilitic aortitis
definition: This is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum, causing inflammation of the aortic wall.
synonyms: Doehle-Heller aortitis | late quaternary syphilitic aortitis | luetic aortitis | syphilitic aortic arteritis

name: Hereditary ichthyosis
definition: A range of disorders of markedly differing severities resulting from genetically determined defects in epidermal keratinization.
synonyms: Congenital ichthyosis

name: Corticosteroid-induced purpura
definition: Purpura attributable to topical, systemically administered or endogenously produced corticosteroids. It is commonly seen in those who have used topical, inhaled or ingested corticosteroids over a prolonged period. It may also be seen in Cushing syndrome.

name: Palmar hyperhidrosis
synonyms: sweaty palms

name: Erythropoietic protoporphyria
definition: Erythropoietic protoporphyria (EPP) is an inherited disorder of the heme metabolic pathway characterised by accumulation of protoporphyrin in blood, erythrocytes and tissues, and cutaneous manifestations of photosensitivity.
synonyms: EPP - [erythropoietic protoporphyria] | Ferrochelatase deficiency | Heme synthetase deficiency | EEP

name: Radionecrosis of skin due to therapeutic ionizing irradiation
definition: Necrosis and ulceration of skin attributable to radiotherapy
synonyms: Radionecrosis of skin due to radiotherapy | Ulceration of skin due to radiotherapy
cn_name: 治疗性电离辐射引起的皮肤放射性坏死

name: Granulomatous cheilitis
definition: Granulomatous cheilitis is the most frequently observed component of orofacial granulomatosis and is characterised by granulomatous inflammation and lymphoedema of the lips.
synonyms: cheilitis granulomatosa

name: Fat hypertrophy
definition: Focal hypertrophy of subcutaneous adipose tissue. It is a common sequela of long-term insulin injection into the skin.

name: Acute noninfectious otitis externa
definition: Rapid onset of eczematous inflammation of the outer ear the cause of which cannot be more precisely classified.
synonyms: acute eczematoid otitis externa | non-infective acute otitis externa

name: Generalised pustular psoriasis
definition: An inflammatory form of psoriasis characterised by the presence of widely distributed areas of visible sterile pustulation.
synonyms: acute generalised pustular psoriasis of von Zumbusch

name: Non-allergic tattoo reaction
definition: Adverse reaction to the administration or presence of a tattoo which does not involve classical allergic mechanisms. These typically present as inflammatory papules or nodules, which may show granulomatous change including frank sarcoidal granulomas.

name: Primary lesions of pinta
definition: The primary stage of pinta is characterised by a sparse eruption of cutaneous papules and erythematous scaly plaques. This stage may last for months to years.
synonyms: Primary chancre of carate | Primary chancre of pinta | Primary papule of carate | Primary papule of pinta

name: Ischaemic ulceration of skin
synonyms: Arterial ulcer of skin

name: Disseminated dermatophytosis due to immunodeficiency
definition: Disseminated and invasive dermatophyte infection in association with profound immunosuppression.

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum G
definition: Xeroderma pigmentosum complementation group G (XPG) is an extremely rare subtype of xeroderma pigmentosum (XP), a rare photodermatosis predisposing to skin cancers. Clinical manifestations can vary as some patients present with a mild XP phenotype (UV sensitivity, hyper- or hypo-pigmented skin lesions and increased incidence of skin cancer) and others combine symptoms of XP with systemic and neurological manifestations of Cockayne syndrome (XP/CS complex).
synonyms: XPG - [xeroderma pigmentosum G]

name: Corrosion of second degree of trunk
Hordeolum externum
definition: An acute focal pyogenic infection of the eyelash follicle commonly known as a stye and caused predominantly by Staphylococcus aureus. It presents as an acute painful inflammatory eyelid swelling which subsequently discharges at the eyelid margin.
synonyms: Boil involving eyelash follicle | External hordeolum | Follicular abscess of eyelash | Folliculitis of eyelash | Stye

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, metatarsus
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, metatarsus

name: Palmoplantar eccrine hidradenitis
definition: Idiopathic plantar hidradenitis is an uncommon condition affecting children where tender, red lumps appear on the soles and palms. The proposed pathomechanism involves eccrine gland rupture due to mechanical and thermal trauma.
synonyms: palmoplantar eccrine hidradenitis | recurrent palmoplantar hidradenitis

name: Ulcer of penis of uncertain nature
synonyms: Penile ulcer

name: Cholesterol atheroembolism to skin
definition: Embolism of fragments of cholesterol crystal-rich atherosclerotic arterial plaque to the cutaneous vasculature of the extremities. It may follow interventional procedures such as angiography. The early signs (localised cyanosis, purpura or livedo reticularis) are often overlooked but prompt recognition and appropriate intervention may prevent more serious consequences, including gangrene or atheroembolism to internal organs such as the kidneys or the gastrointestinal tract. Accompanying blood eo

name: Precancerous malignant melanoma in melanosis, unspecified site
Papillon-Lefèvre syndrome
definition: Papillon-Lefèvre syndrome (PLS) is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterised by palmoplantar hyperkeratosis and severe early onset of destructive periodontitis leading to premature loss of both primary and permanent dentitions.
synonyms: Cathepsin C deficiency

name: Hereditary hypotrichosis of scalp
definition: Hypotrichosis simplex of the scalp (HSS) is a genetic disorder characterised by diffuse progressive hair loss that is confined to the scalp.
synonyms: Hypotrichosis 2 | Hypotrichosis simplex of scalp

name: Angular cheilitis
definition: Angular cheilitis is an acute or chronic inflammation of the skin and contiguous labial mucous membrane at the angles of the mouth. It is most commonly due to infection with Staphylococcus aureus, Candida or both.
synonyms: angular stomatitis

name: Pityriasis rubra pilaris type III, classical juvenile
definition: This is the most common form of pityriasis rubra pilaris in children and follows a similar course to the classical adult form but with an onset between the ages of 5 and 10 years.

name: Cholestatic pruritus
definition: Pruritus due to defective elimination of bile.

name: Functional vascular disorders of the skin
definition: Skin disorders due to disturbances in vascular tone and skin blood flow.

name: Chronic plaque psoriasis
definition: Plaque psoriasis is the most common presentation of psoriasis. It presents as small to large, well demarcated, red, scaly and thickened areas of skin. It most likely to affect elbows, knees, and lower back but may arise on any part of the body.

name: Fear of cancer of skin
definition: A morbid preoccupation with the possibility of developing skin cancer.

name: Actinic prurigo
synonyms: Hutchinson summer prurigo | Prurigo aestivalis | hutchinson's summer prurigo | hydroa aestivale

name: Ophthalmic zoster
definition: A disease of the eyes, caused by the reactivation of a latent infection with varicella zoster virus in the trigeminal nerve. This disease is characterised by a periorbital rash (typically within one dermatome), and conjunctivitis.
synonyms: Herpes zoster involving eye | Herpes zoster ophthalmicus | Herpes zoster with ophthalmic complications | zoster involving eye | zoster ophthalmicus

name: Pressure ulceration grade 3 of head
synonyms: stage 3 decubitus ulcer of head

name: 16p13.3 deletion
synonyms: Monosomy 16p13.3 | RSTS deletion syndrome | Rubinstein-Taybi deletion syndrome

name: Phototoxic reaction to topical psoralen therapy
definition: Phototoxic reaction caused by topical psoralen therapy containing 5- or 8-methoxypsoralen.

name: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 3-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase deficiency
definition: This is an endocrine disorder that is an uncommon and potentially lethal form of congenital adrenal hyperplasia (CAH). This diagnosis is due to 3-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase deficiency.

name: Acquired hypertrichosis lanuginosa
definition: Patients with hypertrichosis lanuginosa acquisita grow lanugo-type (i.e., fetal) hair near their eyebrows and on their forehead, ears, and nose. Some patients have extensive involvement that includes the extremities, axillae, and trunk, but the palms, soles, and perineum are spared. The hair, referred to as malignant down, is fine, thin, and unpigmented, unlike the robust pigmented terminal hairs characteristic of hirsutism. Although acquired hypertrichosis may be provoked by other diseases or d
synonyms: Paraneoplastic hypertrichosis lanuginosa

name: Aspergillus otomycosis
definition: Chronic superficial fungal infection of the external auditory canal and auricle due to saprophytic fungi of the genus Aspergillus.
synonyms: Aspergillosis of external ear | otitis externa in aspergillosis

name: Nakajo-Nishimura syndrome
definition: Nakajo-Nishimura syndrome is a rare autoinflammatory disorder belonging to the proteasome disability syndrome group, and characterised by pernio-like lesions appearing in infancy followed by recurrent fever, nodular skin eruption, partial lipodystrophy (mainly in upper extremities and face) and joint contractures.
synonyms: ALDD - [autoinflammation - lipoDystrophy - dermatosis syndrome] | CANDLE - [chronic atypical neutrophilic dermatosis with lipodystrophy and elevated temperature syndrome] | Joint contractures - Muscular atrophy - microcytic anaemia - Panniculitis-induced lipodystrophy syndrome [JMP]

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to plastics, glues or resin systems
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to exposure to chemicals used in plastics and resin systems. It is normally the uncured chemicals (e.g. uncured epoxy resin or methacrylates) which are responsible.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to plastics or resin systems
cn_name: 塑料、胶水或树脂类引起的变应性接触性皮炎

name: Poikiloderma
definition: Poikiloderma is defined as a combination of skin atrophy, pigmentation and telangiectasia. It is a component of a number of genetic syndromes, of certain non-organ-specific systemic autoimmune disorders and may follow skin injury including from radiotherapy.

name: Sporadic late-onset primary lymphoedema
definition: Non-familial primary lymphoedema usually presenting after age 35

name: Actinic lentiginosis
definition: The presence of multiple actinic lentigines. This is a common finding in people with fair skin who have a long history of repeated exposure to the sun where it most commonly affects the upper extremities, upper back, forehead and scalp (if bald). It can be generalised and occur at an earlier age in people with an addiction to sun-bathing and use of sun beds.

name: Methylmalonyl-CoA epimerase deficiency with sepiapterin reductase deficiency
definition: This refers to deficiency of an enzyme that catalyzes the isomerization of methylmalonyl-CoA to succinyl-CoA and it is involved in key metabolic pathways. It requires a vitamin B12-derived prosthetic group, adenosylcobalamin, to function. This diagnosis is with sepiapterin reductase deficiency.
synonyms: Methylmalonyl-CoA racemase deficiency with sepiapterin reductase deficiency

name: ADULT syndrome
definition: The ADULT (acro-dermato-ungual-lacrimal-tooth) syndrome is characterised by ectrodactyly, excessive freckling, onychodysplasia, obstruction of lacrimal ducts and hypodontia and/or early loss of permanent teeth.
synonyms: Acro-dermato-ungual-lacrimal-tooth syndrome | Pigment anomaly - ectrodactyly - hypodontia | Propping Zerres syndrome | acro-dermato-ungual-lacrimal-tooth syndrome

name: Exacerbation of constitutional dermatitis due to exposure to skin irritants
definition: Flare of constitutional dermatitis such as atopic eczema attributable to contact with skin irritants.
synonyms: Exacerbation of constitutional eczema due to exposure to skin irritants

name: Infectious dermatitis
definition: Infective dermatitis (infective eczematoid dermatitis) is an acute exudative dermatitis developing on normal skin surrounding a focus of suppurative infection. Such foci include infected wounds, suppurating sinuses and acute inflammatory fungal and/or bacterial infection of the toe clefts. The dermatitis may spread well beyond the skin directly affected by the suppuration.
synonyms: Infective eczema | Infective eczematoid dermatitis

name: Disseminated cutaneous leishmaniasis
definition: This is an uncommon form of leishmaniasis reported principally from Brazil, Venezuela, Guyana and Colombia and is associated with L. braziliensis, L. panamensis or L. guayanensis infection. It is defined by the presence of at least ten circumscribed foci of cutaneous leishmaniasis in more than one body surface region. In contrast to diffuse cutaneous leishmaniasis, in which there is anergy to Leishmania, the leishmanin test is positive in most cases, very few parasites are seen on biopsy and the

name: Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to dental materials
Generalised eruptive keratoacanthomas
synonyms: Grzybowski syndrome

name: Erythema nodosum triggered by pregnancy
definition: Erythema nodosum (EN) occurring during pregnancy, which accounts for some 2% of all cases of EN. The mechanisms involved are unknown, though hormonal factors are likely to be relevant as EN is also linked to oral contraceptive use. The EN may be recurrent with each pregnancy.

name: Burn of wrist or hand, full thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of wrist and hand, third degree

name: Familial multiple lipomata
definition: This autosomal dominant inherited condition is characterised by the development during or soon after adolescence of multiple small, well-demarcated, encapsulated lipomata, typically involving the extremities. In contrast to benign symmetrical lipomatosis the neck and shoulders usually are spared.

name: Penile melanotic macule
definition: Discrete circumscribed area of macular hypermelanosis affecting the glans penis or the shaft of the penis. These are much commoner than melanoma of the penis but may be clinically suspected to be melanoma and thus require biopsy. By definition the cause is unknown.

name: Pruritus
definition: An intense itching sensation that produces the urge to rub or scratch the skin to obtain relief.

name: Glomerular disease associated with Fabry disease
Pemphigus erythematosus
definition: Pemphigus erythematosus is a rare variant of pemphigus foliaceus, and is a chronic autoimmune skin disease with clinical and immunological features of lupus erythematosus as well as pemphigus. It is characterised by superficial blister formation giving scaly lesions in a butterfly distribution over the nose and cheeks as well as the trunk and is mediated by auto-antibodies to the desmosome component desmoglein 1.
synonyms: Senear-Usher syndrome | senear-usher syndrome

name: Chronic otitis externa
definition: This is a chronic inflammation of the outer ear and external ear canal (external auditory canal).

name: Cutis verticis gyrata - retinitis pigmentosa - sensorineural deafness
definition: Cutis verticis gyrata - retinitis pigmentosa - sensorineural deafness is an inherited syndrome characterised by microcephaly, primary cutis verticis gyrata, progressive retinitis pigmentosa, cataracts, sensorineural deafness and intellectual deficit.

name: Molluscum contagiosum
definition: A disease of the skin and mucous membranes, caused by an infection with molluscum contagiosum virus. This disease is characterised by papular skin eruptions, commonly 2-3 millimetres in diameter. Transmission is by direct contact.
synonyms: Molluscum verrucosum | Water warts

name: Familial partial lipodystrophy due to AKT2 mutations
definition: Familial partial lipodystrophy associated with AKT2 mutations belongs to the group of lipodystrophy syndromes characterised by anomalies of adipose tissue distribution and often associated with severe insulin resistance manifested by hyperinsulinaemia (insulin levels above 20mUI/l), carbohydrate intolerance or diabetes, hypertriglyceridaemia with low levels of high-density lipoproteins (HDL) and arterial hypertension. Acanthosis nigricans, hepatic steatosis and polycystic ovary syndrome are also

name: Adverse effects of psoralen photochemotherapy
definition: Adverse effects from exposure of the skin to psoralen and ultraviolet A (PUVA) phototherapy
synonyms: Adverse effects of PUVA

name: Cholinergic pruritus
definition: Pruritus resulting from the same stimuli as cholinergic urticaria but without visible weals. The diagnosis is often overlooked.

name: Contractures - ectodermal dysplasia - cleft lip or palate
definition: Contractures - ectodermal dysplasia - cleft lip/palate syndrome is characterised by severe congenital contractures, multiple cutaneous manifestations of ectodermal dysplasia, cleft lip/palate, and psychomotor and growth impairment.

name: Anogenital psoriasis
definition: Psoriasis involving anogenital skin including the vulva, penis or perianal area.

name: Scarring alopecia
definition: Hair loss in which there is irreversible damage to the hair follicle from inflammation, infection, malignant infiltration or trauma, resulting in destruction of the follicle and repair by fibrosis. Regeneration does not occur once the follicle has been destroyed.
synonyms: Cicatricial alopecia | scarring alopecia

name: Lupus anticoagulant-hypoprothrombinaemia syndrome
cn_name: 狼疮抗凝物-低凝血酶原血症综合征

name: Bullous impetigo
definition: Bullous impetigo is a contagious superficial infection of the skin caused by certain strains of Staphylococcus aureus which release toxins into the local environment which are capable of cleaving desmoglein I, a protein involved in intercellular adhesion of epidermal keratinocytes. In contrast to the very superficial, rapidly shed and rarely observed blisters of non-bullous impetigo, the bullae of bullous impetigo are tense and well demarcated, sometimes reaching several centimetres in diameter

name: Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome without pulmonary fibrosis
definition: Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome without pulmonary fibrosis as a complication includes three relatively mild types (HPS-3, HPS-5 and HPS-6) of Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome (HPS; see this term), a multi-system disorder characterized by ocular or oculocutaneous albinism, bleeding diathesis and, in some cases, granulomatous colitis.HPS-3 occurs as a genetic isolate in central Puerto Rico with a prevalence of 1/4000; additionally, around 15 non-Puerto-Rican cases of HPS-3 have been reported. Ten cases of HPS-
synonyms: HPS - [Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome] without pulmonary fibrosis

name: Sequelae of burn or corrosion of wrist or hand
Malassezia folliculitis
definition: Malassezia folliculitis is caused by the invasion of the hair follicle by Malassezia yeasts. Although Malassezia yeasts are a part of the normal human microflora, under certain conditions they can cause superficial dermatological conditions. The invasion results in the development of erythematous papules, and sometimes pustules, which may be either asymptomatic or itchy. Usually Malassezia yeasts are present along with staphylococci and propionibacteria in the follicles.
synonyms: Pityrosporum folliculitis | Seborrhoeic folliculitis

name: Neonatal streptococcal necrotising fasciitis
definition: Neonatal necrotising fasciitis due to beta-haemolytic streptococci.

name: Macroglossia due to primary systemic amyloidosis
definition: Diffuse infiltration of the tongue by amyloid light chain fragments. Primary amyloidosis is the commonest cause of macroglossia in adults and may be the presenting sign of multiple myeloma.

name: Hidradenitis suppurativa, atypical forms
definition: Atypical forms of hidradenitis suppurativa including forms associated with other types of skin inflammation such as pyoderma gangrenosum and acne conglobata.

name: Genitocrural dermatophytosis
definition: Dermatophyte infection of the inguinocrural folds and adjacent external genitalia (tinea cruris). It presents as erythema and inflammation of affected skin with an advancing scaly edge. It is typically itchy and affects adult men much more commonly than women or children. Dermatophyte infection of the toe clefts commonly co-exists.
synonyms: Dermatophytosis of groin | Jock itch | Ringworm of groin | Tinea cruris

name: Irritant contact dermatitis of hands
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis affecting skin of hands. This is the commonest site for the development of irritant contact dermatitis. In the early stages the dorsal finger-webs are affected before the inflammation extends to involve the fingers, the dorsa of the hands and frequently the wrists. The palms are usually but not always less severely affected.
synonyms: Irritant contact hand eczema | Irritant hand dermatitis

name: Painful bruising syndrome
definition: Painful bruising syndrome (Gardner-Diamond syndrome, autoerythrocyte sensitization, psychogenic purpura) is a rare and poorly understood clinical presentation of unexplained painful ecchymoses, mostly on the extremities and/or the face. It has been associated with emotional stress or one or more concomitant mental illnesses.
synonyms: Factitious purpura | Gardner-Diamond syndrome | Psychogenic purpura | autoerythrocyte sensitization | psychogenic purpura

name: Juvenile syphilis of central nervous system with general paralysis
synonyms: juvenilis dementia paralytica

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, sternoclavicular joint
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, sternoclavicular joint

name: Pressure ulceration grade 2 of iliac crest
synonyms: stage 2 decubitus ulcer of iliac crest

name: Drug-induced oral conditions
synonyms: medicament-induced oral conditions

name: Infection of finger
synonyms: infected finger | infection of skin of finger

name: Disorders of cutaneous blood and lymphatic vessels
cn_name: 皮肤血管或淋巴管障碍

name: Centrifugal lipodystrophy
definition: Centrifugal lipodystrophy is characterised by localised lipoatrophy typically affecting the groin or axillae which expands centrifugally. The condition predominantly affects children under the age of four who are otherwise well. Most cases have been reported from Japan. The cause is unknown.
synonyms: centrifugal lipodystrophy

name: Varioliform atrophy of facial skin
definition: Small round or linear asymptomatic depressions in the skin with no preceding inflammation or trauma and occurring most commonly on the cheeks, chin or forehead.
synonyms: Atrophia maculosa varioliformis cutis

name: Lipoid proteinosis
definition: Lipoid proteinosis (LiP), also known as 'hyalinosis cutis et mucosae' or 'Urbach-Wiethe disease' is an autosomal recessive disorder that presents in early infancy with hoarseness, followed by pox-like and acneform scars, along with infiltration and thickening of the skin and certain mucous membranes due to deposition of hyaline-like material and disruption/reduplication of the basement membrane around blood vessels and at the dermal-epidermal junction.
synonyms: Hyalinosis cutis et mucosae | Urbach lipoid proteinosis | Urbach-Wiethe disease | lipid proteinosis | lipidosis cutis et mucosae | lipoid proteinosis of Urbach and Wiethe | hyalinosis cutic et mucosae | urbach-wiethe disease

name: Autosomal recessive epidermolytic ichthyosis
definition: A rare recessive form of epidermolytic ichthyosis due to a nonsense mutation in the KRT10 gene leading to loss of K10 expression in affected homozygous individuals.
synonyms: Autosomal recessive epidermolytic hyperkeratosis

name: Pressure ulceration grade 2 of ischium
synonyms: stage 2 decubitus ulcer of ischium

name: Weathered hair
definition: Weathered hair results from repetitive damage to the hair shaft from excessive sunlight, excessive wetting, chemical insults including hair cosmetics and physical damage. The hair appears dull and lustreless, fractures readily and may show \"split ends\".

name: Seborrhoeic keratosis
definition: Seborrhoeic keratoses are very common benign neoplasms of epidermal keratinocytes which increase in prevalence and number with age. They are commonly multiple and are very variable in shape and colour. Because of the sometimes intense pigmentation they are frequently mistaken for melanocytic tumours.
synonyms: Basal cell papilloma | Seborrheic wart

name: Blepharophyma
definition: Hypertrophy of the sebaceous glands of the eyelids, leading to swelling of the eyelids.

name: Infantile perianal warts
definition: Perianal warts in infants are thought to arise principally as the result of infection with genital subtypes of human papillomavirus acquired during birth from the mother's birth canal.
synonyms: Perianal warts in infancy

name: Chalazion
definition: A chalazion is a small cyst on the eyelid caused by blockage of a meibomian gland.
synonyms: Meibomian cyst | Tarsal cyst | Chalazion | meibomian gland lipogranuloma

name: Burn of ankle or foot, deep partial thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of ankle or foot, deep second degree
cn_name: 踝或足深层部分皮层烧伤

name: Acrodermatitis chronica atrophicans
definition: This is a late cutaneous manifestation of Lyme borreliosis seen mainly in northern, central and eastern Europe. It presents one or more years after the original infection as an erythematous plaque, which slowly enlarges and gradually becomes violaceous and atrophic. Typical sites are the hands, feet, knees and elbows. Spirochaetes have occasionally been cultured but B. burgdorferi serology is strongly positive in all cases.
synonyms: Acrodermatitis atrophicans | Cutaneous atrophy due to Lyme borreliosis | ACA | herxheimer disease | Herxheimer disease | primary diffuse atrophy

name: Irritant contact dermatitis of vulva
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis affecting the vulva and surrounding skin. It is much more frequent than allergic contact dermatitis in this area. It is commonly due to a combination of occlusion and use of feminine hygiene products in the genital area. Leakage of urine or profuse vaginal discharge are sometimes important factors.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema of vulva | Irritant contact vulvitis | Vulval irritant contact dermatitis

name: Olfactory reference disorder with fair to good insight
definition: All definitional requirements of olfactory reference disorder are met. Much of the time, the individual is able to entertain the possibility that his or her disorder-specific beliefs may not be true and is willing to accept an alternative explanation for his or her experience. At circumscribed times (e.g., when highly anxious), the individual may demonstrate no insight.

name: Oculo-osteo-cutaneous syndrome
definition: Oculoosteocutaneous syndrome is characterised by congenital anodontia, a small maxilla, short stature with shortened metacarpals and metatarsals, sparse hair, albinoidism and multiple ocular anomalies.

name: Occupationally-acquired parasitic skin infestation, classified elsewhere
definition: Infestations such as pediculosis and scabies acquired through work. These are relatively common in health care workers, teachers and in those employed in residential homes for the elderly. Agricultural workers may be at increased risk of tropical parasitic infestations such as schistosomiasis.

name: Digital clubbing
definition: Clubbing of the fingers and toenails is an anatomical abnormality resulting from swelling of the soft tissues of the terminal phalanx of a digit which obliterates the normal angle between the base of the nail and the digit. It may become manifest early in life as a hereditary defect in otherwise healthy individuals but is also associated with a range of underlying systemic disorders including cardiac disease (cyanotic congenital heart disease, infective endocarditis), pulmonary disease (empyema,
synonyms: Club finger | Club nail | Clubbed fingers | Clubbing of nails | Drumstick fingers | drumstick fingers | hippocratic fingers | watch-glass nails

name: Skin or soft tissue injury due to exposure to cold
Alopecia universalis
definition: Alopecia universalis is the most severe form of alopecia areata and is defined as complete disappearance of hair from all parts of the body including scalp, eyebrows, eyelids, face, axillae and genital region.
synonyms: Universal alopecia areata

name: Neonatal vitamin K deficiency
definition: There are 3 forms of vitamin K–deficiency bleeding (VKDB) of the newborn. Early VKDB (haemorrhagic disease of the newborn) that occurs at 1-14 days of age. The most common sites of bleeding are the gastrointestinal tract, mucosal and cutaneous tissue, the umbilical stump, and the post-circumcision site. Late VKDB most commonly occurs at 2-12 weeks of age, although cases can occur up to 6 months. The most common site of bleeding is intracranial, although cutaneous and gastrointestinal bleeding ma

name: Cutaneous reactions to beetles
definition: Several blister species (e.g. Lytta vesicatoria, Thelyphassa lineata) contain vesicant fluids including cantharidin which can produce skin irritation and blistering. This usually causes harm only when the beetle is crushed against the skin. Weals form rapidly and are followed by blisters after 12 to 24 hours.
synonyms: Blister beetle reaction | blister beetle dermatitis

name: Cheese itch
synonyms: copra itch

name: Endovascular lymphatic angioendothelioma of unspecified site
Skin problem resulting from colostomy
definition: An entity to be used to link a specific skin problem (e.g. psoriasis or parastomal pyoderma gangrenosum) to its proximity to a colostomy.

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, buttock
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, buttock

name: Pseudoporphyria due to excessive exposure to UV radiation
definition: A blistering eruption clinically indistinguishable from porphyria cutanea tarda which may arise in patients with severe photodamage or prolonged exposure to psoralen photochemotherapy but normal porphyrins.

name: Genetic disorders of the immune system affecting the skin
Irritant contact dermatitis of perianal skin
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis affecting perianal skin. This may be due to anal seepage, use of irritant laxatives or to irritation from the use of topical medications for anal pruritus etc. Over-zealous cleansing may also result in irritant damage.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema of perianal skin | Perianal irritant contact dermatitis

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum variant
definition: Xeroderma pigmentosum variant is a milder subtype of xeroderma pigmentosum (XP), a rare photodermatosis characterised by severe sun sensitivity and an increased risk of skin cancer. Patients present with skin lesions, generally at the sun-exposed parts of the body which develop into skin cancer at about 20-30 years of age with a 1,000 times higher frequency than that of the general population. No neurological manifestations are observed.

name: Hereditary sclerosing poikiloderma with cardiac anomalies
Miscellaneous urticarial disorders
definition: A heterogeneous group of disorders amongst the manifestations of which are urticarial reactions or angioedema.

name: Erythrokeratodermia variabilis et progressiva
definition: Mendes da Costa type erythrokeratodermia variabilis (EKV) is a genodermatosis characterised by the appearance of two independent skin lesions\: transient figurate erythematous patches and hyperkeratosis that is usually localised but occasionally occurs in its generalised form, generally during the first year of life.

name: Sycosis barbae
definition: A chronic deep bacterial folliculitis of the beard area.

name: Mycosis fungoides variants
definition: Uncommon forms of mycosis fungoides with distinctive manifestations

name: Spirillosis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Spirillum minus. This disease is initially characterised by local inflammation, followed by fever, lymphadenitis, and headache. Transmission is commonly by direct contact through the bite or scratch of an infected rat. Confirmation is by identification of Spirillum in blood or tissue samples.
synonyms: Rat-bite fever due to Spirillum minus | Sodoku | Sokosho | Spirillary rat-bite fever

name: Secondary hypertrophic osteoarthropathy
definition: Sporadic hypertrophic osteoarthropathy has been described in association with a large number of malignancies of which bronchial carcinoma is the commonest and with a wide range of non-malignant disorders including congenital cyanotic heart disease, chronic pulmonary disease, especially cystic fibrosis and chronic liver diseases. It is much commoner than primary hypertrophic osteoarthropathy.

name: Familial hypercholesterolaemia - homozygous
definition: This is a homozygous genetic disorder characterised by high cholesterol levels, specifically very high levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL, \"bad cholesterol\"), in the blood and early cardiovascular disease.

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, ankle joint
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, ankle joint

name: Dysaesthetic penodynia
definition: An uncommon but distressing somatoform disorder in which there is a clear and precise complaint of penile pain and/or burning sensation for which no underlying cause can be found.
synonyms: Dysaesthetic penile pain

name: Paucibacillary leprosy
synonyms: Hansen disease, paucibacillary

name: Cutaneous suture granuloma
definition: Granulomatous inflammation surrounding skin sutures may present clinically as inflammatory papules and nodules. This is more likely to occur with absorbable sutures which are left in situ in the dermis than with removable nylon or polypropylene sutures. The suture material is often extruded through the epidermis after which the inflammation will settle. .
synonyms: Stitch granuloma, skin | Suture granuloma, skin

name: Ectrodactyly - ectodermal dysplasia without clefting
definition: Ectrodactyly - ectodermal dysplasia without clefting is the association of ectrodactyly (split hands and feet) and ectodermal dysplasia (mainly hypotrichosis and dentition anomalies).

name: Ecthyma gangrenosum
definition: Ecthyma gangrenosum is a potentially life-threatening infection of skin in patients who are immunocompromised through disease or immunosuppressive therapy. It is most commonly caused by Pseudomonas aeruginosa though a variety of other organisms may be implicated. It is characterised by usually painless erythematous macules or plaques which progress to haemorrhagic blistering and necrosis of the skin. Lesions may be multiple and widely disseminated, though the anogenital area is a common site. Ps

name: Noninfectious enteritis or ulcer due to IgA vasculitis
definition: This refers to a non-infective inflammation of the small intestine and ulcer, due to due to a immune-mediated vasculitis, Henoch-Schönlein purpura, that also often affects skin and kidney.
synonyms: noninfective enteritis and ulcer due to Henoch-Schönlein purpura

name: Cryptophthalmia
definition: Isolated cryptophthalmia is a congenital abnormality in which the eyelids are absent and skin covers the ocular bulb, which is often microphthalmic.
synonyms: Cryptophthalmos NOS | Microblepharon

name: Geroderma osteodysplasticum
definition: Geroderma osteodysplastica belongs to the group of cutis laxa syndromes and is characterised by lax and wrinkled skin (especially on the dorsum of the hands and feet and abdomen), progeroid features, hip dislocation, joint laxity, severe short stature/dwarfism, severe osteoporosis, vertebral abnormalities and spontaneous fractures, and developmental delay and mild intellectual deficit.
synonyms: geroderma osteodysplasticum

name: Corticosteroid-induced stretch marks
synonyms: Striae distensae due to corticosteroids

name: Lymphoedema due to podoconiosis
definition: Lymphoedema of the lower limbs resulting from an inflammatory response within lymphatic vessels to mineral particles from soil in genetically susceptible individuals. It is a leading cause of lower limb lymphoedema in farmers in Africa, Central America and India.
synonyms: Non-filarial elephantiasis | Podoconiosis

name: Insulin-induced localised lipoatrophy
definition: Insulin-induced localised lipoatrophy is the loss of subcutaneous fat at the site of insulin injection.

name: Crumpled ear
definition: Distortion of the course of the normal folds of the ear and the appearance of supernumerary crura and folds.

name: Cutaneous tuberculosis
definition: Tuberculosis involving the skin and mucous membranes including lupus vulgaris, scrofuloderma and periorificial tuberculosis.
synonyms: Tuberculosis cutis | Tuberculosis of skin

name: Chronic radiodermatitis, not elsewhere classified
definition: Chronic radiodermatitis first becomes apparent six months or more following exposure of the skin to ionizing radiation and is characterised by atrophy, fibrosis, dyspigmentation alopecia and telangiectasia. Eventually ulceration may occur as the result of a compromised blood supply.

name: 1q21.1 deletion
definition: 1q21.1 microdeletion syndrome is a recurrent deletion syndrome with variable clinical manifestations including variable intellectual deficit and facial dysmorphism, but without the clinical picture of thrombocytopenia - absent radius (TAR) syndrome, which is also caused by 1q21.1 deletions.
synonyms: 1q21.1 deletion syndrome | Monosomy 1q21.1

name: In situ neoplasms of skin
definition: Cutaneous neoplasms in which cells with cytological changes of the type associated with invasive neoplasms are confined to their location of origin, usually the epidermis or adnexal epithelium.

name: Extranodal marginal zone B-cell lymphoma, primary site skin
definition: A low-grade, extranodal marginal zone B-cell lymphoma of the mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue that arises from the skin. It usually presents with multifocal papular or nodular lesions in the arms or trunk. It rarely disseminates to internal organs or progresses to high grade lymphoma.
synonyms: Primary cutaneous marginal zone B-cell lymphoma | SALT - [skin associated lymphoid tissue-related B-cell lymphoma] | extranodal marginal zone lymphoma of mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue | MALT lymphoma | PCMZL
cn_name: 结外边缘区淋巴瘤，原发皮肤
snomed: 404140004 "primary cutaneous marginal zone b-cell lymphoma";420028002 "primary cutaneous marginal zone b-cell lymphoma"

name: Normal skin
definition: The absence of any demonstrable skin abnormality.

name: HIV-associated seborrhoeic dermatitis
definition: Seborrhoeic dermatitis associated with underlying HIV infection. Although seborrhoeic dermatitis is a common disease affecting 2%-4% of the general population, up to 80% of HIV-infected people develop seborrhoeic dermatitis at some stage after becoming infected. It is postulated that the alteration of the immune system in HIV-AIDS changes the way the skin responds to the presence of Malassezia yeasts on the skin. Seborrhoeic dermatitis is more prevalent and has an earlier onset after acquisition
synonyms: Seborrhoeic dermatitis associated with HIV infection

name: Digital or periungual warts
definition: Viral warts affecting the fingers, thumbs or non-plantar (or non-weight-bearing) skin of the toes. They are often difficult to eradicate, particularly if the nail folds are involved, but most will eventually resolve spontaneously.
synonyms: Verrucae of fingers | periungual viral warts | periungual warts of fingers and toes

name: Annular erythema
definition: Annular erythema is the term given to a group of chronic annular and gyrate eruptions in which irregular rings and arcs of elevated erythema form from initial inflammatory papules which slowly enlarge whilst clearing centrally. The lesions are usually located on the buttocks, thighs and upper arms, but any area may be involved. The condition may persist for months to years. In the majority of cases the aetiology remains obscure.
synonyms: Erythema annulare

name: Palmoplantar keratoderma – digital clubbing – acro-osteolysis
synonyms: Bureau-Barrière-Thomas palmoplantar keratoderma

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, metatarsus
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, metatarsus

name: Photo-allergic contact dermatitis due to preservatives or biocides
definition: Photo-allergic contact dermatitis due to preservatives and biocides such as bithionol, triclosan and chlorhexidine.

name: Cutis laxa
definition: Cutis laxa is the term used for a group of inherited and acquired conditions in which abnormalities of elastic fibres result in loose, redundant, hypoelastic skin. Typically, the skin can easily be pulled away from underlying tissue and only slowly returns to its original position. Unlike some conditions in the differential diagnosis, cutis laxa is not characterised by spontaneous bruising or abnormal scarring. Redundant skin is often most noticeable on the neck, hands, and groin, but can also b
synonyms: dermatochalasia | dermatomegaly | generalized elastorrhexis | generalized elastosis

name: Syndromes with urticarial reactions or angioedema
definition: Periodic and other syndromes in which urticarial reactions or angioedema play an important part.
cn_name: 伴荨麻疹或血管性水肿的综合征

name: Non-specific balanoposthitis
definition: Inflammation of the glans penis and/or prepuce which cannot be attributed to a specific cause. It is usually regarded as an irritant reaction to the presence of certain microorganisms including Pseudomonas and Candida.
synonyms: Non-specific balanitis

name: Fabry disease
definition: Fabry disease (FD) is a progressive, inherited, multisystemic lysosomal storage disease characterised by specific neurological, cutaneous, renal, cardiovascular, cochleo-vestibular and cerebrovascular manifestations.
synonyms: Alpha-galactosidase A deficiency | Anderson-Fabry disease | Ceramide trihexosidase deficiency | Fabry disease X-linked | anderson-fabry disease

name: Lipoedematous alopecia
definition: Lipoedematous alopecia is an uncommon condition of poorly understood aetiology characterised by dermal oedema and thickening of the subcutaneous adipose tissue layer of the scalp with varying degrees of hair loss but no clearly associated medical disorder. It is commoner in women.

name: Miscellaneous specified genetic syndromes affecting the skin
Chondroectodermal dysplasia
definition: Ellis-van Creveld syndrome (EVC) is a chondral and ectodermal dysplasia characterised by short ribs, polydactyly, growth retardation, and ectodermal and heart defects (abnormalities of atrial septation).
synonyms: EVC - [Ellis-van Creveld] syndrome | Ellis-van Creveld syndrome | Mesodermic dysplasia | EVC syndrome

name: Furuncle
definition: A localised infection of a hair follicle by Staphylococcus aureus. It manifests as a painful swollen purulent mass centred on a hair follicle.
synonyms: Boil | boil

name: Syphilis of penis
synonyms: syphilitic chancre of penis | syphilitic penis

name: Endemic non-venereal syphilis
definition: Endemic non-venereal syphilis is caused by Treponema pallidum subspecies endemicum and is transmitted by skin-to-skin or mouth-to-mouth contact rather than sexual contact. Children are at greatest risk of infection. Clinical features are similar to venereal syphilis with a primary ulcer (usually in the mouth or on the nipples of breast-feeding women nursing infected children) and, in the secondary stage, a generalised papular rash, oral mucous patches, condylomata lata and generalised lymphadeno
synonyms: Bejel | Belesh | Dichuchwa | Endemic syphilis | Frenga | Njovera | Siti | Skerljevo | bejel

name: Burn of hip or leg, except ankle or foot, full thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of hip and leg, except ankle or foot, third degree
cn_name: 臀或腿（除外踝或足）全层皮肤烧伤

name: Pressure ulceration grade 1 of sacrum
synonyms: stage 1 decubitus ulcer of sacrum

name: Skin graft failure
definition: Failure of skin graft tissue to engraft as intended

name: Irritant contact blepharoconjunctivitis
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis affecting skin of eyelid and/or conjunctiva. Cosmetics are often responsible.
synonyms: Irritant contact dermatitis of eyelids | Irritant contact eczema of eyelid

name: High grade squamous intraepithelial lesion of anal margin or perianal skin
Pinta
definition: A disease of the skin, caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum carateum. This disease is characterised by hyperkeratosis and hyperpigmentation. Transmission may be by direct contact.
synonyms: Carate | azul | infection by treponema carateum | mal del pinto | pinta NOS | pinta lesion

name: Physiological cutis marmorata
definition: Physiological cutis marmorata is a transient cyanotic mottling of the skin (livedo reticularis) which occurs as a response to cold exposure and disappears with warming. It is usually encountered in healthy infants and resolves during the first year of life. Involvement of the trunk as well as the limbs is common. Physiological cutis marmorata rarely occurs in adults though it may be seen in association with vascular stasis resulting from immobility or paralysis.
synonyms: Physiological livedo reticularis

name: Genetic disorders of keratinisation
definition: Heritable disorders characterised by abnormal epidermal keratinization. They include the ichthyoses and palmoplantar keratodermas.

name: Adiaspirosis
synonyms: haplosporangiosis

name: Clear cell acanthoma
definition: Clear cell acanthoma manifests as a firm, brown-red, dome-shaped nodule or papule with a predilection for the lower legs. Histopathologically it is characterised by sharply demarcated psoriasiform epidermal hyperplasia containing glycogen-rich keratinocytes with pale-staining cytoplasm. The dermoscopic appearance of clear cell acanthoma is very characteristic with dotted blood vessels are arranged as \"pearls on a string\".
synonyms: degos' acanthoma | Pale (Clear cell) acanthoma

name: GAPO syndrome
definition: GAPO syndrome is a very rare genetic disorder which name is the acronym summarizing the manifestations observed in this syndrome\: Growth retardation with delayed bone age, Alopecia (acquired after birth), Pseudoanodontia (unerupted decidual and permanent teeth) and Optic atrophy (inconstant).
synonyms: Growth delay - alopecia - pseudoanodontia - optic atrophy

name: High grade squamous intraepithelial lesion of vulva, HPV-associated
definition: Squamous intraepithelial lesions (SILs) of the vulva (also known as vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia \[VIN\]), HPV-associated, are proliferations of squamous cells driven by HPV infection, showing maturation abnormalities and nuclear hyperchromasia that do not extend beyond the basement membrane.
synonyms: High grade squamous intraepithelial lesion of vulva NOS | VIN II Vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia [grade II] NOS | VIN II, HPV associated | VIN II-III -[vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia grade 2 -3] moderate and severe dysplasia (deprecated) | VIN III Vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia [grade III] NOS | VIN III, HPV associated | Vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia, classical type | Vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia, undifferentiated | Vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia, usual (classical) | Vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia, usual type

name: Maple-syrup-urine disease
definition: Maple syrup urine disease (MSUD) is a disorder of branched-chain amino acids metabolism. Four forms are described. The early onset classic form manifests after birth by lethargy, poor feeding and neurological signs of intoxication. Clinical course without treatment is characterised by deepening coma with maple syrup odour of urine. Subacute MSUD manifests later with encephalopathy, mental disability, major hypotonia, opisthotonus and cerebral atrophy with severe outcome. The intermittent form of
synonyms: BCKD - [branched-chain alpha-ketoacid dehydrogenase deficiency] | Branched chain ketoaciduria | Ketoacid decarboxylase deficiency | Ketoaminoacidaemia | MSUD - [Maple-syrup-urine disease] | Oxoacid decarboxylase deficiency | branched chain ketoacid dehydrogenase deficiency | branched-chain alpha-keto acid dehydrogenase deficiency | ketoacidaemia | maple-syrup-urine disorder

name: Temporal triangular alopecia
definition: Temporal triangular alopecia is a circumscribed, non-scarring, non-inflammatory permanent alopecia of unknown cause confined to the frontotemporal scalp. The area of hair loss is commonly triangular but may be oval or lancet-shaped. It is usually unilateral. The alopecia is apparent from birth in about a third of cases but may not be noted until later childhood. It has, rarely, been associated with phakomatosis pigmentovascularis.
synonyms: Triangular alopecia | congenital triangular alopecia

name: Contact dermatitis of specified site
definition: Allergic or irritant contact dermatitis classified by involved site

name: Chronic urticaria of undetermined aetiology
definition: Chronic urticaria for which no cause can be found (idiopathic)
synonyms: Chronic idiopathic urticaria

name: Late syphilitic polyneuropathy
definition: This is a late, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis with a serious, unpredictable, occasionally progressive, and life threatening neurological disorder that occurs when many nerves throughout the body malfunction simultaneously.

name: Frostbite with tissue necrosis of arm
synonyms: frostbite of upper limb with tissue necrosis

name: Trichilemmal cyst
definition: A trichilemmal (pilar) cyst is a common, typically non-tender, intradermal or subcutaneous cyst. The cysts are typically confined to the scalp and are often multiple. They usually occur sporadically but may be inherited in an autosomal dominant manner. They are derived from the outer root sheath of the hair follicle and as such contain keratin or keratin degradation products. Rarely, they may undergo malignant transformation.
synonyms: Follicular isthmus cyst | Isthmus catagen cyst | Pilar cyst | Sebaceous cyst of scalp | Wen | isthmus-catagen cyst | pilar cyst | wen

name: Irritant contact dermatitis due to friction, sweating or contact with body fluids
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis due to friction, sweating and contact with body fluids. Irritation from body fluids may be due to high or low pH, to proteolytic enzymes or both; the irritant effect may be aggravated or caused solely by sweating and repetitive friction of apposed skin surfaces.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema due to friction, sweating or contact with body fluids
cn_name: 摩擦、出汗或接触体液引起的刺激性接触性皮炎

name: Syphilitic endocarditis
definition: This is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum, causing inflammation of the inner layer of the heart, the endocardium.
synonyms: endocardial involvement in syphilis

name: Acral persistent papular mucinosis
definition: Acral persistent papular mucinosis is a rare chronic form of localised lichen myxedematosus characterised by the development of multiple symmetrical skin-coloured mucinous papules exclusively on the extensor surface of the hands and distal forearms.

name: Syphilitic aortic incompetence
definition: This is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is involving aortic incompetence.

name: Fluid overload with localised oedema
definition: This is the condition where there is too much fluid in the blood. The opposite condition is hypovolemia, which is too little fluid volume in the blood. Fluid volume excess in the intravascular compartment occurs due to an increase in total body sodium content and a consequent increase in extracellular body water. The mechanism usually stems from compromised regulatory mechanisms for sodium handling as seen in congestive heart failure (CHF), kidney failure, and liver failure. It may also be cause

name: Chronic recurrent multifocal osteomyelitis
definition: Chronic nonbacterial osteomyelitis (CNO), also known as chronic recurrent multifocal osteomyelitis (CRMO), is a chronic autoinflammatory syndrome that is characterized by multiple foci of painful swelling of bones, mainly in the metaphyses of the long bones, in addition to the pelvis, the shoulder girdle and the spine.Depending on the studies, prevalence of CNO/CRMO has been grossly estimated at between 1/ 160,000 to 1/ 2,000,000 and incidence at between 1/ 250,000 to 1/ 1,000,000. The disease a

name: Discrete acquired superficial venous or capillary ectasias
Chromoblastomycosis
definition: Chromoblastomycosis is a chronic fungal infection of the skin and subcutaneous tissues caused by a variety of pigmented fungi including Phialophora verrucosa, Fonsecaea pedrosoi, Fonsecaea compacta and Cladophialophora carrionii, which can be found in soil and wood. The infection usually follows trauma, such as a puncture from a splinter of wood and tends to affect exposed sites such as the feet and ankles. Chromoblastomycosis manifests initially as a warty papule which slowly enlarges to form a
synonyms: Drechslera hawaiiensis | Drechslera infection | Fonseca disease | Pedroso disease | Verrucous dermatitis | chromomycosis | chondromycosis | cladosporiosis | fonseca's disease | pedroso's disease

name: Necrotising fasciitis
synonyms: Necrotising fasciitis NOS | necrotic fasciitis | necrotizing fasciitis | flesh-eating bacteria syndrome

name: Morphoea
definition: A group of related diseases of poorly understood aetiology affecting principally skin and subcutaneous tissue and characterised by variable fibrosis, sclerosis and cutaneous atrophy.
