synonyms: Morphea

name: Human T-cell lymphotropic virus type 1 associated infective dermatitis of childhood
definition: An inflammatory dermatitis of childhood which develops in a minority of children with HTLV‐1 infection, acquired usually via breast milk from an infected mother. Although the disease may clear spontaneously in adolescence, there is a high risk of development of Human T-cell lymphotropic virus-associated myelopathy (tropical spastic paraparesis) in later life. The onset is most frequent between the ages of 2 and 3, but it can manifest from as early as a few months of age to late childhood. Sites
synonyms: HTLV‐1 associated infective dermatitis of childhood | Human T-lymphotropic virus type 1 associated infective dermatitis of childhood
cn_name: 人类T细胞淋巴亲和病毒1型相关儿童期感染性皮炎

name: Orofacial granulomatosis
definition: Orofacial granulomatosis (OFG) is an uncommon clinicopathological entity characterised by persistent and/or recurrent lymphoedema and fissuring of the lips and oral mucosa (the latter resulting in a \"cobblestone\" appearance), oral ulceration and the presence on biopsy of non-caseating granulomas. OFG may be an early manifestation of Crohn disease, sometimes presenting months or years before bowel disease. Some cases are associated with dental infections, sarcoidosis, food allergy or contact al
synonyms: cheilitis granulomatosa | granulomatous cheilitis | miescher's cheilitis

name: Homogeneous leukoplakia
definition: Homogenous leukoplakia is usually well defined white patch of uniform, flat appearance and texture, although there may be superficial irregularities.
synonyms: Thick leukoplakia

name: Viral warts due to iatrogenic immunosuppression
definition: Abnormal proliferation of human papillomavirus as a result of deliberately induced immunosuppression, particularly in organ transplant recipients.
synonyms: Viral warts in transplant recipients

name: Malignant metastasis neoplasm involving skin of abdominal wall
Drug-induced oral ulcer
definition: Ulceration of the oral mucosa attributable to medication. Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) and cytotoxic drugs such as methotrexate are well recognised causes but many others have been incriminated.

name: Burn of shoulder or arm, except wrist or hand, deep full thickness or complex burn
synonyms: Burn of shoulder and arm, except wrist and hand, fourth degree
cn_name: 肩或臂（除外腕或手）深全层皮肤烧伤或复合烧伤

name: Actinic keratosis and other discrete epidermal dysplasias
definition: A group of conditions characterised by varying degrees of keratinocytic atypia resulting from damage to keratinocyte DNA. They carry a small propensity to develop into invasive squamous cell carcinoma.
cn_name: 光线性角化病和其他表皮发育不良

name: Amateur tattoo
definition: Tattoos which are self-administered or administered by peers to each other. These are likely to be more crudely performed and often result in a request for them to be removed.

name: Occupational melanosis
definition: Occupational melanosis is an increase in skin pigmentation attributable to chronic occupational exposure to substances such as coal-tar, pitch and asphalt. Photoactive constituents including anthracene and phenanthrene are thought to be responsible. Reticulate pigmentation develops slowly after prolonged exposure and is associated with atrophy, telangiectasia, lichenoid papules and follicular keratosis.

name: Antiphospholipid syndrome
definition: Antiphospholipid syndrome, also known as Hughes syndrome, is a systemic autoimmune condition characterised by the presence of antiphospholipid antibodies (aPL) in the serum of patients with thrombotic events and/or recurrent pregnancy complications.
synonyms: Anticardiolipin syndrome | Hughes syndrome

name: Tropical phagedaenic ulcer
definition: Tropical (phagedaenic) ulcer is an acute or chronic skin disease seen in the tropics and subtropics that is characterised by necrosis of the epidermis and underlying superficial tissue. Ulcers may heal spontaneously, leaving regular depressed scars, but sometimes enlarge to enter a chronic phase. Chronic ulcers are often large and irregular in shape and may involve the whole circumference of a limb. Pseudoepitheliomatous changes can develop which may proceed to the development of frank squamous
synonyms: Phagedaenic ulcer | Tropical ulcer

name: Irritant contact dermatitis due to cosmetics or emollients
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis caused by skin contact with cosmetics and emollients containing substances with irritant capacities such as fragrances, sodium lauryl sulfate, formaldehyde, alcohols, urea, lactic acid, enzymes or peeling particles.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema due to cosmetics or emollients
cn_name: 化妆品或润肤剂引起的刺激性接触性皮炎

name: Fibrous papule of face
definition: Fibrous papule of the face is a benign dermal fibrohistiocytic tumour which contains factor XIIIa. It presents as a small firm smooth flesh-coloured papule, most commonly as an isolated papule on the nose, though they may be multiple and can occur elsewhere on the face.

name: Genital ulcer of vulva
definition: Ulceration of the vulva of unknown or uncertain aetiology but where a sexually transmitted infection, especially Herpes simplex, primary syphilis or chancroid, is suspected. This is a provisional diagnosis which should be amended once the cause of the ulceration is known.
synonyms: genital ulcer disease of vulva | ulcer NOS of vulva | ulceration of vulva | vulval ulcer of uncertain nature | vulval ulceration

name: Seborrhoeic dermatitis of the flexures
definition: Seborrhoeic dermatitis affecting flexural skin including axillae and submammary, perianal area and genitocrural skin.
synonyms: Flexural seborrhoeic dermatitis | Intertriginous seborrhoeic dermatitis

name: Acroscleriasis
synonyms: acrosclerosis

name: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex, generalised severe
definition: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex, generalised severe type (EBS-gen sev) is a basal subtype of epidermolysis bullosa simplex characterised by the presence of generalised vesicles and small blisters in grouped or arcuate configuration.
synonyms: EBS - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex] Dowling-Meara | EBS-DM - [generalised epidermolysis bullosa simplex, Dowling-Meara] | EBS-gen sev - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex generalised type] | Epidermolysis bullosa herpetiformis

name: Myocutaneous flap necrosis, partial
definition: Incomplete necrosis of a surgical flap containing both skin and muscle

name: Exogenous ochronosis
definition: Pigmentation resembling endogenous ochronosis (alkaptonuria) but due to exogenous chemicals. It may occur after excessive use of hydroquinone used to lighten the skin.

name: Inflammatory linear verrucous epidermal naevus
synonyms: Dermatitic epidermal naevus | ILVEN - inflammatory linear verrucous epidermal naevus]

name: Erysipelas
synonyms: erysipelas NOS | st. anthony's fire

name: Aplasia cutis congenita due to adverse intrauterine events
definition: Sporadic aplasia cutis congenita associated with events such as placental infarction, fetus papyraceus, exposure to teratogens or intrauterine infection, e.g. Herpes simplex or Varicella zoster virus.
synonyms: Aplasia cutis congenita secondary to adverse intrauterine events

name: Burn of wrist or hand
definition: Injury to the tissues of the wrist and hand caused by contact with, for example, heat, steam, chemicals, or electricity.
synonyms: burn of wrist or hand of unspecified degree

name: Genitocrural intertrigo
definition: Intertrigo in the skin folds of the genital and groin areas.

name: Scalp psoriasis
definition: The scalp is frequently the site of initial presentation and is the commonest anatomical site to be involved by psoriasis. Morphologies range from discrete plaques to total scalp involvement with either thick plaques or scaly non-thickened areas almost identical to seborrhoeic dermatitis. Sites of predilection include the immediate post-auricular area and occiput.

name: Angular cheilitis due to candidosis
definition: Angular cheilitis associated with candida colonisation. This occurs particularly in denture wearers who have a high incidence of oral candidosis, often also associated with reduced vertical dimension of the oral cavity with redundant skin folds at the corners of the mouth. .
synonyms: Perlèche | candida of lips | candidal cheilitis

name: Congenital nail hypertrophy
synonyms: Congenital nail thickening | Congenital onychauxis

name: Nodular leukoplakia
definition: This is a small aggregation of cells where patches of keratosis appear as adherent white patches on the mucous membranes of the oral cavity, including the tongue, but also other areas of the gastro-intestinal tract, urinary tract and the genitals.

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 1H/S
definition: Hurler-Scheie syndrome is the intermediate form of mucopolysaccharidosis type 1 (MPS1) between the two extremes Hurler syndrome and Scheie syndrome; it is a rare lysosomal storage disease, characterised by skeletal deformities and a delay in motor development.
synonyms: Hurler-Scheie syndrome | MPS1H/S - [Mucopolysaccharidosis type 1H/S] | Mucopolysaccharidosis type 1, intermediate form

name: Atrophoderma
definition: A group of disorders characterised by dermal atrophy.
synonyms: atrophic condition of skin | Atrophoderma | Atrophy - skin

name: Keratolytic winter erythema
definition: Keratolytic winter erythema is an autosomal dominant disorder first described in inhabitants of the province of Oudtshoorn in South Africa. It consists in intermittent and recurrent centrifugal peeling and erythema of the palms and soles, though it may extend up the limbs to the buttocks and the trunk. The age of onset varies from infancy to early adulthood after which it tends to become less pronounced. A striking feature is the onset and recurrence with cold winter weather. The genetic basis i
synonyms: Oudtshoorn disease | keratolytic winter erythema | oudtshoorn disease

name: Primary herpetic vulvovaginitis
definition: A severe form of genital herpes caused by an initial infection with Herpes simplex and often accompanied by constitutional symptoms. In addition to the vulva, the vagina and cervix are commonly involved. The majority of cases are due to Herpes simplex virus type 2 but the condition may also be caused by type 1 virus.
synonyms: Primary genital herpes of female genital tract

name: Neurofibromatoses
definition: The neurofibromatoses (NF) are related genetic disorders which affect bone, soft tissue, skin and nervous system. In NF type 1 neurofibromas develop in the skin and elsewhere\: these can cause problems as a result of their visibility in the skin, compression of vital internal structures or from malignant degeneration. Neuromas of the acoustic nerve are the predominant problem in NF type 2.

name: Nodular actinic elastosis with cysts or comedones
synonyms: Favre-Racouchot syndrome

name: Premalignant or malignant disorders of the penis or scrotum
Vulval eczema
definition: Eczema (dermatitis) of vulval skin
synonyms: Vulval dermatitis

name: Squamous cell carcinoma of neck
synonyms: Epidermoid carcinoma of neck

name: Bubonic plague
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Yersinia pestis. This disease commonly presents with an infection of the lymph nodes leading to swelling and pain. This disease may also present with gangrene of the extremities, chills, malaise, high fever, muscle cramps, or seizures. Transmission is through the bite of an infected flea, by direct contact, or by droplet transmission.
synonyms: Pestis bubonica

name: Congenital absence of lobe of ear
Infestation by Demodex
definition: Infestation with Demodex mites. Demodex folliculorum is a saprophytic mite of the human pilosebaceous unit with a predilection for facial skin and eyelashes. Demodex brevis is found in the sebaceous glands of the eyelash follicle and in the lobules of eyelid meibomian glands. Although infestation is very common and normally symptomless, the mites have been linked to papulopustular rosacea and chronic blepharitis.
synonyms: Demodicosis | infestation by demodex folliculorum

name: Genetic or developmental disorders involving lips or oral mucosa
definition: A group of genetic and developmental disorders characterised by abnormal facial development that particularly involves the lips and oral mucosa.
cn_name: 累及唇或口腔黏膜的遗传性或发育性疾患

name: Oral mucosal fragility and ulceration due to epidermolysis bullosa classified elsewhere
definition: This is a break or defect through the full thickness of the oral epithelium, exposing the underlying connective tissue to saliva and micro-organisms caused by a group of genetically determined disorders characterised by blistering of skin and mucous membranes that occurs secondarily to minor mechanical trauma classified elsewhere.

name: Cutaneous cryptococcosis
definition: Mucocutaneous lesions in cryptococcosis occur almost exclusively as a result of systemic dissemination, and may precede signs of involvement of the central nervous system. Cutaneous lesions, most commonly papules, nodules or ulcers, are reported in about 10% of cases of cryptococcosis.
synonyms: Cryptococcosis involving skin

name: Lupus vulgaris
definition: Lupus vulgaris is a form of cutaneous tuberculosis arising in individuals with a strong cell-mediated immunity against M. tuberculosis. It presents as a slowly expanding plaque containing multiple reddish-brown granulomatous dermal papules. As the plaque expands it causes local scarring and, in longstanding cases, extensive tissue damage.
synonyms: lupus vulgaris of nose

name: Filiform palmoplantar keratoderma
definition: A morphological description for keratodermas characterised by multiple fine spines projecting vertically from palmoplantar skin. They are probably genetically heterogeneous. They may be inherited as an autosomal dominant trait or may be sporadic. In the former case the keratoderma may not become apparent until adulthood.
synonyms: Music-box spine keratoderma | Spiny keratoderma

name: Acanthocheilonema infestation
synonyms: acanthocheilonema | acanthocheilonemiasis

name: Diseases of tongue
definition: Any pathological process affecting the structural tissues of the tongue with or without interference of its normal functions.
synonyms: Glossopathy | disorder of tongue | unspecified condition of the tongue

name: Certain skin disorders attributable to fungal infection
definition: This group incorporates both localised infection of the skin by fungus (e.g. pityriasis versicolor) and systemic fungal infections with important skin manifestations (e.g. cutaneous cryptococcosis).
cn_name: 某些真菌感染性皮肤病

name: Deep subfascial lipoma
definition: Deep subfascial lipomata are benign neoplasms of adipose tissue which arise deep to the deep fascia and have a tendency to infiltrate between and into muscle. They may occur at any body site and may cause diagnostic difficulty. They are well recognised to occur on the forehead beneath the frontalis muscle (frontalis-associated lipoma).
synonyms: Atypical lipoma of soft tissue | Frontalis-associated lipoma | Infiltrating lipoma of soft tissue | Intramuscular lipoma of soft tissue

name: Chédiak-Higashi syndrome
definition: This syndrome is caused by mutations in the LYST gene. It consists of partial OCA, immunodeficiency, mild bleeding tendency and lymphoproliferative infiltration of bone marrow and reticuloendothelial system.
synonyms: Béguez César syndrome | Chediak-Higashi-Steinbrinck syndrome | Chediak-Steinbrinck syndrome | Steinbrinck anomaly

name: Ulcer of skin of uncertain nature, trunk
definition: This denotes the presence of a skin ulcer located on the trunk but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the ulcer might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Delayed pressure urticaria
definition: Delayed pressure urticaria differs from other forms of physical urticaria in that the appearance of weals is delayed for several hours following the provoking stimulus. It is commonly associated with chronic spontaneous urticaria but tends to respond poorly to antihistamine therapy. The palms and soles, the waist, and the buttocks and thighs are commonly affected areas. Wealing can be induced by a variety of stimuli, including standing, walking, wearing of tight clothes, or sitting on a hard sur
synonyms: DPU

name: Vulval pruritus
definition: Intense itching of the external female genitalia.
synonyms: Itching vulva | Pruritus vulvae

name: Idiopathic facial aseptic granuloma
definition: A granulomatous inflammatory dermatosis of childhood characterised by the development of one or more asymptomatic inflammatory nodules on the cheek or eyelid. The nodules may attain a diameter of up to 3 cm before gradually involuting over a period of months. It is regarded as lying within the spectrum of granulomatous rosacea.
synonyms: IFAG - [idiopathic facial aseptic granuloma] | Pyodermite froide du visage

name: 17q21.31 deletion
definition: Monosomy 17q21.31 (17q21.31 microdeletion syndrome) is a chromosomal anomaly characterised by developmental delay, childhood hypotonia, facial dysmorphism, and a friendly/amiable behaviour.
synonyms: 17q21.31 deletion syndrome | Monosomy 17q21.31

name: Carcinoma in situ of skin
definition: Stage 0 includes\: Tis, N0, M0. Tis\: Carcinoma in situ. N0\: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0\: No clinical or radiographic evidence of distant metastasis.
synonyms: skin carcinoma in situ | carcinoma in situ of skin | Cutaneous carcinoma in situ

name: Sacrococcygeal pilonidal abscess
synonyms: Coccygeal fistula with abscess | Pilonidal abscess | Pilonidal fistula with abscess

name: Neonatal listeriosis
definition: A condition affecting fetuses or neonates, caused by an infection with the gram-positive bacteria Listeria. This condition is characterised by respiratory distress in the neonate, by stillbirth, or by abortion. Transmission is by vertical transmission. Confirmation is by identification of Listeria in the neonate and mother.
synonyms: Neonatal disseminated listeriosis | congenital listeria monocytogenes infection | congenital listeriosis | fetal listeriosis

name: Heparin skin necrosis due to immune heparin-induced thrombocytopenia
synonyms: HITTS - [Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia and thrombosis syndrome]

name: Disabling pansclerotic morphoea of childhood
definition: This is a rare, severe, mutilating form of morphoea involving the dermis, fat, fascia, muscle and even bone, usually starting before the age of 14 years. It may develop from linear morphoea. Superficial and deep cutaneous sclerosis involves the trunk and extremities, scalp and face, with sparing of the fingertips and toes. There may be a claw deformity of the hands and contracture of the Achilles tendons. Raynaud phenomenon is not found but oesophageal, pulmonary and periodontal changes may occu

name: Trichomegaly - retina pigmentary degeneration - dwarfism
definition: Trichomegaly - retina pigmentary degeneration - dwarfism syndrome is characterised by growth retardation, alopecia, abnormally long eyelashes and retinitis pigmentosa, and moderate intellectual deficit in the majority of cases.

name: Disseminated paracoccidioidomycosis without central nervous system involvement
definition: Disseminated chronic paracoccidioidomycosis without CNS involvement is associated with considerably lower morbidity and risk of mortality than chronic PCM involving the CNS.
synonyms: Disseminated chronic paracoccidioidomycosis without CNS - [central nervous system] involvement

name: Orthostatic purpura
definition: Purpura of the lower extremities secondary to venous hypertension. Chronic orthostatic purpura results in haemosiderin pigmentation which may dominate the clinical appearance.
synonyms: Gravitational purpura

name: Cutaneous microvascular disturbances due to dysproteinaemia
definition: Skin disorders resulting from abnormal or elevated concentrations of circulating proteins including macroglobulins, cryoglobulins, cryofibrinogens and cold agglutinins.

name: Atypical Werner syndrome
definition: Atypical Werner syndrome refers to a heterogeneous group of cases that are clinically diagnosed as Werner syndrome (WS; ) but do not carry WRN gene mutations. Similar to classical WS caused by WRN mutations, they generally exhibit an aged appearance and common age-related disorders at earlier ages compared to the general population.

name: Granuloma annulare
definition: A common inflammatory disorder in which granulomatous inflammation surrounds foci of degenerate dermal collagen. It presents clinically as dermal papules and annular plaques. It may be localised, especially over bony prominences, or generalised. The cause is unknown.
synonyms: GA - [granuloma annulare] | Granulome annulare

name: Book syndrome
definition: Böök syndrome is the association of premolar aplasia, hyperhidrosis, and premature greying of the hair.

name: Syphilitic hemiplegia
synonyms: syphilis hemianesthesia | syphilitic hemiparesis

name: Miscellaneous specified forms of hair loss
definition: A heterogeneous group of conditions where there is alopecia or hair loss

name: Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to cosmetics or fragrances
Juvenile syphilis of central nervous system with taboparesis
synonyms: Juvenile tabes dorsalis | Juvenile taboparetic neurosyphilis | juvenile syphilis tabes dorsalis | juvenile syphilitic tabes dorsalis | juvenile taboparesis | juvenile taboparesis neurosyphilis

name: Acropustulosis of Hallopeau
definition: An uncommon pustular form of psoriasis which may rarely eventuate into generalised pustular psoriasis. It is characterised by pustules and variable scaling occurring in and around the nails and nail-beds of the fingers and toes. It may cause marked nail destruction and may be associated with a distal interphalangeal joint arthritis, with palmoplantar pustulosis or with plaque psoriasis elsewhere.
synonyms: Acrodermatitis continua of Hallopeau | Acrodermatitis perstans | dermatitis repens | hallopeau's acrodermatitis | acrodermatitis continua of Hallopeau | deficiency of IL-36R antagonist
snomed: 83839005 "acrodermatitis continua of hallopeau";27520001 "acrodermatitis continua of hallopeau"

name: Acquired leukonychia
definition: Non-hereditary white nails. The most common form is punctate or striate leukonychia which is attributable to minor nail matrix trauma as may occur from overzealous nail manicure. Transverse leukonychia presents as a white band across the nail plate and is associated with systemic events which suppress matrix function (e.g. chemotherapy, severe infections).

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, elbow joint
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, elbow joint

name: Occupational protein contact dermatitis
definition: Protein contact dermatitis caused by exposure to proteins at work.

name: Drug-induced annular erythema
definition: Annular erythema may rarely be associated with administration of drugs. Those implicated include antimalarials, oestrogens, salicylates and phenothiazines.

name: Haematoma of surgical wound of skin
definition: Collection of blood within skin and soft tissues following surgical wound of skin usually resulting from defective haemostasis

name: Allergic contact dermatitis of external ear
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis affecting the external ear.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema of external ear | Allergic otitis externa
cn_name: 变应性接触性外耳皮炎

name: Plaque psoriasis
definition: The commonest form of psoriasis, which manifests as well-defined red, scaly plaques on the skin. Typical sites of initial involvement are the scalp, the extensor surfaces of the elbows and knees, the lower back and the shins. In severe disease a majority of the skin surface may be involved.
synonyms: Psoriasis vulgaris

name: Cone shell envenomation
definition: Envenomation by marine molluscs of the genus Conus presents as painful puncture wounds, with local ischaemia, cyanosis and numbness which may become generalised and, particularly with Conus geographus, may be fatal.

name: Foot and mouth disease
definition: A rare infection in humans due to the Aphthovirus Foot-and-mouth-disease virus (FMDV), which is responsible for a highly contagious epidemic infection of cloven-hoofed animals, particularly cattle. It manifests in humans with prodromal fever and malaise followed by vesiculation and ulceration of oral mucous membranes and lips. Vesicles may sometimes involve the digits and palmoplantar skin.
synonyms: Foot-and-mouth disease | aphthae fever | aphthous fever | epidemic stomatitis | epizootic aphthae | epizootic stomatitis | fourth disease | hoof-and-mouth disease | FMD | foot-and-mouth disease

name: Lichen myxoedematosus
definition: Localised lichen myxedematosus is a group of skin diseases characterised by the development of papules, nodules and/or plaques with mucin deposits and a variable degree of fibrosis in the absence of thyroid disease. The group comprises five sub-forms\: nodular lichen myxedematosus, discrete papular lichen myxedematosus, papular mucinosis of infancy, acral persistent papular mucinosis and self-healing papular mucinosis.
synonyms: Papular mucinosis | generalized and sclerodermoid lichen myxedematosus | papular mucinosis

name: Staphylococcus aureus superficial folliculitis
definition: Infection of the follicular ostium with Staphylococcus aureus. There is a predilection for hairy areas including the scalp, beard and thighs.
synonyms: Follicular impetigo of Bockhart
cn_name: 金黄色葡萄球菌性浅表性毛囊炎

name: Recombinant chromosome 8 syndrome
definition: Recombinant 8 (rec(8)) syndrome, also known as San Luis Valley syndrome, is a complex chromosomal disorder that is due to a parental pericentric inversion of chromosome 8 and is characterised by major congenital heart anomalies, urogenital malformations, moderate to severe intellectual deficiency and mild craniofacial dysmorphism.
synonyms: Duplication 8q/deletion 8p | Rec(8) syndrome | Recombinant 8 syndrome | San Luis Valley syndrome

name: Herpes simplex infection of penis
definition: Infection of the penis by Herpes simplex virus. This is usually but not always transmitted by sexual contact and caused by Herpes simplex virus type 2 (HSV-2) rather than Herpes simplex virus type 1, which is traditionally associated with orofacial herpes. Multiple painful vesicles and superficial ulcers develop on the glans and shaft of the penis. Primary infections tend to be more severe than recurrent attacks and are often associated with painful urethritis.
synonyms: Herpetic balanitis | Penile herpes

name: Chorea due to IgA vasculitis
definition: Henoch-Schönlein purpura is a systemic IgA vasculitis that affects small vessels. It is characterised by skin purpura, arthritis, and abdominal and/or renal involvement. SHP usually affects children and is rare in adults.
synonyms: Chorea due to Henoch-Schönlein purpura | Chorea due to anaphylactoid purpura

name: Oleogranuloma
definition: An oleogranuloma is a reaction to the presence of mineral oil or grease in the skin and soft tissues. This may be accidental (e.g. grease-gun injury) but at times is self-inflicted into the lips or genitalia in an attempt to replicate the results of dermal fillers. It presents as a firm mass which may result in marked disfigurement, scarring and chronic fistula formation.

name: Pressure ulceration, ungradable
definition: Pressure ulcer with full thickness skin loss in which actual depth of the ulcer is completely obscured by slough (yellow, tan, grey, green or brown) and/or eschar (tan, brown or black) in the wound bed. Until enough slough and/or eschar are removed to expose the base of the wound, it is not possible to determine whether the ulcer is grade 3 or grade 4.
synonyms: pressure injury with depth unknown | pressure ulcer with at least full thickness skin loss but true depth not known | stage unknown decubitus ulcer
cn_name: 压疮，伴至少全层皮肤丢失，实际深度未知

name: Poikiloderma vasculare atrophicans
definition: Poikiloderma vasculare atrophicans is a cutaneous reaction pattern characterised by mottled hyper- and hypomelanosis, telangiectasia and progressive dermal and epidermal atrophy. It may manifest as a component of established mycosis fungoides but may precede the development of the latter by many years and, in some cases, may persist indefinitely without progression to frank lymphoma. It should be distinguished from other causes of poikiloderma such as may be seen with dermatomyositis.
synonyms: Parapsoriasis variegata | Poikilodermatous parapsoriasis | Retiform parapsoriasis
cn_name: 血管萎缩性皮肤异色症

name: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton concentricum
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton concentricum, a dermatophyte found in Central America, the South Pacific and Southern Asia. Infection results in a characteristic clinical pattern known as Tinea imbricata, in which multiple concentric rings of scaling form as the fungus spreads outwards.
synonyms: Malabar itch | Tinea imbricata | Tokelau

name: Acquired ichthyosis
definition: Acquired ichthyosis resembles autosomal dominant ichthyosis vulgaris but develops in adult life in individuals without a previous history of ichthyosis. It may be caused by certain drugs but when associated with underlying malignancy (i.e. paraneoplastic) it is strongly associated with Hodgkin lymphoma and may be the presenting sign of that disease. It may less commonly be associated with other lymphoid neoplasms or solid tumours.

name: Connective tissue hamartoma
synonyms: Connective tissue naevus

name: Familial camptodactyly
definition: Camptodactyly with demonstrable familial inheritance.

name: Adult dermatomyositis
definition: Adult dermatomyositis is a systemic inflammatory disorder affecting the skeletal muscles, the skin, and other organs. It is characterised by symmetric proximal muscle weakness, increased serum muscle enzymes, myopathic changes upon electromyography, typical histological findings on muscle biopsy, and typical dermatologic manifestations such as heliotrope rash or Gottron’s papules.
synonyms: adult onset dermatomyositis

name: Secondary syphilis of skin or mucous membranes
definition: A disease caused by an infection with Treponema pallidum pallidum. This disease is characterised by lesions of the skin and mucous membranes. Transmission is commonly by sexual contact.
synonyms: Mucocutaneous secondary syphilis

name: Onychomycosis classified according to clinical pattern
definition: Fungal nail infection classified by site and depth of infection, factors which are important in determining management.

name: Anti-synthetase syndrome with dermatomyositis
definition: Dermatomyositis associated with autoantibodies to aminoacyl-transfer RNA synthetases, which confer a high risk of interstitial lung disease (anti-synthetase syndrome) as well as myositis.

name: Nipple eczema
definition: Eczema of the nipple and areola occurs mainly but not exclusively in women and can cause significant distress. It may be unilateral or bilateral. Often no cause can be established, although there is an association with atopy. It needs to be differentiated from Paget disease of the nipple.
synonyms: Dermatitis of nipple and areola

name: Aquagenic pruritus
definition: Aquagenic pruritus is characterised by a widespread severe, intense, pricking and itching of the skin on contact with water. There are no observable skin lesions but the intensity of the itch may impede normal activities, limit bathing and lead to severe depression. Itch associated with polycythaemia vera may be triggered by water contact.

name: Erythema infectiosum
definition: A condition caused by infection with parvovirus B19 (member of the Erythroparvovirus genus). In children, this condition is characterised by fever and cold-like symptoms initially, followed by a skin rash typically in the facial region. In adolescents and adults, this condition may present with painful and swollen joints. Transmission is by droplet transmission, or vertical transmission.
synonyms: Fifth disease | Parvovirus B19 infection | Slapped cheek syndrome | fifth disease | parvovirus B19 infection | slapped cheek disease

name: Adams-Oliver syndrome
definition: Adams-Oliver Syndrome is a rare disorder characterised by the combination of congenital limb abnormalities and scalp defects, often accompanied by skull ossification defects. Aplasia cutis congenita, transverse limb defects and cutis marmorata telangiectica are characteristic of this condition.

name: Dermatoses due to ionizing radiation
synonyms: Radiodermatitis | radiation dermatitis | radiation-induced dermatosis
cn_name: 电离辐射引起的皮肤病

name: Dermatophytosis of face
definition: Dermatophytosis (tinea) affecting the skin of the face.
synonyms: Ringworm of face | Tinea faciale | Tinea faciei

name: Excess and redundant skin
definition: A condition which typically occurs in formerly grossly obese individuals following massive weight loss, as following bariatric surgery or severe calorie restriction.
synonyms: Flabbiness of skin

name: Pityriasis lichenoides et varioliformis acuta
definition: Pityriasis lichenoides et varioliformis acuta, commonly referred to as PLEVA, is an acute form of pityriasis lichenoides which manifests as crops of oedematous pink papules over the proximal limbs and trunk which progress to vesicles and central haemorrhagic necrosis before healing with scarring. The cause of PLEVA is unknown. It may be confused clinically with varicella.
synonyms: Mucha-Habermann disease | PLEVA - [Pityriasis lichenoides et varioliformis acuta] | Pityriasis lichenoides acuta

name: Granulomatosis with polyangiitis
definition: Necrotizing granulomatous inflammation usually involving the upper and lower respiratory tract, and necrotizing vasculitis affecting predominantly small to medium-sized vessels (e.g., capillaries, venules, arterioles, arteries and veins). Necrotizing glomerulonephritis is common. (Arthritis Rheum 1990;33\:1101-1107)
synonyms: Wegener granulomatosis | Necrotizing respiratory granulomatosis | Wegener granulomatosis, formerly

name: Lower limb haemosiderosis
definition: Haemosiderosis of the lower legs resulting from recurrent extravasation of red blood cells into the dermis in people with venous hypertension.
synonyms: haemosiderin pigmentation of lower limbs due to chronic venous insufficiency | hypostatic haemosiderosis

name: Pyoderma gangrenosum associated with inflammatory bowel disease
definition: Pyoderma gangrenosum associated with Crohn disease or ulcerative colitis.

name: Cimicosis
definition: Infestation by bedbugs, which are blood-sucking temporary ectoparasites. The most common species to attack humans is Cimex lectularius. In individuals who are not sensitized by previous exposure, there may be no symptoms or signs other than purpuric macules at the sites of bites. Weals, papules or bullae may occur in sensitized individuals.
synonyms: Bedbug infestation

name: Eumycetoma
definition: A localised chronic infection caused by various species of fungi and characterised by the formation of aggregates of the causative organisms (grains) within abscesses. This results in severe damage to skin, subcutaneous tissues and bones of the feet, hands and other parts of the body, with draining sinuses which discharge grains to the surface. Recognised agents include Madurella mycetomatis, Madurella grisea, Leptosphaeria senegalensis, Curvularia lunata, Scedosporium apiospermum, Neotestudina
synonyms: Eumycotic maduromycosis | Eumycotic mycetoma | Mycetoma due to fungal infection | eumycetoma | Madura foot | maduromycosis | Maduromycosis, mycotic | Mycotic mycetoma

name: Contact dermatitis of eyelid
synonyms: contact eczema eyelid

name: Unsatisfactory outcome from cutaneous cosmetic surgical procedure
definition: The outcome from a surgical intervention designed to improve cosmetic appearance which is considered by the practitioner who performed the procedure to be less satisfactory than anticipated.
cn_name: 皮肤整形手术结果不满意

name: Multiple skin tags
definition: Very common non-neoplastic fibroepithelial skin growths ranging from less than one to several millimetres in diameter. They favour the neck and intertriginous areas and may be very numerous. They are associated with obesity, type II diabetes, insulin resistance and acanthosis nigricans.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 1 of upper back
Trichodysplasia spinulosa
definition: Trichodysplasia spinulosa is a folliculocentric cutaneous viral infection of immunosuppressed patients which causes abnormal hair follicle maturation. It has been linked to the presence of papilloma or polyoma virus particles within follicular keratinocytes. Histopathology shows abnormal follicle maturation, excessive production of inner root sheath epithelium and hyperkeratosis of the infundibulum. The condition presents with widespread follicular papules with a central keratin spicule. The fac
synonyms: (viral-associated) trichodysplasia spinulosa | cyclosporine-induced follicular dystrophy | follicular dystrophy of immunosuppression | folliculodystrophy of immunosuppression | pilomatrix dysplasia | trichodysplasia of immunosuppression | trichodysplasia spinulosa | viral-associated trichodysplasia spinulosa

name: Genetic hamartoneoplastic syndromes affecting the skin
definition: A heterogeneous group of inherited diseases characterised by the presence of multiple hamartomata and associated with an increased risk of malignancy.
cn_name: 影响皮肤的遗传性错构肿瘤综合征

name: Xerostomia due to dehydration
definition: This is the subjective complaint of dry mouth which may or may not be associated with a lack of saliva, due to mouth breathing, secondary to dehydration.
synonyms: Xerostomia secondary to dehydration

name: Skin disorders involving certain specific body regions
cn_name: 累及特定身体部位的皮肤障碍

name: Infantile laryngotracheal haemangioma
definition: This refers to benign tumours derived from cells of the vascular or lymphatic vessel walls (epithelium) or derived from cells of the tissues surrounding these vessels, of the larynx and trachea.

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to nickel
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to sensitization to nickel. Common sources of nickel include inexpensive jewellery, watches and metal studs in clothing.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to nickel | Nickel dermatitis

name: Guttate flare of plaque psoriasis
definition: Acute exacerbation of psoriasis with widespread guttate papules in individual with existing plaque psoriasis. It is typically triggered by streptococcal infection. It is to be distinguished from unstable psoriasis and from forms of stable chronic plaque psoriasis in which small papules may predominate.

name: Oral-facial-digital syndrome type 11
definition: Oral-facial-digital syndrome type 11 is an extremely rare, sporadic form of oral-facial-digital syndrome, characterised by facial (blepharophimosis, bulbous nasal tip, broad nasal bridge, downslanting palpebral fissures and low set ears) and skeletal (post-axial polydactyly and fusion of vertebrae) malformations along with severe mental retardation, deafness and congenital heart defects.

name: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum equinum
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum equinum, the main natural host of which is the horse. It typically causes an inflammatory ectothrix infection of scalp hair.

name: Genetic or developmental disorders affecting the skin
definition: A large group of disorders, some limited to the skin but many involving other organ systems, due to heritable genetic defects, chromosomal abnormalities or embryofetal developmental anomalies.
cn_name: 累及皮肤的遗传性或发育性障碍

name: Mansonelliasis due to Mansonella perstans
synonyms: infection by acanthocheilonema perstans | infection by dipetalonema perstans | infection by mansonella perstans

name: Pustular psoriasis
definition: Psoriasis characterised by clinically visible pustules. Pustular psoriasis may be localised or generalised and life-threatening.
synonyms: generalized pustular psoriasis

name: Folliculotropic mycosis fungoides
definition: Folliculotropic mycosis fungoides (MF) is a rare subtype of mycosis fungoides with infiltration of the hair follicle epithelium by neoplastic T-lymphocytes. It can have a variable presentation which includes focal hair loss, boggy plaques or nodules, acne-like cysts and comedones, often on the head/face. These are often associated with classical MF patches and plaques. It has a worse prognosis than classical MF and does not respond well to topical therapies.
synonyms: folliculocentric mycosis fungoides | folliculotropic MF | MF- associated follicular mucinosis | pilotropic mycosis fungoides

name: Acute urticaria due to IgE-mediated allergy
definition: Acute urticaria provoked by an immediate hypersensitivity reaction, usually to food or drugs.
synonyms: Allergic urticaria

name: Corticosteroid-induced acne
definition: Acne attributable to or exacerbated by the use of moderate to high doses of systemic corticosteroids. It most commonly occurs on the chest but may also develop on the face, neck, back and arms. The lesions tend to be more uniform in appearance than is usual with acne vulgaris.
synonyms: Steroid-induced acne

name: Latrodectism
definition: Latrodectism results from envenomation by widow spiders of the genus Latrodectus (the black widow spider and related species). It is characterised by intense localised pain, muscle rigidity, headache, sweating, nausea and vomiting.
synonyms: Black widow spider bite

name: Marfan syndrome
definition: Marfan syndrome is a systemic disease of connective tissue characterised by a variable combination of cardiovascular, musculo-skeletal, ophthalmic and pulmonary manifestations. Cardiovascular involvement is characterised by 1) progressive dilation of the aorta accompanied by an increased risk of aortic dissection, which affects prognosis and 2) mitral insufficiency. Skeletal involvement is often the first sign of the disease and can include dolichostenomelia, large size, arachnodactyly, joint hy
synonyms: Marfan disease

name: Cervicofacial lymphatic malformation
definition: A congenital lymphatic malformation presumed to arise from an embryonic jugular lymph sac affecting the neck and face. It typically consists of a single large cystic swelling containing clear lymph.
synonyms: Cervicofacial lymphangioma | Cystic hygroma

name: Paramedian nasal cleft
synonyms: Tessier number 1 facial cleft

name: Skin lesion of uncertain nature, trunk
definition: This denotes the presence of a skin lesion on the trunk but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Subcutaneous nodular sarcoidosis
synonyms: Darier-Roussy sarcoid

name: Hallermann-Streiff-François syndrome
definition: Hallermann-Streiff-François syndrome is a premature ageing syndrome marked by a characteristic facies with hypoplastic mandible and beaked nose, proportionate short stature, hypotrichosis, microphthalmia with congenital cataract, hypodontia, hypotrichosis, skin atrophy of the face and hypoplasia of the clavicles and ribs. About 15% of cases display intellectual deficit. Neonatal teeth may be present.

name: Median facial cleft
definition: A condition caused by failure of the structures of the face to correctly develop during the antenatal period. This condition is characterised by a malformation of the midface. This condition may present with a skin-covered gap in the bones of the forehead, hypertelorism, median cleft lip or median cleft nose.
synonyms: Midline facial cleft | Tessier number 0-14 and 30 facial cleft

name: Labial melanotic macule
definition: Labial melanotic macule refers to the formation of a freckle-like lesion on the lower lip. As with freckles, the lesions are harmless, and consist of a flat, brownish black patch, oval in shape, in the centre portion of the lower lip. Lesions may also form inside the mouth or on the genitals. Labial melanotic macules are not accompanied by any symptoms aside from the lesion.
synonyms: Labial lentigo

name: Generalised bullous fixed drug eruption
definition: A rare but potentially life-threatening form of fixed drug eruption in which there is extensive blistering and shedding of skin. It may be mistaken for drug-induced toxic epidermal necrolysis but the history of repeated gradually worsening attacks and the histopathological findings from skin biopsy should help distinguish it from the latter.

name: Oesophageal varices with bleeding in viral hepatic disease
Varicose ulcer of intestine
synonyms: intestinal varicose ulcer | intestine varicose ulcer | varicose ulcer of bowel

name: Other specified forms of dermographism
definition: Less common forms of dermographism including localised, delayed, red and cholinergic dermographism.

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to hairdressing products
Irritant contact dermatitis related to skin contact with prostheses or surgical appliances
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis resulting from friction and sweating between the skin surface and a prosthesis or appliance in contact with the skin, especially limb prostheses.
cn_name: 假体或手术器械刺激性接触性皮炎

name: Burn of hip or leg, except ankle or foot, superficial partial thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of hip and leg, except ankle or foot, superficial second degree
cn_name: 臀或腿（除外踝或足）浅层部分皮层烧伤

name: Wandering rash of the mouth
definition: The counterpart of geographic tongue affecting other parts of the oral epithelium. It is less common than geographic tongue.
synonyms: Oral erythema migrans

name: Dermatophytosis of foot
definition: Dermatophytosis of the skin of the foot (tinea pedis). The lateral interdigital toe clefts are the most common initial site of infection. Longstanding infection with Trichophyton rubrum, the most commonly implicated organism in Europe and North America, characteristically causes dry scaling over the sole of the foot. Other species which regularly invade the skin of the foot include Epidermophyton floccosum and Trichophyton interdigitale.
synonyms: Athlete's foot | Moccasin foot | Ringworm of foot | Tinea pedis | Dermatophytosis of foot | ringworm of foot

name: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency, classic form, simple virilizing
definition: This refers to any of several autosomal recessive diseases resulting from mutations of genes for enzymes mediating the biochemical steps of production of cortisol from cholesterol by the adrenal glands (steroidogenesis). This diagnosis is due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency, classic form, simply virilizing.

name: Multiple mononeuropathy vasculitis with perinuclear antineutrophil cytoplasmic antibody-associated vasculitis
definition: The perinuclear antineutrophilic cytoplasmic antibody (p-ANCA) is closely associated with rapidly progressing glomerulonephritis, microscopic polyangiitis, and allergic granulomatous angiitis. Mononeuropathy due to vasculitis is a neurological manifestation of these conditions. Associated neural autoantibodies include\: pANCA (perinuclear anti-neutrophil Cytoplasmic Antibodies), neutrophilic cytoplasmic antigens, MPO-ANCA (Churg-Strauss), cANCA (Cytoplasmic anti-neutrophil Cytoplasmic Antibodies

name: Familial hypercholesterolaemia
definition: Familial hypercholesterolaemia is a hereditary dyslipidaemia characterised by a permanent and isolated elevation in circulating low-density lipoprotein (LDL) levels caused by mutations in the genes related to LDLR and its function.
synonyms: Fredrickson 2A | Fredrickson type 2A hyperlipoproteinemia | Hypercholesterolaemia due to LDL receptor deficiency | essential familial hypercholesterolaemia | familial cholesterolaemia | familial hyperbetalipoproteinaemia | familial hypercholesterinaemia | hereditary cholesterolaemia | hereditary hypercholesterinaemia | hereditary hypercholesterolaemia

name: Nummular palmoplantar keratoderma
definition: Hereditary painful callosities is a nummular palmoplantar keratoderma characterised by the development of painful keratotic lesions over pressure points in hands and feet.
synonyms: Hereditary painful callosities | Keratosis palmoplantaris nummularis | focal epidermolytic palmoplantar keratoderma | focal EPPK | nummular epidermolytic palmoplantar keratoderma

name: Multiple familial trichoepithelioma
definition: An autosomal dominant hamartoneoplastic condition due to heterozygous mutation in the CYLD gene on chromosome 16q12 and characterised clinically by the development of multiple trichoepitheliomata on the face.
synonyms: Brooke-Spiegler syndrome | Epithelioma adenoides cysticum | MFT1 [multiple familial trichoepithelioma] | brooke-spiegler syndrome | multiple familial trichoepitheliomas | BRSS | BSS | CYLD cutaneous syndrome | SBS | Spiegler-Brooke Syndrome

name: Erosive oral lichen planus
definition: Oral lichen planus in which the epithelium is ulcerated.

name: Hereditary skin peeling
definition: A group of uncommon heritable disorders characterised by abnormal skin peeling

name: Ophiasis
definition: A characteristic and often treatment resistant form of alopecia areata affecting the occipital scalp
synonyms: Ophiasic alopecia areata

name: Myocutaneous flap necrosis
definition: Necrosis of a surgical flap containing both skin and muscle

name: Malignant atrophic papulosis
definition: Degos' disease (malignant atrophic papulosis) is a rare, progressive occlusive vasculopathy of unknown cause affecting small and medium calibre arteries. Its protean manifestations result from focal tissue infarction in skin and other organs. It presents initially in the skin as scattered small pink papules which undergo ischaemic necrosis to leave characteristic circular porcelain-white scars. Although it may remain limited to the skin, it tends over time to progress to involve other organ syst
synonyms: Degos disease | Lethal Cutaneous and Gastrointestinal Arteriolar Thrombosis | erythrokeratoderma en cocardes | kohlmeier-degos syndrome | maladie de degos | malignant atrophic papulosis of degos | degos' disease

name: Spider bite
definition: Bite by any of a large range of biting spiders. Whilst most cause only a mild local reaction, envenomation by some can be life-threatening.

name: Lichen planus of lips
definition: Lichen planus of the lips is usually part of a more widespread oral lichen planus but can present as either white patches or erosions, usually on the vermilion of the lower lip. Biopsy may be required to confirm the diagnosis.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 2 of upper limb
synonyms: stage 2 decubitus ulcer of upper limb

name: Acquired melanotic macules or lentigines
definition: Acquired discrete macules and flat patches of melanin skin pigmentation including freckles and lentigines.
cn_name: 获得性黑斑或雀斑样痣

name: Sturge-Weber syndrome
definition: Sturge-Weber syndrome is a congenital vascular malformation characterised by facial capillary malformations (classically referred to as angiomas even through they are not tumours but port-wine stains), accompanied by variable degrees of ocular (glaucoma) and neurological anomalies (leptomeningeal vascular anomalies responsible for seizures and varying degrees of contralateral motor deficit and leaning disability).
synonyms: encephalotrigeminal angiomatosis | SWS

name: Langerhans cell histiocytosis
definition: A neoplastic proliferation of Langerhans cells which contain Birbeck granules by ultrastructural examination. Three major overlapping syndromes are recognised\: eosinophilic granuloma, Letterer-Siwe disease, and Hand-Schuller-Christian disease. The clinical course is generally related to the number of organs affected at presentation.
synonyms: Histiocytosis X | Langerhans-cell histiocytosis NOS | class 1 histiocytosis | histiocytosis X | LCH
cn_name: 朗格汉斯细胞组织细胞增多症

name: Annular elastolytic giant cell granuloma
definition: An uncommon dermatosis characterised clinically by annular patches with elevated erythematous borders and central mild atrophy and pallor located predominantly on the forehead, face and neck; and histopathologically by a granulomatous reaction to elastin with complete loss of elastic tissue in the dermis. It affects predominantly middle-aged adult women.
synonyms: Atypical annular necrobiosis lipoidica of the face and scalp | actinic granuloma | AEGCG | atypical annular necrobiosis lipodica of the face and scalp | giant cell elastophagocytosis | miescher's granuloma of the face

name: Traumatic balanoposthitis
definition: Inflammation of the penis and prepuce due to trauma, particularly in association with sexual activity.

name: Subcutaneous swelling, mass or lump, lower limb
definition: One or more subcutaneous soft tissue masses of undetermined or unspecified nature localised to the lower limbs
synonyms: localised mass of lower limb | localised swelling of lower limb | localised swelling, mass and lump, lower limb

name: Capillaritis, Schamberg
synonyms: Progressive pigmented purpura of Schamberg | Schamberg disease

name: Submandibular abscess with mention of mediastinal, parapharyngeal or cervical extension
Oral mucosal disorders attributable to mechanical damage
Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with mottled pigmentation
definition: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with mottled pigmentation (EBS-MP) is an autosomal dominant basal subtype of EBS due to mutations in the KRT5 gene which encodes keratin 5. It is characterised by generalised blistering with mottled or reticulate brown pigmentation.
synonyms: EBS - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex] with mottled pigmentation | EBS-MP - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex with mottled pigmentation] | EBSMP | Epidermolysis bullosa simplex-MP | speckled hyperpigmentation with punctate palmoplantar keratoses and childhood blistering

name: Aggressive digital papillary adenocarcinoma
definition: A sweat gland carcinoma characterized by predominantly digital location with a high recurrence rate and metastatic potential.

name: Infantile acne
definition: Infantile acne usually presents at 3–6 months of age but has been reported as late as 16 months. Male infants are affected more commonly than females and there may be a history of severe acne in one or more parents. It may last up to the age of five years. Both comedonal and inflammatory acne with papules, pustules and nodules may be seen; scarring may result.
synonyms: Acne neonatorum | Neonatal acne

name: Hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia
definition: Hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia is a genetic disorder of ectoderm development characterised by malformation of ectodermal structures such as skin, hair, teeth and sweat glands. It comprises three clinically almost indistinguishable subtypes with impaired sweating as the key symptom\: Christ-Siemens-Touraine syndrome (X-linked), autosomal recessive and autosomal dominant hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia, as well as a fourth rare subtype with immunodeficiency as the key symptom.
synonyms: Anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia | anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia | christ-siemens-touraine syndrome | HED | hypohidrotic ED
cn_name: 少汗性外胚层发育不良

name: Burn of trunk except perineum or genitalia
definition: Injury to the tissues of the trunk caused by contact with, for example, heat, steam, chemicals, or electricity.
synonyms: burn and corrosion of trunk | burn of trunk of unspecified degree | corrosion of trunk | torso burn | trunk burn
cn_name: 躯干（除外会阴或生殖器）烧伤

name: Perforating periumbilical calcific elastosis
definition: Perforating calcific elastosis is an acquired localised skin disorder resulting from transepidermal elimination of abnormal shortened calcified elastic fibres. It is found most frequently in obese multiparous middle-aged women and is characterised clinically by lax well-circumscribed reticulated or cobble-stoned plaques surmounted by keratotic papules in the periumbilical region .
synonyms: Localised acquired cutaneous pseudoxanthoma elasticum

name: Sacrococcygeal pilonidal cyst
synonyms: Pilonidal cyst of sacrococcygeal area

name: Subungual haematoma
synonyms: Subungual haemorrhage

name: Oesophageal varices
definition: Abnormally dilated veins developed as portosystemic shunts in the lining of the lower oesophagus in patients with portal hypertension. Once oesophageal varices develop, they continue to grow, and bleeding from oesophageal varices can be fatal.
synonyms: oesophageal varices NOS | oesophageal varix

name: Karyotype 45, X
definition: A disease affecting females, caused by absence of one of the two X chromosomes. This disease may present with short stature, extra folds of skin on the neck, a low hairline at the back of the neck, puffiness or swelling of the hands and feet, skeletal abnormalities, ovarian hypofunction or premature ovarian failure, kidney problems, or heart defects. Confirmation is through observation of only one X chromosome by karyotyping.

name: Normal hair
definition: The absence of any demonstrable abnormality of the hair, particularly the scalp hair.

name: Gestational pemphigoid
definition: Gestational pemphigoid (pemphigoid gestationis) is an autoimmune skin disease characterised by pruritic plaques and blister formation on the skin in association with pregnancy or the trophoblastic tumours, hydatiform mole and choriocarcinoma. The exact causes of the disease are unknown but the disease is mediated by auto-antibodies to the hemidesmosome component BP180/BPAg2/collagen XVII. The maternal antibodies may cause short lived disease in the neonate, neonatal pemphigoid gestationis.  This
synonyms: Herpes gestationis | Pemphigoid gestationis | herpes gestationis | pemphigoid gestationis | Gestational herpes

name: Griscelli syndrome type 2
definition: This type, caused by mutations in RAB27A presents with pigmentary dilution and immunodeficiency
synonyms: GS2 (MIM 607624) | Hypopigmentation - immunodeficiency, with or without neurological impairment | Partial albinism and immunodeficiency syndrome

name: Frostbite of pelvis with tissue necrosis
Mucopolysaccharidosis type 1
definition: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 1 (MPS 1) is a rare lysosomal storage disease belonging to the group of mucopolysaccharidoses. There are three variants, differing widely in their severity, with Hurler syndrome (57% of cases) being the most severe, Scheie syndrome (20% of cases) the mildest and Hurler-Scheie syndrome (23% of cases) giving an intermediate phenotype.
synonyms: Alpha-L-iduronidase deficiency | Gargoylism | Lipochondrodystrophy | MPS1 - [Mucopolysaccharidosis type 1] | dysostosis multiplex | dysostosis multiplex syndrome | l-iduronidase deficiency

name: Congenital erythroderma of uncertain nature
synonyms: Leiner disease

name: Oral aphthae or aphtha-like ulceration
definition: This is a frequent small, shallow, painful ulceration in the oral mucosa. Recurrent oral ulceration that clinically resembles recurrent aphthous stomatitis but presents atypically, including commencement after adolescence, with fever, with a strong family history, or failing to resolve with age.
cn_name: 口腔阿弗他溃疡或阿弗他样溃疡

name: Cutaneous infection specified elsewhere following cosmetic procedure
definition: Infection of skin or subcutaneous tissue attributable to a cosmetic infection. The nature of the infection should be recorded separately.

name: Hydroa vacciniforme-like cutaneous T-cell lymphoma
definition: Hydroa vacciniforme-like cutaneous T-cell lymphoma is a rare and aggressive form of cutaneous lymphoma occurring mainly in children in Latin America and Asia. In common with hydroa vacciniforme, it has been associated with chronic latent Epstein-Barr virus infection.
synonyms: Hydroa vacciniforme-like EBV-associated cutaneous lymphoproliferative disease | Hydroa vacciniforme-like lymphoma

name: Myocutaneous flap necrosis, total
definition: Complete necrosis of a surgical flap containing both skin and muscle

name: Actinic intraepidermal squamous cell carcinoma
definition: Intraepidermal squamous cell carcinoma attributable to chronic exposure to ultraviolet radiation and typically developing from a pre-existing actinic keratosis
synonyms: Intraepidermal squamous cell carcinoma arising from actinic keratosis
cn_name: 光化性表皮内鳞状细胞癌

name: Multiple papillomata
definition: A disease of the skin caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum pertenue. This disease is characterised by numerous papillomatous skin lesions. Transmission is by direct contact with individuals with primary or secondary lesions.
synonyms: Multiple papillomata of yaws | Pianoma | butter yaws | framboesiform syphilid | multiple yaws papillomata

name: Erythema multiforme with oral ulceration
definition: This is a skin condition of unknown cause, possibly mediated by deposition of immune complex (mostly IgM) in the superficial microvasculature of the skin and oral mucous membrane that usually follows an infection or drug exposure.
cn_name: 多形性红斑伴口腔溃疡

name: Cryopyrin-associated periodic syndromes
definition: CAPS is an autoinflammatory disease associated with gain of function changes in the cryopyrin protein, resulting in inflammasome activation and enhanced IL1 beta production. This results in clinical signs and symptoms of inflammation in the form of rash, fever, joint and eye symptoms with increased acute phase reactants.
synonyms: CAPS - [Cryopyrin-associated periodic syndromes] | Cryopyrinopathies
cn_name: 冷凝集素相关周期性综合征

name: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with muscular dystrophy
definition: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with muscular dystrophy (EBS-MD) is an autosomal recessive basal subtype of EBS due to mutations the PLEC gene encoding plectin. It is characterised by generalised blistering associated with muscular dystrophy.
synonyms: EBS - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex] with muscular dystrophy | EBS-MD - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex with muscular dystrophy] | Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with limb-girdle muscular dystrophy

name: Lymphoedema due to obesity
definition: Lymphoedema resulting from morbid obesity. Lymphoedema of the lower limbs and lymphoedema of the abdominal apron fold are common sequelae of chronic morbid obesity.
synonyms: Lymphoedema secondary to obesity

name: Molluscum contagiosum of the napkin area
definition: Molluscum contagiosum affecting the napkin or diaper area in infants and children.

name: Annular oral lichen planus
definition: Annular oral lichen planus is a variant of conventional lichen planus lesions and is characterised by a circumscribed annular configuration that often show slight depression in the centre attributed to atrophy.

name: Infantile eosinophilic pustular folliculitis
definition: Infantile eosinophilic pustular folliculitis is characterised by recurrent outbreaks of non-infective pustular lesions containing eosinophils on the scalp of infants. In the majority of cases the condition commences before the age of six months and remits by the age of three years. In two thirds of cases body areas other than the scalp are affected. The cause is unknown.
synonyms: Infantile eosinophilic pustulosis | Neonatal eosinophilic pustular folliculitis

name: Inverted follicular keratosis
definition: Inverted follicular keratosis is a benign tumour of skin appendages which usually presents as a solitary nodule with a keratotic or scaling surface. A distinct histopathologic feature is the presence of so-called squamous eddies. Many authors regard inverted follicular keratosis as an endophytic viral wart, an endophytic seborrhoeic keratosis or a collision of both.
synonyms: IFK | Inverted folicular keratosis
snomed: 394728005 "inverted follicular keratosis";15112009 "inverted follicular keratosis";201096007 "inverted follicular keratosis";201096007

name: Hemianaesthesia
synonyms: Hemihypalgesia | Hemihypesthesia | Hemiparaesthesia

name: Autosomal recessive dyskeratosis congenita
synonyms: AR-DKC - [Autosomal recessive dyskeratosis congenita]

name: Cutaneous flap necrosis
definition: Necrosis of surgical skin flap

name: Buccal cavity abscess
synonyms: mouth abscess

name: Non-syndromic ichthyosis
definition: Hereditary ichthyoses with clinical manifestations limited to the integument.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 2 of heel
synonyms: stage 2 decubitus ulcer of heel

name: Burn of forearm or elbow, deep partial thickness burn
cn_name: 前臂或肘部深层部分皮层烧伤

name: Rhinosporidiosis
definition: Rhinosporidiosis is a chronic, usually painless localised infection of the mucous membranes. Formerly believed to be a fungus, the causative agent, Rhinosporidium seeberi, has also never been cultured. With 18S rDNA sequencing, this organism has been shown to be a protistan parasite. Rhinosporidiosis occurs worldwide, and the greatest numbers of cases are found in southern India and Sri Lanka.
synonyms: Rhinosporosis

name: Aplasia cutis congenita of scalp
definition: Aplasia cutis congenita limited to the scalp and not associated with other anomalies. Nearly 86% of all solitary lesions of aplasia cutis congenita occur on the scalp. It can be sporadic or inherited as an autosomal dominant condition.

name: Certain protozoal infections affecting the skin
Craniofacial hyperhidrosis
definition: Excessive sweating involving the scalp, face and/or neck.

name: SCARF syndrome
definition: SCARF syndrome is characterised by the association of skeletal abnormalities, cutis laxa, craniostenosis, ambiguous genitalia, psychomotor retardation and facial abnormalities.

name: Bamforth-Lazarus syndrome due to Forkhead box protein E1 \[FOXE1\] mutations
Perioral irritant contact dermatitis due to lip-licking
definition: Perioral irritant contact dermatitis caused by repeated contact with saliva as a result of habitual licking of the lips and perioral skin. This is more commonly seen in children.
synonyms: Lip-licking eczema | Perioral irritant contact eczema due to lip-licking

name: Cardiovascular late syphilis
definition: This is a late, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is involving the cardiovascular area.
synonyms: syphilitic cardiopathy

name: Insulin-induced localised fat hypertrophy
definition: Insulin-induced localised fat hypertrophy is a circumscribed increase in subcutaneous fat at the site or sites of repeated insulin injections by insulin-dependent diabetics.
synonyms: Insulin-induced lipohypertrophy

name: Monilethrix
definition: Monilethrix is a rare genodermatosis characterised by a hair shaft dysplasia resulting in hypotrichosis.
synonyms: Beaded hair | beaded hair

name: Early congenital syphilis, latent
cn_name: 早期先天性梅毒，潜伏性

name: Multifocal basal cell carcinoma
synonyms: multicentric basal cell carcinoma of unspecified site

name: Vulval candidosis
synonyms: Candida vulvitis | Vulval thrush

name: Sinus histiocytosis with massive lymphadenopathy
definition: This is a rare disorder of unknown etiology that is characterised by abundant histiocytes in the lymph nodes throughout the body.
synonyms: Rosai-Dorfman disease | destombes-rosai-dorfman syndrome | sinus histiocytosis with massive lymphadenopathy

name: Lichen aureus
synonyms: <new synonym>lichen purpuricus | lichenoid pigmented purpura

name: Pigmented basal cell carcinoma of skin
Photoaging of the skin
definition: The changes in skin which can be attributed to chronic exposure to ultraviolet radiation and which are clinically manifest principally as actinic elastosis, wrinkles and dyspigmentation.
synonyms: Dermatoheliosis | Sun damage due to chronic sun exposure | photoaging

name: Discoid lupus erythematosus of upper eyelid
Sitosterolaemia
definition: Sitosterolaemia is a genetic disorder caused by deregulation of cholesterol absorption and accumulation of sterols, notably those of plant origin (hence the name phytosterolaemia). The clinical characteristics include xanthomas, premature coronary atherosclerosis, haemolytic anaemia and/or hepatopathy. The disease has been associated with mutations in two genes mapped to the STSL locus\: ABCG5 and ABCG8.
synonyms: Phytosterolaemia | Sisterolaemic xanthomatosis | Xanthomatosis with sisterolaemia

name: Large plaque parapsoriasis
definition: Large plaque parapsoriasis is a chronic skin disorder characterised by the indolent development over years or decades of scaly patches or slightly elevated plaques which may be clinically indistinguishable from early mycosis fungoides but in which no evidence of infiltration by abnormal lymphocytes can be found. Approximately 10% of patients will, however, eventually progress to mycosis fungoides.
synonyms: Premycotic parapsoriasis | parakeratosis variegata | retiform parapsoriasis

name: Hordeolum externum upper eyelid
definition: This is an infection of the sebaceous glands of Zeis at the base of the eyelashes, or an infection of the apocrine sweat glands of Moll. External styes form on the outside of the lids and can be seen as small red bumps. Internal styes are infections of the meibomian sebaceous glands lining the inside of the eyelids. They also cause a red bump underneath the lid with only generalised redness and swelling visible on the outside. This diagnosis is of the upper eye.

name: Pseudoxanthomatous nodular cutaneous mastocytosis
definition: Pseudoxanthomatous nodular cutaneous mastocytosis is a rare variant of cutaneous mastocytosis which presents in infancy with yellowish to red-brown papules and nodules which resemble xanthomata. Histologically there is infiltration of mast cells deep into the lower dermis. It tends to persist longer than other childhood forms of cutaneous mastocytosis.

name: Secondary syphilitic synovitis or tenosynovitis
definition: This is a disorder of inflammation of the joint lining or tendon sheath which causes progressive amounts of pain and is secondary to a syphilitic infection.

name: Fluid overload with generalised oedema
definition: This is the condition where there is too much fluid in the blood. The opposite condition is hypovolemia, which is too little fluid volume in the blood. Fluid volume excess in the intravascular compartment occurs due to an increase in total body sodium content and a consequent increase in extracellular body water. The mechanism usually stems from compromised regulatory mechanisms for sodium handling as seen in congestive heart failure (CHF), kidney failure, and liver failure. It may also be cause

name: Hordeolum internum upper eyelid
definition: This is a cyst in the eyelid that is caused by inflammation of a blocked meibomian gland, usually on the upper eyelid. Chalazia differ from styes (hordeola) in that they are subacute and usually painless nodules. They may become acutely inflamed but, unlike a stye, chalazia usually point inside the lid rather than on the lid margin. This diagnosis is of the upper eyelid.

name: HIV-modified skin disease
definition: Skin disease classified elsewhere the manifestations of which are substantially changed by HIV infection.

name: Schistosomiasis involving the skin
definition: Skin involvement in schistosomiasis may occur either as a result of the initial penetration of the skin by water-borne, free-living cercariae (schistosomal dermatitis), during an immune-complex-mediated phase of infection with S japonica (Katayama fever) or in the later stages of infestation as a result of ectopic localization of worms or ova, especially in paraumbilical and paragenital skin.
synonyms: Cutaneous schistosomiasis

name: General syphilitic paralysis
synonyms: neurosyphilis general paresis

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, knee joint
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, knee joint

name: Dermatoses of the lips or oral cavity
Thermal keratosis
definition: Thermal keratoses are keratotic lesions produced by chronic close proximity of the skin to sources of infrared radiation. Such sources include domestic fires, bed-warmers and heated stoves. They may be associated with erythema ab igne. Thermal keratoses have the potential to develop into invasive squamous cell carcinoma.

name: Ectodermal dysplasia - sensorineural deafness
definition: Ectodermal dysplasia - sensorineural deafness syndrome is characterised by hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia, sensorineural hearing loss, and contracture of the fifth fingers.

name: Vitamin B3 deficiency
definition: Niacin deficiency classically results in pellagra, which is a chronic wasting disease associated with a characteristic erythematous dermatitis that is bilateral and symmetrical, a dementia after mental changes including insomnia and apathy preceding an overt encephalopathy, and diarrhoea resulting from inflammation of the intestinal mucous surfaces. Pellagra occurs endemically in poorer areas of Africa, China and India.
synonyms: amide nicotinic acid deficiency | deficiency of niacin-tryptophan | niacin deficiency NOS | nicotinamide deficiency | nicotinic acid deficiency | vitamin B3 deficiency NOS

name: Premature canities
definition: Premature greying of the hair, usually taken to mean before the age of 20 in Caucasians and before the age of 30 in Africans.
synonyms: Canities | Gray hair | Graying of hair | Grey hair | Greying of hair | Premature greying of hair

name: Sensory or psychological disorders affecting the skin
definition: A group of skin disorders due to disturbances of cutaneous sensation including pruritus and pain, psychological disorders including artefact and delusional states, and dermatoses resulting from nerve damage and other neurological conditions.
cn_name: 累及皮肤的感觉或心理障碍

name: Burn of shoulder or arm, except wrist or hand, deep partial thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of shoulder and arm, except wrist and hand, deep second degree
cn_name: 肩或臂（除外腕或手）深层部分皮层烧伤

name: Malignant neoplasm metastasis in skin of gluteal region
HAIR-AN syndrome
definition: HAIR-AN syndrome is a subphenotype of polycystic ovary syndrome and is characterised by hyperandrogenism, insulin resistance, and acanthosis nigricans. It is one of the most common causes of menstrual disorders, hyperandrogenic symptoms, and insulin resistance among young women.
synonyms: HAIR-AN - [hyperandrogenic-insulin resistance-acanthosis nigricans] syndrome

name: Cutaneous reactions to centipedes or millipedes
definition: Large Chilopoda (centipedes) may inflict painful bites. Diplopoda (millipedes) may expel corrosive secretions composed of benzoquinones and hydroquinones as a defence mechanism. These may produce skin irritation and blistering. They may also cause staining or hypomelanosis.

name: Delusions of malodour
definition: A false, unshakeable belief held with unshakeable certainty by a person that he or she emits an unpleasant odour which is constantly apparent to others. This belief is out of keeping with the patient's educational, cultural and social background.

name: Anaesthesia of skin
definition: Partial or complete loss of sensation affecting the skin, most commonly affecting a circumscribed area and resulting from sensory nerve damage as from injury or leprosy.
synonyms: Hypoaesthesia of skin | Loss of cutaneous sensation | Loss of sensation | Numbness of skin | Paraesthesia of skin | Pins and needles

name: Oligodontia - cancer predisposition syndrome
synonyms: Autosomal dominant ectodermal dysplasia-neoplastic syndrome

name: Occupationally-acquired dermatosis due to heat
definition: Skin disorder caused by occupational exposure to heat or a hot environment.

name: Syphilitic saddle nose deformity
synonyms: congenital syphilitic nose deformity | saddle nose due to syphilis | syphilis saddle nose

name: Cutaneous erythema multiforme
definition: Erythema multiforme confined to the skin and typically triggered by recurrent Herpes simplex infection.
synonyms: Erythema multiforme minus

name: Ocular albinism - late-onset sensorineural deafness
definition: Oculo-cutaneous albinism is a generic term covering a large group of genetic diseases characterised by hypopigmentation of eyes, hair and skin. The ocular anomalies are severe, the patients show reduced visual acuity, photophobia, nystagmus, translucent irides, strabismus, hypermetropic refractive errors, and albinotic fundus with foveal hypoplasia. Skin hypopigmentation induces an increased risk of cutaneous cancer.

name: Syphilis complicating pregnancy, childbirth or the puerperium
definition: Syphilis complicating pregnancy, childbirth or the puerperium
synonyms: syphilis in mother complicating pregnancy, childbirth and/or puerperium

name: Folliculitis keloidalis nuchae
definition: Acne keloidalis refers to the presence of keloid-like papules and plaques on the occipital scalp and posterior neck.
synonyms: Acne keloidalis nuchae | acne keloidalis nuchae | dermatitis papillaris capillitii | folliculitis keloidalis | folliculitis keloidis nuchae | nuchal keloid acne

name: Alopecia totalis
definition: Alopecia totalis is a form of alopecia areata in which hair loss extends to the entire scalp

name: Primary syphilis of nipple
synonyms: chancre of nipple

name: Pityriasis lichenoides chronica
definition: Pityriasis lichenoides chronica is seen predominantly in children and young adults and manifests as multiple asymptomatic small flesh-coloured or reddish scaly flat papules widely distributed over the trunk and proximal limbs. Lesions may appear in crops, waxing and waning spontaneously over months or years. The cause is unknown.
synonyms: Guttate parapsoriasis | Parapsoriasis guttata [Brocq] | guttate parapsoriasis | PLC

name: Anal pruritus
definition: Anal pruritus is irritation of the skin at the anal margin and surrounding perianal skin which results in the desire to scratch.
synonyms: Perianal itching | Pruritus ani

name: Bullous insect bite reaction
definition: Cutaneous blisters resulting from a brisk immune response to insect bites. These are most common around the lower legs and ankles and in children rather than adults.
cn_name: 昆虫叮咬后大疱性反应

name: Mucous membrane pemphigoid of vulval mucosa
definition: Mucous membrane pemphigoid affecting vulval mucosa. This may present with painful erosions and scarring that may be difficult to distinguish from ulcerative lichen planus and intraepithelial neoplasia (VIN) of the vulva.

name: Congenital enterovirus infection
definition: Congenital viral infections with enteroviruses (including coxsackie viruses and ECHO viruses) are infectious embryofetopathies that have been reported to cause fetal malformations, acute systemic illness in the newborn and long-term neurodevelopmental abnormalities.
synonyms: Congenital Coxsackie virus infection

name: Laugier-Hunziker melanosis
definition: Laugier-Hunziker melanosis is an acquired pigmentary disorder characterised by variable numbers of well-defined brown to black lentigines on the lower lip, buccal mucosa and hard palate, frequently in association with longitudinal melanonychia and genital melanosis. It is not associated with gastrointestinal polyposis or other internal disorder.

name: Notalgia paraesthetica
definition: Notalgia paresthetica is a focal intense pruritus typically centred over the medial border of the scapula and thought to be due to a traumatic sensory neuropathy of dorsal spinal nerves.

name: Cutaneous microvascular disturbances due to cold agglutinins
definition: Cold agglutinins are monoclonal or polyclonal IgM immunoglobulins which are able to agglutinate red blood cells at below normal body temperatures. Monoclonal cold agglutinins may be idiopathic or secondary to malignant lymphoproliferative diseases. Polyclonal cold agglutinins are usually associated with infection, especially Mycoplasma pneumoniae but also hepatitis C virus, parvovirus B19 or leptospiral infection. Although cold agglutinins are usually asymptomatic they may be responsible for acr
