name: Dermatophytosis organised by site of involvement
definition: Dermatophytosis organised by anatomical location of infection as in the traditional tinea classification (tinea capitis, tinea pedis etc.) rather than by organism responsible for infection. Additional codes for organism and for clinical manifestation (e.g. kerion) may be required to characterize a dermatophyte infection fully.

name: Lipoedema
definition: Lipoedema is characterised by non-pitting diffuse \"fatty\" swelling, usually confined to the legs, thighs, hips and upper arms. It may be confused with lymphoedema. Lipoedema may also occur in the scalp.

name: Herpes simplex pharyngotonsillitis
definition: This is an inflammation of the pharynx and tonsils caused by human herpes virus types HSV-1 or HSV-2.
synonyms: HSV - [herpes simplex virus] pharyngotonsillitis | herpes pharyngitis | herpes pharyngotonsillitis | herpes sore throat | herpesviral pharyngitis | herpesviral pharyngotonsillitis | herpesviral sore throat

name: Developmental defects of hair or hair growth
synonyms: congenital malformation of hair and hair growth
cn_name: 毛发或毛发生长发育缺陷

name: Syndromic multisystem autoimmune disease due to ITCH deficiency
Miscellaneous specific cutaneous signs of internal malignancy
definition: Miscellaneous skin signs which are attributable to underlying malignancy.

name: Impetigo
definition: A condition of the skin, commonly caused by a secondary infection with the gram-positive bacteria Staphylococcus aureus or group A beta haemolytic streptococci. This condition is characterised by bullous or non-bullous symptoms. Transmission is by direct contact with an infected individual. Confirmation is by identification of the infectious agent in a skin sample.

name: Skin disorders associated with Human immunodeficiency virus infection
definition: The skin manifestations of human immunodeficiency virus infection are manifold and are frequently the presenting sign of initial infection or of advanced disease. Other retroviruses, especially HTLV-1 may present with skin signs.   Code also specified skin disorder.

name: Erythema elevatum diutinum
definition: Erythema elevatum diutinum is a distinctive form of chronic cutaneous vasculitis, belonging to the group of the neutrophilic dermatoses. The disease manifests as chronic, red to purple papules, located mainly on the dorsal aspect of the main joints (knees, elbows, hands and feet). Acute lesions may appear bullous, necrotic or haemorrhagic. Older lesions are yellowish or brown.
synonyms: Bury's disease | EED - [erythema elevatum diutinum] | EED

name: Occupational allergic contact urticaria
definition: Allergic contact urticaria attributable to work. The allergens implicated include latex (especially from gloves), food proteins and a variety of other specific low molecular weight compounds.

name: Traumatic tattoo
definition: Tattoo resulting from retention of chemically inert particles (e.g. coal dust, grit, asphalt or gun-powder) in the skin following abrasions or blast injuries.

name: Melanoma of penis
synonyms: Melanoma of mucocutaneous epithelium of penis | Melanoma of skin of penis

name: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton tonsurans
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton tonsurans and typically causing on endothrix infection of scalp hair.

name: Verrucous haemangioma
definition: Verrucous haemangioma is a congenital capillary vascular malformation involving the dermis and subcutis with overlying verrucous epidermal changes which become more prominent with age. It is normally located on a limb and tends to follow a linear pattern, extending up to 20 cm or more.

name: Cowden syndrome
definition: Cowden syndrome is an autosomal dominant disorder characterised by multiple hamartomas (occurring in the skin, breast, thyroid, gastrointestinal tract, endometrium and brain), and an increased risk of malignant tumours (breast, endometrial and thyroid cancer). Skin is involved in 90-100% of cases (trichilemmomas, oral mucosal papillomatosis, acral keratoses and palmoplantar keratoses). The exact prevalence is unknown, the estimated prevalence is 1 in 200,000. Cowden syndrome results most commonl
synonyms: Multiple hamartoma syndrome | CD | cowden syndrome | MHAM | multiple hamartoma syndrome

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to clothing or footwear
Drug-exacerbated psoriasis
definition: Exacerbation of psoriasis by systemic medication. Lithium and alpha-interferon are well known examples of drugs which may worsen psoriasis.

name: Early cutaneous Lyme borreliosis
definition: Borrelia burgdorferi infection involving the skin, typically as erythema migrans, the commonest presentation of Lyme disease.
synonyms: Stage 1 Lyme disease

name: Neonatal disorders of the oral mucosa
Pigmented skin lesion of uncertain nature, trunk
definition: This denotes the presence of a pigmented skin lesion on the trunk but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Intrinsic ageing of the skin
definition: Skin changes attributable to senescence rather than to chronic photodamage.

name: Calcinosis cutis
synonyms: Cutaneous calcification | Subcutaneous calcification | bone metaplasia in skin | calcinosis cutis

name: Occupational leukoderma
definition: Occupational leukoderma is a partial or complete loss of skin pigmentation caused by damage to melanocytes from occupational exposure to monobenzyl ether of hydroquinone (MBEH), 4-tert-butylcatechol (PTBC), para-substituted phenols (PSP) or other depigmenting chemicals.
synonyms: Occupational vitiligo

name: Congenital lipoid adrenal hyperplasia due to STAR deficiency
definition: This is an endocrine disorder that is an uncommon and potentially lethal form of congenital adrenal hyperplasia (CAH). This diagnosis is due to STAR deficiency.

name: Congenital fissures of tongue
synonyms: Congenitally fissured tongue | Congenitally furrowed tongue

name: Transient neonatal Behçet disease
definition: An uncommon disorder in which the newborn infants of mothers with Behçet disease develop a transient form of the disease with cutaneous pustules and oral and genital ulcers. Although the disease does not persist the infant may be left with scars.

name: Pulmonary late syphilis
definition: This is a pulmonary, late sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum.
synonyms: pulmonary syphilis

name: Acral self-healing collodion baby
definition: Acral self-healing collodion baby (SHCB) is a variant of SHCB characterised by the presence at birth of a collodion membrane only at the extremities.

name: Absence of skin condition
definition: A class enabling normal skin findings or exclusion of suspected skin condition to be recorded.

name: Syphilitic parkinsonism
definition: This is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with a neurological syndrome characterised by tremor, hypokinesia, rigidity, and postural instability.

name: Familial reactive perforating collagenosis
definition: Familial reactive perforating collagenosis is a rare autosomal dominant skin disorder characterised by the development of multiple small umbilicated papules with central horny plugs, typically on the extensor surfaces of the hands, elbows and knees. The plugs are formed from trans-epidermal extrusion of abnormal dermal collagen, though the precise defect is not known. The condition usually starts in early childhood but persists into adult life. It is usually exacerbated by environmental cold or
synonyms: RPC

name: Subcutaneous swelling, mass or lump, head
definition: One or more subcutaneous soft tissue masses of undetermined or unspecified nature localised to the head
synonyms: head mass | head swelling | localised mass of head | localised swelling of head | localised swelling, mass and lump, head | mass of head | swelling of head

name: Chemical burn due to occupational skin contact with corrosive substance
definition: A chemical burn due to contact of the skin with corrosive or caustic substances encountered at work. This is seen most commonly in chemical and metal industries.

name: Cutaneous lymphoma
definition: Primary cutaneous lymphomas are malignant lymphoproliferative diseases of unknown cause that are thought to originate from lymphocytes in the lymphoid tissue of the skin and by definition are confined to the skin at initial diagnosis.

name: Necrotising lymphocytic folliculitis
definition: Necrotising lymphocytic folliculitis (acne necrotica varioliformis) is a rare and poorly understood chronic scarring follicular dermatosis characterised by necrotizing inflammation of follicles close to the scalp margins resulting in multiple small, round varioliform scars.
synonyms: Acne necrotica varioliformis

name: Acute febrile neutrophilic dermatosis, classical type
definition: Classical acute febrile neutrophilic dermatosis (Sweet syndrome) is sporadic and often associated with preceding upper respiratory tract infection.
synonyms: Sweet syndrome, classical type

name: Lymphocutaneous leishmaniasis
definition: A form of leishmaniasis in which there is lymphatic spread to lymph nodes from the initial cutaneous site of infection. Nodules or ulceration along lymphatic channels are associated principally with L.panamensis, L. guyanensis and L. braziliensis.
synonyms: cutaneous leishmaniasis with nodular lymphangitis

name: Cranio-osteoarthropathy
definition: Cranio-osteoarthropathy is a form of primary hypertrophic osteoarthropathy characterised by delayed closure of the cranial sutures and fontanelles, digital clubbing, arthropathy, and periostosis.

name: Spontaneous urticaria
definition: Spontaneous urticaria is a disease characterised by the daily or almost daily eruption of spontaneous weals, angioedema or both.
synonyms: ordinary urticaria | spontaneous urticaria NOS

name: Systemic contact dermatitis due to ingested allergen
definition: Dermatitis provoked by ingestion of allergens to which the individual has been previously sensitized by topical exposure. Recognised causes include nickel, spices containing Myroxylon pereirae (balsam of Peru) or herbal remedies containing sesquiterpene lactones.

name: Subcutaneous phaeohyphomycosis
definition: Phaeohyphomycosis consists of a heterogeneous group of fungal infections caused by more than 80 genera and species. Subcutaneous infection usually follows traumatic implantation of a fungus by a wooden splinter that the fungus inhabits as a saprophyte. The growth of the fungus forms verrucous plaques or a painless subcutaneous abscess. Subcutaneous infection is usually caused by Exophiala and Phialophora species.

name: Secondary oesophageal varices without bleeding
definition: These are extremely dilated sub-mucosal veins in the lower third of the oesophagus, without bleeding in diseases classified elsewhere.
synonyms: Oesophageal varices without bleeding in diseases classified elsewhere

name: Cataract – hypertrichosis – intellectual deficit
definition: Cataract - hypertrichosis - intellectual deficit syndrome is an inherited syndrome characterised by congenital cataract, generalised hypertrichosis and intellectual deficit.
synonyms: CAHMR syndrome | Cataract - hypertrichosis - mental retardation syndrome

name: Ulcerative lichen planus
definition: A rare chronic form of lichen planus typically affecting the soles of the feet. It may also affect the nail unit resulting in permanent loss of nails.

name: Lower limb venous eczema
definition: A pruritic inflammatory dermatitis affecting the lower legs and ankles of individuals with lower limb venous hypertension. It may become acutely exudative, when the possibility of superimposed allergic contact dermatitis should be considered. Treatment of the associated venous hypertension is an important part of management. Venous eczema is not necessarily associated with the presence of varicose leg veins.
synonyms: Varicose dermatitis | Varicose eczema | Venous dermatitis | Venous eczema | congestion eczema | gravitational dermatitis | varicose eczema

name: Inherited patterned lentiginosis
definition: Inherited patterned lentiginosis is an autosomal dominant condition characterised by lentigines on the face and lips without oral mucosal involvement. It is seen particularly in lighter-skinned individuals of African or mixed ethnic ancestry. Lentigines may also be present on the elbows, knees, buttocks, palms and soles.

name: Deletions of the short arm of chromosome 4
synonyms: 4p partial monosomy syndrome | chromosome 4 short arm deletion | chromosome 4 short arm deletion syndrome | deletion of short arm of chromosome 4

name: Generalised excess and redundant skin following bariatric surgery
definition: generalised excess and redundant skin occurring in formerly grossly obese individuals who have achieved significant weight loss as a result of procedures such as gastric banding.

name: Disorders of the dermis or subcutis
definition: This group incorporates disorders of dermal connective tissue, dermal histiocytic and granulomatous disorders and disorders affecting subcutaneous fat.

name: Superficial mixed comedonal and papulopustular acne
definition: Acne in which comedones are accompanied by small inflammatory papules and pustules
synonyms: Superficial mixed comedonal and inflammatory acne
cn_name: 浅表混合性丘疹脓疱型痤疮和粉刺

name: Pseudopelade
definition: Pseudopelade (Brocq) is a chronic, slowly progressive, patchy scarring alopecia of the scalp that develops without any clinical evidence of inflammation, though histologically there may initially be a low grade infiltration of lymphocytes around the hair follicle. Many cases are thought to represent an insidious form of follicular lichen planus of the scalp. It presents with multiple small smooth patches of alopecia within which follicular ostia cannot be discerned.
synonyms: Pseudopelade of Brocq

name: Gram-negative folliculitis
definition: Superficial folliculitis of acne-prone skin due to overgrowth by Gram-negative organisms (e.g. Klebsiella, Escherichia coli, Proteus and Pseudomonas) in individuals receiving long-term antibiotic therapy for acne.

name: Chylomicron retention disease
definition: Chylomicron retention disease (CRD) is a type of familial hypocholesterolaemia characterised by malnutrition, failure to thrive, growth failure, vitamin E deficiency and hepatic (steatosis), neurologic (areflexia, ataxia, myopathy, sensory neuropathy) and ophthalmologic (minor visual abnormalities) complications.
synonyms: Anderson disease | CMRD - [chylomicron retention disease] | Hypobetalipoproteinaemia with selective deletion of Apo B-48

name: Actinic lentigo
definition: A circumscribed grey or brown macule resulting from chronic exposure to the sun or to artificial sources of ultraviolet such as sun-beds. They are typically located on the backs of the hands and forearms, shoulders, forehead and the scalp (if bald). They often coexist with and may be difficult to differentiate from plane seborrhoeic keratoses, which differ clinically in exhibiting fine scaling and histologically in showing proliferation of keratinocytes as well as melanocytes.
synonyms: Age spot | Lentigo senilis | Liver spot | Senile freckle | Solar lentigo | Sun spot | lentigo senilis | liver spot | old age spot | senile freckle

name: Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, type 2B
definition: Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, type 2B (ARCL-2B) is characterised by wrinkled inelastic skin, especially on the dorsa of the extremities and on the abdomen, osteoporosis, hip dislocation, intrauterine and postnatal growth retardation and developmental delay. Distinctive features include triangular dysmorphic facies with progeroid appearance, bulbous nose, prognathism, hypotelorism, epicanthal folds, blue sclerae, large ears and microcephaly. Some of these features overlap with those of geroderm
synonyms: ARCL-2B - [Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, type 2B] (MIM 612940) | Cutis laxa with bone dystrophy | Cutis laxa with joint laxity and developmental delay

name: Inflammatory disorders of the external ear
synonyms: inflammation of external auditory canal

name: Pressure ulceration grade 1 of ischium
synonyms: stage 1 decubitus ulcer of ischium

name: Mucocutaneous perinatal varicella
definition: Varicella normally resulting from transplacental infection from a mother who develops varicella from 5 to 21 days before delivery. This allows time for the mother to produce virus-specific antibodies which can be transferred transplacentally to the fetus and thus limit the severity of infection in the newborn child. The risk of disseminated disease is much less than in mothers who give birth within a shorter time after developing varicella.

name: Anatomical distortion of skin or soft tissues following cutaneous surgical procedure
definition: Unsatisfactory outcome following skin surgery due to failure to restore normal anatomical contours and resulting in, for instance, lip or eyelid retraction

name: Necrolytic migratory erythema
definition: Necrolytic migratory erythema is a rare dermatosis closely associated with an underlying glucagon-secreting tumour of pancreatic islet cells (glucagonoma). It may present clinically with recent onset diabetes mellitus, weight loss and glossitis. Skin and mucous membranes are affected with painful migratory erythematous polycyclic patches or plaques with superficial pustules, vesicles, or bullae. The lesions are typically widespread, but they are accentuated in the intertriginous areas, lower abd
synonyms: Glucagonoma syndrome

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, shoulder region
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, shoulder region

name: Leptospirosis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Leptospira. In the first phase, this disease is characterised by generalised illness (fever, chills, or myalgias) or individuals may be asymptomatic; in the second phase, the heart, liver, kidneys, or brain may be affected by the infection (symptoms are dependent on the site affected). Transmission is by ingestion of contaminated food or water, droplet transmission, or direct cutaneous contact. Confirmation is by identification of
synonyms: EIA - [equine infectious anaemia] | Nanukayami disease | Nanukayami fever | mud fever | seven-day fever | swamp fever | fort bragg fever | pretibial fever | weil's disease

name: Irritant contact dermatitis of specified site
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis organised by the body part affected.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema organised by site

name: Secondary erythromelalgia
definition: Secondary Erythromelalgia can be seen in other disorders like myeloproliferative diseases and small fibre polyneuropathies.

name: Unclassifiable benign cutaneous melanocytic proliferation
definition: A benign proliferation of epidermal melanocytes which do not conform to the criteria for a melanocytic naevus.

name: Childhood linear IgA bullous dermatosis
definition: Childhood linear IgA bullous dermatosis is an autoimmune skin disease characterised by blisters on the skin and mucous membranes. The exact causes of the disease are unknown but the disease is mediated by IgA autoantibodies to hemidesmosome and basement membrane components BP180 and its shed ectodomain, BP230 and LAD285. The disease affects children, often of preschool age. Those rare patients with very prominent mucosal involvement with scarring are now classified as mucous membrane pemphigoid.
synonyms: Chronic bullous disease of childhood | Linear IgA bullous dermatosis, childhood form | benign chronic bullous dermatosis of childhood | CBDC | childhood linear IgA dermatitis herpetiformis | chronic bullous disease of childhood

name: Neutrophilic dermatosis of the hands
definition: A clinically distinct variant of Sweet syndrome in which inflammatory plaques and pustules are largely confined to the dorsal surfaces of the hands. Although criteria for small vessel vasculitis may be present and it has been reported as pustular vasculitis of the hands, it is now considered that the vasculitis is secondary to intense local inflammation. Its relationship to underlying blood dyscrasias is stronger than in classical Sweet syndrome.
synonyms: Acral Sweet syndrome | Neutrophilic dermatosis of the dorsal hands | Sweet disease of the dorsa of the hands

name: Anaphylaxis due to radiocontrast media
definition: Anaphylaxis attributable to the use of diagnostic radiocontrast media, particularly high-osmolality or ionic iodinated contrast media.

name: Erythema nodosum leprosum
definition: Erythema nodosum leprosum is a necrotizing vasculitis involving small to medium-sized vessels of the deep dermis and subcutis. It is provoked by an immune complex-mediated response to release of mycobacterial antigens from effete bacilli in patients with multibacillary leprosy (lepromatous and borderline lepromatous), usually after initiation of treatment.

name: Infections of the nail or perionychium
definition: Infections involving the nail or perionychium for which no information on the infecting organism is available.
synonyms: infection of paronychium

name: Common warts
definition: Common warts are due to an infection of the epidermis by certain human papilloma viruses, most commonly HPV subtypes 1, 2, 4, 27 and 57. They manifest typically as papillomatous, keratinous growths on the hands and feet but may affect any part of the skin (and also adjacent mucous epithelia). They are very common during childhood and adolescence.
synonyms: common warts NOS | verruca vulgaris | verruca vulgaris NOS | viral warts NOS | common warts

name: Necatoriasis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the parasitic worm Necator americanus. This disease is characterised by pruritus at the site of larval penetration. In mild infections, this disease may be asymptomatic; in moderate to severe infections, this disease may present with cough, pharyngeal irritation during larval migration in airways, iron-deficiency anaemia, abdominal pain, nausea, arthralgia, or delayed development (mental or physical). Transmission is by direct contact with larvae from soil o
synonyms: African cachexia | Egyptian anaemia | Egyptian chlorosis | Gotthard tunnel disease | Greisinger disease | Infection due to Necator americanus | St Gotthard disease | brickmaker's anaemia | brickmaker's chlorosis | cachexia aquosa

name: Cutaneous microvascular disturbances due to vascular coagulopathies
definition: A range of disorders characterised by occlusion of small blood vessels in the microvasculature of the skin as a result of abnormal blood coagulability. The disturbances may be limited to the skin (atrophie balnche and livedoid vasculopathy) or associated with more widespread vascular occlusion (malignant atrophic papulosis of Degos).

name: Alopecia due to thyroid disorder
definition: Chronic diffuse telogen hair loss attributable to hyper- or hypothyroidism
synonyms: Alopecia secondary to thyroid disorder

name: Verrucous squamous cell carcinoma of skin
definition: Verrucous squamous cell carcinoma is a rare variant of well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma with low malignant potential. It occurs principally on the glans and prepuce of the penis and on the sole of the foot.
synonyms: Verrucous carcinoma of skin

name: Physalia sting
definition: Envenomation from contact with the marine hydrozoans Physalia physalis (Atlantic Portuguese man-of-war) or Physalia utriculus (Indo-Pacific Portuguese man-of-war), which presents acutely with severe pain distributed like a whip lash with multiple long thin linear urticarial weals which may blister and ulcerate. Systemic involvement with shock, haemolysis and respiratory failure may occur.

name: Acne scarring
definition: Scarring resulting from acne, ranging from mild irregularity of the skin surface to highly disfiguring or functionally disabling distortion of normal skin anatomy.

name: Multiple mononeuropathy vasculitis with Churg-Strauss syndrome
definition: Churg Strauss syndrome is an allergic granulomatosis and angiitis, appearing in all age groups, but in particular in the more advanced age. It presents with a painful mononeuritis multiplex and an asymmetric neuropathy. Rarely also symmetric neuropathies occur. The sensory and motor loss does usually not affect the trunk and cranial nerves are usually spared. In addition to malaise and fatigue, bronchial asthma, pulmonary infiltrates and eosinophilia are suggestive.  Associated neural autoantibo

name: Hereditary sclerosing poikiloderma
definition: Hereditary sclerosing poikiloderma consists in generalised poikiloderma with accentuation in flexural regions and sclerosis of palms and soles. Several variants have been described.
synonyms: Hereditary sclerosing poikiloderma (MIM 173700)

name: Cutis marmorata telangiectatica congenita
definition: Cutis marmorata telangiectatica congenita (CMTC) is a congenital localised or generalised vascular anomaly characterised by a persistent cutis marmorata pattern with a marbled bluish to deep purple appearance, spider nevus-like telangiectasia, phlebectasia and, occasionally, ulceration and atrophy of the affected skin.
synonyms: CMTC - [Cutis marmorata telangiectatica congenita] | Congenital phlebectasia | Reticulate vascular naevus | CMTC

name: Neu-Laxova syndrome
definition: Neu-Laxova syndrome is a rare, multiple malformation syndrome characterised by severe intrauterine growth retardation (IUGR), severe microcephaly with a sloping forehead, severe ichthyosis (collodion baby type), and facial dysmorphism. Severe central nervous system (CNS) defects are present\: lissencephaly type III, hypoplastic cerebellum and brainstem, enlarged ventricles, and sometimes intracerebral calcifications, Dandy-Walker anomaly or agenesis of the corpus callosum.

name: Actinic granuloma
synonyms: O'Brien granuloma

name: Skin tags or polyps
definition: Benign outgrowths of skin consisting of a fibrovascular core covered with normal or thinned epidermis. They may be single or multiple and range in diameter from less than a millimetre to a centimetre or more.

name: Cutaneous disorders attributable to iatrogenic immunosuppression
definition: Skin disorders resulting from or modified by immunosuppressive medication, particularly in transplant recipients. These include viral warts, recalcitrant superficial fungal infections, epidermal dysplasia and multiple skin cancers. This concept should be linked to the particular skin manifestation.

name: Frostbite with tissue necrosis of neck
synonyms: frostbite of neck with tissue necrosis

name: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa, localised types
definition: Subtypes of junctional epidermolysis bullosa with localised blistering

name: Primary venous leg ulcer
definition: A venous leg ulcer developing in skin without preceding episodes of ulceration.

name: Submammary intertrigo
definition: Intertrigo in the skin folds beneath the breasts.
synonyms: Inframammary intertrigo

name: Predominantly tropical or subtropical bacterial infections affecting skin
cn_name: 主要累及皮肤的热带或亚热带细菌感染

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to plastics, glues or resin systems
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to plastics, glues and resin systems (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to plastics, glues and resin systems).

name: Warty tuberculosis
definition: Warty tuberculosis results from exogenous inoculation of M. tuberculosis at sites prone to trauma. Starting as a small warty papule, it gradually enlarges to form a firm, reddish-brown verrucous plaque.
synonyms: Tuberculosis verrucosa cutis | Verrucous tuberculosis

name: Localised lipoatrophy due to specified cause, not elsewhere classified
definition: Localised lipoatrophy the cause of which cannot be precisely captured by other available terms.

name: Woolly hair – hypotrichosis – everted lower lip – outstanding ears
synonyms: Salamon syndrome

name: Idiopathic guttate hypomelanosis
definition: A very common slowly progressive condition presenting in later life as multiple small white macules on exposed skin, particularly the forearms. It is considered to be a component of photoaging.
synonyms: Disseminate lenticular leukoderma

name: Burn from exposure to ultraviolet tanning device
Disseminated perinatal varicella
definition: Disseminated perinatal varicella occurs typically when a Varicella Zoster virus naïve mother develops varicella in late pregnancy, typically within five days of delivery. Because she does not have time to develop protective antibodies to transfer transplacentally to her fetus, her newborn child is at high risk of disseminated infection with widespread cutaneous and visceral lesions and respiratory distress due to varicella pneumonia. There is a high mortality if prompt intervention is not undert

name: Acrokeratosis verruciformis of Darier
definition: A localised form of Darier disease confined to acral sites, with multiple flesh-coloured or lightly pigmented papules resembling plane warts on the dorsa of the hands and feet. There may be associated abnormalities affecting the palms and the nails as in Darier disease.

name: Neonatal skin infection
definition: Any condition of the skin affecting neonates, caused by an infection with a bacterial, viral, fungal, or parasitic source.
synonyms: Skin infection of newborn

name: Childhood atopic eczema
definition: Atopic eczema in children and adolescents first presenting or continuing after infancy up to age 19 years. Its prevalence is highest in northern latitudes (e.g. nearly 20% in Norwegian children as compared with 0.7% in Tanzanian children). The sites most characteristically involved are the elbow and knee flexures, sides of the neck, wrists and ankles. As the disease progresses, lichenification (skin thickening) becomes a typical clinical feature, especially in areas that can be easily reached an
synonyms: Childhood atopic dermatitis

name: Photo-allergic contact dermatitis organised by photo-allergen class
synonyms: Photo-allergic contact eczema organised by photo-allergen class

name: McCune-Albright syndrome
definition: McCune-Albright syndrome (MAS) is classically defined by the clinical triad of fibrous dysplasia of bone (FD), café-au-lait skin spots, and precocious puberty (PP).
synonyms: Albright-McCune-Sternberg syndrome | Fibrous dysplasia with pigmentary skin changes and precocious puberty

name: Auricular cartilage calcification
definition: Petrified ear with calcification of cartilage.

name: Neurofibromatosis type 2
definition: Neurofibromatosis type 2 (NF2) is a tumour-prone disorder characterised by the development of multiple schwannomas and meningiomas.
synonyms: Bilateral acoustic neurofibromatosis | Familial acoustic neuroma | Familial vestibular schwannoma | NF2 - [Neurofibromatosis type 2] (MIM 101000) | Neurofibromatosis, central type | acoustic schwannoma

name: Infestation of the skin by ectoparasites
Erythromelanosis follicularis faciei
definition: A follicular disorder that involves the lateral cheeks and sometimes the lateral aspects of the neck. Multiple pinpoint follicular papules are superimposed on a red-brown patch; the papules appear relatively hypopigmented.
synonyms: erythromelanosis follicularis faciei et colli

name: Syndromic diffuse palmoplantar keratodermas
definition: Diffuse palmoplantar keratoderma associated with extracutaneous abnormalities.

name: Cholinergic erythema
definition: A condition in which there a usually widespread eruption of punctate pink macules weals which appear to be fixed. Careful observation demonstrates that individual macules are short-lived as in cholinergic urticaria but are constantly replaced by new macules. The link to sweating and exercise is not so prominent and the condition is not always pruritic..

name: Wasp sting
definition: Stings by wasps or hornets, the venoms of which cause local toxic reactions in the unsensitised but may result in anaphylaxis in those previously sensitised. The severity of the reaction depends on the dose of venom and degree of previous sensitisation.

name: Pediculosis capitis
definition: A condition of the scalp and hair shaft, caused by an infection with the parasite Pediculus humanus capitis. This condition is characterised by pruritus which may lead to sores or thickened discoloured skin. Transmission is by direct or indirect contact with an infected individual or animal. Confirmation is by identification of Pediculus humanus capitis eggs or Pediculus humanus capitis.
synonyms: Head lice | Head-louse infestation | Infestation by Pediculus capitis | \ | nits | pediculosis capitis

name: Burn of forearm or elbow, full thickness burn
Classical phenylketonuria, phenylalanine hydroxylase total deficiency
definition: Phenylketonuria (PKU) is an autosomal recessive metabolic genetic disorder characterised by a mutation in the gene for the hepatic enzyme phenylalanine hydroxylase (PAH), rendering it nonfunctional, with phenylalanine hydroxylase total deficiency.

name: Renal late syphilis
definition: Renal involvement in late syphilis is uncommon but may manifest as membranous glomerulonephritis, mesangial and endothelial cell proliferative glomerulonephritis, rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis and nephrotic syndrome.

name: Kaposiform haemangioendothelioma
definition: Kaposiform haemangioendothelioma is a rare low-grade malignant cutaneous or visceral vascular tumour that may be associated with severe thrombopaenia with consumption coagulopathy (Kasabach-Merritt syndrome) in paediatric patients.

name: Phototoxic reaction to fragrance or cosmetics
definition: Phototoxic reaction caused by a combination of sun exposure and skin contact with fragrances or cosmetics containing photoactive substances such as oak moss, musk ambrette or bergamot oil.
cn_name: 对香料或化妆品的光毒性反应

name: Corrosion of unspecified degree of head or neck
Drug-related adverse cutaneous reactions to epidermal growth factor receptor inhibitor therapy
synonyms: EGF receptor inhibitor-related papulopustular drug eruption

name: Corrosion of second degree of wrist or hand
Acute haemorrhagic oedema of infancy
definition: Acute haemorrhagic oedema is an immune complex-mediated cutaneous vasculitis usually associated with respiratory infection or immunization. It affects children between the ages of 4 months and 2 years, with males being affected twice as frequently as females. The dramatic clinical appearance of facial and limb oedema with multiple targetoid purpuric macules belies its generally benign course.

name: Postinflammatory hypomelanosis
definition: A reduction in skin pigmentation apparent following the resolution of skin inflammation. This may be from an inflammatory dermatosis such as dermatitis or lichen planus or may follow trauma or interventional procedures. The loss of pigment may cause significant psychological distress in dark-skinned individuals but in most cases is temporary.
synonyms: postinflammatory hypopigmentation | PIH

name: Miscellaneous pemphigoid variants
definition: Other specified form of pemphigoid not elsewhere classified.

name: Pilar smooth muscle hamartoma
synonyms: Arrector pili hamartoma | Congenital pilar and smooth muscle naevus

name: Atopic eczema, special forms
definition: Forms of atopic eczema of therapeutic or other significance

name: Stiff skin syndrome
definition: A skin disease characterized by hard, thick skin, usually over the entire body, limiting joint mobility and causing flexion contractures that has_material_basis_in heterozygous mutation in the FBN1 gene on chromosome 15q21.1.
synonyms: congenital fascial dystrophy | SSKS

name: Acquired mixed hyper- and hypomelanotic disorders of skin pigmentation
Acquired disorders of the hair shaft
Oesophageal varices without bleeding in alcoholic liver disease
definition: These are extremely dilated sub-mucosal veins in the lower third of the oesophagus without bleeding in alcoholic liver disease.

name: Primary cutaneous acral CD8-positive T-cell lymphoma
definition: A rare lymphoproliferative neoplasm characterised by a focal dense dermal infiltrate of atypical CD8-positive lymphocytes. It manifests typically as a solitary nodule on the external ear but has also been described at other sites including the nose, hands and feet.
synonyms: Indolent CD8+ lymphoid proliferation of acral sites | primary cutaneous acral CD8+ T-cell lymphoma

name: Generalised eruptive lentiginosis
definition: This is an uncommon sporadic condition characterised by the widespread development of large numbers of lentigines, often several hundred in number, that appear rapidly over the course of a few months. It is well recognised in adolescents and young adults who show no evidence of systemic abnormalities but has also been linked to treatment with cancer chemotherapeutic agents and to immunosuppression, particularly in transplant recipients.

name: Lymphoedematous acne
definition: Painless but persistent solid symmetrical facial lymphoedema and erythema localised to the forehead and mid face of patients with inflammatory acne vulgaris, typically of several years' duration. It is thought to result from damage to draining lymphatics and is frequently resistant to treatment.
synonyms: Facial lymphoedema secondary to acne vulgaris

name: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton verrucosum
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton verrucosum, the main natural hosts of which are cattle.

name: Atopic eczema, follicular pattern
definition: A distinctive follicular pattern of atopic eczema seen most commonly in children of African ethnicity.
synonyms: Atopic dermatitis, follicular pattern

name: Trichilemmoma
synonyms: tricholemmoma

name: Stevens-Johnson syndrome and toxic epidermal necrolysis due to drug
definition: A spectrum of severe and potentially life-threatening reactions affecting skin and mucous membranes. In the majority of cases a drug can be implicated.
cn_name: Stevens-Johnson 综合征和中毒性表皮坏死松解症

name: Thiamine-responsive maple-syrup-urine disease
definition: This is an thiamine-responsive autosomal recessive metabolic disorder affecting branched-chain amino acids. It is one type of organic acidemia.

name: Drug-induced hair abnormalities
synonyms: medicament-induced hair abnormalies

name: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum gallinae
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum gallinae, the main natural hosts of which are fowl.

name: Pseudoxanthoma elasticum-like papillary dermal elastolysis
definition: Pseudoxanthoma elasticum-like papillary dermal elastolysis (PXE-PDE) is a rare, acquired, idiopathic dermal tissue disorder characterized by numerous, asymptomatic, 2-3 mm, yellowish, non-follicular papules that tend to converge into cobblestone-like plaques which are distributed symmetrically over the posterior neck, supraclavicular region, axillae, and sometimes abdomen. Unlike PXE, these skin lesions show select elimination (absence or marked loss) of elastic fibers in the papillary dermis an

name: Rheumatoid nodule
definition: An extra-articular manifestation of rheumatoid arthritis usually found on a periarticular structure, extensor surface, or other areas subjected to mechanical pressure. It can also develop elsewhere, including the pleura and meninges. Nodules vary in size and consistency and are rarely symptomatic.
synonyms: rheumatoid node | rheumatoid nodulosis | subcutaneous rheumatoid nodule

name: Generalised excess and redundant skin
definition: Widespread excess and redundant skin occurring typically as a result of massive weight loss.

name: Suspected deep pressure-induced tissue damage, depth unknown of upper limb
Prurigo
definition: Prurigo is a cutaneous reaction pattern due to chronic scratching of itchy skin. It is characterised by widespread, symmetrically distributed, itchy, excoriated papules and nodules with focal epidermal acanthosis and hyperkeratosis on histology.

name: Neonatal staphylococcal omphalitis
definition: Infection of the umbilical cord stump by Staphylococcus aureus.

name: Miliaria crystallina
definition: Miliaria crystallina is characterised by the formation of numerous tiny clear vesicles in the stratum corneum as a result of superficial occlusion of the eccrine sweat duct. It is often seen in febrile illnesses associated with profuse sweating. It also commonly occurs in infants due to immaturity of the sweat ducts.
synonyms: Sudamina | miliaria sudamina | superficial ductal occlusion

name: Sacrococcygeal pilonidal disease
definition: Pilonidal disease describes a spectrum of clinical presentations, ranging from asymptomatic hair-containing cysts and sinuses to large symptomatic abscesses of the sacrococcygeal area which tend to recur. It is found predominantly in white males in their second and third decades and is thought to result from penetration of hair into the tissues with the formation of sinuses and a foreign-body granulomatous response. Risk factors for pilonidal disease include male gender, Caucasian ethnicity, sit
synonyms: pilonidal disease of sacrococcygeal area | sacrococcygeal pilonidal disease NOS

name: Trachoma
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Chlamydia trachomatis. This disease is characterised by a roughening of the inner surfaces of the eyes, and inflammation that may lead to superficial vascularization of the cornea (pannus) and scarring of the conjunctiva. Long term effects include blindness or other visual impairments. Transmission is by direct or indirect contact with the eyes or nose of an infected individual.

name: Traumatized seborrhoeic keratosis
definition: A seborrhoeic keratosis the appearance of which has been modified by trauma. Sudden enlargement, bleeding and/or inflammation may give rise to concern about malignancy.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 3 of spinous processes
synonyms: stage 3 decubitus ulcer of spinous processes

name: Hordeolum internum
definition: A focal acute pyogenic infection, usually by Staphylococcus aureus, of a meibomian gland, the normal secretion from which into the eyelash follicle is blocked. It presents as an acute inflammatory swelling which may discharge onto the conjunctival surface of the eyelid, or rarely anteriorly through the eyelid skin. It may predispose to formation of a chalazion.
synonyms: Acute meibomitis | Internal hordeolum | Meibomian gland abscess

name: Taurodontia - absent teeth - sparse hair
definition: Taurodontia - absent teeth - sparse hair syndrome is characterised by congenital absence of the teeth, and sparse or absent hair. Taurodontia is also present in the majority of cases.

name: Condylomata lata
definition: This is a cutaneous condition characterised by wart like lesions on the genitals. They are generally symptoms of the secondary phase of syphilis, caused by the spirochete, Treponema pallidum.
synonyms: Condyloma latum

name: Skin damage resulting from congenital or hereditary neuropathy
Cutaneous complications of surgical, laser or other interventional procedures
synonyms: Postprocedural cutaneous complications of surgical, laser or other interventions

name: Corrosion of third degree of trunk
Gustatory flushing
definition: Gustatory flushing is a response to eating spicy foods and is usually associated with sweating. It is due to a nerve reflex involving autonomic neurones carried by the branches of the trigeminal nerve. It may be a significant handicap in individuals with a low threshold for this response.

name: Noduloulcerative basal cell carcinoma of skin
Irritant contact dermatitis of external ear
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis affecting skin of external ear. This may result from retention of irritants such as soaps and shampoos in the external auditory canal or from friction and maceration from use of hearing-aids etc. Irritant damage to the skin may predispose to secondary infection.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema of external ear | Irritant otitis externa

name: Dermatoosteolysis, Kirghizian type
definition: Dermatoosteolysis, Kirghizian type, is a genetic syndrome characterised by recurrent skin ulceration, arthralgia, fever, peri-articular osteolysis, oligodontia and nail dystrophy.

name: Primary lymphoedema
definition: Lymphoedema as a result of lymphatic vessel hypoplasia
synonyms: Congenital lymphoedema

name: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex, generalised intermediate
definition: generalised intermediate epidermolysis bullosa simplex (EBS-gen intermed), formerly known as epidermolysis bullosa simplex, Kbner type (EBS-K) is an autosomal dominant basal subtype of epidermolysis bullosa simplex characterised by non-herpetiform blisters and erosions arising in particular at sites of friction.
synonyms: EBS - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex] non-Dowling-Meara | EBS, gen-nDM - [generalised epidermolysis bullosa simplex, non-Dowling-Meara] | EBS-gen intermed

name: Glossoplegia
definition: A paralysis of the tongue that can be either unilateral or bilateral

name: Cutaneous microvascular disturbances due to cryofibrinogens
Late syphilitic bursitis, tibia
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, tibia

name: Gingival fibromatosis - hypertrichosis
definition: Gingival fibromatosis - hypertrichosis is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterised by generalised enlargement of the gingivae occurring at birth or during childhood that is associated with generalised hypertrichosis developing at birth, during the first years of life, or even at puberty and prominently affecting the face, upper limbs, and midback.
synonyms: Gingival fibromatosis-hypertrichosis syndrome

name: Cystic hygroma in fetus without hydrops
Streptococcal infection of skin
synonyms: streptococcal skin infected

name: Periorificial dermatitis
definition: Periorificial dermatitis is a term which links two erythematous and papulopustular facial dermatoses that are strongly linked to prolonged potent topical corticosteroid use, namely perioral dermatitis and periocular dermatitis. It is characterised by the development of erythema, papules and pustules in perioral and periocular skin.
synonyms: perioral dermatitis

name: Phakomatosis pigmentovascularis
synonyms: PPV

name: Hepatoerythropoietic porphyria
definition: Hepatoerythropioetic porphyria (HEP) is a very rare form of chronic hepatic porphyria characterised by bullous photodermatitis.
synonyms: HEP - [Hepatoerythropoietic porphyria] | Homozygous porphyria cutanea tarda | Homozygous uroporphyrinogen decarboxylase deficiency | HEP | porphyria cutanea tarda type 3

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, dysfibronectinaemic type
definition: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, fibronectinemic type is a type of Ehlers-Danlos syndromes (EDS), a heterogeneous group of inherited connective tissue disorders characterised by variable joint hypermobility and cutaneous hyperextensibility and is distinguished by platelet dysfunction associated with a fibronectin abnormality.
synonyms: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type 10 | Ehlers-Danlos syndrome with platelet dysfunction from fibronectin abnormality | Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, fibronectin-deficient

name: Zoster scleritis
synonyms: Herpes zoster scleritis | Scleritis due to Herpes zoster

name: Crural vein thrombosis
definition: Venous thrombosis within the crural veins of the lower leg.
synonyms: Calf vein thrombosis | Deep vein thrombosis of lower leg

name: Apocrine sweat disorders
definition: Disorders in which apocrine secretion is abnormal or obstructed.

name: Loose anagen syndrome
definition: Loose anagen is an inherited syndrome affecting blond or dark blond subjects with blue eyes, predominantly females, in who hair can easily be pulled out, causing no pain, but grows back quickly.
synonyms: Loose anagen hair syndrome

name: Pagetoid reticulosis
definition: Pagetoid reticulosis is an indolent cutaneous T-cell lymphoma, most often presenting with a solitary hyperkeratotic acral lesion. It is characterised histologically by a marked pagetoid infiltration of the epidermis. In contrast to classical mycosis fungoides, a greater proportion of cases show CD8+ neoplastic T-cells.
synonyms: Woringer-Kolopp disease | unilesional mycosis fungoides | localized pagetoid reticulosis | WKD | woringer-kolopp disease

name: Irritant contact dermatitis due to solvents
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis caused by skin contact with solvents such as tetrachloroethylene, toluene, turpentine, acetone, methyl acetate, ethyl acetate, hexane, citrus terpenes or ethanol. These have numerous uses including in dry-cleaning chemicals, paint thinners, nail polish removers, glue solvents and perfumes. Occupations where there are substantial risks include painters and decorators, construction workers, dry-cleaners, machinists and workers in the chemical industry.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema due to solvents
cn_name: 溶剂类引起的刺激性接触性皮炎

name: Tricho-retino-dento-digital syndrome
definition: Tricho-retino-dento-digital syndrome is type of ectodermal dysplasia characterised by congenital hypotrichosis and uncombable hair, associated with early-onset cataract, retinal pigmentary dystrophy, brachymetacarpy, and anomalies of dental development that can be represented by either oligodontia or supernumerary teeth.

name: Chronic erythematous skin disorder
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature in which widespread confluent erythema is the predominant feature. Examples include generalised atopic eczema, erythrodermic psoriasis, pityriasis rubra pilaris and Sézary syndrome.

name: Hypoalphalipoproteinaemia
definition: A disorder characterised by low levels of high-density lipoprotein in the blood.

name: Anthozoa coral injury
definition: Physical injury to the skin as a result of grazing against true coral. A foreign body granulomatous reaction may ensue.

name: Acquired dermal melanocytosis
definition: Acquired dermal melanocytosis is a pigmentary disorder which typically presents in young adult women of Asian origin as bilateral pigmented macules on the face (Hori naevi). They resemble naevus of Ota but do not involve mucosal surfaces. Oestrogens and exposure to UV have been implicated. The condition may however affect other sites such as the hands and lower extremities.

name: Relapsing polychondritis
definition: Relapsing polychondritis is a multisystem inflammatory disease of unknown aetiology affecting the cartilage. The disease is characterised by intermittent or fluctuant inflammatory manifestations due to inflammation of the cartilaginous structures, resulting in tissue damage and tissue destruction. Chondritis of auricular, nasal, tracheal cartilage predominates in this disease, suggesting response to tissue-specific antigens such as collagen II and cartilage matrix protein (matrillin-1). In about
synonyms: atrophic polychondritis | systemic chondromalacia

name: Burn of perineum or genitalia, full thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of perineum and genitalia, third degree
cn_name: 会阴或生殖器全层皮肤烧伤

name: Epiblepharon
definition: This is characterised by a congenital horizontal fold of skin near the margin of the upper or lower eyelid caused by the abnormal insertion of muscle fibres. This extra fold of skin redirects the lashes into a vertical position, where they may contact the globe.

name: Keratolysis exfoliativa
definition: Keratolysis exfoliativa is a common, asymptomatic, superficial desquamative process of palmar and (less often) plantar skin; it usually worsens in warm weather and is occasionally associated with hyperhidrosis. Keratolysis exfoliativa is seen in healthy adults and probably reflects low-grade physical or chemical damage to the thick stratum corneum of volar skin.
synonyms: dyshidrosis lamellosa sicca | lamellar dyshidrosis | recurrent focal palmar peeling

name: Hereditary koilonychia
synonyms: NDNC2 - [nail disorder, nonsyndromic, congenital 2] (MIM 149300) | Spoon nails (inherited) | congenital koilonychia

name: Squamous cell carcinoma of leg
synonyms: Epidermoid carcinoma of leg

name: Neonatal pseudomonas skin infection
definition: Skin infection of the newborn by Pseudomonas aeruginosa.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 2 of lower back
Skin problems resulting from tracheostomy
definition: An entity to be used to link a specific skin problem (e.g. irritant contact dermatitis) to its proximity to a tracheostomy.

name: Oral pemphigus
definition: Oral pemphigus is a variant of pemphigus vulgaris, and is a chronic autoimmune skin disease characterised by blister formation on the oral mucous membrane mediated by auto-antibodies to the desmosome component desmoglein 3.

name: Fish sting
definition: Sting from any of a large number of species of fish with venom glands attached to dorsal or caudal spines. Well recognised venomous fish include stingrays (Chondrichthyes), catfish and scorpionfish (Osteichthyes) and the spiny dogfish (Squalus acanthias). Injuries commonly occur on the feet or legs as a result of stepping on the fish and present as painful lacerations or puncture wounds.

name: Parastomal ulcer
definition: Ulceration of the skin adjacent to a stoma which is either attributable to a cause other than drugs or pyoderma gangrenosum or, alternatively, where the aetiology is uncertain or unspecified.

name: Anetoderma
definition: A condition presenting as focal areas of thinned, flaccid skin and resulting from focal defects in dermal elastin. The involved skin is often elevated above the surrounding normal skin but can be depressed. The condition may be primary and without identifiable cause or may be a sequela of a large number of different conditions which may damage elastin in the dermis.
synonyms: Macular atrophy | anetoderma maculosa | anetoderma maculosa cutis | atrophia maculosa cutis | macular atrophy

name: Non-involuting congenital haemangioma
definition: Non-involuting congenital hemangiomas (NICH) are a distinctive type of large congenital hemangioma that are fully formed in utero and differ from rapidly involuting congenital hemangiomas (RICH; see this term) mainly because they do not undergo a postnatal involuting phase.
synonyms: NICH - [non-involuting congenital haemangioma]

name: Oesophageal varices without bleeding
synonyms: ulcerated oesophageal varicose vein | ulcerated oesophageal varicose venous | ulcerated oesophageal varix | varicose oesophageal ulcer
cn_name: 食管静脉曲张不伴出血

name: Papillary hyperplasia of oral mucosa
definition: This is an oral pathologic condition that appears in the mouth as an overgrowth of tissue usually beneath a denture and is associated with poor denture hygiene, denture overuse, and ill-fitting dentures.

name: Disseminated dermatophytosis
definition: Extensive and invasive dermatophyte infection due either to a specific genetic anergy to dermatophytes or to profound immunosuppression. Dermal nodules, abscesses or draining sinuses may occur; rarely bone, central nervous system and lymph nodes may be involved.

name: Symptomatic late syphilis of other sites
cn_name: 晚期其他部位梅毒，有症状

name: Irritant contact dermatitis due to topical medicaments or antimicrobials
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis caused by repetitive or prolonged skin contact with topical medicaments and antimicrobials containing e.g. benzoyl peroxide, hydrogen peroxide, povidone iodine, formaldehyde, salicylic acid, alcohols, 5-fluorouracil, dithranol, chlorhexidine, quaternary ammonium compounds or tretinoin. Their irritancy is usually mild and sometimes part of their therapeutic action.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema due to topical medicaments or antimicrobials
cn_name: 局部药物或抗菌剂刺激性接触性皮炎

name: Epidermal naevus syndrome
definition: Epidermal naevus syndrome is the term used to label a heterogeneous range of developmental disorders in which hamartomatous malformations of the epidermis and its appendages are associated with other abnormalities of the integument or of other body systems.

name: Bockenheimer syndrome
definition: A rare developmental anomaly characterised by abnormally enlarged and tortuous veins (phlebectasia), often in a segmental distribution.
synonyms: Congenital diffuse phlebectasia

name: Corticosteroid-induced rosacea
definition: A rosacea-like facial dermatosis attributable to the use of potent topical corticosteroid preparations.
synonyms: Corticosteroid-induced rosacea-like facial dermatosis

name: Anogenital molluscum contagiosum
definition: Molluscum contagiosum affecting the anogenital area. Transmission may be by sexual contact.

name: Speckled leukoplakia
definition: This is a condition where patches of keratosis appear as adherent white patches on the mucous membranes of the oral cavity, including the tongue, but also other areas of the gastro-intestinal tract, urinary tract and the genitals.

name: Neonatal alloimmune thrombocytopenic purpura
definition: Thrombocytopenic purpura in the newborn resulting from trans-placental transfer of platelet antibodies.

name: Skin pigmentation due to transfusional haemosiderosis
definition: generalised skin pigmentation resulting from repeated blood transfusion as may be required for severe haemoglobinopathies.

name: Acute urticarial skin eruption
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists of urticaria-like papules and plaques. Drugs are a common precipitant.
synonyms: Acute urticarial dermatitis

name: Extragenital lichen sclerosus with morphoea
definition: A disorder in which clinically and histopathologically typical lesions of lichen sclerosus and morphoea co-exist.

name: Minor recurrent aphthous stomatitis
definition: This is a minor recurrent type of mouth ulcer that presents as a painful open sore inside the mouth or upper throat characterised by a break in the mucous membrane.
synonyms: minor aphthous stomatitis

name: Miscellaneous conditions affecting dermal connective tissue
Pressure ulceration grade 2 of lower limb
synonyms: stage 2 decubitus ulcer of other sites of lower limb

name: Postpartum telogen effluvium
definition: A physiological phenomenon in which diffuse hair loss occurs two to three months following parturition. It is due to a postponement of the normal cyclical conversion of anagen hairs to telogen during pregnancy. After parturition a large number of anagen hairs are converted simultaneously to telogen and shed two to three months later. Normal hair cycling is then resumed.

name: Pigmented skin lesion of uncertain nature, lower limb
definition: This denotes the presence of a pigmented skin lesion on a lower limb but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Microcystic lymphatic malformation
definition: Microcystic lymphatic malformations consist of clusters of dilated lymphatic vessels which have developed without connection to the systemic lymphatic circulation. They present with grouped clear or haemorrhagic vesicles anywhere on the skin or mucous membrane.
synonyms: Capillary lymphangioma | Capillary lymphatic malformation | Lymphangioma circumscriptum | Microcystic infiltrating lymphatic malformation | Microcystic lymphangioma | Superficial lymphangioma | lymphangioma circumscriptum

name: Stern-Lubinsky-Durrie syndrome
definition: Stern-Lubinsky-Durrie syndrome is characterised by corneal epithelial changes (associated with photophobia and burning and watering of the eyes), diffuse palmoplantar hyperkeratosis, distal onycholysis, brachydactyly, short stature, dental problems, and premature birth.

name: Irritant contact dermatitis of face or neck
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis affecting skin of face or neck. This is frequently provoked by cosmetics.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema of face or neck

name: DNA instability syndromes affecting the skin
definition: A range of disorders characterised by DNA damage and defective DNA repair. Clinical features of relevance to the skin and external appearance include dysmorphic features, photosensitivity and an increased tendency to skin cancer.

name: Autoinflammatory granulomatosis of childhood
definition: Granulomatous arthritis of childhood designates a chronic inflammatory disease characterised by granulomatous polyarthritis, uveitis and rash with a typical onset before 5 years. It can present in a familial form, called Blau Syndrome (BS), and a sporadic from, known as early onset sarcoidosis.
synonyms: Early onset autoinflammatory granulomatosis | Granulomatous arthritis of childhood

name: Retention hyperkeratosis
definition: A skin condition which may be associated with psychological disturbance or deficient personal hygiene in which normal exfoliation from the stratum corneum does not occur. This can lead to localised or widespread accumulation of keratinous debris on the skin surface.
synonyms: Dermatitis neglecta

name: Oblique facial cleft
definition: Cleft extending from angles of mouth to outer canthi of eyes following no line of embryonic fusion of facial processes
synonyms: Orbitofacial cleft

name: Neonatal micronutrient deficiency affecting the skin
Familial multiple keratoacanthomas
definition: Ferguson Smith multiple keratoacanthoma is a form of familial keratoacanthoma, a benign epithelial tumour of pilosebaceous origin, which is composed of keratinizing squamous cells. The condition is characterised by rapid evolution and, most often, by spontaneous resolution.
synonyms: Ferguson-Smith multiple keratoacanthomas | Multiple self-healing squamous epitheliomas of Ferguson-Smith

name: Cutaneous reactions to arthropods
definition: Cutaneous reactions to bites, stings and other noxious insults from arthropods.

name: Metastatic malignant neoplasm involving skin
definition: Involvement of the skin by metastatic spread from a known or unknown primary malignant neoplasm. The secondary deposit may result from local migration of malignant cells, or from regional lymphatic or haematogenous spread from more distant sites.
synonyms: cutaneous metastasis | metastasis to skin, any site | secondary malignant neoplasm in skin | skin metastasis | skin secondaries | subcutaneous metastases
cn_name: 累及皮肤的转移性恶性肿瘤

name: Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to chromate
Acute febrile neutrophilic dermatosis, drug-induced
definition: Acute febrile neutrophilic dermatosis (Sweet disease) attributable to drug. The most commonly reported agent is granulocyte colony-stimulating factor, usually in patients with underlying haematological malignancy, but other reported agents include minocycline and hydralazine. Peripheral blood neutrophilia is not always present.
synonyms: Sweet syndrome due to drug

name: Flushing
definition: Paroxysmal vasodilatation of skin capillaries.

name: Acanthosis nigricans associated with Type II diabetes and obesity
Superficial thrombophlebitis resulting from infusion or injection of drug
Primary cutaneous CD4-positive small or intermediate pleomorphic T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder
definition: This reactive lymphoproliferative disorder usually presents with a solitary plaque or tumour on the face, neck, or upper trunk. Morphologically, it is composed of small to medium-sized CD4-positive, CD8-negative, and CD30-negative pleomorphic T-lymphocytes. A small number of large pleomorphic T-lymphocytes may also be present. The lymphocytic infiltrate is dermal. Focal epidermotropism and subcutaneous involvement may be present. It has a favourable prognosis.
synonyms: primary cutaneous CD4+ small or intermediate pleomorphic T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder

name: Complications of cutaneous cosmetic procedures
synonyms: Postprocedural complications of cosmetic procedures
cn_name: 皮肤美容操作的并发症

name: Yellow nail syndrome
definition: Yellow nail syndrome is characterised by yellow, dystrophic, thick and slowly growing nails, associated with lymphoedema and respiratory involvement. Less than 100 cases have been described. Lymphoedema occurs more often in the lower limbs. It can appear at birth or later in life. Onset generally follows the onset of ungual abnormalities. Patients usually suffer from chronic bronchitis and in some cases, from chronic sinusitis, bronchiectasia and recurring pneumonitis. They can also present with
synonyms: Hereditary lymphoedema and yellow nails

name: Noninfectious dermatoses of eyelid
synonyms: dermatitis of eyelid | dermatosis of eyelid | eyelid dermatitis NOS | non-infectious dermatosis of eyelid | noninfectious dermatosis of eyelid | Non-infected eyelid dermatoses

name: X-linked recessive ocular albinism
definition: X-linked recessive ocular albinism is a form of ocular albinism, a group of X-linked or autosomal genetic disorders characterised by partial or total lack of melanin pigmentation in the eyes. Eyes may be severely affected with photophobia and reduced visual acuity. Nystagmus or strabismus are often associated. The irides and fundus are depigmented.
synonyms: OA1 - [Ocular albinism type 1] | Ocular albinism type 1 | Ocular albinism, Nettleship-Falls type | OA1

name: Legius syndrome
definition: Legius syndrome, also known as neurofibromatosis type 1-like syndrome, is a very rare, genetic, skin pigmentation disorder characterised by multiple café-au-lait macules with or without axillary or inguinal freckling.
synonyms: NF1-like syndrome | NFLS - [Neurofibromatosis 1-like syndrome] (MIM 611431) | Neurofibromatosis 1-like syndrome | neurofibromatosis 1-like syndrome

name: Serious adverse reaction to injection of filler
definition: Any adverse event with potentially life-threatening and permanent sequelae, especially arterial occlusion or embolism, which can rarely lead to permanent loss of vision. Details of the adverse reaction should be coded separately.

name: Skin infection classified elsewhere due to methicillin resistant Staphylococcus aureus
synonyms: Skin infection classified elsewhere due to MRSA

name: Giant congenital melanocytic naevus
definition: A congenital melanocytic naevus (CMN) with a predicted or final adult maximal diameter of 400 mm or more. Giant CMNs are commonly centred on the dorsal surface of the body between the vertex and the buttocks but may occur elsewhere; they may be associated with multiple smaller satellite naevi (congenital or tardive), hypertrichosis, lipomas or benign proliferative nodules. There is a risk of pre-pubertal melanoma within giant CMN or the central nervous system (CNS). Leptomeningeal melanocytosis
synonyms: Bathing-trunk naevus | Congenital giant pigmented naevus | Garment naevus
cn_name: 巨大先天性黑素细胞痣

name: Cutaneous larva migrans
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the parasitic worm larvae, commonly Ancylostoma braziliense, A. caninum, or Uncinaria stenocephala. This disease is characterised by intense pruritus and erythematous, serpiginous lesions due to migration of parasitic larvae in the upper dermis where the larvae penetrate the skin. Transmission is by direct contact with larvae from soil or sand contaminated with dog or cat faeces (by percutaneous migration of larvae).
synonyms: Creeping eruption | Ground itch | Sand-worm eruption | ancylostomal cutaneous larva migrans | clm - [cutaneous larva migrans] | dew itch | duckhunters itch | hookworm cutaneous vesicle | larva migrans of skin | plumber's itch

name: Hypertrichosis of eyelid
definition: This refers to an abnormal amount of hair growth over the eyelid; extensive cases of hypertrichosis have informally been called werewolf syndrome, because the appearance is similar to the werewolf. Hypertrichosis can be either congenital (present at birth) or acquired later in life.

name: Anogenital warts
definition: Anogenital warts are due to an infection of anogenital skin and mucous membranes by certain human papilloma viruses, most commonly HPV subtypes 6, 11, 16 and 18. Transmission is predominantly by sexual contact. They manifest typically as flat plaques or papillomatous, keratinous growths on and adjacent to the external genitalia and anus. Some HPV subtypes, including types 16 and 18, are oncogenic and predispose to the development of anogenital cancers.
synonyms: Condylomata acuminata | anogenital warts NOS | venereal warts | verrucae anogenitales | anogenital warts

name: Congenital entropion, unilateral
definition: This is an inversion of the edge of an eyelid that occurs only in one eye and is present since birth, resulting in irritation of the cornea.

name: Late syphilitic paresis
synonyms: neurosyphilis paresis | paretic neurosyphilis | syphilitic paraesthesia | syphilitic paralysis | syphilitic paresis

name: Dermatitis or eczema of eyelids
definition: Eczematous blepharitis and contact dermatitis affecting the eyelids.

name: Sequelae of superficial frostbite of wrist or hand
Purpura or bruising due to vascular fragility
definition: Purpura due to leakage or rupture of abnormally fragile cutaneous blood vessels.
cn_name: 因血管脆性增加而产生的紫癜或瘀伤

name: Intracartilaginous pseudocyst of the auricle
definition: This is a fluid-filled false cyst which develops within the cartilage of the external ear. It is thought usually to result from trauma.
synonyms: benign idiopathic cystic chondromalacia of the pinna | endochondral pseudocyst of the ear | pseudocyst of the auricle | cystic chondromalachia | endochondral pseudocyst | intracartilaginous cyst

name: Erythema migrans
definition: The cutaneous eruption which is the presenting manifestation in the majority of cases of Lyme borreliosis. It is characterised by an expanding annular erythematous plaque at the site of inoculation of Borrelia burgdorferi spirochaetes by infected Ixodes ticks. It is initially singular but may be followed by multiple expanding plaques.
synonyms: Erythema chronicum migrans | erythema chronicum migrans

name: Benign cutaneous neoplasms with sebaceous differentiation
synonyms: Sebaceous adenoma | sebaceous epithelioma | adenoma of the Sebaceous gland | skin appendage sebaceous adenoma

name: Post traumatic hypermelanosis
definition: Increased skin pigmentation developing as a consequence of traumatic injury involving the skin and seen particularly in individuals with darker skin types.
synonyms: Post traumatic hyperpigmentation

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to systemic medicaments
Neonatal staphylococcal periporitis
definition: A contagious pustular eruption in neonates caused by Staphylococcus aureus. Although it may resemble folliculitis, the infection is centred on the sweat pore and usually arises on a background of neonatal miliaria.
synonyms: neonatal periporitis staphylogenes

name: Pressure ulceration grade 3 of ischium
synonyms: stage 3 decubitus ulcer of ischium

name: Palpebral cleft or coloboma, unilateral
definition: This is a hole in one of the structures of the eye, such as the iris, retina, choroid or optic disc. The hole is present from birth and can be caused when a gap called the choroid fissure, which is present during early stages of prenatal development, fails to close up completely before a child is born. This diagnosis is occurring in one eye (unilateral).

name: Soft palate ulcer
synonyms: Ulcer of palate

name: Solitary varices of the oesophagus
definition: This refers to abnormally solitary dilated veins in the lining of oesophagus without underlying portal hypertension, superior vena cava or azygos venous obstruction.

name: Pyoderma gangrenosum
definition: An idiopathic, rapidly evolving, and severely debilitating disease occurring most commonly in association with chronic ulcerative colitis. It is characterised by the presence of boggy, purplish ulcers with undermined borders, appearing mostly on the legs. The majority of cases are in people between 40 and 60 years old. Its etiology is unknown.
synonyms: Phagedenic pyoderma | phagedena geometric | PG

name: Familial cold autoinflammatory syndrome
definition: Familial cold autoinflammatory syndrome (also known as Familial cold urticaria) is an autosomal dominant autoinflammatory syndrome characterised by episodes of rash, fever, and joint pain following generalised exposure to cold.
synonyms: Familial cold urticaria | familial cold autoinflammatory syndrome | FCAS

name: Certain dermatoses with suspected viral aetiology
definition: Skin disorders for which there are indications but no proof that viral infection is responsible.
cn_name: 某些疑似病毒病因的皮肤病

name: Lymphatic malformation of large intestine
synonyms: Lymphangioma of large intestine

name: Exercise-induced anaphylaxis
definition: Exercise-induced anaphylaxis is disorder in which anaphylaxis occurs after physical activity. The clinical features may include pruritus, urticarial weals, flushing, wheezing, and gastrointestinal disturbance including nausea, abdominal cramping, and diarrhoea. If physical activity continues, angioedema, laryngeal oedema, hypotension, and, ultimately, cardiovascular collapse may occur. Exercise-induced anaphylaxis is most commonly associated with IgE-mediated allergy to food whereby anaphylaxis
synonyms: EIA

name: Rubella without complication
definition: Rubella was a common childhood viral infection until the advent of mass immunization programmes. It is characterised by a short-lived maculopapular exanthem, lymphadenopathy and mild fever\: the majority of infections are not associated with significant morbidity. Transmission is by inhalation of infected respiratory secretions or by direct contact. Confirmation is by identification of rubella virus in nasal swab, throat swab or blood samples, or by detection of rubella virus specific IgM antibo
synonyms: German measles | Rubella NOS | german measles

name: Traumatic ulcerative granuloma with stromal eosinophilia
definition: This is a chronic, benign, self-limiting lesion of the oral mucosa, manifesting as an ulcer with elevated margins.
synonyms: Eosinophilic granuloma of oral mucosa | TUGSE - [Traumatic ulcerative granuloma with stromal eosinophilia]

name: Psychological disturbance resulting in falsely perceived or actual skin damage or disorder in others
definition: Conditions in which psychological factors lead to one person (usually a mother) holding a false image of or inflicting actual skin injury on another (usually her child).

name: Familial cutaneous collagenoma
definition: Familial cutaneous collagenoma (MIM 115250) is a rare autosomal dominant disorder in which multiple asymptomatic hamartomatous dermal collagenous nodules are found, typically with onset after puberty and symmetrically distributed over the upper trunk and mid back. It has been linked to mutations in the LEMD3 gene and is phenotypically similar to Buschke-Ollendorff syndrome though without evidence of bone changes (osteopoikilosis).

name: Nodular amyloidosis
definition: A rare form of cutaneous amyloidosis characterised by single or multiple waxy nodules or infiltrated plaques on the trunk or limbs. It is due to localised deposition of lambda light chains thought to be produced by plasma cells in the skin. A minority of patients have a detectable circulating paraprotein band. There is a small risk of progression to primary systemic amyloidosis.
synonyms: Amyloidosis cutis nodularis atrophicans | Tumefactive amyloidosis

name: Hypertrophic lichen planus
definition: A chronic recalcitrant form of lichen planus often localised to the lower legs and ankles and characterised by plaques of markedly thickened skin. It is often extremely pruritic. It can leave permanent pigmentation and scarring.
synonyms: Lichen planus hypertrophicus | lichen planus verrucosus

name: Genetically-determined mixed hyper- and hypomelanotic disorders of skin pigmentation
definition: Hereditary disorders characterised by areas both of increased and of decreased skin pigmentation.

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy with spastic paraplegia
definition: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy with spastic paraplegia is characterised by the association of an axonal sensory and autonomic neuropathy with spastic paraplegia.

name: Professional tattoo
definition: Tattoos created by professional tattooists who are generally subject to strict regulation to ensure safety.

name: Adult atopic eczema, generalised erythematous
definition: Acute or subacute generalised inflammatory atopic eczema in adulthood (>18 years).
synonyms: Adult atopic dermatitis, generalised erythematous | Erythrodermic atopic eczema of the adult

name: Vulvovaginal gingival syndrome
definition: The vulvovaginal gingival syndrome is an uncommon and severe variant of lichen planus characterised by erosions or desquamation of vulval, vaginal, and gingival mucosae with a predilection for scarring and stricture formation.

name: Burn of chest wall, epidermal burn
synonyms: Erythema of chest wall

name: Recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, pretibial
definition: This recessively inherited dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa (RDEB-pt) is largely confined to the shins.
synonyms: RDEB - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa] pretibial | RDEB-Pt - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, pretibial]

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to chromate
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to chromium salts\: chromates are used for their anti-corrosive properties and are found in a wide range of products including paints and coolant fluids as well as in tanned leather and cement.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to chromate

name: Trichotemnomania
definition: Self-inflicted alopecia due to an obsessive-compulsive habit of cutting or shaving hair from scalp and other body parts.

name: Cutaneous neuroendocrine carcinoma
definition: Cutaneous neuroendocrine carcinoma is a primary cutaneous cancer arising from a subset of skin neuroendocrine cells (Merkel cells, giving the name Merkel cell carcinoma (MCC)).

name: Scalp folliculitis
definition: A non-scarring chronic superficial folliculitis of the scalp that is typically characterised by multiple minute, very itchy pustules within the scalp and which has in the past been termed acne necrotica miliaris. The cause is not well understood but an inflammatory response to Propionibacterium acnes has been postulated.

name: Cellulitis or abscess of soft tissues of the mouth
definition: Infection of the soft tissues of the mouth resulting in cellulitis and/or abscess formation.
cn_name: 口腔软组织的蜂窝组织炎或脓肿

name: Deafness – onychodystrophy
synonyms: Deafness – onychodystrophy – osteodystrophy – intellectual deficit

name: Oral-facial-digital syndrome type 10
definition: Oral-facial-digital syndrome, type 10 is a malformation syndrome characterised by facial (telecanthus, flat nasal bridge, retrognathia), oral (cleft palate, vestibular frenula) and digital (oligodactyly, preaxial polydactyly) features, associated with remarkable radial shortening, fibular agenesis and coalescence of tarsal bones.

name: Subcutaneous swelling, mass or lump, neck
definition: One or more subcutaneous soft tissue masses of undetermined or unspecified nature localised to the neck
synonyms: localised mass of neck | localised swelling of neck | localised swelling, mass and lump, neck | mass of neck | neck mass | neck swelling

name: Tufted angioma
definition: Tufted angioma is a vascular tumour that may be either congenital or acquired (appearing before the age of 5 years) with slow angiomatous proliferation.
synonyms: acquired tufted angioma | angioblastoma of nakagawa | hypertrophic hemangioma | progressive capillary hemangioma | tufted hemangioma

name: Chilblains
definition: Chilblains are the result of cold-induced damage principally to the microvasculature of acral skin in susceptible individuals. They are commonest in the winter months in cold, damp climates.
