They present as itchy or painful red-purple macules, papules or plaques, most commonly affecting the fingers and toes, though other body extremities including the heels, nose and ears can be involved. Because of insulation from core body temperature, other areas, especially the lateral thighs, may be involve
synonyms: Pernio | Perniosis | chilblains | kibes | perinosis

name: Burn of shoulder or arm, except wrist or hand, superficial partial thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of shoulder and arm, except wrist and hand, superficial second degree
cn_name: 肩或臂（除外腕或手）表皮烧伤

name: Hereditary symmetrical dyschromatosis
definition: Hereditary symmetrical dyschromatosis is an autosomal recessive pigmentary disorder characterised by hypo- and hyperpigmented macules affecting predominantly the dorsal surfaces of the hands and feet. On the face the lesions resemble ephelides and no hypopigmentation appears. The condition is commoner in individuals of oriental origin. It develops during infancy and early childhood and persists into adulthood.
synonyms: Reticulate acropigmentation of Dohi | reticulate acropigmentation of Dohi

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to preservatives or biocides
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to exposure to preservatives and biocides such as parabens, isothiazolinones, formaldehyde, formaldehyde releasers and phenoxyethanol.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to preservatives or biocides
cn_name: 防腐剂或杀菌剂引起的变应性接触性皮炎

name: Cutaneous and subcutaneous melioidosis
definition: A subacute or chronic infection of skin and subcutaneous tissue by the saprophytic bacterium Burkholderia pseudomallei. It manifests as one or more skin abscesses with associated lymphangitis and lymphadenitis.
synonyms: Skin abscess due to Melioidosis

name: Balanoposthitis due to infection
synonyms: Balanitis due to infection

name: Eczema herpeticum
definition: Eczema herpeticum is due to the uncontrolled dissemination over the skin of herpes simplex virus infection in individuals with a background of atopic eczema. It is characterised by the rapid spread of numerous umbilicated vesiculopustules over the skin. These soon break down to form painful punctuate erosions. They may start in areas of active eczema but may also develop in areas which have little obvious preceding eczematous inflammation. Although frequently seen in children, it may also occur
synonyms: Kaposi varicelliform eruption due to Herpes simplex superinfection of atopic eczema | kaposi's varicelliform eruption | herpes simplex dermatitis | Herpes simplex dermatitis of eyelid | Herpes simplex eyelid dermatitis | Herpes simplex virus dermatitis | herpes simplex virus eyelid dermatitis

name: Pressure ulceration grade 2
definition: Pressure injury with partial thickness loss of dermis. It presents as a shallow open ulcer with a red or pink wound bed without slough or as a serum-filled or serosanguinous blister which may rupture.   This category should not be used to describe skin tears, tape burns, incontinence associated dermatitis, maceration or excoriation
synonyms: bedsore stage II | decubitus sore [pressure sore] with partial thickness skin loss involving epidermis or epidermis and dermis | pressure injury stage 2 | pressure injury stage 2 with partial thickness skin loss | pressure sore with superficial ulceration | pressure ulcer category 2 | pressure ulcer with partial thickness skin loss | stage 2 decubitus ulcer | stage 2 pressure ulcer | stage II pressure injury

name: Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome
definition: Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome is a developmental anomalies syndrome characterised by congenital anomalies (microcephaly, specific facial characteristics, broad thumbs and halluces and postnatal growth retardation), intellectual deficit and behavioural troubles.
synonyms: Broad thumb syndrome | Broad thumb-hallux syndrome | RSTS - [Rubinstein-Taybi syndrome]

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, progeroid type
definition: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, progeroid type is a form of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome characterised by a premature aging with sparse hair, macrocephaly, loose elastic skin, failure to thrive, joint laxity, psychomotor retardation, hypotonia, and defective wound healing with atrophic scars.
synonyms: Defective biosynthesis of proteodermatan sulphate | Galactosyltransferase I deficiency | Xylosylprotein 4-beta-galactosyltransferase deficiency

name: Angioedema due to disordered complement activation or kinin metabolism
definition: Angioedema resulting from disturbances in kinin metabolism due to low absolute or functional levels of C1 inhibitor, increased kinin production or inhibition of kinin breakdown.

name: Hypomyopathic dermatomyositis
definition: Hypomyopathic dermatomyositis is a rare subset of dermatomyositis characterised by a lack of subjective symptoms but with subclinical evidence of muscle inflammation on muscle biopsy, electromyography, or other procedures.

name: Acneiform drug eruption
definition: Acneiform drug eruptions resemble papulopustular but can be attributed to administration of a drug. They can be distinguished from acne vulgaris by an absence of comedones in most cases, a history of sudden onset following administration of a well-known precipitant, monotonous lesion morphology, onset at an age outside the range typical of acne vulgaris, and resolution with discontinuation of the medication. Systemic corticosteroids, anabolic steroids (usually taken for body-building), androgens
synonyms: Drug-induced acne | acne medicamentosa

name: Diffuse cutaneous mastocytosis
definition: Diffuse cutaneous mastocytosis is a rare form of cutaneous mastocytosis in which mast cells infiltrate the entire skin diffusely. It usually presents in the neonatal period and is characterised by erythroderma, a doughy ''peau d'orange'' appearance and blistering after minor trauma. Affected infants are at risk of systemic disease and severe complications including anaphylaxis and diarrhoea. The condition may resolve spontaneously.

name: Angiokeratoma of Mibelli
definition: Angiokeratoma of Mibelli is an uncommon disorder which typically presents between the ages of 10 and 15 years with the development of angiokeratomata on the dorsal and lateral aspects of fingers and toes; it may also involve the dorsal surfaces of the hands and feet. It is commonly associated with acrocyanosis and chilblains, suggesting that cold injury is important in the aetiology. It is inherited as an autosomal dominant trait with highly variable penetrance, affecting girls more than boys.

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 2
definition: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 2 (MPS 2) is a lysosomal storage disease belonging to the group of mucopolysaccharidoses. The clinical picture ranges from severe (the most frequent form) with early psychomotor regression, facial dysmorphism (macroglossia, constantly opened mouth, coarse features), hepatosplenomegaly, limited joint motion, carpal tunnel syndrome, dysostosis multiplex, small size, behavioural disorders and psychomotor regression leading to intellectual deficit, deafness, cardiac and re
synonyms: Hunter syndrome | Iduronate 2-sulfatase deficiency | MPS2 - [mucopolysaccharidosis 2] | iduronate 2-sulphatase deficiency | iduronate sulfatase deficiency | iduronate sulphatase deficiency | sulfo-iduronate sulfatase deficiency | sulfoiduronidate sulfatase deficiency | sulpho-iduronate sulphatase deficiency | sulphoiduronidate sulphatase deficiency

name: Hereditary disseminated or invasive dermatophytosis
definition: A rare hereditary disorder reported principally from Algeria in which there is an inability to mount a normal immune response to dermatophyte infection. It presents early in life with extensive infection of scalp and/or glabrous skin before proceeding to invade the dermis, lymph nodes and viscera. It is potentially fatal.

name: Infantile papular acrodermatitis
definition: Infantile papular acrodermatitis (Gianotti-Crosti syndrome) is a cutaneous reaction pattern to a range of infective agents affecting predominantly young children aged from six months to two years. Agents implicated include hepatitis B virus, Epstein-Barr virus and a number of enteroviruses. The rash consists in a profuse eruption of 5-10 mm diameter dull red flat-topped papules which appear first on the thighs and buttocks, then on the extensor aspects of the arms, and finally on the face. There
synonyms: Acrodermatitis papulosa infantum | Gianotti-Crosti syndrome | Papular acrodermatitis of childhood | papular acrodermatitis of childhood | papulovesicular acrolocated syndrome

name: Björnstad syndrome
definition: Björnstad syndrome is a genetic syndrome characterised by congenital sensorineural hearing loss and pili torti.
synonyms: Crandall syndrome | Deafness - pili torti - hypogonadism

name: Fibrous hamartoma of infancy
synonyms: subdermal fibromatous tumor in infancy

name: Drug-induced palmoplantar pustulosis
definition: Palmoplantar pustulosis precipitated by drugs, in particular TNF-alpha blocking biological drugs used in the treatment of Crohn disease.

name: Urticarial vasculitis
definition: An uncommon form of cutaneous leucocytoclastic vasculitis manifested by urticarial weals which, in contrast to those of chronic urticaria, are long-lasting and painful rather than itchy. A cause is often not identified. It may be associated with hypocomplementaemia and systemic inflammation (hypocomplementaemic urticarial vasculitis).
synonyms: chronic urticaria as a manifestation of venulitis | urticaria and arthralgia with necrotizing angiitis

name: Neuropathic skin ulceration due to leprosy
Dermatoses associated with specific classes of medication
definition: A heterogeneous group of adverse skin reactions characteristic for each drug or class of drug involved. Cancer chemotherapeutic agents and systemic corticosteroids are two important examples.
cn_name: 与特定类的药物有关的皮肤病

name: Blepharocheilodontic syndrome
definition: Blepharo-cheilo-odontic syndrome is characterised by ectropion of the lower eyelids and cleft lip and palate.

name: Superficial frostbite
definition: Frostbite in which injury is confined to the skin, where there may be epidermal blistering and sloughing but no significant tissue necrosis.
synonyms: First degree frostbite | Frostnip | Second degree frostbite | frostbite with partial-thickness skin loss | frost nip

name: Scarring alopecia due to malignant infiltration
definition: Fibrosis and atrophy of scalp hair follicles attributable to infiltration by local or metastatic malignant cells.
synonyms: Alopecia neoplastica

name: Vitiligo of lower eyelid or periocular area
definition: This is a condition of the lower eyelid and periocular area that causes depigmentation of sections of skin. It occurs when melanocytes, the cells responsible for skin pigmentation, die or are unable to function. The cause of vitiligo is unknown, but research suggests that it may arise from autoimmune, genetic, oxidative stress, neural, or viral causes.

name: Lentigo maligna melanoma, primary
definition: Lentigo maligna malignant melanoma is a form of melanoma which occurs within a lentigo maligna when neoplastic cells no longer remain confined to the epidermis (in situ radial growth) but invade the dermis (vertical growth). The lentigo maligna from which it arises may have been present as an irregularly pigmented macule on sun-exposed skin for many years before dermal invasion supervenes. Clinical differentiation from lentigo maligna may not be possible in the early stages of invasion but as th
cn_name: 原发性恶性雀斑样痣性黑色素瘤

name: Frostbite of fingers with tissue necrosis
Pruritus of unknown cause
definition: Pruritus without identifiable cause despite thorough investigation.
synonyms: Itch of unknown cause | Pruritus sine materia

name: Papulopustular acne
definition: Acne in which the principal manifestation is the presence of multiple small inflammatory papules and pustules.
synonyms: Pustular acne | Superficial inflammatory acne

name: Cutaneous amoebiasis
synonyms: Amoebiasis of skin | Amoebic ulcer of skin

name: Blepharochalasis
definition: This is a malposition of the eyelid caused either by involution or by inflammation of the eyelid. The inflammation is characterised by exacerbations and remissions of eyelid oedema, which results in a stretching and subsequent atrophy of the eyelid tissue resulting in redundant folds over the lid margins. It typically affects only the upper eyelids, and may be unilateral as well as bilateral.
synonyms: Dermatolysis palpebrarum | adiposa ptosis

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, upper arm
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, upper arm

name: Pigmentary abnormalities of lips
definition: This is an abnormality in the material that changes the colour of reflected or transmitted light as the result of wavelength-selective absorption, of the lips.

name: Lumbosacral dermal melanocytosis
definition: One or more areas of poorly circumscribed slate-brown or blue-black macular pigmentation of the lumbosacral area. The ectopic melanocytes are thought to result from incomplete migration of melanocytes from the neural crest to the epidermis during embryogenesis. The incidence ranges from 90% in babies of oriental ethnicity to less than 2% in babies of European ancestry. The pigmentation tends to fade with age.
synonyms: Blue spot | Mongolian spot | Mongolian Macula

name: Crenated tongue
definition: Presence of notching on the border of an enlarged tongue that faces the lingual portion of the teeth

name: Sjögren syndrome vasculitis
definition: Sjögren syndrome vasculitis is one of the extraglandular manifestations in patients with Sjögren disease. Vasculitis affects small and medium-sized vessels and most commonly manifests as purpura, recurrent urticarial, skin ulceration and mononeuritis multiplex.

name: Unspecified frostbite of trunk, not elsewhere classified
Spindle cell melanocytic naevus
definition: A naevus characterised by the presence of spindle-shaped melanocytes.
synonyms: Starburst melanocytic naevus (dermatoscopy)

name: Pemphigoid nodularis
definition: Pemphigoid nodularis is a subtype of pemphigoid with features of both nodular prurigo and bullous pemphigoid. Patients present with pruritic nodules, papules or plaques. Blisters are usually seen at some point during the course of the disease but may also be absent. Confirming the diagnosis is often challenging but the immunopathology is similar to ordinary bullous pemphigoid. Legs are the commonly affected sites but the condition can be widespread and difficult to manage.

name: Mechanical oral ulceration
synonyms: Traumatic mouth ulcer

name: Neonatal staphylococcal mastitis
definition: A staphylococcal pyoderma affecting the breasts of neonates, whose breast tissue may be enlarged as a result of transplacentally transmitted maternal oestrogens. It is twice as common in female as in male infants; most cases are unilateral. The neonatal breast tissue is swollen, warm and erythematous. In severe cases a purulent discharge may be expressed from the nipples.

name: Carcinoma-induced vasculitis
definition: Emergence of malignancy is reported in up to 5% of patients with vasculitis. In patients with malignancy and vasculitis, hematologic malignancies are most common. Vasculitis can also be associated with solid tumours\: kidney, breast, ovary, lung, nasopharynx, stomach, small bowel, colon, and prostate cancers. Leukocytoclastic vasculitis is most common in vasculitis induced by malignancy, but large-vessel vasculitis may also emerge. Paraneoplastic vasculitis should be kept in mind to accomplish e

name: Iododerma
definition: Iododerma is a rare cutaneous reaction to systemically administered iodine. Formerly it was seen principally as a chronic eruption from prolonged exposure to iodide-containing medications such as cough medicines. More recently it has been associated with a more acute eruption following administration of iodide-containing radiocontrast media. It is characterised by vegetating acneform nodules and plaques typically affecting the face, neck, forearms and flexures.
synonyms: iodide-induced acne eruption

name: Acquired fibrinolytic haemorrhage
synonyms: haemorrhagic fibrinolysis

name: Basal cell tumour of unspecified site
Cutaneous markers of disorders of the haematopoietic or immune systems
definition: Cutaneous symptoms and signs which may point to the presence of underlying disorders of the bone marrow or immune system such as myelodysplasia, haematological malignancy or paraproteinaemia.

name: Infectious blepharitis
definition: A condition of the eyelid, commonly caused by an infection with a bacterial source. This condition is characterised by pruritus, burning, scratchiness, excessive tearing, or crusty debris around the eyelashes. This condition may also present with lid erythema, collarettes, madarosis, trichiasis, or plugged meibomian glands. Transmission is by direct or indirect contact with an infected individual, endogenous spread, or through fomites.
synonyms: anterior lid margin disease | anterior squamous lid disease | blepharitis, nonulcerative | ciliary blepharitis | marginal blepharitis

name: Other elsewhere classified cutaneous complication of diagnostic procedure
definition: Cutaneous complication of a diagnostic procedure, for example, haematoma or skin infection. This entity should be linked to the specific complication which has arisen.

name: HIV-associated immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome
definition: Immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome (IRIS) results from pathological immune responses occurring during immune reconstitution and is strongly associated with antiretroviral therapy for HIV infection and AIDS. There is a wide range of clinical manifestations, incorporating disease resulting from a pathological inflammatory reaction to pathogens such as tuberculosis and cryptococcosis, immune-mediated inflammatory disease and autoimmune disease. Clinical effects range from a mild, self-limi
synonyms: HIV - [human immunodeficiency virus] associated IRIS - [Immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome] | Immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome [IRIS], HIV-associated

name: Acne excoriee
definition: A specific form of skin picking disorder seen particularly in adolescent and young adult females who feel compelled to pick at acne papules, resulting in excoriation and scarring. It may result in significant disfigurement.
synonyms: Excoriated acne | acne excoria | acne excoriee | excoriated acne | picker's acne

name: Meningitis due to Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada
definition: Inflammation of the meninges due to Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada syndrome. The syndrome is clinically characterised by headache, neck stiffness, fever and photophobia. Other clinical features of Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada syndrome, including ophthalmic, dermatological such as depigmentation of eyebrows, eyelashes and scalp hair and other neurological manifestations including sensorineural hearing loss, cranial nerve palsies, transverse myelitis and neuropsychiatric changes may be present. Diagnosis may be con

name: Fluid overload with oedema
definition: This is the condition where there is too much fluid in the blood. The opposite condition is hypovolemia, which is too little fluid volume in the blood. Fluid volume excess in the intravascular compartment occurs due to an increase in total body sodium content and a consequent increase in extracellular body water. The mechanism usually stems from compromised regulatory mechanisms for sodium handling as seen in congestive heart failure (CHF), kidney failure, and liver failure. It may also be cause

name: Hidradenitis suppurativa affecting other specified sites
definition: Hidradenitis suppurativa involving sites other than the axillae and anogenital region. The most important other sites are the breasts.

name: Tuberous sclerosis
definition: A disease caused by a dominant mutation of 9q34 (TSC1) or 16p13 (TSC2). This disease may present with facial angiofibromas, Koenen tumours, fibrous plaques on the forehead and scalp, renal angiomyolipomas, subependymal nodules, multiple cortical tubers or retinal hamartoma, epilepsy, or mental retardation.
synonyms: Bourneville disease | Bourneville phakomatosis | Epiloia | Pringle's disease | bourneville disease | epiloia | TS | tuberous sclerosis

name: Sunburn
definition: An injury to the skin causing erythema, tenderness, and sometimes blistering and resulting from excessive exposure to the sun. The reaction is produced by the ultraviolet radiation in sunlight.
synonyms: sunburn heat | acute inflammatory erythema

name: Early syphilis involving the eye
definition: Ocular secondary syphilis may manifest as acute iritis, posterior uveitis, panuveitis, diffuse chorioretinitis or perivasculitis.

name: Burn of ankle or foot, full thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of ankle or foot, third degree

name: Rosselli-Gulienetti syndrome
definition: Rosselli-Gulienetti syndrome is a type of ectodermal dysplasia characterised by anhidrosis, hypotrichosis, cleft and lip palate, microdontia and syndactyly.
synonyms: rosselli-gulienetti syndrome

name: Acute urticaria
definition: Spontaneous urticaria lasting less than six weeks
synonyms: Acute ordinary urticaria | Acute spontaneous urticaria

name: Disturbances of oral epithelium
synonyms: disorder of oral epithelium NOS

name: Oral leukoplakia
definition: Leukoplakia is a condition where areas of keratosis appear as adherent white patches on the mucous membranes of the oral cavity. Leukoplakia may affect other gastrointestinal tract mucosal sites, or mucosal surfaces of the urinary tract and genitals.
synonyms: Leukoplakia of gingiva | leucoplakia of oral mucosa | leukokeratosis of oral mucosa | leukoplakia of oral epithelium

name: Structural developmental anomalies of mouth or tongue
definition: Embryo fetal anomalies affecting structure of maxillo-labial or mandibular tissues or the tongue.
synonyms: malformations of mouth NOS | malformations of mouth or tongue | malformations of tongue NOS | structural developmental anomalies of mouth NOS | structural developmental anomalies of tongue NOS

name: Immune thrombocytopenic purpura
definition: Immune thrombocytopenic purpura (or immune thrombocytopenia; ITP) is an autoimmune coagulation disorder characterised by isolated thrombocytopenia (a platelet count <100,000/microL), in the absence of any underlying disorder that may be associated with thrombocytopenia.
synonyms: Frank's essential thrombocytopenia | ITP - [idiopathic thrombocytopenia purpura] | Werlhof disease | autoimmune thrombocytopenic purpura | essential thrombocytopenia | haemorrhagic purpura | idiopathic purpura | idiopathic thrombocytopenia | idiopathic thrombocytopenia purpura | idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura
cn_name: 免疫性血小板减少性紫癜

name: Primary cutis verticis gyrata
definition: Cutis verticis gyrata is a giant naevus of the scalp characterised by hypertrophy and thickening of the skin of the scalp forming burrows with deep and cerebriform cutaneous folds.
synonyms: idiopathic cutis verticis gyrata

name: Transient neonatal epidermolysis bullosa acquisita
definition: The development of transient blistering and erosions in the neonate as the result of passive transfer of anti-collagen VII antibodies from its mother.

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to allergenic haptens derived from plants or organic matter
Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy with deafness or global delay
definition: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy with deafness and global delay syndrome is characterised by a sensory and autonomic axonal neuropathy, sensorineural hearing loss and persistent global developmental delay.

name: Stevens-Johnson syndrome
definition: Stevens-Johnson syndrome is an immune-complex–mediated hypersensitivity disorder involving mucous membranes (conjunctivae, oral mucosa and genital mucosa) with, by definition, skin involvement limited to no more than 9% body surface area. It is related to toxic epidermal necrolysis and shares many of the same triggers, notably drugs, but the inflammation is centred on and close to mucosal surfaces. Although mortality is low, acute morbidity is high and conjunctival involvement has the potential
synonyms: SJS

name: Erythema multiforme provoked by viral infection
definition: Erythema multiforme attributable to viral infection, the most important of which by far is Herpes simplex, which can provoke multiple debilitating repeated attacks. Amongst the many other viruses which have been implicated are Adenovirus, Coxsackievirus B5, Cytomegalovirus, Echovirus, Enterovirus, Epstein-Barr virus, hepatitis A, B and C viruses, measles, mumps, paravaccinia, orf, parvovirus B19, poliomyelitis and Varicella zoster virus.

name: Pediculosis
definition: A condition of the skin, hair, or genital region caused by an infection with the parasite Pediculus. This disease is characterised by pruritus. This condition also presents with symptoms depending on the site of infection. Transmission is by direct or indirect contact with an infected individual or animal. Confirmation is by identification of Pediculus.
synonyms: lice NOS | lice infestation | louse infestation | pediculus infestation

name: Curly hair – acral keratoderma – caries syndrome
synonyms: CHAC syndrome - [Curly hair - acral keratoderma - caries] syndrome | CHACS - [Curly hair – acral keratoderma – caries syndrome]

name: Cervical hypertrichosis – peripheral sensory and motor neuropathy
definition: Cervical hypertrichosis - peripheral neuropathy syndrome is characterised by the association of congenital hypertrichosis in the anterior cervical region with peripheral sensory and motor neuropathy.

name: Paraneoplastic vasculitis
definition: Cutaneous vasculitis in association with underlying malignancy where other putative causes cannot be identified and where resolution follows effective treatment of the malignancy. Recurrence of vasculitis may herald relapse of the underlying tumour. Although the vasculitis is usually of small cutaneous vessels (leucocytoclastic vasculitis), other forms have been recorded.

name: Subcutaneous dirofilariasis
definition: Subcutaneous dirofilariasis normally results from the transmission of microfilariae of Dirofilaria repens from the latter’s natural animal host to man via a mosquito bite. The adult worm cannot develop fully in man but typically manifests as a subcutaneous nodule, commonly located on or around the eyelids.

name: Recurrent aphthous stomatitis
definition: This is a recurrent type of mouth ulcer that presents as a painful open sore inside the mouth or upper throat characterised by a break in the mucous membrane.
synonyms: Recurrent oral aphthae | aphthous ulcers | canker sores | recurrent oral aphthae

name: Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada syndrome-associated anterior uveitis
definition: A rare acquired inflammatory syndrome of unknown aetiology involving melanocyte-containing tissues including the eye, inner ear, meninges, skin and hair. Clinical features include bilateral chronic iridocyclitis and posterior uveitis; tinnitus and dysacusis; headache and meningism; and poliosis, vitiligo and alopecia.
synonyms: Harada disease | VKH - [Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada syndrome] | Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada disease | Harada's disease | uveomeningoencephalitic syndrome | Vogt-Koyanagi syndrome

name: Cutaneous markers of internal disorders
definition: A heterogeneous group of skin disorders associated with underlying disease.

name: Discoid lupus erythematosus of lower eyelid
Bullous pemphigoid
definition: Bullous pemphigoid is the most common autoimmune blistering disease in the Western world. It chiefly affects the elderly and typically presents with a pruritic urticated erythema which evolves into a widespread eruption of intact tense blisters. It can sometimes involve mucous membranes. It is characterised by IgG antibodies to the basement membrane zone, leading to subepidermal clefts that are clinically manifest as blisters.
synonyms: BP | pemphigoid

name: Skin disorders associated with pregnancy, the neonatal period or infancy
definition: Dermatoses which are either specific to or occur predominantly in pregnancy, the neonatal period or the first few months of life
cn_name: 妊娠期、新生儿期或婴儿期相关的皮肤障碍

name: Unilateral circumscribed hyperhidrosis
definition: Unilateral circumscribed hyperhidrosis is an idiopathic condition in which profuse sweating occurs episodically in an area of skin sharply demarcated from the surrounding dry skin. Typically the face or arms are the involved territory. Sweating may occur spontaneously or in response to heat, psychological stimuli, or spicy foods.
synonyms: Idiopathic circumscribed hyperhidrosis

name: Erythema induratum of Whitfield
synonyms: erythema induratum of whitfield

name: Disseminated superficial actinic porokeratosis
definition: This is the most common form of disseminated porokeratosis and is often referred to by the acronym DSAP. The clinical and histological features are essentially the same as seen in disseminated superficial porokeratosis but the distribution is limited to chronically sun-exposed skin, particularly of the lower legs and extensor forearms. It is seen predominantly in individuals with limited protection by epidermal melanin.
synonyms: DSAP - [disseminated superficial actinic porokeratosis] | DSAP
snomed: 201086003 "disseminated superficial actinic porokeratosis";41495000 "disseminated superficial actinic porokeratosis"

name: Generalised morphoea
definition: A rare disabling and potentially fatal condition in which diffuse symmetrical sclerosis of the skin develops insidiously in the absence of associated systemic disturbances or Raynaud phenomenon. The aetiology is unknown. Its onset is usually in early to middle adult life, starting with plaques on the trunk which become confluent and then spread to limbs, neck and head. It may impede breathing because of restriction of thoracic wall movement.
synonyms: generalised scleroderma

name: Chronic radiation keratosis
definition: Chronic radiation keratoses are precancerous keratotic lesions that may arise on the skin many years after exposure to ionizing radiation. Exposure may be from x-ray therapy, from chronic occupational exposure to ionizing radiation (e.g. dentists, radiotherapists or workers exposed to radioactive materials in their places of work) or to environmental exposure as in survivors of nuclear accidents.

name: Pigmented skin lesion of uncertain nature, head
definition: This denotes the presence of a pigmented skin lesion on the face or scalp but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Actinic folliculitis
definition: A rare and incompletely understood light-induced dermatosis characterised by the appearance within 24-36 hours of exposure to sunlight of a non-pruritic monomorphic eruption of follicular papules and pustules involving the face, arms and/or upper chest. It may recur annually after the first sun exposure of the year.
synonyms: Actinic superficial folliculitis

name: Full thickness burns or corrosion
synonyms: third degree burns or corrosions

name: Meningomyelitis due to Syphilis
synonyms: Syphilitic meningomyelitis | cerebrospinal syphilis

name: Bladder varices
synonyms: Varix of bladder

name: Frostbite of toes with tissue necrosis
Symptom or complaint relating to nails
definition: A very non-specific term to indicate an actual or perceived problem affecting the finger- or toenails which cannot be more precisely coded elsewhere.

name: Oto-onycho-peroneal syndrome
synonyms: Oculo-oto-nasal malformations with osteo-onychodysplasia (MIM 259780)

name: Choroidal atrophy - alopecia
definition: Choroidal atrophy - alopecia syndrome is the association of choroidal atrophy and other manifestations of ectodermal dysplasia, including fine and sparse hair.

name: Late acute graft-versus-host disease
definition: Acute graft-versus-host disease (GVHD) present more than 100 days after engraftment. The clinical features of acute graft-versus-host disease can normally be readily distinguished from those of chronic GVHD. Late acute GVHD may be due to late onset, persistence beyond 100 days or reactivation, often after discontinuation of immunosuppressive therapy. Its management and prognosis are different from chronic GVHD.
synonyms: late acute GVHD - [graft-versus-host disease]

name: Genetic or developmental disorder classified elsewhere involving lips or oral mucosa
Hypertrichosis of lower eyelid
definition: This refers to an abnormal amount of hair growth over the eyelid; extensive cases of hypertrichosis have informally been called werewolf syndrome, because the appearance is similar to the werewolf. Hypertrichosis can be either congenital (present at birth) or acquired later in life.

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, ankle joint
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, ankle joint

name: Exanthematic drug eruption
definition: Acute skin eruption typically resembling viral infections such as measles, rubella or scarlatina attributable to drug. Antibiotics are common causes.
synonyms: Drug exanthem | Drug-induced exanthem | Drug-induced toxic erythema | Toxic erythema due to drug

name: Cumulative irritant contact dermatitis of hands
definition: A common form of hand dermatitis due to repeated exposure to low-grade irritants and defatting agents such as soaps and detergents. It tends to affect the dorsal finger webs initially. It is common in individuals who undertake repetitive tasks involving wet work and detergents, e.g. young mothers, nurses, catering staff and hairdressers.
synonyms: Chronic irritant hand dermatitis

name: Traumatic ulceration of skin
definition: Ulceration of the skin attributable to external trauma

name: Hypotrichosis – acro-osteolysis – onychogryphosis – palmoplantar keratoderma – periodontitis syndrome
synonyms: HOPP - [Hypotrichosis – acro-osteolysis – onychogryphosis – palmoplantar keratoderma – periodontitis] syndrome

name: Ablepharon, bilateral
definition: This is the congenital absence of the eyelids. It is a component of the Ablepharon macrostomia syndrome. This diagnosis is occurring in both eyes.

name: Achromic naevus
definition: A congenital circumscribed area of absent skin pigmentation. It is usually present as a single rounded amelanotic macule but segmental and systematized forms may occur.
synonyms: Naevus depigmentosus

name: Nummular dermatitis, dry type
definition: A form of nummular dermatitis or discoid eczema in which the inflammatory response is less acute than in the exudative type with the result that the involved skin is scaly and hyperkeratotic.
synonyms: Discoid eczema, dry type

name: Forefoot callosities
definition: Callosities and corns affecting the forefoot.
synonyms: Callus of forefoot | Clavus of forefoot | Heloma of forefoot

name: Benign proliferations, neoplasms and cysts of the skin
cn_name: 皮肤良性增生、肿瘤和囊肿

name: Angiokeratoma
definition: Angiokeratomas are acquired vascular lesions that result from the ectatic dilatation of pre-existing vessels in the papillary dermis, accompanied by hyperkeratotic epidermis. There are several clinical variants\: solitary papular angiokeratoma, angiokeratoma corporis diffusum, angiokeratoma of Mibelli and angiokeratoma of Fordyce, amongst which the last, in which the lesions are located on the vulva or scrotum, is the most common.

name: Cutaneous microvascular disturbances due to myeloproliferative disorder-associated platelet plugs
definition: Skin changes including retiform purpura, livedo reticularis, Raynaud phenomenon, erythromelalgia, superficial thombophlebitis and cutaneous necrosis, attributable to neoplastic thrombocytosis in essential thrombocythaemia and polycythaemia vera.

name: Gaucher disease type 1
definition: Gaucher disease type 1 is the chronic non-neurological form of Gaucher disease (a lysosomal storage disorder caused by a deficiency in glucocerebrosidase) characterised by organomegaly, bone involvement and cytopenia.
synonyms: Adult Gaucher disease | Noncerebral juvenile Gaucher disease

name: Exacerbation of constitutional dermatitis due to occupation
definition: Exacerbation of constitutional dermatitis, most commonly atopic eczema, attributable to work. Although this is most commonly due to contact with irritants or allergens, environmental factors such as low ambient humidity or excessive heat may also be responsible.

name: Juvenile dermatomyositis with overlap to nonorgan specific systemic autoimmune disorders
definition: Juvenile dermatomyositis with overlap to non-organ specific systemic autoimmune disorders is an early-onset systemic inflammatory disorders affecting the skeletal muscles, the skin, and other organs, associated with other autoimmune or connective tissue diseases. Juvenile dermatomyositis is most frequently associated with systemic sclerosis and mixed connective tissue disease.

name: Corrosion of second degree of shoulder and arm, except wrist or hand
synonyms: corrosion of second degree of shoulder and upper limb, except wrist and hand

name: Burn of perineum or genitalia, deep partial thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of perineum and genitalia, deep second degree
cn_name: 会阴或生殖器深层部分皮层烧伤

name: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum nanum
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum nanum, the main natural hosts of which are swine.

name: Onychogryphosis
synonyms: Hook nail | Horn nail | ram's horn nails

name: Apocrine hamartoma
definition: Hamartomatous malformation of elements originating from the developing apocrine gland.

name: Sclerodactyly resulting from disease classified elsewhere
definition: Sclerodactyly may be seen in a range of conditions including scleromyxoedema, porphyria cutanea tarda, hand-arm vibration syndrome and vinyl chloride disease.

name: Purpura or bruising
definition: Purpura is a non-blanchable multifocal purple skin discolouration due to bleeding into the skin and manifested as petechiae (pinpoint foci of intradermal haemorrhage) and ecchymoses (larger areas of intradermal haemorrhage). It has many causes and may be the presenting sign of diseases as diverse as thrombocytopenia, primary amyloidosis, meningococcal septicaemia and scurvy. It will often be accompanied by haemorrhage into the subcutaneous tissues (spontaneous bruising or haematoma).

name: Phototoxic reactions to skin contact with photoactive agents
definition: Non-allergic skin inflammation caused by cellular damage from reactive oxygen species in the skin produced by the interaction between ultraviolet or visible light and a photoactive substance in contact with the skin.
synonyms: Photocontact dermatitis
cn_name: 皮肤接触光活性物质引起的光毒性反应

name: Streptococcal toxin-mediated perineal erythema
definition: Streptococcal toxin-mediated perineal erythema is characterised by a striking diffuse macular erythema in the perineum occurring abruptly after a streptococcal pharyngitis. It is often recurrent.
cn_name: 链球菌毒素介导的会阴红斑

name: Bromhidrosis
synonyms: Fetid sweat | Malodorous sweat | Osmidrosis

name: Malignant neoplasm metastasis in skin of lip
synonyms: lower lip metastasis | upper lip metastasis

name: Xeroderma of eyelid
synonyms: acquired xeroderma of eyelid

name: Juvenile dermatomyositis with necrotising cutaneous vasculitis
definition: Type of idiopathic inflammatory myopathy that is characterized by rash accompanied by muscle weakness, anorexia, and fatigue. Juvenile form presents more insidiously with muscle weakness, particularly of the neck flexors, often preceded by fatigue and low grade fevers. Associated with heliotropic rash, periorbital erythema, Gottron papules over the knuckles, elbows, and knees, and periungal telangectasias. Juvenile form is associated with greater involvement of blood vessels and vasculopathy com

name: Lymphatic malformation of liver
definition: Lymphangioma of liver is a rare benign cavernous/cystic vascular lesion composed of dilated lymphatic channels in liver containing proteinaceous fluid or blood.
synonyms: Lymphangioma of liver

name: Hereditary coproporphyria
definition: Hereditary coproporphyria is a form of acute hepatic porphyria characterised by the occurrence of neuro-visceral attacks and, more rarely, by the presence of cutaneous lesions.
synonyms: Coproporphyrinogen oxidase deficiency | HCP

name: Basal cell carcinoma of skin
definition: Basal cell carcinoma or BCC is the most common malignancy in humans. It is believed that BCCs arise from pluripotential cells in the basal layer of the epidermis or, less commonly, hair follicle. BCCs typically occur in areas of chronic sun exposure and present as slowly enlarging reddish papules, plaques or nodules on the head and neck, although the superficial variant is often located on the trunk. BCCs frequently ulcerate and become crusted. Although they rarely metastasize, they can cause si
synonyms: BCC - [basal cell carcinoma] of skin | Basal cell epithelioma of skin | Basal cell epithelioma of unspecified site | Basalioma | Rodent ulcer of skin | rodent ulcer of unspecified site

name: Vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia, HPV-independent
definition: Vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia (VIN), HPV-independent, is a non-invasive precursor of HPV-independent squamous cell carcinoma of the vulva, characterized by atypia of the basal and parabasal keratinocytes in an otherwise well-differentiated epithelium.
synonyms: DVIN [Differentiated vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia] | Differentiated vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia | Vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia grade III, simplex (deprecated) | Vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia grade III, simplex, differentiated type, non-HPV associated (deprecated) | Vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia, differentiated (dVIN)
cn_name: 外阴上皮内瘤变，HPV非相关性

name: Urachal sinus
definition: Urachal sinus occurs when an urachal cyst becomes infected producing a tract that may drain into the bladder or to the surface of the skin producing persistent infection and swelling of the umbilicus in the newborn.

name: Drug-induced anal ulceration
definition: Anal ulceration attributable to drug treatment, typically with the anti-anginal agent, nicorandil. It presents with anal pain.
synonyms: medicament-induced anal ulceration

name: Sequelae of frostbite with tissue necrosis of thorax
Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, classical type
definition: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, classic type is a type of Ehlers-Danlos syndromes (EDS), a heterogeneous group of hereditary connective tissue diseases characterised by joint hyperlaxity, cutaneous hyperelasticity and tissue fragility, and is characterised by the following major clinical diagnostic criteria\: hyperextensible skin, atrophic cutaneous scars due to tissue fragility and joint hyperlaxity.

name: Superficial acne without nodules
synonyms: Superficial acne NOS

name: Frostbite of ankle with tissue necrosis
synonyms: frostbite with tissue necrosis of ankle

name: Developmental anomalies of cutaneous vasculature
definition: Congenital vascular malformations affecting the skin
cn_name: 皮肤脉管系统发育异常

name: Dermatophytosis due to zoophilic dermatophytes
definition: Infection by dermatophytes which normally infect mammals other than man. When they do infect man they often induce a marked inflammatory reaction.

name: Angiokeratoma of the vulva
definition: Fordyce angiokeratomas occurring on the labia majora in older women.

name: Hereditary sclerosing poikiloderma with tendon and pulmonary involvement
definition: In this condition, reported from South Africa, hereditary sclerosing poikiloderma is associated with pulmonary disease which is potentially lethal in adulthood. Skin manifestations which facilitate diagnosis include facial telangiectasia, mottled hypo- and hyperpigmentation, papules and epidermal atrophy. The scalp, facial and body hair are fine and scanty. The tendon contractures lead to progressive digital flexion deformities and abnormalities of the ankles and feet, with disturbance of gait.

name: Melanocytic naevus with severe melanocytic dysplasia
definition: Melanocytic naevus with severe melanocytic dysplasia is a histopathological diagnosis based on the presence of severe cytological atypia, defined as enlarged, spindle- and epithelioid-shaped melanocytes with hyperchromatic nuclei (typically at least twice the size of those of basal keratinocytes) and distinct nucleoli. Such naevi tend to be irregular in size and pigmentation and to have been excised because of concern that they may represent early melanoma.
synonyms: Dysplastic naevus with severe melanocytic dysplasia | Severely dysplastic melanocytic naevus
cn_name: 黑素细胞痣伴重度黑素细胞非典型增生

name: Syphilitic leukoderma
definition: An uncommon presentation of secondary syphilis which may be mistaken for vitiligo.

name: Dermatophytide
definition: A dermatophytide is a reactive inflammatory eruption secondary to dermatophyte infection at a distant site. Most commonly it manifests as a vesicular rash affecting the extremities.
synonyms: Trichophytide

name: Ectrodactyly - ectodermal dysplasia - cleft lip or palate
definition: EEC syndrome is a genetic developmental disorder characterised by ectrodactyly, ectodermal dysplasia, and orofacial clefts (cleft lip/palate).
synonyms: EEC - [Ectrodactyly - ectodermal dysplasia - cleft lip or palate] syndrome

name: Granuloma or granuloma-like lesions of oral mucosa
definition: This is a specific type of chronic inflammatory response of the oral mucosa characterised by a localised accumulation of epithelioid macrophages, multi nucleate giant cells, and lymphocytes.
synonyms: denture granuloma | granuloma of oral mucosa
cn_name: 口腔黏膜肉芽肿或肉芽肿样损害

name: Verrucous squamous cell carcinoma of vulva
synonyms: Verrucous carcinoma of vulva

name: Generalised essential telangiectasia
cn_name: 泛发性毛细血管扩张症

name: Genetic disorders of adipose tissue or lipid metabolism affecting the skin
Desmoplastic melanoma, primary
definition: Desmoplastic malignant melanoma is characterised by its subtle and misleading clinical and histological morphology. For this reason diagnosis is often missed or delayed. Usually it has an innocuous clinical appearance described as an indurated discoid papule, plaque or nodule. Pigmentation is frequently absent, although a lentigo- or lentigo maligna-like discolouration adjacent to the nodule is not uncommon. Desmoplastic malignant melanoma has a high propensity for local recurrence and, although
synonyms: Neurotropic malignant melanoma

name: Lateral facial cleft
definition: A condition caused by failure of the structures of the face to correctly develop during the antenatal period. This condition is characterised by a malformation positioned horizontally on the face.

name: De Sanctis-Cacchione syndrome
definition: De Sanctis-Cacchione syndrome is a rare disorder characterised by the skin and eye symptoms of xeroderma pigmentosum (XP) occurring in association with microcephaly, progressive mental retardation, retarded growth and sexual development, deafness, choreoathetosis, ataxia and quadriparesis.

name: Parasitic infestation of eyelid in onchocerciasis
Gastric varices with bleeding
definition: This refers to abnormally dilated submucosal veins in the lining of stomach with portal hypertension, with sign of bleeding.

name: Purpura or bruising due to increased intravascular pressure
definition: Purpura resulting from either acute or chronic elevation of pressure in dermal capillaries and venules.

name: Delusion of infestation
definition: A delusion that one is infested with living organisms, most typically insects or parasites and most commonly reported as consisting of 'bugs' or insects crawling on or under the skin. The experience of the sensation of formication, a form of tactile hallucination, may accompany the delusion.
synonyms: Delusions of parasitosis | Parasitophobia | delusional disorder, somatic type | delusional infestation | delusional parasitosis | ekbom syndrome | monosymptomatic hypochondriacal psychosis

name: Congenital haemangioma
definition: This is a congenital benign, and usually a self-involuting tumour, (swelling or growth) of the endothelial cells that line blood vessels and is characterised by increased number of normal or abnormal vessels filled with blood.

name: Adverse cutaneous reactions to medication
definition: This group incorporates not only drug rashes but also other acute and chronic cutaneous and mucocutaneous effects of topical or systemic medicaments, whether conventional or \"alternative\".
cn_name: 药物引起的皮肤不良反应

name: Squamous cell carcinoma of arm
synonyms: Epidermoid carcinoma of arm

name: Keratosis linearis – ichthyosis congenita – keratoderma
synonyms: KLICK - [Keratosis linearis – ichthyosis congenita – keratoderma] syndrome

name: Panniculitis
definition: Panniculitis is the name given to a heterogeneous group of diseases all characterised by inflammation of subcutaneous adipose tissue.
synonyms: Nodular panniculitis

name: Recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa inversa
definition: Recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa inversa is a rare subtype of dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, characterised by blisters and erosions which are primarily confined to intertriginous skin sites, the base of the neck, the uppermost back, and the lumbosacral area.
synonyms: RDEB - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa] inversa | RDEB-I - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa inversa] | RDEB-inv - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa inversa] | RDEB inversa subtype

name: Multiple minute digitate keratoses
definition: An uncommon condition of unknown cause characterised by the development of numerous digitate keratoses across the trunk and limbs with sparing of the face, palms, and soles. Fine, spiny non-follicular keratoses measuring up to 5 mm in length project from otherwise normal skin.
synonyms: Digitate keratoses of Goldstein | Disseminated spiked hyperkeratosis | Multiple minute digitate hyperkeratoses | Multiple minute digitate hyperkeratosis

name: Drug-induced angioedema
definition: Non-allergic angioedema due to drugs, in particular angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitors and nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs.
synonyms: medicament-induced angioedema

name: Chondrodysplasia punctata, X-linked dominant
definition: X-linked dominant chondrodysplasia punctata (CDPX2), also known as Conradi-Hünermann-Happle syndrome, is a rare form of skeletal dysplasia that affects the skeleton producing short stature, asymmetric shortening of the limbs and scoliosis, as well as affecting the skin, hair and eyes.
synonyms: CDPX2 - [Chondrodysplasia punctata, X-linked dominant] | Calcinosis universalis | Chondrodystrophia calcificans congenita | Conradi-Hünermann-Happle syndrome | Sterol D8-D7 isomerase deficiency

name: Pox virus infections of the skin
definition: Infection by pox viruses from the genera orthopox (cowpox, vaccinia, monkeypox), parapox (orf), yatapox (tanapox) and molluscipox (molluscum contagiosum). All characteristically involve the skin.

name: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa, LOC
definition: An autosomal recessive syndrome largely confined to children of Punjabi origin due to a mutation in the LAMA3 gene encoding laminin alpha-3a, a component of the lamina lucida. It is characterised by recurrent skin ulceration, shedding of nails, and granulomatous inflammation affecting the conjunctivae and vocal cords, which may result in blindness and fatal airway obstruction respectively.
synonyms: JEB-LOC - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa laryngo-onycho-cutaneous] syndrome | LOC - [laryngo-onycho-cutaneous] syndrome | LOGIC | Laryngo-onycho-cutaneous syndrome | Shabbir syndrome | JEB-LOC | LOC syndrome

name: Recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, generalised intermediate
definition: Recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, generalised intermediate (RDEB-gen intermed), also known as RDEB non-Hallopeau-Siemens type, is a subtype of dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa characterised by generalised cutaneous and mucosal blistering that is milder than in the generalised severe form and is not associated with severe deformities.
synonyms: RDEB-O - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, generalised other] | RDEB-gen intermed - [recessive dystrophic epidermolysis, generalised intermediate]

name: Nail involvement in other specified genetic disease
Generalised eruptive melanocytic naevi
definition: This phenomenon describes the rapid simultaneous appearance of multiple melanocytic naevi, often hundreds in number, on previously uninvolved sun-exposed skin. The phenomenon has been linked to immunosuppression, particularly in renal transplant recipients and in individuals receiving cancer chemotherapy, and may be considered a more advanced counterpart of generalised eruptive lentiginosis.
cn_name: 广泛性发疹性黑素细胞痣

name: Punctate palmoplantar keratoderma, type 2
definition: Punctate palmoplantar keratoderma type 2 is a type of isolated, punctate, hereditary palmoplantar keratoderma characterized by multiple, asymptomatic, 1 to 2 mm-long, firm, hyperkeratotic projections ('spiny keratosis') on the palms, soles and digits (typically confined to their volar and/or lateral aspects). Histopathologically, compact columnar parakeratosis over hypo- or agranular epidermis is observed.
synonyms: PPPK2 - [Punctate palmoplantar keratoderma, type 2] | Porokeratosis of Mantoux | Porokeratosis palmaris et plantaris disseminata | PPPD

name: Primary erythromelalgia
definition: Primary Erythromelalgia is characterised by intermittent burning pain, redness and warmth in the distal hands and feet. The primary form is often familial and inherited in an autosomal dominant fashion. Some patients have gain of function mutations in the SCN9A gene which encodes a voltage gated sodium channel ?-subunit, Nav 1.7.
synonyms: idiopathic erythromelalgia | primary erythromelalgia

name: Bacterial cellulitis, erysipelas or lymphangitis
definition: Diffuse, spreading infections of skin and soft tissues by a range of bacterial organisms, most commonly beta-haemolytic streptococci and Staphylococcus aureus. The clinical presentation is dependent not only on the organism but also on the manner in which it invades the tissues.
synonyms: cellulitis NOS

name: Burn of chest wall
synonyms: thoracic burn | thorax burn

name: Blepharochalasis upper eyelid
definition: This is an inflammation of the eyelid that is characterised by exacerbations and remissions of eyelid oedema, which results in a stretching and subsequent atrophy of the eyelid tissue resulting in redundant folds over the lid margins. This diagnosis is of the upper eyelid.

name: Pityriasis rubra pilaris type V, atypical juvenile
definition: This chronic childhood form of pityriasis rubra pilaris is uncommon and probably genetically heterogeneous. It may be present at birth or start in early childhood with widespread erythema and hyperkeratosis. Follicular hyperkeratosis is a prominent feature and keratoderma is common. Scleroderma-like changes have been described in the hands and feet.

name: Congenital hypertrophy of the lateral fold of the hallux
definition: A congenital abnormality of the periungual soft tissues of the great toe characterised by hypertrophy of the nail fold which partially covers the nail plate and is frequently associated with inflammation and pain due to ingrowth of the nail plate.

name: Acquired abnormalities of nail colour
synonyms: nail discolouration

name: Psoriatic nail pitting
definition: Multiple irregularly spaced \"thimble\" pits develop in one or more nails. Isolated psoriatic nail pitting may pass unnoticed.

name: Autosomal ichthyosis syndromes with neurological manifestations
definition: Hereditary autosomal disorders in which ichthyosis and neurological abnormalities are prominent features.

name: Drug-induced hirsutism
synonyms: medicament-induced hirsutism

name: Abnormal sensitivity to light or UV radiation of uncertain or unspecified nature
synonyms: Photosensitivity

name: Rothmund-Thomson syndrome type 2
definition: Rothmund-Thomson syndrome type 2 differs from Rothmund-Thomson syndrome type 1 in that it is associated with mutations in the RECQL4 gene and an increased risk of osteosarcoma and squamous cell carcinoma.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 1 of lower limb
synonyms: stage 1 decubitus ulcer of other sites of lower limb

Labia cancer

name: Hyperphosphataemic familial tumoural calcinosis
definition: This is an electrolyte disturbance in which there is an abnormally low level of phosphate in the blood, in a rare condition in which there is calcium deposition in the soft tissue in periarticular location i.e. around joints.

name: Dermatoses of the eye, eyelids or eyebrows
definition: A heterogeneous group of disorders involving the mucosal surface of the eyes, the skin of the eyelids or the eyebrows.

name: Congenital melanocytic naevus
definition: Congenital melanocytic naevi are circumscribed areas of skin pigmentation present at birth as a result of abnormal intrauterine proliferation of melanocytes within the dermis, the epidermis or both. They may range in size from a few millimetres to many centimetres in diameter. If their projected or final adult maximal diameter is greater than 20 cm they are termed giant congenital melanocytic naevi.
synonyms: Congenital pigmented naevus | Globular melanocytic naevus (dermatoscopy)

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, clavicle
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, clavicle

name: Cutaneous mastocytosis
definition: Cutaneous mastocytosis is characterised by abnormal accumulation and proliferation of cutaneous mast cells. Most types are isolated but cutaneous mastocytosis can occur in association with systemic disease. Clinical forms include cutaneous mastocytoma, urticaria pigmentosa (the most frequent form), pseudoxanthomatous nodular cutaneous mastocytosis, telangiectasia macularis eruptiva perstans and diffuse cutaneous mastocytosis.
synonyms: CM

name: Generalised granuloma annulare
definition: This form of granuloma annulare typically manifests as numerous small asymptomatic skin-coloured or erythematous infiltrated but barely elevated dermal papules on the trunk and proximal limbs. These tend to be arranged in arcs or rings which may coalesce as they enlarge, leaving behind a violaceous discolouration of the skin. In contrast to the localised form, it is commonest in middle-aged women.

name: Inclusion body fibromatosis
definition: Inclusion body fibromatosis is a benign fibro/myofibroblastic proliferation that occurs almost exclusively on the fingers and toes. It is characterised by bright, round, intracytoplasmic, eosinophilic inclusions. Lesions typically present at birth or in the first year of life as multiple small nodules with on the dorsal or dorsolateral aspects of the third, fourth and fifth digits.
synonyms: Recurring digital fibrous tumour of childhood | infantile digital fibromatosis | inclusion body fibromatosis | recurring digital fibrous tumor of childhood

name: Inflammatory dermatoses of the newborn
definition: A range of inflammatory skin disorders presenting in the neonatal period.

name: Generalised eczematous dermatitis of unspecified type
definition: Generalised eczema of uncertain, unknown or unspecified aetiology.
synonyms: Erythrodermic eczema
cn_name: 未特指类型的泛发性湿疹样皮炎

name: Ocular albinism
synonyms: autosomal form of ocular albinism

name: Paramedian facial cleft
definition: A condition caused by failure of the structures of the face to correctly develop during the antenatal period. This condition is characterised by a malformation positioned adjacent and parallel to the midline of the face.
synonyms: Tessier number 1, 2, 12 or 13 facial cleft

name: Eczematous nail dystrophy
definition: Nail dystrophy attributable to eczema affecting paronychial tissues.

name: Intermittent maple-syrup-urine disease
definition: This is an intermittent autosomal recessive metabolic disorder affecting branched-chain amino acids. It is one type of organic acidemia.
synonyms: Intermittent branched chain ketoaciduria

name: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton gourvilii
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton gourvilii and typically causing on endothrix infection of scalp hair.

name: Chronic mucocutaneous candidosis
definition: Chronic Mucocutaneous Candidiasis is a primary immune deficiency characterised by persistent and/or recurrent infections of skin, nails and mucous membranes, caused by organisms of the genus Candida, mainly C. albicans.
synonyms: Chronic mucocutaneous candidiasis | candida granuloma | candidal granuloma | candidiasis granuloma | chronic localised mucocutaneous candidiasis | chronic localised mucocutaneous candidosis | monilia granuloma | monilial granuloma | mucocutaneous candidiasis | CANDF

name: Oral-facial-digital syndrome
definition: A condition caused by failure of the head and digits to correctly develop during the antenatal period.
