synonyms: Skin sclerosis | induration of skin

name: Pleomorphic fibroma
definition: An asymptomatic, solitary, slowly growing, flesh coloured and non-ulcerated dome-shaped to polypoid cutaneous papule which, although benign, contains cytologically atypical fibrohistiocytic cells.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 1 of trochanter
synonyms: stage 1 decubitus ulcer of trochanter

name: Dermatofibroma
definition: A common benign skin tumour which presents as a firm dermal papule or nodule, most commonly on the lower limbs. Histologically it is characterised by coarse, haphazardly arranged collagen bundles and a variable cellular infiltrate including fibrocytes.
synonyms: Fibrous histiocytoma | benign fibrous histiocytoma | dental dendrocytoma | fibroma simplex | histiocytoma | nodular subepidermal fibrosis | sclerosing hemangioma | benign Cutaneous fibrous Histiocytoma | dermatofibroma | fibrohistiocytic tumor

name: Herpes simplex infection of vulva
definition: Infection of the vulva and surrounding skin by Herpes simplex virus. This is usually but not always transmitted by sexual contact and caused by Herpes simplex virus type 2 (HSV-2) rather than Herpes simplex virus type 1, which is traditionally associated with orofacial herpes. It is characterised by multiple painful vesicles and superficial ulcers affecting vulval skin and mucosa. Primary genital herpes in women is more likely to involve the vagina, cervix and urethra, whereas recurrent infectio

name: Erythematotelangiectatic rosacea
definition: Erythematotelangiectatic rosacea manifests as facial erythema and a flushing tendency affecting but not confined to the central face and forehead\: the lateral cheeks, the ears and the sides of the neck may also be involved. It is commonest in fair-skinned individuals and tends to be made worse by exposure to wind and sunlight. With time permanent telangiectasia develops.
cn_name: 红斑与毛细血管扩张型酒渣鼻

name: Late syphilis of skin or mucous membranes
definition: This is a late, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is involving the skin and mucous membranes.

name: Infection of toe
synonyms: infected toe

name: Contact blepharoconjunctivitis
definition: Contact blepharoconjunctivitis comprises both allergic and irritant contact reactions to substances in contact with eyelid skin and/or conjunctiva. It presents clinically with\: itching and burning of the eyelids and conjunctivae; oedema, erythema, exudation, crusting and scale of eyelid skin; and conjunctival erythema and oedema.

name: Hereditary progressive mucinous histiocytosis
definition: Hereditary progressive mucinous histiocytosis is a rare, benign, non-Langerhans cell histiocytosis characterized by childhood or adolescence onset of multiple, small, asymptomatic, slowly progressing, skin-colored to red-brown papules with predilection for the face, dorsal hands, forearms and legs, without associated mucosal or visceral involvement. Histologically, papules are well-circumscribed, unencapsulated, nodular aggregates of histiocytes with abundant mucin in the upper and middermis.

name: Chronic lichenified skin disorder
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature in which lichenification is the prominent feature. Examples include chronic eczema, lichen simplex and lichen planus.

name: Drug-induced hypermelanosis
definition: Hypermelanosis resulting from interaction of a systemic drug or its metabolites and epidermal melanocytes. A variety of mechanisms are involved.

name: Late congenital syphilitic encephalitis
definition: This is a late congenital, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with encephalitis.

name: Acne conglobata
definition: An uncommon chronic severe inflammatory form of acne characterised by the development of multiple abscesses and sinuses followed by extensive hypertrophic and atrophic scarring. It may be associated with spondyloarthropathy or with other follicular occlusive diseases including dissecting cellulitis of the scalp and hidradenitis suppurativa.
synonyms: Conglobate acne

name: Lichen striatus
definition: Lichen striatus is a distinctive usually self-limiting and asymptomatic inflammatory dermatosis characterised by pink or red papules in a linear distribution that develop in the lines of Blaschko. These usually occur as isolated lesions on the limbs in children aged 5-15 years and generally resolve over 6-12 months. The aetiology is incompletely understood but approximately 40% of cases have a background of atopy.
synonyms: Linear lichenoid dermatosis | BLAISE | Blaschko linear acquired inflammatory skin eruption | lichenoid trophoneurosis | linear lichenoid dermatosis

name: Benign acantholytic dermatosis of vulva
definition: A rare sporadic condition, also described as papular acantholytic dyskeratosis of the vulva, in which multiple, dome-shaped, smooth white papules are localised to the skin of the vulva and surrounding area. The papules are composed of acantholytic and dyskeratotic cells simulating Hailey-Hailey disease or Darier disease.
synonyms: Papular acantholytic dyskeratosis of the vulva | Papular genitocrural acantholysis

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, vascular-like type
definition: Ehlers-Danlos, vascular-like type is a form of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome characterised by spontaneous dissection of medium-sized arteries during young adulthood, including mainly the iliac, femoral, and renal arteries.

name: Cri-du-chat syndrome
definition: The cri-du-chat (French for \"cat's cry\") syndrome is a genetic disease resulting from a deletion of variable size occurring on the short arm of chromosome 5 (5p-). The main clinical features are a high-pitched monochromatic cry, microcephaly, broad nasal bridge, epicanthal folds, micrognathia, abnormal dermatoglyphics, and severe psychomotor and intellectual deficit.
synonyms: 5p deletion | 5p minus syndrome | Lejeune syndrome | cat cry syndrome

name: Special forms of dermatophytosis
definition: Clinically distinctive forms of dermatophyte infection.

name: Familial porphyria cutanea tarda
definition: This refers to a familial disease which is named because it is a porphyria that often presents with skin manifestations later in life. The disorder results from low levels of the enzyme responsible for the fifth step in heme production.
synonyms: Familial PCT - [porphyria cutanea tarda]

name: Hordeolum externum lower eyelid
definition: This is an infection of the sebaceous glands of Zeis at the base of the eyelashes, or an infection of the apocrine sweat glands of Moll. External styes form on the outside of the lids and can be seen as small red bumps. Internal styes are infections of the meibomian sebaceous glands lining the inside of the eyelids. They also cause a red bump underneath the lid with only generalised redness and swelling visible on the outside. This diagnosis is of the lower eye.

name: Nail dystrophy, not otherwise specified
synonyms: Dystrophic nail | Onychodystrophy
cn_name: 甲营养不良，其他方面未特指

name: Pruritus due to central nervous system injury or degeneration
definition: Pruritus attributable to CNS injury or degeneration such as stroke or multiple sclerosis. Unilateral pruritus may follow hemiplegic stroke.
synonyms: Central neuropathic itch

name: Congenital symblepharon, unilateral
definition: This is a congenital partial or complete adhesion of the palpebral conjunctiva of the eyelid to the bulbar conjunctiva of the eyeball. It results either from disease (conjunctival sequelae of Trachoma) or trauma. Cicatricial pemphigoid and, in severe cases, rosacea may cause symblepharon. This diagnosis is occurring in one eye.

name: Circinate balanitis
definition: Circinate balanitis is strongly associated with HLA-B27 and reactive arthropathies, in particular that associated with chlamydial urethritis in men (Reiter syndrome). It is readily recognised by the presence of annular and serpiginous scaly plaques on the glans penis. These show parakeratosis and intraepithelial spongiform neutrophilic pustules on histopathological examination.

name: Downhill varices of the oesophagus with bleeding
Certain disorders affecting the nails or perionychium
definition: Abnormalities of the nails and perionychium (the soft tissues surrounding the nail plate including the matrix, nail folds, eponychium and hyponychium) which are not classified elsewhere.
cn_name: 影响甲或甲周的某些疾病

name: Female pattern hair loss, non-androgenetic
definition: Patterned alopecia in women which cannot be attributed to androgens

name: Non-pustular psoriasis of the palms or soles
definition: Psoriasis of the palms and soles may appear as confluent redness and scaling, discrete plaques, ill-defined scaly/fissured areas, or confluent plaques extending to the wrists and to the margins of the soles and heels. It not infrequently extends to involve the dorsal surfaces of the fingers and hands. It may sometimes be difficult to distinguish from hyperkeratotic eczema of the palms and soles and from palmoplantar pustulosis.
synonyms: Palmoplantar non-pustular psoriasis

name: Melanoma in situ of skin
definition: Malignant melanoma confined to the epidermis and described as being in radial growth phase.
synonyms: Radial growth phase malignant melanoma

name: Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to topical medicaments
Woolly hair – palmoplantar keratoderma – dilated cardiomyopathy
definition: This syndrome, widely known as Carvajal syndrome, is characterised by woolly hair, striate palmoplantar keratoderma and left-sided arrhythmogenic ventricular cardiomyopathy, associated with severe ventricular dysfunction, arrhythmias of left ventricular origin and sudden cardiac death. It is inherited as autosomal recessive disorder due to a mutation in the desmoplakin gene.
synonyms: Carvajal syndrome | Carvajal-Huerta syndrome | dilated cardiomyopathy with woolly hair and striate palmoplantar keratoderma | DCWHK | dilated cardiomyopathy with woolly hair and keratoderma | palmoplantar keratoderma with left ventricular cardiomyopathy and woolly hair

name: Genetic disorders of skin pigmentation
definition: Genetic disorders of the skin characterised by disordered pigmentation, including albinism and inherited forms of lentiginosis.
synonyms: genetic disorders of skin pigmentation NOS

name: Compound melanocytic naevus
definition: A nevus composed of neoplastic melanocytes that infiltrate both the epidermis and the dermis.
synonyms: Benign compound naevus | Compound naevus

name: Penicillamine-induced acquired pseudoxanthoma elasticum
definition: Pseudoxanthoma elasticum limited to the skin resulting from long-term administration of penicillamine. Inhibition of cross-linking of collagen and elastic fibres results in production of vastly increased amounts of abnormal elastin in the dermis.

name: Purpura fulminans
definition: Purpura fulminans is a life-threatening, rapidly progressive thrombotic disorder affecting mainly neonates and children, characterised by purpuric skin lesions and disseminated intravascular coagulation. The lesions are initially well-demarcated erythematous macules, rapidly developing irregular central areas of blue-black haemorrhagic necrosis, becoming painful, dark and raised, sometimes with vesicle or bulla formation. Lesions later lead to irreversible endothelial ischemic injury with gangre

name: Sporadic porphyria cutanea tarda
definition: This refers to a sporadic disease which is named because it is a porphyria that often presents with skin manifestations later in life. The disorder results from low levels of the enzyme responsible for the fifth step in heme production.
synonyms: Sporadic PCT - [porphyria cutanea tarda]

name: Partial albinism
definition: Griscelli syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder that results in pigmentary dilution of the skin and the hair (silver hair), the presence of large clumps of pigment in hair shafts, and an accumulation of melanosomes in melanocytes. It may be associated with neurological (type 1) or haematological (type 2) abnormalities.
synonyms: Griscelli

name: Porokeratotic eccrine ostial and dermal duct naevus
Relapsing fever
synonyms: febris recurrens | novy febris recurrens | novy relapsing fever | relapsing fever NOS

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, lower leg
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, lower leg

name: Limited systemic sclerosis
definition: Combination of calcinosis, Raynaud phenomenon, oesophageal dysfunction, sclerodactyly and telangiectasia.
synonyms: CREST - [calcinosis, Raynaud phenomenon, oesophageal dysmotility, sclerodactyly, and telangiectasia] syndrome | CRST - [calcinosis, Raynaud phenomenon, sclerodactyly and telangiectasia] syndrome

name: Epidermodysplasia verruciformis
definition: Epidermodysplasia verruciformis (EV) is a rare inherited genodermatosis characterised by chronic infection with human papillomavirus (HPV) leading to polymorphous cutaneous lesions and high risk of developing non-melanoma skin cancer.

name: Leukaemic infiltration of skin
definition: Cutaneous involvement by an acute or chronic leukaemia.
synonyms: Leukaemia cutis

name: Acral skin peeling syndrome
definition: Acral peeling skin syndrome is caused by mutations in the TGM5 gene, the product of which is transglutaminase 5, an enzyme which plays a critical role in the formation of the cornified cell envelope which surrounds keratinocytes in the upper epidermis and is vital to epidermal integrity. Skin peeling is usually evident from birth and is most noticeable on the hands and feet.
synonyms: APSS | acral peeling skin syndrome | localized peeling skin syndrome | PSS2

name: Adult dermatomyositis with overlap to nonorgan specific systemic autoimmune disorders
Anonychia or onychodystrophy – hypoplasia or absence of distal phalanges
definition: Cooks syndrome is characterised by nail anomalies ranging from onychodystrophy (dystrophic nails) to anonychia (absence of nails), associated with brachydactyly of the fifth finger, and digitalization of the thumbs (triphalangism).
synonyms: Cooks syndrome

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum C
definition: Xeroderma pigmentosum complementation group C (XPC) is the most frequent subtype of xeroderma pigmentosum (XP), a rare photodermatosis predisposing to skin cancers). XPC patients demonstrate typical XP manifestations of the skin (progressive freckling and dryness of skin, skin cancer, ocular malignancies) but generally do not display any acute sunburn reactions. In addition XPC patients do not have any neurological abnormalities.
synonyms: XPC - [xeroderma pigmentosum C]

name: Immunosuppressive therapy as contributor to skin infection classified elsewhere
Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type 6B
definition: Brittle cornea syndrome is a form of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome characterised by severe ocular manifestations due to extreme corneal thinning and fragility with rupture in the absence of significant trauma. It generally progresses to blindness.
synonyms: Brittle cornea syndrome | ehlers-danlos syndrome type 6B

name: Superficial frostbite of head
definition: Damage to surface tissues of the head as the result of low environmental temperatures.

name: Hennekam lymphangiectasia-lymphoedema syndrome
definition: Hennekam lymphangiectasia-lymphoedema syndrome (Hennekam syndrome) is characterised by the association of lymphoedema, intestinal lymphangiectasia, intellectual deficit and facial dysmorphism. Lymphoedema affects the face, lower limbs and genitalia; if not present at birth, it tends to appear by early infancy. Intestinal lymphangiectasia may result in protein-losing enteropathy, growth retardation, peripheral oedema and ascites. Facial signs include a flat face, a broad and depressed nasal bridg
synonyms: Hennekam syndrome

name: Congenital cytomegalovirus infection
definition: A condition affecting neonates, caused by an infection with cytomegalovirus in utero. This condition is characterised by jaundice, low birth weight, splenomegaly, hepatomegaly, or pneumonia if symptoms develop shortly after birth, or may be asymptomatic. This condition commonly presents later in life with loss of hearing, loss of vision, or developmental disabilities. Transmission is by vertical transmission. Confirmation is by detection of cytomegalovirus in neonatal urine, saliva, blood, or ot
synonyms: congenital CMV infection
cn_name: 先天性巨细胞病毒感染
snomed: 157118007 "congenital cytomegalovirus infection";268875000 "congenital cytomegalovirus infection";59527008 "congenital cytomegalovirus infection"

name: Anogenital verrucous carcinoma of Buschke and Lowenstein
definition: An invasive verrucous squamous cell carcinoma usually arising on the prepuce or glans penis of uncircumcised men, although it may occur elsewhere in the anogenital area. It develops from a slow but uncontrolled proliferation of human papilloma virus-infected genital epithelium and presents clinically as a papillomatous cauliflower-like tumour (giant condyloma acuminatum of Buschke and Löwenstein). It can infiltrate deeply and radical surgery may be required to eradicate it.
synonyms: Giant condyloma of Buschke and Löwenstein

name: Burn of abdominal wall, superficial partial thickness burn
Lipoedema of the scalp
definition: Lipoedema of the scalp is a rare condition of unknown aetiology. It is characterised by a thick, boggy, oedematous scalp which may reach double its normal thickness due to expansion of the subcutaneous fat layer. Varying degrees of hair loss may accompany these changes (lipoedematous alopecia). No associated medical or physiological abnormalities have been identified. The primary pathology is likely to be related to abnormal lymphatics.
synonyms: Lipoedematous scalp

name: Skin lesion of uncertain nature
definition: This denotes the presence of a skin lesion but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.
synonyms: Cutaneous neoplasm of uncertain nature | Neoplasm of skin of uncertain nature
cn_name: 性质未确定的皮肤损害

name: Ichthyoses
definition: Genetically-determined and acquired disorders of epidermal keratinization characterised by diffuse scaling and/or thickening of the stratum corneum.

name: Erythropoietic protoporphyria due to ferrochelatase deficiency
synonyms: EPP due to ferrochelatase deficiency | FECH-deficient EPP

name: Anogenital ulceration due to Behçet disease
Cartilage-hair hypoplasia
definition: Cartilage-hair hypoplasia is a disease affecting the bone metaphyses causing small stature from birth and is associated with fine, slow growing hair, and primary immune deficiency.

name: Tattoos or tattoo reactions
definition: Tattoos are the result of injection into the skin of insoluble coloured pigments as ornamentation of the body, or of inert materials such as coal dust as a result of superficial trauma or blast injury. They may provoke both allergic and foreign body reactions.

name: Subcutaneous panniculitis-like T-cell lymphoma
definition: Subcutaneous panniculitis-like T-cell lymphoma is a neoplasm of alpha/beta, usually CD8+ T-cells, mainly confined to the subcutis, presenting clinically as subcutaneous nodules which are usually not ulcerated.
synonyms: SPTCL
cn_name: 皮下脂膜炎样T细胞淋巴瘤
snomed: 103682005 "subcutaneous panniculitis-like t-cell lymphoma";404133000 "subcutaneous panniculitis-like t-cell lymphoma"

name: Tingling of skin
definition: A prickling sensation in the skin which may result from external factors such as rain falling on the skin or may be due to transient or permanent peripheral nerve damage.

name: Trichorrhexis nodosa
definition: Trichorrhexis nodosa is a condition characterised by focal weakening of the hair shaft in response to a variety of insults. The multiple microscopic nodules which develop along the hair represent focal fraying of the cortex of the shaft and a weak point where the hair will tend to fracture. Causes include hair straightening procedures, thermal damage from hair-dryers, swimming-pool chemicals, excessive traction (e.g. tight braids) or compulsive hair twisting. It is also a component of Pollitt sy

name: Atrophic surgical scar
definition: A surgical scar in which there is thinning of the skin giving it a wrinkled appearance.

name: Herpes simplex Kaposi varicelliform eruption complicating other dermatosis except atopic eczema
definition: Disseminated cutaneous Herpes simplex infection arising in conditions other than atopic eczema (e.g. in Darier disease).

name: Burn of chest wall, deep partial thickness burn
Cutaneous early yaws
synonyms: Cutaneous yaws, less than five years after infection | early yaws cutaneous macular | early yaws cutaneous maculopapular | early yaws cutaneous papular | early yaws cutaneous, micropapular | framboeside of early yaws | hyperkeratotic palmoplantar yaws | macular early yaws | maculopapular yaws | micropapular early yaws

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to preservatives or biocides
Involvement of eyelid by dermatosis classified elsewhere
definition: Involvement of eyelid by skin diseases such as psoriasis or lichen planus.

name: Darier disease
definition: Darier disease is a keratinization disorder characterised by the development of keratotic papules in seborrhoeic areas and specific nail anomalies. Skin lesions include greasy and coloured (yellow-brown or brown) keratotic papules, which may be isolated or grouped forming plaques that often become infected and malodorous, and are responsible for major discomfort. Palms and soles frequently exhibit small pits or punctate keratoses that are specific for Darier disease.
synonyms: Darier disease (MIM 124200) | Darier-White disease | Dyskeratosis follicularis | Keratosis follicularis | darier-white disease | dyskeratosis follicularis | follicular dyskeratosis | keratosis follicularis | Darier's disease | DARIER-WHITE DISEASE

name: Amniotic bands
definition: Amniotic bands are developmental anomalies thought to be due to a rupture of the amnion during the first trimester of gestation, resulting in adhesions leading to annular constrictions, and bands between torn amnion and chorionic mesenchyme. Besides annular constrictions, pseudo-syndactyly, intra-uterine amputations, and umbilical cord constrictions may also be seen. In addition, the decrease in amniotic fluid and/or a possible tethering of a limb by an amniotic band may result in a decrease in

name: Meibomian gland dysfunction
definition: This refers to the dysfunction of a special kind of sebaceous gland at the rim of the eyelids inside the tarsal plate, responsible for the supply of meibum, an oily substance that prevents evaporation of the eye's tear film. Meibum prevents tear spillage onto the cheek, trapping tears between the oiled edge and the eyeball, and makes the closed lids airtight.

name: Amoebiasis of penis
definition: Penile amoebiasis can present as a purulent balanoposthitis or as an ulcer of the prepuce or glans, when it may simulate a squamous carcinoma. It generally follows penetrative anal intercourse with an individual with intestinal amoebiasis.

name: Pyoderma gangrenosum associated with inflammatory polyarthropathy
definition: Pyoderma gangrenosum associated with inflammatory polyarthropathies including psoriatic arthritis, gastrointestinal arthritis and rheumatoid arthritis.

name: Protein contact dermatitis due to plant protein
definition: Immediate contact dermatitis due to contact with proteins from plants and other vegetable matter. Rubber latex is an important cause.

name: Burn of face except eye or ocular adnexa, superficial partial thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of face except eye and adnexa, superficial second degree
cn_name: 面部（除外眼或眼附属器）浅层部分皮层烧伤

name: Ramon syndrome
synonyms: Cherubism – gingival fibromatosis – epilepsy – mental deficiency – hypertrichosis – stunted growth (Ramon) syndrome

name: Plantar cerebriform collagenoma
definition: A form of collagenoma presenting as a cerebriform plaque on the sole of the foot and often associated with Proteus syndrome.

name: Odonto-onycho-dermal dysplasia
definition: Odonto-onycho-dermal dysplasia is a form of ectodermal dysplasia characterised by hyperkeratosis and hyperhidrosis of the palms and soles, atrophic malar patches, hypodontia, conical teeth, onychodysplasia, and dry and sparse hair. Less than 15 cases have been described so far. Transmission is autosomal recessive. Homozygous mutations have been described in the WNT10A gene in six patients born to three consanguineous families.
synonyms: OODD (MIM 257980) | schopf-schulz-passarge syndrome

name: Cholestasis - lymphoedema syndrome
definition: Cholestasis-lymphoedema syndrome is characterised by chronic severe lymphoedema and severe neonatal cholestasis that usually lessens during early childhood and becomes episodic. It has been described in six large families, mainly from Norway. The common feature of this autosomal recessive condition is a generalised lymphatic anomaly.
synonyms: Aagenæs syndrome

name: Disseminated gonococcal infection
definition: Disseminated gonococcal infection occurs when there is bacteremic dissemination of Neisseria gonorrhoeae from its initial focus of infection mainly in female pelvic organs. It manifests as pain and swelling around one or more joints, intermittent crops of erythematous papules and pustules on the limbs, fever and rigors. Blood cultures may be but are not always positive.
synonyms: Gonococcal bloodstream infection | arthritis-dermatosis syndrome

name: Myeloproliferative disorder associated vasculitis
definition: There are very few reports about myeloproliferative disorders complicated by vasculitis, found mainly in cutaneous blood vessels. Use of corticosteroids or chemotherapy for the underlying malignancy inconsistently affects the clinical course of the vasculitis. Myeloproliferative disorders should be considered among disorders with cutaneous vasculitis.

name: Lobomycosis
definition: A disease of the skin, caused by an infection with the fungi Lacazia loboi. This disease commonly presents with dermal nodules (either lenticular or in plaques), keloids, subcutaneous mycoses, or malignant tumours. Transmission is commonly by direct contact with contaminated water, soil, vegetation, or may be by direct contact with an infected dolphin. Confirmation is by identification of Lacazia loboi in a lesion exudate or tissue sample.
synonyms: Jorge Lobo's disease | Lobo disease | keloidal blastomycosis | lacaziosis | lobo's disease | Cutaneous blastomycosis | Cutaneous lobomycosis | infection by Loboa loboi

name: Drug-induced telogen hair loss
definition: Telogen hair loss due to drug. Many drugs may occasionally cause telogen hair loss. Commonly implicated drugs include retinoids and anticonvulsants.
cn_name: 药物引起的休止期脱发

name: Neonatal ecthyma gangrenosum
definition: A necrotizing infection of the skin and soft tissues of the newborn due to Pseudomonas aeruginosa.

name: Adnexal carcinoma of skin
definition: A carcinoma arising from the sebaceous glands, sweat glands, or the hair follicles. Representative examples include sebaceous carcinoma, apocrine carcinoma, eccrine carcinoma, and pilomatrical carcinoma.
synonyms: Appendageal carcinoma of skin | Malignant neoplasm of skin appendage | Microcystic adnexal carcinoma | Primary cutaneous adenoid cystic carcinoma | Primary cutaneous mucinous carcinoma | Spiradenocarcinoma | Tubular carcinoma of the skin | malignant spiradenoma | MAC | sclerosing sweat duct carcinoma

name: Noninfectious colitis due to IgA vasculitis
definition: This refers to a non-infective inflammation and ulcer of the large intestine, due to due to a immune-mediated vasculitis, Henoch-Schönlein purpura, that also often affects skin and kidney.

name: Dermatophytosis due to Epidermophyton floccosum
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Epidermophyton floccosum, a common cause of tinea pedis

name: Follicular occlusion tetrad
definition: The combination of major acne, pilonidal sinus, chronic scalp folliculitis, and hidradenitis suppurativa.
synonyms: dissecting cellulitis of the scalp, hidradenitis suppurativa and pilonidal cysts, and acne conglobata
snomed: 404176003 "follicular occlusion tetrad";404176003 "Follicular occlusion tetrad - hidradenitis, acne conglobata, dissecting cellulitis, pilonidal sinus"

name: Sphingolipidoses with skin manifestations
definition: A heterogeneous group of conditions including Fabry disease and Gaucher disease types 1 and 2 (see Liver disease due to disorders of lysosomal storage) which may present with skin manifestations.

name: Stoma-related chronic papillomatous dermatitis
definition: Papillomatous skin reaction provoked by prolonged irritant contact dermatitis related to stoma or fistula.

name: Allergic contact blepharoconjunctivitis
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis affecting the eyelid and conjunctivae.
synonyms: Allergic contact dermatitis of eyelid | Allergic contact eczema of eyelid | Contact and allergic dermatitis of eyelid

name: Progressive hemifacial atrophy
definition: Progressive facial hemiatrophy (Parry–Romberg syndrome) is a rare acquired disorder of unknown aetiology with onset most commonly during childhood. Progressive atrophy of the skin, soft tissues and bones of one half of the face may be accompanied by ocular, dental and neurological problems. Its relationship to linear morphoea of the face and scalp (morphoea \"en coup de sabre\") has not been fully elucidated. The former tends not to involve the scalp and forehead and in the latter there is more
synonyms: Parry-Romberg syndrome | Romberg hemifacial atrophy | hemifacial atrophy | progressive hemifacial atrophy

name: Asteatosis cutis
definition: Cracking and crazing of the epidermal stratum corneum with associated reticulate erythema. It occurs particularly on the lower legs. It is more common in the elderly and is provoked by a combination of defatting and desiccation of the epidermis. It may progress to a more inflammatory stage, asteatotic eczema. \[Dermatology TAG\]

name: Johnson neuroectodermal syndrome
definition: Johnson neuroectodermal syndrome is characterised by alopecia, anosmia or hyposmia, conductive deafness with malformed ears and microtia and/or atresia of the external auditory canal, and hypogonadotropic hypogonadism.

name: Hereditary universal dyschromatosis
definition: A pigmentation disease characterized by reticulate hyper- and hypo-pigmentated macules in a generalized distribution.
synonyms: DUH1 - [Dyschromatosis universalis hereditaria] (MIM 127500) | Dyschromatosis universalis hereditaria | DUH

name: Primary cutaneous follicle centre lymphoma
definition: A primary lymphoma of the skin composed of various numbers of small and large irregular neoplastic follicle center cells. Its morphologic pattern can be nodular, diffuse, or nodular and diffuse. It presents with solitary or grouped plaques and tumours, and it usually involves the scalp, forehead, or trunk. It rarely involves the legs. This type of cutaneous lymphoma tends to remain localised to the skin, and it has a favorable prognosis.
synonyms: PCFCL
cn_name: 原发性皮肤滤泡中心淋巴瘤
snomed: 404143002 "primary cutaneous follicle centre lymphoma";419662008 "primary cutaneous follicle centre lymphoma"

name: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa, late onset
definition: Late-onset junctional epidermolysis bullosa is a subtype of junctional epidermolysis bullosa presenting in childhood or young adulthood
synonyms: Epidermolysis bullosa progressiva | JEB - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa] late onset | JEB-LO - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa late onset]

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, knee joint
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, knee joint

name: Granulomatous dermatophytosis
definition: Granulomatous dermatophytosis (Majocchi granuloma) is a deep folliculitis due to deep penetration of dermatophytes, usually affecting the lower legs and most commonly caused by Trichophyton rubrum. It may be promoted by shaving the legs or by the inappropriate use of potent topical corticosteroids in the presence of unsuspected tinea. It can also develop as a result of impaired immunity.
synonyms: Majocchi granuloma

name: Irritant contact dermatitis due to incontinence
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis from prolonged contact with urine or faeces as a result of incontinence.
synonyms: Incontinence-associated dermatitis | Irritant contact eczema due to incontinence
cn_name: 尿失禁引起的刺激性接触性皮炎

name: Complete trisomy 21, translocation
synonyms: chromosome; 21, trisomy, translocation | translocation down syndrome | trisomy 21 - translocation | trisomy syndrome, 21, translocation

name: Chronic skin disorder of uncertain or unspecified nature
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder (of at least six weeks' duration) of unknown, uncertain or unspecified nature.
synonyms: Chronic rash | Chronic skin disorder without established diagnosis
cn_name: 性质未确定或未特指的慢性皮肤疾病

name: Dermal nerve sheath myxoma
definition: A benign neoplasm arising from nerve sheaths. It is characterised by the presence of a myxoid stroma.
synonyms: Cutaneous lobular neuromyxoma | Myxomatous perineurioma

name: Erythema nodosum provoked by bacterial infection
definition: Erythema nodosum which can be attributed to distant bacterial infection. The most commonly implicated organisms are M. tuberculosis, Streptococcus pyogenes, Mycoplasma pneumoniae and Yersinia enterocolitica.

name: Skin fragility-woolly hair syndrome
definition: A rare autosomal recessive form of suprabasal epidermolysis bullosa due to mutations in the DSP gene encoding desmoplakin. It presents in early infancy with cutaneous, erosions and crusts, sparse woolly hair and palmoplantar keratoderma.
synonyms: Desmoplakin deficiency | EBS-desmoplakin

name: Developmental hamartomata of the epidermis and epidermal appendages
definition: Keratinocytic, pilosebaceous, eccrine, apocrine and other complex hamartomatous malformations of the skin.
cn_name: 表皮及表皮附属器发育性错构瘤

name: Angina bullosa haemorrhagica
definition: Angina bullosa haemorrhagic is a condition characterised by acute, benign, and generally subepithelial oral mucosal blisters filled with blood that are not attributable to a systemic disorder or defect of haemostasis.

name: Ackerman syndrome
definition: Ackerman syndrome is characterised by pyramidal molar roots and taurodontism, associated with variable anomalies.

name: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum persicolor
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Microsporum persicolor, the main natural hosts of which are voles.

name: Generalised inflammatory peeling skin syndrome
definition: A peeling skin syndrome that has_material_basis_in homozygous mutation in the CDSN gene on chromosome 6p21.33.
synonyms: Peeling skin syndrome, type B | generalized inflammatory peeling skin syndrome | inflammatory peeling skin syndrome | peeling skin syndrome type B | PSS1

name: Cutaneous anthrax
definition: Cutaneous anthrax caused by bacteria called Bacillus anthracis occurs when anthrax touches a cut or scrape on the skin which is one of the three main routes. Symptoms of cutaneous anthrax are blister or ulcer that later forms a black scab; the blister or ulcer is usually surrounded by a lot of swelling.
synonyms: charbon | malignant carbuncle of skin due to anthrax | malignant pustule of skin due to anthrax | milzbrand

name: Atopic eczema, photoaggravated
definition: A minority of patients with atopic eczema experience an exacerbation rather than amelioration of eczema on exposure to sunlight or other sources of ultraviolet radiation.
synonyms: Atopic dermatitis, photoaggravated

name: Latent late syphilis
definition: Syphilis (acquired) without clinical manifestations, with positive serological reaction and negative spinal fluid test, two years or more after infection.
synonyms: Syphilis positive serology, late or 2 years or more after infection

name: Oral-facial-digital syndrome type 9
definition: Oral-facial-digital syndrome, type 9 is characterised by highly arched palate with bifid tongue and bilateral supernumerary lower canines, hamartomatous tongue, multiple frenula, hypertelorism, telecanthus, strabismus, broad and/or bifid nasal tip, short stature, bifid halluces, forked metatarsal, poly- and syndactyly, mild intellectual deficit and specific retinal abnormalities (bilateral optic disc coloboma and retinal dysplasia with partial detachment).

name: Progressive nodular histiocytosis
definition: Progressive nodular histiocytosis is a rare benign histiocytic disorder of unknown aetiology. It is characterised clinically by the development of multiple superficial skin-coloured or reddish-orange papules and deep nodules distribution at random over the body. Lesions may also occur in the oral cavity, larynx and conjunctival mucosa. Although benign, it may be very disfiguring and have a major impact on quality of life.
synonyms: Progressive nodular histiocytoma | Spindle cell xanthogranuloma

name: Polymorphic eruption of pregnancy
definition: Polymorphic eruption of pregnancy is a dermatosis which occurs almost exclusively in primigravidae or women with multiple pregnancy. It is associated with above average weight gain in pregnancy and it is thought that distension of abdominal skin is important in the pathogenesis\: the precise mechanism is not understood. The eruption characteristically first appears in the third trimester of pregnancy as intensely itchy erythematous urticarial papules and plaques in and around the abdominal stria

name: Absence of fingerprints-congenital milia syndrome
definition: A rare syndrome syndrome characterized by neonatal blisters and milia (small white papules, especially on the face) and congenital absence of dermatoglyphics on the hands and feet. It has been reported in two kindreds (one of which contained 13 affected individuals spanning three generations) and in an unrelated individual. Some affected patients also showed bilateral partial flexion contractures of the fingers and toes, and webbing of the toes. The syndrome is inherited as an autosomal dominant

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum A
definition: Xeroderma pigmentosum complementation group A (XPA) is a severe form of xeroderma pigmentosum (XP), a rare photodermatosis predisposing to skin cancers, and shows typical XP manifestations (photosensitivity of skin with burning, freckling, and skin cancers) associated with a spectrum of mild to severe neurological anomalies (e.g. cognitive deterioration, dysarthria, balance disturbance, areflexia) and sometimes delay of growth and sexual development.
synonyms: XPA - [xeroderma pigmentosum A]

name: Lymphoedema due to surgery or radiotherapy
definition: Lymphoedema resulting from damage to draining lymphatics as a result of surgery or radiotherapy.
synonyms: Lymphoedema secondary to surgery or radiotherapy

name: Acrokeratosis paraneoplastica
definition: Acrokeratosis neoplastica is a rare acral psoriasiform dermatosis associated with internal malignancy, most frequently squamous cell carcinoma of the upper aerodigestive tract. It commonly presents before the malignancy has become apparent. It is characterised by symmetrical, scaly, violaceous plaques affecting the skin of the hands and feet with a prominent associated nail dystrophy. In severe cases blisters may form and the nails may be shed. Skin changes may spread to the helices of the ears,
synonyms: Bazex syndrome

name: Tendinous xanthoma
definition: Tendinous xanthomata manifest most commonly as subcutaneous nodules attached to the extensor tendons over the knuckles or the Achilles tendon, though other tendons can sometimes be affected. They are most frequently seen in familial hypercholesterolaemia but may also be associated with secondary hypercholesterolaemia due to prolonged cholestasis.

name: Hereditary mucoepithelial dysplasia
definition: This is a rare autosomal dominant multiepithelial disorder causing systemic maldevelopment of the epithelia and mucus membranes that line the surface of tissues and structures throughout the body, particularly affecting systems affiliated with mucosa, which includes the respiratory, digestive, urinary, reproductive and immune systems.

name: Syphilitic pulmonary regurgitation
definition: This is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is involving pulmonary regurgitation.

name: Bullous impetigo of the napkin area
definition: Bullous impetigo occurring in the napkin (diaper) area. It is a diagnosis to be considered in infants with a napkin eruption.

name: Lipoedema of the lower limbs
definition: This is the commonest presentation of lipoedema and may be confused with or coexist with lymphoedema (see lipo-lymphoedema)

name: Melanosarcoma of unspecified site
synonyms: melanotic sarcoma of unspecified site

name: Contact dermatitis of feet
definition: Contact dermatitis not specified as irritant or allergic affecting the feet.

name: Syphilitic alopecia
definition: Secondary syphilis may result in a “moth-eaten” alopecia of the scalp, resembling a rather diffuse and patchy alopecia areata; other body areas, especially the eyebrows and beard, may be affected. Less commonly, diffuse hair loss occurs in the absence of other cutaneous signs of syphilis and resembling the diffuse hair loss of telogen effluvium (essential syphilitic alopecia). Syphilitic alopecia is non-scarring except in the rare nodulo-ulcerative form of secondary syphilis known as lues malign

name: Acute urticaria due to pseudoallergy
definition: Acute urticaria caused by food or drug intolerance. Salicylates are a common cause.

name: Alopecia areata
definition: A microscopically inflammatory, usually reversible, patchy hair loss occurring in sharply defined areas and usually involving the beard or scalp
synonyms: AA - [alopecia areata] | pelade | Circumscribed alopecia

synonyms: stage 2 decubitus ulcer of trochanter

name: Reynolds syndrome
definition: Reynolds syndrome is a rare autoimmune disease, consisting of the combination of primary biliary cirrhosis and systemic sclerosis. In some patients this syndrome has also been associated with Sjögren syndrome and haemolytic anaemia. Typical clinical features include jaundice, elevated blood levels of alkaline phosphatase, calcinosis cutis, telangiectasia and pruritus.

name: Skin disorder attributable to food fads or unhealthy diet
definition: Abnormal skin attributable to insufficient or poor diet not because of disease or lack of provision but an unwillingness or inability to eat a healthy balanced diet. This may result in quite marked skin changes including ichthyosis, purpura, brittle nails and poor hair growth and is seen particularly in association with anorexia nervosa.

name: Ulcer of skin of uncertain nature, head
definition: This denotes the presence of a skin ulcer located on the head but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the ulcer might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Certain skin disorders attributable to bacterial infection
definition: Disorders of the skin and/or subcutaneous tissues caused by bacteria which a) cause infection normally limited to the skin (e.g. erythrasma); b) characteristically involve the skin at the same time as other organs (e.g. syphilis); c) which may cause disease in the skin as well as in other organs (e.g. cutaneous tuberculosis) or d) which infect other organs but which may manifest in the skin as a result of release of toxins or other indirect mechanism (e.g. streptococcal toxic shock syndrome).

name: Ascending bacterial lymphangitis due to Streptococcus pyogenes
Noonan syndrome
definition: Noonan Syndrome is characterised by short stature, facial dysmorphism and congenital heart defects. The main facial features of NS are hypertelorism with down-slanting palpebral fissures, ptosis and low-set posteriorly rotated ears with a thickened helix. The cardiovascular defects most commonly associated with this condition are pulmonary stenosis and hypertrophic cardiomyopathy. Other associated features are webbed neck, chest deformity, mild intellectual deficit, cryptorchidism, poor feeding

name: Endogenous ochronosis
definition: Greyish discolouration of the skin, ear cartilage and sclerae due to accumulation of homogentisic acid granules in the tissues of individuals with defective homogentisic acid oxidase (alkaptonuria).

name: Ichthyosis follicularis – atrichia – photophobia
definition: Ichthyosis follicularis - atrichia - photophobia (IFAP) is a rare genetic disorder characterised by the triad of ichthyosis follicularis, atrichia, and photophobia from birth.
synonyms: IFAP - [Ichthyosis follicularis – atrichia – photophobia] syndrome | ichthyosis follicularis - alopecia - photophobia

name: Lymphoedema-distichiasis syndrome
definition: An autosomal dominant condition with hereditary lower limb lymphoedema and distichiasis, a double row of eyelashes. The latter can result in corneal irritation. Mutations in the FOXC2 gene are implicated in this condition. Up to one third have associated ptosis.
synonyms: LDS - [lymphoedema distichiasis syndrome]

name: Dyskeratosis congenita
definition: Dyskeratosis congenita (DC) is a multisystem disorder characterised by the mucocutaneous triad of abnormal skin pigmentation, nail dystrophy and mucosal leucoplakia. A variety of other abnormalities have been reported (dental, gastrointestinal, genitourinary, hair greying/loss, immunological, neurological, ophthalmic, pulmonary and skeletal). Bone marrow (BM) failure is the principal cause of early mortality with an additional predisposition to malignancy and fatal pulmonary complications.
synonyms: zinsser-engman-cole syndrome | DKCD

name: Pubic infestation by Pthirus pubis
definition: Infestation of human pubic hair by the crab louse, Pthirus pubis.
synonyms: Pediculosis pubis | Phthyriasis pubis | Pthiriasis of pubic hair | Pubic crab-louse infestation | Pubic infestation by Phthiriasis | Pubic lice | Pubic louse infestation

name: Miliaria rubra
definition: A syndrome of cutaneous changes associated with sweat retention and extravasation of sweat at different levels in the skin resulting from apocrine duct obstruction. The sweat then seeps into the epidermis, producing pruritic erythematous papulovesicles. Also known as prickly heat.
synonyms: Heat rash | Prickly heat | intermediate ductal occlusion | prickly heat

name: Multiple carboxylase deficiency due to biotinidase deficiency
definition: Biotinidase deficiency is a late-onset form of multiple carboxylase deficiency (see this term), an inborn error of biotin metabolism that, if untreated, is characterised by seizures, breathing difficulties, hypotonia, skin rash, alopecia, hearing loss and delayed development.

name: Calcification of skin and subcutis due to metabolic disturbance

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 6
definition: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 6 (MPS VI) is a lysosomal storage disease with progressive multisystem involvement, associated with a deficiency of arylsulfatase B (ASB) leading to the accumulation of dermatan sulfate. The disorder shows a wide spectrum of symptoms from slowly to rapidly progressing forms.
synonyms: ARSB - [Arylsulfatase B] deficiency | Arylsulfatase B deficiency | Maroteaux-Lamy syndrome | N-acetylgalactosamine 4-sulfatase deficiency

name: Trichoepithelioma of eyelids
definition: These are benign cutaneous adnexal neoplasms with follicular differentiation. They may occasionally simulate basal cell carcinoma clinically. The tumours are typically small, firm, dome-shaped skin-coloured papules located on the cheeks, eyelids and around the nose. Numerous tumours may develop in the familial form (Multiple familial trichoepithelioma).
synonyms: trichoblastoma of eyelashes

name: Pyoderma gangrenosum without identifiable trigger

definition: Febrile ulceronecrotic Mucha-Habermann disease (FUMHD) is a rare fulminant form of pityriasis lichenoides et varioliformis (PLEVA) in which large necrotic cutaneous lesions are accompanied by lymphadenopathy, general malaise, myalgia, neuropsychiatric symptoms and high fever. Most cases occur in adults. As is the case with PLEVA, the cause is unknown.
synonyms: FUNMH - [Febrile ulceronecrotic Mucha-Habermann disease]

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, arthrochalasic type

Other signs include hyperextensible skin, fragile tissues with atrophic scars, and muscular hypotonia.
synonyms: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type 7

name: Oral mucosal involvement by immunobullous disorder classified elsewhere

name: Keratinocytic epidermal hamartoma
definition: Keratinocytic epidermal hamartoma or epidermal naevus is a congenital hamartomatous epidermal malformation composed of keratinocytes. It is thought to arise as a result of somatic mutation\: early embryonic mutations can give rise to extensive systematised naevi, though typically epidermal naevi are localised linear papillomatous or verrucous plaques. Histologically they exhibit acanthosis, papillomatosis and acanthosis.

synonyms: Rat-bite fever, unspecified

name: Conidiobolomycosis
definition: Conidiobolomycosis is a subcutaneous infection involving nasal mucosa and paranasal sinuses, leading to formation of firm, subcutaneous nodules or polyps. The infection may be acquired via inhalation of spores or a minor trauma such as an insect bite. The infected host is frequently an otherwise healthy individual working outdoors in tropical areas. Conidiobolomycosis can, however, cause major facial disfigurement. In individuals with impaired immune responses more invasive and potentially fatal
synonyms: Entomophthoromycosis conidiobolae | Rhinoentomophthoromycosis

definition: Congenital generalised lipodystrophy is a rare autosomal recessive disease, characterised by almost complete absence of adipose tissue at birth or during early infancy. Main clinical features are generalised muscular hypertrophy and consequently a markedly muscular appearance, severe insulin resistance and hypertriglyceridaemia. synonyms: congenital lipomyodystrophy

name: Female pattern hair loss
definition: Female pattern hair loss differs from male pattern hair loss not only in being generally less pronounced than in men but also by the fact that the normal frontal hair line is usually preserved. A quarter of women will develop clinically detectable pattern hair loss by the age of 70. In the majority of cases it can be attributed to the effects of androgens.

It is characterised by large numbers of poorly-defined macules, papules and plaques with a tendency to be distributed symmetrically. Neuropathy tends to be of a diffuse \"glove and stocking\" type. Initiation of treatment, however, may be accompanied by recovery of cell-mediated immunity and a risk

It is characterised by generalised blistering and crusting with mild mottled pigmentary changes.

definition: Chronic cutaneous lupus erythematosus (LE) is characterised by the presence of circumscribed cutaneous plaques showing varying degrees of oedema, erythema, scaling, follicular plugging and atrophy. It most commonly presents as discoid plaques involving the face, ears and scalp but can be widely disseminated or may affect predominantly the extremities (chilblain LE) or subcutaneous fat (lupus panniculitis). It can cause marked disfigurement with prominent facial scarring and permanent hair loss.

definition: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, hypermobile form (formerly EDS type III) is the most frequent form of EDS and is characterised by generalised joint hyperlaxity, which is often quite severe (Beighton score above 5) and variable degrees of skin hyperextensibility.
synonyms: Benign joint hypermobility syndrome | Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type 3 | Familial ligamentous laxity | ehlers-danlos syndrome type 3

name: Hereditary follicular keratosis
definition: Heritable skin disorders characterised by abnormal keratinization of the hair follicle.

definition: Human papillomavirus infection of anal mucosa. This is usually the result of inoculation during penetrative sexual activity.

name: Adverse reaction to chemical peel
definition: Any adverse reaction attributable to the use of chemical peels on the skin for cosmetic enhancement. Examples include infection, chemical burns, pustular acneform eruptions, dyspigmentation and scarring. The precise adverse reaction should be documented separately.

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, vascular type
definition: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type IV, also known as the vascular type of Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (EDS), is an inherited connective tissue disorder defined by characteristic facial features (acrogeria) in most patients, translucent skin with highly visible subcutaneous vessels on the trunk and lower back, easy bruising, and severe arterial, digestive and uterine complications, which are rarely, if at all, observed in the other forms of EDS.

name: Naevus of Ito
definition: A circumscribed area of slate-brown or blue macular pigmentation involving the skin supplied by the posterior supraclavicular and the lateral brachial cutaneous nerves, which supply the shoulder, scapular area and upper arm. It is usually unilateral and usually present at birth. It is commonest in the Japanese. Pigmentation persists throughout life.

name: Eczematous drug eruption
definition: Drug eruptions are rarely eczematous in nature and a diagnosis of eczematous drug eruption should be made only when there is convincing evidence that the eczema is related to drug exposure. They may occur as a result of prior topical sensitization to an allergen contained in or which cross reacts with a compound present in a medication administered systemically (systemic allergic contact dermatitis).

name: Generalised eruptive histiocytosis
definition: generalised eruptive histiocytosis s a rare benign cutaneous histiocytosis of unknown aetiology which presents typically in adult life with crops of asymptomatic skin-coloured, brownish or violaceous papules on the face, trunk and proximal extremities that resolve spontaneously to leave residual pigmentation. Histology shows a proliferation of monomorphic histiocytic cells in the upper and mid dermis.

name: Atopic eczema of eyelids
definition: Atopic eczema affecting the eyelids. This is a common manifestation of atopic eczema and can result in a significant impact on normal vision and on well-being.

synonyms: Deep vein thrombosis of femoral vein | Femoral vein thrombosis

name: Piebaldism
definition: Piebaldism is a rare autosomal dominant disorder of melanocyte development. Common characteristics include a congenital white forelock, scattered hypermelanotic and hypomelanotic macules and a triangular shaped hypomelanotic patch on the forehead. Typically, islands of hyperpigmentation are present within and at the border of hypomelanotic areas. Piebaldism differs from albinism in that the affected cells maintain the ability to produce pigment but have that specific function turned off. In albi

name: Cutaneous markers of musculoskeletal disorders
definition: Cutaneous symptoms and signs which may indicate the presence of underlying disorders of the musculoskeletal system, e.g. rheumatoid nodules or pyoderma gangrenosum.

name: Interdigital pilonidal sinus
definition: Interdigital pilonidal sinus is an uncommon, usually occupationally-acquired disorder in which penetration of hair clippings or wool through the skin of the interdigital web space initiates a foreign body reaction, most commonly between the ring and middle fingers. The sinus tracts which then form may remain symptom-free for long periods of time but may become chronically infected and suppurative. The condition is found principally in male barbers and sheep-shearers.
synonyms: Barbers' sinus | Sheep-shearers' sinus | interdigital PNS - [pilonidal sinus]

name: Camptodactyly or streblodactyly

Camptodactyly of fingers is clinically more evident than camptodactyly of toes. Sporadic cases are common; familial camptodactyly may be associated with a number of genetic syndromes such as Blau syndrome. Camptodactyly may not become apparent until adult life. name: Hailey-Hailey disease
definition: Hailey–Hailey disease is a rare hereditary autosomal dominant intraepidermal, blistering disease characterised by recurrent vesicles and erosions, usually affecting the neck, axillae and groins. It is caused by mutations in one copy of ATP2C1, a gene on chromosome 3q21 that encodes the human secretory-pathway Ca2+/Mn2+-ATPase isoform 1 (hSPCA1) on the Golgi membrane.

synonyms: HSV - [herpes simplex virus] otitis externa | Herpesviral infection of external ear | Otitis externa in herpes simplex infection | Otitis externa in herpesviral infection

name: Coumarin necrosis
definition: Coumarin necrosis presents as sudden onset of severe pain and erythema in one or more localised areas of skin (typically of the breast, hip, buttock or thigh) a few days after commencing therapy with warfarin or other coumarin anticoagulant. The process may then rapidly progress to full thickness cutaneous infarction. A temporary imbalance of procoagulant and anticoagulant activity, in particular low levels of protein C, is thought to be responsible.

name: Mansonelliasis due to Mansonella streptocerca
synonyms: Infection due to Mansonella streptocerca | Streptocerciasis | infection by acanthocheilonema streptocerca | infection by dipetalonema streptocerca

name: Niemann-Pick disease type A
definition: Niemann-Pick disease type A is a very severe subtype of Niemann-Pick disease, an autosomal recessive lysosomal disease, and is characterised clinically by onset in infancy or early childhood with failure to thrive, hepatosplenomegaly, and rapidly progressive neurodegenerative disorders.

name: Lipomatosis dolorosa
definition: Lipomatosis dolorosa \[Dercum disease\] is a syndrome largely limited to obese middle-aged women in which multiple painful subcutaneous fatty deposits develop in association with easy bruisability and a symptom complex which includes psychiatric disturbances, fatigue, weakness and musculoskeletal aches. The fatty deposits may be diffuse or nodular in form and may be generalised, localised or juxta-articular in distribution. Pain may be extreme and can occur spontaneously or as a response to pres
synonyms: Adiposis dolorosa | Dercum disease | Neurolipomatosis | dercum disease

name: Sea-blue histiocytosis
synonyms: sea-blue histiocytosis

name: Fibrous or myofibroblastic proliferations in infants or children
Plane xanthoma
definition: Plane (planar) xanthomata are flat cutaneous xanthomata and contrast with eruptive and tuberous xanthomata which present as papules and nodules. They are often completely macular but may develop into elevated plaques. The commonest type is the xanthelasma of the eyelid but also included is the palmar xanthoma and a less common widespread form, diffuse plane xanthoma, which may be mimicked by a clinically similar disorder associated with paraproteinaemia, diffuse normolipidaemic plane xanthomatos
synonyms: Planar xanthoma

name: Acantholytic epidermolysis bullosa
definition: A rare autosomal recessive form of suprabasal epidermolysis bullosa simplex due to mutations in the DSP (6p24) gene encoding desmoplakin, a protein essential for epidermal cohesion. It normally leads to premature death in the neonatal period.
synonyms: EBS-acanth | LAEB | LAEB - [lethal acantholytic epidermolysis bullosa] due to desmoplakin deficiency (MIM 609638) | Lethal acantholytic epidermolysis bullosa

name: Lymphoedema
definition: Swelling due to the excess accumulation of lymph in the tissues caused by inadequate lymph drainage. It typically affects the extremities but may involve any body site. It is disfiguring and increases susceptibility to recurrent infection and local malignancy.
synonyms: Lymphatic oedema

name: Skin infection classified elsewhere due to abnormally virulent or therapy resistant bacteria
definition: Skin infection which is difficult to eradicate because of virulence or resistance to standard treatment.

name: Flea bite
definition: Bites by insects of the order Siphonaptera. Fleas are wingless blood-sucking ectoparasites of mammals and birds. The human flea, Pulex irritans, is rare other than in congested communities with low standards of hygiene. Humans are however commonly bitten by dog, cat or bird fleas, particularly when fleas do not have access to their usual host.

name: Allergic contact dermatitis of feet
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema of feet

name: Vulval melanotic macule
definition: Benign genital melanosis affecting the vulva.

name: Chronic telogen hair shedding secondary to specified cause
definition: Diffuse telogen hair shedding lasting longer than six months secondary to an identified cause. To be a true cause of chronic diffuse telogen hair loss, the relationship between the trigger and the hair loss must be reversible and reproducible. Firm diagnosis requires exclusion of other known causes of shedding (especially androgenetic alopecia) and reversal of the hair loss following correction of the causative factor. Accepted causes of chronic diffuse telogen hair loss are thyroid disorders (b

name: Alopecia - contractures - dwarfism - intellectual deficit
definition: Alopecia-contractures-dwarfism-intellectual deficit syndrome is characterised by the association of alopecia/sparse hair with severe growth retardation of prenatal onset, multiple joint contractures (elbows, fingers, and knees) and fusions of various bones, and microcephaly with intellectual deficit.

name: Borrelial lymphocytoma cutis
definition: Borrelial lymphocytoma cutis is a form of cutaneous Lyme borreliosis seen principally in Europe and most commonly linked to Borrelia afzelii. It typically presents as a solitary nodule or plaque, usually located on the earlobe in children and on the areola of the breast or on the genitals in adults. It is characterised histologically by a dense dermal infiltrate of B-lymphocytes.
synonyms: Lyme borreliosis-associated lymphocytoma cutis

name: Allergic contact dermatitis
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis is an eczematous response provoked by a Type IV delayed immune reaction in the skin to a substance or substances to which the individual has previously been sensitized.
synonyms: ACD - [allergic contact dermatitis] | allergic contact dermatitis, unspecified cause | allergic contact eczema | allergic dermatitis | allergic eczema | allergy dermatitis | allergy eczema | ACD | contact eczema

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type IIA
definition: HSAN Type IIA is an autosomal recessive disorder due to mutations in the WNK1 gene. It presents in early childhood or congenitally with severe distal sensory loss to touch, pain, temperature, acral mutilations and mild or asymptomatic autonomic dysfunction.
synonyms: HSAN2A - [Hereditary sensory autonomic neuropathy type 2A]

name: Talaromycosis
definition: Talaromycosis is an infection due to Talaromyces marneffei, an ubiquitous saprophyte of soil and decomposing organic matter. This dimorphic fungus, formerly known as Penicillium marneffei, is endemic to Southeast Asia and the southern part of China. Once considered rare, its occurrence has increased due to AIDS. It is now the third most common opportunistic infection in HIV-positive individuals. The most common symptoms are fever, skin lesions, anaemia, generalised lymphadenopathy, and hepatomeg
synonyms: Penicilliosis | Penicillosis

name: Toxic erythema
definition: Toxic erythema, also known as an exanthematous reaction, is a descriptive term for an acute widespread usually symmetrical erythematous rash such as may be triggered both by drugs and by infections but which also may be the prodrome of a wide variety of dermatoses. The precise cause can often not be identified. It is characterised by erythema without significant surface changes such as scale, exudation or ulceration. The erythema may be morbilliform (like measles), urticated (resembling urticari
synonyms: Exanthematous rash | Toxic urticated erythema

name: Confluent and reticulated papillomatosis
definition: Confluent and reticulated papillomatosis (CARP) is an uncommon disorder of epidermal keratinization characterised by the development of hyperkeratotic papules which coalesce into confluent and, in places, reticulated plaques on truncal skin. An abnormal reaction to commensal microorganisms has been postulated to play an aetiological role.
synonyms: Gougerot-Carteaud syndrome

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum B
definition: Xeroderma pigmentosum complementation group B (XPB) is an extremely rare subtype of xeroderma pigmentosum (XP), a rare photodermatosis predisposing to skin cancers. Some XPB patients present with classic XP features of varying severity (photosensitivity of skin with burning and freckling, skin and eye tumours) and mild neurological abnormalities, while others combine classical XP features with systemic and neurological manifestations of Cockayne syndrome such as short stature, bilateral sensorin
synonyms: XPB - [xeroderma pigmentosum B]

name: Dermatoses attributable to corticosteroid therapy
cn_name: 糖皮质激素治疗引起的皮肤病

name: White piedra
definition: A disease of the hair shaft, caused by an infection with the fungi Trichosporon beigelii. This disease is characterised by irregular, soft, white, or light brown nodules which adhere to the hair follicle. Transmission is by direct contact with contaminated soil or water, or by airborne transmission. Confirmation is by identification of Trichosporon beigelii in a hair follicle sample.
synonyms: Trichosporosis nodosa

name: Haemoglobinopathy-associated acquired pseudoxanthoma elasticum
definition: Haemoglobinopathy-associated acquired pseudoxanthoma elasticum has been associated with a variety of haemoglobinopathies including sickle cell anaemia, beta-thalassaemia and hereditary spherocytosis. Clinical manifestations are similar to the genetic form but of variable severity and generally of late onset. The pathogenesis is not fully understood.

name: Urticaria, angioedema or other urticarial disorders
definition: A heterogeneous group of disorders characterised by dermal and/or subcutaneous and submucosal oedema. The most common underlying mechanism is release of histamine from mast cells with consequent capillary dilatation and tissue oedema. This is responsible for the weals of spontaneous and most physical urticarias. A variety of other mechanisms are involved in other urticarial disorders.
cn_name: 荨麻疹、血管性水肿或其他荨麻疹类障碍

name: Sequelae of frostbite with tissue necrosis of hip or thigh
Dyschromatosis due to chronic arsenic toxicity
definition: Dyschromatosis from chronic arsenic toxicity presents as hypermelanotic and hypomelanotic symmetrically distributed freckle or raindrop-like macules on the torso and limbs. These may be preceded by diffuse hyperpigmented patches in the intertriginous, genital, and acral areas.
synonyms: arsenic pigmentation

name: Oculocutaneous albinism
definition: Oculocutaneous albinism is a genetically heterogeneous congenital disorder characterised by decreased or absent pigmentation in the hair, skin, and eyes.
synonyms: OCA - [Oculocutaneous albinism] | OCA

name: Hidrocystoma
definition: A hidrocystoma is a cystic cutaneous swelling lined by either apocrine or eccrine ductal epithelium. It presents typically as a small solitary bluish translucent papule on or around the eyelids. It is not always possible to be certain whether it is of apocrine or eccrine origin though the majority show apocrine differentiation.
synonyms: cystadenoma | moll's gland cyst | sudoriferous gland | apocrine cystadenoma | Eccrine Cystadenoma | Eccrine hidrocystoma of skin

name: Adult T-cell leukaemia or lymphoma, skin
definition: Adult T cell leukaemia / lymphoma (ATLL) is a malignancy of mature CD4+/ CD25+ T cells caused by the human T-cell leukaemia virus type I (HTLV-1). The primary cutaneous tumoural type tends to show a favourable course.

name: Auricular tag
definition: Small protrusion within the pinna.

name: Acute or subacute juvenile paracoccidioidomycosis
definition: Acute or subacute juvenile paracoccidioidomycosis is a disease of children in which reticuloendothelial system involvement predominates. Lymphadenopathy and hepatosplenomegaly are usually accompanied by systemic symptoms such as fever, weight loss, and malaise. Multiple skin lesions may be present.

name: Pityriasis rubra pilaris, adult
definition: Pityriasis rubra pilaris with onset in adulthood, the majority of cases of which are the classical type I

name: Human papillomavirus proliferation in immune-deficient states
Melanoma arising from melanocytic naevus, primary
definition: Melanoma arising from within a pre-existing benign melanocytic naevus, especially blue naevi and giant congenital melanocytic naevi.

name: Alopecia areata of eyebrows
definition: Loss of eyebrows (superciliary madarosis) due to alopecia areata

name: Acquired angioedema type I
definition: An acquired disorder which resembles hereditary angioedema but is associated with an underlying disorder, in the majority of cases lymphoproliferative.
synonyms: AAE - [Acquired angioedema type I]

name: Early syphilis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum pallidum, including primary and secondary stages of syphilis, and early latent syphilis of less than 2 years duration. This disease is characterised by a single chancre in the primary stage, and diffuse rash in the secondary stage. Transmission is commonly by sexual contact.
synonyms: Infection due to Treponema pallidum NOS | acquired syphilis | acquired syphilis NOS | conjugal syphilis | early syphilis, unspecified | general syphilis | organic syphilis | syphilis causing death under 2 years of age, stated to be acquired

name: Verruciform xanthoma of oral mucosa
definition: This is an uncommon benign lesion that has a verruciform (wart-like) appearance, but it may appear polypoid, papillomatous, or sessile, on the oral mucosa.

name: Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, type 3
definition: Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, type 3 (De Barsy syndrome) is a rare syndrome characterised by distinctive facial features, cutis laxa and mental retardation. Other distinguishing features include bilateral corneal opacification, progeroid appearance, reduced subcutaneous fat, and athetoid movements early in life. The genetic cause remains poorly defined though there is some evidence that mutations both of ATP6V0A2 and of PYCR1 may be associated with this phenotype.
synonyms: ARCL-3 - [Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, type 3] (MIM 219150) | Cutis laxa - corneal clouding - intellectual deficit | De Barsy syndrome | Progeroid syndrome, De Barsy type | cutis laxa-corneal clouding-intellectual disability syndrome

name: Atrophic scarring of the skin
definition: The process whereby healing of damaged skin results in a reduction of dermal thickness as well as scarring, thus the counterpart of hypertrophic scarring.

name: Purpura due to paroxysmal coughing or vomiting
definition: Petechial haemorrhage into the skin resulting from paroxysmal rises in venous pressure from coughing or vomiting.

name: Preauricular tag
definition: Small non-cartilaginous protrusion anterior to the insertion of the ear.

name: Down syndrome leukemoid reaction-associated transient neonatal pustulosis
definition: An eruption of vesicles and pustules containing blast cells which presents at or immediately after birth in neonates with Down syndrome or trisomy 21 mosaicism and associated transient myeloproliferative disorder. The eruption typically involves the face more than the trunk or extremities. The eruption can thus serve as a pointer to the underlying haematological abnormality.

name: Ulceration of penis or scrotum due to Behçet disease
synonyms: Penile or scrotal ulceration due to Behçet disease

name: Scalp dysaesthesia
definition: An uncommon but distressing somatoform disorder in which there is a clear and precise complaint of scalp pain or burning sensation for which no underlying cause can be found.

name: Complex or combined developmental vascular malformations involving the skin
cn_name: 累及皮肤的复杂性或组合性血管发育畸形

name: Lobular defects
synonyms: Ear lobe creases | Up lifted earlobe

name: Intermediate maple-syrup-urine disease
definition: This is an intermediate autosomal recessive metabolic disorder affecting branched-chain amino acids. It is one type of organic acidemia.
synonyms: Intermediate branched chain ketoaciduria | Partial branched-chain ketoacid dehydrogenase deficiency

name: Drug-induced parastomal ulceration
definition: Parastomal ulceration attributable to drug, most commonly nicorandil.
synonyms: medicament-induced parastomal ulceration

name: Acute maculopapular skin eruption
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists of multiple macules and papules. Viral infections and drugs are common precipitants.

name: Hyperkeratotic fissured palmar dermatitis
definition: A distinct and common form of hand eczema characterised by highly irritable, scaly, fissured, hyperkeratotic patches on the palms and palmar surfaces of the fingers. It may be accompanied by similar changes on the soles of the feet. The aetiopathogenesis is poorly understood.
synonyms: Hyperkeratotic dermatitis of palms | Hyperkeratotic fissured palmar eczema

name: Dermatoses attributable to hyperviscosity or microvascular occlusion
definition: A range of disorders characterised by vascular occlusion but attributable not to primary vascular inflammation but to intravascular occlusion.
cn_name: 归因于高粘滞度或微血管闭塞的皮肤病

name: Postinflammatory hypermelanosis
definition: Melanin pigmentation of the skin resulting from preceding cutaneous inflammation, particularly when this is centred on the dermo-epidermal junction as in lichen planus. Damage to melanocytes results in release of melanin into the dermis (pigmentary incontinence).
synonyms: Postinflammatory hyperpigmentation | PIH | postinflammatory hypermelanosis

name: Occupational tattoo
definition: Tattoo resulting from exposure to inert pigmented particles at work. These commonly occur in coal-miners.

name: Acral lentiginous melanoma, primary
definition: Acral lentiginous malignant melanoma is a distinct and uncommon form of melanoma affecting either palmar and plantar skin or the nail apparatus. It is usually preceded by a slowly progressive in situ phase which may be overlooked. It typically presents either as an enlarging area of macular pigmentation on the palms and soles or as a longitudinal pigmented band within the nail plate. More aggressive tumours present as ulcerated nodules which, when involving the nail apparatus, can destroy the na
cn_name: 原发性肢端着色斑性黑色素瘤

name: Atypical melanocytic naevus
definition: Solitary or multiple, slightly raised, pigmented lesions with irregular borders, usually measuring more than 0.6cm in greatest dimension. Morphologically, there is melanocytic atypia and the differential diagnosis from melanoma may be difficult. Patients are at an increased risk for the development of melanoma.
synonyms: Dysplastic naevus, unspecified

name: Juvenile hereditary haemochromatosis, Type 2
definition: Hemochromatosis type 2 (juvenile) is the early-onset and most severe form of rare hereditary hemochromatosis, a group of diseases characterised by excessive tissue iron deposition of genetic origin. This juvenile form of hemochromatosis has the classical features of HH but is also characterised by severe cardiomyopathy and hypogonadism. Arthropathy, hepatic fibrosis, glucose intolerance, and increased skin pigmentation are frequent.

name: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia
definition: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia (CAH) refers to a group of conditions associated with either complete (classical form) or partial (non-classical) anomalies in the biosynthesis of adrenal hormones. The condition is characterised by insufficient production of cortisol, or of aldosterone (classical form with salt wasting), associated with overproduction of adrenal androgens. In the classical form, metabolic decompensation (dehydration with hyponatraemia, hyperkalaemia and acidosis associated with mi
synonyms: Congenital adrenogenital disorders associated with enzyme deficiency | congenital adrenal cortical hyperplasia | congenital adrenal gland hyperplasia | congenital adrenogenital syndrome | congenital adrenogenitalism | congenital female adrenal pseudohermaphroditism | congenital hyperadrenocorticism | CAH

name: Macrocephaly - capillary malformation
definition: Macrocephaly-capillary malformation (M-CM) is characterised by cutaneous capillary malformation occurring in association with macrocephaly with a tendency to progressive enlargement, abnormalities of somatic growth with body and brain asymmetry, developmental delay, and typical face with full cheeks, frontal bossing, and nevus flammeus of the nose and/or philtrum and upper lip.
synonyms: Macrocephaly - cutis marmorata telangiectatica congenita (MIM 602501)

name: Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, type 1
definition: Type 1 autosomal recessive cutis laxa (ARCL-1) manifests at birth with abnormal facies, redundant folds around the face and neck, an aged appearance, joint laxity and muscular hypotonia. Compared with autosomal dominant cutis laxa, ARCL-I is more often associated with severe systemic complications, especially emphysema, diaphragmatic defects, arterial tortuosity and aneurysms. Many patients die from pulmonary or cardiac complications in early childhood. Mental and motor development are usually n
synonyms: ARCL-1 - [Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, type 1] (MIM 219100) | Autosomal recessive cutis laxa with severe systemic involvement | Autosomal recessive cutis laxa, pulmonary emphysema type | autosomal recessive cutis laxa type 1

name: Black-fly bite
definition: Bites inflicted by simuliid flies, small black flies which breed in areas of fast-flowing water and are found in all parts of the world. Several species of black-fly are responsible for transmission of onchocerciasis. Their bites tend to be painful and can render some areas unpleasant to live or work in at certain times of year.

name: Irritant contact dermatitis due to foods
definition: A non-allergic form of skin inflammation caused by food items such as sugar, flour, fruits, vegetables, meat, fish and spices, as well as other foods.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema due to foods
cn_name: 食物引起的刺激性接触性皮炎

name: Protruding ear
synonyms: Congenital prominent auricle | bat ear | outstanding ears

name: Perioral dermatitis
definition: The most common form of periorificial dermatitis, this manifests as erythema, papules and pustules affecting perioral skin. There is normally a border of spared skin immediately adjacent to the lip margins. It is strongly associated with the use of potent topical corticosteroids.

name: Cauliflower ear
definition: Cauliflower ear is the end result of fibrosis of the skin and soft tissues of the pinna following a traumatic subperichondrial haematoma, usually due to trauma. It manifests as permanent swelling and deformity of the ear, described as resembling a cauliflower. It is found most commonly amongst men involved in contact sports such as boxing, wrestling, martial arts and rugby football.
synonyms: Chronic haematoma of pinna | Deformity of pinna following traumatic perichondrial haematoma | boxer ear | prizefighter ear | wrestler ear | cauliflower ear | wrestler's ear

name: Early syphilis involving the digestive tract
