definition: This is an early sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is involving the digestive tract.

name: Acute febrile neutrophilic dermatosis, paraneoplastic
definition: Sweet disease, often recurrent, associated with underlying myelodysplasia, myelofibrosis, myeloid leukaemia or other form of haematological malignancy.
synonyms: Sweet syndrome associated with malignancy | Sweet syndrome, paraneoplastic

name: Chondrodermatitis nodularis
definition: A common pressure-induced painful nodule or ulcer affecting the external ear. It results from ischaemia of skin and underlying cartilage from a focal point of high pressure from the weight of the recumbent head. The site of involvement is dependent on the shape of the pinna (most commonly the helix is involved but in some individuals it is the antihelix which is more prominent) and whether the sufferer is limited to sleeping on one side and is thus unable to spare the ear from constant pressure
synonyms: Chondrodermatitis nodularis helicis | chondrodermatitis nodularis chronica helicis | chondrodermatitis nodularis chronica helicis et antihelicis | CNH | ear corn
snomed: 76583009 "chondrodermatitis nodularis helicis";393584002 "chondrodermatitis nodularis helicis";194195009 "chondrodermatitis nodularis helicis";194196005 "chondrodermatitis nodularis helicis"

name: Hereditary sclerosing poikiloderma, Weary type
definition: The cardinal features of this rare autosomal dominant condition are generalised poikiloderma with accentuation in flexural regions, sclerosis of palms and soles, linear hyperkeratosis and sclerotic bands in the axillae and antecubital and popliteal fossae, clubbing of the fingers. Tissue calcinosis may occur \[OMIM\].

name: Cutaneous reactions to venomous or noxious aquatic invertebrates
Extramammary Paget disease of anogenital skin
definition: An intraepithelial adenocarcinoma of apocrine gland-bearing anogenital skin. It presents as irritating, itchy, burning, red scaly patches or plaques that may be solitary or multifocal and may be mistaken for an inflammatory dermatosis.

name: Dermatophytosis of trunk or limbs
definition: Dermatophytosis affecting the skin of the trunk and limbs usually presents with one or more expanding scaly annular plaques. Similar appearances may be due to a variety of non-infectious inflammatory dermatoses including eczema, psoriasis and annular erythemas. If possible, the diagnosis should therefore be confirmed by appropriate mycological investigation before being accepted as definitive.
synonyms: Ringworm of trunk and limbs | Tinea circinata | Tinea corporis | dermatophytosis of the body | Dermatophytosis of the trunk

name: Primary anal syphilis
synonyms: Anal syphilitic chancre | primary syphilis of anus

name: Sabinas brittle hair syndrome
definition: Sabinas brittle hair syndrome is a form of nonphotosensitive trichothiodystrophy characterised by brittle hair, neuroectodermal dysplasia and a low-sulphur content in the hair.
synonyms: Brittle hair - mental deficiency | Trichothiodystrophy type B

name: Cutaneous erysipeloid
definition: A skin infection due to the bacterium Erysipelothrix rhusiopathiae. It is most commonly due to direct trauma allowing the bacterium to enter the skin from infected meat. Rarely the infection may become disseminated over the skin surface with multiple lesions.

name: Capillaritis
definition: Capillaritis results from extravasation of red blood cells from leaky capillaries into the dermis and manifests initially as a finely stippled pink to purple purpura, most commonly affecting the lower limbs. As iron is released and converted into haemosiderin, the skin stains gold or brown. Various patterns of capillaritis have been described and given separate names depending on the distribution, time course, extent and degree of pigmentation and presence or otherwise of epidermal thickening. T
synonyms: Pigmented purpura | Pigmented purpuric dermatosis | Progressive pigmented purpura | Purpura simplex | capillaritis | majocchi-schamberg disease | purpura pigmentosa chronica | purpura simplex

name: Chemical burn of skin categorized by depth of injury
Xerostomia due to disturbance of salivary secretion
definition: Xerostomia resulting from hyposalivation caused by salivary gland dysfunction rather than suppression. This may be due to surgery, radiotherapy or inflammation (Sjögren disease or sicca syndrome).

name: Chronic hyperplastic oral candidosis
definition: A form of oral candidosis characterised by very persistent firm irregular white plaques on the buccal mucosa or tongue. In contrast to pseudomembranous oral candidosis the plaques cannot be easily removed. It may resemble other forms of oral leukoplakia.
synonyms: Candida leukoplakia | Chronic plaque oral candidosis

name: Solitary median maxillary central incisor syndrome
definition: Presence of a single maxillary central incisor possibly caused by a mutation in the Sonic hedgehog gene

name: Vasculitis affecting medium or large cutaneous blood vessels
definition: A heterogeneous group of disorders characterised by vasculitis centred on medium/large cutaneous blood vessels.

name: Proximal subungual onychomycosis
definition: Proximal subungual onychomycosis is the least common form onychomycosis in healthy persons. It occurs when the infecting organism, usually Trichohyton rubrum, invades the nail unit through the proximal nail fold, penetrating the soft, newly formed nail plate before then migrating distally. Fingernails and toenails are equally affected. It usually occurs in immunocompromised persons, especially in association with HIV, but can develop following local trauma.

name: Indeterminate leprosy
definition: The early stages of leprosy following contact with M. leprae may have few signs and symptoms other than subtle slightly hypopigmented or erythematous macules on the skin. At this stage it is often not possible to know whether it will progress towards tuberculoid or lepromatous leprosy and it is therefore known as indeterminate leprosy. Nerve damage, either sensory or motor, can occur in this stage and may be the presenting sign \[Dermatology TAG\].
synonyms: Hansen disease, indeterminate

name: Acute purpuric skin eruption
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists predominantly of disseminated purpura. Potential causes are numerous and include thrombocytopenia, coagulopathies, vasculitides and sepsis.
synonyms: Acute haemorrhagic skin eruption | Acute petechial skin eruption

name: Keloidal acne
definition: The development of keloidal papules, nodules and plaques at sites of inflammatory acne. The most commonly affected sites are the upper back, shoulders and presternal area.
synonyms: Acne keloid

name: Nail involvement in miscellaneous specified disorders
Acquired hypomelanotic disorders
definition: Acquired disorders characterised by diminution or loss of pigment from the skin. The most important of these is vitiligo.
cn_name: 获得性色素脱失性疾病

name: Syndromic epidermolysis bullosa
definition: A small group of disorders including Kindler syndrome in which skin blistering is associated with other defects.
cn_name: 综合征性大疱性表皮松解症

name: Actinic lichen planus
definition: Actinic lichen planus is a rare variant of cutaneous lichen planus characterised by the development of photo-distributed lichenoid lesions.
synonyms: lichen planus actinicus | lichen planus subtropicus | lichen planus tropicus | lichenoid melanodermatitis

name: Rapp-Hodgkin syndrome
definition: Rapp-Hodgkin syndrome (RHS) is characterised by the association of anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia with cleft lip/palate.
synonyms: ankyloblepharon-ectodermal defects-cleft lip/palate syndrome | hay-wells syndrome | rapp-hodgkin syndrome

name: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton violaceum
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton violaceum and typically causing on endothrix infection of scalp hair.

name: Foot eczema
definition: Eczema involving the feet which cannot be more precisely categorised either by morphology or by aetiology.

name: Lipschütz ulcer of vulva
definition: Lipschütz ulcer or acute ulceration of the vulva is a rare disease characterised by painful genital ulcers, fever, and lymphadenopathy, occurring most commonly in adolescents and young women. It is not a sexually transmitted disease and is often misdiagnosed. The most common presentation is a single large, deep ulcer on the internal surface of one or both labia minora.
synonyms: Acute ulceration of the vulva | Ulcus vulvae acutum

name: Congenital ectropion, bilateral
definition: This is an eversion of the edges of eyelids that occurs in both eyes and is present since birth, resulting in the exposure of the palpebral conjunctiva.

name: Dermatophytosis of hand
definition: Dermatophyte (tinea) infection of the hand. Trichophyton rubrum produces a characteristic dry scaling of the palm which may pass unrecognised for many years. Dermatophytosis of the fingernails often accompanies dermatophytosis of the hands.
synonyms: Ringworm of the hand | Tinea manus | Tinea manuum | Dermatophytosis of hand

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, radius
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, radius

name: Sequelae of burn or corrosion of hip or lower limb, except ankle or foot
Chronic actinic dermatitis
synonyms: PD/AR syndrome | persistent light reaction | photosensitivity dermatitis and actinic reticuloid syndrome

name: Pruritus due to antimalarials
definition: Pruritus induced by chloroquine or other antimalarials.

name: Linear lichen planus
definition: Linear lichen planus (LLP), also referred to as Blaschkoid LP, is a rare type of lichen planus characterised by a linear distribution of lichenoid lesions along the lines of Blaschko, which are embryonic pathways of skin development.

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to metals or metal salts
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to metals and metal salts (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to metals and metal salts).
synonyms: Metal allergy

name: Adverse cutaneous reactions to herbal, homoeopathic or other alternative therapies
definition: These may range from \"drug\" eruptions, phototoxicity, contact allergy to skin infections and scarring. (The primary code should be the adverse cutaneous reaction but this may be used to add supplementary information.)
cn_name: 对中草药、同种疗法或其他替代疗法的不良皮肤反应

name: Carbuncle
definition: A deep follicular pyogenic staphylococcal skin infection involving a group of adjacent hair follicles. It manifests as a painful boggy mass containing multiple purulent discharging sinuses.

name: Scleroedema
definition: Scleroedema is characterised by diffuse induration of the dermis and subcutis normally affecting just the upper half of the body. The woody infiltration of the skin is due both to infiltration by hyaluronan and to increased collagen synthesis. Histologically the epidermis is unaffected but the dermis is thickened with swelling and separation of collagen bundles and variable amounts of mucin deposited in the deep dermis and subcutis. There are several distinct clinical forms of scleroedema with d

name: Ichthyosis – hypotrichosis syndrome
definition: Ichthyosis hypotrichosis syndrome is characterised by congenital ichthyosis and hypotrichosis.

name: Muckle Wells Syndrome with neurosensorial hearing loss
Congenital syphilis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum pallidum in utero. This disease may present with clinical signs depending on the stage of disease. Transmission is by vertical transmission.
synonyms: congenital syphilis, unspecified | hereditary syphilis | heredosyphilis

name: Developmental defects of hair or nails
cn_name: 毛发或指（趾）甲发育缺陷

name: Keratosis pilaris
definition: Keratosis pilaris is a very common abnormality of keratinization characterised by keratinous plugging of follicular orifices with varying degrees of perifollicular erythema. It is seen in up to half of normal children and in three quarters of children with ichthyosis vulgaris. The sides of the face and the extensor surfaces of the upper arms are sites of predilection. Autosomal dominant inheritance can often be demonstrated. In some variants atrophy or pigmentation may be more prominent than ker

name: Abscess of eyelid
synonyms: lid abscess

name: Erythema induratum of Bazin
definition: Erythema induratum of Bazin is a form of lobular panniculitis occurring on the calves of individuals with tuberculosis. Subcutaneous nodules develop, particularly after exposure to cold, and may break down to form irregular ulcers. The condition is seen most commonly in women.
synonyms: bazin's disease | erythema induratum of bazin

name: Congenital extramedullary dermal haematopoiesis
definition: Congenital extramedullary haematopoiesis may present in the skin as widely distributed erythematous or violaceous papules, nodules and plaques which have given the skin an appearance which has been likened to a blueberry muffin. It is most commonly associated with congenital TORCH infection (toxoplasmosis, rubella, cytomegalovirus and herpes virus) but may be seen with other congenital infections, with severe prenatal anaemias and with certain congenital malignancies.
synonyms: Blueberry muffin baby

name: Paediatric onset systemic sclerosis
definition: Systemic sclerosis arising before the age of 16. Involvement of internal organs is less common but arthritis and myositis are more common than in adults.

name: Curly hair – ankyloblepharon – nail dysplasia syndrome
definition: CHAND is an acronym for Curly Hair-Ankyloblepharon-Nail Dysplasia syndrome and belongs to the group of ectodermal dysplasias. Patients had curly woollen scalp hair, partial fusion of the eyelids (ankyloblepharon), and hypoplastic nails.
synonyms: Baughman syndrome | CHANDS - [Curly hair - ankyloblepharon - nail dysplasia syndrome]

name: Familial partial lipodystrophy associated with PLIN1 mutations
definition: This refers to an autosomal dominant skin condition characterised by the loss of subcutaneous fat. This diagnosis is associated with PLIN1 mutations.

name: Respiratory disorders in IgA vasculitis
definition: This encompasses pathological conditions affecting the organs and tissues that make gas exchange possible in higher organisms, and includes conditions of the upper respiratory tract, trachea, bronchi, bronchioles, alveoli, pleura and pleural cavity, and the nerves and muscles of breathing. This diagnosis is in a disease of the skin and other organs that most commonly affects children.

name: Rothmund-Thomson syndrome type 1
definition: Rothmund-Thomson syndrome type 1 differs from Rothmund-Thomson syndrome type 2 in that it is not associated with mutations in the RECQL4 gene or with an increased risk of osteosarcoma and squamous cell carcinoma.

name: Adult atopic eczema, chronic lichenified
definition: Chronic atopic eczema in adulthood characterised by thickened, lichenified skin.
synonyms: Adult atopic dermatitis, chronic lichenified | Lichenified atopic eczema of the adult

name: Painful dorsolateral fissure of the fingertip
Inherited deformities of nails
definition: Genetically-determined abnormalities of nail development.

name: Neonatal scurvy
definition: Breast milk contains sufficient vitamin C to prevent deficiency throughout infancy. Infants consuming pasteurized or boiled animal milk are at significant risk of developing deficiency. Neonates whose feeding has been delayed because of clinical conditions can also suffer from ascorbic acid deficiency.

name: Oesophageal varices with bleeding
synonyms: BOV - [bleeding oesophageal varices] | bleeding oesophageal varices | bleeding oesophageal varicose ulcer | bleeding oesophageal varicose vein | bleeding oesophageal varix | bleeding varicose oesophagus ulcer | oesophageal variceal bleeding | oesophageal variceal haemorrhage | oesophageal varices with haemorrhage | oesophageal varix haemorrhage

name: Ant bites or sting
definition: The bites of many species of ant may cause local reactions in human skin. Certain ant species inject venom into the skin which, in the case of bullet ants and fire ants, can produce intense local pain and swelling. Anaphylactic reactions to ant venom are uncommon but well recognised.

name: Diffuse primary cutaneous mucinosis
definition: Forms of primary cutaneous mucinosis in which there is diffuse infiltration of the dermis by mucin.

name: Hypomelanosis of Ito
definition: A variant of hypomelanotic pigmentary mosaicism characterised by extensive hypomelanotic streaks and whorls.
synonyms: HI | incontinentia pigmenti achromians | linear nevoid hyperpigmentation | pigmentary mosaicism | Bloch-Siemans syndrome | Incontinentia pigmenti achromians syndrome | Ito's nevus | nevus of Ito
snomed: 218358001 "hypomelanosis of ito";254202001 "hypomelanosis of ito";254816001 "hypomelanosis of ito";367520004

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, wrist joint
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, wrist joint

name: Acquired angioedema type II
definition: An acquired autoimmune disorder which resembles hereditary angioedema but is due to antibodies directed against C1 inhibitor (C1-INH).
synonyms: AAE-II - [Acquired angioedema type II]

name: Invasive melanoma of the nail apparatus
Deep full thickness burns or corrosion
Benign cutaneous neoplasms
definition: Abnormal growth of the cells that comprise the tissues of the skin, without any evidence of malignancy.

name: Beare-Stevenson cutis gyrata syndrome
definition: Beare-Stevenson cutis gyrata syndrome consists of skin furrows of corrugated appearance, acanthosis nigricans, craniofacial anomalies, particularly craniosynostosis and ear defects, anogenital anomalies, skin tags, and prominent umbilical stump. Cutis verticis gyrate variably affects the scalp, forehead, face, pre-auricular area, neck, trunk, hands and feet. Performance and life expectancy appear to be related to the presence or absence of cloverleaf skull.

name: Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia
definition: Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia is a relatively common immune-mediated disorder with the potential for serious thromboembolic complications. It is associated with the use of unfractionated heparin (UFH) and may be defined as a decrease in platelet count during or shortly after exposure to this anticoagulant.
synonyms: Heparin-induced thrombosis/thrombocytopenia syndrome

name: Recurrent venous leg ulcer
definition: A venous leg ulcer developing in skin which has been damaged by previous episodes of ulceration. The chances of long-term healing are reduced in comparison with primary venous leg ulcers.

name: Fixed drug eruption
definition: The term fixed drug eruption describes the development of one or more annular or oval inflamed erythematous patches on the skin as a result of systemic exposure to a drug. The patches may develop into bullae. The inflamed patches normally resolve with post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation but typically recur at the same site(s), often with progressively more involved sites, following each reexposure to the drug. In extreme cases (generalised bullous fixed drug eruption) the clinical picture may mi

name: Familial hypercholesterolaemia - heterozygous
definition: This is a heterozygous genetic disorder characterised by high cholesterol levels, specifically very high levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL, \"bad cholesterol\"), in the blood and early cardiovascular disease.

name: Eruptive vellus hair cysts
definition: Eruptive vellus hair cysts typically present as multiple small red or brown papules on the chest during adolescence. They arise as a result of occlusion and cystic dilatation of vellus hair follicles.

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, sacroiliac joint
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, sacroiliac joint

name: Small plaque parapsoriasis
definition: The benign form of parapsoriasis, a chronic multifocal skin disease characterised by atrophic erythematous patches located preferentially on the trunk and proximal extremities. The aetiology is unknown.
synonyms: Chronic superficial dermatitis | Digitate dermatosis | chronic superficial dermatitis | digitate dermatosis | xanthoerythrodermia perstans

name: Seborrhoea
definition: The secretion of excessive amounts of sebum resulting in an excessively greasy skin, a situation which may cause considerable distress. It may be associated with a number of conditions including acne, acromegaly and Parkinson disease. It is not normally a prominent component of seborrhoeic dermatitis, which is an inflammatory dermatitis.

name: Juvenile hereditary haemochromatosis, Type 2B
definition: This is the maintenance of iron homeostasis, and it is necessary for the regulation of iron storage in macrophages, and for intestinal iron absorption.
synonyms: Hepcidin-related haemochromatosis

name: Male pattern hair loss
definition: Male pattern hair loss (common baldness; male androgenetic alopecia) is the result of a progressive, patterned hair loss mediated by exposure to androgens. Although over 90% of men demonstrate some degree of frontoparietal recession of the hairline by the age of 20, the extent of hair loss is genetically determined and only 30% of men ever develop extensive hair loss.
synonyms: Balding - male pattern | Common balding | Male pattern balding | male androgenetic alopecia | AGA | common balding | hereditary balding | MAGA | male androgenic alopecia | MPHL

name: Syphilitic myocarditis
definition: This is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum, causing inflammation of heart muscle (myocardium).
synonyms: chronic syphilitic myocarditis | myocardial gumma | myocardial syphilis

name: X-linked ichthyosis syndromes
definition: Hereditary disorders in which X-linked ichthyosis is associated with significant other abnormalities.

name: Actinomycetoma due to Actinomadura species
definition: Actinomycetoma due to Actinomadura species. These are the predominant organisms responsible for mycetoma in West and North Africa but are also important pathogens in West Asia and Central America.
synonyms: Actinomadura Actinomycetoma

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to plastics, glues or resin systems
Pregnancy dermatoses
definition: A group of skin disorders which are specific to pregnancy.

name: Skin manifestation of other specified neonatal micronutrient excess or toxicity classified elsewhere
Pemphigus herpetiformis
definition: Pemphigus herpetiformis is a chronic autoimmune skin diseases characterised by papules and vesicles on the skin mediated by auto-antibodies to the desmosome components desmoglein 1 and more rarely desmoglein 3.

name: Syphilitic breast
synonyms: syphilis of breast

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to rubber chemicals
Downhill varices of the oesophagus
definition: Downhill oesophageal varices (DEV) which develop in the upper third of the oesophagus and are often related to superior vena cava obstruction, and are less common than distal oesophageal varices, i.e. the uphill type, which is usually produced by portal hypertension.

name: Skin disorders involving specific cutaneous structures
cn_name: 涉及特定皮肤结构的皮肤障碍

name: Dabska tumour
synonyms: Endovascular papillary angioendothelioma | dabska tumor | endovascular papillary angioendothelioma | hobnail hemangioendothelioma | malignant endovascular papillary angioendothelioma | papillay intralymphatic angioendothelioma | PILA

name: Dorsal pterygium of nail
definition: An abnormality of the nail in which the proximal nail fold extends forward and fuses with the nail matrix, interfering with the growth of the nail plate.

name: Wound myiasis
definition: Infestation of wounds by the larvae of flies. Both obligatorily parasitic larvae (which always pass their larval stage in the body of an animal) and facultatively parasitic larvae (which normally develop in decaying flesh or vegetable matter) may cause wound myiasis.
synonyms: Traumatic myiasis

name: Corrosion of first degree of shoulder and arm, except wrist or hand
synonyms: corrosion of first degree of shoulder and upper limb, except wrist and hand

name: Lichen simplex
definition: Circumscribed pruritic lichenification of the skin of any origin. If a circumscribed area of skin is subjected to repeated rubbing or scratching, localised epidermal thickening or lichenification will ensue. The nape of the neck, genitalia, perianal area and lateral calf are commonly affected sites.
synonyms: Circumscribed neurodermatitis | Lichen simplex chronicus | Neurodermatitis

name: Pthiriasis
definition: Infestation most commonly of pubic hair and less commonly of body hair or eyelashes by the crab louse, Pthirus pubis. Transmission is by direct, typically sexual contact with an infected individual. Confirmation is by identification of Pthirus pubis or its eggs.
synonyms: Infestation by Phthirus pubis | Infestation by crab lice | Phthiriasis | crabs | Pediculosis Pubis | Pediculus pubis | Phthiriasis pubis | Phthirus pubis | Phthirus/pediculus pubis - pubic lice - crabs

name: Leprosy
definition: A disease caused by an infection with Mycobacterium leprae. This disease commonly presents with a long asymptomatic period followed by granulomatous lesions of the skin, respiratory tract, and peripheral nerves. Transmission is commonly by droplet transmission. Confirmation is by identification of Mycobacterium leprae with skin biopsy.
synonyms: Hansen disease | Infection due to Mycobacterium leprae | anaesthesia leprosy | anaesthetic leprosy | leprosy unspecified | macular leprosy | maculoanaesthetic leprosy | hansen's disease

name: Bullous diffuse cutaneous mastocytosis
definition: Bullous diffuse cutaneous mastocytosis is a form of diffuse cutaneous mastocytosis characterised by generalised erythroderma and severe blistering associated with the accumulation of mast cells in the skin.

name: Contact dermatitis of face or neck
definition: Contact dermatitis not specified as irritant or allergic affecting the face and/or neck.

name: Alezzandrini syndrome
definition: A rare syndrome characterised by the development of unilateral retinal degeneration with ipsilateral facial vitiligo, poliosis and hearing loss.

name: Simple dermographism
definition: Simple dermographism is a physiological response to firm skin stroking, resulting in the development of non-pruritic linear weals. It is present in up to 5% of healthy people.

name: Lymphatic malformation of appendix
synonyms: Lymphangioma of appendix

name: Familial partial lipodystrophy, Dunnigan type
definition: Familial partial lipodystrophy, Dunnigan type belongs to the group of partial lipodystrophies with insulin resistance. Clinically, it is mainly characterised by the loss of subcutaneous adipose tissue in the lower parts of the body (limbs, buttocks, trunk) accompanied by an accumulation of adipose tissue in the face and neck, resulting in pseudo-cushingoid facies.
synonyms: Dunnigan syndrome | FPLD2 - [Familial partial lipodystrophy type 2] | Familial partial lipodystrophy type 2

name: Miscellaneous specified eczematous dermatoses
definition: A heterogeneous group of eczematous dermatoses not classified elsewhere.
cn_name: 其他特指的湿疹性皮肤病

name: Symmetrical drug-related intertriginous and flexural erythema
definition: A characteristic reaction pattern of systemic allergic contact dermatitis in which there is diffuse erythema of the buttocks, upper inner thighs and axillae. Agents recognised to provoke this include penicillins and terbinafine.
synonyms: Baboon syndrome | SDRIFE - [symmetrical drug-related intertriginous or flexural erythema]

name: Cutaneous reactions to food mites
definition: Reactions caused by mites which infest stored food products such as grains, flour, nuts and dried fruits. The eruptions provoked by such mites generally consist of multiple intensely pruritic papules or papulovesicles on exposed skin, particularly the head and neck.
synonyms: Storage mite reaction

name: Periocular dermatitis
definition: This form of periorificial dermatitis is the periocular counterpart of perioral dermatitis and is attributable to the use of potent topical corticosteroid preparations to or around the eyes. It presents with erythema, papules and pustules affecting eyelids and periocular skin.

name: Leptospira interrogans autumnalis infection
synonyms: fever due to leptospira autumnalis | fort bragg fever | pretibial fever

name: Generalised seborrhoeic dermatitis
definition: An uncommon disseminated form of seborrhoeic dermatitis which may become erythrodermic.

name: Dermatoses provoked or exacerbated by exposure to cold
definition: Skin conditions provoked by exposure to low temperatures.
cn_name: 暴露于寒冷诱发或加剧的皮肤病

name: Phylloid hypomelanosis
definition: Phylloid hypomelanosis is a rare neurocutaneous syndrome characterised by a pattern of hypopigmentation consisting of leaflike or oblong macules reminiscent of floral ornaments. Associated extracutaneous anomalies include cerebral, ocular, and skeletal defects.

name: Disorders of essential minerals or their metabolism affecting the skin
definition: This group includes skin conditions resulting from deficiency or abnormal metabolic processing of metals, in particular iron and zinc

name: Chronic effects of ionizing radiation on the skin
definition: The long-term sequelae of prior exposure of skin to ionizing radiation.
cn_name: 电离辐射皮肤的慢性皮应

name: Psoriasiform napkin dermatitis
definition: A napkin eruption characterised by sharply marginated confluent erythema and scale in the napkin area. It is considered a component of infantile seborrhoeic dermatitis, the disseminated form of which may start in the napkin area. In some cases, however, the same clinical picture may eventuate into psoriasis (napkin psoriasis). The clinical picture is essentially identical.
synonyms: Infantile seborrhoeic dermatitis of the napkin area | Napkin psoriasis

name: Pyoderma gangrenosum linked to haematological disorder
definition: Pyoderma gangrenosum associated with haematological disorders including monoclonal paraproteinaemias, myelodysplasia, myeloid leukaemia, hairy cell leukaemia and myelofibrosis.

name: Sjögren syndrome
definition: Sjögren syndrome (SS) is a slowly progressive, systemic inflammatory autoimmune disease affecting primarily the exocrine glands. Lymphocytic infiltrates replace functional epithelium, leading to oral and ocular dryness. Characteristic autoantibodies (e.g., anti-Ro/SS-A and/or anti-La/SS-B) are produced. The disorder can occur alone (it is then known as “primary SS”) or in association with another autoimmune disease (it is then known as “secondary SS”).
synonyms: Sicca syndrome | Sjögren syndrome NOS

name: Hyperkeratotic actinic keratosis
synonyms: Hyperkeratotic solar keratosis

name: Roseola infantum
definition: A disease caused by an infection with roseolovirus (human herpesvirus type 6 or 7). This disease is characterised by acute fever, followed by macular or maculopapular exanthem in some individuals. Transmission is by inhalation of infected respiratory secretions or direct contact.
synonyms: Exanthema subitum | Sixth disease | roseola | exanthem subitum | sixth disease

name: Candida paronychia
definition: A chronic inflammatory condition of the nail folds of the thumb and/or fingers associated with the presence of Candida yeasts. It is seen particularly in those whose hands are frequently immersed for prolonged periods in water (e.g. chefs and kitchen assistants). The nail folds of the affected digits are swollen with disruption of the cuticle.
synonyms: candida intertrigo | candidal paronychia | candidal perionyxis | paronychia candidiasis | paronychial candidiasis

name: Deafness, lymphoedema and leukaemia syndrome
definition: Deafness - lymphoedema - leukemia syndrome is characterised by sensorineural deafness, mutism, lymphoedema of the lower limbs with early onset and haematological anomalies (acute myeloblastic leukaemia, cytopaenia).
synonyms: Emberger syndrome

name: Kindler syndrome
definition: Kindler syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive genophotodermatosis. It combines features of acral blistering and photosensitivity from infancy, which tend to improve through childhood, with progressive poikiloderma and cutaneous atrophy developing later.
synonyms: Bullous acrokeratotic poikiloderma of Kindler and Weary | Congenital poikiloderma with bullae and progressive cutaneous atrophy | Hereditary acrokeratotic poikiloderma | Weary–Kindler syndrome | bullous acrokeratotic poikiloderma of kindler and weary | congenital poikiloderma with blisters and keratoses | congenital poikiloderma with bullae and progressive cutaneous atrophy | hereditary acrokeratotic poikiloderma | kindler-weary syndrome | hereditary acrokeratotic poikiloderma of Kindler-Weary

name: Superficial basal cell carcinoma of skin
definition: Superficial basal cell carcinomas are often multiple and appear as pink or red barely elevated patches varying in size from a few mm to over 10 cm in diameter. A fine pearly border can usually be seen on careful inspection. They occur most frequently on the trunk.
cn_name: 皮肤浅表性基底细胞癌

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to dental materials
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to dental materials (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to dental materials).

name: Late syphilitic optic atrophy
definition: This is a late, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with an optic atrophy.

name: Genetic lipodystrophy
definition: Genetic lipodystrophies represent a heterogeneous group of rare diseases characterised by a generalised or localised loss of body fat (lipoatrophy).
synonyms: Hereditary lipodystrophy

name: Cutaneous strongyloidiasis
definition: An infestation of the skin with larvae of the nematode roundworm, Strongyloides stercoralis. Larvae may reach the skin either by direct penetration of the skin or by autoinfection from larvae of intestinal worms. Clinical signs are similar to cutaneous larva migrans but the larvae move across the skin faster than in that condition and there is often associated urticaria.
synonyms: Larva currens | Strongyloidal ground itch

name: Disseminated paracoccidioidomycosis with central nervous system involvement
definition: Disseminated chronic paracoccidioidomycosis with CNS involvement is associated with high morbidity and risk of mortality.
synonyms: Disseminated chronic paracoccidioidomycosis with CNS - [central nervous system]involvement

name: Genitoperineal median raphe cyst
definition: Median raphe cysts are formed from epithelial remnants thought either to be trapped as a result of defective fusion of the urethral folds during embryonic formation of the male urethra or as anomalous outgrowths of the epithelium after primary closure. They can develop at any site along the midline of the ventral aspect of the penis, scrotum or perineum. They may not present until adolescence or adult life when one or more ventral midline cystic swellings are noted

name: Napkin candidosis
definition: Secondary infection of napkin dermatitis with Candida species. Inappropriate prolonged treatment of napkin dermatitis with potent topical corticosteroids may produce a nodular form of candidosis of the napkin area.
synonyms: diaper candidosis

name: Angioedema due to angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitors
definition: Angioedema resulting from inhibition of metabolic breakdown of bradykinin and other mediators. It occurs in up to 1% of patients given angiotensin I receptor antagonists but does also less frequently occur with angiotensin II antagonists.
synonyms: Angioedema due to ACE inhibitors

name: Acute febrile neutrophilic dermatosis
definition: Sweet syndrome (the eponym for acute febrile neutrophilic dermatosis) is characterised by a constellation of clinical symptoms, physical features, and pathological findings which include fever, neutrophilia, tender erythematous skin lesions (papules, nodules, and plaques), and a diffuse infiltrate consisting predominantly of mature neutrophils that are typically located in the upper dermis. Sweet syndrome presents in three clinical settings\: classical (or idiopathic), malignancy-associated, and
synonyms: Sweet disease | Sweet syndrome | acute febrile neutrophilic dermatosis | SS | Acute Febrile Neutrophilic Dermatosis | Sweet's syndrome
cn_name: 急性发热性嗜中性皮病

name: 22q13 deletion
definition: Monosomy 22q13 syndrome (deletion 22q13.3 syndrome or Phelan-McDermid syndrome) is a chromosome microdeletion syndrome characterised by neonatal hypotonia, global developmental delay, normal to accelerated growth, absent to severely delayed speech, and minor dysmorphic features.
synonyms: 22q13 deletion syndrome | Phelan-McDermid syndrome

name: Localised granuloma annulare
definition: This is the commonest form of granuloma annulare and typically presents in children and young adults as one or more rings of small, smooth, flesh-coloured or erythematous papules which enlarge centrifugally. The dorsa of the hands, the fingers and the feet are the commonest sites.

name: Transient neonatal pustular melanosis
definition: Transient neonatal pustular melanosis is thought to be a variant of neonatal toxic erythema occurring particularly in neonates of African descent. Fragile superficial pustules located especially on the chin, neck, forehead, back and buttocks are noted at or soon after birth. When they rupture they initially form a crust or scale which is then shed to leave multiple discrete pigmented macules which fade over the first few months of life.
synonyms: Transient neonatal melanosis | lentigines neonatorum | transient neonatal pustulosis

name: Haemodialysis-associated pseudoporphyria
definition: Pseudoporphyria is a photodistributed vesiculobullous disorder that clinically and histologically resembles porphyria cutanea tarda but without the associated porphyrin abnormalities. Its association with long-term haemodialysis is well recognised with estimates of its frequency as high as 18% reported in the past. The precise pathomechansim is poorly understood.
synonyms: Pseudoporphyria associated with renal haemodialysis

name: Certain skin disorders attributable to viral infection
definition: This group incorporates both localised infection of the skin by virus (e.g. viral warts) and systemic or distant viral infections with important skin manifestations (e.g. viral exanthemata).
cn_name: 某些归因于病毒感染的皮肤疾患

name: Diffuse bleeding diathesis due to vitamin K deficient haemorrhagic disease of fetus or newborn, early
definition: Early vitamin K deficiency bleeding occurs during first 24 hours of life in mothers taking anticonvulsants or antituberculosis medication. Bleeding occurs in the umbilicus, GI tract, skin, nose and surgical sites (circumcision).
synonyms: Diffuse bleeding diathesis secondary to vitamin K deficient haemorrhagic disease of fetus and newborn, early

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to metals or metal salts
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to exposure to metals and metal salts such as nickel, cobalt or chromate.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to metals or metal salts
cn_name: 金属或金属盐引起的变应性接触性皮炎

name: Oral-facial-digital syndrome type 8
definition: Oral-facial-digital (OFD) syndrome, type 8 is a malformation syndrome characterised by tongue lobulation, median cleft upper lip, broad or bifid nasal tip, hypertelorism or telecanthus, bilateral preaxial and postaxial polydactyly, abnormal tibiae and/or radii, short stature, hypoplasia of the epiglottis, duplication of the halluces and mild intellectual deficit.

name: Subcutaneous lipomatosis of other specified or unspecified type
definition: Subcutaneous lipomatosis occurring in isolation or as a component of genetic syndromes including Proteus syndrome.

name: Multiple mononeuropathy vasculitis with mixed cryoglobulinemia
Methylmalonic aciduria, vitamin B12 unresponsive
definition: Vitamin B12-unresponsive methylmalonic acidemia is an inborn error of vitamin B12 (cobalamin) metabolism characterised by recurrent ketoacidotic crises or transient vomiting, dehydration, hypotonia and intellectual deficit, which does not respond to administration of vitamin B12. There are two types of vitamin B12-unresponsive methylmalonic acidemia\: mut0 and mut- (see these terms).

name: Scarring alopecia due to infection
definition: Scarring alopecia resulting from infection. This ensues where there has been significant inflammation and may follow severe dermatophytosis of the scalp or lepromatous leprosy as two examples.

name: Drug-induced anagen effluvium
definition: Anagen effluvium due to medication, most commonly from cytotoxic cancer chemotherapy.
cn_name: 药物引起的生长期脱发

name: Chronic discoid or annular skin disorder
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature consisting of multiple circular or ring-shaped patches and plaques. Although infection due to dermatophyte infection (tinea or ringworm) can produce this pattern, many other skin disorders may have a discoid or annular configuration\: these are frequently misdiagnosed as tinea. Common examples include atopic eczema, nummular dermatitis and psoriasis.
cn_name: 慢性盘状或环形皮肤病

name: Anagen effluvium
definition: Anagen effluvium occurs after any insult to the hair follicle that impairs its mitotic or metabolic activity. Patients present with diffuse hair loss after an exposure to drugs or toxic chemicals. Chemotherapeutic agents are most commonly responsible for hair loss. The most severe hair loss occurs in association with doxorubicin, the nitrosoureas, and cyclophosphamide. Hair loss usually begins 7-14 days after a single pulse of chemotherapy. The hair loss is clinically most apparent after 1-2 mon

name: Urachal cyst
definition: A urachal cyst is a fibrous remnant of the urachus resulting in a cystic fluid filled space.
synonyms: Cyst of urachus

name: Initial stage of trachoma
definition: This refers to the initial stage of an infectious disease caused by the Chlamydia trachomatis bacterium which produces a characteristic roughening of the inner surface of the eyelids.
synonyms: Trachoma dubium

name: Blistering distal dactylitis
definition: A specific form of bullous impetigo in which large bullae develop on the finger pulps or, less commonly, more proximally on the fingers or on the palms. Either Staphylococcus aureus or Streptococcus pyogenes may be implicated. It typically affects infants and children.

name: Adult-onset citrullinaemia type 1
definition: Adult-onset citrullinemia type 1 is a form of citrullinemia type 1 characterised clinically by adult onset of symptoms including variable hyperammonemia and less striking neurological findings which may include intense headache, scotomas, migraine-like episodes, ataxia, slurred speech, lethargy, and drowsiness. Serious increased intracranial pressure may occur.

name: Classical phenylketonuria
definition: Classical phenylketonuria is a severe form of phenylketonuria (PKU) an inborn error of amino acid metabolism characterised in untreated patients by severe intellectual deficit and neuropsychiatric complications.
synonyms: Folling disease | PAH - [phenylalanine hydroxylase] deficiency | Typical PKU - [phenylketonuria] | Typical phenylketonuria | hyperphenylalaninaemia Type I | imbecilitus phenylpyruvica | oligophrenia phenylpyruvica | phenylpyruvic aciduria | phenylpyruvic oligophrenia

name: Other cutaneous reactions to arthropods
definition: Cutaneous reactions to arthropods other than discrete bites and stings. Although the most common injuries inflicted by arthropods are discrete bites and stings, some arthropods (including beetles, butterfly and moth caterpillars and certain species of mites) tend to produce more diffuse irritation of the skin with, for instance, irritant dermatitis or blistering.

name: Pseudofolliculitis
definition: Pseudofolliculitis is an inflammatory follicular and perifollicular foreign body reaction to hair trapped beneath the skin surface as may occur from penetration or retraction of the cut ends of hair into the skin following shaving or as the result of disturbed hair growth following plucking or waxing.
synonyms: Pili incarnati

name: Acquired palmoplantar keratodermas
synonyms: Acquired keratoderma palmaris et plantaris | Keratoderma of palms and soles, acquired

name: Ectodermal dysplasia, pure hair-nail type
definition: Pure hair and nail ectodermal dysplasia refers to the association of onychodystrophy and severe hypotrichosis, which is mainly limited to the scalp but may also affect the eyelashes and eyebrows.

name: Diffuse cutaneous leishmaniasis
definition: Diffuse cutaneous leishmaniasis results from anergy to leishmania infection which allows infection to spread widely over the skin surface. It manifests as progressively spreading painless nodular infiltration of the skin by either L. aethiopica or L. amazonensis. In contrast to disseminated cutaneous leishmaniasis, the leishmanin test is negative, nodules contain abundant parasites and response to therapy poor.
synonyms: anergic leishmaniasis | disseminated leishmaniasis | lepromatous leishmaniasis | pseudolepromatous leishmaniasis

name: Trichorrhexis invaginata
synonyms: Bamboo hair | bamboo hair | ichthyosis linearis cifcumflexa | netherton syndrome | NTS | trichorrhexis invaginata

name: Chronic cutaneous lupus erythematosus of scalp
definition: This form of lupus erythematosus presents with patchy scarring alopecia, often associated with prominent perifollicular inflammation and scaling around the active edges of the plaques. It may occur in isolation or be associated with discoid lupus elsewhere, especially on the head and neck. It is often recalcitrant to treatment and results in permanent and frequently disfiguring hair loss.
synonyms: Scarring alopecia due to lupus erythematosus

name: Other specified isolated inherited deformity of nails
Paraneoplastic acquired ichthyosis
definition: Paraneoplastic ichthyosis is a form of acquired ichthyosis which resembles autosomal dominant ichthyosis vulgaris but develops in adult life in individuals with underlying malignancy. It is strongly associated with Hodgkin lymphoma and may be the presenting sign of that disease. It may less commonly be associated with other lymphoid neoplasms or solid tumours.

name: Cutaneous microvascular disturbances due to hyperglobulinaemia
definition: Skin changes including retiform purpura, livedo reticularis, Raynaud phenomenon and acrocyanosis, attributable to abnormal levels of circulating globulins. Such changes are particularly associated with Waldenström macroglobulinaemia but may occur with other monoclonal paraproteinaemias or with hyperglobulinaemia associated with infection or systemic autoimmune disorders.

name: Chronic urticarial skin disorder
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a rash consisting of persistent urticated papules and plaques for which a more precise diagnosis has not been or cannot be made. Diagnoses which should be considered include immunobullous disorders, urticarial vasculitis and drug reactions.
synonyms: Chronic dermal hypersensitivity reaction | Chronic urticarial dermatitis

name: Oral mucositis due to other specified cause
definition: This is the painful inflammation and ulceration of the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, usually as an adverse effect of chemotherapy and radiotherapy treatment for cancer, due to other specified cause.

name: Crohn disease of lips or oral mucosa
definition: Granulomatous inflammation of the lips and oral mucosa with the clinical features of orofacial granulomatosis and evidence of Crohn disease manifest as granulomatous inflammation affecting other body sites, in particular the gastrointestinal tract.

name: Food-dependent exercise-induced anaphylaxis
definition: An IgE-mediated severe allergic reaction to food occurring only if ingestion of a specific food is followed by exercise, with neither food nor exercise alone inducing manifestations. Substances reported to cause this include wheat, hazelnuts and shellfish.
synonyms: Exercise-induced anaphylaxis dependent on food | Food-derived exercise-induced anaphylaxis

name: Superficial thrombophlebitis of upper limbs
synonyms: Phlebitis of superficial veins of arm | Phlebitis of superficial veins of upper extremity

name: Scarring of skin due to burn
definition: Cutaneous scarring following the healing of a burn injury. Burn scars are particularly prone to contracture, often necessitating surgical intervention.
synonyms: burn scar | cicatricial contracture of skin following burn

name: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex, migratory circinate
definition: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex with circinate migratory erythema (EBS-migr) is a basal subtype of epidermolysis bullosa simplex characterised by belt-like areas of erythema with multiple vesicles and small blisters at the advancing edge of erythema.
synonyms: EBS - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex] migratory circinate

name: Juvenile dermatomyositis with calcinosis
definition: Juvenile dermatomyositis with calcinosis is a rare childhood systemic autoimmune disease characterised by proximal muscle weakness, rashes due to chronic muscle and skin inflammation, and calcinosis due to the abnormal deposition of insoluble calcium salts within the skin, subcutaneous tissue, myofascia, or muscle. The sites most frequently affected by calcinosis are the elbows, knees, trunk, hands, feet, buttocks, and head, although it may occur virtually anywhere over the body. The onset of ca

name: Pyemotes mite dermatitis
definition: An intensely pruritic urticated and papulovesicular eruption due to contact with Pyemotes mites, which are parasites of insects and insect larvae that infest grain, straw hay and stored foodstuffs.
synonyms: Barley itch | Cotton-seed dermatitis | Grain-shovellers’ itch | Straw itch | grain itch | grocer's itch | infestation by grain mite | infestation by pyemotes ventricosus

name: Angiokeratoma corporis diffusum
definition: Angiokeratoma corporis diffusum consists in multiple vascular papules located symmetrically on the lower abdomen, the genitalia, the buttocks, and the thighs in a bathing-trunk distribution. It is closely associated with and may be the presenting sign of Fabry disease, a progressive, inherited, multisystem disorder of glycosphingolipid metabolism. It may also develop in other lysosomal diseases including fucosidosis, alpha-N-acetylgalactosaminidase deficiency and galactosialidosis.

name: Malignant neoplasm metastasis in skin of lower extremity
Monogenic autoinflammatory syndromes
definition: Monogenic hereditary autoinflammatory diseases characterised by apparently unprovoked generalised inflammation in the absence of infection or high titre autoantibodies.
cn_name: 单基因自身炎症综合征

name: Zoster infection of eyelid
definition: A disease of the eyes caused by the reactivation of a latent infection with varicella zoster virus in the trigeminal nerve. This disease is characterised by a periorbital rash (typically within one dermatome), and conjunctivitis.
synonyms: Herpes zoster infection of eyelid | Herpes zoster ulceration and scarring of eyelid | Zoster ulceration and scarring of eyelid

name: Pressure ulceration grade 4 of lower back
Talar callosity
definition: A distinct form of callosity located on the anterolateral aspect of the ankle overlying the talus bone. It is due to sitting cross-legged or sitting back onto the feet from a kneeling position, thereby exposing the skin overlying the talus to repeated pressure and friction.
synonyms: Callosity of ankle | Cross-legged callosity

name: Epithelioid cell melanosarcoma of unspecified site
Cerebral dysgenesis – neuropathy – ichthyosis – keratoderma syndrome
definition: CEDNIK syndrome is a neurocutaneous syndrome characterised by severe developmental abnormalities of the nervous system and aberrant differentiation of the epidermis. Clinically, it is defined by a unique constellation of clinical signs described with the acronym CEDNIK\: Cerebral Dysgenesis, Neuropathy, Ichthyosis, and palmoplantar Keratoderma.
synonyms: CEDNIK - [Cerebral dysgenesis – neuropathy – ichthyosis – keratoderma syndrome] syndrome

name: Pallor
definition: Paleness of the skin such as may ensue from severe anaemia or from hypovolaemic shock.

name: Juvenile dermatomyositis with lipodystrophy
definition: Juvenile dermatomyositis with lipodystrophy is a chronic systemic autoimmune disease characterised by early-onset proximal weakness and characteristic skin rashes, associated with loss of subcutaneous fat in a localised or generalised distribution, frequently with resultant metabolic abnormalities such as insulin resistance, diabetes and hyperlipidaemia. These patients have a loss of mature, functional adipocytes, as opposed to an absence of lipid in otherwise normal adipocytes.

name: Chromhidrosis
definition: A rare condition characterised by the secretion of coloured sweat. It is caused by the deposition of lipofuscin in the sweat glands.
synonyms: Secretion of coloured sweat

name: Syphilitic monoplegia
synonyms: syphilis monoplegia

name: Lymphatic filariasis
definition: Infestation by filarial nematodes of the genera Wuchereria and Brugia. It is acquired via transcutaneous injection of larvae by mosquitoes previously infested with microfilariae from the blood of a human host. The adult worms live in the lymphatics but release microfilariae into the bloodstream to complete the life cycle of the parasite. Clinical disease occurs in only a minority of those infected. In the acute stage this may present as an acute painful adenolymphangitis with fever. Chronic infe
synonyms: Filarial lymphangitis | Tropical elephantiasis | filarial chylocele | elephantiasis | lymphatic filariasis

name: Severe combined immunodeficiency with hypereosinophilia
definition: Omenn syndrome (OS) is an inflammatory condition characterised by erythroderma, desquamation, alopecia, chronic diarrhea, failure to thrive, lymphadenopathy, and hepatosplenomegaly, associated with severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID; ).
synonyms: Omenn syndrome | SCID - [severe combined immunodeficiency] with hypereosinophilia

name: Macrocephaly – alopecia – cutis laxa – scoliosis syndrome
synonyms: MACS - [Macrocephaly – alopecia – cutis laxa – scoliosis syndrome] | MACS syndrome (MIM 613075)

name: Trichodynia
definition: Trichodynia is a somatoform disorder in which there is an unaccountable perception of distressing pain or burning arising in scalp hair. The symptoms are limited to the hair itself and are typically aggravated by touching the hair.
synonyms: Burning scalp syndrome | Hair pain

name: Tumour necrosis factor receptor 1 associated periodic syndrome
definition: TRAPS is an autoinflammatory disease associated with heterozygous mutations in the gene coding for tumour necrosis factor (TNF) receptor 1 (TNFR1). This results in clinical attacks of inflammation in the form of fever and serositis in the form of peritoneal, pleural or synovial inflammation along with increased acute phase reactants.
synonyms: Autosomal dominant periodic fever | Familial Hibernian fever | TNF - [tumour necrosis factor] receptor 1 associated periodic syndrome | TRAPS - [tumour necrosis factor receptor 1 associated periodic syndrome] | TRAPS | tumor necrosis factor receptor-associated periodic syndrome
cn_name: TNF受体-1相关的周期性综合征

name: Oedema of skin or soft tissues
definition: Swelling of skin and soft tissue due to accumulation of interstitial fluid. In many cases there are several contributory factors to the development of oedema including increased capillary blood flow, increased venous pressure and hypoproteinaemia.

name: Genetic defects of the hair shaft with increased fragility
Subacute nodular migratory panniculitis
synonyms: chronic erythema nodosum | erythema nodosum migrans

name: Mucocutaneous paracoccidioidomycosis
definition: Mucous membrane involvement occurs in 50% of patients with acute pulmonary paracoccidioidomycosis, resulting in dysphagia, dysphonia, and granulomatous plaques affecting the lips and perioral skin. Gingival involvement may lead to tooth loss. Oral lesions may be associated with nasal and pharyngeal ulcers and with mandibular or cervical lymph node enlargement.
synonyms: mucocutaneous-lymphangitic paracoccidioidomycosis | paracoccidioidal granuloma

name: Truncated form of apolipoprotein B-100
definition: This refers to a truncated form of the primary apolipoproteins of chylomicrons and low-density lipoproteins (LDL - known commonly by the misnomer \"bad cholesterol\" when in reference to heart disease), which is responsible for carrying cholesterol to tissues.

name: Incontinentia pigmenti
definition: Incontinentia pigmenti is an X-linked dominant gene disorder due to abnormalities of the NF-kappa-B (NEMO) gene on chromosome Xq28. It is lethal in male fetuses but the presence of a normal second X chromosome in females results in a mosaicism which is compatible with life. Affected females present in infancy with skin blisters in linear arrays (Blaschko lines) typically on the scalp and limbs. Within the first few months of life these are succeeded by warty changes and hyperpigmentation. These
synonyms: Bloch-Siemens syndrome | Bloch-Sulzberger syndrome | Incontinentia pigmenti of Bloch-Sulzberger | nevus pigmentosus systematicus | bloch-sulzberger syndrome | IP | Incontinentia pigmenti | Incontinentia pigmenti syndrome

name: Mycobacterium marinum infection
definition: Mycobacterium marinum infection typically presents as a single inflammatory, sometimes ulcerated skin nodule or warty plaque at the site of inoculation. It may then spread proximally with development of secondary nodules along the course of draining lymphatics (sporotrichoid spread). Common sources of infection are tropical fish tanks and swimming-pools \[Dermatology TAG\].
synonyms: Fish-tank granuloma | Swimming pool granuloma | swimming pool granuloma

name: Hidradenitis suppurativa of multiple sites
definition: Hidradenitis suppurativa involving more than a single site, typically both anogenital region and axillae
synonyms: Acne inversa of multiple sites

name: Pressure ulceration grade 1 of lower back
Dermatitis of feet
definition: Dermatitis (eczema) involving predominantly the feet.
synonyms: dermatitis of foot | eczema of feet | eczema of foot | feet eczema | foot eczema

name: Frostbite of abdominal wall with tissue necrosis
Nodular melanoma, primary
definition: Variant of melanoma carrying a poor prognosis due to the fact that there is little or no prodromal radial (superficial) growth phase before deep invasion (vertical growth). The lesion presents as an elevated, reddish, bluish or dark brown coloured, dome-shaped tumour. Ulceration or bleeding from the lesion occurs frequently. It occurs most frequently in the fifth or sixth decade.
cn_name: 原发性结节性黑色素瘤

name: Infection specified elsewhere following cutaneous laser surgery
definition: Cutaneous infection, the details of which are specified separately, attributable to laser surgery

name: Congenital generalised hypertrichosis
definition: Ambras syndrome is a distinct subtype of generalised congenital hypertrichosis characterised by excessive hair apparent at birth, described as silky, lightly coloured hair that can reach several centimetres in length and is most prominent on the face, ears and shoulders.
synonyms: Ambras syndrome

name: Occupational phototoxic reactions to skin contact with exogenous photoactive agents
definition: Non-allergic cutaneous inflammation caused by occupational skin contact with photoactive toxins produced by photoactive agents (chromophores) on activation by ultraviolet radiation. No previous sensitisation is required.

name: Photoaggravated skin disease
definition: Skin disease which is or is prone to be exacerbated by exposure to ultraviolet radiation or visible light.

name: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome
definition: Bazex-Dupré-Christol syndrome is a rare genodermatosis with a predisposition to early-onset basal cell carcinomas. It manifests during the neonatal period or during infancy with hypotrichosis, hypohidrosis, and milia. Follicular atrophoderma is frequent and most common on the dorsum of the hands and feet, the extensor surfaces of the elbows and knees, and the face. Hypotrichosis affects the scalp and sometimes the eyebrows. The milia papules and basal cell carcinomas are located predominantly on
synonyms: Follicular atrophoderma and basal cell carcinoma syndrome [Bazex]

name: Maffucci syndrome
definition: Maffucci syndrome is defined as a rare nonhereditary mesodermal dysplasia characterised by multiple enchondromatosis associated with soft tissue hemangiomas.
synonyms: Chondrodysplasia with haemangiomata | Dyschondroplasia with cavernous haemangiomata

name: Dracunculiasis
definition: A disease resulting from drinking water contaminated with water fleas infected with larvae of the nematode Dracunculus medinensis. It may take up to a year from ingestion of larvae for a mature gravid female worm to migrate to the skin and discharge immature larvae on contact with water. Dracunculiasis typically manifests as an intensely pruritic papule on a lower extremity from which part of the worm may emerge. Secondary pyogenic infection is common. This may be preceded by generalised symptom
synonyms: Dracunculosis | Dracunculus medinensis infestation | Guinea worm infestation | Medina worm infestation | dracontiasis

name: Genetic defects of hair or hair growth
synonyms: genetic defect of hair growth NOS | genetic defect of hair or hair growth NOS
cn_name: 毛发或毛发生长的遗传性缺陷

name: Psoriasis
definition: Psoriasis is a common, chronic, relapsing, inflammatory skin disorder characterised by abnormal epidermal keratinization and hyperproliferation. It has a strong genetic component and affects some 2% of the populations of many regions of the world. Up to 10-20% of patients with psoriasis also experience an inflammatory polyarthritis (psoriatic arthritis). Although many people with psoriasis have limited disease, both psoriasis and its associated arthritis often cause major functional and psychoso

name: Cutaneous complications of secondary syphilis
definition: Less common cutaneous manifestations of secondary syphilis

name: Bloom syndrome
definition: Bloom syndrome is a rare autosomal recessive disorder belonging to a group of chromosomal breakage syndromes, and is characterised by marked genetic instability, including a high level of sister chromatid exchanges, associated with a greatly increased predisposition to a wide range of cancers commonly affecting the general population. The constant clinical features of BS are proportionate pre- and postnatal growth retardation and cancer predisposition. Additional clinical features include dolich
synonyms: Bloom Machacek syndrome | Bloom-Torre-Machacek syndrome | congenital telangiectatic erythema

name: Keratosis lichenoides chronica
definition: Keratosis lichenoides chronica is a rare chronic lichenoid inflammatory dermatosis of adults with striking clinical and histological features. The characteristic widespread striate and reticulate verrucous plaques which form on the trunk are formed by coalescence of a profusion of papules which develop around follicular and eccrine duct ostia. The latter exhibit a lichenoid interface dermatitis with numerous necrotic keratinocytes beneath a parakeratotic epidermis rich in neutrophils. It is typi
synonyms: Lichen ruber moniliformis | Nékám disease | Parakeratosis lichenoides striata | lichen ruber acuminatus verrucosus et reticularis | lichen verrucosus et reticularis | lichenoid trikeratosis | nekam's disease | porokeratosis striata lichenoides

name: Localised bullous pemphigoid
definition: Bullous pemphigoid may be localised to a limited area of skin in up to 30% of cases. A characteristic site is the skin of the lower limbs. The course of the disease is mild and the disease is responsive to treatment. There are suggestions that it might be triggered by factors such as trauma, radiotherapy, cryotherapy and ultraviolet therapy. The immunopathological features are the same as bullous pemphigoid.

name: Postprocedural disorders of the skin
definition: This group of disorders incorporates drug eruptions, other cutaneous side effects of medication and adverse reactions to medical and surgical interventions.

name: Erythrocyanosis
definition: Erythrocyanosis is a persistent, dusky erythema occurring at sites with a thick layer of underlying subcutaneous fat, such as the thighs and lower legs. Erythrocyanosis is seen less commonly on the buttocks and arms. It is exacerbated by cold and therefore usually more prominent during the winter. It has been hypothesized that the subcutaneous fat in these sites acts to insulate the superficial vessels from the warmth of the underlying blood supply, thus rendering them susceptible to cold exposu

name: Physiological hypermelanosis
definition: The response of normal skin to exposure to natural or artificial ultraviolet radiation.

name: Balanitis or balanoposthitis
definition: Inflammation of the foreskin and/or glans penis. It may be associated with specific disorders such as lichen planus, lichen sclerosus, reactive arthritis or contact dermatitis etc. but is most commonly \"non-specific\".

name: Petrified ear
definition: A rare clinical entity in which the cartilage of the ear is replaced by ectopic calcification or ossification. It manifests as a partially or wholly rigid auricle, which may cause discomfort. Causes include frostbite, trauma, radiation therapy and several systemic diseases, notably Addison disease.

name: Opitz BBB/G syndrome
definition: Opitz BBB/G syndrome is a genetic disorder characterised by the association of craniofacial anomalies (including hypertelorism with telecanthus, a wide nasal bridge, and sometimes cleft palate/ lip) genito-urinal abnormalities (with hypospadias in affected males) malformations of larynx, pharynx and/or trachea (causing difficulties to swallow and breathe) and, less frequently, other anomalies of the ventral midline. Intelligence may be normal but mild intellectual deficit may occur

name: Tungiasis
definition: A disease of the skin, caused by an infection with the parasite Tunga penetrans. This disease is characterised by lesions (white patch with a black dot in the middle), skin inflammation, or pruritus surrounding the lesion. This disease may also be asymptomatic. Transmission is through the bite of an infected flea, or by direct contact with an infected animal. Confirmation is by identification of Tunga penetrans or travel history.
synonyms: Chigoe infestation | Jigger disease | Jiggers infestation | Sand-flea infestation | Tunga penetrans infestation | burrowing flea infestation | chigoe flea infestation | jigger flea infestation | sand flea infestation

name: Capillaritis, Majocchi
synonyms: Majocchi pigmented purpuric dermatosis | Purpura annularis telangiectodes of Majocchi | majocchi's disease

name: Oral mucosal secondary syphilis
synonyms: Secondary syphilis of mouth | Syphilitic mucous patches | Syphilitic snail-track ulcers

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, neck
synonyms: panniculitis affecting neck | panniculitis of neck | panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, neck | panniculitis specified as affecting neck

name: Leukoderma acquisitum centrifugum
definition: The presence of a halo of hypomelanosis surrounding one or more melanocytic neoplasms. These are most commonly benign melanocytic naevi (halo naevi)\: multiple halo naevi may be present concurrently. The phenomenon is also seen surrounding malignant melanomata.

name: Cutaneous mucinosis
definition: Skin disorders characterised by accumulation of mucin in the skin
synonyms: Mucinosis of skin

name: Staphylococcal ecthyma
definition: Ecthyma due to a monoinfection with Staphylococcus aureus. It is less common than streptococcal ecthyma.

name: Extragenital lichen sclerosus
definition: Lichen sclerosus affecting non-genital skin typically presents as multiple guttate, shiny white macules or papules affecting the upper trunk, neck or volar aspects of the wrists\: these may coalesce to form white plaques. There may be associated genital disease, especially in women. It may also be seen in conjunction with plaque morphoea, to which it is considered to be related.

name: Congenital lipoid adrenal hyperplasia due to STAR deficiency, classic form
definition: This is an endocrine disorder that is an uncommon and potentially lethal form of congenital adrenal hyperplasia (CAH). This diagnosis is due to STAR deficiency, classic form.

name: Raynaud phenomenon
definition: Raynaud phenomenon describes an exaggerated vascular response to cold temperature or emotional stimuli resulting in episodic digital ischaemia. It is characterised by paroxysmal vasoconstriction producing initially pallor, an essential component for the diagnosis, followed by cyanosis and erythema. Primary Raynaud disease is an isolated innocuous disorder. Secondary Raynaud phenomenon occurs in association with a wide range of different disorders including dysproteinaemias and non-organ-specific

name: Intrahepatic cholestasis of pregnancy
definition: Intrahepatic cholestasis of pregnancy (ICP) is a cholestatic disorder characterised by (i) pruritus with onset in the second or third trimester of pregnancy, (ii) elevated serum aminotransferases and bile acid levels, and (iii) spontaneous relief of signs and symptoms within two to three weeks after delivery.
synonyms: Obstetric cholestasis | cholestasis of pregnancy | ICP | jaundice of pregnancy | obstetric cholestasis | prurigo gravidarum | pruritis gravidarum
snomed: 950004 "intrahepatic cholestasis of pregnancy";235888006 "intrahepatic cholestasis of pregnancy"

name: Pharyngeal varices
synonyms: pharyngeal varix rupture | pharynx varix | varices of pharynx

name: Sequelae of superficial frostbite of hip or thigh
Genetic disorders of amino acid metabolism or transport affecting the skin
Lymphatic malformation of conjunctiva
synonyms: Lymphangioma of conjunctiva

name: Black heel
definition: A condition which may be mistaken for acral melanoma caused by extravasation of red blood cells from capillaries in the skin of the back of the heel. This area may be subject to extreme shear-stress during strenuous sporting activity (e.g. tennis). It is often first noticed as a circumscribed area of stippled black dots and may not be immediately connected with the activity which provoked it.
synonyms: Talon noir | calcaneal petechiae | post-traumatic cutaneous intracorneal blood | post-traumatic punctate intraepidermal hemmorhage | tache noir | talon noir

name: Purpura due to disorders of platelets
definition: Purpura due to thrombocytopenia, of which there are many causes, or to defective platelet function. Paradoxically increased platelet production can result in purpura as in essential thrombocythaemia.

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to other specified metal or metal salt
Hypertrophic scar
definition: Hypertrophic scars result from the production of excessive amounts of collagen in the dermis during connective tissue repair following inflammation, injury or surgery. In contrast to keloid scars, they do not expand beyond the boundary of the initial injury or inflammation and may undergo spontaneous resolution.
synonyms: hypertrophic scarring

name: Multibacillary leprosy
synonyms: Hansen disease, multibacillary

name: Acneiform inflammatory disorders
definition: Disorders characterised by acne-like follicular inflammation.
synonyms: acneform inflammatory disorder NOS

name: Cutaneous microvascular occlusion due to cardiac emboli
definition: Emboli from atrial myxomas (including NAME syndrome/Carney complex) or heart valve vegetations (in infective endocarditis) may result in acral splinter haemorrhages and petechiae, livedo reticularis and focal ischaemic necrosis at acral sites.

name: Hypomelanotic pigmentary mosaicism
definition: Hypomelanotic streaks in Blaschko line distribution as a result of a somatic mutation. It may be associated with eye, skeletal and CNS abnormalities.

name: Other specified non-organ specific systemic autoimmune disorders, not elsewhere classified, associated with anti-RNP antibodies
Superficial frostbite of hip or thigh
Anogenital ulcer of uncertain nature
definition: This denotes the presence of an anogenital skin ulcer but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the ulcer might be of serious significance (e.g. sexually transmitted infection or suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Burn of ankle or foot, deep full thickness or complex burn
synonyms: Burn of ankle or foot, fourth degree
cn_name: 踝或足深全层皮肤烧伤或复合烧伤

name: Congenital primary lymphoedema
definition: Lymphoedema presenting at or soon after birth (< 1year) as a result of lymphatic vessel hypoplasia
synonyms: Hereditary lymphoedema type I | Milroy disease

name: Burn of face except eye or ocular adnexa
synonyms: burn of face of unspecified degree
cn_name: 面部（除外眼或眼附属器）烧伤

name: Lipodystrophy due to peptidic growth factors deficiency
definition: Lipodystrophy due to peptidic growth factors deficiency is a lipodystrophic syndrome characterised by loss of subcutaneous fat layers on the limbs, lipodystrophy in the face and trunk and scleroderma-like skin disorders (thickened skin on the palms and soles and skin pigment changes on the limbs and trunk).
synonyms: Combined insulin, insulin-like growth factor 1 (IGF1) and epidermal growth factor (EGF) deficiency | Hoepffner-Dreyer-Reimers syndrome | Werner-like syndrome due to combined growth factor deficiency

name: Prepubertal vulval lichen sclerosus
definition: Lichen sclerosus of the vulva of prepubertal girls. It may present as early as at one year of age. The signs may be misinterpreted as being due to child sexual abuse.
synonyms: Lichen sclerosus of vulva, prepubertal

name: Pityriasis rubra pilaris type II, atypical adult
definition: An uncommon chronic form of pityriasis rubra pilaris in adults, in which the rapid progression seen in type I is not seen and where eczematous features may be prominent.

name: Acute bacterial paronychia
definition: Acute bacterial paronychia is an acute infection, usually by Staphylococcus aureus, of the paronychial tissues of a digit. It may result from local injury, e.g. a thorn prick in a lateral nail groove, a splinter, torn hangnails or nail biting, but also occurs frequently as an episode during the course of chronic paronychia, when other organisms may be involved, including streptococci, Pseudomonas aeruginosa, coliform organisms and Proteus vulgaris.
synonyms: Acute paronychia | Whitlow

name: Contact dermatitis of perianal skin
definition: Contact dermatitis not specified as irritant or allergic affecting the perianal skin.
synonyms: Perianal contact dermatitis

name: Abrasion of buccal mucosa
definition: An area of the buccal mucosa that has been scraped

name: Trichiasis without entropion upper eyelid
definition: This refers to abnormally positioned eyelashes that grow back toward the eye, touching the cornea or conjunctiva. This can be caused by infection, inflammation, autoimmune conditions, congenital defects, eyelid agenesis and trauma such as burns or eyelid injury. This diagnosis is without a condition in the upper eyelid which the eyelid folds inward. It is very uncomfortable, as the eyelashes constantly rub against the cornea and irritate it.

name: Neonatal necrotising fasciitis
definition: Neonatal necrotising fasciitis is a life-threatening acute necrotising infection of fascia, subcutaneous tissues, and overlying skin similar to the condition seen in adults. It is rare in neonates but, in contrast to the adult form, tends to affect otherwise healthy babies. It has followed omphalitis, mastitis and postoperative wound infection, though preceding sites of infection are not always found. It has more commonly been associated with Staphylococcus aureus than with streptococcal infecti

name: Nail-patella syndrome
definition: Nail patella syndrome is a hereditary osteo-onychodysplasia characterised by nail dysplasia with triangular lunula, hypoplastic or absent patellas, iliac exostoses (`iliac horns') and dysplastic elbows.
synonyms: HOOD - [Hereditary onycho-osteodysplasia] syndrome | Hereditary onycho-osteodysplasia | NPS - [Nail-patella syndrome] (MIM 161200) | Österreicher-Turner syndrome | fong disease | onycho-osteodysplasia

name: Allergic contact dermatitis of female genitalia
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis affecting the vulva and surrounding skin. This is commonly due to feminine hygiene products but rarely may be due to a delayed hypersensitivity reaction to condoms or to semen.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema of female genitalia

name: Lichen planopilaris of scalp
definition: A scarring form of lichen planus involving the hair follicles of the scalp and resulting in a patchy cicatricial alopecia. It may be associated with follicular or other forms of lichen planus in other body areas.
synonyms: Cicatricial alopecia due to lichen planus

name: Secondary oesophageal varices with bleeding
definition: This refers to abnormally dilated veins in the lining of oesophagus with sign of bleeding in the specific disease which is classified elsewhere.
synonyms: Oesophageal varices with bleeding in diseases classified elsewhere

name: Penile psoriasis
definition: Psoriasis may affect any part of the male external genitalia but may not be recognised as such if confined to the glans penis, particularly in uncircumcised men in whom the plaques do not have scale and present merely as red patches.

name: Chrysiasis
definition: Chrysiasis results from excessive parenteral administration of gold salts which, when deposited in the dermis, produce blue-grey or purplish pigmentation of light-exposed skin and sclerae.
synonyms: Chrysoderma

name: Paraneoplastic syndromes involving skin
cn_name: 累及皮肤的副肿瘤综合征

name: Post traumatic eczema
definition: Eczema localised to skin damaged by physical trauma or by chemical or thermal burns. It may appear months or years after the injury has healed, usually with visible scarring.

name: Ulcer of skin of uncertain nature
definition: This denotes the presence of a skin ulcer but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the ulcer might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.
cn_name: 性质未确定的皮肤溃疡

name: Pressure ulceration grade 4 of head
synonyms: stage 4 decubitus ulcer of head

name: Acromegaly or pituitary gigantism
definition: Acromegaly is an acquired disorder related to excessive production of growth hormone (GH) and characterised by progressive somatic disfigurement (mainly involving the face and extremities) and systemic manifestations. The main clinical features are broadened extremities (hands and feet), widened thickened and stubby fingers, and thickened soft tissue. The disease also has rheumatologic, cardiovascular, respiratory and metabolic consequences which determine its prognosis. In the majority of cases
synonyms: Launois syndrome | Marie disease | Overproduction of growth hormone | acromegalia | acromegalic gigantism | acromegaly | anterior pituitary adenoma syndrome | disorders with tall stature | genetic giant | giant
cn_name: 肢端肥大症或垂体性巨人症

name: Permanent cutaneous sensory impairment resulting from cutaneous surgical procedure
