definition: Permanent loss of sensation as the result of damage to sensory nerve from skin surgery

name: Necrolytic acral erythema
definition: Necrolytic acral erythema is a distinctive acrally-located dermatosis affecting patients with active viral hepatitis C. It manifests principally on the dorsal surfaces of the feet and hands as well circumscribed dusky erythematous areas with flaccid blisters in its early stages and elevated scaly psoriasiform plaques in its chronic form. Microscopically there is keratinocyte necrosis in the upper epidermis.

name: Dermatitis or eczema of anogenital region
definition: Dermatitis (eczema) affecting the external genitalia, crural folds and/or perianal skin.
synonyms: dermatitis of anogenital region NOS | eczema of anogenital region NOS
cn_name: 肛门生殖器部位皮炎或湿疹

name: Seborrhoeic dermatitis of unspecified location
synonyms: Seborrhoeic eczema of unspecified location

name: Tuberculoid leprosy
definition: A polar form of leprosy in which cell-mediated immunity is high, bacillary load is low and skin lesions are few. Peripheral nerve involvement is pronounced and may cause severe functional impairment.
synonyms: Hansen disease, tuberculoid | tuberculoid leprosy type T

name: Drug-induced flushing
definition: Flushing provoked by ingestion of drugs. Important causes are vasodilators including calcium channel blocking agents, nitrates and sildenafil, angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors, fumaric acid esters and drugs such as metronidazole when taken together with alcohol.

name: Localised hyperhidrosis
definition: Excessive sweating in specific and localised sites.
synonyms: Focal hyperhidrosis

name: Xanthoma in association with primary combined hyperlipidaemia
definition: Xanthomata associated with primary combined hyperlipidaemia are typically palmar and tuberous.

name: Retroperitoneal lymphatic malformation
synonyms: Retroperitoneal lymphangioma

name: Acute exudative skin eruption
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and in which exudation and crusting are prominent features. Common causes are infected eczema, acute allergic contact dermatitis and impetigo.

name: Cutaneous cysts
synonyms: Follicular cysts of skin and subcutaneous tissue

name: Talc granuloma
definition: Foreign body granulomatous reaction to talc in the skin. This used to be a complication of surgery when talc was used to coat surgical gloves. Talc granulomas may arise from illicit intravenous drug use when the drug in question has been extracted from talc-containing tablets.

name: Adverse cutaneous effects of therapeutic ionizing irradiation
cn_name: 治疗性电离辐射的皮肤不良效应

name: Non-bullous impetigo
definition: Non-bullous impetigo is due to superficial skin infection with either Streptococcus pyogenes or Staphylococcus aureus or both. The very superficial blisters which form in the upper epidermis are soon shed and rarely seen (cf. bullous impetigo) so that it normally presents with areas of superficial oozing and crusting on the skin surface. It often following minor skin injury or on skin damaged by a preexisting dermatosis such as atopic eczema or scabies.
synonyms: crusted impetigo

name: Postinflammatory atrophic scarring of the skin
Certain sexually transmissible bacterial infections affecting skin
definition: Predominantly sexually transmitted bacterial infections affecting the skin.

name: Congenital symblepharon
definition: This is a congenital partial or complete adhesion of the palpebral conjunctiva of the eyelid to the bulbar conjunctiva of the eyeball. It results either from disease (conjunctival sequelae of Trachoma) or trauma. Cicatricial pemphigoid and, in severe cases, rosacea may cause symblepharon.

name: Neurocutaneous melanocytosis
definition: Neurocutaneous melanocytosis (NCM) is a rare congenital neurological disorder characterised by abnormal aggregations of naevomelanocytes within the central nervous system (leptomeningeal melanocytosis) associated with large or giant congenital melanocytic naevi (CMN; see this term). NCM can be asymptomatic or present as variably severe and progressive neurological impairment, sometimes resulting in death. Prevalence is estimated at 1/50,000-1/200,000. The incidence of symptomatic NCM appears to
synonyms: neurocutaneous melanocytosis

name: Tongue spasm
synonyms: tongue spasticity

name: Ankyloglossia
definition: A condition of the tongue, caused by short, tight, lingual frenulum or fusion of the tongue to the floor of the mouth. This condition is characterised by difficulty in speech articulation due to limitation or restriction in tongue movement.
synonyms: Aberrant insertion of frenum of tongue | Aberrant insertion of labial frenulum | Tongue tie | short frenulum linguae | short frenulum of tongue

name: Malignant neoplasms of skin
definition: A primary or metastatic tumour involving the skin. Primary malignant skin tumours most often are carcinomas (either basal cell or squamous cell carcinomas that arise from cells in the epidermis) or melanomas that arise from pigment-containing skin melanocytes. Metastatic tumours to the skin include carcinomas and lymphomas.
synonyms: skin cancer | CA - skin cancer | cancer of skin | malignant neoplasm of skin | melanoma and Non-melanoma skin cancer

name: Postinfectious scleroedema
definition: Postinfectious scleroedema occurs days to months after a bacterial or viral infection. It has been particularly associated with streptococcal sore throat and half the cases occur in children. Spontaneous resolution normally occurs within six months to two years.

name: Sequelae of unspecified frostbite of thorax, abdomen, lower back and pelvis
Pruritus due to neurological disorder
definition: Pruritus resulting from damage or irritation of sensory nerves or their central connections.
synonyms: Neuropathic pruritus
cn_name: 神经性疾病引起的瘙痒症

name: Erythema marginatum rheumaticum
definition: A cutaneous exanthem occurring in up to 10% of cases of rheumatic fever. The rash appears as widespread pink or red macules or papules that expand centrifugally with central clearing to form annular or polycyclic erythematous plaques. The exanthem may persist intermittently for weeks to months.
synonyms: erythema annulare rheumaticum | erythema marginatum rheumaticum

name: PUVA-associated pruritus
definition: Psoralen photochemotherapy-associated pruritus (PUVA itch) tends to affect people with fair skin types and is usually dose-related. Patients describe a crawling under the skin that affects both involved and normal-looking skin.
synonyms: PUVA itch | pruritus due to psoralen photochemotherapy

name: Cutaneous necrosis following injection of filler
definition: Necrosis of skin attributable to the use of a filler for soft tissue augmentation. This can occur due to local arterial occlusion.

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, metacarpus
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, metacarpus

name: Diseases of the skin complicating pregnancy, childbirth or the puerperium
definition: Diseases of the skin or subcutaneous tissue not specifically related to pregnancy but which contribute to increased morbidity during pregnancy, childbirth or the puerperium, for example genetic blistering disorders or lichen sclerosus.

name: Mouth ulcers
definition: Oral ulceration of other specified or unspecified type.
synonyms: Buccal ulceration | Ulcer of mouth | Ulcer of oral mucosa | buccal ulcer | superficial mouth ulcer | ulceration of oral mucosa

name: Oesophageal varices without bleeding in schistosomiasis
definition: These are extremely dilated sub-mucosal veins in the lower third of the oesophagus without bleeding in a parasitic disease caused by several species of trematodes (platyhelminth infection, or \"flukes\"), a parasitic worm of the genus Schistosoma.

name: Ocular late syphilis
definition: This is a late, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with ocular.

name: Hereditary generalised hypotrichosis
definition: Hypotrichosis simplex or hereditary hypotrichosis simplex is a genetic hair defect characterised by reduced pilosity over the scalp and body (with sparse, thin, and short hair) in the absence of other anomalies. A scalp-limited form, hypotrichosis simplex of the scalp (see this term) has also been reported
synonyms: Hypotrichosis 1 (MIM605389) | generalised hypotrichosis simplex

name: Buffalopox
definition: Buffalopox is caused by an orthopox virus related to vaccinia virus. It is acquired in humans by direct inoculation from infected water buffalo. It is generally a mild illness similar to cowpox with just a few lesions on the hands and arms. It leaves minor pock-like scars.

name: Genital melanosis
definition: Macular pigmentation of genital skin with no overt evidence of a preceding inflammatory dermatosis. The pigmentation may vary in its intensity and is typically irregular. The problem usually affects several sites including cutaneous and mucosal surfaces. The pigmentation develops slowly and can be very extensive.

name: Telogen effluvium
definition: Increased shedding of telogen hairs from the scalp. There are numerous triggers of which severe systemic illness and pregnancy are important examples.
synonyms: telogen effluvium NOS

name: Cat-eye syndrome
definition: Cat-eye syndrome is a chromosomal anomaly syndrome characterised by two major features\: anal atresia and coloboma of the iris, from which the name of the syndrome derives. However, patients present with a very heterogeneous range of manifestations, including \: preauricular skin tags or/and pits, cardiac defects, cleft palate and urinary tract or skeletal anomalies and moderate intellectual deficit.
synonyms: Ocular coloboma - imperforate anus | chromosome 22 inversion or duplication

name: Hydroa vacciniforme
synonyms: bazin's hydroa vacciniforme | HV

name: Intraepidermal squamous cell carcinoma
definition: It arises most frequently on chronically sun-exposed glabrous skin of the head and neck or lower legs. It typically presents as single or multiple well-demarcated scaly erythematous patches, nodules or plaques which histologically show extensive keratinocytic atypia. It may develop from preexisting actinic keratosis (Actinic intraepidermal squamous cell carcinoma). Although it is most commonly associated with exposure to ultraviolet radiation, other carcinogens such as arsenic and tar may be imp
synonyms: IEC - [intraepidermal carcinoma] | Intraepidermal carcinoma | Squamous cell carcinoma in situ of skin

name: Squamous cell carcinoma of vulva, HPV associated
Diffuse epidermolytic palmoplantar keratoderma
definition: Diffuse epidermolytic palmoplantar keratoderma of Vörner-Thost (MIM #144200) is an autosomal dominant disorder which is clinically indistinguishable from diffuse non-epidermolytic palmoplantar keratoderma (Unna) but has different histological appearance (epidermolytic hyperkeratosis) and aetiology (keratin 9 mutations). Diffuse keratoderma develops in infancy, leading to confluent keratoderma, sparing dorsal surfaces, with a sharp demarcation and erythematous edge by adult life.
synonyms: Vörner-Thost palmoplantar keratoderma

name: Malignant neoplasm metastasis involving skin of chest wall
Pili bifurcati
definition: Pili bifurcati is an uncommon transitory hair shaft dysplasia characterised by a segmental duplication of the hair shaft\: a ramification generates two parallel branches which fuse to form a single shaft again. Each branch is covered by its own cuticle.

name: Noninfectious erosive or ulcerative disorders of oral mucosa
definition: A group of erosive and ulcerative disorders of oral mucosa without infection.
synonyms: ulcerative disorders of oral mucosa
cn_name: 口腔黏膜非感染性糜烂或溃疡性疾患

name: Chronic excoriation of skin
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature characterised by the presence of multiple excoriations. In some cases the excoriation is secondary to intense pruritus arising either from an underlying systemic disorder such as uraemia or from a pruritic skin disease such as eczema. In many cases, however, psychogenic factors may be responsible.

name: Drug-induced urticaria
definition: Urticaria provoked by drug. This may be due to immunological or non-immunological mechanisms. Mild anaphylactic reactions may cause little more than urticaria but may serve as a warning of more severe reactions if the responsible agent is encountered again. Aspirin is a well-known cause of non-allergic urticaria.

name: Pigmented skin lesion of uncertain nature
definition: This denotes the presence of a pigmented skin lesion but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.
synonyms: Melanocytic skin lesion of uncertain nature | Pigmented skin tumour of uncertain nature
cn_name: 性质未确定的色素沉着性皮肤损害

name: Pilomatrixoma
definition: A benign adnexal neoplasm arising from hair-bearing skin surfaces, usually the head and neck and upper extremities. It usually presents as a solitary, slow-growing nodular mass. Morphologically, it displays differentiation towards the matrix and inner sheath of the normal hair follicle and the hair cortex. Complete surgical excision is usually curative. Occasionally, it may recur.
synonyms: Epithelioma calcificans of Malherbe | Malherbe calcifying epithelioma | Pilomatricoma | calcifying epithelioma | pilomatrixoma | trichomatrioma

name: 2p15p16.1 deletion
definition: 2p15p16.1 microdeletion syndrome is a chromosomal anomaly characterised by developmental delay and facial dysmorphism (receding forehead, telecanthus, epicanthic fold, short and down-slanting palpebral fissures, ptosis, broad and high nasal bridge, retrognathia, flat philtrum, small mouth with high, narrow palate and everted lower lip).

name: Stevens-Johnson and toxic epidermal necrolysis overlap syndrome
definition: A severe reactive skin disorder with features of both toxic epidermal necrolysis and Stevens-Johnson syndrome. It is defined by the presence of mucosal involvement and between 10% and 30% body surface area detachment of skin. It may be regarded as an intermediate form of these two disorders and, as with them, it can in most cases be attributed to a drug.
cn_name: Stevens-Johnson综合征和中毒性表皮坏死松解症重叠综合征

name: Neonatal viral infections involving the skin
cn_name: 新生儿病毒感染性皮肤病

name: Dermatoses resulting from cytotoxic or cancer chemotherapy
cn_name: 由细胞毒性或癌症化疗引起的皮肤病

name: Neurofibromatosis type 1
definition: Neurofibromatosis type 1 (NF1) is an inherited, multi-system, neurocutaneous disorder that predisposes to the development of benign and malignant tumours. Two of the following criteria are required to diagnose NF1\: six or more café au lait patches, neurofibromas, i.e. peripheral nerve sheath tumours manifesting as cutaneous, sub-cutaneous or plexiform lesions, skin-fold freckling, two or more iris Lisch nodules, an optic pathway glioma, a specific bony dysplasia (thinning of the long bone corte
synonyms: NF1 - [Neurofibromatosis type 1] | Neurofibromatosis, peripheral type | Recklinghausen disease | von Recklinghausen disease | von Recklinghausen neuropathy | von recklinghausen disease

name: Skin manifestations of work-related poisoning
definition: Skin manifestations of systemic exposure to toxic substances and poisons at work. Examples include arsenic and heavy metals.

name: Anetoderma of Schweninger-Buzzi
definition: Primary anetoderma without preceding inflammation

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, kyphoscoliotic type
definition: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, kyphoscoliotic type is a type of Ehlers-Danlos syndromes (EDS), a group of hereditary connective tissue diseases characterised by joint hyperlaxity, cutaneous hyperelasticity and tissue fragility, and is characterised from birth onwards by progressive congenital scoliosis, severe muscle hypotonia, hyperextensibility of all joints, and fragile eyeballs. Ocular fragility can lead to retinal haemorrhage, glaucoma, sclera coloration, or even rupture of the ocular globe.
synonyms: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type 6

name: 15q26.3 deletion
synonyms: 15q26 deletion syndrome | Distal 15q deletion syndrome | Monosomy 15q26

name: Vitamin B12 responsive methylmalonic aciduria type cbl B
definition: This refers to vitamin B12 responsive, type cbl B, stemming from several genotypes, all forms of the disorder usually diagnosed in the early neonatal period, presenting progressive encephalopathy, and secondary hyperammonemia.

name: Skin problem resulting from urinary diversion procedure
definition: An entity to be used to link a specific skin problem (e.g. irritant contact dermatitis) attributable to leakage of urine onto skin surrounding a nephrostomy, urostomy, cystostomy or urethrostomy.

name: Immunobullous disorder of undefined type
definition: Any blistering skin disease presumed to be due to the effects of circulating auto-antibodies directed against skin or mucous membrane antigens but of as yet undefined type.

name: Skin disorders associated with prematurity
cn_name: 与早产有关的皮肤疾患

name: Pseudomonas folliculitis
definition: An acute superficial folliculitis acquired by bathing in heated swimming pools and whirlpool baths contaminated by Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Any part of the body surface which has been immersed may be affected.
synonyms: Hot tub folliculitis | hot tub folliculitis

name: Drug-induced photo-onycholysis
synonyms: medicament-induced photo-onycholysis

name: Chronic papular skin disorder
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown, uncertain or unspecified nature and characterised by the presence of multiple skin papules.

name: Tricho-dento-osseous syndrome
definition: Tricho-dento-osseous dysplasia (TDO) belongs to the ectodermal dysplasias and is characterised by curly/kinky hair at birth, enamel hypoplasia with discolouration and molar taurodontism, increased overall bone mineral density (BMD) and increased thickness of the cortical bones of the skull.

name: Oesophageal varices without bleeding in autoimmune hepatitis
definition: These are extremely dilated sub-mucosal veins in the lower third of the oesophagus without bleeding and inflammation of the liver characterised by the presence of inflammatory cells in the tissue of the organ.

name: Subcutaneous granuloma annulare
definition: Subcutaneous granuloma annulare occurs predominantly in children as subcutaneous nodules, especially around the anterior aspects of the lower legs, hands, head, and buttocks. In only about a quarter of cases are they associated with classical dermal granuloma annulare. They bear a resemblance both clinically and histologically to rheumatoid nodules but are not associated with rheumatoid disease.
synonyms: subcutaneous granuloma annulare

name: Madarosis of eyelid or periocular area
definition: Partial or complete loss of eyelashes and/or eyebrow hairs. Alopecia areata and chronic cutaneous lupus erythematosus are well recognised causes. If the underlying cause is known this should be coded as well.

name: Loiasis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the parasitic worm Loa loa. This disease is characterised by Calabar swellings found anywhere on the body (commonly found near joints). This disease may also present with generalised itching, muscle pain, joint pain, fatigue or may be asymptomatic. Transmission is through the bite of an infected fly (genus Chrysops). Confirmation is by identification of adult Loa loa in the skin or eye, Loa loa microfilariae in a blood sample obtained in the day (1000 - 1400
synonyms: African eyeworm disease | Calabar swelling | Eye worm disease of Africa | Loa loa infestation

name: Arsenical keratosis
definition: Arsenical keratoses manifest as multiple punctate hyperkeratotic papules located preferentially on the palms and soles as the result of chronic arsenic poisoning. They may eventuate into invasive squamous cell carcinoma and may act as a pointer to arsenic as the underlying cause of in situ and invasive squamous cell carcinoma elsewhere on the skin.

name: Superficial spreading melanoma, primary
definition: The commonest form of melanoma, superficial spreading malignant melanoma accounts for about 70 per cent of all melanomas. It characteristically presents as an asymptomatic pigmented cutaneous macule which is asymmetrical in shape and displays variability in both hue and pigment intensity. It has a relatively long phase of progressive superficial extension (radial growth) before penetrating deeper into the dermis and entering an invasive vertical growth phase.
synonyms: Pagetoid melanoma
cn_name: 原发性表浅播散性黑色素瘤

name: Porphyria cutanea tarda
definition: Porphyria cutanea tarda (PCT) is due to an accumulation of uroporphyrins in plasma from blockage of the normal haem synthetic pathway in the liver at the level of uroporphyrinogen decarboxylase (URO-D). The majority of cases are sporadic and frequently associated with iron overload. PCT manifests as skin fragility and blistering in light-exposed skin, particularly on the dorsa of the hands, together with hypertrichosis.
synonyms: Heterozygous uroporphyrinogen decarboxylase deficiency | PCT - [porphyria cutanea tarda] | Porphyria, hepatocutaneous type | UROD - [uroporphyrinogen decarboxylase] deficiency | PCT

name: Asymptomatic xerosis cutis
definition: Xerosis cutis without itch or inflammation.

name: Acquired angioedema
definition: Acquired angioedema is clinically similar to hereditary angioedema and is not associated with urticaria. It may be associated with a lymphoproliferative disorder (type I) or may be an isolated phenomenon due to an autoantibody directed against C1 inhibitor (type II).
synonyms: AAE - [Acquired angioneurotic oedema] | Acquired angioneurotic oedema

name: Xanthelasma of eyelid
definition: Xanthelasmata are a form of plane xanthoma which manifest as sharply demarcated yellowish deposits of lipid within the skin of the eyelid. While they are neither harmful nor painful, these minor growths may be disfiguring and may be the presenting sign of hypercholesterolaemia. They are common in people of Asian origin and those from the Mediterranean region.
synonyms: Xanthelasma palpebrarum | Xanthoma of eyelid | plane xanthoma of the eyelids | xanthelasma palpebrarum

name: Palmoplantar keratoderma – oral leukokeratosis – oesophageal carcinoma
definition: This condition is inherited as an autosomal dominant syndrome and characterised by palmoplantar keratoderma, oral precursor lesions particularly on the gums (leukoplakia) and a high lifetime risk of oesophageal cancer.
synonyms: Howel-Evans syndrome | Tylosis with oesophageal cancer | non-epidermolytic palmoplantar keratoderma with carcinoma of the esophagus | TOC | tylosis with esophageal cancer

name: Blue rubber bleb naevus syndrome
definition: Blue rubber bleb nevus (BRBNS) is a rare vascular malformation disorder with cutaneous and visceral lesions frequently associated with serious, potentially fatal bleeding and anaemia. Multifocal venous malformations most commonly involve the skin and gastrointestinal tract but can also affect the brain, kidneys, lungs, eyes, bones and other organs. Consumption coagulopathy may occur. The lesions, which are generally present in childhood but can develop later in life, are usually blue coloured an
synonyms: Bean syndrome (MIM 112200)

name: Lupus erythematosus of oral mucosa
definition: Lupus erythematosus affecting oral mucosa. This may be associated with either cutaneous or systemic lupus erythematosus and presents most commonly as mucosal ulceration. Histologically there is a lichenoid mucositis.

name: Syphilis of vagina
synonyms: syphilitic vagina | syphilitic vaginitis

name: Genetic syndromes affecting the skin
cn_name: 累及皮肤的遗传综合征

name: Keratosis follicularis spinulosa decalvans
definition: Keratosis follicularis spinulosa decalvans is a rare genodermatosis occurring during infancy or childhood, predominantly affecting males, and characterised by diffuse follicular hyperkeratosis associated with progressive cicatricial alopecia of the scalp, eyebrows and eyelashes. Additional findings can include photophobia, corneal dystrophy, facial erythema, and/or palmoplantar keratoderma.
synonyms: KFSD

name: Tertiary yaws
definition: Tertiary yaws develops in <10% of untreated infected individuals after and interval of 5 years or more. The late stage skin lesions are characterised by gummatous nodules with necrotic tissue destruction, followed by debilitating scarring and contracture. Destructive osteitis can result in ulceration of the palate and nasopharynx (‘gangosa’), or bowing of the tibia (sabre shins). Hypertrophic periostitis at periarticular sites can lead to exostosis of the paranasal maxillae (‘goundou’).

name: Drug-induced hypertrichosis
definition: Excessive hair growth due to drug not confined to areas of skin where hair growth is under androgen control. Ciclosporin is a common cause.
synonyms: medicament-induced hypertrichosis

name: Cutaneous mastocytoma
definition: Cutaneous mastocytoma is a limited form of cutaneous mastocytosis which presents with a solitary red pink or yellowish nodule or plaque in infancy or early childhood and in nearly all cases involutes within the first few years of life. Occasionally there may be more than one lesion. Mastocytoma accounts for about 15% of all cases of cutaneous mastocytosis presenting early in life.
synonyms: mast cell neoplasm NOS | mast cell tumour NOS | mastocytoma NOS

name: Trichostasis spinulosa
definition: Trichostasis spinulosa is a common but often underdiagnosed follicular disorder of unknown etiology. Asymptomatic comedo-like lesions that contain keratin and multiple vellus hairs are seen.

name: Berardinelli-Seip congenital lipodystrophy
definition: Berardinelli-Seip congenital lipodystrophy (BSCL) is characterised by the association of lipoatrophy, hypertriglyceridemia, hepatomegaly and acromegaloid features. BSCL belongs to the group of extreme insulin resistance syndromes.
synonyms: BSCL - [Berardinelli-Seip congenital lipodystrophy] | Berardinelli type lipodystrophy | Berardinelli-Seip syndrome | Brunzell syndrome | Lipoatrophic diabetes | berardinelli-seip syndrome | brunzell syndrome | CGL

name: Eosinophilic pustular folliculitis
definition: Eosinophilic pustular folliculitis is an uncommon inflammatory cutaneous reaction pattern of poorly understood aetiology which is characterised by infiltration of pilosebaceous follicles by large numbers of eosinophils. Three distinct clinical patterns are recognised\: classical adult which is predominantly facial and is reported principally from Japan; immunosuppression-associated which is strongly associated with HIV infection and is usually extrafacial; and infantile occurring predominantly i

name: Childhood atopic eczema, nummular pattern
definition: A nummular or discoid pattern of atopic eczema occurring during childhood. It is commoner in children of African or Asian descent than in children of European descent.
synonyms: Childhood atopic dermatitis, nummular pattern | Discoid atopic eczema of the child

name: Skin lesion of uncertain or unspecified nature
definition: To be used where there is either significant uncertainty or alternatively no information as to the nature of a circumscribed skin lesion. This is of particular importance with regard to whether or not the lesion may be malignant.
synonyms: Pigmented skin lesion of unspecified nature | Skin lesion without established diagnosis | Ulcer of skin of unspecified nature | cutaneous lesion NOS | skin lesion NOS
cn_name: 性质未确定或未特指的皮肤损害

name: Thickening of tongue
definition: Thickening (swelling) of tongue is a condition that may be associated but not limited to viral or bacterial infections, allergic reactions, metabolic disturbances, malignancies or trauma.

name: Diabetic necrobiosis lipoidica
definition: Necrobiosis lipoidica associated with diabetes mellitus. About two thirds of cases of necrobiosis lipoidica show such an association. The skin changes may appear before the diabetes becomes clinically apparent.
synonyms: Oppenheim-Urbach disease

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy Type ID
definition: HASN Type ID is an autosomal dominant disorder due to mutations in the ATL1 gene. It presents in early adulthood with severe loss of pain, temperature and vibration sensations, ulcero-mutilation, spastic paraparesis and rare autonomic involvement
synonyms: HSAN1D - [Hereditary sensory autonomic neuropathy Type ID]

name: Unilateral naevoid telangiectasia
definition: Unilateral nevoid telangiectasia is an asymptomatic disorder characterised by telangiectasia involving a dermatome, a group of dermatomes or following Blaschko lines. The restricted distribution is presumed to be due to postzygotic somatic mutations affecting cutaneous vasculature. The disorder may be apparent early in life but onset may only become apparent when the skin is exposed to increased oestrogen levels as at puberty, during pregnancy or from oral contraceptive use. It has also been obs

name: Pyoderma gangrenosum, pustular
definition: A variant of pyoderma gangrenosum associated with flares of inflammatory bowel disease and characterised by the eruption on the extensor aspects of the limbs of multiple discrete painful pustules, each surrounded by an erythematous halo.
synonyms: Pyoderma gangrenosum, atypical pustular

name: Diffuse eosinophilic fasciitis
definition: Also called Shulman disease/diffuse fasciitis, diffuse eosinophilic fasciitis is a rare idiopathic disorder associated with induration of the skin (orange-peel sign) that generally develops rapidly. It is a dermal and hypodermal sclerosis associated with fibrotic thickening of the subcutaneous adipose lobular septa, superficial fascia, and perimysium. Full thickness excisional biopsy of skin lesions revealing fibrosis of the subcutaneous fascia is generally required for diagnosis. Onset follows
synonyms: Diffuse fasciitis | Eosinophilic fasciitis | Fasciitis with eosinophilia syndrome | Shulman syndrome

name: Cutaneous leishmaniasis
definition: Cutaneous leishmaniasis results from bites by sandflies infected by protozoan parasites of the genus Leishmania. Phlebotomus is the principal vector in the Old World (Mediterranean, North Africa, Ethiopia and Asia), where L. major, L. tropica, L. aethiopica and L. donovani infantum predominate. Other sandflies are responsible for transmitting the New World species, L. mexicana and L. brasiliensis. The commonest presentation is with one or more crusted nodules or ulcers on exposed sites which gra
synonyms: Old world cutaneous leishmaniasis | Asian Desert Cutaneous Leishmaniasis | diffuse cutaneous leishmaniasis | Leproid leishmaniasis

name: Zoster without complications
definition: A painful blistering skin eruption following a dermatomal distribution resulting from reactivation of Varicella zoster virus in dorsal nerve root ganglia.

name: Noninfective enteritis or ulcer due to graft-versus-host disease
definition: This refers to a non-infective inflammation of the small intestine and ulcer, due to a common complication following an allogeneic tissue transplant.

name: Cutaneous amyloidosis
definition: Amyloidosis involving the skin. This is most commonly localised to the skin but primary systemic amyloidosis may affect the skin or oral cavity, particularly the tongue.

name: Rosacea
definition: The term rosacea encompasses a spectrum of changes that occur mainly in facial skin but may also involve the eyes. Most patients with rosacea have facial erythema and vascular instability which are variably associated with inflammatory papules and pustules, hypertrophic changes and ocular involvement. The cause of rosacea is unknown. It is doubtful that any single aetiological factor is responsible for the diverse features that comprise this disorder.
synonyms: Acne rosacea | Acne roscea | Acne, erythematosa

name: Chlamydial lymphogranuloma
definition: A disease of the inguinal lymph glands, caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Chlamydia trachomatis. This disease is characterised by a genital ulcer, buboes, abscesses in the groin, blood in faeces, tenesmus, or proctocolitis. Transmission is by sexual contact. Confirmation is by identification of Chlamydia trachomatis in a blood sample or by polymerase chain reaction tests.
synonyms: Durand-Nicolas-Favre disease | Frei disease | Nicholas-Favre disease | chlamydial lymphogranuloma venereum | lymphogranuloma venereum | climatic bubo | durand-nicolas-favre disease | LGV | lymphogranuloma inguinale | poradenitis inguinale

name: Melanoma arising in giant congenital melanocytic naevus
Pyodermatitis–pyostomatitis vegetans
definition: This rare disorder, which may be confined to the oral mucosa (pyostomatitis vegetans) or may affect the skin as well (pyodermatitis vegetans) is strongly associated with inflammatory bowel disease, particularly ulcerative colitis. It has also been associated with lymphoproliferative disorders, acne conglobata, hidradenitis suppurativa and dissecting cellulitis, amongst other conditions. The oral lesions consist of multiple pustules, plaques and erosions, which may have a ‘snail‐track’ appearance
synonyms: Pyodermatitis vegetans | Pyodermite végétante Hallopeau | Pyostomatitis vegetans

name: Telangiectasia macularis eruptiva perstans
definition: Telangiectasia macularis eruptiva perstans (TMEP) is a form of cutaneous mastocytosis in which telangiecatasia is the predominant clinical feature. It usually presents in adult life with red telangiectatic macules, especially on the trunk. Urtication on rubbing is generally not seen. TMEP tends to be very persistent and unresponsive to treatment.

name: Congenital non-bullous ichthyosiform erythroderma
definition: Congenital ichthyosiform erythroderma (CIE) is a variant of autosomal recessive congenital ichthyosis (ARCI; see this term), a rare epidermal disease, characterized by fine, whitish scales on a background of erythematous skin over the whole body.Prevalence is estimated at approximately 1/200,000- 1/1,000,000 individuals.Since birth, patients present with fine white-grayish scales of various sizes associated with erythroderma. Some newborns are encased in a mild collodion membrane (taut, shiny, t
synonyms: Congenital ichthyotic ichthyosis

name: Hypertrophic surgical scar
definition: An elevated surgical scar containing an excess of fibrous tissue which, in contrast to a keloidal scar, does tend to flatten with time.

name: Iatrogenic injuries involving the skin of the neonate
cn_name: 涉及新生儿皮肤的医源性损伤

name: Immunobullous diseases of the skin
definition: A group of disorders characterised by the presence of circulating auto-antibodies directed against specific skin or mucous membrane antigens and resulting in blisters or erosions.

name: Rothmund-Thomson syndrome
definition: Rothmund-Thomson syndrome (RTS) is a genodermatosis in which facial poikiloderma is associated with short stature, sparse scalp and eyebrow hair, juvenile cataracts, dental and skeletal abnormalities and premature ageing. The skin is usually normal at birth but telangiectasia, dyspigmentation and atrophy appear, initially on the cheeks, from 3-6 months of age; nail dystrophy and palmoplantar keratoses are common. RTS is genetically heterogeneous\: two thirds of affected individuals have mutation
synonyms: Poikiloderma congenitale | Poikiloderma of Rothmund-Thomson | Congenital poikiloderma | RTS

name: Systemic mycoses affecting skin
cn_name: 系统性真菌病累及皮肤

name: Paraneoplastic acanthosis nigricans
definition: Paraneoplastic acanthosis nigricans is thought to be due to production by the associated malignant tumour of factors which stimulate epidermal keratinocyte and dermal fibroblast proliferation. It is most commonly linked to underlying gastro-intestinal malignancy. In a third of cases it precedes the diagnosis of cancer. In contrast with benign acanthosis nigricans it often involves the extremities (tripe palms) and oral cavity.
synonyms: Malignant acanthosis nigricans

name: Burn of trunk except perineum or genitalia, epidermal burn
synonyms: Burn of trunk except perineum and genitalia, first degree
cn_name: 躯干（除外会阴或生殖器）表皮烧伤

name: Lichen planopilaris of vulva
definition: Lichen planus centred on hair follicles of vulva and perigenital skin. This may be seen as a component of the Graham-Little syndrome, in which axillary, truncal and scalp skin is also involved.

name: Tyrosinase-positive oculocutaneous albinism
definition: This type of OCA is the commonest form of albinism in Sub-Saharan Africa and is characterised by the development of pigmented naevi and freckles in sun-exposed skin.

name: Lichenoid dermatoses
definition: Conditions other than lichen planus in which there is histological damage to the lower epidermis accompanied by a chronic inflammatory infiltrate in the papillary dermis disturbing the interface between the epidermis and dermis.

name: Burn of perineum or genitalia
synonyms: burn of peritoneum or genitalia of unspecified degree

name: Hyperhidrosis
definition: Excessive sweating. In the localised type, the most frequent sites are the palms, soles, axillae, inguinal folds, and the perineal area. Emotional factors may play a part. Generalised hyperhidrosis may be induced by a hot, humid environment, by fever, or by vigorous exercise.
synonyms: Excessive sweating

name: Neonatal marasmus due to fetal malnutrition
definition: A condition seen in \"small-for-dates\" babies usually as a result of placental insufficiency. The skin appears dry and wrinkled because of a lack of vernix and subcutaneous fat respectively.

name: Hidradenitis suppurativa
definition: Hidradenitis suppurativa is a chronic disease characterised by recurrent, painful, deep-seated, rounded nodules and abscesses due to follicular occlusion with secondary inflammation and destruction of the pilo-sebaceo-apocrine apparatus and extension to the adjacent subcutaneous tissue. Subsequent hypertrophic scarring and suppuration of apocrine gland-bearing skin (axillae, groins, peri-anal and perineal regions) are the main clinical features. Infection and hormonal influence are described but
synonyms: Acne inversa | Inverse acne | Suppurative hidradenitis | acne inversa | pyoderma fistulans significa | verneuil's disease | Acne inversa, familial

name: Sensory disturbance of the vulva
definition: Disorders characterised by vulval pruritus, dysaesthesia or pain.

name: Systemic sclerosis with lung involvement
synonyms: lung disease with systemic sclerosis | pulmonary scleroderma | scleroderma lung disease

name: Pressure ulceration grade 2 of head
synonyms: stage 2 decubitus ulcer of head

name: Drug-induced nail pigmentation
synonyms: medicament-induced nail pigmentation

name: Cutaneous polyarteritis nodosa
definition: Cutaneous polyarteritis nodosa (CPAN) is a rare limited form of polyarteritis nodosa (PAN, see this term), characterized by cutaneous vasculitis and mild and transient extracutaneous manifestations such as mild arthralgia, arthritis,myalgia, and rarely peripheral neuropathy.
synonyms: cutaneous PAN

name: Papular prurigo
definition: Prurigo with a predominance of small papules.
synonyms: Subacute prurigo

name: Late congenital syphilitic osteochondropathy
definition: This is a late congenital, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with osteochondropathy.

name: HIV-associated eosinophilic folliculitis
definition: Eosinophilic folliculitis is a non-infective folliculotropic inflammatory condition of unknown aetiology. It is characterised by recurrent crops of sterile follicular pustules and papules affecting particularly the head and neck and which are often intensely pruritic. It is particularly associated with HIV infection and immunosuppression. It may persist for years with multiple cycles of exacerbations and remissions.

name: Postinflammatory elastolysis and cutis laxa
synonyms: PECL - [postinflammatory elastolysis and cutis laxa]

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, ulna
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, ulna

name: Malignant neoplasm metastasis in skin of labia
synonyms: labial metastasis | labium metastasis

name: Knuckle-biting
definition: Knuckle-biting biting is an obsessive-compulsive disorder characterised by compulsive biting or chewing the skin overlying or between the metacarpophalangeal and proximal interphalangeal joints of the hands.

name: Seborrhoeic dermatitis of eyelids
definition: Seborrhoeic dermatitis of eyelids (seborrhoeic blepharitis) is common. It is characterised by redness and scaling on the skin of the eyelids with variable involvement of the eyelid margins.
synonyms: Seborrhoeic blepharitis | blepharitis in seborrhoeic eczema

name: Burn of perineum or genitalia, superficial partial thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of perineum or genitalia, superficial second degree
cn_name: 会阴或生殖器浅层部分皮层烧伤

name: Induced pemphigus
definition: Induced pemphigus is a chronic autoimmune skin diseases characterised by superficial blister formation precipitated by an exogenous stimulus, usually a drug, mediated by auto-antibodies to the desmosome components desmoglein 1 and less commonly desmoglein 3.
synonyms: Drug-induced pemphigus

name: BCG-induced lupus vulgaris
definition: Lupus vulgaris resulting from BCG immunization \[Dermatology TAG\]

name: Oral mucositis due to graft-versus-host disease
definition: This is the painful inflammation and ulceration of the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, usually as an adverse effect of chemotherapy and radiotherapy treatment for cancer, due to a common complication following an allogeneic tissue transplant.

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to nickel
Classical adult eosinophilic pustular folliculitis
definition: This variant of eosinophilic pustular folliculitis is not associated with immunosuppression and has been reported principally from Japan. It manifests as a chronic relapsing inflammatory dermatosis typically located on facial skin with recurrent annular clusters of sterile follicular papules and pustules forming plaques with central clearing and peripheral extension.
synonyms: Ofuji disease

name: Angular cheilitis due to bacterial infection
definition: Angular cheilitis associated with bacterial infection, most commonly Staphylococcus aureus but also beta-haemolytic streptococci. Bacterial infection is at least as important a cause of angular cheilitis as candidosis, though co-infection is not uncommon.

name: Benign proliferations or neoplasms of cutaneous blood vessels
definition: This group is comprised principally of benign vascular neoplasms of infancy and of pyogenic granuloma, a reactive proliferation of cutaneous blood vessels.

name: Neutrophilic eccrine hidradenitis
definition: Neutrophilic eccrine hidradenitis is an uncommon cutaneous eruption condition characterised histologically by necrosis of the eccrine epithelium and eccrine secretory coil with a dense surrounding neutrophilic infiltrate. The typical clinical presentation is of solitary or disseminated purpuric macules, nodules or plaques arising on the limbs, neck, face and ears 8–10 days after the start of chemotherapy for malignancy, especially acute myeloid leukaemia. It is often associated with fever and ma
synonyms: chemotherapy-associated eccrine hidradenitis | drug-associated eccrine hidradenitis

name: Cutaneous reactions to moths or butterflies
definition: Adverse reactions to adult moths and butterflies (lepidopterism) are rare. Moths of the Hylesia genus may release \"clouds\" of bristles from their abdomens\: these can penetrate human skin and cause an irritant dermatitis.
synonyms: Lepidopterism | caterpillar dermatitis

name: Poikiloderma with neutropaenia
synonyms: Poikiloderma with neutropaenia, Clericuzio type

name: Acute skin injury due to skin contact with corrosive substance
cn_name: 皮肤接触腐蚀性物质引起的急性皮肤损伤

name: Simple primary lymphoedema
definition: Primary lymphoedema is the only manifestation

name: Sequelae of superficial frostbite of abdominal wall, lower back and pelvis
Congenital syphilitic pneumonia
synonyms: early congenital syphilitic pneumonia

name: Pili multigemini
definition: This describes an uncommon developmental defect resulting from fusion of a group of adjacent hair follicles such that multiple hairs from a composite follicular structure emerge through a single pilosebaceous canal. Multigeminate follicles occur mainly on the face, especially along the lines of the jaw. Tufts of hair may be seen emerging from a few or many follicles.

name: Burn of back, full thickness burn
synonyms: Full thickness burn of any part of back

name: Phakomatosis spilorosea
synonyms: Phakomatosis pigmentovascularis type 3

name: Genetic defects of nails or nail growth
cn_name: 甲或甲生长的遗传性缺陷

name: Pruritus due to skin disorder
definition: Pruritus due to skin disorder, especially those such as xerosis cutis or psoriasis where itch may occur but is not an inherent component of the disorder.
synonyms: Pruritus secondary to skin disorder
cn_name: 皮肤疾病引起的瘙痒症

name: Zoster anterior uveitis
definition: This is a viral disease characterised by a painful skin rash with blisters in a limited area on one side of the body, often in a stripe. This diagnosis is with inflammation of the uvea.
synonyms: Herpes zoster anterior uveitis | Iridocyclitis in zoster | Iritis due to Herpes zoster | Zoster iritis | zoster iridocyclitis

name: BCG-induced regional lymphadenopathy
definition: localised regional lymphadenopathy secondary to BCG immunization \[Dermatology TAG\]

name: Corns or callosities
definition: Callosities are areas of focal hyperkeratosis due to repeated friction and pressure. A corn is a sharply demarcated callosity occurring over a bony prominence, usually on the foot, and is painful.

name: Lymphomatoid papulosis
definition: Lymphomatoid papulosis is a proliferation of T-cells, often clonal, characterised clinically by the appearance of crops of dome-shaped papules and nodules which tend to ulcerate and then heal with scarring.
synonyms: LyP

name: Hypohidrosis due to genetic abnormalities of eccrine gland structure or function
definition: Hypohidrosis due to a heritable disorder of sweat gland or duct development. Sweating may be severely diminished or absent due to a paucity or absence of eccrine glands or to defective autonomic innervation. An absence of sweating leads to an inability to thermoregulate by evaporative cooling, and hyperthermia can occur with physical exertion or in a warm environment.
cn_name: 小汗腺结构或功能的基因异常引起的少汗症

name: Cutis laxa with severe pulmonary, gastrointestinal and urinary anomalies
synonyms: URDS - [Urban-Rifkin-Davis syndrome] (MIM 613177) | Urban-Rifkin-Davis syndrome

name: Gnathostomiasis due to Gnathostoma spinigerum
synonyms: Gnathostoma spinigerum infestation

name: Hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia, Christianson-Fourie type
definition: Hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia, Christianson-Fourie type is a rare ectodermal dysplasia syndrome characterized by tricho- and onychodysplasia in association with cardiac rhythm abnormalities. Patients present with sparse scalp hair and eyelashes, absent or sparse eyebrows, dystrophic thickened nails (on fingers distal end may be lifted from the nail bed) and supraventricular tachicardia or sinus bradicardia.

name: X-linked hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy with deafness
definition: X-linked hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy with deafness is characterised by the association of an axonal sensory and autonomic neuropathy with hearing loss.

name: Angular cheilitis exacerbated by mechanical factors
definition: Angular cheilitis resulting from factors, principally loss of dentition, which reduce the vertical height between the underside of the mandible and the maxilla. Maceration of the resultant oblique folds of redundant skin at the corners of the mouth results from constant contamination with saliva.

name: Hydroxyethyl starch-induced pruritus
definition: Hydroxyethyl starch-induced pruritus is an intense itching, lasting for as long as one year, occurring following hydroxyethyl starch intravenous infusion for vascular insufficiency.

name: Xerostomia
definition: Dry mouth. This may result from many causes including dehydration, salivary gland dysfunction, suppression of saliva production by drugs (e.g. anticholinergics) or habitual mouth-breathing.

name: Skin damage resulting from acquired neuropathy
Acute sarcoidosis with erythema nodosum
definition: This is an acute, acquired syndrome involving abnormal collections of chronic inflammatory cells (granulomas) that can form as nodules in multiple organs. This diagnosis is with an inflammation of the fat cells under the skin (panniculitis) characterised by tender red nodules or lumps that are usually seen on both shins.
synonyms: Löfgren syndrome

name: Simple lentigo
definition: The most common form of the skin disorder lentigo, characterised by a single or multiple benign, pigmented macules of unknown etiology that are present at birth or develop in early childhood.
synonyms: Lentigo simplex | Naevus incipiens | multiple lentiginosis | simple lentigo

name: Hamartomata derived from dermal connective tissue
definition: Hamartomatous malformations of dermal collagen and elastin.
cn_name: 真皮结缔组织来源的错构瘤

name: Tuberculids
definition: The tuberculids are a heterogeneous group of cutaneous immune-mediated reactions to foci of tuberculosis in other organs

name: Cardio-facio-cutaneous syndrome
definition: Cardiofaciocutaneous (CFC) syndrome is a multiple congenital anomalies/mental retardation (MCA/MR) syndrome characterised by psychomotor delay, muscular hypotonia, feeding problems, short stature, relative macrocephaly, typical face, ectodermal abnormalities consisting typically of sparse and curly hair, absent eyebrows, ulerythema ophryogenes and congenital heart defects (mainly pulmonic stenosis, atrial septal defects and hypertrophic cardiomyopathy).

name: Familial premature canities
synonyms: Familial premature greying of the hair

name: Occupationally-acquired disorders of skin pigmentation
definition: Disturbances of skin pigmentation attributable to work. This may be due not only to decreased or increased melanin but also to other pigments such as silver (argyria).

name: Primary cutaneous coccidioidomycosis
definition: Coccidioidomycosis may rarely result from direct inoculation, usually through a puncture of the skin by a thorn or other vegetative structure. The infection generally remains confined to this area with local lymphangitic spread and is not considered indicative of disseminated disease. Coccidioidal serology tests may be positive.
synonyms: Coccidioidomycosis of skin due to direct inoculation
cn_name: 原发性皮肤球孢子菌病

name: Miscellaneous skin disorders in the neonate
Irritant contact dermatitis due to other agents
synonyms: irritant contact eczema due to other specified agent or mechanism

name: Tuberculosis of anal canal
definition: Periorificial tuberculosis involving the anus and anal canal. It is usually secondary to tuberculosis of the digestive tract.

name: Skin infection classified elsewhere due to community-acquired methicillin resistant Staphylococcus aureus
synonyms: Skin infection classified elsewhere due to community-acquired MRSA

name: Burn of abdominal wall, deep partial thickness burn
Photosensitivity due to drug
definition: A photosensitive skin reaction to a medicament, most commonly a phototoxic reaction to a systemically administered drug, although photoallergy to drugs may rarely occur.

name: Primary cutaneous blastomycosis
definition: Primary cutaneous blastomycosis is rare. It is due to direct inoculation of the fungus into traumatised skin and is seen principally in laboratory workers (e.g. pathologists carrying out autopsy examinations). After inoculation, an erythematous, indurated area with a chancre appears in 1–2 weeks with associated lymphangitis and lymphadenopathy. There may be some constitutional reaction. There is a strong tendency towards spontaneous recovery.

name: Symptomatic early neurosyphilis
definition: A rare manifestation of syphilis which usually occurs within the first twelve months of infection. The clinical forms of the condition include acute or subacute meningitis, cranial nerve dysfunction and inflammatory cerebral vasculitis presenting as stroke. The risk is greatly increased by co-infection with HIV.
synonyms: Acute syphilitic meningitis

name: Burning mouth syndrome
definition: Chronic burning mouth pain is chronic orofacial pain with an intraoral burning or dysaesthetic sensation that recurs for more than two hours per day on 50 % of the days over more than three months, without evident causative lesions on clinical investigation and examination. It is characterised by significant emotional distress (anxiety, anger/frustration or depressed mood) or interference with orofacial functions such as eating, yawning, speaking etc. Chronic burning mouth pain is multifactorial
synonyms: BMS - [burning mouth syndrome] | Orodynia | Sore mouth syndrome

name: Hypoparathyroidism - deafness - renal disease
definition: Hypoparathyroidism - deafness - renal disease, or HDR syndrome is an inherited condition consisting of hypoparathyroidism, bilateral sensorineural deafness and renal disease (nephrotic syndrome, cystic kidney, renal dysplasia, hypoplasia or aplasia, pelvicalyceal deformity, vesicoureteral reflux, chronic renal failure, haematuria, proteinuria and renal scarring).
synonyms: Barakat syndrome | HDR - [Hypoparathyroidism - deafness - renal disease] syndrome

name: Mucous membrane pemphigoid with ocular involvement
definition: Mucous membrane pemphigoid (MMP) involving the conjunctivae is also known as ocular pemphigoid. This may be confined to the conjunctivae or may be associated with involvement of other sites as well. Its importance lies in its potential to cause loss of vision and it may thus warrant more aggressive therapy than would be considered for MMP of other sites.
synonyms: Cicatricial pemphigoid involving the eye | Ocular cicatricial pemphigoid | Ocular pemphigoid

name: Genetic disorders affecting dermal collagen, elastin or other matrix proteins
definition: A heterogeneous group of disorders due to genetically-determined abnormalities of dermal structural proteins including collagen and elastin.
cn_name: 累及真皮胶原、弹性蛋白或其他基质蛋白的遗传性障碍

name: Pruritus due to other specified drug
definition: Pruritus without rash attributable to other specified drug.

name: Gardner syndrome
definition: Gardner syndrome develops adenomatous polyps throughout the gastrointestinal tract, accompanied by extracolonic manifestations, including periampullary adenomas, papillary carcinoma of the thyroid, hepatoblastoma, osteomas of the mandible and skull, epidermal cysts, and desmoid tumours. Gardner syndrome is a term used to refer to patients in whom these extraintestinal features are unusually prominent.
synonyms: familial colorectal polyposis | familial polyposis of the colon | GS

name: Burn of breast, superficial partial thickness burn
Skin problem resulting from gastrostomy
definition: An entity to be used to link a specific skin problem (e.g. psoriasis or parastomal pyoderma gangrenosum) to its proximity to a gastrostomy.

name: Adverse reaction to BCG immunization
definition: Other adverse reaction to BCG immunization \[Dermatology TAG\]

name: Digital fibrokeratoma
definition: Digital fibrokeratoma is a benign fibrous tumour that arises most commonly in the periungual area. Multiple periungual fibrokeratomas are a characteristic feature of tuberous sclerosis. They tend to arise around at the time of puberty.
synonyms: Acquired digital fibrokeratoma | Acquired periungual fibrokeratoma | Acral fibrokeratoma | Koenen tumour | Periungual fibroma | acquired digital fibrokeratoma

name: Osteoma cutis
definition: Ectopic bone formation in the skin or subcutis.
synonyms: Cutaneous ossification | osteoma cutis

name: Congenital blepharophimosis
definition: This is a congenital condition where the patient has bilateral ptosis with reduced lid size, vertically and horizontally. The nasal bridge is flat and there is hypoplastic orbital rim. Both the vertical and horizontal palpebral fissures (eyelid opening) are shortened.

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type II
definition: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy, type 2 (HSAN2) is an autosomal recessive disorder characterised by profound and universal sensory loss involving large and small fibre nerves, marked hypotonia, painless fractures, trophic changes, tonic pupils, constipation, apnoeic spells.
synonyms: HSAN2 - [Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type 2]

name: Occupational phototoxic reaction to skin contact with plant matter
definition: Phototoxic reactions due to occupational exposure to photoactive plant constituents. Those at risk include gardeners, ground maintenance workers (\"strimmer dermatitis\") and market gardeners (e.g. celery-picking).

name: Knuckle pads
definition: These are benign, asymptomatic, well-circumscribed, smooth, firm, skin-coloured papules, nodules, or plaques, located in the skin over the dorsal aspects of the metacarpophalangeal (MCP) and interphalangeal (IP) joints.
synonyms: Garrod pads | holoderma

name: Immunosuppression-associated eosinophilic pustular folliculitis
definition: Eosinophilic pustular folliculitis (EPF) arising in the setting of HIV/AIDS or other cause of immunosuppression. In contrast to classical adult EPF it tends to be highly pruritic and is more likely to affect other areas than the face. It may paradoxically appear or worsen after initiation of highly active antiretroviral therapy (HAART) and accompany the immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome (IRIS).

name: Cutaneous malakoplakia
definition: Malakoplakia is a rare chronic suppurative granulomatous disorder due to inadequate bacterial killing within macrophages and monocytes. It is closely associated with impaired immunity such as seen with HIV infection, diabetes and organ transplantation. It most commonly involves the genitourinary or gastrointestinal tracts and may present with cutaneous nodules, plaques, ulcers or draining sinuses affecting particularly the vagina, vulva and perianal skin.
synonyms: cutaneous malakoplakia

name: Neonatal nutritional zinc deficiency
definition: An eruption clinically indistinguishable from acrodermatitis enteropathica but due to nutritional deficiency rather than a genetically-determined zinc malabsorption as in the former condition. It occurs in breast-fed infants when there is insufficient zinc in the mother's breast milk to fulfil the needs of the growing infant. Premature infants appear to be at greatest risk as the have extra requirements for zinc and absorb zinc less well than mature infants. It responds rapidly and dramatically

name: Urachal diverticulum
definition: Urachal diverticulum is an out pouching of the bladder at the insertion site of the urachus. It occurs when the umbilical end but not the bladder end of the urachus had closed.
synonyms: Prolapse of urachus

name: Mid-dermal elastolysis
definition: A rare, acquired, dermis elastic tissue disease characterized by asymptomatic, well-demarcated, symmetric patches and/or plaques of finely wrinkled skin arranged parallel to skin cleavage lines (type I), associated with perifollicular papular protrusions (type II) or with persistent reticular erythema (type III), occurring predominantly on the shoulders, trunk, back, and proximal extremities, associating, on histopathology, a selective loss of elastic tissue in the midreticular dermis. Erythema

name: Connexin palmoplantar keratoderma with sensorineural deafness
definition: Palmoplantar keratoderma - deafness syndrome is characterised by the association of progressive perceptive hearing loss and non-epidermolytic palmoplantar keratoderma.
synonyms: Cicatrizing palmoplantar keratoderma with hearing loss (Vohwinkel) | Congenital deafness with keratopachydermia or constrictions of fingers or toes | Vohwinkel keratoderma (MIM 124500)

name: Genetic syndromes with alopecia or hypotrichosis
definition: Hereditary syndromes in which sparse or absent hair is a component
cn_name: 伴脱发或少毛的遗传综合征

name: Granulomatous rosacea
definition: Granulomatous rosacea is an uncommon and distinctive condition which is accepted as a variant of rosacea. It is characterised by chronic flesh-coloured, reddish-yellow or yellow-brown papules particularly affecting the skin around the eyes, nose and mouth. Lesions may appear singly or in profusion. The intervening skin is usually normal. Diascopy reveals the granulomatous nature of the papules and histology may show either caseating or non-caseating epithelioid-cell granulomas. It has been sugge
synonyms: Lupoid rosacea of Lewandowsky

name: Dermatitis or eczema of hands or feet
definition: Dermatitis (eczema) involving the hands and/or feet.
synonyms: Dermatitis of hands and feet | Eczema of hands and feet

name: Neonatal toxic erythema
definition: Neonatal toxic erythema is a common rash in neonates, appearing in up to half of newborns carried to term, usually between day 2-5 after birth; it does not occur outside the neonatal period and typically resolves within first two weeks of life. It is characterised by blotchy erythema with crops of evanescent small white or yellow papules or pustules. It is a benign condition and is thought to cause no discomfort to the baby.
synonyms: Neonatal erythema toxicum | Toxic erythema of the newborn | erythema toxicum | toxic erythema of the newborn

name: Cutaneous vascular disorders caused or exacerbated by occupation
Infantile atopic eczema
definition: Infantile atopic eczema is defined as atopic eczema present during the first year of life. It typically first manifests between the ages of 2 and 6 months\: approximately 50% of people with atopic eczema first present in infancy. The face and non-flexural areas are commonly affected. The napkin area tends to be relatively spared. Involvement of the limb flexures, as is typical in childhood atopic eczema, is also often seen in infancy.
synonyms: Infantile atopic dermatitis

name: Apolipoprotein A-I deficiency
definition: This refers to the deficiency in a protein that in humans is encoded by the APOA1 gene. It has a specific role in lipid metabolism.

name: Actinomycetoma
definition: Actinomycetoma is a chronic progressive subcutaneous infection caused by implantation of aerobic branching actinomycetes through a skin wound. These organisms are filamentous bacteria which live as saprophytes in soil or on plants; the commonest infecting agents are Nocardia brasiliensis, Actinomadura madurae and Streptomyces somaliensis. The earliest stage of infection is a firm painless nodule but with time the whole area becomes hard and swollen with multiple papules, pustules and draining si
synonyms: Actinomycotic Madura foot | Actinomycotic mycetoma | Mycetoma due to filamentous bacteria

name: Anonychia with bizarre flexural pigmentation
definition: Anonychia with flexural pigmentation syndrome is characterised by anonychia and skin abnormalities (hyper- and hypopigmentation in axillae and groins, dry palmar and plantar skin leading to sore and cracked soles).
synonyms: Anonychia with flexural pigmentation (MIM 106750)

name: Lichenoid keratosis
definition: Lichenoid keratosis presents clinically as a solitary, usually asymptomatic, pink or brown and variably scaly papule located typically on the upper chest or forearms. Histologically it is characterised by a lichenoid infiltrate with vacuolar degeneration of the basal layer of the epidermis. It is thought to represent a destructive inflammatory reaction to the presence of an actinic lentigo, actinic keratosis or seborrhoeic keratosis.
synonyms: Lichen planus-like keratosis | benign lichenoid keratosis | BLK | lichen planus-like keratosis | LPLK | SLK | solitary lichen planus | solitary lichenoid keratosis

name: Dermatoses from distant or systemic viral infection
cn_name: 远隔或全身性病毒感染引起的皮肤病

name: Spitzoid tumour of uncertain malignant potential
definition: A spindle cell and epithelioid cell melanocytic neoplasm in which there are sufficient features distinguishing it from a benign Spitz naevus to cast doubt on its benign nature. These atypical features include development in adult life, asymmetry, large diameter (>6 and especially >10 mm), significant thickness (particularly subcutaneous extension), lack of “maturation” and nodule formation, cytological atypia and a high mitotic rate.
synonyms: Atypical Spitz naevus | STUMP - [Spitzoid tumour of uncertain malignant potential] | atypical Spitz tumour | atypical Spitz tumour of uncertain malignant potential
cn_name: 恶性潜能未定的斯皮茨样肿瘤

name: Pityriasis rosea
definition: Pityriasis rosea is an acute, self-limiting skin disease, probably infective in origin, affecting mainly children and young adults, and characterised by a distinctive skin eruption and minimal constitutional symptoms. The cause of pityriasis rosea is uncertain, but many epidemiological and clinical features suggest that an infective agent may be implicated. In the majority of cases the disease follows a characteristic course whereby a so-called herald patch, larger than subsequent lesions, appea
synonyms: Gibert pityriasis | Pityriasis circinata | pityriasis rosea gibert | roseola annulata

name: Burn of back, any part, superficial partial thickness burn
Lichenoid drug eruption
synonyms: Drug-induced lichenoid eruption

name: Neonatal staphylococcal necrotising fasciitis
definition: Neonatal necrotising fasciitis due to Staphylococcus aureus

name: Erdheim-Chester disease
definition: Erdheim-Chester disease is a Class IIa (non-Langerhans cell) histiocytosis of unknown aetiology characterised by a xanthogranulomatous infiltration of viscera, bones, retroperitoneum and skin by lipid-laden histiocytes and surrounding fibrosis. It most commonly presents in adult life with bone pain, typically around the knees and ankles. Exophthalmos, diabetes insipidus, cutaneous xanthomata, pulmonary fibrosis and a broad range of complications resulting from retroperitoneal and CNS infiltratio

name: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome type 6A
synonyms: ehlers-danlos syndrome type 6A

name: Acute erythematous skin eruption
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists predominantly of diffuse cutaneous erythema. A classical cause is scarlet fever but reactions to other bacterial toxins, drugs and certain foods or acute graft-versus-host disease may present with a similar picture.

name: Diabetic scleroedema
definition: Diabetic scleroedema is probably the most common from of scleroedema though is often not recognised. It is associated particularly with obese type II diabetics. The back of the neck and shoulders are usually the most prominently affected areas. It tends to be persistent but is often asymptomatic.   Always assign an additional code for the type of diabetes mellitus.
synonyms: Buschke scleredema | Diabetic scleredema | Scleroedema adultorum | Scleroedema diabeticorum | Scleroedema of Buschke

name: Dermatoses provoked by contact with irritant or noxious substance
definition: This group includes contact dermatitis and chemical burns due to exposure to skin irritants or to caustic or corrosive substances.

name: Argininosuccinic aciduria
definition: Argininosuccinic aciduria is an autosomal recessive inherited deficiency of argininosuccinate lyase, an enzyme involved in the urea cycle that leads to severe hyperammonemic coma in neonates or, in childhood, to hypotonia, growth failure, anorexia and chronic vomiting or behavioural disorders. Onset can also occur later with hyperammonemic coma or behavioural disorders that simulate psychiatric disorders.
synonyms: Argininosuccinase deficiency | Argininosuccinate lyase deficiency | argininosuccinic acidaemia | metabolic disorder of arginosuccinic acid

name: Irritant contact dermatitis of lower leg
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis affecting the skin of the lower leg(s). Contamination of clothing or the inside of rubber boots with irritants such as cement may be responsible.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema of lower leg

name: Oral-facial-digital syndrome type 1
definition: Orofaciodigital syndrome type 1 (OFD1) is characterised by malformations of the face (frontal bossing, facial asymmetry, hypertelorism, broadened nasal bridge, facial milia), oral cavity (pseudoclefting of the upper lip, cleft palate and tongue, high arched palate, tethering of the tongue by frenulae, abnormal dentition), and digits (syndactyly, brachydactyly, clinodactyly, polydactyly).
synonyms: OFD1 - [orolfacialdigital syndrome 1] | Orofaciodigital syndrome type 1 | Papillon-Léage-Psaume syndrome

name: Cutaneous microvascular disturbances due to paroxysmal nocturnal haemoglobinuria-associated platelet plugs
definition: Skin changes including retiform purpura, petechial haemorrhages, haemorrhagic bullae and leg ulcers attributable to paroxysmal nocturnal haemoglobinuria.

name: Filiform warts
synonyms: digitate warts

name: Sea anemone dermatitis
definition: Envenomation by sea anemones produces painful irregular erythematous papules and plaques at points of contact with their tentacles.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 3 of sacrum
synonyms: stage 3 decubitus ulcer of sacrum

Skin complications of BCG immunisation
definition: Complications secondary to immunization with attenuated Mycobacterium bovis (Bacillus Calmette-Guérin or BCG).

name: Focal oral mucinosis
definition: #Draft# Oral focal mucinosis (OFM) is an uncommon clinicopathological entity which is considered to be the oral counterpart of cutaneous focal mucinosis and/or cutaneous myxoid cyst. It is comprised of a clinically elevated mass with a histological picture of localised areas of myxomatous connective tissue.

name: Cockayne syndrome
definition: Cockayne syndrome (CS) is a multisystem condition characterised by short stature, a characteristic facial appearance, premature aging, photosensitivity, progressive neurological dysfunction, and intellectual deficit. The annual incidence of CS is close to 1/200,000 in European countries. Disease severity and the age of onset are variable. In classical type I CS, the first symptoms usually appear during the first year of life. Early-onset cases with more severe symptoms (type II) and late-onset c

name: Sequelae of superficial frostbite of ankle or foot
Composite graft failure
definition: Failure of composite graft tissue (e.g. skin and cartilage) to engraft as intended

name: Pityriasis alba
definition: A common low grade inflammatory dermatosis of unknown aetiology in which multiple small finely scaling macules appear on the face, and less commonly the shoulders and upper arms, of children. These may initially be mildly erythematous but soon become hypopigmented with fine surface scale. Histopathologically there is a mild subacute spongiotic dermatitis with reduction in melanin. It is more prominent in children with pigmented skin and may give rise to concern about vitiligo.

name: Congenital malalignment of the great toenails
definition: Congenital malalignment of the great toenail is a congenital deformity of one or both great toenails resulting from lateral rotation of the nail matrix with respect to the longitudinal axis of the distal phalanx. Ingrowth of the toenail and onychogryphosis are common complications. The deformity may correct itself spontaneously with time.

name: Submammary intertriginous candidosis
definition: Secondary infection of submammary intertrigo with Candida species.
synonyms: Candidosis of submammary skin

name: Malignant neoplasm metastasis in skin of inguinal region
synonyms: inguinal metastasis

name: Extragenital condylomata acuminata
definition: Anogenital warts transmitted to extragenital sites (i.e. beyond the anogenital region). This may be through autoinoculation of anogenital wart virus to moist, intertriginous sites on the abdomen or under the breasts, or as a result of sexual activity, particularly to the lips and oral cavity.
synonyms: Anogenital warts affecting sites other than the anogenital area | Extragenital anogenital warts

name: Overgranulating wound of skin
definition: A surgical or other skin wound in which healing is retarded by the production of exuberant granulation tissue at the surface of the wound.
synonyms: Hypergranulating wound of skin

name: Benign lipoma of spermatic cord
definition: A benign adipose tissue neoplasm of the spermatic cord. This is the most common tumour amongst the benign paratesticular lesions.

cn_name: 累及生殖器和肛周区域的皮肤障碍

name: Drug-induced urticaria, angioedema or anaphylaxis
definition: Adverse reaction to drugs due to release of histamine or vasoactive kinins.

Vulval pemphigus

name: Dyspigmentation following cutaneous laser surgery
definition: Cutaneous pigmentary disturbance with either increased or decreased pigmentation attributable to laser surgery

name: Staphylococcal scarlatina
definition: An exanthem mediated by staphylococcal toxins from distant Staphylococcus aureus infection but without the systemic complications seen in staphylococcal toxic shock syndrome, of which it may be regarded as a mild form. Clinically it resembles scarlet fever.

name: Frostbite with tissue necrosis of trunk, not elsewhere classified
synonyms: frostbite with tissue necrosis of trunk NOS

name: Secondary impetiginisation of the skin
definition: Secondary infection of dermatoses such as atopic eczema by streptococci or staphylococci.
synonyms: Impetiginisation of other dermatoses | Impetiginized eczema

name: Lymphatic malformation of small intestine
synonyms: Lymphangioma of small intestine

name: Traction alopecia
definition: Traction alopecia is a form of permanent scarring alopecia brought about by hair styles that impose sustained pulling on the hair roots. The clinical features in the many variants of this syndrome include folliculitis, hair casts, reduction in hair density with vellus hairs and sometimes broken hairs in the affected areas, and eventually scarring alopecia. Traction alopecia is seen most commonly in African and Afro-Caribbean hair styles where the hair is tightly braided. The hair loss commonly b

name: Burn of head or neck except face, deep full thickness or complex burn
synonyms: Burn of head and neck except face, fourth degree
cn_name: 头或颈部（除外面部）深全层皮肤烧伤或复合烧伤

name: Allergy to substances in contact with the skin
definition: This group includes both allergic contact sensitization and the cutaneous expressions of that sensitization, allergic contact dermatitis, photo-allergic contact dermatitis, allergic contact urticaria and protein contact dermatitis.

name: BIDS syndrome
definition: BIDS syndrome (or Amish Brittle Hair syndrome) is a form of trichothiodystrophy (sulphur-deficient brittle hair) characterised by Brittle hair, Intellectual impairment, Decreased fertility, and Short stature.
synonyms: Amish brittle hair syndrome | Trichothiodystrophy type D

name: Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura
definition: This condition is idiopathic. This condition is characterised by abnormality of blood coagulation causing extensive microscopic clots to form in the small blood vessels throughout the body resulting in low platelet count. This condition may present with seizures, hemiplegia, paresthesias, visual disturbance, and aphasia or anaemia. Confirmation is by identification of thromboses.
synonyms: Moschcowitz disease | Moschcowitz syndrome | TTP - [thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura]

synonyms: demodicoidosis

name: Congenital ectropion
synonyms: congenital ectropion of eyelid

name: Periorificial tuberculosis
definition: Orificial or periorificial tuberculosis results from autoinoculation of M. tuberculosis in individuals with impaired immunity and advanced internal tuberculosis (especially pulmonary or intestinal). It presents as one or more undermined ulcers within or around a natural body orifice.
synonyms: Acute tuberculous ulcer | Orificial tuberculosis | Tuberculosis cutis orificialis

name: Posterior blepharitis
definition: Posterior blepharitis is inflammation of the eyelids secondary to dysfunction of the meibomian glands. Like anterior blepharitis it is a bilateral chronic condition and manifested by a broad spectrum of symptoms involving the lids including inflammation and plugging of the meibomian orifices and production of abnormal secretion upon pressure over the glands. It may be associated with skin rosacea.
synonyms: rosacea posterior blepharitis | rosacea-associated blepharitis

name: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa
definition: Junctional (lucidolytic) epidermolysis bullosa is due to defects at the level of the lamina lucida of the epidermal basement membrane. The generalised severe form (Herlitz) is associated with widespread mucosal involvement of internal organs and has a high mortality in infancy.
synonyms: JEB - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa] | Junctional EB - [epidermolysis bullosa] | Lucidolytic epidermolysis bullosa | JEB | congenital junctional epidermolysis bullosa
cn_name: 交界性大疱性表皮松解症

name: Certain specified inherited disorders of keratinization
definition: Other specified genetically-determined skin disorder characterised by abnormal keratinization.

name: Epiblepharon, unilateral
definition: This is characterised by a congenital horizontal fold of skin near the margin of the upper or lower eyelid caused by the abnormal insertion of muscle fibres. This extra fold of skin redirects the lashes into a vertical position, where they may contact the globe. This diagnosis is occurring in one eye.

name: Chronic scaling or hyperkeratotic skin disorder
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature in which scaling and hyperkeratosis are prominent features. Examples include psoriasis, ichthyoses, small plaque parapsoriasis and mycosis fungoides.
cn_name: 慢性剥脱性或角化性皮肤病

name: Florid cutaneous papillomatosis
definition: Florid cutaneous papillomatosis is a disorder consisting of the rapid onset of numerous warty papules on the trunk and the extremities that are clinically indistinguishable from viral warts. It usually occurs in association with malignant acanthosis nigricans and an internal malignancy.

name: Rhinophyma
definition: Rhinophyma is the most common presentation of phymatous rosacea and is characterised by hypertrophy of the nasal soft tissue due to variable combinations of fibrosis, sebaceous hyperplasia and lymphoedema. This may result in prominent distortion in the contour of the nose. Features of rosacea such as telangiectasia commonly co-exist.
synonyms: hypertrophy of the sebaceous glands in nasal skin

name: Intellectual disability – enteropathy – deafness – neuropathy – ichthyosis – keratoderma syndrome
definition: MEDNIK syndrome, previously known as Erythrokeratodermia Variabilis type 3 (EKV3), is a genetic disorder characterised by intellectual deficit, enteropathy, sensorineural hearing loss, peripheral neuropathy, lamellar and erythrodermic ichthyosis, and keratodermia (MEDNIK stands for Mental retardation, Enteropathy, Deafness, peripheral Neuropathy, Ichthyosis, Keratodermia).
synonyms: MEDNIK - [Mental retardation – enteropathy – deafness – neuropathy – ichthyosis – keratoderma] syndrome | Mental retardation – enteropathy – deafness – neuropathy – ichthyosis – keratoderma syndrome
