synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, fingers

name: Lichen planus of penis
definition: Penile lichen planus presents typically as an annular configuration of lichenoid papules on the glans. Less commonly, linear white striae can be found. It may cause concern about possible sexually-transmitted disease. Associated oral lichen planus is common but may not have been noticed by the patient.

name: Erythema gyratum repens
definition: A rare migratory figurate erythema characterised by multiple annular, often scaly erythematous plaques which form concentric rings and other bizarre polycyclic patterns on the skin. It is strongly associated with underlying malignancy, particularly of lung, breast or oesophagus.
synonyms: gammel's disease

name: Acute papular onchodermatitis
definition: Acute papular onchodermatitis (APOD) is characterised by a solid, scattered, pruritic papular rash. The diameter of the papules is at least 1 mm. Vesicles or pustules at the apex may or may not be present. The obliteration of the skin creases due to oedema also may or may not be present.

name: Disseminate or recurrent infundibulofolliculitis
definition: A dermatosis of poorly understood aetiology affecting principally the chest, shoulders and upper arms of young black men and characterised by a disseminated low-grade spongiotic dermatitis involving the infundibula of multiple adjacent follicles. It manifests clinically as sheets of small monomorphic pruritic papules.
synonyms: DRIF - [Disseminate or recurrent infundibulofolliculitis]

name: Pili torti
definition: Pili torti describes an abnormal hair structure\: hair shafts are flat and twisted at irregular intervals (180 torsion) along their longitudinal axis. Pili torti can be isolated or is observed in Menkes, Bjornstad, Bazex and Dupre and Crandall syndromes, in pseudomonilethrix, and in anidrotic ectodermal dysplasia.
synonyms: Twisted hair

name: Polyarteritis nodosa involving the skin
definition: Polyarteritis involving the skin may be associated with polyarteritis involving internal organs but may also occur as a chronic organ-limited cutaneous arteritis limited to arterioles and small arteries of the subcutis, cutaneous polyarteritis nodosa. Classically there is a predominantly neutrophilic inflammatory infiltrate though in resolving or mild cases lymphocytes may predominate.

name: Psychogenic pruritus
definition: Chronic episodic pruritus in the absence of an identifiable organic cause and typically associated with stress and/or depression.
synonyms: Psychogenic itch | Somatoform pruritus

name: Warty dyskeratoma
definition: Warty dyskeratoma is a rare, usually solitary, papulonodular benign skin tumour exhibiting a distinct histopathological appearance with suprabasal clefts containing numerous acantholytic and dyskeratotic cells and an overlying keratinous plug.
synonyms: isolated darier's disease

name: Certain specified allergic reactions to substances in contact with skin or mucous membranes
definition: Allergic reactions other than dermatitis or urticaria to substances in contact with skin or mucous membranes. The most common are lichenoid reactions to dental amalgam and tattoo pigment.

name: Furunculosis
definition: The presence of multiple furuncles, this condition is associated with disorders such as malnutrition and diabetes mellitus. Treatment-resistant furunculosis may be associated with Panton-Valentine leucocidin-producing Staphylococcus aureus.

name: Non-homogeneous leukoplakia
definition: Non-homogenous leukoplakia is a lesion of non-uniform appearance. The colour may be predominantly white or a mixed white and red. The surface texture is irregular compared to homogenous leukoplakia, and may be flat (papular), nodular or exophytic. Non-homogenous leukoplakias have a greater risk of malignant transformation than homogenous leukoplakias.

name: Genitocrural seborrhoeic dermatitis
definition: Seborrhoeic dermatitis affecting the genitocrural folds and external genitalia.

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to preservatives or biocides
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to preservatives (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to preservatives and biocides).

name: Sclerema neonatorum
definition: Sclerema neonatorum is an uncommon condition which typically affects gravely ill, preterm neonates in the first week of life. It manifests as a diffuse hardening of skin and subcutaneous adipose tissue such that the skin cannot be pitted or picked up and pinched into a fold. Histologically there is minimal inflammation without fat necrosis. It is associated with a high mortality.
synonyms: Underwood's disease | newborn scleroderma

name: 2q37 deletion
definition: Deletion 2q37 or monosomy 2q37 is a chromosomal anomaly involving deletion of chromosome band 2q37 and manifests as three major clinical findings\: developmental delay, skeletal malformations and facial dysmorphism.
synonyms: 2q37 deletion syndrome | Albright hereditary osteodystrophy 3 | Albright hereditary osteodystrophy-like syndrome | Brachydactyly-intellectual deficit | Monosomy 2q37

definition: This is the subjective complaint of dry mouth which may or may not be associated with a lack of saliva, due to mouth breathing.

Segmental lentiginosis
synonyms: Partial unilateral lentiginosis

Cervicofacial actinomycosis

The typical presentation is a slowly progressive development from painless tissue infiltration and induration of soft tissues of the face and neck to multiple abscesses and d
synonyms: lumpy jaw

name: Early or late osteitis of yaws
Penoscrotal pruritus
definition: An intense itching sensation that produces the urge to rub or scratch the skin of the scrotum and/or base of the penis to obtain relief.

name: Secondary syphilitic proctocolitis
definition: This refers to a proctocolitis due to syphilis in the second of three stages, that is venereal disease caused by Treponema pallidum.

Dermal cellular melanocytic naevus
definition: A nevus characterised by the proliferation of nevus cells in the dermis without involvement of the dermal-epidermal junction.
synonyms: Cellular naevus | Intradermal melanocytic naevus | Intradermal naevus | dermal naevus of unspecified site

definition: A congenital haemangioma which undergoes only partial spontaneous resolution

name: Intertriginous psoriasis

name: Radiotherapy-induced skin malignancy
definition: Malignant neoplasm of skin attributable to the effects of ionising radiation from previous radiotherapy. Skin tumours associated with radiotherapy include basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, atypical fibroxanthoma and fibrosarcoma.

name: Dermatitis or eczema of lower legs
definition: Dermatitis (eczema) affecting the lower legs, most commonly associated with lower limb venous insufficiency, lymphoedema and/or immobility.
synonyms: dermatitis of lower legs | eczema of lower legs

name: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex, Ogna
definition: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex, Ogna type (EBS-Og) is a basal subtype of epidermolysis bullosa simplex characterised by sometimes widespread but primarily acral blistering. It is due to mutations in the PLEC gene which encodes the protein plectin.
synonyms: EBS, Ogna - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex, Ogna] | EBS-Og - [epidermolysis bullosa simplex, Ogna]

name: Scalp pruritus
definition: Pruritus of the scalp of any aetiology.

name: Scrofuloderma
definition: Scrofuloderma begins as a firm, subcutaneous \"cold abscess\" arising from an underlying tuberculous focus, usually in lymph nodes, especially those of the neck, or bones. The tuberculous abscess becomes fluctuant and breaks down to form irregular ulcers and sinus tracts which heal with scarring.
synonyms: Tuberculosis cutis colliquativa

name: Oral lichen planus, unspecified
definition: Lichen planus of oral mucosa without mention of presence or absence of ulceration.

name: Disseminated superficial porokeratosis
definition: A condition characterised by the development of multiple small pink or flesh-coloured macules or thin plaques, each with a well-defined fine circular rim of parakeratotic keratinocytes (cornoid lamella). It most commonly appears in adult life as a result of chronic sun exposure (disseminated superficial actinic porokeratosis) but may be seen in patients who are immunosuppressed as a result of therapy (e.g. organ transplant recipients) or disease (e.g. haematological malignancy, HIV infection). I

name: Disorders of the epidermis and epidermal appendages
definition: This group incorporates skin disorders involving principally the epidermis, including abnormalities of keratinization and pigmentation, and the epidermal appendages, namely the hair follicular unit (hair, hair follicle, sebaceous gland, apocrine duct and apocrine gland), the eccrine sweat gland apparatus (eccrine duct and gland) and the nail apparatus.

name: Juvenile spring eruption
definition: A localised form of polymorphic light eruption. Polymorphic light eruption (PLE) is the most common of the photodermatoses. Juvenile spring eruption is a variant of PLE, affecting only the ears.

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 2A
definition: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 2, severe form, is associated with a massive accumulation of glycosaminoglycans and a wide variety of symptoms including a rapidly progressive cognitive decline; it is most often fatal in the second or third decade.
synonyms: Hunter syndrome type A | Iduronate 2-sulfatase deficiency type A | Mucopolysaccharidosis type 2, severe form

name: Malignant neoplasms of vulva
definition: Squamous cell carcinoma of the vulva occurs predominantly in the older age group. Although the incidence rate of vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia is increasing, that of squamous cell carcinoma of the vulva is declining, reflecting earlier detection and more successful treatment. In addition to human papillomavirus infection, cigarette smoking is a putative risk factor for vulvar squamous cell carcinoma. There are three known precursor lesions\: vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia, lichen sclerosus
synonyms: cancer of vulva | malignant tumour of vulva | vulval cancer | vulvar carcinoma

name: Lymphatic malformations
definition: Lymphatic malformations (LM), formerly referred to by the term lymphangioma, are malformations of the lymphatic system which result in obstructed lymphatic drainage. There are two types of LM\: macrocystic LM (including cystic hygroma/lymphangioma) and tissue-infiltrating microcystic LM (lymphangioma circumscriptum). The macro and microcystic forms of LM may occur in association.

name: Malignant neoplasm of glans penis
synonyms: cancer of glans penis | malignant tumour of glans penis | primary malignant neoplasm of glans penis

name: Atopic cheilitis
definition: Atopic eczema affecting the lips
synonyms: atopic eczema of the lips

name: Inflammatory disorders of eyelid
synonyms: inflammatory disorders of eyelid NOS

name: Hyperkeratotic fissured plantar dermatitis
definition: A distinct and disabling form of eczema characterised by irritable, scaly, fissured and often painful hyperkeratotic patches affecting the soles of the feet and plantar surfaces of the toes. It may be accompanied by similar changes on the palms of the hands. The aetiopathogenesis is poorly understood.
synonyms: Hyperkeratotic dermatitis of soles of feet | Hyperkeratotic fissured plantar eczema

name: PUVA burn
definition: Adverse cutaneous reaction to psoralen photochemotherapy characterised by erythema, pain, blistering and oedema.

name: Dahlberg-Borer-Newcomer syndrome
definition: Dahlberg-Borer-Newcomer syndrome is an inherited syndrome characterised by the association of congenital lymphoedema, hypoparathyroidism, nephropathy, mitral valve prolapse and brachytelephalangy.
synonyms: Lymphoedema - hypoparathyroidism

name: Transverse grooving of nails

name: Excoriation disorder
definition: Excoriation disorder is characterised by recurrent picking of one’s own skin leading to skin lesions, accompanied by unsuccessful attempts to decrease or stop the behaviour. The most commonly picked sites are the face, arms and hands, but many individuals pick from multiple body sites. Skin picking may occur in brief episodes scattered throughout the day or in less frequent but more sustained periods. The symptoms result in significant distress or significant impairment in personal, family, soci
synonyms: dermatillomania | skin picking disorder

name: Complete trisomy 13
definition: Trisomy 13 is a chromosomal anomaly caused by the presence of an extra chromosome 13 and is characterised by brain malformations (holoprosencephaly), facial dysmorphism, ocular anomalies, postaxial polydactyly, visceral malformations (cardiopathy) and severe psychomotor retardation.
synonyms: Patau syndrome | abnormal autosomes 13 | chromosome 13 trisomy | complete trisomy 13 syndrome | d1 trisomy | d1 trisomy syndrome | d>1< trisomy syndrome | patau | trisomy 13 | trisomy 13 syndrome

definition: Erythromelalgia triggered by medication. Calcium‐channel blockers and bromocriptine are recognised causes.

definition: Disturbed skin pigmentation following a cosmetic procedure

name: Palpebral cleft or coloboma, bilateral
definition: This is a hole in one of the structures of the eye, such as the iris, retina, choroid or optic disc. The hole is present from birth and can be caused when a gap called the choroid fissure, which is present during early stages of prenatal development, fails to close up completely before a child is born. This diagnosis is occurring in both eyes (bilateral).

name: Xerosis senilis

name: Lichen planus pemphigoides
definition: Lichen planus pemphigoides is characterised by bullae appearing on lichen planus papules and plaques or uninvolved skin of patients with lichen planus. Histopathology shows both features of lichen planus and subepidermal clefting. Immunopathology studies are identical to bullous pemphigoid, although certain epitopes of pemphigoid autoantigens have been associated with lichen planus pemphigoides. The condition tends to affect a younger age group compared with bullous pemphigoid and is usually res

name: Paraprotein-associated scleroedema
definition: This form of scleroedema is associated with a monoclonal paraprotein band, usually of IgG kappa type. It may be the harbinger of multiple myeloma and is more likely to affect other organs. It tends to be chronic and progressive. Signs of systemic involvement include enlargement of the tongue and parotid glands, skeletal and cardiac muscle infiltration, and pleural, pericardial and peritoneal effusions.

name: Aglossia
synonyms: congenital absence of tongue | congenitally absent tongue | tongue agenesis

name: Congenital infiltrating lipomatosis of the face
definition: Congenital infiltrating lipomatosis of the face is a rare condition in which there is diffuse infiltration of the subcutaneous tissues and muscles of the face by non-encapsulated mature adipose tissue. It is manifested as tumour-like swellings in infancy or early childhood. Osseous hyperplasia of subjacent bone is also seen. There is a high recurrence rate after attempted surgical removal.

name: Platinum oculocutaneous albinism
definition: Platinum OCA is a variant of OCA-1B where patients have a \"platinum\" hair colour.

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to food flavours or additives
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to flavouring agents (e.g. cinnamyl alcohol) and other food additives (e.g. ammonium persulfate, benzoic acid). Such agents may be encountered at work in food processing or preparation, or may rarely cause allergic contact cheilitis or stomatitis.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to food flavours or additives

name: Onchocerciasis of the skin
definition: A disease of the skin, caused by an infection with the parasitic worm Onchocerca volvulus. This disease is characterised by subcutaneous nodules on the skin (commonly affecting the iliac crests, ribs, knees, or trochanters). Transmission is through the bite of an infected Simulium fly. Confirmation is by identification of Onchocerca volvulus in a skin sample.
synonyms: Cutaneous onchocerciasis

name: Octopus bite
definition: Envenomation by certain species of octopus, especially the blue-ringed octopus (Hapalochlaena maculosa), the bite of which may be fatal\: locally there is a painful puncture wound with ischaemia, cyanosis and numbness. Injection of salivary toxins can cause severe systemic toxicity culminating in death, usually from respiratory failure.

name: Burn of head or neck except face, superficial partial thickness burn

name: Contact gingivostomatitis
definition: Inflammation of the gingivae and oral mucosa due to contact with irritants or allergens but without specification of which mechanism is involved.

name: Type I leprosy reaction
definition: This phenomenon, also named “upgrading reaction,” occurs in borderline leprosy states and is associated with an increase in cell-mediated immunity. It occurs typically within the first 6 months of treatment in previously untreated patients but may be related to stress, intercurrent infections, or pregnancy. synonyms: Leprosy reversal reaction | Leprosy upgrading reaction

Naevus of Ota
definition: A circumscribed area of slate-brown or blue macular pigmentation involving the skin supplied by the first and second branches of the trigeminal nerve and the ipsilateral conjunctiva. It is usually unilateral and usually present at birth. It is commonest in the Japanese. Pigmentation persists throughout life.

Infection-associated immune complex vasculitis
definition: Certain infections may directly trigger an inflammatory vasculitic process. Infective vasculitis has a wide variety of causes, including bacteria, fungi, protozoa, viruses, spirochetes, and rickettsiae. Leukocytoclastic vasculitis predominantly involving the skin with occasional involvement of other organ systems may be a minor component of many infections. Compelling laboratory and spontaneously occurring animal models of this disease, combined with the known associations among HBV, HCV, and va

name: Cranioectodermal dysplasia
definition: Cranioectodermal dysplasia (CED) is a rare developmental disorder characterised by congenital skeletal and ectodermal defects associated with dysmorphic features, nephronophthisis, hepatic fibrosis and ocular anomalies (mainly retinitis pigmentosa).
synonyms: Sensenbrenner syndrome

name: Milium
definition: A milium is a small 1-2 mm diameter subepidermal cyst which typically presents as multiple white papules (milia or, in the neonate, milk spots) most commonly on the upper cheeks and eyelids and thought to be due to immaturity, blockage or disruption of the superficial portion of the sweat duct (acrosyringium).
synonyms: Milia

name: Carotenoderma
definition: Carotenoderma results from excessive ingestion of carotene from foods such as oranges and carrots. Carotene contributes a yellow component to the colour of normal skin but when taken in excess may impart a diffuse yellow or orange-yellow tinge to the skin, especially on the palms and soles.

name: Nail disorder associated with specified dermatosis
definition: Abnormality of the nail plate attributable to other specified skin disease.
cn_name: 与特指的皮肤病有关的甲疾病

name: Disseminated adiaspiromycosis
definition: An increasingly reported fulminant fungal infection caused by Emmonsia and Emmonsia-like fungal species. It is seen in the context of profound immunosuppression, especially from HIV infection. This is in contrast with pulmonary adiaspiromycosis, which is also caused by Emmonsia species but typically affects immunocompetent individuals. Its clinical presentation is similar to those of histoplasmosis and blastomycosis. Skin and lung involvement is characteristic but the CNS and blood may also be a
synonyms: Disseminated emmonsiosis
cn_name: 播散性不育大孢子菌病

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, elbow joint
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, elbow joint

name: Cutaneous horn
definition: Cutaneous horn is a clinical morphological description for a firm, white or yellow conical horn-like protuberance arising from the skin surface. Abnormal keratinization of the underlying epidermis produces variable acanthosis beneath a vertically-orientated column of compacted cornified cells. The underlying epidermal derangement may be due, amongst other things, to actinic keratosis, squamous cell carcinoma, viral wart or seborrhoeic keratosis. Actinic keratosis is, however, the commonest under
synonyms: Cornu cutaneum | Keratin horn

name: Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome with pulmonary fibrosis
definition: Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome with pulmonary fibrosis as a complication includes two types (HPS-1 and HPS-4) of Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome (HPS; see this term), a multi-system disorder characterized by oculocutaneous albinism, bleeding diathesis and, in some cases, pulmonary fibrosis or granulomatous colitis.Prevalence of all types of HPS is estimated at between 1/500,000 and 1/1,000,000 in non-Puerto Rican populations. In northwestern Puerto Rico the prevalence of HSP-1 is 1/1,800 due to a founder e

name: Angio-osteohypertrophic syndrome
definition: Angio-osteohypertrophic (AOH) syndrome is a congenital vascular bone syndrome characterised by the presence of vascular malformations in a limb resulting in limb overgrowth. Depending on whether the malformations are slow flow venous or fast flow arteriovenous the syndrome may be divided into two subtypes, Klippel-Trénaunay and Parkes-Weber syndromes respectively. Some cases of the latter are associated with mutations in the RASA1 gene.
synonyms: Haemangiectatic hypertrophy

name: Late syphilitic episcleritis
definition: This is a late, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with a benign, self-limiting inflammatory disease affecting part of the eye called the episclera.

name: Acrogeria
definition: A rare premature aging syndrome characterized by atrophy of the skin and subcutaneous tissue involving predominantly the distal parts of the extremities, resulting in prematurely aged appearance of the hand and feet. Another prominent feature is the characteristic facies with hollow cheeks, beaked nose, and owl-like eyes. Additional, non-dermatological manifestations, like bone anomalies have been described in some patients. Mode of inheritance has not been definitively established.
synonyms: Gottron syndrome | acrogeria | erythrokeratoderma progressiva symmetrica | gottron syndrome | PSEK

name: Langer-Giedion syndrome
definition: Langer-Giedion syndrome or trichorhinophalangeal syndrome type 2 is a chromosomal anomaly syndrome characterised by the association of intellectual deficit and numerous other anomalies including redundant skin, multiple cartilaginous exostoses, characteristic facies and cone-shaped phalangeal epiphyses.

name: Occupational acne or folliculitis due to exposure to pitch or tar
definition: Acne and folliculitis caused by occupational exposure to pitch and tar (e.g. in roofing pitch, artificial asphalt or wood preservatives). Those at risk include roofers, road-construction workers and those employed in coke and coal gas production or aluminium smelting. Friction from clothing is often a contributory factor.

name: Pyogenic abscess of the skin
definition: A pus-producing abscess of the skin most commonly due to bacterial infection by Staphylococcus aureus. It is prone to develop where the normal anatomy is disturbed as in pilonidal disease, an epidermoid cyst or around foreign bodies such as surgical sutures.
synonyms: abscess NOS

name: Exaggerated physiological flushing
definition: Flushing develops as part of the normal thermoregulatory response in a hot environment or following exercise. A similar mechanism is responsible for facial flushing due to hot drinks, which cause a rise in temperature of blood in the oral cavity which in turn leads, by a counter-current heat-exchange process involving the internal jugular vein and internal carotid artery, to a rise in temperature of blood perfusing the hypothalamus. This reaction may be exaggerated in some individuals and can th

name: Smokeless tobacco keratosis
definition: Grey-white plaque of keratotic characteristics that forms on the oral mucosa and coincides with the area of chronic placement of tobacco

name: Cowpox
definition: Cowpox is due to infection by an orthopoxvirus. Human disease is caused by cutaneous inoculation from an infected host. Cowpox is endemic in Europe amongst small rodents, particularly wood mice and wood voles. After a seven day incubation it causes a systemic febrile flu-like illness. Lesions are solitary or few, mainly affecting the face and hands. An initial erythematous papula or blister later forms a crusted eschar which heals slowly leaving a deep pock-like scar.

name: Superficial bacterial folliculitis
definition: Bacterial infection of the follicular ostium manifested as follicular papules and pustules with perifollicular erythema. The most commonly isolated organisms are coagulase-negative staphylococci and Staphylococcus aureus. The infection may be acute but is more commonly subacute or chronic; individual lesions heal without scarring. Commonly affected sites include the scalp, beard area, thighs and buttocks.
synonyms: Superficial folliculitis | bockhart's impetigo

name: Focal facial dermal dysplasia
definition: Focal facial dermal dysplasia type 1, also known as Brauer syndrome, is a focal facial dermal dysplasia characterised by congenital bitemporal cutis aplasia.

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to topical medicaments
Blastomycosis dacryoadenitis
definition: This is a fungal infection caused by the organism Blastomyces dermatitidis. Endemic to portions of North America, blastomycosis causes clinical symptoms similar to histoplasmosis. This diagnosis is with inflammation of the lacrimal glands (the tear-producing glands).

name: Acute graft-versus-host disease
definition: Graft-versus-host disease presenting normally within the first 100 days of engraftment. It presents most commonly with a maculopapular rash accompanied by fever. The prognosis correlates with the extent of skin involvement, which may progress to widespread epidermal necrolysis, and the severity of gastrointestinal and liver involvement which may manifest as diarrhoea and jaundice respectively. There is a high mortality in severe acute graft-versus-host disease.
synonyms: acute GVHD - [graft-versus-host disease] | aGVHD

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to rubber chemicals
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to exposure to rubber chemicals such as thiurams, mercaptobenzothiazoles, N-isopropyl-N-phenyl-p-phenylenediamine (IPPD), thiourea derivatives or carbamates.

name: Prenatally-acquired mucocutaneous candidosis
definition: Infection of skin and mucous membranes due to in utero contamination of the fetal skin surface with candida yeasts during late pregnancy, typically as the result of Candida chorioamnionitis associated with premature rupture of the membranes. An extensive eruption of scattered pinkish red macules and papules is present at birth or appears within a few hours before progressing within days to a vesicular or pustular phase. In otherwise healthy neonates the infection responds to topical therapy.
synonyms: congenital cutaneous candidosis | congenital mucocutaneous candidosis

name: Apocrine miliaria
definition: Fox-Fordyce disease is characterised by the development of itchy bumps around the hair follicles of the underarm area, pubic region, and/or around the nipples. It results from inflammation of the aprocrine sweat glands, which are found only in these areas.

name: Certain specified forms of hereditary sensory or autonomic neuropathy
definition: This is a group of genetic disorders with various transmission patterns, in which autonomic dysfunction is prominent.

name: Miscellaneous disorders involving the hair follicle
definition: A heterogeneous group of disorders involving the hair follicle.

name: Acute telogen effluvium
definition: Acute telogen effluvium is an acute-onset scalp hair loss that occurs two to three months after a triggering, often life-threatening, stress which interrupts normal anagen hair growth. It is commonly seen in survivors who have required intensive care for severe sepsis, blood loss, inflammatory disease or trauma. It may also result from acute starvation. Large numbers of anagen hairs are converted to telogen and are shed two to three months later resulting in a diffuse alopecia.

name: Skin disorder attributable to malnutrition or malabsorption
definition: Abnormal skin which is attributable to involuntary malnutrition or malabsorption and as may result, for example, from unavailability of food, persistent vomiting, difficulties with eating and swallowing, intestinal parasites or intestinal failure. Skin signs include ichthyosis, melanosis brittle nails and poor hair growth.

name: Drug-induced erythroderma
definition: Erythroderma (defined as erythema and scaling involving at least 90% of the skin surface) which is attributable to drug administration but which cannot be more precisely categorized, thus excluding more specific severe cutaneous adverse reactions to drugs reaction patterns such as DRESS syndrome, acute generalised exanthematous pustulosis (AGEP) and toxic epidermal necrolysis. Many drugs have been implicated.
synonyms: Drug-induced exfoliative dermatitis | Generalised exfoliative dermatitis due to drug

name: Tick-borne relapsing fever
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the bacteria Borrelia. This disease is characterised by repeated episodes of fever, with the febrile episode lasting for approximately 3 days, followed by the afebrile state of approximately 7 days. Transmission is through the bite of an infected soft tick (from the genus Ornithodoros). Confirmation is by identification of spirochete bacteria from a blood smear, bone marrow, or cerebrospinal fluid.
synonyms: African tick-borne fever | Relapsing fever due to any Borrelia species other than Borrelia recurrentis

name: Fanconi-ichthyosis-dysmorphism syndrome
definition: Fanconi-ichthyosis-dysmorphism syndrome is characterised by the association of Fanconi anaemia, ichthyosis, musculo-skeletal anomalies, jaundice, and diarrhoea.

name: Melkersson syndrome

name: Sand-fly bite
definition: Bites by psychodid flies including the genera Phlebotomus, responsible for transmitting leishmaniasis in the Old World, and Luztomyia, which are vectors of leishmaniasis and bartonellosis in the New World.

name: Childhood granulomatous periorificial dermatitis
definition: This is a rare dermatosis of prepubertal children, especially those of African descent. It manifests as an eruption of asymptomatic flesh-coloured dome-shaped papules affecting principally perioral and periocular skin but can affect other parts of the head and neck. Its cause is unknown and it generally resolves spontaneously over the course of months to a few years.
synonyms: FACE - [Facial Afro-Caribbean childhood eruption]

name: Mondor disease of the penis
definition: A well-recognised usually benign and self-limiting entity occurring in sexually active men and presenting with a cord-like thickening of penile veins or lymphatics located around the coronal sulcus or, less commonly, along the dorsal vein of the penis. Infrequently it may be associated with non-sexual trauma or with hypercoagulable states.
synonyms: Penile Mondor disease | Thrombophlebitis of penis

definition: Adult pulmonary Langerhans Cell Histiocytosis (PLCH) is a rare histiocytic lung disease characterised by the accumulation of Langerhans and other inflammatory cells in the small airways, resulting in the formation of nodular inflammatory lesions.

name: Tessier number 5 facial cleft
definition: Cleft extending from the commissure of the mouth, and lateral to the philtral column, to the lower eyelid.

name: Focal infantile haemangioma
definition: Infantile haemangioma is a common benign vascular neoplasm which develops in about 4% of infants. It appears within weeks of birth as a blanched, blushed, or telangiectatic area that then rapidly proliferates for several months before entering a prolonged process of involution lasting up to 12 years, leaving a residual variably prominent scar. definition: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton yaoundei and typically causing on endothrix infection of scalp hair.

name: Generalised excess and redundant skin due to intentional calorie restriction
definition: generalised excess and redundant skin occurring typically in formerly grossly obese individuals who have been able successfully to reduce their weight by dieting. It may also be seen as a result of anorexia nervosa.

name: Segmental vitiligo
definition: Segmental vitiligo usually appears during childhood and is confined to a segment or dermatome.

name: Frostbite of foot with tissue necrosis
synonyms: frostbite with tissue necrosis of foot

name: Pretibial myxoedema
definition: Pretibial myxoedema is a form of diffuse cutaneous mucinosis in which there is an accumulation of excess glycosaminoglycans, especially hyaluronic acid, in the dermis and subcutis. It is most commonly seen on the shins (pretibial areas) but does occur elsewhere on the lower extremities. name: Oculocerebral syndrome with hypopigmentation
definition: Rare syndrome characterised by hypopigmentation of the skin, microphthalmia and cerebral abnormalities which result in spasticity and mental retardation.

name: Acquired poliosis
definition: Circumscribed loss of hair pigment. This is most commonly associated with vitiligo (especially segmental vitiligo), alopecia areata or regressing melanocytic naevi. It is also a component of Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada syndrome and Alezzandrini syndrome.

name: Congenital symblepharon, bilateral
definition: This is a congenital partial or complete adhesion of the palpebral conjunctiva of the eyelid to the bulbar conjunctiva of the eyeball. It results either from disease (conjunctival sequelae of Trachoma) or trauma. Cicatricial pemphigoid and, in severe cases, rosacea may cause symblepharon. This diagnosis is occurring in both eyes.

name: Erythema
definition: Redness of skin due to the presence of increased amounts of oxygenated haemoglobin within dilated skin capillaries. It may be due to localised or generalised inflammatory processes but may result from increased cutaneous blood flow following exertion or associated with pyrexia.

name: Sea-bather's eruption
definition: A pruritic papular eruption that develops in areas covered by swimsuits due to contact with the larvae and adult forms of the thimble jellyfish, Linuche unguiculata.

name: Pseudotumour Cerebri related to Hypervitaminosis A
Linear morphoea
definition: Linear morphoea is a form of morphoea which usually presents in childhood or adolescence and is usually unilateral, affecting a limb with a linear induration of skin, subcutis and occasionally underlying muscle and bone. It may also affect the scalp and forehead (\"en coup de sabre\") producing a depressed scar likened to a healed sabre wound, with sclerosis of the skin and alopecia of affected scalp.

Limited fixed drug eruption
definition: The most common form of fixed drug eruption in which one or only a few patches of inflammation develop.

The syndrome appears during therapy with certain medications (e.g., procainamide, hydralazine, phenytoin) and tumour necrosis factor inhibitors. These fungi normally invade only the outer keratinous layer of the epidermis (stratum corneum), the hair shaft and the nail. They count amongst the commonest infections in man. synonyms: Infections due to species of Epidermophyton, Microsporum and Trichophyton | cutaneous mycosis | dermal mycosis | dermatitis fungoid | dermatophytosis, unspecified | fungal dermatitis | fungal dermatosis | fungus infection of skin | mycotic infection of skin | ringworm

The onset is early in life, and both sexes are affected equally. Other common sites include the tongue, floor of the mouth, and alveolar mucosa.

name: Keratitis – ichthyosis – deafness syndrome
definition: Keratitis (and hystrix-like) ichthyosis deafness (KID/HID) syndrome is a rare congenital ectodermal disorder characterised by vascularizing keratitis, hyperkeratotic skin lesions and hearing loss.
synonyms: Hystrix-like ichthyosis - deafness | KID - [Keratitis – ichthyosis – deafness syndrome] syndrome | KID/HID - [Keratitis - ichthyosis - deafness/Hystrix-like ichthyosis - deafness] syndrome | Keratitis - ichthyosis - deafness/Hystrix-like ichthyosis - deafness | Senter syndrome

name: Perianal streptococcal cellulitis of childhood
definition: Perianal streptococcal cellulitis is due to infection of the perianal skin of children by Group A beta-haemolytic streptococci. Infants and young children appear to be more susceptible to this than older age groups. It manifests as sharply demarcated intense erythema with variable oedema and oozing of perianal skin. The penis or vulva may sometimes be involved as well. It is often painful and may impede normal defaecation. There is often a considerable delay in reaching the correct diagnosis.
synonyms: Perianal streptococcal dermatitis of childhood

name: Foreign body granulomatous reaction to injected filler
definition: This is characterised by nodule formation at the sites of injection of fillers used for soft tissue augmentation.
synonyms: Nodule formation attributable to injected filler

name: Irritant contact dermatitis due to exposure to acids, alkalis or other specified chemical irritants
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema due to exposure to acids, alkalis or other chemical irritants
cn_name: 接触酸、碱或其他特指的化学刺激物而引起的刺激性接触性皮炎

name: Disorders of the hair follicle
definition: This group incorporates disorders of the hair shaft and hair follicular unit including, for example, hirsutism, alopecia, acne and hidradenitis suppurativa.

name: Miscellaneous non-specific skin-related symptoms and signs
definition: Other specified skin changes which cannot be more precisely defined.
cn_name: 其他非特异性皮肤相关症状和体征

name: Spitz naevus
definition: A benign melanocytic neoplasm with a characteristic clinical appearance arising most commonly during childhood. Typically it develops rapidly over the course of weeks or months into a smooth dome-shaped reddish-brown papule or nodule. It can be difficult to differentiate histologically from melanoma.
synonyms: Spindle and epithelioid cell naevus

name: Dermatoses resulting from disturbed metabolic processes
definition: This group comprises dermatoses where abnormal quantities of biological material accumulate in the skin. The effects of such accumulations depend on the particular material involved. Examples include lipid, mucin, amyloid, porphyrins and calcium.

name: Occupational photo-allergic contact dermatitis
definition: Photo-allergic contact dermatitis attributable to allergen exposure at work.
synonyms: Occupational photo-allergic contact eczema

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type I
definition: Hereditary sensory autonomic type I neuropathies are autosomal dominant sensory-autonomic sensory polyneuropathies
synonyms: HSAN1- [Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type I]

name: Metastatic cutaneous Crohn disease
definition: An uncommon manifestation of Crohn disease involving cutaneous sites not contiguous with the gastrointestinal tract. It may present as cutaneous ulcers, plaques, papules or nodules. It has a predilection for the genitalia, where it may cause lymphoedema. Other recognised sites include the skin folds, infra-mammary area and the limbs. It may precede or follow its presentation in the gastrointestinal tract. There is little correlation between the activity of the cutaneous and intestinal disease.

name: Primary syphilis of tonsils
synonyms: primary syphilitic tonsils

name: Distichiasis
definition: This is an eyelash that arises from an abnormal spot on the eyelid. Distichiae usually exit from the duct of the meibomian gland at the eyelid margin.

name: Crush injury of skin
definition: Traumatic injury of the skin and subcutaneous tissue caused by compression from a solid heavy object such as a car wheel. Underlying muscle and bone will also often be damaged.

name: Angioma serpiginosum
definition: A skin hemangioma that is characterized by the presence of small red dots that cluster together to form a linear or snake-like array or ring-shaped pattern located_in the blood vessels of the skin.
synonyms: Angioma serpiginosum of skin

name: Sequelae of frostbite, not elsewhere classified
Nummular dermatitis of hands
definition: A form of nummular dermatitis or discoid eczema which tends to affect the dorsal surfaces of the hands and fingers.
synonyms: Discoid hand eczema

name: Diseases of the skin
definition: Diseases of the skin incorporate conditions affecting the epidermis, its appendages (hair, hair follicle, sebaceous glands, apocrine sweat gland apparatus, eccrine sweat gland apparatus and nails) and associated mucous membranes (conjunctival, oral and genital), the dermis, the cutaneous vasculature and the subcutaneous tissue (subcutis).
synonyms: Diseases of cutaneous vasculature | Diseases of subcutaneous tissue | Diseases of the dermis | Diseases of the epidermal appendages (hair, hair follicle, sebaceous glands, apocrine sweat gland apparatus, eccrine sweat gland apparatus and nails) | Diseases of the epidermis

name: Acute blistering skin eruption
definition: A provisional diagnosis for an acute blistering skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature. Examples include vesicular dermatitis of the hands and feet (pompholyx), acute phototoxic reactions, especially to contact with plants (phytophotodermatitis), sunburn and immunobullous disorders such as bullous pemphigoid.

name: Torg-Winchester syndrome
definition: Winchester syndrome is a hereditary disorder characterised by extremely short stature, coarse face, corneal opacities, generalised osteolysis and progressive painful arthropathy with joint stiffness and contractures of distal phalanges in combination with skin changes.

name: Malignant melanoma in freckle of unspecified site
Drug-induced acute generalised exanthematous pustulosis
definition: This uncommon reaction to systemic medication is characterised by fever (generally on the same day as the start of the rash) and multiple, small, non-follicular pustules that arise on a widespread inflammatory erythema centred on the upper trunk and body folds. It may be difficult to differentiate from acute generalised pustular psoriasis. It usually appears within 24 hours of drug exposure. Antibiotics are probably the commonest precipitants although many drugs have been implicated.
synonyms: AGEP - [acute generalised exanthematous pustulosis] due to drug | Drug-induced toxic pustuloderma
cn_name: 药物诱发的急性泛发性发疹性脓疱病

name: Squamous cell carcinoma of vulva, HPV independent
Anogenital streptococcal cellulitis of infancy or childhood
definition: Infection of the perianal, vulvovaginal and/or penile skin of infants and children by Group A beta-haemolytic Streptococcus. It usually affects children aged 6 months to 10 years and most commonly presents as a tender, raw, well-demarcated, beefy red perianal erythema (perianal streptococcal cellulitis). It is associated with symptomatic or asymptomatic pharyngeal infection in up to 60% of cases. There is a small risk of progression to either guttate psoriasis or post-streptococcal glomeruloneph
synonyms: Anogenital streptococcal dermatitis of infancy and childhood | Perineal streptococcal dermatitis

name: Superficial epidermolytic ichthyosis
definition: Superficial epidermolytic ichthyosis is an autosomal dominant inherited form of genetic ichthyosis characterised by diffuse hyperkeratosis that may be palmoplantar and superficial erosion of the skin.
synonyms: Ichthyosis bullosa of Siemens | ichthyosis bullosa of siemens | ichthyosis exfoliativa | bullous type ichthyosis | ichthyosis bullosa of Siemens | superficial epidermolytic ichthyosis

name: Chylocele of tunica vaginalis in filariasis due to Wuchereria bancrofti
definition: This refers to chylocele of tunica vaginalis in a parasitic disease (usually an infectious tropical disease) that is caused by thread-like nematodes (roundworms) belonging to the superfamily Filarioidea, also known as \"filariae\". This diagnosis is due to a parasitic filarial nematode (roundworm) spread by a mosquito vector.

name: Ascending bacterial lymphangitis
definition: A complication of a focal acute pyogenic bacterial infection in which the draining lymphatics become red, inflamed and tender as the result of ascending infection. It is most commonly caused by Streptococcus pyogenes.
synonyms: acute bacterial lymphangitis | acute lymphangitis
cn_name: 上行性细菌性淋巴管炎

name: Immersion foot
synonyms: Trench foot | foxhole foot | jungle rot | paddy-field foot | peripheral vasoneuropathy | shelter foot | swamp foot | trench foot | tropical jungle foot

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to hairdressing products
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to hairdressing products (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to hairdressing products).

name: Atopic xeroderma
definition: Dry skin in patients with an atopic diathesis. Histologically evidence of eczematous inflammation may be seen.
synonyms: Atopic dry skin | xeroderma NOS

name: Pressure ulceration grade 1 of head
synonyms: stage 1 decubitus ulcer of head

name: Lipo-lymphoedema
definition: Lipoedema complicated by the development of secondary lymphoedema. The normal lymphatic channels in the lower limb are compressed by excessive fatty tissue deposited in lipoedematous limbs.

name: Smoker's keratosis
definition: This is an oral pathological condition that appears in the hard palate of the mouth as a white lesion and is not considered to be premalignant and results from tobacco smoking (especially pipes or cigars) or long-term drinking of very hot beverages.
synonyms: Leukokeratosis nicotina palati | Smoker palate

name: Waardenburg syndrome
definition: Autosomal dominant inherited syndrome consisting in congenital sensorineural hearing loss and hypomelanosis of skin, hair and irides.
synonyms: White forelock syndrome | WS

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to allergens from clothing or footwear
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to constituents of clothing and footwear (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to clothing and footwear).

name: White fibrous papulosis of the neck
definition: White fibrous papulosis of the neck is a rare, acquired, dermal elastic tissue disorder characterized by multiple, 2-3 mm sized, non-confluent, asymptomatic, white or pale-colored, non-follicular, firm papular lesions occurring predominantly on the lateral or posterior aspects of the neck. Other, rarely reported sites include inferior axillae, central mid-back and upper sternal region.

name: Hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia, Clouston type
definition: Clouston syndrome (or hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia) is an inherited disorder characterised by the clinical triad of nail dystrophy, alopecia, and palmoplantar hyperkeratosis.
synonyms: Clouston syndrome (MIM 129500)
cn_name: 出汗性外胚层发育不良，Clouston型

name: Spinal cord defect affecting the skin
definition: Abnormalities of spinal cord development may present with signs in the skin, particularly in the lumbosacral area\: these include lipomas, port-wine stains, haemangiomas, ‘faun tail’ hypertrichosis and giant congenital melanocytic naevi. These should prompt consideration of imaging to ensure that any intervention to prevent neurological damage is undertaken. If the spinal cord is damaged from whatever cause, the lower extremities tend to be cold and cyanosed and skin injuries are slow to heal.

name: Brown oculocutaneous albinism
definition: Individuals with Brown-OCA2 may present with the classic OCA2 phenotype (yellow/blond hair and blue/hazel irides) except for more evident skin pigmentation. It is caused by mutations in the OCA2 (P) gene and occurs principally in Sub-Saharan Africa.

name: Sequelae of superficial frostbite of head
Benign adipocytic neoplasms of skin or soft tissue
definition: Benign soft tissue tumours of adipocytes (lipocytes) of which the subcutaneous lipoma is the commonest form.

name: Plantar hyperhidrosis
synonyms: sweaty feet

name: Rapidly involuting congenital haemangioma
definition: Rapidly involuting congenital hemangiomas (RICH) are a distinctive type of congenital hemangioma that are fully formed in utero and differ from non-involuting congenital haemangiomas (NICH; see this term) mainly because they undergo rapid postnatal involution.
synonyms: RICH - [rapidly involuting congenital haemangioma]

name: Occupational contact dermatitis, contact urticaria or allergy
definition: Contact dermatitis, contact urticaria and allergy attributable to work or occupation.

name: Parasitic infestation of eyelid by tick
3q29 deletion
definition: 3q29 microdeletion syndrome is a recurrent subtelomeric deletion syndrome with variable clinical manifestations including intellectual deficit and mild dysmorphic features.
synonyms: 3q29 deletion syndrome | Monosomy 3q29

name: Purpura or bruising due to genetically-determined skin fragility
definition: Purpura and bruising due to vascular fragility in conditions such as Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, Marfan syndrome and pseudoxanthoma elasticum.

name: Sclerosing basal cell carcinoma of skin
definition: This form of basal cell carcinoma is composed of thin strands, cords and columns of malignant cells which infiltrate between collagen bundles of the dermis. It may infiltrate widely and deeply before it becomes clinically obvious. It typically starts as a pale, poorly-defined indurated plaque which may not come to medical attention until it starts to bleed and crust.
synonyms: Cicatrising basal call carcinoma | Morpheaform basal cell carcinoma of skin | Morphoeic basal cell carcinoma of skin | morphea basal cell carcinoma of unspecified site
cn_name: 皮肤硬化型基底细胞癌

name: Bruising in fetus or newborn
synonyms: contusion of fetus or newborn

name: Occupational irritant contact dermatitis
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis attributable to exposure to skin irritants at work.
synonyms: Occupational irritant contact eczema

name: Melanocytic naevus with eczematous halo
definition: A benign melanocytic nevus surrounded by eczematous changes.
synonyms: Meyerson naevus

name: Abscess of skin or subcutaneous tissue attributable to injection of filler
definition: An uncommon but well-recognised adverse event from the use of injected fillers for soft tissue augmentation

name: Lower limb superficial venous ectasia
definition: Subdermal ectatic veins measuring from 1-3 mm in diameter and commonly seen in association with lower limb venous hypertension and larger calibre varicose veins.
synonyms: Corona phlebectatica paraplantaris | Venous flare | superficial venous varicosities

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, multiple sites
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, multiple sites

name: Suspected deep pressure-induced tissue damage, depth unknown of head
Burn of chest wall, full thickness burn
Suprabasal epidermolysis bullosa simplex
definition: Forms of epidermolysis bullosa in which the abnormality responsible for blistering involves the upper epidermis. Desmoplakin and plakophilin-1 are two proteins, absence or abnormalities of which are linked to suprabasal epidermolysis.
synonyms: Suprabasal EBS | suprabasal EBS

name: Hereditary brachyonychia
synonyms: Racquet nails

name: 19q13.11 deletion
definition: 19q13.11 microdeletion is characterised by several major features including pre and postnatal growth retardation, slender habitus, severe postnatal feeding difficulties, microcephaly, intellectual deficit with speech disturbance, hypospadias and ectodermal dysplasia presented by scalp aplasia, thin and sparse hair, eyebrows and eyelashes, thin and dry skin and dysplastic nails.
synonyms: 19q13.11 deletion syndrome | Monosomy 19q13.11

name: Pemphigoid
definition: The pemphigoid group of immunobullous diseases is characterised by the production of IgG antibodies to the epidermal basement membrane zone, leading to subepidermal clefts which are clinically manifest as blisters or erosions of skin or mucous membranes.

name: Dermatopolymyositis in neoplastic disease of uncertain behaviour
Corrosion of first degree of hip and leg, except ankle or foot
synonyms: corrosion of first degree of hip and lower limb, except ankle and foot

name: 1q41q42 deletion
definition: 1q41q42 microdeletion syndrome is a chromosomal anomaly characterised by a severe developmental delay and/or intellectual disability, typical facial dysmorphic features, brain anomalies, seizures, cleft palate, clubfeet, nail hypoplasia and congenital heart disease.
synonyms: 1q41q42 microdeletion syndrome | Monosomy 1q41q42

name: Sequelae of frostbite with tissue necrosis of wrist or hand
Acquired zinc deficiency syndrome
definition: A syndrome resembling acrodermatitis enteropathica but due either to inadequate dietary zinc or to an acquired inability to absorb zinc. Pure nutritional deficiency is rare. Extensive small bowel disease (e.g. from Crohn disease) or surgical resection (e.g. following mesenteric artery occlusion) may result in malabsorption of zinc from the gastrointestinal tract.

name: Dermatophytosis of perianal region
synonyms: ringworm of perianal region | tinea of perianal region

name: Miscellaneous specified infections affecting the skin
definition: Other infectious diseases which cannot be classified elsewhere under the heading Infections and infestations affecting the skin, either because the organism does not belong within the named categories (viral, bacterial, fungal, parasitic, neonatal) or because the infecting organism is not specified and could be from more than one major category.

name: Cheyletiellosis
definition: An intensely itchy papulovesicular skin eruption due to Cheyletiella mites, which are obligate parasites of certain mammals including dogs and cats. The distribution of lesions corresponds to areas of contact with an infested animal, the abdomen and thighs being frequently involved as a result of an animal sitting on its owner's lap. The chest and arms may also be affected from carrying the animal.
synonyms: Cheyletiella dermatitis

name: Primary autonomic disorders with acquired anhidrosis
Corrosion of second degree of hip and leg, except ankle or foot
synonyms: corrosion of second degree of hip and lower limb, except ankle and foot

name: Hereditary angioedema
definition: Hereditary angioedema is caused in the majority of cases by genetically determined low absolute (type I) or functional (type II) levels of C1 inhibitor, a plasma proteinase inhibitor involved in regulation of complement activation. It is characterised clinically by recurrent subcutaneous and/or submucosal oedema and can result in life-threatening laryngeal obstruction. Involvement of the digestive tract commonly causes abdominal pain. This and the absence of accompanying urticarial weals or itch
synonyms: Bannister disease, hereditary | Familial angioedema | HAE - [hereditary angioneurotic oedema] | Hereditary angioneurotic oedema | Quincke disease or oedema | hereditary Quincke oedema | HAE | inherited  angioedema | HANE | Hereditary angioneurotic edema

name: Mucous membrane pemphigoid of penile mucosa
definition: Mucous membrane pemphigoid affecting the glans penis and inner surface of the prepuce. It may be difficult to distinguish from other erosive and scarring dermatoses of the penis such as ulcerative lichen planus or intraepithelial neoplasia (PIN).

name: Somatic delusion directed at the skin
definition: Disorders characterised by an all-pervading, unshakeable but delusional belief that something is amiss with the skin, e.g. that it emits a foul odour or that it is infested by insects.

name: Varicella without complication
definition: A disease caused by an infection with varicella zoster virus. This disease is characterised by a vesicular rash and fever. Transmission is by inhalation of infected respiratory secretions, or direct contact with fluid from vesicles.
synonyms: Chickenpox | varicella

name: Burn of forearm or elbow, superficial partial thickness burn
cn_name: 前臂和肘部浅层部分皮层烧伤

name: Burn of trunk except perineum or genitalia, full thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of trunk except perineum and genitalia, third degree
cn_name: 躯干（除外会阴或生殖器）全层皮肤烧伤

name: Mycosis fungoides, tumour stage
definition: Mycosis fungoides with one or more tumours measuring > 1 cm in diameter
synonyms: T3 stage mycosis fungoides | Tumour stage mycosis fungoides

name: Drug-induced hypomelanosis
definition: Focal or generalised loss of epidermal pigment attributable to pharmacologically active topical or systemical agent. This may be intentional with topical use of hydroquinone or monobenzylether of hydroquinone used with the intention of reducing skin pigmentation temporarily or permanently respectively. It is a rare side effect of chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine and has been described with the tyrosine kinase inhibitor, imatinib.
synonyms: medicament-induced hypomelanosis

name: Watson syndrome
definition: Watson syndrome is an autosomal dominant disorder characterized by pulmonary valvular stenosis, cafe-au-lait spots, decreased intellectual ability, and short stature. Most affected individuals have relative macrocephaly and Lisch nodules and about one-third of those affected have neurofibromas. It is thought that Watson syndrome is a variant of type 1 neurofibromatosis.
synonyms: Café-au-lait macules with pulmonary stenosis (MIM 193520) | pulmonic stenosis with cafe-au-lait spots

name: Fibroepithelial polyp of skin
definition: A common polypoid, often pedunculated non-neoplastic benign skin growth consisting of a fibrovascular core covered with normal or thinned epidermis. If torsion of the stalk occurs they may become painful, swollen and necrotic.
synonyms: Acrochordon | Fibroma molle | Skin tag | Soft fibroma | acrochordon | fibroepithelial polyp | soft fibroma | cutaneous tag | Fibroepithelial polyp | Fibroepithelial polyp of skin

name: Psoriatic nail hypertrophy
definition: Psoriatic nail hypertrophy results from excessive keratinisation under the nail plate and may lead to marked thickening and deformity.

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, buttock
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, buttock

name: HIV-associated pityriasis rubra pilaris
definition: A subtype of pityriasis rubra pilaris (PRP) found in patients with HIV infection, usually without significant immunosuppression. It is thought to have a poorer prognosis than the classical adult PRP and is commonly associated with concomitant nodulocystic acne.

name: Senile purpura
definition: A very common form of purpura affecting principally the photo-aged skin of the dorsal forearms and hands.
synonyms: Actinic purpura

name: Blepharoptosis - myopia - ectopia lentis
definition: Blepharoptosis - myopia - ectopia lentis syndrome is characterised by bilateral congenital blepharoptosis, ectopia lentis and high myopia.

name: Carcinoma in situ of vulva
synonyms: vulvar carcinoma in situ

name: Wilson disease
definition: Wilson disease is an autosomal recessive disorder of copper metabolism characterised by the toxic accumulation of copper, mainly in the liver and central nervous system that may present as hepatic, neurologic or psychiatric forms.
synonyms: Copper retention | Copper storage disease | Hepatocerebral degeneration | Kinnier-Wilson disease | Neurohepatic degeneration | Progressive hepatolenticular degeneration | Westphal-Strumpell Syndrome | Wilson's syndrome | lenticular degenerative disease | lenticular syndrome

name: Late syphilitic bursitis
definition: This is a long-standing disorder of inflammation of one or more bursae (small sacs) of synovial fluid in the body which usually results in pain and is caused by a syphilitic agent.
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis

name: Cheilodynia
definition: This is a diagnosis of exclusion where no alternative cause for a persistent unpleasant burning sensation affecting the lips can be found. It is regarded as a form of burning mouth syndrome.
synonyms: Painful lips

name: Primary cutaneous CD30-positive anaplastic large cell lymphoma
definition: An anaplastic large cell lymphoma that is limited to the skin at the time of diagnosis. Most patients present with solitary or localised skin lesions in a form of nodules or papules, which may be tumours. The t(2;5) translocation that is present in many cases of systemic anaplastic large cell lymphoma is not found in this disease.
synonyms: primary cutaneous CD30+ anaplastic large cell lymphoma | primary cutaneous CD30+ large T-cell lymphoma | primary cutaneous anaplastic large-cell lymphoma
cn_name: 原发皮肤CD30阳性大细胞淋巴瘤

name: Herpes simplex gingivostomatitis
synonyms: HSV - [herpes simplex virus] gingivostomatitis | herpes gingivostomatitis | herpes simplex infection of oral mucosa | herpes stomatitis | herpesviral gingivostomatitis | herpesviral stomatitis | herpetic gingivostomatitis | herpetic stomatitis | primary herpetic gingivostomatitis

name: Contact dermatitis due to skin damage from friction or micro-trauma
definition: Inflammatory reaction of the skin provoked by acute, prolonged or repeated mechanical injury from friction and micro-trauma. These include a variety of superficial skin insults such as repetitive cuts, tears, abrasions, friction and pressure.
synonyms: Frictional dermatitis | Frictional eczema | Wear-and-tear dermatitis

name: Benign multilocular cyst of unspecified site
Nail disorder associated with specified systemic disease
definition: Nail dystrophy attributable to systemic disorder. A wide range of systemic disorders may produce abnormalities of the nails.
cn_name: 系统性疾病相关的甲病

name: Radiotherapy-induced xerostomia

name: Anti-laminin-g1 pemphigoid
definition: A rare immunobullous dermatosis resembling epidermolysis bullosa acquisita but mediated by antibodies to the extracellular matrix glycoprotein, laminin g-1.
synonyms: anti-p200 pemphigoid

name: Disorders of the external ear involving the skin
Zoster conjunctivitis
synonyms: Conjunctivitis due to Herpes zoster | Herpes zoster conjunctivitis

name: Bowel-associated dermatosis-arthritis syndrome
definition: Bowel‐associated dermatosis–arthritis syndrome (BADAS) is a chronic relapsing disorder characterised by intermittent episodes of polyarthralgia, flu‐like symptoms and a papulopustular vasculitic rash. It is associated with gastrointestinal surgery, including bowel bypass procedures and bariatric surgery, but may also arise in other conditions such as inflammatory bowel disease or diverticulosis in which abnormal bacterial overgrowth may occur. It is thought that antibodies to bacterial cell wall
synonyms: BADAS - [Bowel-associated dermatosis-arthritis syndrome] | Bowel by-pass syndrome | bowel bypass syndrome | bowel-bypass syndrome without bowel bypass | intestinal bypass arthritis-dermatitis syndrome

name: Sequelae of burn or corrosion of ankle or foot
Tophaceous gout
definition: Tophi are precipitates of monosodium urate in the tissues of patients with hyperuricaemia and may be associated with other manifestations of hyperuricaemia including gouty arthropathy. They present particularly in the skin and subcutaneous tissue. One of the more common sites for them is the helix of the ear.
synonyms: Gouty tophi

name: Dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, pretibial
definition: A form of dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa in which blistering and scarring is largely confined to the shins.
synonyms: DDEB-Pt - [dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, pretibial] | DDEB-pretibial - [dominant dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa, pretibial]

name: Leukoedema of oral mucosa
definition: leukoedema a variant condition of the buccal mucosa, consisting of an increase in thickness of the epithelium and intracellular oedema of the stratum spinosum or stratum germinativum

name: Vasculitis affecting small cutaneous blood vessels
synonyms: Vasculitic purpura
cn_name: 累及小血管的皮肤血管炎

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to chromate
Contact dermatitis of lower leg
definition: Contact dermatitis not specified as irritant or allergic affecting the lower legs.

name: Drug-induced xerostomia
definition: Xerostomia due to drug-induced suppression of saliva production (e.g. from anticholinergic drugs).

name: Angular cheilitis exacerbated by nutritional deficiency
definition: Angular cheilitis which results from nutritional deficiency, particularly of riboflavin, folate or iron.

name: Primary cutaneous B-cell lymphomas
definition: Primary cutaneous B-cell lymphomas (CBCL) are malignant lymphoproliferative diseases of unknown cause that are thought to originate from B-lymphocytes in the lymphoid tissue of the skin and by definition are confined to the skin at initial diagnosis.

name: Marginal papular palmoplantar keratoderma
definition: A punctate palmoplantar keratoderma that is characterized by hyperkeratinization of the palms and soles, has_material_basis_in autosomal dominant inheritance of mutation in the AAGAB gene.
synonyms: Acrokeratoelastoidosis of Costa | Focal acral hyperkeratosis | acrokeratoelastoidosis of Costa | punctate palmoplantar hyperkeratosis type 3 | punctate palmoplantar keratoderma type 3

name: Sweat gland tumour of unspecified site
Foreign body reaction to inorganic matter in the skin
definition: A usually granulomatous, often sarcoidal reaction to the presence in the skin of inorganic foreign material which cannot be degraded or eliminated. Responsible agents include tattoo pigment, suture materials, silica, zirconium, aluminium, paraffin, and silicone.
cn_name: 无机物引起的的皮肤异物反应

name: Changes in skin texture
definition: Alterations in skin texture of unspecified cause.
synonyms: Skin textural disturbance

name: Raynaud phenomenon due to other specified disorder
definition: Raynaud phenomenon due to disorders other than non-organ specific systemic autoimmune diseases.
synonyms: Raynaud phenomenon secondary to other specified disorder

name: Onycho-tricho-dysplasia – neutropaenia syndrome
definition: Onychotrichodysplasia - neutropaenia (ONMR syndrome) is a form of trichothiodystrophy (sulphur-deficient brittle hair) characterised by hypoplastic fingernails, trichorrhexis, chronic neutropaenia, and mild psychomotor retardation.
synonyms: Itin syndrome | ONMR (MIM 258360) | ONMR syndrome | Trichothiodystrophy type G

name: Drug-induced nail abnormalities
definition: Abnormalities of nails or nail growth attributable to drugs.
synonyms: medicament-induced nail abnormalities

name: Pityriasis amiantacea
definition: Pityriasis amiantacea refers to a reaction pattern on the scalp where large adherents are attached to the growing hairs and overlap like tiles on a roof. The abnormality may be localised and confined to a small patch or widespread involving the entire scalp. The underlying scalp is often moist and inflamed. The condition may occur on its own or may be associated with inflammatory disorders such as seborrhoeic dermatitis and psoriasis or with underlying dermatophytosis (tinea capitis).
synonyms: Tinea amiantacea

name: 9q34 deletion
synonyms: 9q34 deletion syndrome

name: Emotional flushing
definition: Flushing or blushing triggered by embarrassment is common in adolescents and often improves with time. Anger may also trigger flushing in some people. If the reaction is severe it may represent a significant handicap.
synonyms: Blushing | blushing

name: Cutaneous reactions to venomous or noxious vertebrates
definition: Cutaneous reactions from bites or stings of certain vertebrates.

name: X-linked recessive Fabry disease
definition: This is a rare X-linked lysosomal storage disease, which can cause a wide range of systemic symptoms. The inheritance is X-linked recessive.

name: Generalised vitiligo
definition: Vitiligo affecting more than one body site, commonly the face and extremities (acrofacial vitiligo), the axillae, groins and genitalia.
synonyms: generalized vitiligo

name: Allergic contact urticaria
definition: Allergic contact urticaria is a Type I IgE-mediated immediate immune reaction from cutaneous or mucosal contact to a substance or substances to which the individual has previously been exposed.
synonyms: allergic contact urticaria NOS

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, wrist joint
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, wrist joint

name: Allergic contact dermatitis due to other specified metal or metal salt
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to any of a wide range of metals or metal salts other than nickel or chromate\: examples include palladium and gold.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema due to other specified metal or metal salt

name: Deafness – onychodystrophy, autosomal dominant
definition: Autosomal dominant deafness-onychodystrophy syndrome is a very rare condition characterised by congenital hearing impairment, absent or hypoplastic nails on the hands and feet and brachydactyly.
synonyms: DDOD syndrome (MIM 124480) | DOOR syndrome, autosomal dominant | Dominant deafness-onychodystrophy

name: Staphylococcal toxin-mediated reactions involving skin
definition: A condition of the skin, caused by an infection with toxins released from the gram-positive bacteria Staphylococcus.

name: Juvenile dermatomyositis
definition: Juvenile dermatomyositis is the early-onset form of dermatomyositis, a systemic autoimmune inflammatory muscle disorder, characterised by proximal muscle weakness, evocative skin lesion, and systemic manifestations.
synonyms: childhood type dermatomyositis | childhood Dermatomyositis | Juvenile dermatomyositis

name: Late lesions of pinta
definition: Late lesions of pinta are confined to the skin and are characterised by dyschromia and atrophy. They typically take between two and four years to develop following initial infection. The skin appears mottled and atrophic with numerous irregular and variegated hypermelanotic, hypomelanotic and amelanotic patches typically involving the wrists, palms, ankles, and elbows, as well as the skin around and within old lesions.
synonyms: Achromia due to late pinta | Achromic cutaneous late pinta | Cardiovascular late pinta | Cicatricial cutaneous late pinta | Cutaneous late pinta | Dyschromic cutaneous late pinta | Hypomelanosis due to late pinta

name: Secondary disorders of external ear
definition: This refers to disorders of the external portion of the ear, which consists of the pinna and external auditory meatus, in diseases classified elsewhere.
synonyms: Disorders of external ear in diseases classified elsewhere

name: Granuloma faciale
synonyms: Granuloma, eosinophilic, skin | facial granuloma with eosinophilia

name: Erythema multiforme provoked by Mycoplasma pneumoniae
definition: Erythema multiforme is a skin condition of unknown cause, possibly mediated by deposition of immune complex (mostly IgM) in the superficial microvasculature of the skin and oral mucous membrane that usually follows an infection or drug exposure. This diagnosis is provoked by a very small bacterium in the class Mollicutes. It causes the disease mycoplasma pneumonia, a form of atypical bacterial pneumonia, and is related to cold agglutinin disease.

name: Trombiculosis
definition: A pruritic, papular or papulovesicular skin eruption due to bites by the larvae of trombiculid mites (harvest mites or chiggers). The larva injects digestive enzymes into the skin in order to facilitate feeding from host tissue. The cutaneous response is considered to be due both to an irritant effect of the larval saliva and an acquired hypersensitivity to salivary antigens. Areas favoured by the mites are those where the skin is thin (genital region, axillae, limb flexures).
synonyms: Harvest mite infestation | chigger bites | chigger infestation | harvest itch | scrub itch | trombicula infestation | trombiculiasis | trombiculid mite infestation | trombidiosis

name: Immunobullous dermatosis classified elsewhere precipitated by drug
definition: Certain drugs may precipitate immunobullous diseases. The most commonly provoked diseases are pemphigus foliaceus, bullous pemphigoid and mucous membrane pemphigoid. Drug-induced linear IgA bullous dermatosis and epidermolysis bullosa acquisita have also been reported. The majority of reactions are to drugs with or whose metabolites contain a thiol group. Examples include penicillamine and captopril.

name: Inducible urticaria or angioedema
definition: The inducible or physical urticarias are a heterogeneous subgroup of urticarias in which pruritic weals, angioedema or both are triggered reproducibly by specific external physical stimuli. The onset of wealing is characteristically rapid with resolution within an hour. The exception is delayed pressure urticaria where weals take longer to develop and resolve. Individual susceptibility to a physical urticaria may be short-lived or may last for years.
synonyms: angioedema NOS | inducible urticaria NOS | physical urticaria or angioedema
cn_name: 诱导性荨麻疹和血管性水肿

name: Deafness – enamel hypoplasia – nail defects
definition: This syndrome is characterised by sensorineural hearing loss, generalised enamel hypoplasia of the permanent dentition with normal primary dentition, and nail defects (Beau's lines and leukonychia). Less than 10 patients have been described so far. Transmission is autosomal recessive.
synonyms: Heimler syndrome | Sensorineural hearing loss with enamel hypoplasia and nail defects

name: Submandibular abscess without mention of mediastinal, parapharyngeal or cervical extension
Metastatic tuberculous abscess
definition: A metastatic tuberculous abscess (tuberculous gumma) is a tuberculous ulcer arising in the skin as a result of haematogenous spread of M. tuberculosis from an internal focus of infection. They can be multiple.
synonyms: Tuberculous gumma

name: Non-pyogenic bacterial infections of the skin
definition: Skin infection by bacteria which do not characteristically induce pus formation.
cn_name: 皮肤非脓性细菌性感染

name: Syringotropic mycosis fungoides
definition: A rare variant of mycosis fungoides characterised by prominent involvement of the eccrine glands with syringometaplasia.
synonyms: syringotropic cutaneous T-cell lymphoma

name: Indeterminate cell histiocytosis
definition: A very rare dendritic cell tumour composed of spindle to ovoid cells with a phenotype that is similar to the Langerhans cells. Patients usually present with cutaneous papules, nodules, and plaques. Systemic symptoms are usually absent. The clinical course is variable.
synonyms: Indeterminate dendritic cell tumour
cn_name: 不确定型树突细胞肿瘤

name: Female syphilitic pelvic inflammatory disease
definition: Inflammation of the uterus, fallopian tubes, or ovaries due to infection with Treponema pallidum (Syphilis), which may progress to scar formation with adhesions to nearby tissues and organs.

name: Arterial dissection - lentiginosis
definition: Arterial dissection refers to a tear within the wall of a blood vessel, which allows blood to separate the wall layers, creating a pseudoaneurysm. Lentiginosis refers to the presence of lentigines in large numbers or in a distinctive configuration.

name: Argyria
definition: Argyria is a dermatosis resulting from prolonged contact with or ingestion of silver salts. It is initially characterised by grey to grey-black or grey-blue staining of the gums, later progressing to diffuse skin involvement through silver deposition.

name: Laryngeal lymphatic malformation
synonyms: Laryngeal lymphangioma

name: Oesophageal varices with bleeding in alcoholic liver disease
definition: This refers to abnormally dilated veins in the lining of oesophagus with sign of bleeding in patients with alcoholic disease.

name: Trichodysplasia - amelogenesis imperfecta
definition: Trichodysplasia - amelogenesis imperfecta is defined as the association of amelogenesis imperfecta and a microscopically typical hair dysplasia.

name: Corrosion of first degree of trunk
Miscellaneous skin conditions arising predominantly in infancy
Melanocytic naevus with mild to moderate dysplasia
Glandular cheilitis
definition: A rare skin disease of unknown origin characterized by macrocheilia and secretions of thick saliva from swollen labial minor salivary glands.Cheilitis glandularis (CG) prevalence is unknown. Less than a 100 cases have been reported to date, predominantly in adult males, and more rarely in adult females.The clinical picture of CG consists of variable degrees of macrocheilia accompanied by the presence of red, dilated ostia of minor salivary glands on the vermilion area. A thick, mucoid material c

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, pelvis
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, pelvis

name: Antineutrophil cytoplasmic antibody-associated vasculitis
definition: Necrotizing vasculitis, with few or no immune deposits, predominantly affecting small vessels (i.e., capillaries, venules, arterioles and small arteries), associated with MPO-ANCA or PR3-ANCA. Not all patients have ANCA. Add a prefix indicating ANCA reactivity, e.g. PR3-ANCA, MPO-ANCA, ANCA-negative.
synonyms: ANCA - [Antineutrophil cytoplasmic antibodies] associated vasculitis | ANCA [Antineutrophil cytoplasmic antibodies] positive vasculitis
