name: Pythiosis
definition: Pythiosis is a life-threatening infection by the oömycete Pythium insidiosum. Although infection in animals occurs widely across the world, human pythiosis is largely confined to Thailand and, with the exception of ocular disease, is closely associated with underlying haematological disease, especially thalassaemia. There is a high mortality in patients with disseminated or vascular disease. In the latter form, invasion of arterial wall results in vascular occlusion and a frequent need for amput

name: Frostbite with tissue necrosis of abdominal wall, lower back or pelvis
Secondary anetoderma
definition: Anetoderma arising following a large variety of skin conditions where there has been focal damage to dermal elastin. It is commonly seen in children following varicella.

name: Malignant neoplasm metastasis in skin of cheek
synonyms: cheek metastasis

name: Certain fluke infestations affecting the skin
Subcutaneous fat necrosis due to birth injury
definition: Subcutaneous fat necrosis is a rare acute transient hypodermatitis that develops within first weeks of life in term infants.

name: Diffuse bleeding diathesis due to vitamin K deficient haemorrhagic disease of fetus or newborn, late
definition: Late on-set vitamin K deficiency bleeding usually occurs at 2-12 weeks and is seen in breast-fed infants who did not receive vitamin K prophylaxis.
synonyms: Diffuse bleeding diathesis secondary to vitamin K deficient haemorrhagic disease of fetus and newborn, late

name: Aphthosis of penis or scrotum
definition: Genital aphthosis in men is a non-infective but often painful ulceration of penile, scrotal or perigenital skin. It may be associated with oral ulceration (orogenital aphthosis) or with Behçet disease but may occur on its own. Both genital and orogenital aphthosis may represent formes frustes of Behçet disease.
synonyms: Genital aphthosis in the male | male genital aphthosis | male genital organ aphthous ulcer

name: Guttate lichen sclerosus
definition: Extragenital lichen sclerosus in which the disorder presents as multiple 1-2 mm diameter white guttate macules or papules.
synonyms: Guttate scleroderma | White spot disease

name: Trichorrhexis nodosa syndrome
synonyms: Pollitt syndrome | Trichothiodystrophy-neurocutaneous syndrome

Scorbutic anaemia
definition: Scorbutic anaemia is a common finding in infants and young children with scurvy and is related to impaired iron absorption and coexistent haematopoietic nutrient deficiencies including iron, vitamin B12 and folate.
synonyms: anaemia due to ascorbic acid deficiency | anaemia due to scurvy | anaemia due to vitamin c deficiency | scorbutus anaemia

synonyms: Congenital leukonychia | NDNC3 - [nail disorder, nonsyndromic, congenital 3] (MIM 151600) | Porcelain nails | True leukonychia

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type IV
definition: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy, type 4 (HSAN4) is an inherited disorder characterised by anhidrosis, insensitivity to pain, self-mutilating behaviour and episodes of fever.
synonyms: Congenital insensitivity to pain with anhidrosis | HSAN4 - [Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type 4]

name: Skin infiltration by nodal or non-cutaneous extranodal lymphoma
definition: Involvement of skin by lymphoma other than primary cutaneous lymphoma. Well-recognised causes are HTLV-1-associated adult T-cell leukaemia-lymphoma and angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma. The cutaneous signs are highly variable and include maculopapular eruptions which may be mistaken for a drug eruption, large tumour nodules which may ulcerate and erythroderma.

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, forearm
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, forearm

name: Syphilitic pericarditis
synonyms: syphilitic hydropericarditis | syphilitic hydropneumopericarditis

name: Congenital ptosis, bilateral
definition: This is a congenital drooping or falling of the upper or lower eyelid. The drooping may be worse after being awake longer, when the individual's muscles are tired. This condition is sometimes called \"lazy eye\", but that term normally refers to amblyopia. If severe enough and left untreated, the drooping eyelid can cause other conditions, such as amblyopia or astigmatism. This diagnosis is occurring in both eyes.

name: Congenital pneumonia due to Rubella
synonyms: Congenital rubella pneumonitis

name: Immunosuppressive therapy as contributor to dermatosis classified elsewhere
Ulcerative or erosive disorders of the vulva
Burn of breast, full thickness burn
Pityriasis rubra pilaris
definition: Pityriasis rubra pilaris (PRP) is the name given to a group of clinically similar papulosquamous dermatoses of unknown aetiology. They initially present with erythematous, hyperkeratotic perifollicular papules, which tend to coalesce into plaques, but may progress to erythroderma, particularly in adults. The distribution, age of onset and speed of onset differ markedly between patients and these differences have been used to classify PRP into a number of clinically distinct subtypes.
synonyms: Devergie's disease | PRP - [pityriasis rubra pilaris] | devergie's disease | lichen ruber acuminatus | lichen ruber pilaris | PRP

name: Xanthoma in association with primary hypertriglyceridaemia
definition: Cutaneous xanthomata resulting from grossly elevated circulating triglyceride levels in patients with genetically-determined dyslipidaemia. The most common presentation is with eruptive xanthomata.

name: Angiomyolipomatosis and isolated cysts associated with tuberous sclerosis
Acute effects of ionizing radiation on the skin
definition: Injury caused by high doses of ionizing radiation reaching the skin over a short period of time as may be seen focally following radiotherapy for cutaneous malignancies or more extensively as the result of the unintentional or intentional exposure of a person or population to such radiation.

name: Callosities of other sites
definition: Callosities located other than on the hands and feet. They may arise as the result of friction from appliances (e.g. trusses, calipers, prostheses) or from often repeated minor frictional trauma as, for instance, over the ischial tuberosities in cyclists or, in devout Muslims, on the central forehead from repeatedly touching the head on a prayer stone (prayer nodules).

name: Syphilitic cerebral arteritis
definition: This is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with cerebral arteritis.

name: Oesophageal varices with bleeding in toxic liver disease
definition: This refers to abnormally dilated veins in the lining of oesophagus with sign of bleeding in patients with toxic liver disease (chemical-driven liver damage).

name: Oculocutaneous albinism type 1A
definition: OCA-1 is caused by mutation in the tyrosinase gene (TYR). OCA-1A is due to complete absence of TYR and a more severe clinical phenotype than OCA-1B.
synonyms: Albino | OCA1A - [Oculocutaneous albinism type 1A] | Tyrosinase-negative oculocutaneous albinism

name: Late congenital syphilitic sensorineural deafness
definition: This is a late congenital, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with sensorineural deafness.

name: Papular elastorrhexis
definition: Papular elastorrhexis is an asymptomatic skin disorder which presents as firm non-follicular well-demarcated white papules distributed evenly over the trunk, including the chest, abdomen, back and shoulders, upper extremities and, rarely, thighs. There is typically no history of antecedent trauma, local inflammation, or acne. On histopathological examination there is substantial fragmentation or nearly complete loss of elastic tissue in the reticular dermis.
synonyms: Eruptive collagenoma | Nevus Anelasticus

name: Pyoderma gangrenosum, bullous
definition: A pattern of pyoderma gangrenosum which is most commonly located on the face, upper limbs and trunk and remain more superficial than classical ulcerative pyoderma gangrenosum. It is particularly associated with haematological disorders including myelodysplasia and IgA paraproteinaemias.
synonyms: Pyoderma gangrenosum, atypical

name: Infectious disorders of eyelid
synonyms: infectious disorders of eyelid NOS

name: Corticosteroid-modified dermatophytosis
definition: Dermatophyte infection modified by application of potent topical corticosteroid preparations or, less commonly, by systemic corticosteroid therapy. Continued exposure to corticosteroids, typically prescribed for a presumed inflammatory dermatosis, obscures the true diagnosis by reducing scaling and inflammation but allows the infection to spread widely.
synonyms: Tinea incognita | steroid-modified tinea infection | tinea incognito

name: Jellyfish, Cubozoa sting
definition: Envenomation by box jellyfish (Cubozoa) including the sea wasp, Chironex fleckeri. It manifests with severe localised pain and inflammation and can cause life-threatening systemic toxicity.

name: Diffuse plane xanthomatosis
definition: This is a rare normolipidaemic disease in which multiple flat xanthomatous macules and plaques develop in the skin in association with a monoclonal paraproteinaemia. The disease generally occurs in adults but rare cases in children have been reported. The xanthomata typically involve the eyelids, neck, upper trunk, buttocks and flexures. Serum lipids are usually normal. Nearly all patients have a circulating monoclonal paraprotein which in about half the cases is associated with a myeloprolifera
synonyms: Diffuse normolipaemic plane xanthomatosis

name: Chronic seborrhoeic otitis externa
definition: Chronic seborrhoeic dermatitis of the external ear.

name: Sarcoidosis of oral cavity
definition: This is a syndrome involving abnormal collections of chronic inflammatory cells (granulomas) that can form as nodules in the oral cavity.

name: Punctate leukoderma
definition: A dermatosis associated with both psoralen photochemotherapy and UVB phototherapy in which multiple round or oval sharply demarcated punctate macules measuring 0.5 – 1.5 mm in diameter form principally on the fronts of the shins and on the extensor aspects of the arms. It is attributed to phototoxic damage to melanocytes.

name: Tuberculous chancre
definition: A tuberculous chancre arises as a result of inoculation of M. tuberculosis into the skin and/or mucous membranes of individuals without prior exposure to tuberculosis. It presents initially as a painless reddish-brown papule which slowly enlarges before breaking down into a sharply demarcated ulcer.
synonyms: Primary inoculation tuberculosis

name: Atrichia with keratin cysts
definition: Atrichia with papular lesions is a rare inherited form of alopecia characterised by irreversible hair loss during the neonatal period on all hear-bearing areas of the body, later associated with the development of papular lesions all over the body and preferentially on the face and extensor surfaces of the extremities.
synonyms: Atrichia with papular lesions

name: Menkes disease
definition: Menkes disease (MD) is a usually severe multisystemic disorder of copper metabolism, characterised by progressive neurodegeneration and marked connective tissue anomalies as well as typical sparse abnormal steely hair. MD is an X-linked disease primarily affecting males while females are usually unaffected carriers.
synonyms: Kinky hair disease | Steely hair disease | Trichopoliodystrophy | kinky hair disease | x-linked recessive copper deficiency syndrome

name: Aquagenic wrinkling of the fingers or palms
definition: A condition characterised by excessive wrinkling and oedema of the glabrous skin of the hands after brief immersion of the hands in water. It is strongly associated with cystic fibrosis and with carriers of the cystic fibrosis gene.
synonyms: Aquagenic wrinkling of the palms | Water-induced finger wrinkling

name: Yaws
definition: An infectious disease caused by Treponema pallidum subsp. pertenue which mainly affects children in rural communities in the humid tropics. It affects the skin and bones, is spread by skin to skin contact, and is not sexually transmitted, but cannot be distinguished serologically from syphilis.
synonyms: Bouba | castellani | frambesia | frambesia tropica | framboesia | framboesia tropica | framboesioma | infection by treponema pertenue | parangi | pian

name: Tanapox
definition: Tanapox is caused by a yatapoxvirus. It is endemic amongst monkeys in East Africa. In humans it presents as a single or a small number of umbilicated papules or nodules, usually on the lower limbs or trunk. It is accompanied by systemic malaise and lymphadenopathy. The lesions heal with scarring.

name: Secondary syphilitic periostitis
definition: This refers to a sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum, causing inflammation of the periosteum.

definition: Allergic contact dermatitis due to low molecular weight allergens from organic matter including plants and woods such as Primula obconica, sesquiterpene lactones and teak.

definition: Hereditary autosomal disorders in which ichthyosis and hair abnormalities are prominent features.

synonyms: I-cell disease | Inclusion cell disease | N-acetyl-glucosamine 1-phosphotransferase deficiency

Paroxysmal haematoma of the finger
definition: Paroxysmal haematoma of the finger (Achenbach syndrome) is a rare clinical entity consisting of spontaneous paroxysmal haemorrhage into the volar aspects of a digit (less commonly the palm) associated with burning pain and swelling. The cause is unknown.
synonyms: Achenbach syndrome | Paroxysmal hematoma of the finger

name: Oral aphthae
synonyms: Aphthous stomatitis | Aphthous ulcer | Oral aphthous ulcers

name: Certain mycobacterial infections affecting the skin

name: Paraneoplastic hypermelanosis

name: Pubertal stretch marks

name: Congenital entropion, bilateral
definition: This is an inversion of the edges of eyelids that occurs in both eyes and is present at birth, resulting in irritation of the corneas.

Arthropathy associated with acromegaly
definition: Osteoarthrosis of joints associated with acromegaly that results when the anterior pituitary gland produces excess growth hormone(GH). This clinical state usually occurs after epiphyseal plate closure at puberty.
synonyms: acromegalic arthritis | acromegalic arthropathy | acromegaly with arthropathy

name: Papulonecrotic tuberculid
definition: Papulonecrotic tuberculid is characterised by widely scattered but symmetrically distributed crops of dusky red papules or papulopustules on the extensor surfaces of the limbs and on the buttocks. The centres of the papules may become necrotic before healing with scarring. It is associated with underlying tuberculous infection.

name: Primary cutaneous diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, leg type
definition: An aggressive primary cutaneous B-cell lymphoma, usually involving the lower leg. It is composed of a generally monotonous proliferation of immunoblasts, or less frequently centroblasts, with few admixed reactive cells. This type of lymphoma occurs most often in the elderly who present with rapidly growing tumours, usually on one or both legs. Dissemination to extracutaneous sites is frequent.
synonyms: DLBCLLT

name: Bromoderma
definition: Bromoderma describes a particular cutaneous reaction to bromides, which were historically used therapeutically as sedatives and anticonvulsants. The eruption is characterised by vegetating acneform nodules and plaques on the face or limbs. Because bromides are only very slowly excreted the eruption may not appear until several weeks after exposure.
synonyms: bromide-induced acne eruption

name: PUVA-associated pseudoporphyria
definition: A blistering eruption clinically indistinguishable from porphyria cutanea tarda which may arise in patients who have received psoralen photochemotherapy over a prolonged period. It is not associated with demonstrable abnormalities of porphyrins.

name: Cutaneous complications of surgical procedures
definition: Adverse effects on the skin resulting from cutaneous surgical procedures or from surgical procedures requiring access through the skin.

Perinatal Herpes simplex infection

name: Shagreen patch
definition: Shagreen patch is a form of connective tissue naevus which is strongly associated with tuberous sclerosis but which cannot be differentiated clinically or histologically from an innocent connective tissue nevus. Its name derives from its resemblance to the surface of untanned leather (rawhide). The presence of a shagreen patch should prompt investigation for underlying tuberous sclerosis.

name: Pityriasis rubra pilaris, juvenile
definition: Pityriasis rubra pilaris with onset in childhood, the majority of cases of which are the classical juvenile type III.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 3
definition: Pressure ulcer with full thickness skin loss. Subcutaneous fat may be visible but bone, tendon or muscle are not exposed. Slough may be present but does not obscure the depth of tissue loss. There may be undermining and tunnelling into adjacent structures. The depth varies by anatomical location\: grade 3 pressure ulcers can be shallow in areas with little or no subcutaneous fat (e.g. bridge of the nose, ear, occiput and malleolus). In contrast, grade 3 pressure ulcers can be extremely deep in a
synonyms: bedsore stage III | pressure injury stage 3 | pressure injury stage 3 with full thickness skin loss | pressure ulcer category 3 | pressure ulcer with full thickness skin loss | stage 3 decubitus ulcer | stage 3 pressure ulcer | stage III pressure injury

synonyms: calcifying fibroma | cartilage analogue of fibromatosis | juvenile aponeurotic fibroma

definition: Irritation from sea sponges occurs either as a mechanical reaction to tiny spicules released from the sponge or due to an allergic reaction to the sponge itself. Initial symptoms of pricking and stinging may be followed by severe pruritus, burning pain, oedema and blistering.

The major cause of hyporiboflavinosis is inadequate dietary intake as a result of limited food supply, which is sometimes exacerbated by poor food storage or processing. Children in developing countries will commonly demon
synonyms: Ariboflavinosis | Hyporiboflavinosis | Riboflavin deficiency

Histidinaemia
definition: Histidinemia is a disorder of histidine metabolism caused by a defect in histidase, and seems to be benign in most affected individuals.

name: Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome
definition: Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome (HSP) is a multi-system disorder characterised by oculocutaneous albinism, bleeding diathesis and, in some cases, neutropaenia, pulmonary fibrosis, or granulomatous colitis. name: Trichotillomania
definition: Trichotillomania is characterised by recurrent pulling of one’s own hair leading to significant hair loss, accompanied by unsuccessful attempts to decrease or stop the behaviour. Hair pulling may occur from any region of the body in which hair grows but the most common sites are the scalp, eyebrows, and eyelids. Hair pulling may occur in brief episodes scattered throughout the day or in less frequent but more sustained periods. The symptoms result in significant distress or significant impairmen
synonyms: Compulsive hair plucking | Hair pulling disorder | habitual hair pulling

name: Irritant contact dermatitis due to saliva

name: Mosaicism, 45, X or other cell line with abnormal sex chromosome
definition: A disease caused by embryonic fusion or the structural mutation of a sex chromosome early in embryonic development, resulting in a subset of cells in the body having one normal copy of the X chromosome and one abnormal sex chromosome. name: Cutaneous or lymphocutaneous sporotrichosis

This disease is characterised by small red, pink, or purple nodules at the site of infection. This disease may also present with bumps or nodules surrounding the original lesion. Transmission is by direct contact with infected thorny plants, sphagnum moss, soil, bales of hays, or infected plant material. Confirmation is by identification of Sporothrix schenckii from a tissue or skin sample.

Atopic eczema

synonyms: atopic dermatitis | atopic eczema NOS | besnier prurigo | AD | atopic eczema | eczema

definition: A disease caused by determinants arising after birth. This disease is characterised low levels of riboflavin (vitamin B2) leading to low levels of red blood cells in the body. This disease may present with fatigue, muscle weakness, loss of appetite, weight loss, diarrhoea, nausea, fast heartbeat or numbness in extremities. name: Scleromyxoedema

name: Vulvodynia
definition: Vulvodynia describes a chronic sensation of pain, burning or rawness of vulval skin which cannot be ascribed to any specific cause and persists for at least three months. Symptoms may be diffuse and unprovoked (dysaesthetic vulvodynia) or localised, usually to the vulval vestibule, and provoked by touch (vestibulodynia). synonyms: Burning sensation of vulva | Vulval pain syndrome

name: Sclerodactyly
definition: Sclerodactyly is a morphological term used to describe thickening and sclerosis of the skin and soft tissues of the fingers with resultant limitation of movement and joint contractures. In severe cases ulceration may occur. It is typically seen in autoimmune disorders including limited systemic sclerosis (CRST syndrome), diffuse systemic sclerosis and mixed connective tissue disease, in which case it is likely to be associated with Raynaud phenomenon. It may also be associated, however, with lon

name: Sublingual varices

It has been described predominantly in otherwise healthy middle-aged individuals

synonyms: Cleft tongue | Double tongue | Glossoschisis

Fissures are common and may cause significant discomfort. It is commonly seen in association with involvement of other flexural surfaces or the scalp.

definition: Benign cephalic histiocytosis is a rare, self-limiting class IIa (non-Langerhans cell) histiocytosis that typically starts in early childhood with the development of asymptomatic erythematous papules, nodules and macules on the cheeks, forehead, earlobes and neck. With time they turn reddish-brown before gradually resolving. and may spread onto the trunk, upper limbs and rarely the buttocks. Mucous membrane involvement has not been described.

It is characterised by the rapid onset of extensive erythema, necrosis, and bullous detachment of the epidermis (> 30% body surface area). Commonly, the mucous membranes are also involved. Death may result from a combination of sepsis, fluid depleti
synonyms: Lyell syndrome | Lyell syndrome (toxic epidermal necrolysis) | TEN - [toxic epidermal necrolysis] | lyell's syndrome | TEN

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum D
definition: Xeroderma pigmentosum complementation group D (XPD) is a subtype of xeroderma pigmentosum (XP), a rare photodermatosis predisposing to skin cancers, and is a clinically heterogeneous form that shows typical XP manifestations (photosensitivity of skin with burning, freckling, and dryness of skin, skin cancers) associated with a spectrum of neurological anomalies (from no abnormality to severe neurological disease).
synonyms: XPD - [xeroderma pigmentosum D]

name: Klippel-Trénaunay syndrome

It differs from Parkes-Weber syndrome in which limb hypertrophy and capillary malformations are associated with fast flow arteriovenous malformations and, in some cases, with mutations in the RASA1 gene .
synonyms: Klippel-Trenaunay-Weber syndrome

synonyms: Calciphylaxis | calcific arteriopathy | calcific uremic arteriolopathy | calcifying panniculitis | uremic gangrene syndrome

definition: Trichothiodystrophy is a heterogeneous group disorders characterised by short, brittle hair with low-sulphur content (due to an abnormal synthesis of the sulphur containing keratins). The clinical appearance is always characterised by brittle and fragile hair, often combined with growth retardation and intellectual deficit, congenital ichthyosis and nail abnormalities, among ot

definition: This is a cutaneous condition that is characterised by a macular, depigmented eruption found mainly on the face, arms, and upper part of the trunk. It occurs years after the successful treatment of visceral leishmaniasis.

definition: Ectodermal dysplasias (EDs) are a heterogeneous group of syndromes characterised by developmental dystrophies of ectodermal structures, such as hypohidrosis, hypotrichosis, onychodysplasia and hypodontia or anodontia. definition: Callosities attributable to work. There are many different occupations in which the hands in particular are subjected to repetitive friction and pressure. definition: An uncommon form of granuloma annulare in which necrobiotic collagen is extruded through the epidermis.

Hypertrophic scars on the other hand remain confined to the area of injury or inflammation and may undergo spontaneous resolution.

definition: A heterogeneous group of disorders which result in deposition of calcium in skin and soft tissues.

name: Keratosis circumscripta
definition: A condition characterised by the development during early childhood of persistent prominent grouped follicular papules on the neck, posterior axillary folds, elbows, sacral area, hips and knees. There may be associated thickening of palmoplantar skin. It appears to be most prevalent in Sub-Saharan Africa.

name: Autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease type 1 with tuberous sclerosis
definition: Autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease type 1 with tuberous sclerosis (PKD-TSC) is characterised by early-onset and severe polycystic kidney disease with various manifestations of tuberous sclerosis (multiple angiomyolipomas, lymphangioleiomyomatosis and periventricular calcifications of the central nervous system).
synonyms: Tuberous sclerosis/polycystic kidney disease contiguous gene syndrome

name: Irritant contact dermatitis of male genitalia
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis affecting male genitalia. This may be due to prolonged contact with urine and/or faeces, e.g. resulting from neglect and poor hygiene. If incontinence is the primary cause it should be coded as Irritant contact dermatitis due to incontinence. An occupational cause is repeated contact with wet or contaminated work clothing.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema of male genitalia

name: Johanson-Blizzard syndrome
definition: Johanson-Blizzard syndrome encompasses a spectrum of clinical features but the hallmarks of the syndrome are hypoplastic alae nasi, dental anomalies, short stature, microcephaly, mental retardation, hypothyroidism, and deafness.

name: Dermographism
definition: Dermographism is characterised by the development of short-lived itchy weals in response to stroking of the skin. The weals are typically linear. Symptomatic dermographism is triggered by light skin stroking or friction and is pruritic, whereas simple dermographism is a common physiological response to firm skin stroking and is not pruritic.
synonyms: Dermatographic urticaria | Dermatographism | Factitious urticaria | Urticaria factitia

name: Sparse hair - short stature - skin anomalies
definition: Sparse hair - short stature - skin anomalies syndrome is a rare form of ectodermal dysplasia that combines short stature, sparse hair, skin hyperpigmentation and urticaria-like reactions on the hands and arms. An upper central incisor, hypoplastic thumbs and/or palmoplantar hyperkeratosis may also be present.

name: Calcific panniculitis
definition: Calcific panniculitis presents as discrete, firm subcutaneous masses, often affecting the thighs and hips. It is strongly associated with hyperparathyroidism, particularly in the context of chronic renal failure. It may occur in conjunction with but is clinically distinct from calcific arteriolopathy (calciphylaxis).
synonyms: Benign nodular calcification | Calcifying panniculitis | Metastatic calcinosis cutis

name: Loxoscelism
definition: Reaction to the bite of any one of several species of recluse spiders of the genus Loxsceles. Severe envenomation can result in extensive local tissue necrosis and, rarely, in systemic manifestations including disseminated intravascular coagulation.
synonyms: Recluse spider bite

name: Endemic pemphigus foliaceus
definition: Endemic pemphigus foliaceus is a chronic autoimmune skin diseases characterised by superficial blister formation on the skin mediated by auto-antibodies to the desmosome component desmoglein 1. Endemic pemphigus foliaceus has unique epidemiological features of geographical, temporal and familial clustering.
synonyms: Brazilian pemphigus | Fogo selvagem | Wild fire

name: Extracutaneous infantile haemangioma
definition: Infantile haemangioma located elsewhere than the skin, in particular in the liver, where they may result in congestive cardiac failure, or in the subglottic portion of the larynx, where they may obstruct the airway. They may also present as masses in the orbit or in the tarsal conjunctiva.
synonyms: infantile haemangioma of rare localisation

name: Pyoderma gangrenosum classified by aetiological associations
definition: Pyoderma gangrenosum classified according to the presence or absence of associated antecedent or coincident recognised predisposing condition.

name: Non-syndromic nummular or linear palmoplantar keratodermas
Hypertrophy of lip
definition: An acquired structural abnormality of one or both lips in which there is a fixed increase in the size of the lip resulting from hypertrophy of its glandular or fibrous stromal elements, from infiltrative inflammatory or non-inflammatory processes or from fixed vascular dilatation as may develop in a vascular malformation.

name: Burn of abdominal wall
synonyms: abdominal burn | abdominal wall radiation burn

name: Aplasia cutis congenita
definition: Congenital absence of skin. The commonest form presents as a defect limited to the scalp. It is also a component of a number of genetic syndromes.
synonyms: ACC | congenital absence of skin | congenital scars | cutis aplasia

name: Bathing suit ichthyosis
definition: Bathing suit ichthyosis (BSI) is a rare variant of autosomal recessive congenital ichthyosis (ARCI; ) characterised by the presence of large dark scales in specific areas of the body.
synonyms: ARCI1 | bathing suit ichthyosis

name: Jellyfish, Scyphozoa sting
definition: Envenomation by one of the many species of true jellyfish (Scyphozoa), normally resulting in localised pain, erythema and swelling without systemic manifestations.
synonyms: Scyphozoa envenomation | Sea nettle sting

name: Poikiloderma of Civatte
definition: Pigmentation, telangiectasia and atrophy due to repeated contact with photoactive fragrances. This is most commonly seen on the sides of the neck in women as a result of perfume use.

name: Discoid lupus erythematosus of eyelid
synonyms: discoid lupus eyelid | erythematosus lupus eyelid

name: Uncombable hair syndrome
definition: Uncombable hair syndrome (UHS), or pili trianguli et canaliculi, is a rare scalp hair shaft dysplasia.
synonyms: Cheveux incoiffables | Pili trianguli et canaliculi | Spun-glass hair | pili trianguli et canaliculi | uncomable hair

name: Latent early syphilis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum pallidum. This disease is characterised by serologic proof of infection without symptoms of disease less than 1 year after secondary syphilis. Transmission is commonly by sexual contact.
synonyms: latent syphilis, positive serology, early or less than 2 years after infection

name: Infantile perianal streptococcal cellulitis
definition: Infantile perianal streptococcal cellulitis is due to infection of the perianal skin of infants by Group A beta-haemolytic streptococci. Infants and young children appear to be more susceptible to this than older age groups. It manifests as sharply demarcated intense erythema with variable oedema and oozing of perianal skin. The penis or vulva may sometimes be involved as well. It is often painful and may impede normal defaecation. It is an important cause of a rash in the napkin area of infants
synonyms: Infantile perianal streptococcal dermatitis

name: Dermatoses provoked by light or UV radiation
cn_name: 光或紫外线辐射诱发的皮肤病

name: Disorders of vitamins or their metabolism which may affect the skin
definition: This group includes skin conditions resulting from deficiency or abnormal metabolic processing of vitamins such as folic acid and vitamin C

name: Rash generalised
definition: A very non-specific term to denote a widespread acquired visible alteration of the skin from normal in situations where a more precise description or diagnosis cannot be made. If a diagnosis cannot be made then a choice from the classes Acute skin eruption of uncertain or unspecified nature and Chronic skin disorder of uncertain or unspecified nature is preferred.

name: Sequelae of unspecified frostbite of lower limb
Late syphilitic female pelvic inflammatory disease
definition: This is a late sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is involving inflammation of the uterus, fallopian tubes, and/or ovaries as it progresses to scar formation with adhesions to nearby tissues and organs.

name: Ecthyma
definition: Ecthyma is a superficial ulcerative bacterial pyoderma. It is characterised by small, purulent, shallow, punched-out ulcers with thick, brown-black crusts and surrounding erythema. The commonest form is caused by beta-haemolytic streptococci, often in association with Staphylococcus aureus. It is associated with poor hygiene and malnutrition. Ecthyma gangrenosum is an uncommon severe variant caused by Pseudomonas aeruginosa.

name: Paravaccinia
definition: This is a viral infection by Pseudocowpox virus, a Parapoxvirus in the Poxvirus family. It is also known as \"milker's nodule\". It has a benign course and is caused by contact with infected cattle. It manifests as a nodule, usually affecting a finger and closely resembling orf.
synonyms: Pseudocowpox | milker's nodule | milkers' node | bovine papular stomatitis | pseudocowpox

name: Corrosion of unspecified degree of ankle or foot
Subacute cutaneous lupus erythematosus
definition: Subacute cutaneous lupus erythematosus is a non-scarring form of lupus erythematosus typified by the presence of circulating anti-Ro/SSA antibodies and discoid or annular inflamed red patches with variable fine scaling on sun-exposed skin, especially the sides of the face and neck, the vee of the neck, the extensor surfaces of the upper arms and the upper back\: in contrast with systemic lupus erythematosus the cheeks tend to be spared. Visceral disease is less frequent than in systemic lupus er
synonyms: SACLE - [Subacute cutaneous lupus erythematosus] | SCLE - [Subacute cutaneous lupus erythematosus] | SCLE
cn_name: 亚急性皮肤型红斑狼疮
snomed: 200943009 "subacute cutaneous lupus erythematosus";239891002 "subacute cutaneous lupus erythematosus"

name: Fournier gangrene of the scrotum or perineum
Malignant neoplasm metastasis in skin of calf
Acute desquamating skin eruption
definition: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and in which desquamation (shedding of skin scales) is a prominent feature. This is seen characteristically in the later stages of many acute viral exanthemata but may also be seen in drug reactions and in unstable and erythrodermic psoriasis.

name: BCG-induced tuberculid
definition: Tuberculid secondary to BCG immunization \[Dermatology TAG\]

name: Herpes simplex labialis
synonyms: HSV - [herpes simplex virus] labialis | cold sore | cold sores | fever blister | fever blisters | herpes simplex infection of lip | herpetic fever | herpes febrilis | herpes labialis

name: Ergotism
definition: Vasoconstriction of the extremities induced by ergot alkaloids derived either from mouldy cereals (Claviceps purpurea) or from ergotamine and related drugs. Signs include pallor and skin peeling of the fingers and toes, weak peripheral pulses, loss of peripheral sensation, oedema and ultimately dry gangrene. Convulsions, diarrhoea and psychological disturbances may also occur.

name: Secondary syphilitic female pelvic inflammatory disease
Irritant contact dermatitis from specified external agents
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis from external agents grouped according to the type of causative agent.
synonyms: Irritant contact eczema from external agents organised by irritant class
cn_name: 特指的外部物质引起的刺激性接触性皮炎

name: Pressure ulceration grade 2 of spinous processes
synonyms: stage 2 decubitus ulcer of spinous processes

name: Miscellaneous pyogenic bacterial infections of the skin
definition: A heterogeneous group of skin infections due to pyogenic bacteria.

name: Hypoglossia
synonyms: congenital hypoplasia of tongue | congenitally hypoplastic tongue | microglossia

name: Basal epidermolysis bullosa simplex
definition: Forms of epidermolysis bullosa in which the abnormality responsible for blistering involves the basal epidermis. Keratins 5 and 14 are two proteins, absence or abnormalities of which are linked to basal epidermolysis.
synonyms: Basal EBS - [basal epidermolysis bullosa simplex] | basal EBS

name: Blepharochalasis lower eyelid
definition: This is an inflammation of the eyelid that is characterised by exacerbations and remissions of eyelid oedema, which results in a stretching and subsequent atrophy of the eyelid tissue resulting in redundant folds over the lid margins. This diagnosis is of the lower eyelid.

name: Glutaric aciduria type 1
definition: Glutaric acidemia type 1 is an inborn error of lysine and triptophan metabolism due to glutaryl-Coenzyme A (CoA) dehydrogenase deficiency clinically characterised by a acute encephalopathy that results in bilateral striatal damage leading to a severe dystonic dyskinetic movement disorder.
synonyms: Glutaric acidaemia type 1 | Glutaryl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency

name: Burn from exposure to artificial source of ultraviolet radiation
cn_name: 人工紫外线照射后烧伤

name: Primary syphilis of fingers
synonyms: primary syphilitic fingers

name: Eruptive xanthoma
definition: Eruptive xanthomata manifest as crops of small yellow papules which erupt in large numbers over extensor surfaces, particularly the buttocks, back, legs and arms. They are associated with severe hypertriglyceridaemia and may be associated with uncontrolled diabetes mellitus.

name: Paediatric onset Sjögren syndrome
cn_name: 儿童干燥综合征；儿童舍格伦综合征

name: Reactive dermatoses due to distant bacterial infection
Burn of upper limb
synonyms: corrosion of arm | upper extremity burn NOS

name: Neonatal fungal infections involving the skin
cn_name: 新生儿真菌感染性皮肤病

name: Palmoplantar pustulosis
definition: Palmoplantar pustulosis (PPP) is a chronic inflammatory skin condition characterised by crops of sterile pustules on the palms and soles which erupt repeatedly over months or years. The affected areas tend to become red and scaly; cracks may form and these are often painful. It is strongly associated with smoking. It is associated with psoriasis elsewhere on the body in up to 24% of patients though appears to have a genetic profile distinct from psoriasis vulgaris. Interleukin-36 receptor gene p
synonyms: PPP - [palmoplantar pustulosis] | Palmoplantar pustular psoriasis | Pustular bacterid | Pustular psoriasis of the palms and soles | Pustulosis palmaris et plantaris | palmoplantar pustulosis

name: Iatrogenic neonatal dystrophic calcification
synonyms: Heel-prick calcinosis

name: Distal and lateral subungual onychomycosis
definition: The commonest pattern of onychomycosis and due in the majority of cases to dermatophyte infection. The lateral edges of the hyponychium provide a route of entry for the fungus which then spreads proximally under the nail plate, particularly adjacent to one or both lateral nail folds. The process often results in distal onycholysis.

name: Cutaneous mucormycosis
definition: Infection of the skin and subcutaneous tissues with fungi of the order Mucorales. Cutaneous mucoromycosis may be due to primary inoculation or to haematogenous spread from disseminated disease. Primary infection tends to occur in those with burns or extensive skin trauma (e.g. crash victims) and produces an acute inflammatory response with pus, abscess formation, tissue swelling and necrosis. It may progress to necrotizing fasciitis and/or to disseminated disease. Secondary infection from dissem
synonyms: Subcutaneous mucoromycosis

name: Pili torti - onychodysplasia
definition: Pili torti-onychodysplasia is a form of ectodermal dysplasia characterised by dystrophy of the distal part of the nails and trichodysplasia.

name: Postnatal iatrogenic skin injury
definition: Injuries resulting from perinatal and postnatal medical procedures
cn_name: 出生后医源性皮肤损伤

name: Foreign body reactions to hair
definition: Hair is biologically inert and when trapped under the skin surface can provoke a foreign body reaction. This is most commonly seen in the beard area (pseudofolliculitis barbae) and sacral area (pilonidal sinus).

name: Photo-allergic contact dermatitis due to nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs
definition: Photo-allergic contact dermatitis due to non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs such as ketoprofen and dexketoprofen.
synonyms: Photo-allergic contact dermatitis due to NSAIDs

name: Lymphoedema with atrial septal defects and facial changes
definition: lymphoedema - atrial septal defects - facial changes syndrome is characterised by congenital lymphoedema of the lower limbs, atrial septal defect and a characteristic facies (a round face with a prominent forehead, a flat nasal bridge with a broad nasal tip, epicanthal folds, a thin upper lip and a cleft chin).

name: Cutaneous schwannoma
definition: A benign, usually encapsulated slow growing tumour of the peripheral nervous system composed of well differentiated Schwann cells. It recurs infrequently and only rare cases associated with malignant transformation have been reported.
synonyms: Cutaneous neurilemmoma | Cutaneous neurolemmoma

name: Corrosion of second degree of ankle or foot
Palmoplantar keratodermas
definition: A range of genetic and acquired disorders in which there is thickening of the epidermal keratin of the skin of the palmar surfaces of the hands and plantar surfaces of the feet.
synonyms: Hyperkeratosis of palms and soles | Palmoplantar hyperkeratosis

name: Melanoma of skin
definition: A primary melanoma arising from atypical melanocytes in the skin. Precursor lesions include acquired and congenital melanocytic nevi, and dysplastic nevi. Several histologic variants have been recognised, including superficial spreading melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, nodular melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma.
synonyms: MM - [malignant melanoma] | Malignant melanoma of skin | cutaneous malignant melanoma | cutaneous melanoma | malignant mole, meaning melanoma of unspecified site | melanoma NOS | melanoma of unspecified site | Cutaneous Malignant Melanoma | malignant ear melanoma | malignant lip melanoma

name: Glanders
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Burkholderia mallei. This disease presents with symptoms depending on the route of infection. Transmission is by contact with tissues or body fluids from infected animals (typically horses), or inhalation of infected aerosol. Confirmation is by identification of Burkholderia mallei in blood, sputum, urine, or skin samples.
synonyms: Infection due to Burkholderia mallei | Infection due to Pseudomonas mallei | actinobacillosis | actinobacillus infection | equina | equine glanders | farce | farcy | farcy buds | farcy cords

name: Gouty panniculitis
definition: A rare complication of gout manifesting typically as painful ulcerating nodules on the lower legs.

name: Panniculitis, unspecified, hip joint
synonyms: panniculitis of undetermined or unspecified etiology, hip joint

name: Non-HFE-related hereditary haemochromatosis, Type 4
definition: Hemochromatosis type 4 (also called ferroportin disease) is a rare form of hereditary hemochromatosis, a group of diseases characterised by excessive tissue iron deposition of genetic origin. It is phenotypically heterogeneous with two sub-types. Ferroportin disease form A is the usual form and is generally asymptomatic with no tissue damage and further complications. Ferroportin disease form B is rarer and resembles hemochromatosis type 1, but can affect children.
synonyms: Autosomal dominant hereditary haemochromatosis | Ferroportin disease | Haemochromatosis due to defect in ferroportin

name: Keloid
definition: A keloid is a progressively enlarging scar resulting from formation of excessive amounts of collagen in the dermis during connective tissue repair following inflammation, injury or surgery. It differs from a hypertrophic scar in that a keloid expands beyond the boundaries of the initial wound or site of inflammation.
synonyms: Cheloid | keloid scar | non-surgical keloid

name: Keratosis of skin of uncertain or unspecified nature
synonyms: Keratosis of skin
cn_name: 性质未确定或未特指的皮肤角化病

name: Sea-urchin injury
definition: Penetrating skin injury by the spines and pedicellariae of by sea-urchins, starfish and sea cucumbers, causing acute intense localised burning pain. Some species inject venom from their pedicellariae, producing oedema, blistering and occasionally skin necrosis\: injected venom may rarely cause systemic toxicity.

name: Chronic papillomatous dermatitis due to incontinence
definition: Papillomatous skin reaction seen in chronically incontinent individuals.

name: Cradle cap
definition: Cradle cap is a form of seborrhoeic dermatitis that manifests as yellowish, crusty, greasy patches of scaling on the scalp of infants between the second week and sixth month of life. The forehead and eyebrows are frequently affected. It is usually asymptomatic. It may be associated with infantile seborrhoeic dermatitis of other areas including the trunk and napkin area.
synonyms: Infantile seborrhoeic dermatitis of the scalp | Complement 5 dysfunction | cradle cap | Generalized seborrheic dermatitis of infants | Infantile seborrheic dermatitis | Infantile seborrhoeic dermatitis | Pityriasis capitis | seborrhea capitis | Seborrhea sicca | Seborrhoea capitis

name: Osteomyelitis due to Klebsiella granuloma inguinale
Renal varices
synonyms: renal papilla varices | renal papilla varix | renal papilla varix rupture

name: Disseminated intravascular coagulation
definition: A disorder that is characterised by the systemic intravascular activation of the coagulation system, simultaneously leading to intravascular thrombi, compromising an adequate blood supply to the organs, and to bleeding as the consequence of consumption of platelets and coagulation factors. It may be provoked by a wide range of disorders including infections, inflammatory disorders and malignancy.
synonyms: Consumption coagulopathy | DIC - [disseminated intravascular coagulation] | Defibrination syndrome | Diffuse intravascular coagulation | Fibrinolytic purpura | disseminated intravascular coagulopathy | fibrinolysis NOS | thrombolytic purpura

name: Acneiform reactions to halogenated aromatic hydrocarbons
definition: Acne caused by exposure to halogenated hydrocarbons such as chlorinated naphthalene, dioxins and dibenzofurans. Numerous comedones and noninflammatory cysts are a common feature. The course is often chronic. Frequently affected body parts are the face, neck, axillae and groin area.
synonyms: Chloracne

name: Miscellaneous complications of cutaneous surgical procedures
definition: Complications of cutaneous surgical procedures such as sensory disturbance and anatomical distortion

name: Infantile onset panniculitis with uveitis and systemic granulomatosis
Cutaneous sarcoma
definition: A group of generally rare malignant neoplasms arising from mesenchymal elements in the dermis including fibroblasts, pilar smooth muscle and vascular endothelium.
synonyms: Cutaneous sarcoma

name: Red-beard sponge dermatitis
synonyms: Red-beard sponge dermatitis (Micronia prolifera)

name: LEOPARD syndrome
definition: LEOPARD syndrome is a rare multiple congenital anomalies condition, mainly characterised by skin, facial and cardiac anomalies. LEOPARD is an acronym for the major features of this disorder, including multiple Lentigines, ECG conduction abnormalities, Ocular hypertelorism, Pulmonic stenosis, Abnormal genitalia, Retardation of growth, and sensorineural Deafness.
synonyms: Noonan with multiple lentigines syndrome

name: Analphalipoproteinaemia
definition: Tangier disease is a recessively inherited hypoalphalipopproteinaemia characterised by cholesterol deposition in the cornea, tonsils, rectum, liver and spleen, and sometimes associated with neurologic symptoms.
synonyms: Tangier disease | cholesterol thesaurismosis

name: Oesophageal varices with bleeding in liver disorders
Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to food flavours or additives
Actinic elastosis
synonyms: Elastosis senilis | Rhytides | Solar elastosis | Wrinkles secondary to chronic sun damage

name: Progressive isolated segmental anhidrosis
definition: This is a condition characterised by asymmetric, progressive, segmental sweat loss
synonyms: Harlequin syndrome

name: Misshapen ear
definition: A condition caused by the malformation of the ear during the antenatal period.

name: Nodular basal cell carcinoma of skin
definition: This is the most common form of basal cell carcinoma and is typically located on the head or neck. It starts as a small translucent nodule which will frequently necrose and ulcerate as it enlarges. Telangiectatic blood vessels can often be detected just under the tumour surface. A minority may be pigmented and give rise to difficulty in distinguishing them from melanoma.
synonyms: Circumscribed solid basal cell carcinoma of skin
cn_name: 皮肤结节状基底细胞癌

name: Occupationally-acquired skin infection, classified elsewhere
definition: Skin infection acquired as a result of occupation or work. At above average risk are health care workers, veterinarians, farmers and medical laboratory technicians.

name: Late congenital syphilis
definition: Congenital syphilis presenting after two years of age and often not until many years later. Saddle nose and peg-shaped (Hutchinson's) teeth are pathognomonic. As in late acquired syphilis, gummata, periostitis, optic atrophy, deafness, paralysis and dementia may develop when treatment is delayed.

name: Pyogenic infection of skin
definition: Purulent infection of the skin due to unknown or unspecified bacteria.
synonyms: pyogenic infection of skin and subcutis | suppurative skin lesion

name: Cholinergic urticaria
definition: Cholinergic urticaria presents as an eruption of multiple small 2-3 mm monomorphic papular weals in response to sweat-inducing stimuli such as physical exertion, hot baths, spicy foods or sudden emotional stress. The weals tend to be pink in milder cases but white with surrounding macular erythema when the oedema is more intense. Angioedema and systemic manifestations including faintness, headache, palpitations and wheezing may occur in severe cases.

name: Mixed lesions of pinta
synonyms: Achromic and hyperchromic skin lesions of carate | Achromic and hyperchromic skin lesions of pinta

name: Miscellaneous specified, Class IIb non-Langerhans cell histiocytoses involving the skin
definition: A group of rare non-Langerhans cell histiocytic disorders which characteristically present in the skin
synonyms: Class IIb histiocytoses | Non-Langerhans cell histiocytoses of other or unknown cell lineage

name: Papular palmoplantar keratodermas
definition: Palmoplantar keratoderma characterised by the presence of multiple small discrete hyperkeratotic papules involving palmar and plantar skin.

name: Paraneoplastic dermatomyositis
definition: Dermatomyositis (DM) associated with an underlying solid malignancy has been reported in up to 40% of patients in reported case series. Malignancy is much commoner in patients with DM presenting after the age of 40. The range of associated cancers is wide and largely reflects the prevalence of different cancers in the populations studied. Cancer-associated DM has been strongly associated with anti-TIF1-gamma autoantibodies which are directed against transcription intermediary factor 1-gamma.

name: Suspected deep pressure-induced tissue damage, depth unknown
definition: An area of soft tissue damage due to pressure or shear which is anticipated to evolve into a deep pressure ulcer but has not yet done so. The affected skin is typically discoloured purple or maroon and may display haemorrhagic blistering. It may be painful and oedematous. It can be either warmer or cooler than adjacent tissue. Evolution into a deep ulcer may be rapid even with optimal treatment.
cn_name: 怀疑是深层组织致压损伤，深度未可知

name: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex
definition: Epidermolysis bullosa simplex is the name given to a heterogeneous group of genetically-determined defects in epidermal cell-cell adhesion. These give rise to blistering in response to frictional and shearing stresses.
synonyms: Epidermolytic epidermolysis bullosa | EBS
cn_name: 单纯性大疱性表皮松解症

name: Measles without complication
definition: A disease caused by an infection with Morbillivirus. This disease is characterised by fever, cough, coryza, conjunctivitis, enanthema, or maculopapular rash, without any additional secondary pathological conditions. Transmission is by inhalation of infected respiratory secretions, or direct contact. Confirmation is by detection of Morbillivirus RNA, or detection measles-specific IgM antibodies.
synonyms: Koplik spots | Rubeola | measles NOS | rubeola

name: Miliaria
definition: Miliaria is a common skin disorder resulting from occlusion of eccrine sweat ducts. It is precipitated by hot, humid conditions.
synonyms: Sweat retention syndrome | eccrine miliaria | heat rash | sweat rash

name: Hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia - hypothyroidism - ciliary dyskinesia
definition: Hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia - hypothyroidism - ciliary dyskinesia is characterised by alopecia, nail dystrophy, ophthalmic complications, thyroid dysfunction (primary hypothyroidism), hypohidrosis, ephelides, enteropathy, and respiratory tract infections due to ciliary dyskinesia, leading to suggestion of the acronym ANOTHER syndrome as alternative name for this condition. Transmission is autosomal recessive.
synonyms: ANOTHER syndrome

name: Lichen planopilaris
definition: An inflammatory form of follicular lichen planus which may affect any hairy part of the body. It most commonly affects the scalp but, in the Graham-Little syndrome, it also affects the trunk, axillae and pubic areas with the widespread development of horny keratotic follicular spines. Scalp, axillary and pubic hair is lost.
synonyms: follicular lichen planus | lichen planus acuminatus | LPP

name: Autosomal dominant dyskeratosis congenita
synonyms: AD-DKC - [Autosomal dominant dyskeratosis congenita]

name: Infection by Dipetalonema
definition: A filariasis that is a zoonotic infection caused by the nematode of the genus Dipetalonema, which is transmitted accidentally to humans from porcupines, beavers and other mammals by mosquitoes. The adult worms live subcutaneously or in body cavities or in the eyes of humans. Dead worms cause hypersensitivity necrosis with eosinophils, followed by granulomatous reaction and fibrosis.
synonyms: dipetalonema | dipetalonemiasis | dipetalonemosis | dipetalonema infectious disease | Infection by Dipetalonema | Infection by Dipetalonema perstans

name: Sequelae of frostbite with tissue necrosis of abdominal wall, lower back and pelvis
Alopecia – psychomotor epilepsy – periodontal pyorrhoea – intellectual disability syndrome
definition: Alopecia - epilepsy - pyorrhoea - intellectual deficit syndrome is characterised by the association of complete alopecia, mild intellectual deficit, psychomotor epilepsy and pyorrhoea (parodontitis).
synonyms: Shokeir syndrome

name: Flexural or intertriginous candidosis
definition: Candidosis of flexural and intertriginous skin, where the warm, moist conditions favour the growth of Candida yeasts.
synonyms: Candidal intertrigo | candida infection of flexural skin | candidiasis intertrigo | candidiasis of finger web | candidosis of finger web | erosio interdigitalis blastomycetica | flexural candidosis | interdigital moniliasis

name: Corn
synonyms: Clavus | Heloma | clavus | heloma
