Naevoid melanoma, primary
definition: Malignant melanoma variant which may be difficult to distinguish both clinically and histologically from benign melanocytic naevi
synonyms: Naevoid malignant melanoma | Small cell melanoma | Spitzoid malignant melanoma

name: Cystic hygroma in fetus
definition: Development abnormalities of the lymphoid system that occur at sites of lymphatic-venous connection, most commonly in the posterior neck but may be anterior and may extend into chest. Frequently associated with karyotypic abnormalities, various malformation syndromes, and several teratogenic agents. When diagnosed prenatally, the overall prognosis is poor. Cystic hygroma diagnosed after birth is usually associated with a good prognosis.
synonyms: cystic hygroma in born fetus | hygroma colli cysticum

name: Glomerular disorders in thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura
definition: This refers to a disorder of the tubular structure called the nephron filters blood to form urine, in a rare disorder of the blood-coagulation system, causing extensive microscopic clots to form in the small blood vessels throughout the body.

name: Progeria
definition: Hutchinson-Gilford progeria syndrome is an extremely rare disorder characterised by premature aging of postnatal onset. The main clinical and radiological features include alopecia, thin skin, hypoplasia of nails, loss of subcutaneous fat, stiffness of joints and osteolysis, and normal intelligence.
synonyms: Gilford-Hutchinson syndrome | Hutchinson-Gilford syndrome | HGPS | hutchison-gilford syndrome | progeria

name: Purpura or bruising due to friction or trauma
definition: Purpura resulting from such diverse causes as bites, friction from clothing (e.g. shoulder-straps) or self-inflicted trauma.

name: Pigmentary disturbances of oral mucosa
definition: This is a disturbance in the material that changes the colour of reflected or transmitted light as the result of wavelength-selective absorption, of the oral mucosa.

name: Arthrogryposis - renal dysfunction - cholestasis
definition: Arthrogryposis - renal dysfunction - cholestasis (ARC) syndrome is a multisystem disorder characterised by neurogenic arthrogryposis multiplex congenita, renal tubular dysfunction and neonatal cholestasis with low serum gamma-glutamyl transferase activity.
synonyms: ARC - [Arthrogryposis - renal dysfunction - cholestasis] syndrome | Familial arthrogryposis - cholestatic hepatorenal syndrome

name: Dystrophic calcification of the skin due to inflammatory disease
definition: Dystrophic calcification may be seen in a number of inflammatory diseases but is particularly associated with dermatomyositis and systemic sclerosis.
synonyms: Calcinosis cutis secondary to inflammatory disease | Dystrophic calcification of the skin secondary to inflammatory disease

name: Posterior uveitis due to Vogt Koyanagi Harada syndrome
definition: This refers to inflammation in the back of the eye. This diagnosis is with an uncommon multisystem disease of presumed autoimmune etiology that is characterised by chronic, bilateral, diffuse, granulomatous uveitis with accompanying dermatologic, neurologic, and auditory involvement.

name: Keratoacanthoma
definition: Keratoacanthoma is a relatively common keratinocytic epidermal tumour which shows resemblances to squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, from which it may be difficult to distinguish either clinically or histopathologically. It is characterised by rapid growth over a few weeks to months, followed by spontaneous resolution over 4-6 months. Because it is not possible to predict its benign behaviour with complete certainty during its initial growth phase, the designation \"Well-differentiated squamou
synonyms: KA - [keratoacanthoma] | Well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma (keratoacanthoma type) | KA

name: Primary hypercholesterolaemia
definition: This is a genetic disorder characterised by high cholesterol levels, specifically very high levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL, \"bad cholesterol\"), in the blood and early cardiovascular disease.
synonyms: LDL - [low density lipoprotein} hyperlipoproteinemia | cholesterolaemia | essential cholesterolaemia | essential hypercholesterolaemia | group A hyperlipemia | group A hyperlipidaemia | increased low density lipoprotein | low-density-lipoid-type hyperlipoproteinemia | low-density-lipoprotein-type | low-density-lipoprotein-type hyperlipoproteinemia

name: Suspected deep pressure-induced tissue damage, depth unknown of ischium
Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome type 8
synonyms: HPS8 - [Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome type 8] (MIM 614077)

name: Frostbite with tissue necrosis of hip or thigh
Norum disease
definition: This is the disorder of lipoprotein metabolism. Lecithin cholesterol acyltransferase catalyzes the formation of cholesterol esters in lipoproteins.

name: Xeroderma pigmentosum E
definition: Xeroderma pigmentosum complementation group E (XPE) is an extremely rare subtype of xeroderma pigmentosum (XP), a rare photodermatosis predisposing to skin cancers. XPE patients present with mild XP symptoms and no neurological abnormalities.
synonyms: XPE - [xeroderma pigmentosum E]

name: Phototherapy burn
definition: Burn resulting from UVB phototherapy

name: Actinomycetoma due to Nocardia species
definition: Actinomycetoma due to infection with Nocardia bacteria. This organism is the commonest causative agent of actinomycetoma in the Western hemisphere.

name: Seborrhoeic otitis externa
definition: Seborrhoeic dermatitis affecting the skin of the external ear. It is usually accompanied by evidence of seborrhoeic dermatitis at other sites. In mild cases it may be asymptomatic but it can present acutely with severe pain, oedema and exudation. Longstanding cases may be complicated by chronic lymphoedema and occlusion of the external auditory canal.
synonyms: Seborrhoeic dermatitis of external ear

name: Burn of temple region
synonyms: temple burn | temporal burn

name: Secondary syphilis of other sites
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the gram-negative bacteria Treponema pallidum pallidum. This disease is characterised by less common symptoms of syphilis, including hepatitis, kidney disease, arthritis, periostitis, optic neuritis, uveitis, or interstitial keratitis. Transmission is commonly by sexual contact.

name: Disorders of the nail or perionychium
definition: Disorders affecting the nail and surrounding tissues
synonyms: Nail disorders
cn_name: 指（趾）甲或甲周组织的障碍

name: Punctate palmoplantar keratoderma
definition: A palmoplantar keratosis characterized by keratoses with a raindrop pattern on the palmoplantar surface, skin lesions which may involve the whole of the palmoplantar surface, or may be more restricted in their distribution.
synonyms: Buschke-Fischer-Brauer keratoderma | Keratoderma punctata palmaris et plantaris | keratosis punctata | punctate keratosis palmoplantaris | punctate palmoplantar hyperkeratosis

name: Ectodermal dysplasia - intellectual deficit - central nervous system malformation
Venous aneurysm
synonyms: aneurysm of vein | vein aneurysm

name: Perianal seborrhoeic dermatitis
definition: Seborrhoeic dermatitis of perianal skin. This may be associated with seborrhoeic dermatitis at other sites. The erythema tends to be less well defined than psoriasis in this area. It is frequently pruritic and may be exudative.

name: Localised hyperhidrosis due to focal neurological disorder
definition: Excessive sweating may be due to neurological lesions involving any part of the sympathetic pathway from the brain to the nerve ending. It may be the presenting symptom but it is quite exceptional for this to occur as an isolated phenomenon in the absence of other neurological symptoms or signs. Such lesions may be within the central nervous system (cortex, basal ganglia or spinal cord), the sympathetic pathway and ganglia, or in the peripheral nerves.

name: Onycholysis
synonyms: Detachment of nail | Separation of nail plate

name: Circumscribed café-au-lait hypermelanosis
synonyms: Café-au-lait macule

name: Erythromelalgia
definition: An uncommon condition which presents as episodic bouts of severe pain, heat and erythema involving the extremities. Exercise and increase in temperature are precipitating factors. The majority of cases are sporadic and associated with a small-fibre neuropathy involving sensory and sympathetic nerves. It presents as episodic bouts of intense burning pain, heat and erythema involving the extremities lasting from a few minutes to several hours. Exercise and increase in temperature are precipitating
synonyms: Erythermalgia | EM | erythralgia | erythromalgia

name: Disorders involving the apocrine follicular unit
definition: A group of disorders involving apocrine glands and their associated follicular units. The most important of these is hidradenitis suppurativa.
cn_name: 累及顶泌汗腺毛囊单位的疾病

name: Syphilitic Charcot arthropathy
definition: A form of neuropathic arthropathy which arises as a complication of late syphilitic polyneuropathy. Joint destruction, most commonly involving the lower extremities, results from damage to sensory nerves.

name: Oculocutaneous albinism type 2
definition: OCA-2 is caused by mutations in the OCA2 (P) gene. Affected individuals have pigmented hair at birth and may develop iris pigmentation, pigmented melanocytic naevi and freckles later in life.
synonyms: OCA2 - [Oculocutaneous albinism type 2]

name: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton mentagrophytes
definition: Dermatophytosis due to Trichophyton mentagrophytes, the main natural hosts of which are rodents and hedgehogs.

name: Acute phototoxic reaction to fragrance
definition: Acute phototoxic reaction caused by skin contact with fragrances.
synonyms: Photocontact dermatitis due to fragrance

name: Dermatoses resulting from distant or systemic fungal infection
Occupational allergic contact dermatitis due to nickel
Ulceration of vulva due to Behçet disease
definition: Vulval ulceration due to Behçet disease.

name: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa, generalised intermediate type
definition: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa, generalised intermediate type (JEB-gen intermed) is an autosomal recessive blistering disorder characterised by generalised trauma-induced epidermal separation at the level of the lamina lucida from birth.
synonyms: GABEB - [generalised atrophic benign epidermolysis bullosa] | JEB - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa] non-Herlitz, generalised | JEB-gen intermed - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa] | JEB-nH gen - [junctional epidermolysis bullosa, non-Herlitz, generalised] | generalised atrophic benign epidermolysis bullosa

name: Pressure ulceration, ungradable of lower limb
Sequelae of frostbite of multiple body regions
Common acquired melanocytic naevus
synonyms: common acquired melanocytic naevus NOS
cn_name: 普通获得性黑素细胞痣

name: Alopecia areata of the nails
definition: Fine nail pitting in association with alopecia areata.

name: Purpura or bruising due to disorders of coagulation
definition: Purpura resulting from genetically-determined or acquired deficiencies or dysfunction of clotting factors.
synonyms: Purpura and bruising secondary to disorders of coagulation
cn_name: 胶原疾病所致的紫癜或瘀血

name: Eosinophilic granuloma
synonyms: unifocal Langerhans-cell histiocytosis

name: Dystrophic calcification of the skin due to localised skin injury
definition: localised cutaneous calcification secondary to traumatic or other tissue injury, localised inflammation or tissue necrosis.
synonyms: Calcinosis cutis secondary to localised skin injury | Dystrophic calcification of the skin secondary to localised skin injury

name: Pressure ulceration grade 3 of lower limb
synonyms: stage 3 decubitus ulcer of other sites of lower limb

name: Suspected deep pressure-induced tissue damage, depth unknown of lower back
Ventral pterygium of nail
definition: An abnormality of the nail in which there is a forward extension of the hyponychium anchoring it to the underlying nail bed and obliterating the distal nail groove.
synonyms: Pterygium inversum unguis

name: Hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type IIC
definition: HSAN Type IIC is an autosomal recessive disorder due to mutations in the KIF1A gene. It presents in childhood with severe loss of pain, temperature, vibration and position sensation, ulcero-mutilation, distal muscle weakness, developmental delay and short stature.
synonyms: HSAN2C - [Hereditary sensory autonomic neuropathy type 2C]

name: Gummatous syphilis
synonyms: Syphilitic gumma

name: Vaginal candidosis
synonyms: Candida vaginitis | Vaginal thrush

name: Acute cutaneous lupus erythematosus
definition: Acute cutaneous lupus erythematosus is closely associated with systemic lupus erythematosus and double-stranded DNA antibodies. Its hallmark is the “butterfly” rash across the face occurring in half of patients with systemic lupus. There may however be widespread involvement of the skin with patchy erythema, oedema and, in fulminant cases, blistering and epidermal necrosis. Photosensitivity is prominent and exposure to ultraviolet radiation may trigger flares not only in the skin but also in int
synonyms: Systemic lupus erythematosus skin rash | ACLE

name: Complete trisomy 18, meiotic non-disjunction
synonyms: trisomy 18 syndrome, meiotic nondisjunction | trisomy 18, meiotic nondisjunction

name: Lichen spinulosus
definition: A condition in which crops of grouped flesh-coloured keratotic follicular papules typically arise spontaneously on the skin of the trunk and upper arms in children and adolescents. The cause is unknown.
synonyms: Keratosis spinulosa | keratosis spinulosa

name: Cold panniculitis
definition: Cold panniculitis is a distinctive form of injury to subcutaneous fat provoked directly by cold exposure. Infants are particularly predisposed to this as a result of an increased ratio of saturated to unsaturated fat at birth with a consequent elevation of the freezing point of fat (cold panniculitis of the newborn). It may also be provoked by prolonged exposure to low environmental temperatures. Localised forms include so-called \"Popsicle panniculitis\" involving the cheeks of young children a
synonyms: haxthausen's disease

name: Human papillomavirus infection of skin or mucous membrane
definition: Infection of the skin and mucous membranes by the human papillomavirus (HPV), the agent responsible for viral warts in humans. Clinical manifestations depend on the virus subtype and the anatomical site involved.
cn_name: 皮肤或黏膜的人乳头瘤病毒感染

name: Factitious skin disorder imposed on another
definition: The inflicting of skin injury or factitious skin disorder on others (usually a child) for personal gain, such as obtaining sympathy or attention from others. The perpetrator is most commonly a mother and the victim her child.
synonyms: Skin disorder resulting from Munchhausen syndrome by proxy

name: Hidradenitis suppurativa of axillae
definition: Hidradenitis suppurativa affecting the skin of the axillary vault. This is a classical site of involvement. It is also more amenable to surgical treatment than other areas such as the anogenital region.
synonyms: Acne inversa of axillae

name: Hirsutism associated with hyperandrogenaemia
definition: Excessive male-pattern facial and body hair in women, mostly caused by PCOS, by hyperandrogenemia combined with normal ovulation, or idiopathically, after exclusion of androgen-secreting neoplasm, congenital adrenal hyperplasia and HAIR-AN syndrome. The severity of hirsutism can be assessed by the Ferriman-Gallwey score.
synonyms: Hirsuties

name: Non-syndromic diffuse palmoplantar keratodermas
definition: Diffuse palmoplantar keratoderma not associated with abnormalities of other organs than skin

name: Corrosion of third degree of ankle or foot
Glossodynia
definition: This is a condition characterised by a burning sensation affecting the tongue which cannot be attributed to any alternative identifiable cause.
synonyms: Burning tongue syndrome | Glossalgia | Glossopyrosis | Painful tongue | Sore tongue

name: Allergic contact sensitisation to systemic medicaments
definition: Allergic contact sensitization to systemic medicaments (for more detailed information on allergens see Allergic contact dermatitis due to systemic medicaments).

name: Seborrhoeic dermatitis of the scalp
definition: Seborrhoeic dermatitis of the scalp is characterised by varying degrees of scaling, inflammation, exudation and crusting affecting the scalp. It may occur in isolation or may be accompanied by seborrhoeic dermatitis of other sites. It may be difficult to differentiate from scalp psoriasis. Pityriasis capitis (dandruff) is considered to represent a mild form of seborrhoeic dermatitis of the scalp.
synonyms: Seborrhoeic eczema of scalp

name: Pruritus of pregnancy
definition: Pruritus in pregnancy can usually be attributed to a specific cause such as cholestasis, a pregnancy-specific dermatosis such as pruritic urticarial papules and plaques of pregnancy, or to exacerbation of a preexisting inflammatory dermatosis such as atopic eczema. Not uncommonly, however, a specific cause cannot be identified.
synonyms: Pruritus gravidarum

name: Segmental heterochromia of hair
definition: The presence of hair of different colours in a segmental or Blaschkoid distribution, in many cases attributable to somatic mosaicism.

name: Glomuvenous malformation
definition: Glomuvenous malformations (GVMs) are hereditary vascular malformations characterised by the presence of small, multifocal bluish-purple venous lesions involving the skin.
synonyms: glomangioma | infiltrating glomus tumor | multiple glomus tumors

name: Haemosiderin pigmentation of skin
definition: Dermal haemosiderin deposition causes a yellowish-brown or bronze discolouration of the skin. The deposition may be focal as seen following repeated extravasation of red blood cells (e.g. in association with venous hypertension or chronic vasculitis) or from generalised iron overload (e.g. haemochromatosis). Haemosiderin may stimulate melanogenesis and thus the colour is due to variable proportions of haemosiderin and melanin.
synonyms: Iron pigmentation | iron pigmentation disorder

name: Sea-urchin granuloma
definition: Sea urchin granuloma is a chronic granulomatous skin disease caused by penetration of sea urchin spines which have not been removed from wounds after injury. The spines are composed of inorganic matter including calcium carbonate and silica which incite a foreign body reaction. The granulomata are typically small pink to blue papules and nodules which later turn brown and are located most commonly on the feet and ankles or on the hands.

name: Gastrocutaneous syndrome
definition: A rare, syndromic, hyperpigmentation of the skin characterized by multiple lentigines and café-au-lait spots associated with hiatal hernia and peptic ulcer, hypertelorism and myopia. There have been no further descriptions in the literature since 1982.

name: Drug-induced cheilitis
definition: Severe cheilitis may be caused by medication, in particular systemic retinoids, which interfere with the normal epithelial barrier to transepithelial water loss.
synonyms: medicament-induced cheilitis

name: Alopecia areata of eyelashes
definition: Loss of eyelashes (ciliary madarosis) due to alopecia areata

name: Ablepharon, unilateral
definition: This is the congenital absence of the eyelids. It is a component of the Ablepharon macrostomia syndrome. This diagnosis is occurring in one eye.

name: Sjögren-Larsson syndrome
definition: Sjögren-Larsson syndrome is a neurocutaneous disorder caused by an inborn error of lipid metabolism and characterised by congenital ichthyosis, intellectual deficit, and spasticity.
synonyms: Fatty acid alcohol oxidoreductase deficiency

name: Burns of external body surface, specified by site
synonyms: burns and corrosions of external body surface, specified by site | cutaneous burns

name: Acquired bilateral naevus of Ota-like macules
Deep bacterial folliculitis or pyogenic abscess of the skin
definition: Single or multiple focal infections of skin and soft tissues most commonly centred on the hair follicle and most commonly due to Staphylococcus aureus. Pyogenic abscesses may develop in other locations in skin which has been injured as a result of either trauma or surgery.
cn_name: 皮肤深层细菌性毛囊炎或脓性脓肿

name: Abscess of upper eyelid
definition: This is a collection of pus (neutrophils) that has accumulated within a tissue because of an inflammatory process in response to either an infectious process (usually caused by bacteria or parasites) or other foreign material, of the upper eyelid.

name: Nonoccupational allergic contact dermatitis due to industrial biocides, cutting oils or disinfectants
Congenital onychodysplasia of the index fingers
definition: Congenital onychodystrophy of the index fingers comprises\: unilateral or bilateral hypoplasia of the index fingernails; deformities of the nails on other fingers; radiographic abnormalities of the distal bony phalanx on the affected fingers; congenital occurrence, which can be either hereditary or sporadic.
synonyms: COIF - [Congenital onychodysplasia of the index fingers] syndrome | Kikuchi syndrome

name: Odontotrichomelic syndrome
definition: Odontotrichomelic syndrome is a malformation syndrome characterised by malformations of all four extremities, hypoplastic nails, ear anomalies, hypotrichosis, abnormal dentition, hyperhidrosis and nasolacrimal duct obstruction.

name: Cardiomyopathy associated with acromegaly
definition: This is the measurable deterioration of the function of the myocardium (the heart muscle) for any reason, usually leading to heart failure; common symptoms are dyspnoea (breathlessness) and peripheral oedema (swelling of the legs). This diagnosis is associated with a syndrome that results when the anterior pituitary gland produces excess growth hormone (GH) after epiphyseal plate closure at puberty.

name: Elastoderma
definition: An extremely rare, acquired, dermis elastic tissue disorder characterized by localized increased skin laxity associated with delayed skin recoil, typically ocurring on the elbows, knees and/or neck. Histologically, focal abundace of elastic tissue in the dermis with pleomorphic and fragmented elastic fibers, without calcification, is observed.

name: Symptomatic late neurosyphilis
definition: A diverse constellation of neuropsychiatric signs resulting from prolonged untreated or inadequately treated syphilis. The protean clinical manifestations include chronic, insidious meningeal inflammation with cranial nerve palsy, cognitive and/or behavioural impairment, ataxia, stroke, seizures and visual or auditory impairment.
synonyms: symptomatic neurosyphilis
cn_name: 有症状性晚期神经梅毒

name: Trachyonychia
synonyms: sandpapered nails | twenty-nail dystrophy

name: Burn of shoulder or arm, except wrist or hand, epidermal burn
synonyms: Burn of shoulder and arm, except wrist and hand, first degree
cn_name: 肩或臂（除外腕或手）表皮烧伤

name: Ulcerative disorders of the penis or scrotum
Acute generalised pustular psoriasis of von Zumbusch
definition: This form may either be preceded by plaque psoriasis or arise de novo, classically after withdrawal of systemic glucocorticosteroids. The appearance is of sheets of small, monomorphic pustules often involving the edges of expanding, intensely inflammatory plaques or developing within erythrodermic skin. Constitutional upset is a frequent concomitant finding.
synonyms: Von Zumbusch disease

name: Superficial incisional site infection
definition: A surgical site infection involving only skin and subcutaneous tissue of the incision.
synonyms: drainage site cellulitis

name: Ichthyosis - oral and digital anomalies
definition: Ichthyosis - oral and digital anomalies syndrome is characterised by ichthyosis, unusual facies (small mouth with a thin upper lip and lower lip with a midline groove) and digital anomalies (tapered fingers with a lack of distal flexion creases and wide spacing between the second and third fingers).
synonyms: Clayton Smith-Donnai syndrome

name: Drug-induced telangiectasia
definition: Telangiectasia associated with administration of systemic calcium channel blocking agents such as nifedipine.
synonyms: medicament-induced telangiectasia

name: Psoriatic onycholysis
definition: Psoriatic onycholysis denotes separation of the nail plate from the underlying nail bed and is common in psoriasis. The separation usually starts at the distal free edge and may be accompanied by erythema of the adjacent nail bed (\"salmon patches\"). In severe cases this may result in nail shedding.

name: Cutis verticis gyrata - intellectual deficit
definition: Cutis verticis gyrata - intellectual deficit syndrome is characterised by the association of cutis verticis gyrata and intellectual deficit.

name: Interstitial keratitis and keratoconjunctivitis in syphilis
synonyms: keratitis and keratoconjunctivitis in syphilis

name: Acute neonatal citrullinaemia type 1
definition: Acute neonatal citrullinemia type 1 is a severe form of citrullinemia type 1 characterised clinically by hyperammonemia, progressive lethargy, poor feeding and vomiting, seizures and possible loss of consciousness, within one to a few days of birth, with variable signs of increased intracranial pressure. The condition can lead to significant neurologic deficits.

name: Nummular dermatitis
definition: Cutaneous eruption otherwise known as discoid eczema characterised by discoid or coin-shaped plaques of eczema. The lesions usually occur on the extensor surfaces of the extremities, but the face and trunk may also be involved. The cause is unknown.
synonyms: Discoid eczema | Nummular eczema | discoid eczema | microbial eczema | nummular eczema

name: Macrostomia
definition: Congenital macrostomia or transverse facial cleft is a rare congenital craniofacial anomaly. It is usually associated with deformities of other structures developed from the first and second branchial arches and is thought to be part of the manifestations of hemifacial microsomia, the second most common congenital craniofacial anomaly.
synonyms: Congenital macrostomia | Transverse facial cleft

name: Hereditary benign intraepithelial dyskeratosis
definition: Hereditary benign intraepithelial dyskeratosis is a rare autosomal dominant disorder characterised by elevated epibulbar and oral plaques and hyperaemic conjunctival blood vessels.

name: Autosomal recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa
definition: Dystrophic forms of epidermolysis bullosa with autosomal recessive inheritance.

name: Chapping of the lips
definition: Chapping of the lips occurs as a result of exposure to adverse environmental conditions, particularly freezing cold or hot dry winds. Desiccation of the vermilion results in soreness, cracking and scaling.

name: Simulated skin disease
definition: Simulated skin disease can present in a variety of ways such as application of glue, dyes or make-up to the skin (particularly by children or adolescent girls) and is usually though not always readily recognised for what it is. The motivation for the simulation can vary but may point to some form of psychopathology or may be purely experimentation.
synonyms: Dermatitis simulata | Feigned skin disease

name: Elastosis perforans serpiginosa
definition: An uncommon dermatosis due to transepidermal extrusion of degenerate dermal elastin. It is particularly associated with Down syndrome but may also be seen in association with Ehlers-Danlos, Marfan, and Rothmund-Thomson syndromes, osteogenesis imperfecta and pseudoxanthoma elasticum. Rarely it may be associated with penicillamine therapy. It presents as multiple small keratotic papules arranged in an annular or serpiginous pattern and typically is located on the sides of the neck, the face or the
synonyms: Perforating elastoma | EPS
snomed: 201333007 "elastosis perforans serpiginosa";238876005 "elastosis perforans serpiginosa";49428008 "elastosis perforans serpiginosa"

name: Postmastectomy lymphoedema syndrome
synonyms: Lymphoedema secondary to mastectomy

name: Autosomal dominant hypodontia with nail dysplasia
definition: Hypodontia-nail dysplasia syndrome is a form of ectodermal dysplasia characterised by usually normal primary teeth with permanent teeth that often fail to erupt. The mandibular incisors, second molars, and maxillary canines are frequently absent and the crowns may be small and conical. Lip eversion may be present. Nails are usually small, thin and brittle with longitudinal ridges, pitting and koilonychias. Congenital absence of the nail plate has also been reported. Toenails are usually more sev
synonyms: Tooth and nail syndrome | Witkop syndrome (MIM 189500) | hypodontia with nail dysgenesis | tooth and nail syndrome | witkop syndrome

name: Congenital syphilitic rhinitis
synonyms: Early congenital syphilitic rhinitis

name: Paraneoplastic pemphigus
definition: Paraneoplastic pemphigus is a severe, often fatal autoimmune disease characterised by blisters and erosions not only on the skin and the mucous membranes but also involving other organs including the respiratory system. Auto-antibodies to a variety of plakin components of desmosomes and hemidesmosomes and to the protease inhibitor, Alpha-2-macroglobulin-like-1 protein, have all been implicated in its pathogenesis. It is strongly associated with lymphoproliferative disease.

name: Apocrine adenocarcinoma in situ of unspecified site
Irritant contact dermatitis
definition: Irritant contact dermatitis is an eczematous reaction provoked by acute or prolonged and repeated contact with a substance or substances which are injurious to the skin. Common irritants include defatting agents (solvents, soaps and detergents), acids (both inorganic and organic) and alkalis (e.g. sodium hydroxide and wet cement).
synonyms: ICD - [irritant contact dermatitis] | Irritant contact eczema | irritant contact dermatitis, unspecified cause | irritant dermatitis | non-allergic contact dermatitis | primary irritant dermatitis | chemical burn | housewife's eczema | ICD | irritant dermatitis syndrome

name: Pigmented skin lesion of uncertain nature, upper limb
definition: This denotes the presence of a pigmented skin lesion on an upper limb but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Bowen disease of skin
definition: Intraepidermal squamous cell carcinoma due to predisposing factors including chronic human papilloma virus infection, arsenic ingestion, ionising radtiation and chronic immunosuppression.
synonyms: Bowen type epidermoid carcinoma in situ | Keratinocyte intraepithelial neoplasia grade III

name: Adverse cutaneous reactions to parenteral administration of proteins
definition: Undesired effects with cutaneous manifestations resulting from parenteral administration of proteins. These include vasculitis, serum sickness reaction and anaphylaxis.

name: Moderate plaque psoriasis
definition: Psoriasis may be regarded as moderate if it involves no more than 10% of the skin surface and a) cannot be adequately managed by the patient or carer with topical therapy alone or b) has a significant impact on functioning and quality of life.

name: Cutaneous markers of disorders of the respiratory system
definition: Cutaneous disorders which are commonly associated with lung disease and which may assist in the diagnosis of the latter.

name: Localised lipoatrophy due to panniculitis
definition: Localised lipodystrophy resulting from localised inflammation of subcutaneous fat
synonyms: Localised lipoatrophy secondary to panniculitis

name: Fear of skin disease
definition: A morbid preoccupation with the possibility of developing (e.g. skin cancer), contracting (e.g. contagious skin infections) or being disfigured by skin disease.

name: Neuroectodermal melanolysosomal disease
synonyms: Elejalde disease

name: Periodic fever - aphthous stomatitis - pharyngitis - adenopathy
definition: PFAPA is an autoinflammatory disease associated with classical clinical signs associated to a systemic inflammation. Flares with high fever are alternating with totally asymptomatic periods.
synonyms: Marshall syndrome with periodic fever | PFAPA - [Periodic fever - aphthous stomatitis - pharyngitis - adenopathy] syndrome

name: Chronic autoimmune urticaria
definition: Chronic urticaria due to functional autoantibodies which degranulate basophils and skin mast cells.

name: Eruptive disseminated porokeratosis
definition: A rare widespread eruptive form of porokeratosis which may extend over the whole body surface. It has been associated both with cancer and with underlying immunosuppression.

name: Pemphigus vulgaris
definition: Pemphigus vulgaris is a chronic autoimmune skin disease characterised by blister formation on the skin and the mucous membranes mediated by auto-antibodies to the desmosome components desmoglein 1 and 3.
synonyms: familial pemphigus vulgaris

name: Cryoglobulinaemic vasculitis
definition: Vasculitis with cryoglobulin immune deposits affecting small vessels (predominantly capillaries, venules, or arterioles) and associated with cryoglobulins in serum. Skin and glomeruli are often involved.
synonyms: Mixed cryoglobulinaemia
cn_name: 冷球蛋白血症性血管炎

name: Neonatal streptococcal mastitis
definition: Neonatal mastitis due to Streptococcus pyogenes. This organism is a less common cause of mastitis in the newborn than is Staphylococcus aureus.

name: Food-independent exercise-induced anaphylaxis
definition: Exercise-dependent exercise-induced anaphylaxis which is not known to be associated with IgE-mediated allergy to food

name: Drug-induced pruritus
definition: Pruritus attributable to drugs, in particular opioids.

name: Thrombophlebitis migrans
definition: Thrombophlebitis migrans is characterised by the development of recurrent and migratory superficial thrombophlebitis. It is an acquired coagulopathy that is strongly associated with malignancy, especially solid tumours of the adenocarcinoma type.

name: Lumbosacral naevoid hypertrichosis
definition: A congenital tuft of terminal hair overlying the lower spine which may be associated with underlying spinal dysraphism and spinal cord tethering.
synonyms: Faun tail

name: Plantar warts
definition: Viral warts affecting the plantar surfaces of the feet including the weight-bearing skin of the toes. They are often painful and are difficult to eradicate. In most cases, however, they do eventually resolve spontaneously.
synonyms: Mosaic plantar warts | Verruca plantaris | Verrucae on sole of foot

name: Vasodilatation of extremities
definition: Disorders due to failure of normal vasoconstrictive mechanisms in the cutaneous vasculature.

name: Disseminated zoster
definition: Disseminated herpes zoster indicates the presence of widespread cutaneous involvement extending beyond the primarily affected and directly adjacent dermatomes. It may be associated with impaired immunity resulting from disease or from therapy.
synonyms: disseminated Herpes zoster

name: Developmental anomalies of skin pigmentation
definition: Hamartomatous cutaneous malformations involving melanocytes including congenital pigmented naevi.
cn_name: 皮肤色素沉着发育异常

name: Secondary eczema
definition: An eczematous reaction to the presence of another, usually inflammatory, skin disorder. This may be observed in some forms of psoriasis when the underlying disease may be obscured by a concurrent eczematous reaction.
synonyms: Eczema secondary to disorder classified elsewhere

name: Dermal dendrocyte, Class IIa histiocytoses
definition: A sub-class of cutaneous histiocytic disorders involving dermal dendritic cells.
synonyms: Class IIa histiocytoses | Non-Langerhans cell histiocytoses of dermal dendrocyte lineage
cn_name: 真皮树突细胞，IIa类组织细胞增生症

name: Expanded surgical scar
definition: A widened surgical scar, often resulting from inadequate deep suturing or poor surgical technique. They are common in disorders of connective tissue such as Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
synonyms: Stretched scar | Widened scar

name: Protothecosis
definition: Protothecosis is a rare opportunistic infection in humans caused by achloric algae of the genus Prototheca. The infection is usually localised and may be associated with antecedent local trauma. It is generally located on exposed sites and remains confined to skin and subcutaneous tissues. In immunocompromised patients, however, widespread cutaneous, subcutaneous or deep infection may occur.

name: Bone and joint lesions of yaws
definition: This refers to bone and joint lesions, caused by a tropical infection of the skin, bones and joints caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum pertenue.
synonyms: yaws of bone

name: Cutaneous reactions to Echinoidea
definition: Adverse cutaneous reactions to contact with sea-urchins.
synonyms: Adverse reactions to sea-urchins

name: Burn of head or neck except face, deep partial thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of head and neck except face, deep second degree | corrosion of head or neck except face, deep second degree
cn_name: 头或颈部（除外面部）深层部分皮层烧伤

name: Non-melanin pigmentation due to exogenous substances
definition: Temporary or permanent pigmentation of the skin due to application of or exposure to substances which stain the skin.

name: Papular urticaria
definition: A reaction pattern to insect bites with the formation of multiple itchy, urticated papules or papulovesicles.

name: Secondary lymphoedema
definition: Lymphoedema as a result of an identifiable cause that renders insufficient the function of existing lymphatic vessels.

name: Eczema of male genitalia
definition: Eczema affecting the penis and/or scrotum. Both atopic and seborrhoeic eczema may cause troublesome symptoms in the genital area. The latter may be confined to the genital area.

name: Ichthyosis vulgaris
definition: Ichthyosis vulgaris accounts for 95% of all cases of hereditary ichthyosis. It is an autosomal dominant condition due to filaggrin gene mutations. At birth the skin may appear normal but it gradually becomes dry, rough and scaly, with most signs and symptoms appearing by the age of 5. Ichthyosis vulgaris can affect all parts of the skin surface including the face and scalp though the limb flexures are usually spared. Hyperlinearity of the palms is a characteristic feature. It is closely associat
synonyms: autosomal dominant ichthyosis | ichthyosis simplex | Dominant congenital ichthyosiform erythroderma

name: Oedema due to hypoproteinaemia
definition: Oedema due to reduced intravascular colloidal oncotic pressure. This occurs when the concentration of plasma proteins is significantly reduced as from severe malnourishment, malabsorption, protein loss from the gut (protein-losing enteropathy) or kidneys (nephrotic syndrome), and reduced synthesis (hepatic cirrhosis).
synonyms: Oedema secondary to hypoproteinaemia

name: Extramammary Paget disease of penis
definition: An intraepithelial adenocarcinoma of apocrine gland-bearing skin of penile skin or glans penis. It presents as irritating, itchy, burning, red scaly patches or plaques that may be solitary or multifocal and is frequently misdiagnosed as psoriasis, eczema or Bowen disease.

name: Lower limb venous telangiectases
definition: Finely dilated superficial veins of lower limbs resulting from chronic venous hypertension.
synonyms: Lower limb spider veins | Lower limb venous flares | Reticular leg veins
cn_name: 下肢静脉毛细血管扩张

name: H syndrome
definition: H syndrome is a genodermatosis due to mutations of the SLC29A3 gene and is characterised by progressive sclerodermatous thickening of the skin, with overlying hyperpigmentation and hypertrichosis. It primarily affects the thighs, with variable involvement of the genitalia, lower trunk, and limbs. Systemic findings of hepatosplenomegaly, heart anomalies, hearing loss, hypogonadism and low height give H syndrome its name.
synonyms: histiocytosis-lymphadenopathy plus syndrome

name: Chilblain lupus erythematosus
definition: Chilblain lupus is characterised by the development of red or dusky purple plaques with scarring and atrophy on the fingers and toes especially; the remainder of the skin of the hands and feet, the nose, ears, elbows and knees may, however, also be affected.
synonyms: Chilblain LE - [lupus erythematosus]

name: Ulcer of skin of uncertain nature, lower limb
definition: This denotes the presence of a skin ulcer located on a lower limb but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the ulcer might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

name: Polymorphic light eruption
definition: Polymorphic light eruption is a common delayed‐onset abnormal inflammatory cutaneous reaction to sunlight or other source of ultraviolet radiation. It typically manifests as an eruption of papules and vesicles affecting normally sun-protected skin sites following exposure to sunlight. Tolerance is commonly seen after repeated sunlight or UV exposure.
synonyms: PLE - [polymorphic light eruption] | Polymorphous light eruption | PMLE | polymorphous light eruption

name: Lymphoedema due to chronic inflammation
definition: Lymphoedema resulting from failure of lymphatic drainage when an increased demand for removal of tissue fluid which has accumulated as a result of chronic inflammation overwhelms the capacity of the lymphatics to cope with the increased demand.
synonyms: Lymphoedema secondary to chronic inflammation

name: Folliculitis keloidalis
definition: A type of acneform disorder in which secondary pyogenic infection in and around pilosebaceous structures ends in keloidal scarring. It manifests as persistent folliculitis of the back of the neck associated with occlusion of the follicular orifices and hypertrophic scarring. The process may extend upwards into the scalp. It is most often encountered in black or Asian men.
synonyms: Acne cheloidalis

name: Drug-induced thrombocytopenic purpura
definition: Thrombocytopenic purpura attributable to drug toxicity (e.g. cytotoxic chemotherapeutic or immunosuppressive agents) or to an idiosyncratic drug-associated allergic thrombocytopenia (e.g. quinine, thiazides).
cn_name: 药物性血小板减少性紫癜

name: Cercarial dermatitis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the parasitic worm Schistosoma. This disease is characterised by tingling, burning, itching of the skin, small reddish pimples, or small blisters. Transmission is by direct contact with contaminated water. Confirmation is by identification of Schistosoma eggs in a faecal, urine, or blood sample.
synonyms: Clam-digger's itch | Rice-paddy itch | Sawah itch (Bahasa, Malaysia) | Schistosomal cercarial dermatitis | Sea bather's itch | Swimmer's itch | cercarial dermatitis | clam digger's itch | Cutaneous schistosomiasis | Sea bather's eruption

name: Ectodermal dysplasia - ectrodactyly - macular dystrophy
definition: EEM syndrome is characterised by the association of ectodermal dysplasia, ectrodactyly, and macular dystrophy.
synonyms: EEM - [Ectodermal dysplasia - ectrodactyly - macular dystrophy] syndrome

name: Pigmented hairy epidermal naevus
synonyms: Becker melanosis | Becker naevus

name: Longitudinal melanonychia
definition: Longitudinal melanonychia describes the presence of one or more pigmented streaks arising from under the proximal nail fold and extending towards the free edge of the nail. It is a normal finding in people with deeply pigmented skin but in people with lightly pigmented skin may be the first sign of a nail unit melanoma. More commonly, however, it is due to a benign junctional melanocytic naevus of the nail unit. It may be necessary to perform a biopsy of the nail matrix to distinguish the two.

name: Phototoxic reaction to skin contact with plant matter
definition: Phototoxic reaction caused by a combination of sun exposure and skin contact with plant matter containing photoactive substances, especially furocoumarins found in plants such as cow parsley (Anthriscus sylvestris), giant hogweed (Heracleum mantegazzianum) and celery (Apium graveolens).
synonyms: Phytophotodermatitis | phytophotodermatitis

name: Self-inflicted hair-damaging disorder
definition: Hair disorders resulting from an irresistible compulsion to inflict damage on the hair by pulling, twisting, cutting etc.

name: Erythema nodosum provoked by systemic fungal infection
definition: Erythema nodosum provoked by acute systemic fungal infection including pulmonary histoplasmosis, pulmonary blastomycosis and coccidioidomycosis.

name: Erythrokeratodermia variabilis
definition: A skin disease that is characterized by areas of sharply demarcated, brown hyperkeratosis and has_material_basis_in mutations in genes encoding for connexin channels proteins in the epidermis.
synonyms: Mendes da Costa syndrome | EKV | erythrokeratodermia figurata variabilis | erythrokeratodermia variabilis et progressiva | mendes da costa disease | Erythrokeratodermia Figurata Variabilis | Greither Disease

name: Complete trisomy 18, translocation
synonyms: partial trisomy 18 in Edwards syndrome | trisomy 18 chromosome translocation | trisomy 18 translocation | trisomy 18 translocation syndrome

name: Labial melanin incontinence
definition: This is a condition characterised by spillage of melanin from the basal keratinocytes into the underlying connective tissue of the lips. It is commonly found in inflammatory lesions.

name: Nummular dermatitis, exudative type
definition: An acute or relapsing form of nummular dermatitis or discoid eczema in which the involved plaques tend to be exudative and crusted with secondary impetiginisation.
synonyms: Discoid eczema, exudative type

name: Phakomatosis caesioflammea
synonyms: Phakomatosis pigmentovascularis type 2

name: Non-erosive lichen planus of oral mucosa
definition: Oral lichen planus in which the epithelium remains intact.
cn_name: 口腔黏膜非糜烂性扁平苔藓

name: Burn of trunk except perineum or genitalia, deep partial thickness burn
synonyms: Burn of trunk except perineum and genitalia, deep second degree
cn_name: 躯干（除外会阴或生殖器）深层部分皮层烧伤

name: Concurrent acute and chronic graft-versus-host disease
definition: Graft-versus-host disease in which criteria for both acute and chronic forms of the disease are present concurrently.
synonyms: concurrent acute and chronic GVHD - [graft-versus-host disease]

name: Acne fulminans
definition: Acne fulminans is a severe systemic disease in which acute inflammatory acne with multiple follicular abscesses and skin ulceration is accompanied by fever, weight loss and arthralgia. It typically affects adolescent white males.
synonyms: acute febrile ulcerative acne

name: Wolf-Hirschhorn syndrome
definition: Wolf-Hirschhorn syndrome (WHS) is a developmental disorder characterised by typical craniofacial features, prenatal and postnatal growth impairment, intellectual disability, severe delayed psychomotor development, seizures, and hypotonia.
synonyms: 4p minus syndrome | 4p16.3 deletion

name: Chronic relapsing vesiculosquamous dermatitis of hands
definition: A pattern of chronic hand eczema in which repeated episodes of acute vesicular eczema result in chronic scaling and fissuring of the skin of the fingers and palms.
synonyms: Chronic relapsing vesiculosquamous eczema of hands

name: Late congenital syphilitic oculopathy
definition: This is a late congenital, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is with oculopathy.

name: Lymphocutaneous sporotrichosis
definition: This is the most common type of sporotrichosis and follows implantation of Sporothrix schenckii spores into a cutaneous wound, most commonly on the upper extremity. In addition to a localised nodule or pustule, a chain of nodules develops along draining lymphatics. In longstanding cases regional lymph nodes may become involved.
synonyms: Schenck disease | sporotrichoid sporotrichosis

name: Gnathophyma
definition: hypertrophy of the sebaceous glands of the central chin.

name: Hot comb alopecia
definition: A form of Central centrifugal scarring alopecia which can be attributed to the use of hot combs for hair straightening.

name: Staphylococcal toxin-mediated recalcitrant erythema and desquamation
definition: A syndrome provoked by staphylococcal toxins in which recurrent and prolonged episodes of erythema and extensive cutaneous desquamation occur in association with hypotension, tachycardia, and multiple organ involvement described typically in patients with severe HIV-related immunosuppression.

name: Pressure ulceration grade 2 of upper back
Mucocutaneous leishmaniasis
definition: Mucocutaneous leishmaniasis is a secondary infection of nasal and oral mucosae, predominantly by Leishmania braziliensis. It usually first manifests within two years of initial cutaneous infection but often after the latter has healed. It results from lymphatic or haematogenous spread of infection and can cause severe local tissue destruction.
synonyms: American mucocutaneous leishmaniasis | Espundia | Leishmania braziliensis infection | bush yaws | chiclero ulcer | espundia | forest yaws | pian boi | ulcera de bauru | uta

name: Late syphilitic bursitis, femur
synonyms: syphilitic bursitis, femur

name: Acquired partial lipodystrophy
definition: Acquired partial lipodystrophy, or Barraquer-Simons syndrome, is characterised by the association of lipoatrophy of the upper part of the body and lipohypertrophy of the thighs.
synonyms: Barraquer-Simons syndrome | Progressive cephalothoracic lipodystrophy | APLD | barraquer-simons syndrome | cephalothoracic lipodystrophy | progressive lipodystrophy

name: Malignant neoplasms of penis
definition: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the penis. Representative examples include penile carcinoma and penile sarcoma.
synonyms: cancer of penis | carcinoma of penis | malignant neoplasm of penis, part unspecified | malignant neoplasm of penis, unspecified | malignant neoplasm of skin of penis NOS | malignant tumour of penis | penile cancer | primary malignant neoplasm of penis

name: Adult dermatomyositis with calcinosis
definition: Dermatomyositis with calcinosis is an unusual manifestation in adult patients with dermatomyositis. Calcinosis cutis is manifested by firm, coloured nodules, commonly over bony prominences, that can extrude through the surface of the skin, leading to infections. Calcification of the muscles is generally asymptomatic, although severe forms of calcinosis can cause loss of muscle function.

name: Lichen nitidus
definition: A chronic inflammatory dermatosis of poorly understood aetiology characterised by shiny, flat-topped, asymptomatic, usually flesh-coloured micropapules no larger than the head of a pin. Lesions are localised in the early stages and found chiefly on the lower abdomen, penis, and inner surface of the thighs. Distribution may become generalised as the disease progresses.
synonyms: Pinkus' disease

name: Biotin deficiency
definition: Isolated biotin deficiency is rare. Signs of biotin deficiency in humans have been demonstrated in individuals who consume raw egg white over long periods and in total parenteral nutrition (TPN) before biotin supplementation in patients with malabsorption. The clinical findings of biotin deficiency include dermatitis, conjunctivitis, alopecia, and central nervous system abnormalities. In adults fed raw egg white (which contains avidin, a protein that binds biotin with such high affinity that it
synonyms: biotan vitamin deficiency | biotin deficiency disease

name: Type 1 IgG4 related autoimmune pancreatitis
definition: This is an inflammation of the pancreas that is the pancreatic manifestation of immunoglobulin G4-associated systemic diseases.
synonyms: LPSP - [lymphoplasmacytic sclerosing pancreatitis]

name: Genital warts
definition: Infection of anogenital mucosa or skin by the human papillomavirus. The infection is commonly asymptomatic but manifests typically as flat, papular or pedunculated growths depending on the site of infection. Transmission is normally by sexual contact.
synonyms: Verrucae genitales

name: Epidermoid cyst
definition: A cutaneous cyst with an epidermoid wall filled with keratin and its breakdown products. It most commonly forms as the result of squamous metaplasia in a damaged sebaceous gland but may result from trauma (traumatic inclusion cyst), especially when situated on the extremities. It typically presents as a spherical skin-coloured or yellowish nodule, often with a central pore opening onto the skin surface.
synonyms: Epidermal cyst | Epidermal inclusion cyst | Sebaceous cyst | epidermal cyst | epidermal inclusion cyst | epidermoid inclusion cyst | infundibular cyst

name: Facial clefts
definition: Any condition caused by failure of the structures of the face to correctly develop during the antenatal period. These conditions are characterised by a partition in bone, soft tissue, or skin of the face.
synonyms: Craniofacial clefts

name: Oral-facial-digital syndrome type 3
definition: Oral-facial-digital syndrome, type 3 is characterised by anomalies of the mouth (lobulated hamartomatous tongue, dental abnormalities), eyes (myoclonic twitching of the eyelids, conjugate deviation of the eyes) and digits (postaxial polydactyly), associated with severe intellectual deficit.

name: Heat oedema
definition: A very common form of generally mild and reversible peripheral oedema provoked by exposure to high environmental temperatures and attributable to increased peripheral blood flow with increased capillary filtration overwhelming the ability of lymphatics to remove tissue fluid. The ankles, feet and hands are commonly affected.
synonyms: Reversible peripheral oedema provoked by exposure to heat

name: Late chronic graft-versus-host disease
definition: Late chronic-graft-versus-host disease (GVHD) usually presents between 150 and 300 days after engraftment. It may develop from the early chronic lichenoid form of GVHD or may arise de novo. It is characterised by widespread lichen sclerosus or morphoea-like thickening of the skin and poikiloderma. Blisters, erosions, alopecia, conjunctival scarring and, eventually, deep fibrosis with joint contractures may occur. These may be accompanied by fibrosis of other organs including lung and liver. Late
synonyms: Sclerodermatous chronic graft-versus-host disease | late chronic GVHD - [graft-versus-host disease] | sclerodermatous GVHD - [graft-versus-host disease] | sclerodermatous graft-versus-host disease

name: Hypermelanosis due to endocrine disorder
definition: Diffuse hypermelanosis due to secretion of abnormal amounts of melanocyte stimulating hormones including beta MSH and ACTH. This may be seen with pituitary tumours (e.g. pituitary Cushing syndrome) or following adrenal failure (Addison disease) or adrenalectomy (Nelson syndrome). More rarely it may be seen with acromegaly and with hyperthyroidism.

name: Vitiligo
definition: Vitiligo is an acquired pigmentary disorder of the skin and mucous membranes where progressive destruction of melanocytes results in loss of skin pigmentation. Half of all cases first appear before the age of 20. The clinical course is unpredictable but gradual extension of the areas involved is the norm. The disease may have a devastating psychological impact, particularly in people with dark skin.

name: Chronic meningococcaemia
synonyms: chronic meningococcal septicaemia (deprecated)

name: Diffuse palmoplantar keratodermas
definition: Palmoplantar keratoderma in which there is confluent epidermal thickening affecting the palms and soles.

name: Adult atopic eczema
definition: Atopic eczema in adults (19 years or greater) may persist from childhood, recur in adulthood in individuals with a history of atopic eczema in childhood or, less commonly, may develop de novo in adult life.
synonyms: Adult atopic dermatitis

name: Candida balanoposthitis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the fungi Candida (commonly Candida albicans). This disease is characterised by inflammation of the glans or prepuce. This disease may also present with eroded white papules, or white discharge. Transmission is by sexual contact. Confirmation is by identification of Candida in a sub-preputial swab or urine sample.
synonyms: Balanitis due to candida | Candidal balanitis | Candidosis of penis | Penile thrush

name: Tumour necrosis factor receptor 1 associated periodic syndrome with amyloidosis
Late syphilis involving the musculoskeletal system
definition: This is a late, sexually transmitted infection caused by the spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum. This diagnosis is involving the musculoskeletal system.
cn_name: 晚期梅毒累及肌肉骨骼系统

name: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa, generalised types
definition: Subtypes of junctional epidermolysis bullosa with generalised blistering

name: Pain or tenderness of skin
synonyms: Hyperpathia

name: Zoster keratitis
synonyms: Herpes zoster keratitis | Keratitis due to zoster

name: Miscellaneous inflammatory disorders of oral mucosa
definition: A group of disorders characterised by inflammation of the oral mucosa.

name: Dermatomyofibroma
definition: Dermatomyofibroma is a distinct, biologically benign fibroblastic/myofibroblastic cutaneous proliferation occurring predominantly but not exclusively in young women. It usually presents as a solitary, circumscribed, slow-growing, firm indurated dermal nodule or plaque measuring 1 -2 cm in diameter. It is most commonly located around the axilla, shoulder or neck. Cases with giant or multiple lesions have been reported.
synonyms: Plaque-like dermal fibromatosis

name: Junctional epidermolysis bullosa with respiratory and renal involvement
Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome
definition: Cerebrooculofacioskeletal (COFS) syndrome is a rare genetic disorder, belonging to a family of diseases of DNA repair, characterised by a severe sensorineural involvement. COFS syndrome constitutes the prenatal extreme form of Cockayne syndrome. Clinically, the following criteria are found\: congenital microcephaly, congenital cataract and/or microphthalmia, arthrogryposis, severe psychomotor developmental delay, height-weight growth delay (principally postnatal) and facial dysmorphism (prominen
synonyms: COFS - [Cerebro-oculo-facio-skeletal syndrome] | COFS - [Cerebrooculofacioskeletal syndrome] | Cerebrooculofacioskeletal syndrome | Pena-Shokeir syndrome type 2

name: Chronic infantile neurological cutaneous and articular syndrome with CNS disease
Late congenital neurosyphilis
definition: Neurological sequelae of longstanding (> 2 years) untreated congenital neurosyphilis include mental delay, hydrocephalus, seizures, cerebral infarction and cranial nerve palsies.
synonyms: Congenital neurosyphilis | Juvenile neurosyphilis | juvenile central nervous system syphilis

name: Vasculitis associated with probable aetiology
definition: Vasculitis that is associated with a probable specific etiology. The name (diagnosis) should have a prefix term specifying the association (e.g. hydralazine-associated microscopic polyangiitis, hepatitis B virus-associated vasculitis, hepatitis C virus-associated cryoglobulinemic vasculitis, etc.). (Chapel Hill Consensus Conference, 2011)

name: SAPHO syndrome
definition: SAPHO syndrome is characterised by a constellation of symptoms and signs including synovitis, acne conglobata or fulminans, palmoplantar pustulosis, hyperostosis and osteitis. Its aetiology is poorly understood.
synonyms: Pustulo-psoriatic hyperostotic spondylarthritis | SAPHO - [synovitis, acne, pustulosis, hyperostosis, osteitis] syndrome | synovitis, acne, pustulosis, hyperostosis and osteitis syndrome

name: Chronic radiodermatitis following radiotherapy
definition: The late cutaneous sequelae of the therapeutic use of ionising radiation. It may take five to ten years to develop and is characterised by cutaneous atrophy, fibrosis, dyspigmentation, alopecia and telangiectasia with associated damage to underlying subcutaneous fat.
synonyms: Late radiation reaction
cn_name: 放射治疗后的慢性放射性皮炎

name: Hidrotic ectodermal dysplasia, Halal type
definition: Ectodermal dysplasia Halal type is a hidrotic type of ectodermal dysplasia. Patients have trichodysplasia with absent eyebrows and eyelashes, normal teeth, onychodysplasia (abnormal nails), normal sweating, mild retrognathia, abnormal dermatoglyphics and intellectual deficit.

name: Localised vulval pemphigoid
definition: localised vulval pemphigoid is a rare subtype of bullous pemphigoid that affects children. It is characterised by non-scarring blisters on the vulva and perineum. The identified antigens are the same as bullous pemphigoid and the condition usually responds well to topical treatment.

name: Allergic contact dermatitis of lower leg
definition: Allergic contact dermatitis affecting the lower leg. This is a frequent complication arising from the treatment of lower limb venous eczema.
synonyms: Allergic contact eczema of lower leg

name: Contact urticaria due to food allergen
definition: Contact urticaria due to food allergen is a IgE-mediated immediate immune reaction from cutaneous or mucosal contact to food allergen in a sensitized patient.
synonyms: Food allergen-induced contact urticaria | Food-induced allergic contact urticaria
cn_name: 食物变应原引起的接触性荨麻疹

name: Mosaicism 45, x; 46, xy
synonyms: 45, x/46, xy mosaicism

name: Papular xanthoma
definition: Papular xanthoma is a rare benign histiocytic disorder of unknown aetiology which may occur both in adults and children. It is characterised by yellow or reddish-yellow papules and nodules affecting both skin and mucous membranes. The back and head are most commonly affected. Histologically, there is an upper- and mid-dermal infiltrate of foamy histiocytes and giant cells. In adults the disease may be progressive whereas in children slow spontaneous resolution is the norm.
synonyms: Papular xanthogranuloma

name: Angiosarcoma of skin
definition: A malignant tumour arising from the endothelial cells of the blood vessels. Microscopically, it is characterised by frequently open vascular anastomosing and branching channels. The malignant cells that line the vascular channels are spindle or epithelioid and often display hyperchromatic nuclei. Angiosarcomas most frequently occur in the skin and breast. Patients with long-standing lymphoedema are at increased risk of developing angiosarcoma.
synonyms: Cutaneous angiosarcoma | Haemangiosarcoma of skin | Lymphangiosarcoma of skin | Malignant haemangioendothelioma of skin | angiosarcoma of skin | hemangiosarcoma of the skin

name: Heat contact urticaria
definition: Contact urticaria triggered by local warming of the skin.

name: Tabes dorsalis
definition: This is a slow degeneration (specifically, demyelination) of the sensory neurons that carry afferent information. The degenerating nerves are in the dorsal columns (posterior columns) of the spinal cord (the portion closest to the back of the body) and carry information that help maintain a person's sense of position (proprioception), vibration, and discriminative touch.
synonyms: neurosyphilis taboparesis | tabes paresis

name: Diffuse neonatal haemangiomatosis
definition: Diffuse neonatal hemangiomatosis is a rare vascular tumour from unknown origin characterised by multiple, progressive, rapidly growing cutaneous hemangiomas (e.g. in the scalp, face, trunk and extremities) associated with widespread visceral hemangiomas in the liver, lungs, gastrointestinal tract, brain, and meninges.

name: Corrosion of first degree of ankle or foot
Neuropathic skin ulceration
definition: Ulceration of the skin resulting from impairment of pain sensation due to sensory nerve dysfunction.
synonyms: Neuropathic ulcer

name: Dermatophytosis of scalp
definition: Dermatophytosis (tinea) affecting scalp and scalp hair. Clinical features range from limited patchy alopecia and scaling to widespread inflammation and suppuration with occipital lymphadenopathy. The scalp is a typical site for a kerion (q.v.), often due to a zoophilic dermatophyte acquired from an infected animal.
synonyms: Scalp ringworm | Tinea capitis

name: Dermatoses which may presage cutaneous lymphoma
definition: Dermatoses which may represent the earliest stages of cutaneous lymphoma but where it is not possible to confirm their neoplastic nature.
cn_name: 可进展为皮肤淋巴瘤的皮肤病

name: Subcutaneous swelling, mass or lump of uncertain or unspecified nature
definition: One or more localised subcutaneous soft tissue masses of undetermined or unspecified nature
synonyms: localised mass | localised skin mass | localised skin swelling | localised subcutaneous nodules | localised swelling | localised swelling, mass or lump of skin and subcutaneous tissue | subcutaneous nodules | superficial mass | superficial mass NOS | superficial subcutaneous nodules
cn_name: 性质未确定或未特指的皮下肿胀、肿物或肿块

name: Comedo naevus
synonyms: Naevus comedonicus

name: Acrocyanosis
definition: Acrocyanosis is a persistent cyanotic or erythrocyanotic mottled discoloration of the hands and, less commonly, feet and face. It may be idiopathic or secondary to a range of systemic disorders, including anorexia nervosa, antiphospholipid antibody syndrome and paraproteinaemia.

name: Follicular lichen planus
definition: Lichen planus involving the hair follicle rather than the epidermis. It typically involves the scalp but may be seen elsewhere. Clinically it presents as grouped small, slightly scaly erythematous follicular papules.
synonyms: Lichen planus follicularis

name: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency, non-classic form
definition: This refers to any of several autosomal recessive diseases resulting from mutations of genes for enzymes mediating the biochemical steps of production of cortisol from cholesterol by the adrenal glands (steroidogenesis). This diagnosis is due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency, nonclassic form.
synonyms: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia due to 21-hydroxylase deficiency, non salt-losing

name: Prolidase deficiency
definition: Prolidase deficiency is a very rare inborn error of metabolism characterised by mild to severe skin lesions particularly on the face, palms, lower legs and soles, together with other variable features.
synonyms: Hyperimidodipeptiduria | Imidodipeptiduria | Proline dipeptidase deficiency
cn_name: 脯氨酰氨基酸二肽酶缺乏

name: Dermochondrocorneal dystrophy
definition: Dermochondrocorneal dystrophy is a rare autosomal recessive genetic disorder characterised by abnormalities in the skin, the bones of the extremities and the corneae. Nodules resembling xanthomata develop over the dorsa of the fingers, the tips of the elbows and on the nose and ears; their histogenesis is unclear but may be due to anomalous control of fibroblast or chondrocyte proliferation and secretion. Deformities of the hands and feet result from abnormal ossification of cartilage. Additiona

name: Drug-induced pseudoporphyria
definition: The development of blistering of exposed skin on the extremities resembling porphyria cutanea tarda without demonstrable abnormalities of porphyrin metabolism but with exposure to a known precipitant drug. High dose furosemide, non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, especially naproxen, nalidixic acid, tetracyclines and sulphonylureas have all been implicated.

name: Mucopolysaccharidosis type 7
definition: Mucopolysaccharidosis type VII (MPS VII) is a very rare lysosomal storage disease belonging to the group of mucopolysaccharidoses. Clinical signs are extremely variable\: there are prenatal forms with non-immune hydrops fetalis, and severe neonatal forms with dysmorphism, hernias, hepatosplenomegaly, club feet, dysostosis, severe hypotonia and neurological disorders that ultimately lead to profound intellectual deficit and small stature in patients that survive. Finally, there are also very mild
synonyms: Beta-glucuronidase deficiency | Sly disease | Sly syndrome

name: Localised lipoatrophy of undetermined cause
definition: Localised lipoatrophy the cause or nature of which it has not been possible to determine.

name: Amalgam-associated oral lichenoid reaction
definition: Amalgam-associated oral lichenoid reaction is an uncommon allergic reaction following long exposure to dental amalgam fillings. The buccal mucosa and the border of the tongue are the most common sites. Patch testing to mercury is commonly positive.
synonyms: Lichenoid amalgam reaction | contact lichenoid reaction | lichenoid amalgam reaction

name: Acne
definition: Acne without further specification.
synonyms: Acne vulgaris | acne

name: Congenital toxoplasmosis
definition: A disease caused by an infection with the protozoan parasite Toxoplasma gondii in utero. This disease is characterised by chorioretinitis, hydrocephalus, intracranial calcifications, anaemia, or neurological deficits that develop after birth. This disease may present at birth with jaundice, premature birth, hepatosplenomegaly, myocarditis, pneumonitis, or rash. Transmission is by vertical transmission. In the fetus, confirmation is by identification of Toxoplasma gondii in amniotic fluid; in the

name: Cyst of tongue
synonyms: tongue cyst

name: Macrotia
definition: 1) Median longitudinal ear length greater than 2 SD above the mean and median ear width greater than 2 SD above the mean (objective).  2) Apparent increase in length and width of the pinna (subjective).
synonyms: Congenital big ears

name: Bullous pemphigoid variants
definition: Less common variants of bullous pemphigoid include lichen planus pemphigoides, localised pemphigoid, vulval pemphigoid and pemphigoid nodularis.

name: Piezogenic pedal papules
definition: Skin-coloured papules and nodules on the sides of the heels induced by weight-bearing. They are thought to be due to herniation of fat through defects in the subcutaneous fascia. They may occasionally be painful.
synonyms: piezogenic papules

name: Fanconi anaemia
definition: Fanconi anaemia (FA) is a hereditary DNA repair disorder characterised by progressive pancytopenia with bone marrow failure, variable congenital malformations and predisposition to develop haematological or solid tumours.
synonyms: Fanconi pancytopenia | Fanconi pancytopenia syndrome

name: Extragenital granuloma inguinale
definition: Klebsiella granulomatis, the causative organism of granuloma inguinale, may rarely cause localised or disseminated infection of extragenital sites including lymph nodes and bone.
synonyms: Disseminated granuloma inguinale

name: Skin deposit, not elsewhere classified
synonyms: deposition in skin NOS

name: Bannayan-Riley-Ruvalcaba syndrome
definition: Bannayan-Riley-Ruvalcaba syndrome is characterised by an abnormally large head (macrocephaly), multiple hamartomatous polyps in the intestines, benign tumours of fatty tissues just below the skin, and excessive growth before and after birth.
synonyms: BRRS

name: Impairment of motor nerve function resulting from cutaneous surgical procedure
definition: Injury to motor nerve from skin surgery resulting in impairment of motor function. The facial nerve is at particular risk.

name: Epithelioid haemangioma of skin
definition: A benign neoplasm most commonly affecting the skin and characterised by local proliferation of vascular channels. Affected individuals typically present with a cluster of small, translucent nodules on the head and neck, particularly around the ear or the hairline. The neoplasm may also rarely arise in oral mucosa or in internal organs.
synonyms: Angiolymphoid hyperplasia with eosinophilia of skin | Pseudopyogenic granuloma of skin

name: Sea mat dermatitis
definition: An allergic contact dermatitis from prolonged contact with sea chervil, Alcyonidium diaphanum.
synonyms: Dogger Bank itch | Sea chervil dermatitis

name: Hypertrichosis of upper eyelid
definition: This refers to an abnormal amount of hair growth over the eyelid; extensive cases of hypertrichosis have informally been called werewolf syndrome, because the appearance is similar to the werewolf. Hypertrichosis can be either congenital (present at birth) or acquired later in life.

name: Herpes simplex infection of perianal skin or rectum
definition: Herpes simplex infection of perianal skin and rectum. This is commonly due to Herpes simplex virus type 2 and acquired through anal sexual contact.
synonyms: Herpetic proctitis
cn_name: 肛周皮肤或直肠单纯疱疹病毒感染

name: Disturbances of skin sensation of unspecified aetiology
definition: A group of cutaneous symptoms for which it is frequently impossible to identify a precise cause.
cn_name: 未特指病因的皮肤感觉异常

name: Cicatricial pemphigoid, Brunsting-Perry type
definition: A rare scarring immunobullous skin disorder due to autoantibodies to components of the epidermal basement membrane, particularly the anchoring filament component laminin 5 and the hemidesmosome-associated proteins BP180 and BP230. A proportion of patients can be shown to have antibodies to collagen VII and are therefore considered to have a variant of epidermolysis bullosa acquisita (Epidermolysis bullosa acquisita\: Brunsting-Perry type).

name: Annular and circinate pustular psoriasis
definition: Patterns of subacute or chronic pustular psoriasis which may be confused with erythema annulare centrifugum. Lesions begin as discrete areas of erythema which become raised and oedematous before expanding centrifugally to form annular and circinate plaques. Pustules appear at the advancing margins and leave a trailing fringe of scale as the plaques enlarge.

name: Histiocytic-granulomatous disorders of the skin
definition: A range of disorders characterised by the presence of increased numbers of histiocytes in the skin as a result of granulomatous inflammation or histiocytic infiltration.
synonyms: granulomatous disorders of the skin | granulomatous skin diseases | histiocytic disorders of the skin | histiocytosis of the skin | skin granuloma NOS
